data.markdown:
[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FNo-Mail-Delivery) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000008yTAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery) ## No mail delivery? ## Reasons there may be no mail delivery. - Oct 30, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007110 Customer Information It is normal for an address to not have any mail slated for delivery on a given day, or to have mail delivery times fluctuate daily. If you have not received any mail within a two (2) day period or if you are not receiving mail regularly (i.e. on a specific day of the week) for two (2) or more weeks in a row, please [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm). **Conditions and events that may prevent delivery of mail:** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Mailbox blocked](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#Mailbox_Blocked)** | **[Dog on premises](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#DogOnPremises)** | **[Hazardous conditions /\**
**\**
**natural disasters](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#HazardousNatural)** | | **[Full mailbox / overflow](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#FullMailboxAndOverflow)** | **[Travel obstructions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#TravelObstructions)** | [**No Mailbox**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#NoMailbox) | **Mailbox blocked** - Customers are required, as a condition of delivery, to ensure that proper access is provided to mail receptacles.  Proper access includes the removal of large accumulations of snow from the area around curb line receptacles and from sidewalks leading to door or other house‑mounted receptacles. Without such access, the safety of the carrier is jeopardized. - Mailbox blockage by a vehicle may also prevent the delivery of mail. According to our policy, the city or rural carrier should get out of the vehicle to make delivery if the mailbox is temporarily blocked by a vehicle. However, if the carrier continually experiences a problem in serving curb line or rural boxes where the customer is able to control on street parking, the postmaster may withdraw delivery service. **Dog on the premises** - Delivery service may be suspended when there is an immediate threat (including, but not limited to, threats due to loose animals) to the delivery employee, mail security, or postal property. - Delivery service may be temporarily withdrawn when animals interfere with our ability to complete mail delivery. Owners must confine their dogs during delivery hours and be notified promptly if service is suspended.  Mail delivery will resume as soon as the Postal Service is confident the animal is no longer a threat. - Loose dogs can affect mail delivery for multiple addresses and an entire neighborhood. **Hazardous conditions / natural disasters** - Mail delivery service may be delayed or curtailed whenever streets or walkways present hazardous conditions to our carriers and/or vehicles. The Postal Service™ curtails delivery only after careful consideration, and only as a last resort. We appreciate your understanding of our responsibility for the safety of our employees, as well as of our customers. - [USPS Service Alerts](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/) provide information to consumers, small businesses and business mailers about postal facility service disruptions due to weather-related and other natural disasters or events. If your mail cannot be delivered because accumulated snow prevents the carrier from reaching your box, you may choose any one of the following alternatives: - Arrange with a neighbor to receive your mail. - Put up a suitable temporary mail box (for example, a sturdy box). - Meet the carrier at your box. - Pick up your mail at your local Post Office™ location. Your postmaster will be pleased to discuss these alternatives so that you may select the one most convenient for you. [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#top) **Full mailbox and overflow** - If a mail receptacle is deemed by the letter carrier to be full, the letter carrier will leave a ["We ReDeliver for You" form (PS Form 3849](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)[)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) in that receptacle and return the overflow mail to the local Post Office™ location for pickup. The LPO will automatically hold accumulated mail for up to 10 days. After ten days, mail is returned to sender. The recipient can get the overflow mail by: - Scheduling a Redelivery by completing the back of the [PS Form 3849](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) \- Someone must be available to receive the overflow mail when Redelivery occurs. - Picking up the overflow mail from the LPO listed on the Delivery Notice. You will need to bring [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) for pickup. **Travel obstructions** - Persons responsible for road maintenance must be notified of road conditions obstructing the delivery of mail. If repairs are not made promptly, service may be withdrawn. **No Mailbox** - A mailbox/ mail receptacle is required for mail delivery. For additional information on box requirements please see one of the following articles: - [Requirements for city delivery mail receptacles](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles) - [General Guidelines and Policies for Rural Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/General-Guidelines-and-Policies-for-Rural-Delivery) [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title No mail delivery? ## What is the USPS Mail Recovery Center?

## The Mail Recovery Center is the U.S. Postal Service's official "lost and found" department for undeliverable and non-returnable mail.      - Jan 22, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007943

Customer Information

Undeliverable mail determined to be of value, meaning the content is worth more than $25.00 ($20.00 for mailpieces containing cash), will be held for 60 days if the mailpiece is barcoded, or 30 days for non-barcoded mailpieces.

Mailpieces are scanned and those that are determined to contain items of value are opened in an attempt to identify an address where the piece can be forwarded or returned.

The Mail Recovery Center (MRC) is the U.S. Postal Service's official "lost and found" department for undeliverable and non-returnable mail. Undeliverable mailpieces are letters, flats or parcels that cannot be delivered as addressed nor returned to the sender.

**How Does the USPS® Dispose of Items of Value if the Owner Cannot Be Located?**

Occasionally, the MRC will auction off the items held. More information on upcoming auctions, including locations and directions, can be found at [United States Postal Service® auctions](https://www.govdeals.com/unitedstatespostalservice). Auctions usually start at 10:00 AM with the viewing of the available merchandise beginning at 8:30 AM. Merchandise is sold in lots, not by individual item, and catalogs may be purchased for a nominal fee on the day of the auction.

The MRC disposes of unclaimed merchandise and items not meeting retention guidelines in the following manner:

- Charitable or Welfare Organizations - donations to nonprofit organizations
- Trash, Recycle and Shred - food items, metals, cardboard, paper, etc.
- Auction - [contracted company handling the sale of auctionable merchandise](https://www.govdeals.com/index.cfm?fa=Main.AdvSearchResultsNew&agency=4703 "GovDeals Online Auction")

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000aY9AAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) ## Delayed mail and packages? ## A chart with delivery standards based on the class of mail, when your carrier or mail will arrive, and what to do if mail or periodicals are considered late. - Mar 31, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007099 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[**When will my letter carrier or mail arrive?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#carrierarrive)** | **[**When is mail considered late? Check the mail delivery standards**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#check_mail_delivery_standards)** | **[**Check USPS Tracking®**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#check_USPS_Tracking)** | | **[**Contact the shipper**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#contact_shipper)** | **[**Who do I contact if my magazine / periodical is late?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#contact_if_magazine_is_late)** | **[**Who do I notify if my mail is late?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#who_do_I_notify_if_late)** | USPS® provides several tools and options to help determine where your mailpiece is in the mailstream and if it is delayed or not. Sometimes packages are delayed or rerouted because of unusual circumstances. Before contacting us, please check the mail delivery standards, [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action) page (if available), and/or contact the shipper for further information on your expected mail. **When will my letter carrier or mail arrive?** **We do not have the ability to find out when a carrier or delivery person will arrive at a specific location.** The volume of mail fluctuates daily; therefore, delivery times are not guaranteed. All deliveries should be made by 5:00 p.m. local time Monday through Saturday. Unusual circumstances (such as traffic, staffing fluctuations, severe weather, natural disaster, changes in carrier route, etc.) can cause deliveries to be made after this time. **Please Note**: Priority Mail Express® mailpieces and Amazon parcels are the only pieces of mail delivered on Sunday. **When is mail considered late? Check the mail delivery standards.** Delivery time varies from one class of mail to another. It may be possible that the mailpiece is still in transit and is not lost or delayed depending on what class of mail is being used and how long ago it was mailed. The following chart outlines the delivery standards for various mail classes and indicates how long you should wait before you contact customer assistance with a concern. | | | | --- | --- | | **MAIL CLASS** | **DELIVERY STANDARD** | | _**Priority Mail Express®**_ | **1-3** calendar days (guaranteed) | | **_Priority Mail®_** | **2-3** business days (not guaranteed) | | **_First-Class Mail®_** | **1-5** business days (not guaranteed) | | **_USPS Ground Advantage®_** | **2-5** business days\* (not guaranteed) | | | --- | | \* **_If it contains [Hazardous Materials](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials) / Live Animals_**
**2-8** business days\* (not guaranteed) | | | **_Parcel Select®_** | **2-8** business days\* (not guaranteed) | | **_Package Services:_**
- **_Media Mail®_**
- **_Bound Printed Matter_**
- **_Library Mail_** | **2-8** business days\* (not guaranteed) | | _**Periodicals**_ | **3-9** business days\* (not guaranteed) | | **_USPS Marketing Mail®_** | **3-10** business days (not guaranteed) | **Note:****\*** Except Alaska, Hawaii and US Territories - estimate provided by the [postage price calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/ "USPS - Postage Price Calculator"). If you wish to contact a customer representative, [email us](https://usps.force.com/emailus/s/) your concern with as much detail as possible and we'll get back to you. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages#top) **Check USPS Tracking®** If you have a tracking number for your item you may go to the [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") page and enter the tracking number from the mailing label or receipt. [Click here to view sample tracking numbers by mail service](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action). Enter up to 35 tracking numbers separated by commas and click 'Track.' The most recent tracking status of your mailpiece(s) should appear. If [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") status indicates your item is eligible for additional research, the appropriate next steps will be provided on the website. **Contact the shipper** **If you DO NOT HAVE the tracking number from the mailing label or receipt (recipient), please contact the shipper.** If you were notified by a shipper that they have sent out a package and believe it has been **delayed** or possibly lost, contact the mailer of the item and request them to: 1. Provide the tracking number, or; 2. Track the package **Who do I contact if my magazine / periodical is late?** If you are experiencing a delay in receiving magazines or periodicals, please contact the publisher of the magazine to ensure they have your correct information and the subscription has not expired. **Who do I notify if my mail is late?** Your [local Post Office](https://www.usps.com/locator/welcome.htm) ™ location should be able to address most of your concerns. Often there are identifying marks on an envelope that pinpoint the cause of a delay. Record the delivery date on the envelope and present to your [local Post Office](https://www.usps.com/locator/welcome.htm) ™location for examination. [Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FUSPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000wnBAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) ## USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics ## How to set up USPS Delivery Instructions™ and item eligibility. - Dec 18, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007839 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[What is it and why should I use it?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#whatiseasr)**
**[Is my shipment (mailpiece) eligible for Delivery Instructions?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#eligible_for_Delivery_Instructions)**
**[How do I set up Instructions for my deliveries?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#how_do_I_set_up_instructions)**
**[Is there a fee for USPS Delivery Instructions service?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#is_there_a_fee_for_usps)** | **[Will I receive any email notification of my USPS Delivery Instructions service request?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#notification)**
**[Can I cancel my USPS Delivery Instructions request?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#cancel)**
**[What location options are available with Delivery Instructions?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#locations)** | **[Can I send my package to another location?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#can_I_send_my_package)**
**[Damaged Packaging](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#damaged_packaging)**
**[How can I request a refund?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#How_can_I_request_a_refund)** | **What is it and why should I use it?** With USPS Delivery Instructions service, you--the addressee/intended recipient--can specify and authorize how certain incoming shipments and mailpieces are to be delivered, BEFORE they are delivered. Once you set up Instructions electronically, items can be left at a specific location at your address, your [Local Post Office](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6eq0000000azZAAQ/view), or even at a different domestic address – all options are listed online. If the mail item is eligible, you can even upgrade the mail class and/or add Extra Services. **NOTE**: To see **all** Delivery Instructions setup options – free AND paid – be sure to access Delivery Instructions through your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Is my shipment (mailpiece) eligible for Delivery Instructions?** The main eligibility condition is a barcode. Your mailpiece must bear a USPS Tracking® barcode or an Extra Service barcode so that you can check online and confirm eligibility. Here’s how to do it on [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/) or the [USPS Tracking page](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input): 1. Enter the [tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number) from the Shipping Label or Receipt into the field labeled "Tracking Number" and click on "Search." 2. The most recent tracking status of your item should appear. 3. If your shipment is eligible for USPS Delivery Instructions service, the words "Delivery Instructions" will be displayed under the tracking status information. Click on "Delivery Instructions" to see the available options. **Not EligibleYou should note that these items / situations ARE NOT eligible for Delivery Instructions:** - Shipments that are insured for $500 or more - International mail items - Mailpiece is out for delivery, or already delivered - Addressed to a military address - Delivery signature is required - Shipments with Collect on Delivery service - Address with an active Hold Mail request - Item has an active Hold For Pickup - Item has an active USPS Package Intercept® service - The address has a Temporary Forwarding service - Item has an invalid, insufficient or otherwise undeliverable address If any of the above situations is in place, Delivery Instructions will **not appear** as an option for you when you check your tracking number online. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **How do I set up Instructions for my deliveries?** After checking to ensure eligibility on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) (see above), follow these steps and online prompts: 1. Click on “Delivery Instructions” next to your shipment tracking number. (If the link for Delivery Instructions is not shown next to your shipment tracking number, this indicates your item is not eligible for Delivery Instructions.) 2. The system will ask you to login to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account (you can set one up at that time, if you don’t already have one). 3. Follow the prompts to see if your address is eligible and to then specify instructions for delivery. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Is there a fee for USPS Delivery Instructions service?** **That depends on which Delivery Instruction service you choose:** **No Cost Options** - Delivery (redelivery) to a specific location at your address. - Delivery to a neighbor’s home. (MUST be on the same carrier route). - Hold for Pickup at your post office (your ZIP Code). **Postage / Upgrade Fee Options** - Directing mail item delivery to a different domestic address (outside your carrier’s route/ZIP Code) – _Pay the additional postage_. - Hold for Pick up at a different Post Office (different ZIP Code) – _Pay the additional postage_. - Upgrading mail service to Priority Mail® or Priority Mail Express® - _Pay the upgrade fee_. - Adding Extra Services (when eligible) – _Pay the Extra Service fee_. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Will I receive any email notifications of my USPS Delivery Instructions request?** **Yes. Based on the option(s) you picked when you set up Delivery Instructions, you will receive one or more of the following:** - An email to confirm that your request was successful and an email to confirm your shipment has been delivered. - An email if you made a request that requires payment, and the actual postage exceeds the estimated postage that was provided at the time of the request. - If the article has to be repackaged, you will receive an email with the new USPS Tracking Number. - If the article is undeliverable, you will be notified of the reason for non-delivery. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Can I cancel my USPS Delivery Instructions request?** No. Once you submit your request, it is final. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **What location options are available with Delivery Instructions?** **You can request that your eligible mailpiece be left at the following locations options if the item does not fit in your mailbox:** - Front Door - Back Door - Side Door - In/At Mailbox - On the Porch - Neighbor \[address required - must be on the same street, ZIP Code, and delivery route as your address\] - Garage - Other \[additional instructions required\] [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Can I send my package to another location?** Yes - you (the addressee) have the option to send your eligible package to a different address or to a Post Office™ for pickup, but there is a postage fee. Here’s how it works: 1. You will not be charged a base fee to place a request to change your package’s destination; however, you will be required to pay for the postage once the package is successfully acquired from the mailstream and on its way to the new address. 2. When the online request is made, you will be provided with an “estimated postage” amount. It’s “estimated” because USPS must later weigh and rate your package when it arrives at the Post Office™ in order to assess the actual postage due. If the amount calculated at the Post Office is more than the estimate given to you online, we will collect the difference upon delivery. You can pay in cash, check, or money order when your package arrives. 3. You will be notified via email or text if your package was or was not successfully delivered to the new address/location. Some common conditions for non-delivery include: - The package is out on the street for delivery. - The credit or debit card is no longer valid. - The post office was unable to locate the package. - The package was intercepted by the merchant (sender). [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **Damaged Packaging** If the original mailpiece is damaged, the Delivery Instructions request cannot be completed and the mail item will be treated as any other damaged mail item: We will place the damaged mailpiece in a secure, plastic bag, mark it “Damaged”, and deliver to the original mailing address. In this case, you will receive an email explanation as to why your Delivery Instructions request was not fulfilled. For other mail delivery issues, see " [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages)" [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) **How can I request a refund?** **You may contact the Payment Inquiries Team by e-mail** ( [cnspayment@usps.gov](mailto:cnspayment@usps.gov)) **for any issues or concerns regarding payment for USPS Delivery Instruction requests. Please include the words, “Delivery Instructions” in the subject line of your email request along with the following order details:** - Payment Related Issue (Including Refund Requests) - Tracking Number - Order or Confirmation Number - Date of Order/Request [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics URL Name USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FTypes-of-First-Class-Mail) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq000000155hAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail) ## Types of First-Class Mail® ## First-Class Mail® service is the least expensive, most immediate option for mailing postcards, letters, and large envelopes (flats). - Mar 3, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006797 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | **[Types of First-Class Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#what_are)** | **[USPS Tracking® and Other Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#other_extra)** | | **[Basic Pricing for First-Class Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#Basic_pricing_Retail)** | **[Weight and Size Standards for First-Class Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#weight_standards_retail)** | **Types of First-Class Mail®** **First-Class Mail® service** is the least expensive, most immediate options for mailing postcards, letters, and large envelopes (flats) to retail customers at retail pricing. Commercial pricing, which is discounted from retail pricing, is available as **First-Class Mail** service for customers with large volumes of mail and who invest time preparing and sorting mail. | | | --- | | **Some qualifications for mailing at the Commercial Prices for First-Class Mail Service include:**
- Signing a customer commitment agreement.

- Different prices are available for cards, letters, and flats compatible with different standards. To qualify for lower rates, mailpieces must meet specific presorting, addressing, barcoding, and design standards.

- For additional information on Commercial Mail, see:
- Business Shipping - Rates, Discounts, Services ( [https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm "Business Shipping - Features & Tools | USPS"))

- [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) | **Estimated delivery time** is one (1) to five (5) business days (not including Sundays and [postal holidays](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/events/)) for First-Class Mail. - If you want an official record of the exact mailing date, you may wish to pay an extra service fee for a Certificate of Mailing. [See all insurance and extra services](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm). - If you want to ensure that your mailpiece receives a postmark and that the postmark date aligns with the date of mailing, bring your mailpiece to any USPS retail location and ask for a manual postmark at the counter. It will be applied free of charge. See [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail). First-Class Mail (retail and commercial) items are **sealed against inspection**. | | | --- | | First-Class Mail (retail) is:
- **Not a guaranteed service**. Delivery by a specific date or at a specific time is not available.
- **Not eligible for postage refunds.** Exceptions are if no service is rendered or if the amount collected was more than the lawful price. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#top) **USPS Tracking® and Other Extra Services** **Can I track a First-Class Mail® item?** No. First-Class Mail does not include USPS Tracking®. Although First-Class Mail postcards, letters, and flats are not eligible for USPS Tracking, those items are eligible for other extra services that provide barcodes for scanning. These extra services can be purchased, providing some tracking information. See " [What Domestic Mail Extra Services are Available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available)" for a list of all extra services and what mail classes are eligible. **What can I do if my First-Class Mail item has not arrived?** If the expected item has not arrived after **five (5)** days from its date of mailing, you can document your mail loss complaint by [emailing us](https://emailus.usps.com/s/?_gl=1*1xwirqx*_gcl_au*OTMyMjg1MjIuMTY4MjAwNjE1NA..*_ga*MTkwOTc2MDUxMi4xNjU4MjQ1NTAz*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY4Njc3MDE3MC40NC4xLjE2ODY3NzAzOTMuMC4wLjA.). If your mailpiece is located, it will be returned to you. | | | --- | | First-Class Mail **does not include insurance against loss, damage, or missing contents**. These items are eligible for [extra services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) that can be purchased to provide insurance. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#top) **Basic Pricing for First-Class Mail® Service** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | | **Retail Mail Pricing** | **Commercial Mail Pricing** | | **First-Class Mail Postcard** | - Single postcard: **$0.61**
- Stamped postcard: **$0.67** | See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c096 "Notice 123 | Postal Explorer") for detailed prices | | **First-Class Mail Letter** | - First ounce: **$0.78**
- Each additional ounce: **$0.29** | See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c096 "Notice 123 | Postal Explorer") for detailed prices | | **First-Class Mail Metered Mail Letter** | - First ounce: **$0.74**
- Each additional ounce: **$0.29** | | **First-Class Mail Flat (Large Envelope)** | - First ounce: **$1.63**
- Each additional ounce:
- **$0.27 (up to first 4 ounces)**
- **$0.28 (4-9 ounces)**
- **$0.30 (9-13 ounces)** | See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c096 "Notice 123 | Postal Explorer") for detailed prices | | The price of a First-Class Mail item varies depending on the: | | | | --- | --- | | - Type of mailing
- Weight of the item
- Size and shape of the mailing | - How postage is paid
- Whether it is subject to a nonmachinable surcharge | To determine the price of your specific First-Class Mail item, go to the [Postage Price Calculator](http://postcalc.usps.gov/). The [Business Price Calculator](https://dbcalc.usps.com/ "USPS Business Price Calculator") is available to determine prices for First-Class Mail Commercial. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#top) **Weight and Size Standards for First-Class Mail®** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | | ****Retail Mail**** | ****Commercial Mail**** | | **First-Class Mail Postcards** | ➢ Price is less than a one-ounce letter. Uses a Postcard stamp.
➢ Size requirements:
- Minimum Size: 3½ inches by 5 inches

- Maximum Size: 4¼ inches by 6 inches

- Thickness: 0.007 inch to 0.016 inch

➢ Pieces that do not meet the minimum dimensions are nonmailable
➢ Postcards exceeding the size requirements are subject to [letter-size pricing](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#First-Class_Mail_Letters_Size) | ➢ Price is less than a one-ounce letter. Uses a Postcard stamp.
➢ Size requirements:
- Minimum Size: 3½ inches by 5 inches

- Maximum Size: 6 inches by 9 inches

- Minimum Thickness:
- Postcards up to 4¼ inches by 6 inches: 0.007 inches

- Postcards more than either 4¼ inches high or 6 inches long: 0.009 inches
- Maximum Thickness: 0.016 inches | | **First-Class Mail Letters**​​​ | ➢ Price paid on a per ounce basis – based on the First-Class Mail stamp price
- Maximum Weight: 3.5 ounces – Additional ounces above one ounce have a separate per ounce price

➢ Size Requirements:
- Minimum Size: 3½ inches by 5 inches

- Maximum Size: 6⅛ inches by 11½ inches

- Thickness: 0.009 inch to ¼ inch

➢ Pieces that do not meet the minimum dimensions are nonmailable.
➢ Letters that do not meet certain size and shape requirements may also be charged a separate [Nonmachinable Surcharge](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-Non-Machinable-Surcharge-for-First-Class-Mail).
➢ Pieces that exceed the maximum dimensions or weight are priced as Flat (Large Envelope) mailpiece. | ➢ Price paid on a per ounce basis
- Maximum Weight: 3.5 ounces – Additional ounces above one ounce have a separate per ounce price

➢ Size Requirements for Machinable First-Class Mail Letters:
- Minimum Size: 3½ inches by 5 inches

- Maximum Size: 6⅛ inches by 11½ inches

- Thickness: 0.007 inch to ¼ inch | | **First-Class Mail Metered Mail** | ➢ A price category for First-Class Mail letters that applies when metered postage (such as PC Postage) is affixed. This does **not** include regular stamps, such as postage purchased through a self-service kiosk. In addition:
- Retail Metered Mail must also be single-piece.

➢ Commercial Metered Mail (for commercial mailers):
- Prices would apply to residual pieces from automation or presorted FCM letter mailings, presented in letter trays.

- Does not have a minimum volume (except for the minimum 200 pieces needed for permit imprint).

➢ When you are depositing Commercial Metered Mail, you do not need a minimum number of pieces for the nonpresorted portion when those residual pieces:
- Are part of a permit imprint mailing for an automation or presorted mailing, and

- Are claimed on the same postage statement as the primary mailing. | | **First-Class Mail Flats (Large Envelopes)**​​​ | ➢ Price paid on a per ounce basis – based on the price of a First-Class Mail stamp
- Maximum weight: 13 ounces – Additional ounces above one ounce have a separate per ounce price

- Flats that are for the round-trip mailings of a disc and that weigh up to 2 ounces can be mailed at the postage price of a 1 ounce Flat. Round-trip mailing of a disc is mailing, under specific criteria, from a mailer who expects to receive the disc back from a subscriber.

- Large Envelopes (Flats) that exceed 13 ounces are mailed at the Priority Mail® rate

➢ Size Requirements:
- Minimum Size: 6⅛ inches by 11½ inches

- Maximum Size: 12 inches by 15 inches

- Thickness: ¼ inch to ¾ inch

➢ Pieces that exceed any of the maximum dimensions or are rigid, nonrectangular, or not uniformly thick are charged and shipped as USPS Ground Advantage parcels. | ➢ Price paid on a per ounce basis
- Maximum weight: 13 ounces – Additional ounces above one ounce have a separate per ounce price

➢ Size Requirements:
- Minimum Size: 6⅛ inches by 11½ inches

- Maximum Size: 12 inches by 15 inches

- Thickness: ¼ inch to ¾ inch | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Types of First-Class Mail® URL Name Types-of-First-Class-Mail [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Mail & Shipping Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGpGAM/mail-shipping-services) ## Related Articles - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Types of First-Class Mail®

data.markdown:
[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Premium-Forwarding-Services# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Premium-Forwarding-Services?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FPremium-Forwarding-Services) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000014xdAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Premium-Forwarding-Services) ## Premium Forwarding Services ## Information about USPS Premium Forwarding Services: Residential, Commercial and Local. These are paid options to have your mail packaged and sent to a different address at scheduled intervals. - Mar 4, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008349 Customer Information **USPS® Premium Forwarding Services provide residential and business customers flexibility and control over where and when you receive mail. Premium Forward Service® is a paid service, designed to provide temporary forwarding of all your mail on a weekly basis via Priority Mail® or Priority Mail Express®. Options include: Premium Forwarding Service Residential®, Premium Forwarding Service Commercial® and Premium Forwarding Service Local®.** Premium Forwarding Service-Residential® **Premium Forwarding Service - Residential® (PSF-R) is a paid service available for domestic addresses only, designed to provide temporary forwarding of all customers mail on a weekly basis via Priority Mail®.** PFS-Residential gives residential customers and certain PO Box™ holders the option of having all of their mail shipped on a predictable basis to a temporary address while they are away from home. - For a one-time enrollment fee and a nominal weekly shipment fee, virtually all of the customer's mail will be packaged weekly and sent by Priority Mail® (PM) service to the temporary address. Some items, such as Registered Mail® and Priority Mail Express® (PME) are shipped immediately upon receipt at the Post Office™. PFS-R can be used for a minimum of two weeks or up to a maximum of one year. - **Note:** If a customer would like to continue PFS-R service beyond a year, they may submit another request. - PFS-R provides customers an additional option to the current piece-by-piece temporary forwarding and Hold Mail services. PFS-R Fees, Restrictions and Additional Charges **PFS Basic Fees** **In order to use Premium Forwarding Service Residential, you must pay a one-time enrollment fee, either online or at your local Post Office, and a weekly shipment fee during the time period that you are using the service.** **For detailed information on Premium Forwarding Service, including the fees, please go to [https://www.usps.com/manage/forward.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/forward.htm).** **USPS Tracking® and Other Accountable Mail Sent Via PFS-Residential®** USPS Tracking®, Accountable Mail, Priority Mail Express® service, and other mail pieces that require a scan or signature are not included within the weekly Priority Mail® service shipment for Premium Forwarding Service Residential®. These items are individually readdressed to the temporary address, scanned as “forwarded”, and rerouted to the temporary address subject to the following: - Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail, USPS Ground Advantage® – Retail parcels, USPS Ground Advantage® – Commercial pieces, and Periodicals parcels are rerouted separately at no additional charge. - USPS Marketing Mail® parcels are rerouted separately and charged postage due at the appropriate 1-pound USPS Ground Advantage – Retail price. - Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail®, and Library Mail pieces are rerouted separately and the mailer charged postage due at the appropriate single-piece price for the class or subclass of mail in which the piece was originally shipped. - Parcel Select pieces are rerouted separately and the mailer charged postage due at the appropriate USPS Ground Advantage – Commercial price. **PFS-Residential Restrictions** - A Hold Mail request or Forwarding Order, temporary or permanent, may not be active simultaneously with enrollment in PFS. - PFS cannot be combined with any ancillary or extra services beyond those purchased by the original sender. - PFS Residential is available only to and from customers with domestic addresses. - PFS is not available to or from an APO/FPO or DPO as mail sent to an APO/FPO or DPO is subject to certain conditions and restrictions. - PFS Residential cannot be used when the temporary address is within the 969 3-digit ZIP Code™ area or is otherwise in a U.S. territory or possession that requires a customs declaration. - There can be different PFS Residential applications on file for multiple individuals from one primary address. However, there can only be one PFS Residential application on file when the request is for an entire household from a primary address to a single temporary address during an enrollment period. - For customers whose primary address is a PO Box, only the box customer is authorized to initiate the application and “Entire Household” must be designated on the application. ****PFS is not available for**:** - Individuals whose primary address is a size-three, size-four, or size-five PO Box™. Residential customers who use these PO Box™ sizes due to the unavailability of smaller boxes may request a waiver of this restriction. - Individuals whose primary address is a business delivery address. - Individuals whose primary address is a central point to which the United States Postal Service® provides delivery in bulk to a third party, such as a commercial mail receiving agency (CMRA), RV park, trailer park, or hotel. Additional Charges Additional charges may be applied when you have a Premium Forwarding Service – Residential in effect.  This can occur due to mailpieces that have to be [shipped separately](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6BY00000002ICYAY/view#shipped_separately) or items [mailed with postage due](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6BY00000002ICYAY/view#postagedue). **Mail Shipped Separately** Mailpieces that require a scan, such as Accountable Mail, pieces with USPS Tracking and Priority Mail Express® service, are not included within the weekly Priority Mail® service shipment.  The same is true for mailpieces that do not fit in the shipment container. Mailpieces that do not fit in the shipment container or that require a scan or signature at delivery are scanned (when applicable) and then rerouted separately to the temporary address, subject to the following: 1. Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail, USPS Ground Advantage pieces and Periodicals parcels are rerouted separately at no additional charge. 2. USPS Marketing Mail® parcels pieces are rerouted separately and charged postage due at the appropriate USPS Ground Advantage-Retail price. 3. Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail, Library Mail, and Parcel Select pieces are rerouted separately and charged postage due at the appropriate single-piece price for the class or subclass of mail in which the piece was originally shipped. | United States Postal Service Premium Forwarding Service (PFS)
Disposition of Mail Chart
This chart identifies what mail could incur additional postage charges | | --- | | **Shape** | **USPS Marketing Mail** **® (With Extra Services Requiring Scan or Signature)** | **USPS Marketing Mail** **® (Without Extra Services) (bulletins, small parcels, Direct Advertising Mail)** | **Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail, Library Mail** | **USPS Ground Advantage®** | | Letters and Flats | N/A | Included | Postage Due | Postage Due | | Parcel (small) | Postage Due | May be included | Postage Due | Postage Due | | Parcel (too large for PFS pkg.) | Postage Due | Postage Due | Postage Due | Postage Due | | **LEGEND:**
- **Included** \- Included in weekly PFS® shipment at no additional cost.
- **May be included** \- Include in weekly PFS shipment only if it fits after all the letters and flats (magazines and large envelopes) are included. If item does not fit, it will be sent separately incurring postage due charges at the level of service initially paid.
- **N/A** - Not applicable, Extra Services are not available on USPS Marketing Mail® letters and flats.
- **Postage Due** \- Not included in weekly PFS shipment. These items are sent separately incurring postage due charges at the level of service initially paid.
**Note**: To avoid incurring postage due charges, you should have USPS Marketing Mail parcels, USPS Ground Advantage-Retail, USPS Ground Advantage, Package Services, Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail, and Library Mail sent directly to your temporary address. | **Mail with Postage Due** Postage due items at the Post Office™ that would deliver mail to an address where a PFS request is in effect **are not** shipped in the weekly Priority Mail® service shipment. These postage due pieces are rerouted to the temporary address individually as follows: - First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage, USPS Ground Advantage-Retail, and Priority Mail items are charged only for the original postage due amount. - Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail, Library Mail, and Parcel Select pieces are rerouted postage due at the appropriate single-piece price for the class, or subclass, of mail the mailpiece was originally shipped plus the postage due amount already indicated on the mailpiece. PFS-R Refunds The Premium Forwarding Service Residential® (PFS-Residential®) enrollment fee is nonrefundable even if the customer terminates service early. Refunds may be available for those customers whose shipments were not processed in a given week, were charged, and did not receive the shipment. All refund requests are reviewed for eligibility. **NOTE**: The following are not eligible for a refund, **at any time**: - Enrollment fee - Weekly shipping fees, **unless** pre-paid at Retail and unused - Delayed shipments **PFS-Residential® at Retail** **Refund Request for Service Termination**: If you pay for the service at Retail, you prepaid the weekly shipping charge. If you request that your Premium Forwarding Service Residential® ends early (cancel the PFS-Residential®), the Postal Service can refund the portion of the weekly shipment charge that was prepaid, but has not been used. For example, a customer who terminates PFS-Residential® early (e.g., a customer prepays for 10 weeks but returns to a primary address after 8 weeks) may request a refund for any unused weekly shipment charges from the Post Office™ facility serving the primary address. To request a refund, start by contacting your primary Post Office™ facility (the Post Office where you ordered PFS-Residential®) and fill out PS Form 3533, _Application for Refund of Fees, Products and Withdrawal of Customer Accounts, Modified With Barcode_, and present a valid form of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://usps.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). PFS-Residential® shipments are placed in the mail each Wednesday, so you must cancel your request by close of business (COB) on Tuesday to receive any prepaid fee refund. If you request a refund for early termination, you will be issued a no-fee money order right away. **_Remember_: ONLY the portion of the weekly shipment charge that has not been used is refundable.** **Refund Request for Service Issue**: Contact your Local Post Office™ facility to file a service complaint and request a PFS refund. You will need to fill out Form 3533 and present a valid form of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://usps.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). If you request a refund due to a service issue, your request will be processed in 2-3 business days. Processing may take longer if additional information is needed. Once your request is processed, you will be notified via phone or email if your service-related refund request was approved or denied. If approved, you will be mailed a no-fee money order to your primary address. The refund covers any shipping fees, only.(Enrollment fees are not refundable) Handling fees are not refundable. [Back to Top](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6BY00000002cvYAA/view#back_to_top) **PFS-Residential® Online** **Early Service Termination**: If you enroll in PFS-Residential® using the Online application, the credit card designated to process the weekly shipment fee is charged weekly. You can stop the PFS-Residential® shipment and future weekly shipment charges by going online and updating your information. **Timing**: If the service is not stopped before the weekly fee is charged, that fee is not refundable. In order to cancel without getting charged for the upcoming week, you must cancel your PFS-Residential® enrollment by Monday at 11:59pm CST. If you do not cancel the PFS-Residential® enrollment before Monday at 11:59pm, CST, your credit card will be charged the weekly shipment fee and there are **no refunds** for this fee. **Refund Request for Service Issues**: You can submit a refund request for PFS-Residential® created online by following directions on the [Global Refund Page](https://uprt.usps.com/pfs/pfsRefund.html "Global Refund Page"). To request a Premium Forward Service Residential® (PFS-Residential®) refund, you must provide, at a minimum: - The Order Number for the weekly shipment(s) you wish to refund; - The reason for the refund request; and - The specific PFS-Residential® shipment you are requesting the refund for (please, include dates). [Request a PFS Refund](https://uprt.usps.com/pfs/pfsRefund.html "Request a PFS Refund") Your request will be processed in 2-3 business days. Once your request is processed, you will be notified via email if it was approved or denied. Processing may take longer if additional information is needed. If approved, please allow up to 2 billing cycles from notification for a refund to appear on the credit card associated with the account. **Checking Your Online PFS Refund Status:** If you submitted a refund request for the PFS-Residential® order made online, you can check the status of your order at your convenience by viewing the order in your [Activity History](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=ATG&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fstore.usps.com%2Fstore%2Fmyaccount%2FmyOrders.jsp%3Fselpage%3DMY%2BORDERS). Premium Forwarding Service-Commercial® **Premium Forwarding Service - Commercial® (PFS-C) is a domestic service for business commercial customers who receive mail at different business PO Boxes or business street addresses. This enables businesses to consolidate their operations and plan for any additional staff they may require for processing incoming mail volume.** Getting Started with PFS-C Businesses interested in using PFS-Commercial must first sign up through the [Customer Onboarding Portal (COP)](https://cop.usps.com/). They can then enroll in USPS Ship with a valid payment account and permit to pay for PFS-Commercial service and postage. **PFS-Commercial Features:** - There are no weight restrictions for PFS-Commercial that differ from the normal Postal Service™ weight restrictions. - A customer can request to have mail reshipped up to as many days of the week that mail is normally delivered — Monday through Saturday. - Customers pay postage through their USPS® Ship account which is linked to the Enterprise Payment System (EPS).​ - For more information on the price of the service, contact your local Postmaster or visit [Notice 123, Price List](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#Premium%20Forwarding%20Service "Notice 123 - Postal Explorer"). PFS-C Payment and Service Issues - For issues with payment and postage, customers should contact USPS Ship by email at [USPSShipSupport@usps.gov](mailto:USPSShipSupport@usps.gov) or by phone at 877-264-9693. - Customers should contact their [local Postmaster](https://tools.usps.com/locations/?_gl=1*18qyxyc*_ga*MTYwNzY1MDU0LjE3Mzc4MTMwNTI.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3NTc1MTA4OTQkbzE2MCRnMCR0MTc1NzUxMDg5NyRqNTckbDAkaDA.) for concerns pertaining to PFS-C service. Premium Forwarding Service-Local® **Premium Forwarding Service Local® (PFS-Local) allows residential/individual and business/organization Post Office Box (PO Box) holders the option to have the USPS gather their mail addressed to their PO Box for delivery to their street address, when both addresses are within the same local servicing postal facility. An annual enrollment fee is required, and a reshipment fee is charged for each reshipment container.** - Customers can select the frequency that meets their needs, Monday-Saturday. - All shipments are sent via Priority Mail. Customers pay a per container fee based on the volume of mail. - Email notifications are sent to the customer regarding reshipments or when there is no mail available to forward. - Enrollment for PFS-Local is available through: - [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) for residential PO Box holders. A valid credit card is required for payment. - The Business Customer Gateway at ( [https://gateway.usps.com/](https://gateway.usps.com/)) for business PO Box Holders. Customers must have an active permit and be enrolled in USPS Ship. - **For current fees for Premium Forwarding Service Local®:** See [https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#\_c199](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c199) Premium Forwarding Services® Quick Compare | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | **Premium Forwarding Service Local (PFS-L)** | **Premium Forwarding Service Residential (PFS-R)** | **Premium Forwarding Service Commercial (PFS-C)** | | **Overview** | Allows residential and business PO Box owners to have their PO Box mail delivered to their street address if serviced by the same facility. | Allows residential customers to have all mail reshipped to a temporary address weekly. | Allows business/commercial customers to have all mail reshipped in bulk to a new location. | | **Frequency** | Customer selects frequency | Weekly only; every Wednesday | Customer selects frequency | | **Fees** | 1. Enrollment Fee
2. Per container charge based on volume | 1. Enrollment Fee
2. Weekly shipment charge | 1. Enrollment Fee
2. Postage for sacks, half and full tray boxes based on volume | | **Mail Class Used for Forwarding** | Priority Mail | Priority Mail | Customer chooses between Priority Mail Express and Priority Mail | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Premium Forwarding Services URL Name Premium-Forwarding-Services [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Premium Forwarding Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGQGA2/premium-forwarding-services) ## Related Articles - [What is the best way to contact the United States Postal Service®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-best-way-to-contact-the-United-States-Postal-Service) - [Ordering Free Shipping Supplies](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies) - [Refunds for Premium Forwarding Service Residential®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refunds-for-Premium-Forwarding-Service-Residential) - [Can I Change or Cancel Premium Forwarding Service® Residential?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Change-or-Cancel-Premium-Forwarding-Service-reg-Residential) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Premium Forwarding Services

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000007KrAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailbox-The-Basics) ## Mailboxes - The Basics ## Managing mailboxes and general delivery services. - Oct 18, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006983 Customer Information To better serve you, the Postal Service offers delivery services to meet your needs. Select from the list below to learn more about the options and see what restrictions apply. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Managing Mailbox Receptacles / Deliveries** | | **Caller Service /**
**Reserve Service** | A service that allows both business and residential customers to go to their [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) and obtain their mail from that Post Office’s call window or loading dock during business hours. 
You may also visit Mange Business Mail at [https://www.usps.com/business/manage-mail.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/manage-mail.htm). | [_**PS Form 1093-C** (Application for Post Office™ Caller Service_](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps1093c.pdf); details on pricing [Notice 123, _USPS Price List_](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm).
**Benefits:** You may collect mail periodically throughout the day (not just once). | | **General Delivery** | A mail service for those without a permanent address, often used as a temporary mailing address.
Post Office™ locations without city carrier delivery service. 
General Delivery is intended to be used for:
- Non-city delivery offices for those who prefer not to use Post Office Box service and for whom use of Post Office box, Caller Service, or delivery by letter carrier, would be an unreasonable inconvenience. 
- Post Office™ locations without city carrier delivery service.
- Any Post Office to serve transients (people who travel extensively) and those without a permanent address.
- Anyone who wants Post Office box service when Post Office boxes are unavailable. | **Address a mailpiece sent to General Delivery**
NAME
GENERAL DELIVERY
City, State, Zip Code
**Note:**  General Delivery mail is not delivered on Sunday or Holidays
You may visit your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action). | | **Who is Responsible for the Mailbox?**
(Broken/damaged) | Verify whether or not the boxes are owned and maintained by the US Postal Service®. | If the box is owned and maintained by the US Postal Service and is damaged/broken then contact your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action).
If the LPO does not service the mailbox, this means the property owner or manager is responsible for the maintenance. | | **Reporting Damage to a Personal Mailbox** | The US Postal Service® does not maintain personal mailboxes: The property owner is responsible for the repair of personal boxes.
Contact the local Post Office before erecting, moving or replacing mailboxes and supports. | If you are reporting damage to a private mailbox, past or present, it is suggested to call your local police. | | **Your Mailbox Needs Attention** | Prepared by the postmaster and delivered when there is a problem with your mailbox or receptacle. | [PS Form 4056](http://blue.usps.gov/formmgmt/forms/4056.pdf)
Action must be taken to correct the problems or risk having your mail service suspended until the problems are resolved. | | **Policy Regarding Locked Mailboxes and Mailbox Keys** | **Replacing Locks and Keys**
Determine who owns and maintains the mailbox. For additional information see:[What is a Cluster Box?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Cluster-Box) | **If owned** by the United States Postal Service, contact your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action).
**Postal owned Cluster Box:**  keys and locks (including parcel boxes), contact the Local Post Office. 
**If Privately owned** or maintained by a landlord, apartment complex, condo association or other housing group, then that management is responsible for maintaining the box including keys. | | **Missing, Lost, or Stolen Mailbox Keys** | See [Locked Mailboxes and Mailbox Keys](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Locked-Mailboxes-and-Mailbox-Keys) | | | **Mailbox Installation Guidelines** | Mailbox installation and maintenance information can be found here: | [Mailbox Guidelines](https://www.usps.com/manage/mailboxes.htm) | | **Newspaper Receptacle** | A Receptacle for newspaper delivery may be attached to the post of a curbside box. | Allowable if the receptacle:
1. Does not touch the mailbox or use any part of the mailbox for support.
2. Does not interfere with the delivery of mail, obstruct the view of the mailbox flag, or present a hazard to mailperson.
3. Does not extend beyond the front of the mailbox when the box door is closed.
4. Does not display advertising, except the publication title. | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Mailboxes - The Basics URL Name Mailbox-The-Basics [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Mailboxes](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGLGA2/mailboxes) ## Related Articles - [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Money Orders - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) - [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000014w1AAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-Business-Customer-Gateway-Website) ## What is the Business Customer Gateway Website? ## The Business Customer Gateway provides a single entry point for Postal Service online commercial services. - Mar 2, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006891 Customer Information **Business Customer Gateway Purpose** - The purpose of the [Business Customer Gateway website](https://gateway.usps.com/ "USPS Business Customer Gateway") is to make doing business with United States Postal Service® easier for valued business mail customers like you. - This website provides a "one-stop-shop" to access relevant information and content online. The long-term vision is to enable a robust business-to-customer website customized to meet all your business needs online. USPS® will continue to provide you with features that will make doing business with the United States Postal Service easier, including: - Improved customer reporting - Enhanced mail tracking capabilities - Online customer service support **Benefits** The site will provide you with significant business benefits: - Information is organized around relevant content and centralized to save you time currently spent accessing multiple websites. - Content is customized for you according to your business needs. - Streamlined solutions that support the mailing process, such as mail tracking and centralized reporting improve the efficiency of conducting business with us. **Products and services offered** Numerous services are available from the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/) website. The following is a list of the services provided: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **_Mailing Services_** | **_Shipping Services_** | **_Other Business Services_** | | - Automated Business Reply Mail
- Customer/Supplier Agreements (CSAs)
- Every Door Direct Mail
- Incentive Programs
- Informed Visibility
- Mailer ID
- Manage Mailing Activity
- Schedule a Mailing Appointment (FAST) | - Click-N-Ship Business Pro™
- Electronic Verification Service (eVS)
- Incentive Programs
- Mailer ID
- Manage APIs
- Manage Mailing Activity
- Official Mail Shipping Labels
- Online Enrollment
- Parcel Data Exchange (PDX)
- Premium Forwarding Service Commercial™
- Scan Based Payment (SBP)
- USPS Package Intercept | - Mail Transport Equipment Ordering System (MTEOR)
- Meters and PC Postage
- Retail Business Partners | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-Business-Customer-Gateway-Website#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is the Business Customer Gateway Website?
