Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000R1pAAE%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery) ## Collect on Delivery ## Broad information on the Collect on Delivery service including what items are eligible for it, how recipients can pay the fee, and how much it costs (cost can be found through Notice 123 or https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm). - Jan 20, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006805 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**What is Collect on Delivery?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#what_is_collect_on_delivery) | [**Eligibility and Restrictions for COD and COD Restricted Delivery**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#eligibility_restrictions) | | [**Additional Conditions and Restrictions**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#additional_conditions_restrictions) | [**Fees and Payments**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#fees_payments) | **What is Collect on Delivery?** _**Collect on Delivery (COD)**_ is a service that allows the sender to collect from the recipient money for postage, fees, merchandise, or any combination thereof. Each COD item has an identification number, enabling delivery confirmation. - COD is used by senders who want to collect payment for merchandise and/or postage and fees when the item is delivered to the receiver. - Sender pays postage plus fees at time of mailing. - The mailer guarantees to pay any return postage, unless otherwise specified on the mail. - Amount collected may not exceed $1,000. - Must be presented for mailing at a Post Office™ facility or given to a rural carrier. COD mail cannot be deposited: - In a Post Office maildrop - In a street letterbox - In a rural mailbox - Given to the carrier (for that delivery address) unless it is a rural carrier Tracking of mailpieces using Collect on Delivery service is available using [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action "USPS Tracking® - USPS.com") on USPS.com®. Recipients will automatically be signed up to receive email or SMS text tracking notifications from USPS if the commercial shipper provides an email address or text-capable phone number with their shipping manifest. Collect on Delivery (COD) does NOT provide: - Expedited delivery service - Guaranteed delivery time USPS-approved, privately printed versions of [PS Form 3816](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3816.pdf "Collect on Delivery Form") can also be used. Use of PS Form 3816-AS has been discontinued. | | | | --- | --- | | Sample of a commercial single-ply COD Hold For Pickup Label
- **Note**: This type of single-ply COD label cannot be used for COD without Hold For Pickup service.) | ![Commercial Priority Mail 1-Day Collect on Delivery Hold for Pickup Label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/commercial_priority_mail_COD_HFPU_Label.jpg)
_Click image to make larger_ | | Sample of a manual non-Retail Software System (RSS) Post Office single-ply COD Hold For Pickup Label
- **Note**: This type of single-ply COD label cannot be used for COD without Hold For Pickup service, and it cannot be used by commercial mailers, RSS Post Offices, or created as a privately printed version by a mailer. Mailer’s sending COD Hold For Pickup items from their manual non-RSS Post Office can obtain these labels from that office.) | ![Shipping label where the sender fills in sender/recipient information; label also fields to fulfill Collect on Delivery and Hold for Pickup services](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/nonretail_manual_priority_mail_COD_HFPU_Label.jpg)
_Click image to make larger_ | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#top) **Eligibility and Restrictions for Collect on Delivery (COD) and COD Restricted Delivery** - ​The mail has the complete names and addresses of the mailer and addressee. - The mailer guarantees to pay any return postage, unless otherwise specified on the mail. - The goods shipped are ordered by the addressee. Available with: | | | | --- | --- | | - •Priority Mail Express®
- •Priority Mail®
- •First-Class Mail®
- •USPS Ground Advantage® -Retail
- •USPS Ground Advantage® -Commercial | - •Media Mail®
- •Library Mail
- •Bound Printed Matter
- •Parcel Select® | - The following must apply for all eligible mail classes: - Collect On Delivery service (when purchased with First-Class Mail® or Priority Mail items only) may only be combined with: - Registered Mail® items - COD or COD Restricted Delivery service may be combined with: - Return Receipt (hardcopy or electronic) - Signature Confirmation™ (excluding Priority Mail Express COD) - Hold for Pickup service - When a COD mailpiece cannot be delivered to the recipient or an agent, the item will be held at the local Post Office for up to 10 days unless the mailer specifies fewer days on the item. - COD amount to be collected from the recipient may not exceed $1,000. Registered Mail® and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery items can have a declared value over $1,000, but must be sent "Registered with Insurance", along with the Registered Mail COD Collection fee being included, but the amount to be collected may still not exceed $1,000. - [USPS Tracking®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) is included at no cost for all classes of mail except USPS Marketing Mail® parcels. - Any article sent COD can also be sent by Priority Mail Express® when a delivery signature is requested. The maximum amount collectible from the recipient on an individual article is $1,000, and indemnity for an article is limited to $1,000. Priority Mail Express® postage and the proper COD fees must be paid. - When COD is used with one of the following, both the respective label and the COD form must be placed on the front of the article. - ​​Priority Mail Express - Registered Mail - Hold for Pickup service (except for COD Hold For Pickup items bearing a commercial mailer’s single-ply COD Hold For Pickup label, single-ply USPS COD Hold For Pickup Label 3816 (privately printed versions are not permitted), or a single-ply COD Hold for Pickup label generated at the Retail System Software (RSS) Post Office locations). - A separate barcoded shipping label - In addition, for delivery receipt and indemnity claims purposes: - The Priority Mail Express article number is used - The Registered Mail number is used - ​USPS maintains a record of delivery (including the recipient’s signature) for two years. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#top) **Additional Conditions and Restrictions** - COD is **not** available with International mail. - COD may not be used to return merchandise over which there is some dispute. - COD may not be used for moving-picture films mailed by exhibitors to moving-picture manufacturers, distributors, or exchanges. - COD may not be used for articles sent to or from an APO, FPO or DPO address, including official mail and shipments to Armed Forces agencies. - COD may not be used for articles sent to or from the Republic of the Marshall Islands and the Federated States of Micronesia. - COD may not be used for collection agency purposes. - COD may not be used for mailing of **only** bills or statements of account. Further information is available in the [_Domestic Mail Manual_](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/503.htm#1064058 "Domestic Mail Manual 503.9.0 Collect on Delivery (COD)"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#top) **Fees and Payments** - **The Collect on Delivery (COD) fee** is determined by the amount to be collected OR amount of insurance coverage desired (whichever is higher). The Collect on Delivery fee is not eligible for a refund. However, you may be eligible for a refund of the insured amount according to the Insured Mail guidelines if there was insurance on the mailpiece. Go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm "Shipping Insurance, Certified Mail & Extra Services | USPS") and select “Show Details” under Collect on Delivery for additional information on COD fees. - Recipient may pay by cash, pin-based debit card, or a personal check or money order made payable to the sender. (Sender may not specify payment method.) - If payment is made by cash, then a money order fee is included in the collected amount. - The fees for COD include insurance against loss, rifling, or damage to the article or failure to receive a postal money order or the recipient's check. - Remittance (payment) is sent by First-Class Mail® service unless: - The sender participates in electronic funds transfer and the item is delivered from an office with Retail System Software, or - You may report delays in remittance / payment (more than 60 days for domestic mailings) by contacting 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Collect-on-Delivery#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Collect on Delivery URL Name Collect-on-Delivery [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Insurance & Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGrGAM/insurance-extra-services) ## Related Articles - [No mail delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery) - [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) - [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) - [What is General Delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-General-Delivery) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Collect on Delivery

## Pay, Print and Ship Online

## With just a few clicks USPS offers small businesses that are shipping packages an easy and convenient option to set shipping preferences, pay for and print postage right from their desktop.      - Feb 28, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007465

Customer Information

All you need to get started is a free USPS.com account, computer and standard printer.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Click-N-Ship](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Pay-Print-and-Ship-Online#CNS)** | **[Business Customer Gateway](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Pay-Print-and-Ship-Online#BusinessCustomerGateway)** | **[Calculating Postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Pay-Print-and-Ship-Online#CalculatingPostage)** |

## **What is Click-N-Ship®?**

Click-N-Ship® brings the Post Office™ to a computer desktop at home or at the office with no additional fees. You can ship 1 or 10 packages per month and easily pay for postage and print shipping labels online.

- Pay with a credit card, [Apple Pay](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted) or PayPal
- Ship domestically or internationally
- Create batch shipments of up to 20 identical packages

Learn more: [https://www.usps.com/ship/online-shipping.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/online-shipping.htm)

## **What is Business Customer Gateway?**

The [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/publichomepage.jsp?) (BCG) is the central USPS hub for managing volume mailing and shipping for all sizes and types of businesses.

- Connect to online tools for business mailers
- Access customer package return services
- Sign up for promotions and incentives

## **How do I calculate postage?**

When calculating postage follow these key steps:

1. Weigh your item and round up
2. Select the right mail class
3. Measure your package
4. Label your package correctly
5. Double check the zone

Calculate Postage: [https://postcalc.usps.com/DomesticZoneChart](https://postcalc.usps.com/DomesticZoneChart)

For more detailed postage information click here: [https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm)

See [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) for additional shipping information and resources.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000014HhAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) ## Money Orders - The Basics ## A Money Order is a document similar to U.S. currency that can be purchased in varying amounts. - Feb 26, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006987 Customer Information | | | --- | | **Important Notice on International Postal Money Orders:**
- Effective October 1, 2025, USPS stopped cashing all international money orders. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Purchase or Cash a Postal Money](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#buy_postal_money_order) [Ord](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#buy_postal_money_order) [er](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#buy_postal_money_order)** | **[Filing a money order claim / inquiry](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#filing_money_order_claim)** | **[What do postal money orders look like?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#postal_money_orders_look_like)** | | [**2025 Enhanced Money Order Redesign - Frequently Asked Questions**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#February_2025_Money_Order_Redesign) | **[How do I spot a fake money order?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#verify_postal_money_order)** | **[How can I replace a money order?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#replace)** | **Purchase or Cash a Postal Money Order** **Domestic money orders** Money orders can be purchased at any Post Office™ location. Money orders can also be purchased from rural carriers and some contract stations. Contact the contract station to confirm availability. **Military money orders** Military money orders must be purchased at an APO/FPO/DPO facility. **Money order fee** Fees vary depending on the amount of the money order and whether it is for domestic use or is being sent to an APO/FPO/DPO address. For additional information on money order fees, visit [https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm). **Purchase limits** - The single **Domestic** money order maximum is $1,000.00 if sending within the United States. - Multiple money orders may be purchased during the same visit (no daily limit). - If your daily total of purchased money orders is $3,000 or more (regardless of the number of visits to one or more postal facilities), you will be required to complete [PS Form 8105-A, _Funds Transaction Report (FTR)_](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps8105a.pdf) and produce [acceptable ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). - **Note**: This applies to all money orders combined that are purchased during the span of a day, including domestic and military money orders, if also purchased that day. **How can I pay for a money order?** - U.S. currency and coins. - ATM / Debit Cards at locations where the customer's Personal Identification Number (PIN) must be entered on a keypad connected to a Credit/Debit terminal. - Checks are not accepted to purchase money orders. - Credit cards may NOT be used to purchase money orders. - Note: Traveler's Checks are no longer accepted for money order payment. **Cashing Money Orders** - **Domestic Money Orders**can be cashed at any Post Office location in the country,  APO/FPO locations, and many banks: - Cashing a money order at a Post Office location is subject to the availability of funds at that location. - Do not sign the money order prior to cashing; money orders must be signed in front of a USPS employee if cashing at a Post Office location. - An [acceptable primary form of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) is required to cash a money order. - Questions regarding whether a specific Post Office location or financial institution (bank) can or will cash a Postal Money Order need to be directed to the specific location. Questions regarding whether a specific financial institution will cash a Postal Money Order need to be directed to the specific institution. - **International Money Orders** can be cashed at Postal facilities in countries that accept them. However, there is no requirement that banks or other facilities must cash the money orders. Questions regarding whether a specific financial institution will cash an International Postal Money Order need to be directed to the specific institution. | | | --- | | For more information on how to cash a money order, see [https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm). | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#Customer_Top) **Filing a money order claim / inquiry** **Checking the status of a money order inquiry** - To initiate a Money Order Inquiry, take the original money order purchase receipt (that was attached to the money order) to any Post Office location. Complete a _Money Order Inquiry_. Pay the applicable inquiry fee for [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c198) or [international](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c432) money order and **submit the form to postal personnel for mailing**. _To check the status of a money order inquiry after filing, call our toll free number: **1-866-974-2733.** To find out if a money order has been cashed, go online to [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) to [check the status](https://tools.usps.com/money-orders.htm). The money order serial number, Post Office number, and issued amount—all printed on the money order receipt—are required in order to obtain near real-time status information. For a copy of a cashed money order, follow the inquiry process below:_ _Take the original money order purchase receipt (that was attached to the money order) to any Post Office location._ _Complete a Money Order Inquiry if appropriate:_ _File a money order inquiry at any time after purchasing the money order._ _For a domestic money order inquiry, it may take up to 15 days after the inquiry is filed to receive information. A photocopy of the cashed money order is only available for 2 years from the date the money order was cashed. Pay the applicable inquiry fee for [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c198) or [international](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c432) money orders. If appropriate: The money order inquiry fee is per item and provides a copy of the item if cashed. The fee is not required if the Money Order Inquiry form is filed by a bank, another financial institution, a government agency that processes money orders directly with the Federal Reserve Bank, or a Postal Service official engaged in official USPS® business. **Note:** For additional information on fees for money order inquiries: For **Domestic** money orders, look under [Domestic Money Orders](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm) [\- Replace Lost or Damaged Money Orders](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm) For **International** money orders, look under [International Money Orders - Replace Lost or Damaged Money Orders](https://www.usps.com/international/money-transfers.htm)_ **Is there a way to identify the specific Post Office facility where a money order has been purchased?** - It is possible to identify the specific Post Office facility where a specific Money Order was purchased. However, it is not possible either to stop payment or to track or trace a Money Order in the mailstream. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#Customer_Top) **What do postal money orders look like?** Domestic Postal Money Order | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Legacy, Green Money Orders** | **Feb 2025 Redesign/ Red and Blue Money Orders** | **2025 Enhanced/ Red, Blue, Red Money Orders** | | ![Green postal money order sample](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/Domestic_MO.jpg)
**Out of production** | ![Sample red and blue postal money order](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/postal_store/2.13.25_new_money_order.jpg)
**Out of production** | ![](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq00000014Hh&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000T3CC1)
**Available for purchase 08/2025** | International Money Order (MP1) **![Yellow and light blue international postal money order sample.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/International_MO.jpg)** Customer's Receipt of Postal Money Order ![Image of a sample Customer's Receipt attached to a Postal Money Order under it. "Customer's Receipt" is written along the top left and "Postal Money Order" along the center left. The Customer's Receipt section has, left to right, text that says "SEE BACK OF THIS RECEIPT FOR IMPORTANT CLAIM INFORMATION NOT NEGOTIABLE", fill-in sections for "Pay to" and "Address", a scannable QR Code, and text that says "KEEP THIS RECEIPT FOR YOUR RECORDS". The last row of the Customer's Receipt section lists the Serial Number, Year/Month/Day, Post Office, Amount, and Clerk.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/postal_store/12.11.25_US%20Postal%20Money%20Order%20Customer%20Receipt.jpg) **Note: Receipts are referred to as "Customer's Receipt".** Text from Back of Customer's Receipt (top portion of the MP1): - Please Detach And Keep This For Your Records - This receipt is your guarantee for a refund of your money order if it is lost or stolen, provided you fill in the _Pay To_ and _From_ Information on the money order in the space provide - No claim for improper payment permitted 1 year after payment. - If your money order is lost or stolen, present this receipt and file a claim for a refund at your Post Office. A Money Order Inquiry may be filed at any time for a fee (for [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c198) or [international](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c432) money orders). A replacement will not be issued until 60 days after the money order purchase date, provided the money order has not been paid. US Postal Money Order Point-of-Sale Retail Receipt ![Receipt with a line dedicated to a money order purchase. The line has written Money Order - Value with the Serial Number below.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/postal_store/10.8.25_money%20order%20point%20of%20sale%20receipt.jpg) **Note: Receipts are referred to as "Customer Receipt".** Text from Back of Customer's Receipt (top portion of the MP1): - Please Detach And Keep This For Your Records - This receipt is your guarantee for a refund of your money order if it is lost or stolen, provided you fill in the _Pay To_ and _From_ Information on the money order in the space provide - No claim for improper payment permitted 1 year after payment. - If your money order is lost or stolen, present this receipt and file a claim for a refund at your Post Office. A Money Order Inquiry may be filed at any time for a fee (for [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c198) or [international](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c432) money orders). A replacement will not be issued until 60 days after the money order purchase date, provided the money order has not been paid. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) How to Spot a Fake Money Order USPS Money Orders have distinguishing marks you can check to make sure that the money order is valid. By holding a money order up to a light, you should see: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Legacy, Green Money Orders**
**(Out of Production)** | **Feb 2025 Redesign / Red and Blue Money Orders**
**(Out of production)** | **2025 Enhanced / Red, Blue, Red Money Orders**
**(Available for purchase)** | | 1. Ben Franklin watermarks repeated on the left side (top to bottom).
2. A vertical, multicolored thread that weaves in and out of the paper to the right of the Franklin watermark. In the light, the thread appears continuous with alternating horizontal dark and light bars behind it and the letters "USPS" repeating backward and forward throughout the thread. | 1. Watermarks of a Pony Express rider and the words "United States Postal Service" in a rectangular box.
2. A security thread that runs from the top to the bottom of the money order, just to the right of the Pony Express rider watermark. In the light, the thread reveals the letters "USPS" alternating right side up and upside down throughout the thread. | 1. Watermarks of a Pony Express rider and the words "United States Postal Service" in a rectangular box.
2. A security thread that runs from the top to the bottom of the money order, just to the right of the Pony Express rider watermark. In the light, the thread reveals the letters "USPS" alternating right side up and upside down throughout the thread. | | **Note:** The Feb. 2025 redesign and the Aug. 2025 enhanced Money Orders both share the same distinguishing watermarks and security threads when held up to the light.
- The enhanced version also shows a 3 phase color gradient; red, blue, red in place of the 2 phase red/blue color gradient present on the Feb. 2025 redesign.
- The "see reverse warning... " statement was moved and is now located in the top left corner of the Enhanced Money Order. | **Signs of a fraudulent money order include:** - If the denomination is discolored, it may have been erased (indicating fraud). - The dollar amount should be imprinted twice. - Maximum value for domestic money orders is $1,000. - Maximum value for international money orders is $700 ($500 for El Salvador and Guyana). **Security features include, but are not limited to:** - Red, blue, red color pattern with subtle design features of an eagle head and an American flag in the body. - The "See reverse warning…" statement has changed location to the top left corner - Two watermarks that are only visible when held up to a light source: 1. An image of a Pony Express rider. 2. The words "United States Postal Service" in a rectangular box. - A security thread that runs from the top to the bottom of the money order, just to the right of the Pony Express Rider watermark. When held to the light, the thread reveals the letters "USPS" alternating right side up and upside down throughout the thread. - A Quick Response (QR) code that directs users to the [USPS website](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm#spotfake) to verify the status of money orders using three pieces of information. - For more information, refer to the [How to Spot a Fake.pdf](https://www.uspis.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/2025_How_to_Spot_a_Fake.pdf "How to Spot a Fake.pdf") section of the Money Orders page on [USPIS.gov.](https://www.uspis.gov/) **Still not sure whether your money order is valid?** 1. **Verify** \- Call the money order verification system at **1-866-459-7822.** 2. **Report** \- If you suspect fraud, call the U.S. Postal Inspection Service® at **1-877-876-2455** (Follow the prompts). How can I Replace a Money Order? The United States Postal Service replaces, without charge, a **damaged** or **defective** Money Order or one that is **"spoiled"** during imprinting or customer completion if the customer returns the negotiable portion of the Money Order and the matching customer receipt. USPS also [replaces lost or stolen money orders](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics#lost_stolen_money_orders) if they have not been cashed. **There are no stop payments on Postal Money Orders.** **Spoiled or Damaged** To have a spoiled or damaged Money Order replaced (including one that was made out to an incorrect recipient): 1. Take both of the following to any Post Office facility: 1. The original Postal Service Postal Money Order. 2. The original detached money order customer's receipt for the original Money Order. 2. Fill out a Money Order Inquiry form _._ 3. The Money Order will be immediately reissued. **Notes:** - When replacing a Money Order using this procedure, there is no fee (for the Money Order Inquiry form or the new Money Order). - You will _not_ be given a refund, but you can write your name as the recipient on the replacement money order and cash it. (It is advisable to cash money orders for large amounts later in the day. Window clerks begin the day with little or no cash on hand and accumulate funds as the day progresses.) - If you do not have the original money order purchase receipt, you may still complete inquiry form with all of the required information and return it to the window clerk. If it is determined through the Money Order inquiry process that fraud was involved, a report should be filed. Re-issuance of the Money Order is determined by the Postal Service. **Lost or Stolen** The United States Postal Service replaces lost or stolen money orders **only** **if they have not been cashed**. Keep in mind that: - There are no stop payments on Postal Money Orders - Money order loss or theft may take up to 30 days to confirm. - Investigating a money order's lost or stolen status may take up to 60 days. - There is an inquiry / processing fee to replace a lost or stolen [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c198) or [international](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c432) money order that has not been cashed. - Re-issuance of the Money Order is determined by the Postal Service. If it is determined through the Money Order inquiry process that fraud was involved, a report should be filed. Call the U.S. Postal Inspection Service® at **1-877-876-2455** (Follow the prompts). To begin the inquiry process for lost or stolen money orders: 1. Take your money order receipt to any Post Office facility. 2. Talk to a retail associate at the counter to start a Money Order Inquiry by filling out a Money Order Inquiry form and paying the inquiry / processing fee. 3. After starting the inquiry, you will be able to check the status of your money order and inquiry progress by visiting the [Money Orders Application](https://tools.usps.com/money-orders.htm). 4. When your money order is confirmed lost or stolen and it has not been cashed, you will be issued a replacement money order. **2025 Enhanced Money Order Redesign** Frequently Asked Questions The U.S. Postal Service is introducing an enhanced money order design for release in August, due to a change in vendor. The newly designed money order should be available for sale in USPS retail units beginning in August 2025. Postal money orders do not expire. Legacy Postal Money Orders as well as the Feb. 2025 redesign Money Orders should be accepted as valid, provided they meet the basic security requirements listed on the[USPS.com Postal Money Order page](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm "USPS.COM Postal Money Order page"). There is no change in the money order fee with the new design. - **Where can the redesigned money orders be purchased or cashed?** You can buy, or cash, postal money orders at any [Post Office location](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm). Additional information is available on the [Sending Money Orders](https://www.usps.com/shop/money-orders.htm) page of [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/). - **How can I check the status of my money order?** Customers can track the status of a money order that was purchased from USPS on the [Check Money Order Status](https://tools.usps.com/money-orders.htm) page of [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/). Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Money Orders - The Basics URL Name Money-Orders-The-Basics [Postal Store](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFzGAM/postal-store)[Money Orders / Sure Money (DineroSeguro)](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HH2GAM/money-orders-sure-money-dineroseguro) ## Related Articles - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Money Orders - The Basics

## Multifactor Authentication (MFA)

## Information on the Multifactor Authentication process, how to enroll and how to modify MFA.      - Feb 25, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008234

Customer Information

Multifactor Authentication (MFA) is a security process that requires users to perform multiple steps or methods of authentication to gain access to certain programs or applications. It provides an additional level of security to help protect identities, passwords, and other personal data from unauthorized access and misuse.

Beginning March 6, 2025, all _new_ accounts will be required to enroll in MFA.

Here's what you need to know about the MFA process:

- If you sign up for an account _after_ March 6, 2025, you will be required to enroll in MFA.
- If you signed up for an account prior to March 6, 2025, you will have the option to enroll in MFA.
- When you enroll in MFA, you will be required to verify your email.


**How do I set up MFA?**

1. After entering your username and password, you will be presented with a **Verify Your Email** screen.

   - EXISTING customers who signed up for their account _before_ March 6, 2025 will be presented with an MFA enrollment screen where they can select **Enroll in MFA** to set up MFA or **Not Now** to opt out of MFA.
     - If you select **Enroll in MFA,** select **Yes** to use your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) email or select **No, I'd like to change my email** to enter a different email address, then select **Continue**\[Note that entering a different email here does not change the email address in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account\].
     - If you selected **No, I'd like to change my email,** enter the email you would like to use and select **Send Passcode**.
     - If you select **Not Now**, you will not be prompted to enroll in MFA for 30 days.
   - NEW customers who signed up for their account _after_ March 6, 2025 will have to verify their email before they can complete mandatory MFA setup.

- Select **Yes** to use your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) email or select **No, I'd like to change my email** to enter a different email address, then select **Continue**\[Note that entering a different email here does not change the email address in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account\].

2. If you selected **No, I'd like to change my email,** enter the email you would like to use and select **Send Passcode**. A six-digit, one-time passcode will be sent to the email you entered on the **Verify Your Email** screen.
3. Go to your email and find your passcode, then enter the code and select **Submit**. If you can't find the email, be sure to check your spam or junk folders.
4. After successfully verifying your email, you will be presented an option to setup additional MFA methods (SMS, Authenticator App, or "Skip For Now").
5. Select the MFA option you would like to set up and select **Continue**.
6. Follow the prompts on the screen, or the instructions below, to set up your MFA options.

**Note:** If you selected the "Skip for Now" option, you will be logged in. You can choose to add or modify additional MFA options at any future time you log in.

- The 6-digit passcode will come from [USPostalService@usps.com](mailto:USPostalService@usps.com).
- **The MFA temporary passcode will expire in 5 minutes**.
- You will have the option to resend the One-Time Passcode (OTP) if needed.

For additional assistance setting up MFA for Click-N-Ship®, [please see our user guide](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/MFA/2.26_USPS%20MFA%20User%20Guide.pdf).

**How do I set up the Phone (SMS) MFA option?**

1. On the MFA options screen, select the **SMS** MFA option and select **Continue**.
2. On the Verify Your Phone Number screen, enter your mobile phone number and select **Send Passcode**. _International phone numbers are not accepted._
3. You will receive a six-digit, one-time passcode via SMS to the mobile phone number you entered.
4. Enter the six-digit, one-time passcode on the MFA enrollment screen and select **Submit**.

**How do I set up the Email MFA option?**

1. On the MFA options screen, select the **Email** MFA option and select **Continue**.
2. You will receive a six-digit, one-time passcode to your USPS verified email.
3. Enter the six-digit, one-time passcode on the MFA enrollment screen and select **Submit**.

**How do I set up the Authenticator App MFA option?**

An authenticator app is a mobile app that provides an additional layer of security beyond a simple password by generating a time-based one-time password to enable multi-factor authentication. After linking your app to an online account by scanning a QR code, the app will generate a rotating, short-lived 6 digit code that you enter along with the account password. This helps verify your identity and protect you from unauthorized logins.

**To set up the Authenticator App MFA option:**

1. Download any desired authenticator app from the Apple App or Google Play store.
2. On the MFA options screen, select the Authenticator App MFA option and select **Continue**.
3. Open your authenticator app and scan the QR code on the screen, then select **Next**.
4. Enter the six-digit, one-time passcode on the Authenticator App screen and select **Submit**.

\*If you are logging in from your smartphone and are not able to scan the QR code, select **"On a mobile device?", t** hen choose your downloaded authenticator app and follow the prompts to import your authenticator app MFA.

**How do I modify my MFA options?**

1. On the MFA selection screen, select **Modify MFA Options.**
2. Continue the sign in process with your preferred MFA option.
3. Select the MFA option you would like to modify (SMS or Email) and follow the prompts on your screen to update your MFA options.

**What does "Remember my device" mean?**

When you enroll in MFA or sign in and enter your one-time passcode for MFA, you may see a checkbox labeled "Remember my device". If you check this box, you will not be asked to entered an MFA code again on this device for the next 30 days, unless you clear your browser cookies, use a different browser or log in from a different device.

For security reasons, you should only select this option on trusted, personal devices. Do not use this option on shared public or shared computers.

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for the Customer Onboarding Portal (COP)?**

If you have any trouble with the Customer Onboarding Portal MFA process, please send your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account username and phone number to the Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center (MSSC) at [mssc@usps.gov](mailto:mssc@usps.gov) with "Multifactor Authentication" in the subject line.

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for Click-N-Ship®?**

For assistance with MFA for Click-N-Ship, please contact us at: [https://emailus.usps.com/s/click-n-ship-inquiry](https://emailus.usps.com/s/click-n-ship-inquiry)

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for Informed Delivery®?**

For assistance with MFA for Informed Delivery, please contact us at:[https://emailus.usps.com/s/informed-delivery-inquiry](https://emailus.usps.com/s/informed-delivery-inquiry)

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for Post Office Box™ Online?**

If you created your account on or after March 6, 2025, you cannot opt out of MFA.

For all other assistance with MFA for Post Office Box Online, please contact us at: [https://emailus.usps.com/s/my-online-account-inquiry](https://emailus.usps.com/s/my-online-account-inquiry)

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for Every Door Direct Mail® (EDDM®)?**

If you created your account on or after March 6, 2025, you cannot opt out of MFA.

For all other assistance with MFA for EDDM®, please contact us at: [https://emailus.usps.com/s/my-online-account-inquiry](https://emailus.usps.com/s/my-online-account-inquiry)

**Who do I contact if I'm unable to set up or modify my MFA for Business Customer Gateway (BCG)?**

If you created your account on or after March 6, 2025, you cannot opt out of MFA.

For all other assistance with MFA for BCG, please contact the Mailing and Shipping Solutions Center (MSSC) at:

- Phone: 1-877-672-0007
- Email: [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov)

**Hours of Operation:**

- Mon - Fri from 8:00 am to 8:00 pm Eastern
- Closed: Saturday/Sunday/Holidays

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000013IPAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service) ## Priority Mail Express® Service ## Priority Mail Express® provides 1-3 day delivery service by 6 PM with a money-back guarantee. - Feb 19, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008250 Customer Information What is Priority Mail Express® Service? Our fastest domestic shipping service, Priority Mail Express® (PME) delivers 7 days a week, 365 days a year (with limited exceptions) to most U.S. addresses and PO Box™ addresses with a money-back guarantee. | | | --- | | **PME Benefits & Features** | | Delivery times of 1-3 days with a money-back guarantee[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#pme_benefits-note1) to most U.S addresses and PO Boxes™ by 6pm local time.
- Find the scheduled delivery time for your Priority Mail Express® item with [Priority Mail Express® Service Commitments](https://postcalc.usps.com/ServiceCommitments/Index "USPS Service Commitments").
- See [1-3-Day Deliveries](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#1-3-day_deliveries) and [Money-Back Guarantee](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#money-back_guarantee) for more information.
\*[Exceptions to the money-back guarantee policy](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top_pme_refund_eligibility) | | Request signature for proof of delivery. See [What is a Waiver of Signature? What is Signature Required?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Waiver-of-Signature-What-is-Signature-Required) for more information. | | Sunday and holiday delivery available in many major markets for an additional fee. | | See the [Priority Mail Express page](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm) for additional PME Features. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top) Service Commitments and Guarantees **1-3 Day Deliveries** Priority Mail Express® 1-3 Day delivery is available when you need to send shipments in a hurry. These items may be: - Mailed at Post Offices™, stations, and branches - Mailed at Contract Postal Units - Dropped into Priority Mail Express collection boxes - Handed to carriers - Picked up through Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand® The time of day a PME item is accepted by or dropped off to USPS and other factors impact if it will be delivered in 1 to 3 days by 6pm. Use the [Service Commitments tool](https://postcalc.usps.com/ServiceCommitments/Index) to find: - The drop-off times and locations that meet the desired delivery time. - When you can expect your package to arrive (Scheduled Delivery Day) and whether USPS can offer a money-back guarantee. - The Scheduled Delivery Day is based on the ZIP Codes you are mailing from and mailing to and the date and time you plan to mail the item. Service Commitments Tool Screenshot ![Screenshot from the Postage Price Calculator. the Service Commitments page. After the fields for entering "What ZIP Code are you mailing from?", "What ZIP Code are you mailing to?", "What Date do you plan to mail the item?", and "What's the Time you plan to mail the item?", there is a "Continue" button. Clicking "Continue" displays mail class options by in tables. The header row has columns for "Priority Mail Express" and "Scheduled Delivery Day", which is highlighted for emphasis. The next row has "Priority Mail Express - Money Back Guarantee" in the first column and the date "Tue, May 25 by 6:00 PM" (which is also highlighted for emphasis) under the second column. The next row is a table with columns dedicated to "Drop-Off By" time and date, "Facility Type", "Address", and "Guarantee".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/postcalc-pme-scheduled_delivery_day.png) **Money-Back Guarantee** If your shipment doesn't meet the [delivery commitment](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#delivery_commitment) found on your [receipt](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/retail_receipt_pme_trackingnumber.png), you can [submit a refund request](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#pme) for failure of service within 30 days of the mailing date or within 60 days if an Extra Service was purchased. See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#pme_refund_eligibility) for more information on refunds. | | | --- | | **Scan events meeting delivery commitment standards** [**\***](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#delivery_commitment-note1)
- An " **Attempted** delivery" scan event meets the delivery commitment (i.e. the mailpiece was taken to the address, but the recipient was unavailable to accept the item).
- An " **Arrival at Pickup Point**" scan event meets the delivery commitment for PO Box and Firm Caller addresses (i.e. the mailpiece was available for the caller or PO Box holder, but the recipient did not come in to claim the mailpiece).
- A " **Forwarded**" scan event meets the delivery commitment (i.e. the actual delivery address is different than what is indicated on the label).
\*This is not a complete list. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top) Pricing For detailed retail and commercial pricing, visit [the Price List - Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm) or [https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm). To calculate the price for your shipment, see the [Retail Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/Calculator) or the [Business Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/Business). **Retail Prices** - Priority Mail Express® pricing is based on weight and zone [(Zone Charts)](http://postcalc.usps.gov/zonecharts/) - Three (3) different [Flat Rate Envelopes](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm#flatrate) are available at retail pricing - The Flat Rate price is charged for matter mailed in the Flat Rate Envelope provided by the Postal Service®, regardless of weight (weight below the maximum 70 pounds) or destination - [Price List - Notice 123 section on retail prices](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c026) **Commercial Prices** - Lower than retail prices; see [Price List - Notice 123 section on Commercial prices](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c068) - Available to customers who purchase postage on the [Click-N-Ship application](https://cnsb.usps.com/) or are registered end users of PC Postage service (e.g. EasyPost, [Endicia.com](http://endicia.com/), Pitney Bowes, and [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/)) and produce individual shipping labels - Three (3) different [Flat Rate Envelopes](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm#flatrate) are available at Commercial pricing - The Flat Rate price is charged for matter mailed in the Flat Rate Envelope provided by the Postal Service®, regardless of weight (weight below the maximum 70 pounds) or destination - See [Priority Mail Express](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm) and [Shipping for Business](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) for additional information for businesses **Notes**: - [Shape, weight and size](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) of parcels can impact retail and commercial prices. - Where available, customers may select the following options: - _**Sunday/Holiday Delivery**_ for an additional fee - [_**Hold for Pickup**_](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Hold-for-Pickup-Service) at the destination Post Office [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top) Refund Eligibility Priority Mail Express® service items may be eligible for a refund. The United States Postal Service® refunds the postage and, if purchased, the Sunday or holiday premium fee: - When the item is not delivered by the guaranteed delivery date and time specified at the time of mailing - For an item for which delivery was not attempted by the guaranteed delivery date and time specified at the time of mailing - If the item was not made available for pickup or claim by the delivery date and time specified at the time of mailing. - [Exceptions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#money-back-guarantee-exceptions) to the refund apply. Postage refund requests must be made by the sender of the item within the time limits below: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Timeline by Mail Type or Service** | **When to File** | | **No Sooner Than** | **No Later Than** | | Priority Mail Express Delivery
Without an Extra Service | 2 days (after the date of mailing) | 30 days (after the date of mailing) | | Priority Mail Express Delivery
With Extra Service(s) | 30 days (after the date of mailing) | 60 days (after the date of mailing) | | | | --- | | **Notes:**
- The guaranteed 6:00pm delivery time is the local time at the recipient's address.
- An attempted delivery meets the delivery commitment (i.e. the mailpiece was taken to the address, but the recipient was unavailable to accept the item). [Other events](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#money-back_guarantee) also meet the delivery commitment.
- Postage refunds may not be considered if the delay of the item was caused by [specific reasons](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#money-back-guarantee-exceptions) beyond the control of the USPS.
- In addition, postage for guaranteed service is not refunded for PME items:
- Containing live animals or hazardous materials delivered or when delivery was attempted within 3 days of the date of mailing. | **How to Request a Refund** The quickest way for the sender to submit a postage refund request for the [above reasons](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#pme_refund_eligibility) is [online](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm). You can also take either the mailing receipt or your original customer copy of the [Priority Mail Express® label](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#priority_mail_express_label) to any Post Office and complete a PS Form 3533, _Application and Voucher for Refund of Postage and Fees_. Denied refund requests can be appealed. Online refund requests must be appealed online within 30 days of receipt of the denial. Refund requests submitted through PS Form 3533 are appealed through a postmaster to the manager of Pricing and Classification Service Center who issues the final agency decision. | | | --- | | For additional information, see:
- [Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services)
- The [_Domestic Mail Manual_](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#ep1184958) | **Receiving Approved Refunds** Approved refunds are paid in different ways depending on the service and how you applied. - Approved online refunds will receive a check by mail. - Approved refunds that were requested at the Post Office will be refunded in cash, check, or money order. - Approved Click-N-Ship refunds are credited to the original payment account. - Business customers who applied for a refund online will be refunded by check, not credited back to their account. **Refund Eligibility Exceptions** Postage is not refunded if the money-back guarantee service was not provided due to any of the following: 1. The item/shipment was: - Properly detained for law enforcement purposes. - Delayed due to strike or work stoppage. - Delayed because of an incorrect ZIP Code™ or address; or forwarding or return service was provided after the item was made available for claim. - Available for delivery, but the addressee made a written request that the shipment be held for delayed delivery. - Undeliverable as addressed. 2. If authorized by USPS Headquarters, and the delay was caused by governmental action beyond the control of USPS or air carriers; war, insurrection, or civil disturbance; delay or cancellation of flights; projected or scheduled transportation delays; breakdown of a substantial portion of USPS transportation network resulting from events or factors outside the control of USPS; or acts of God. 3. The shipment contained live animals or hazardous materials and was delivered, or delivery was attempted within 3 days of the date of mailing. 4. The Priority Mail Express 1-Day shipment was mailed December 22 through December 25 and was delivered or delivery was attempted within 2 business days of the date of mailing. 5. The postage refund requested is other than for loss, and the Priority Mail Express mailpiece was destined: - To or originated from Alaska or Hawaii. - To or originated from Guam, American Samoa, or the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands (see [608.2.4.1](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/608.htm#ep1255689) for ZIP Codes). - To the Republic of Palau, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, or the Federated States of Micronesia (see [608.2.4.1](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/608.htm#ep1255689) for ZIP Codes). Additional exceptions apply to [Priority Mail Express Military® (PMEM) Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS), a type of PME service. The item was: 1. Delayed by Customs. 2. Destined for an APO/FPO or DPO that was closed on the intended day of delivery and delivery was not attempted the next business day. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top) Hold for Pickup Priority Mail Express® shipments can be sent as Hold for Pickup rather than have a carrier attempt delivery. Items are held for pickup for five postal business days at the designated location and then will be returned to sender. Hold for Pickup is available for acceptance and pickup at most retail locations and self-service kiosks. **Hold for Pickup is not available for PME items containing cremated remains.** - Service includes an automated call back, email, or text notification to customers about the arrival status of their package. For Priority Mail Express®, if required, a second automated notification is sent after 3 days or a final automated notification that the shipment will be returned to the sender after 5 calendar days. - Recipients can pick up their packages within 5 days when the destination office is open for retail business. - The Sunday/Holiday fee will not be applied to Hold for Pickup service even though the customer may be able to pickup their package on a Sunday and/or holiday. See **[Hold for Pickup Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Hold-for-Pickup-Service)** for additional information. SAMPLE Label 11-F, _Priority Mail Express Service Hold for Pickup Label,_ MAY 2021 | | | --- | | ![Label 11F - Priority Mail Express Hold for Pickup Label, MAY 2021](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/11-f-may-2021.png)
[Click to display larger image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/11-f-may-2021.png) | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Priority Mail Express® Service URL Name Priority-Mail-Express-Service [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Mail & Shipping Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGpGAM/mail-shipping-services) ## Related Articles - [Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services) - [Money Orders - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Priority Mail Express® Service
