**•** | **Election Mail, Ballot Mail and Balloting Materials** **What is Election Mail?** Election Mail is any item mailed to or from authorized elections officials that enables citizens to participate in the voting process, such as balloting materials, voter registration cards, absentee applications, and polling place notifications. Election Mail may be mailed as First-Class Mail® or USPS Marketing Mail® or USPS Marketing Mail at nonprofit rates. Completed ballots mailed by voters are First-Class Mail, regardless of whether they are prepaid by election officials or mailed with a stamp affixed by the voter. The only situation where completed ballots are not First-Class Mail is where the voter opts instead to pay for a premium service like Priority Mail® or Priority Mail Express® services. **What is Ballot Mail?** Ballot Mail is a subset of Election Mail that consists of any mailpiece sent to or from an authorized election official and containing a live ballot that may be used to cast a vote in an election. Live ballots are a specific type of balloting material. **What are Balloting Materials?** Balloting materials are materials sent to or from an authorized election official that facilitate a voter's ability to receive and complete a ballot, including postcard ballot applications, all ballot types (i.e., sample ballots and live ballots), voting instructions, mailing instructions, and return envelopes. | | | --- | | See [Voting by Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail) for additional information, including a video, on the vote by mail process. | **Is Political Mail sent by candidates running for elected offices considered to be Official Election Mail?** No, Election Mail and [Political Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail) are different. Political Mail is any material mailed for campaign purposes by a registered political candidate, campaign committee, or committee of a political party, or any material mailed by a political action committee or organization engaging in issues advocacy or voter mobilization. Political Mail may be sent for any public election — partisan or nonpartisan — for which there is a ballot. For more information on Political Mail, see [Preparing and Sending Political Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail). **Is it okay to print on the back of return ballot envelopes?** The U.S. Postal Service® recommends printing only the address and barcode of the election office on the front of the ballot return envelope and leaving the back blank. That said, some jurisdictions are required by law to include certain information on the back of the voter's return envelope, like a block for the vote's signature, address, and delivery barcode. Although we are able to deliver these envelopes to their intended destination, occasionally design issues may cause our mail processing equipment to read the voter's address and barcode on the return envelope instead of the election office address. This may cause the ballot mail to be delayed or returned to the voter. Election officials should consult a Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA) to ensure that mailpiece design and layout meet Postal Service guidelines and regulations. Customers may connect with an MDA between 7:00AM - 7:00PM Central Time by calling 877-672-0007 (select option 3 for mailpiece design) or submitting a design review request at [https://electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review](https://electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review). **Is the use of Intelligent Mail® barcodes (IMb) required on ballots?** No, but the Postal Service strongly recommends that jurisdictions and election officials use uniquely serialized Intelligent Mail barcodes (IMbs) with a Ballot Mail Service Type IDentifier (STID). IMbs and STIDs facilitate the tracking of individual ballots to and from individual voters and are critical for using the Informed Visibility® Mail Tracking and Reporting (IV®-MTR) service. | | | --- | | An STID is a unique three-digit code embedded within an IMb. It denotes the mail class, address correction option and whether Informed Visibility® Mail Tracking and Reporting (IV®-MTR) is being used for an individual mailpiece. The Postal Service makes available specific Ballot Mail STIDs to election officials to better track live ballots. The IV-MTR application provides information about when and where the Postal Service sorts a mailpiece on mail-processing equipment. | For more information on IV-MTR, see the [Tracking Domestic Ballot Mail section](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#tracking_domestic_ballot_mail) below. **What about postage for balloting materials sent to and mailed from others?** Balloting materials provided to voters for any election, whether disseminated in hardcopy or electronically, must indicate in a prominent location on the material that the proper amount of First-Class Mail® postage must be paid on return mailings. This information must be included in the balloting materials with the following marking options: - "First-Class Mail postage must be applied." - "Apply First-Class Mail postage here" may be printed in the upper right corner of the address side of the return envelope provided to the voter. - Approved variations of the above markings. The marking requirements do not apply to balloting materials that meet one of the following exceptions: - The balloting materials are qualified under the special exemption for military and overseas voting. - The ballot is returned under Business Reply Mail® service. - Return postage is guaranteed through a postage due account. - Postage on the ballot is prepaid by stamps, meter, or Permit Reply Mail. **Note**: If a return ballot is entered into the mailstream with insufficient or unpaid postage, it is the Postal Service's policy not to delay the delivery of completed absentee balloting materials, including mail-in ballots. We are proactively working with state and local election officials on mailing requirements, including postage payment. In cases where a ballot enters the mailstream without the proper amount of postage, the Postal Service will deliver the ballot and thereafter attempt to collect postage from the appropriate Board of Elections afterwards. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) **Tag 191** | | | | --- | --- | | **What is Tag 191?**
Tag 191, _Domestic and International Mail-In Ballots,_ is a green container tag that may be used by election officials to identify trays and sacks of Ballot Mail destined for either domestic or international addresses. Tag 191 cannot be used to identify other types of Election Mail. The tag provides greater visibility for Ballot Mail upon entry.
Tags are available at your local Post Office™ or your Business Mail Entry Unit, which can be found on Postal Pro at [https://postalpro.usps.com/node/1623](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/1623). Alternatively, the online order form for the tags can also be found under the Election Officials tab at [https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/](https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/). | **Image of Tag 191, _Domestic and International Mail-In Ballots_**![](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq00000012e5&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000kHp3e)
_Click image to see larger version_ | | | **Can Tag 191 be used with any type of Election Mail that displays the Official Election Mail Logo?** No, the green Tag 191 may be used only to identify ballots addressed for domestic or international delivery. The tag may NOT be used to identify other types of Election Mail, such as sample ballots, polling place notices, voter registration notices, or absentee applications. **Can Tag 191 be used with Political Mail?** No, the green Tag 191 may not be used by organizations or individuals who are not election officials or who send materials that promote political candidates, referendums, political campaigns, or a political message. To identify qualified Political Mail, political mailers may use the red Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_. **Is Tag 191 required to be on containers of ballot mail?** No. Although the use of Tag 191 is optional, Postal Service recommends the use of Tag 191 as it provides greater visibility to containers of Ballot Mail as they enter Postal Service processing and distribution operations. **Can election officials use Tag 57 to identify their mailing containers?** No. [Red Tag 57 is for _Political Campaign Mailings_](https://www.usps.com/political-mail/tag-57.pdf). It is **only** to be used to identify trays and sacks of Political Mail. **Is Tag 191 reusable?** Yes. Once containers of Official Election Mail have been emptied, Tag 191 should be removed from the trays and sacks and retained by Postal Service employees for reuse. **How can I get a supply of Tag 191?** Tags are available at your local Post Office™ or your Business Mail Entry Unit, which can be found on Postal Pro at [https://postalpro.usps.com/node/1623](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/1623). Alternatively, the online order form for the tags can also be found under the Election Officials tab, under the Election Mail visibility heading, at [https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/](https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/). **How do I identify ballots that I am mailing?** The Postal Service™ strongly recommends that election officials use the Official Election Mail logo on ballot envelopes and Tag 191 for the containers. Although both are optional, Official Election Mail logo and green Tag 191 both distinguish your mail from the millions of other mailpieces that Postal employees process every day. Ballot Service Type IDs (STIDs) are also strongly recommended for use in the barcode to improve Ballot Mail visibility within the automation environment as well as providing the correct handling and services for Undeliverable As Addressed (UAA) mail. Consult the STID Table for Ballot Mail available at [https://postalpro.usps.com/stid-tool](https://postalpro.usps.com/stid-tool). Detailed descriptions of the various Ballot Mail STIDs can be found in the Ballot Mail & Political Mail STIDs Fact Sheet available at [https://postalpro.usps.com/Political\_Election\_Mail\_STIDs\_FactSheet](https://postalpro.usps.com/Political_Election_Mail_STIDs_FactSheet). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) **Official Election Mail Logo** | | | | --- | --- | | **What is the Official Election Mail Logo?**
The Official Election Mail logo is a unique, registered trademark designed exclusively for inclusion in the design of Official Election Mail. The logo design features an interpretation of the stars and stripes of the American flag, and the words "Official Election Mail," clearly visible over the designation — "Authorized by the U.S. Postal Service." The design, color, and placement specifications for the logo are detailed in [Publication 631](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub631.pdf "Publication 631, Official Election Mail Graphic Guidelines and Logos").
The Official Election Mail logo should be used on any mailpiece created by, or on behalf of, an election official that is mailed to or from a citizen of the United States for the purpose of participating in the voting process. This includes balloting materials, voter registration cards, absentee applications, polling place notifications, and voter reply mail. The logo should be used on qualifying mailpieces in all classes of mail and all processing categories; however, the logo is not a substitute for postage. | ![The logo design features an interpretation of the stars and stripes of the American flag, and the words “Official Election Mail,” clearly visible over the designation — "Authorized by the U.S. Postal Service." This version is blue.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/official-election-mail-logo-blue.jpg)
_Click image to see larger version_ | **Are election officials required to use the Official Election Mail logo?** No, use of the logo is not required but the Postal Service does encourage election officials to use the logo on their Official Election Mail materials. When the Official Election Mail logo appears on a mailpiece, voters recognize the mail as important and distinct from partisan political mailings. Additionally, the logo serves to identify Official Election Mail for Postal Service workers and distinguish it from the millions of other mailpieces that are processed daily. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) **Publications** **Are there any Postal Service publications that are designed to help election officials with preparing their mail?** Yes, the Postal Service has many publications and resources that guide officials and our own internal employees in the preparation of Election Mail. Many of these can be accessed from the [_Postal Bulletin_](https://about.usps.com/resources/postal-bulletin.htm?year=2024) and at [https://about.usps.com/gov-services/election-mail/](https://about.usps.com/gov-services/election-mail/). Of note: - KIT 600 — _2026 Election Mail Program Kit_, is your guide to all the tools necessary to use the U.S. Mail™ as a secure, efficient, and effective way to facilitate the election process. - Publication 631, _Official Election Mail—Graphic Guidelines and Logos_, offers information on proper usage of the Official Election Mail logo for inclusion on mailpieces. - Publication 632, _State and Local Election Mail — User's Guide_, is designed to help election officials make decisions involving the design of their mailpiece, address list cleansing, class of mail, options for reply mail, and obtaining postage discounts. - The Postal Service strongly recommends that election officials consult local USPS Mailpiece Design Analysts (MDA) prior to mail printing. | | | --- | | MDAs are specially trained USPS employees who can answer questions about mailpiece design, give advice on evaluating pieces of mail for automation discounts, provide technical assistance on envelope standards, and help construct mailing plans. For assistance from an MDA, election officials can submit a design review request at [electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review](https://electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review), or call 877-672-0007 (select option 3). Hours of operation are Monday through Friday, 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. Central Time (closed federal holidays). | - The [_Domestic Mail Manual_](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/703.htm#ep1174014) provides additional details on requirements and recommendations for Ballot Mail. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) ## **Ballots for Members of the Uniformed Services, Overseas Voters, and their Families** **When can balloting materials be sent free of postage for members of the uniformed service, their families, and overseas voters?** Balloting materials may be sent through the mail without prepayment of postage to enable individuals in the categories below to apply for registration and vote by absentee ballot (in elections for Federal office) when absent from the place of voting residence and otherwise eligible to vote as an absentee: - Members of the Armed Forces in active service and their spouses and dependents | | | --- | | The Armed Forces includes the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, and the commissioned corps of the Public Health Service and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. | - Members of the U.S. Merchant Marine and their spouses and dependents - U.S. citizens residing outside the United States **Are there requirements for the deposit of mailpieces prepared as free absentee ballots for qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families?** Yes. Balloting materials, including return absentee ballots, to or from the individuals identified above must be deposited at the following locations to be mailable without the prepayment of postage: - U.S. Post Offices - Army and Air Force Post Offices / Fleet Post Offices (APO/FPO) - Diplomatic Post Offices (DPO), or - American Embassies or American Consulates - If balloting materials are deposited at other locations, postage must be paid. Envelopes containing these balloting materials must have the appropriate indicia to deposit without prepayment of postage: - The words "Official Absentee Balloting Material — First-Class Mail" (or similar wording required by state law) must be printed across the face of the envelope in a rectangular box. - Immediately below, the words "No Postage Necessary in the U.S. Mail — DMM 703.8.0" must be printed. - In the upper right corner of the envelope, in a rectangular box, the words "U.S. Postage Paid, 39 USC 3406" must be printed. - Lastly, an appropriate inscription or blank spaces for the return address of the sender must be shown in the upper left corner. - For more information, please see [_Domestic Mail Manual_ (DMM) 703.8.0](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#a_8_8). Another option is available for absent overseas uniformed services voters returning their absentee ballots. They may use Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD with Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA)-authorized APO/FPO/DPO addresses to mail from military APO or FPO locations, DPO locations, or Department of State (DOS) locations without the prepayment of postage. The label includes "Waiver of Signature" and "Guaranteed by End of Day" endorsements so ballots can be delivered the day they arrive at the destination Post Office. See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#priority_mail_express_label_11-DOD) and [_Domestic Mail Manual_ (DMM) 703.8.9](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#a_8_9) for more information on Label 11-DOD. All other types of ballots and balloting materials that do not meet the above requirements must bear postage using a permit imprint, postage meter, adhesive stamp, or other acceptable method of postage payment prior to mailing. **When I see the indicia that reads, "U.S. Postage Paid, 39 USC 3406," does that mean the mailpiece only has 39-cent postage?** No. That statement refers to Section 3406 of Title 39 of the United States Code, which is a federal statute requiring the Postal Service to carry certain balloting materials covered under the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA) free of postage. These mailpieces are not short-paid, and no additional postage is needed. **Can absentee ballots for state or local elections be mailed free?** No. The postage-free mailings for absentee balloting materials for uniformed services and overseas voters apply only to elections for Federal office. This includes any general election of electors for President and Vice President, or of senators and representatives in Congress, and other general, primary, and special elections. Combined Federal and non-Federal balloting materials also may be sent postage free under this rule. ## **Where can I find more information about military and overseas voting by qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families?** The [Federal Voting Assistance Voting Program (FVAP) (www.fvap.gov)](http://www.fvap.gov/) is dedicated to providing voting assistance for uniformed service members, their families and citizens living outside the United States. Contact the FVAP by visiting their website at [www.fvap.gov](https://www.fvap.gov/) or calling 1-800-438-VOTE (8683). **Where can I find the latest information for handling absentee ballots addressed to military and overseas absentee ballots?** The latest information can be found in the "Military & Overseas" tab on [www.usps.com/electionmail](https://www.usps.com/electionmail). **Where can I find more information about balloting material for uniformed services and overseas voters?** More information about the standards applicable to absentee balloting materials for uniformed services and overseas voters can be found in [DMM Section 703.8.0](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#ep1174014) or can be found by contacting your local Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA). **Can my absent overseas and uniformed services ballot be tracked?** | | | | --- | --- | | Yes, absent overseas uniformed services voters may return absentee ballots from overseas APO/FPO military locations, DPO locations, or Department of State (DOS) locations by using Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD (see image to the right) which provides the ability to track the absentee ballots all the way to delivery. Items with Label 11-DOD must be scanned for tracking and accounting purposes. | ![Image of a sample envelope addressed to a non-existent Registrar of Voters. Above this sample address is "OFFICIAL ABSENTEE VOTER BALLOT (To be Opened only by Election Canvassing Board)". The Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD is affixed across the top right corner. The label contains a barcode with a tracking number below it. The label states "ONLY FOR USE WITH ABSENTEE BALLOTS FROM THE MILITARY POSTAL SYSTEM". The label indicates that: "Postage Paid for by the Department of Defense", that there is a "WAIVER OF SIGNATURE", and that item delivery is "Guaranteed By End of Day".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/label_11_dod_absentee.jpg)_Image of a sample envelope addressed to a non-existent Registrar of Voters._
_Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD is affixed across the top right._ | **Are there special procedures in place to handle APO/FPO/DPO absentee ballots?** Yes, special procedures for those ballots are in effect between September 1, 2026, and November 30, 2026. The U.S. Postal Service and Military Postal Service Agency have joined forces to expedite the delivery of absentee ballots to/from overseas qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families. | | | --- | | If you are eligible to vote by mail and choose to do so, you should plan ahead to give yourself enough time to complete and return your ballot by your state's deadlines. You can look for information about your state's requirements and recommendations here:
- [Federal Voting Assistance Program: Find your state election website](https://www.usa.gov/election-office) | Details of other [special procedures](http://about.usps.com/kits/kit600.pdf#page=7 "Special Procedures: APO/FPO/DPO Absentee Ballots") are available. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) **Tracking Domestic Ballot Mail** **As an Election Official or Board of Elections, can I track ballots mailed within the US?** Yes. Tracking of Election Mail can be facilitated through the use of Intelligent Mail barcodes (IMbs) and the Informed Visibility® Mail Tracking & Reporting (IV®-MTR) application. **What does IV-MTR do?** The IV-MTR application provides election officials with near-real-time tracking and enhanced visibility of uniquely barcoded mailpieces as they move through the mailstream, from virtually any device and any location. When uniquely serialized barcodes are used, IV-MTR provides mailers with enhanced visibility and tracking across the mail supply chain, from acceptance to delivery. - Mailers are able to track letters and flats, and the associated mail aggregates such as bundles, handling units, containers, and transportation, through IV-MTR. - IV-MTR leverages the intelligence of Full-Service Intelligent Mail® and nesting associations. The application creates assumed handling events for nested mail whenever the mail aggregate containing the nested mail is scanned. - And, it provides users with near-real-time mail tracking data, flexible data provisioning, and flexible data delegation options in an easy-to-use web application. Informed Visibility® (IV®) improves ease of use by providing flexible data provisioning, allowing the user to get what data they want, when they want, and how they want it. - IV provides one-time queries and data feeds in a variety of file formats and delivery methods. - We have also released APIs that facilitate users building a mobile app or web service to interface with the IV-MTR system for single IMb information and receive campaign or job information delivered to their application. **How does IV-MTR help Election Officials?** For outgoing mail, IV-MTR data allows election officials to: - Obtain near-real-time notification when mail receives its last processing scan - Know when mailpieces reach their destination so election officials can better manage election activities - Identify mail delivery trends and anticipate delivery dates For reply mail returning to election officials, IV-MTR data helps them to: - Obtain near-real-time notification when return mail enters the mailstream by utilizing a serialized IMb and - Know when voters' return items are on the way and where they are in the Postal Service™ network **How do I get started with IV-MTR?** To get started with IV-MTR, you need to: 1. Register for a Business Customer Gateway (BCG) account, enable the IV-MTR service, and set up your Election Mail data feeds. Step-by-step instructions are available in the Applying for Access presentation on the IV-MTR PostalPro™ page at [https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility/ApplyForAccess](https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility/ApplyForAccess). 2. Set up your Election Mail queries or data feeds. Step-by-step instructions are provided in the IV-MTR User Guides and Training section of PostalPro at [https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility](https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility). Other resources for using the IV-MTR application, including Orientation Trainings and resources for understanding your tracking data, are available on the IV-MTR PostalPro page at [https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility](https://postalpro.usps.com/InformedVisibility). **What factors impact the level of visibility of Ballot Mail?** Ballot Mail visibility refers to the range of tools available to provide increased insight and trackability for mailpieces within the postal network. Ballot Mail visibility is enabled or enhanced by using green Tag 191, dedicated Ballot Mail Service Type IDentifiers (STIDs), serialized Intelligent Mail barcodes for ballots, and the Ballot Mail check box. The Postal Service uses the Ballot Mail checkbox in eDoc, Mail.Dat, Mail.XML, Postal Wizard, and Intelligent Mail for Small Business (IMsb) tool to get increased visibility of Ballot Mail in the mailstream. In addition, the Official Election Mail® logo should be used for all Election Mail, including ballots. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) # **Additional Information** **Can a ballot collection receptacle be placed in a Post Office lobby?** No, Postal Service policy does not permit placement of an election office's ballot collection receptacle in a Post Office lobby. **May election-related materials be placed in Postal Service lobbies?** Postal Service regulations generally prohibit the deposit of any flyers, pamphlets or other literature in Post Office lobbies or anywhere on postal property. However, Postmasters or installation heads should allow the deposit of voter registration forms and absentee ballot request forms in the Post Office Box lobby area of retail facilities if there is adequate space available. **Where can election officials get information about mailing?** Visit our election officials' tab on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) at[https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/](https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/). **I am a local Election Official. Who should I contact if I have questions about Official Election Mail?** - **For assistance with selecting appropriate electronic documentation submission method**, please discuss your mailing needs with a mailing requirements clerk. Contact the Mailing and Shipping Solutions Center (MSSC) by emailing [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov) or calling 877-672-0007 (select option 2). - **To have Election Mail envelopes, including previously approved designs, be reviewed by a Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA)**, submit a design review request at [electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review](https://electionmail.usps.com/s/election-mail-review) or call 877-672-0007 (select option 3). - Hours of operation for both MDAs and MSSCs are Monday through Friday, 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. Central Time (closed federal holidays). - Connect with a local Manager, Customer Relations (MCR) under the Election Officials tab at [about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/welcome.htm.](https://about.usps.com/what/government-services/election-mail/) **Does all Ballot Mail bear an address correction service request endorsement? If so, how is this handled?** No, there is no requirement for Ballot Mail to bear an address correction service request endorsement. Election officials may use one, if desired, to obtain a notice that a voter has submitted a Change of Address for the purpose of mail forwarding. The Board of Election can update their voter records, if permitted by their state's laws. This would allow the election office to contact the voter to determine if the voter has moved or if the voter is simply having mail forwarded for a reason not related to a move. However, ballots typically are not delivered if the intended recipient's address is out of date or if the mailpiece is not properly addressed. Accurate and updated addresses are necessary to help ensure the timely delivery of Ballot Mail. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Election Mail URL Name Election-Mail [Contacts & Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG8GAM/contacts-help)[Seasonal Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHGGA2/seasonal-services)[Hot Topics](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHTGA2/hot-topics) ## Related Articles - [Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office) - [Mail Forwarding Options](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Forwarding-Options) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [What is the best way to contact the United States Postal Service®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-best-way-to-contact-the-United-States-Postal-Service) - [No mail delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Election Mail

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq000000144nAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies) ## Ordering Free Shipping Supplies ## How and where to order free shipping supplies from The Postal Store®, Click-N-Ship® or by phone. - Feb 26, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007007 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[How do I order free shipping supplies?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#order_free_shipping_supplies)** | **[What else do I need to know about these free shipping supplies?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#need_to_know)** | **[Are there any restrictions on free shipping supplies?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#shipping_supplies_restrictions)** | | **[What USPS® shipping supplies can I order?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#shipping_supplies_to_order)** | **[Expedited shipping supplies](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#expedited_shipping_supplies)** | | The Postal Service offers over 65 standard packaging supplies that customers may choose from to ship Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail, Priority Mail Express International and Priority Mail International. There is no charge for this packaging when used as intended. It can be ordered online or by phone and will be delivered to your home or business. Limited packaging is available is small quantities at most retail locations. **How do I order free shipping supplies?** To order free labels, boxes, and envelopes online at [The Postal Store](https://store.usps.com/store/home): 1. Go to The Postal Store® at [www.usps.com/shop](http://www.usps.com/shop) and select "Supplies" – OR – move your cursor over "Shop" in the top navigation and select "Shipping Supplies." 2. Order free supplies by selecting "Free Shipping Supplies" in the left-hand navigation. To order free shipping labels, boxes, and envelopes online through [Click-N-Ship®](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=CARP&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fcnsb.usps.com%2F): 1. Add a label to your cart 2. Select the "Add Shipping Supplies" button under the label in the cart. To order free shipping supplies by phone, call the **Package Supply Center** at 1-800-610-8734. Hours of operation are: - Monday through Friday - 7:00 a.m. to 11:00 p.m. Eastern Time - Saturdays - 7:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Eastern Time - Sundays and Holidays - Closed (see [USPS® Holiday Service Schedule](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Holiday-Service-Schedule)) **What else do I need to know about these free shipping supplies?** - Orders for free shipping supplies are shipped within one business day from order receipt. They are shipped via USPS Ground Advantage® service with an expected delivery of 2-5 business days at no cost to the you. Free shipping supplies can be shipped via Priority Mail® service with an expected delivery of 1-3 business days for a fee. - If a single quantity of an item is desired but unavailable for individual order online at The Postal Store: - Order the desired item in the lowest quantity available then return extra (unused) supplies to your local Post Office or save them for future use. - Pick up single quantities of Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail®, and [other supplies](https://store.usps.com/store/shipping-supplies/free-shipping-supplies/_/N-alnx4j?Nrpp=48) free of charge at most Post Office™ retail locations. - Label availability: At this point, pre-printed labels are not available online. You may request Priority Mail® service labels by calling the Package Supply Center. - If you want to order Priority Mail labels with a company name and address should call the Package Supply Center. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#top) **Are there any restrictions on free shipping supplies?** Any supplies mailed in Priority Mail Express® or Priority Mail® packaging provided by the US Postal Service is charged the appropriate Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail price, regardless of how the packaging is reconfigured (turned inside out) or how the markings are obliterated. If any Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail packaging or packaging supplies (e.g., Priority Mail tape to seal a Parcel Select® package) are used on any other class of mail, the applicable Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail price will be charged. - Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail packaging provided by the US Postal Service can only be used for the class of mail specified on the box (Priority Mail Express® service or Priority Mail® service). Misuse may be a violation of federal law. - If you want to complain or report misuse of supplies, please contact your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action). - Priority Mail and Priority Mail Express packages and envelopes can be used to ship in the United States or to another country. - Most Post Office™ retail locations provide free Priority Mail® and Priority Mail Express® packaging supplies for customer pick-up. - USPS-produced packaging is not eligible for shipping mailable hazardous material or live animals. - **Note**: USPS Ground Advantage and USPS Connect® Local USPS-produced packaging may be used for shipping mailable hazardous material. In addition, if you are a large volume mailer or you cannot get to a Post Office, you can also order shipping supplies online for free from [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/home). Go to [www.usps.com/shop](http://www.usps.com/shop) and click on "Supplies". From there, you can search for all of the free shipping supplies by product type. If you don't have internet access (or wish to place an order that is larger than the maximum amount allowed online), you can call the USPS Package Supply Center. - The Package Supply Center can be reached via phone at **1-800-610-8734.** - The hours of operation are: - Monday through Friday - 7:00 a.m. to 11:00 p.m. ET - Saturdays - 7:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#top) **What USPS® Shipping Supplies Can I Order?** The following list is an overview of some of the various shipping products you can order online through The Postal Store® or Click-N-Ship®. These products can be ordered in various bundles (ex. 10 and 25). Please see the Postal Store for specific bundle types of each product: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Priority Mail® Service [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#note-shipping_supplies)** | Flat Rate Supplies
- Flat Rate Boxes (Small, Medium, and Large)
- Flat Rate APO/FPO/DPO Boxes
- Flat Rate Envelopes
- Flat Rate Padded Envelopes
- Flat Rate Letter Size Envelopes
- Flat Rate Window Envelopes
- Flat Rate Legal Size Envelopes | Regular Mailing Supplies
- Mailing Boxes (Various sizes)
- Envelopes
- Mailing Labels and Stickers
- APO/FPO/DPO Envelopes
- Large Video Boxes
- Mailing Tubes (Small and Large) | | **Priority Mail Express® Service [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#note-shipping_supplies)** | Flat Rate Supplies
- Flat Rate Envelopes
- Flat Rate Legal Size Envelopes | Regular Mailing Supplies
- Tyvek Envelopes
- Window Envelopes
- Mailing Boxes
- Mailing Labels and Stickers
- Mailing Tubes (Small and Large) | | **Commonly Used Postal Forms** | -- | Regular Mailing Supplies
- USPS Tracking® Forms
- Signature Confirmation™ Forms
- Custom Forms
- Return Receipt Forms
- Certified Mail® Receipts
- Insurance Receipts | | **\\* Please Note**: Many of these products can be used to mail both domestic and international packages. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#top) **Shipping Supplies Fulfilled by Local Post Office** Select supplies are available in smaller quantities for expedited delivery in 1-2 business days. | | | | --- | --- | | **When requesting Expedited Shipping** **Supplies, is there a limited amount a customer can order?** | Yes. Customers may order a maximum of five (5) of each item type per order. | | **What types of supplies are available?** | The types of packaging supplies available for an expedited request are:
- Priority Mail Flat Rate® Envelope
- Priority Mail Small Flat Rate Box
- Priority Mail Medium Flat Rate Box 1
- Priority Mail Medium Flat Rate Box 2
- Priority Mail Large Flat Rate Box
- Priority Mail Express® Flat Rate Envelope | | **How will expedited shipping supplies be delivered?** | Supplies will be delivered by the carrier. | | **How will customers be notified if expedited shipping supplies aren't available at the local Post Office™ and will be sent from the fulfillment center?** | Customers will receive an email informing them of exception handling by the fulfillment center. Supplies will be delivered via USPS Ground Advantage service with an expected delivery of 2-5 business days at no cost to the customer. | | **How do I receive customer service support for my order?** | Call 1-800-ASK-USPS® (800-275-8777). | | **Note**: Rural carriers are NOT required to handle or return the Priority Shipping Supplies manifest. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Ordering Free Shipping Supplies URL Name Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies [Postal Store](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFzGAM/postal-store)[Shipping Supplies](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HH1GAM/shipping-supplies) ## Related Articles - [Ordering Online - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics) - [Money Orders - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) - [USPS Ground Advantage®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage) - [Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup) - [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Ordering Free Shipping Supplies

## Return to Sender Mail

## How long it takes for mail to be returned to the sender.      - Feb 25, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006166

Customer Information

**How long does it take for a mailpiece to be returned to sender?**

The amount of time that it takes mail to be returned to sender will vary based on the services that have been purchased for the mailpiece, the mail class used to send the mailpiece, and mail services that affect delivery time - such as a Change of Address or Hold. Certain services or mailpieces also require the intended recipient to be available at time of delivery. In these situations, if that intended recipient is not available, the carrier will leave a notice, and the item will be stored at the Post Office™ for a specified period of time.

Keeping these factors in mind, it is best not to have expectations regarding how long it will take a mailpiece to be returned to the sender - except in the case of mail that is on the [Second and Final Notice and Return Dates Chart](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery).

- **Note:** Mail that is returned to sender returns in the same mail class used to send it.

**Will I receive notification if my mailpiece is returned to sender?**

No. Unfortunately, we cannot provide a notification if a (non-trackable) mailpiece you sent is returned to sender.

- **Note:** You can sign up for tracking alerts (on eligible items, with USPS Tracking® included) on the [USPS Tracking website](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) or by texting your tracking number to 28777 (2USPS). For additional information see [USPS Tracking The Basics.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics)

**My mailpiece was returned to me twice**

Postal employees and our high-speed equipment have only a brief second to look at an envelope, read information and enter it for processing. Mail with insufficient information or mail that has insufficient postage is rejected and returned to the sender with the endorsement “Return to Sender." This endorsement is very prominent and is designed to be eye-catching. For this reason, we ask that any returned mail, upon being mailed again, be enclosed in a separate (new) envelope with new postage to ensure prompt processing. This also helps to avoid mail being returned to the sender a second time.

|     |
| --- |
| For information on Endorsements (An authorized marking on a mailpiece that shows handling instructions, an extra service, or a request for an ancillary service) please refer to the Domestic Mail Manual - [DMM, section 507.1](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm). |

USPS Ground Advantage will feature 2-5 day service standards for packages up to 70 pounds. - Feb 17, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008072 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**How it works**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#how_it_works) | [**Benefits**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#benefits) | | [**Pricing and product details**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#pricing_product_details) | [**Background information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#background_information) | As part of the United States Postal Service's strategy to enhance its shipping offerings, the product **USPS Ground Advantage® provides a simple, reliable and more affordable way to ship packages from 1oz up to 70lbs in two-to five business days**. This streamlined product offering for consumers and businesses of all sizes is available now. | | | --- | | Visit the [_USPS Ground Advantage_](https://www.usps.com/ship/ground-advangtage.htm) page to learn more about USPS Ground Advantage. | **How it works** USPS Ground Advantage, available to retail and commercial customers, is a better fit by combining three existing USPS® services into one comprehensive product. It is a perfect option for shippers of all sizes who want affordable domestic ground shipping to all 50 states, U.S. military bases, territories, possessions, and Freely Associated States. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **USPS Parcel Select Ground® service**
_discontinued_ | | **USPS Ground Advantage** | | **USPS First-Class Package Service®**
_renamed to USPS Ground Advantage_ | **→** | | **USPS Retail Ground® service**
_discontinued_ | | In addition, business customers can use USPS Ground Advantage return service as a convenient option for their customers who need to send items back. It combines returns options such as First-Class Package Return® service and Ground Returns. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#top) **Benefits** | | | --- | | **For All Customers** | | - $100 insurance is included (for both outbound and return shipments), and you can purchase up to $5,000 in additional coverage against loss or damage for merchandise.
- Tracking is included.
- Pay for postage using stamps, a self-service kiosk, Click-N-Ship® service, or at a Post Office™ location.
- Includes forwarding, undeliverable as addressed (UAA), and return to sender (RTS) at no additional cost. | | **For Businesses** | | - Business customers can use USPS Ground Advantage Return service as a convenient option for customers who need to send items back. | | | --- | | For businesses interested in getting commercial pricing for USPS Ground Advantage, using USPS Ground Advantage Return Service to manage customer returns, or learning other ways USPS can power up your shipping and logistics, go to the [USPS Ground Advantage product page](https://www.usps.com/ship/ground-advantage.htm) and click "Contact a USPS Specialist". | | **Pricing and product details** USPS Ground Advantage prices are based on weight and zone (distance between ZIP Codes™). See the _Price List_ for [retail prices](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c450) and [commercial prices](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c451). Weight ranges are: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Up to 4oz
- Up to 8oz | - Up to 12oz
- Up to 15.999oz | - Each 1 pound increment up to 70lbs | | | | | --- | --- | | ​**Product​ details​** | **​USPS Ground Advantage​** | | **Delivery Service Standard** ​ | 2-5 business days [¹](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#table_note_1), [²](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#table_note_2), [³](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#table_note_3) | | **Attributes​** | - Tracking is included

- Free Package Pickup service at your home or office

- $100 of included insurance to cover shipments, including USPS Ground Advantage service shipments

- Up to $5,000 in additional insurance can be purchased

- Includes package forwarding and return to sender endorsements

- Sealed against inspection | | **Requirements** ​ | - Max 130 inches in combined length + girth​

- No minimum number of pieces required for entry | | **Special Pricing Considerations** ​ | Oversized fee assessed (between 108" and 130” length + girth)​
- Non-Standard Length fees apply (both Retail and Commercial)​

- [Dimensional weight](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) eligible​

- Primary option for sending hazardous materials (HAZMAT) that are only mailable using Ground Transportation​[1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#table_note_1) | | ¹Packages going to certain places (including Alaska, Hawaii, and offshore destinations), or containing [hazardous materials](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials) or live animals may receive slower service. Packages containing hazardous materials typically take 2-8 business days (not guaranteed). 
²Actual delivery time depends on origin, destination, and drop-off time. For additional information, visit the [Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/).
³Items weighing 15.99 oz or less travel by air to Alaska, Hawaii, and offshore destinations. Items weighing over 15.99 oz travel by ship to Alaska, Hawaii, and offshore destinations. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage#top) **Background information** USPS filing notices with the Postal Regulatory Commission for USPS Ground Advantage include the May 10, 2023 notice when USPS established proposed published pricing for the product and the February 10, 2023 notification to rename First-Class Package Service® with USPS Ground Advantage. **Why did the Postal Service launch Ground Advantage?** USPS is focused on strengthening its shipping solutions as part of the Postal Service’s 10-year strategic plan for service excellence, on-time delivery, and revenue generation from enhanced package delivery services. The plan seeks to balance the Postal Services’ shipping products portfolio by streamlining its product suite to meet the demands of the modern consumers and businesses it serves. This will be achieved by the Postal Service modernizing its network, improving mailing and shipping options for customers, and expanding reach to offer cost-efficient and reliable products and services. **What approach did the Postal Service take to launch USPS Ground Advantage?** Over the past year, the Postal Service focused on enhancing its ground product offerings by upgrading service standard to 2-5 days, improving service reliability, simplifying shipping product offerings, and providing affordable pricing. - **Lower Prices for Shipping Services.** The Postal Service filed notice with the Postal Regulatory Commission (PRC) on May 10, of price adjustments for some Shipping Services to take effect July 9, 2023. Published prices for USPS Ground Advantage will decrease 1.4 percent relative to current Parcel Select Ground and First-Class Package Service pricing. USPS Ground Advantage Retail prices will decrease 3.2 percent and USPS Ground Advantage Commercial published prices will decrease 0.7 percent. - **Improved Reliability Through Upgraded Service Standards.** In August 2022, the Postal Service implemented upgraded service standards for its USPS Retail Ground and Parcel Select Ground products, aligning service standards with the current First-Class Package Service product within the contiguous United States. Service standards for these products were accelerated from two-to-eight-days to two-to-five-days for the same affordable price. - **Simplifying Shipping Product Offerings.** On February 10, the Postal Service filed a procedural filing with the Postal Regulatory Commission (PRC) notifying the commission of the Postal Service’s intention to rename its existing First-Class Package Service category with USPS Ground Advantage. USPS Ground Advantage will feature two-to-five day service standards for packages up to 70 pounds. Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000013LdAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-M-bag-Service) ## What is M-bag Service? ## M-bags are sacks of printed matter such as books or advertising material sent to a single foreign addressee at a single address. - Feb 20, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006974 Customer Information M-bags are defined as direct sacks of printed matter sent to a single foreign addressee at a single address. The "sack" is a canvas bag for mailing printed items, such as books or advertising material. **General Information** The only items admissible in M-bags are Printed matter (newspapers, magazines, journals, books, sheet music, catalogues, directories, commercial advertising, and promotional matter). This service is frequently utilized by foreign students who attend school in the U.S. and want an inexpensive way to send their books home. **Note**: Effective January 1, 2025, certain articles are no longer admissible in M-bag services to any countries. These certain articles include: - Discs, tapes, and cassettes - Commercial samples shipped by manufacturers and distributors - Other non-dutiable commercial articles or informational materials that are not subject to resale For more information, refer to the [International Mail Manual (IMM) Sect. 260](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc2_026.htm "260 Direct Sacks of Printed Matter to One Addressee (M–bags) | Postal Explorer"). **Extra Services may be added.** - Certificate of Mailing is available. - Insurance, Registered Mail service, and Return Receipt service are not available. **Dimensions and Weight Limits for M-bags:** - M-bags are approximately 4 feet tall and 36 inches in diameter. - Weight - _**Minimum**_ \- There is no minimum-weight threshold for M-bag usage. However, if your M-bag weighs less than 11 pounds, you are still required to pay the applicable 11-pound postage price. - _**Maximum**_ \- 66 pounds, but lower limits apply to certain countries. **There are three types of M-bags:** - **Airmail® M-bags** - **International Priority Airmail® (IPA®) go to:** [International Priority Airmail / International Surface Airlift](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Priority-Airmail-International-Surface-Airlift) **Country Availability for M-bag Service** January 1, 2025 revisions to Universal Postal Union (UPU) regulations resulted in changes to Airmail M-bag and IPA M-bag services. The revised UPU regulations allow countries to stop accepting M-bags from other countries. If a country chooses to stop accepting M-bags, USPS will discontinue M-bag service to that country. The following countries do not have M-bag service available. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Aruba
- Australia
- Austria
- Belgium
- Bhutan
- Brunei Darussalam
- Burma (Myanmar)
- Canada
- Czechia
- Denmark
- Djibouti
- Estonia
- Finland
- France
- French Polynesia
- French Guiana
- Georgia
- Germany | - Gibraltar
- Guadeloupe (including Saint Bartholomew and Saint Martin)
- Hungary
- Iceland
- Indonesia
- Jamaica
- Japan
- Jordan
- Laos
- Lebanon
- Lesotho
- Libya
- Liechtenstein
- Lithuania
- Maldives
- Malta
- Martinique
- Mauritius | - Mayotte
- Netherlands
- New Caledonia
- Nicaragua
- Norway
- Oman
- Reunion
- Romania
- Saint Pierre and Miquelon
- Samoa
- Saudi Arabia
- Slovenia
- Suriname
- Sweden
- Switzerland
- Syria
- Vanuatu
- Vietnam | **Preparing M-bags** The sacks and mailing tags (i.e., PS Tag 158) needed for M-bag entry can be obtained from local Post Office facilities and are free to those using the service. An [electronically generated customs form PS Form 2976](https://cfo.usps.com/ "USPS.com® - Customs Forms") must be fully completed and affixed to PS Tag 158. Items must be placed into one or more individual packages (before being placed in the M-bag sack either at home or at the Post Office) bearing the name and delivery information of the sender and recipient. Each package sent in the M-bag must be marked "Postage Paid - M-bag." Mailpieces prepared as a bulk mailing must bear a U.S. return address. The return address must be visible on the outside of the mailpiece. M-bags may be sent to PO Boxes™. M-bags are returned to sender if they are undeliverable at no charge. When IPA M-bags are dropped at an International Service Center (ISC), the drop shipment is not subject to the 11-pound minimum price; rather, it is subject to the 5-pound minimum price. **Delivery Time** - Sent First-Class Mail International® - Varies by destination - Sent Priority Mail International® - 6 to 10 business days [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-M-bag-Service#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is M-bag Service? ## What is a Cluster Box? What is a Parcel Locker?

## Description of and guidelines for cluster boxes (aka NDCBU, CBU) and parcel lockers associated with a cluster box unit.      - Feb 20, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006975

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Cluster Box Keys** | **General Guidelines** |

This unit is a centralized unit of individually locked compartments for the delivery and collection of mail referred to as a Cluster Box Unit (CBU) Cluster Box units or “community mailboxes’ offer the greatest amount of fuel savings and carbon emission reductions because carriers can deliver mail to multiple customers during a single stop with less truck idle time. Increasing the use of centralized delivery for new and existing customers helps the Postal Service create “greener” neighborhoods across the country.

A Parcel Locker is a unit that is installed either within a CBU or alongside neighborhood delivery and collection box units or in conjunction with PO Boxes in a retail facility. They are used for parcel and package deliveries.

**Cluster Box Keys**

**Privately Owned Cluster Box**

- If the mailbox is for a house, condo, apartment or other physical residence, customers make arrangements to pick up the mailbox key(s) from the owner, manager or prior resident when they move in. The builder or property owner is responsible for providing lock and key service for privately owned cluster boxes.
- Carriers are prohibited from accepting keys for locks on private mail receptacles, buildings, or offices, Exceptions can only be authorized by local management.
- The builder or property owner is responsible for providing lock and key service for privately owned CBUs.

**USPS-owned Cluster Box**

- The Postal Service is responsible for providing every customer a compartment lock and three (3) keys to his or her postal-owned Cluster Box Unit (CBU).
- No key deposits are required and customers may duplicate their keys at no expense to the Postal Service.
- The Postal Service keeps no duplicate keys and if the customer loses all the keys, the Postal Service will have to install a new lock and issue a new set of keys at the customer's expense.
- Customers should return all compartment keys to the Post Office when they move from their residence. When a customer moves, the Post Office will change the lock before reissuing the compartment. The new customer is not charged for the first lock and keys even though the Postal Service replaced it.

**What are the General Guidelines for Cluster Box and Parcel Locker Delivery?**

- Letter carriers are instructed to deliver mail as addressed if they are unfamiliar with the recipient’s name. (Numbers on the boxes may not necessarily correlate with a mail recipient’s address.)
- Cluster Box units often have parcel lockers. The parcel locker is used for items that are too large to fit in an individual box (compartment) within the Cluster Box. A key will be left in the mailbox to be used as a parcel locker key. This key has a tag that indicates the parcel locker that contains the item. Insert the key into the correct parcel locker, and the mail piece can be retrieved. The key will remain in the locker once it is inserted.
- Letter carriers deliver accountable mailpieces to the point of address on the mail piece (condo, mobile home, etc.).

**Some Cluster Box units also contain a slot for "Outgoing Mail". Mail is collected from this slot by the carrier during regular mail delivery.**

|     |
| --- |
| For information on cluster box keys or locks, please see [Locked Mailboxes and Mailbox Keys](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Locked-Mailboxes-and-Mailbox-Keys). |

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Cluster-Box#top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000TObAAM%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online) ## USPS Electronic Signature Online® ## How to sign up for USPS Electronic Signature Online®, a service available through Informed Delivery®, and how to apply that electronic signature to select mailpieces and packages that require a signature. - Apr 24, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006798 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**Signing Up for USPS Electronic Signature Online®**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online#signing_up) | [**Using USPS Electronic Signature Online®**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online#using_eSOL) | **Signing Up for USPS Electronic Signature Online®** **What is USPS Electronic Signature Online?** - USPS Electronic Signature Online (USPS eSOL™) is a new offering that allows customers to electronically sign for their domestic Priority Mail Express® (PME), and Signature Confirmation™, and insured items over $500, thus authorizing carriers/clerks to deliver their items without capturing a physical signature. Please note: Business users who have access to Informed Delivery are not eligible for Electronic Signature Online (USPS eSOL™). **What are the benefits of this service to the United States Postal Service?** - The benefits for the Postal Service include being able to successfully deliver more packages on the first attempt, and reduce the costs associated with printing and maintaining [PS Form 3849, We Redeliver for You!](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) **Is USPS eSOL secure?** - Yes. USPS eSOL has Identity Verification Service that allows you to either: - Identity proof with your mobile phone and receive a one-time passcode, - Request in-person identity proofing at a Post Office™ location, or - Request an invitation code to be mailed to your address. All standard security protocols for protecting customer signatures are in place. To sign up for the service customers must first successfully pass the identity proofing process in Informed Delivery®. Once a signature is provided they are maintained on a secure Postal server. **Can a commercial shipper use their Mailer ID to require a physical signature and opt out of the use of USPS eSOL on an eSOL®-eligible item?** - Yes, commercial shippers have the option to require a physical signature instead of an electronic Signature. Commercial mailers that wish to prohibit the use of an electronic signature for a particular shipment have the following options: - **Option 1**: Include a value of “3” in the Waiver of Signature/Carrier Release/Customer Delivery Prefers column within Shipping Services Files, 1.6, 1.7, and 2.0. - If using USPS Click-N-Ship®, the mailer will be required to check the preference box indicating that they do not want to allow an electronic signature. - **Option 2**: Using the Mailer ID, send Product Tracking and Reporting (PTR) a Waiver of Signature code ‘3’; Mailer should request the opt out or cancel in advance via the [impb@usps.gov](mailto:impb@usps.gov) mailbox. **How do I sign up for USPS eSOL?** - The USPS eSOL offering is only available to Informed Delivery® (ID) customers. Once you enroll in Informed Delivery at [https://informeddelivery.usps.com/](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/), you can opt-in to USPS eSOL by clicking on enrollment ads in Informed Delivery or by logging in to your personal account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and going to the “Preferences” page within your profile. Note that business users who have access to Informed Delivery are not eligible for eSOL. ![Screenshot of "My Preferences" page under an individual's USPS.com account. This screenshot focuses on the "Account Management" section which has links the options "INFORMED DELIVERY - Manage your mail and packages" and "USPS ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE ONLINE - Digitally sign for your packages". The Electronic Signature option has been clicked and expanded to display an overview of the service, a checkmark indicating that you've read, understood, and agree to the Terms & Conditions of Informed Delivery and Electronic Signature, and a "Manage My Signature" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-preferences_eSOL_selection.jpg) **How do I provide my signature electronically?** - You can provide your signature either by using your desktop mouse, laptop mouse pad, or via a touch screen on your tablet or mobile device after selecting the USPS eSOL icon on the Customer Registration Preferences page. | | | | --- | --- | | **Screenshot of "Receipt of My Electronic Signature";**
**Purpose of signature and privacy act statement** | **Screenshot of "Capture Your Signature";**
**Signature provided on a desktop computer via mouse** | | ![Screenshot of "Receipt of My Electronic Signature". A paragraph provides an overview of USPS Electronic Signature and is followed by "Your Electronic Signature will expire on 10/01/2020" which indicates the expiration of the signature. That is followed by the Privacy Act Statement. After the Privacy Act Statement, there is a Signature Box that customers can click to open a new window.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_receipt_electronic_signature.jpg) | ![Small window titled "Capture Your Signature" opened as a result of clicking the Signature Box in the previous window. Customer puts in their signature and clicks either the left "Clear" button or the right "Cancel" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_capture_signature.jpg) | **How long are the digital signatures stored by the Postal Service?** - The digital signatures are stored for one year in an active state before they are deactivated on the expiration date, and then are held for two years after expiration in a deactivated state. You are also notified 90, 60, 30, and 1 day(s) before expiration that your signature will expire. After one year, you must go through the verification process again to opt-in to the service. **How do I stop or deactivate my USPS eSOL prior to the year ending?** - You must go to the Manage My Signature box and select the Opt-out of Electronic Signature option to deactivate your USPS eSOL. ![Screenshot of the "Manage My Electronic Signature" page. It allows customers to "Access and manage all your USPS Electronic Signature Online settings here." Five options are listed under that with the 4th option, "Opt Out of Electronic Signature", highlighted.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_manage_electronic_signature.jpg) **What if an item is to be delivered after my signature has expired?** - If you have indicated that your electronic signature should be applied to an item, the request is valid until 11:59 PM on the day of expiration. As long as the expected delivery date is before the expiration date of the signature, the electronic signature can be used. **How will delivery employees know that a package is to be delivered without obtaining a physical signature due to the request of an electronic signature?** - The Mobile Delivery Device (MDD) will alert USPS Carriers during the delivery workflow that a package has an electronic signature applied to it and can be delivered without obtaining a physical signature from the recipient. The MDD will not prompt the USPS Carrier to collect a signature for items that the recipients have applied their electronic signature to. The MDD will also indicate any delivery attributes regarding where to leave the package if the recipient has specified through [USPS Delivery Instructions®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics). **Can anyone in a household who is USPS Electronic Signature Online™ enrolled apply their USPS eSOL on any eSOL-eligible item displayed in the Informed Delivery dashboard?** - Yes. Anyone who has an Informed Delivery® account to use Electronic Signature Online can apply their electronic signature to any eSOL-eligible packages in the Informed Delivery dashboard. However, only the first signature applied will be used. **Can I, a PO Box customer, apply my eSOL for eligible products going to my PO Box?** - Yes. eSOL is also available for items that are going to PO Boxes. **If I received a [redelivery notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) for a package requiring a signature that is eSOL-eligible, can I sign up and apply eSOL?** - Yes, you can apply your eSOL to an item(s) being redelivered, but **you must do so before scheduling the redelivery.** You must be enrolled in [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action). Once enrolled, you can opt-in to USPS eSOL by clicking on enrollment ads in Informed Delivery or logging into your personal account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), and going to the “Preferences” page within their profile. **Once I sign up for eSOL, does it apply to every eligible shipment?** - No. You must select the eSOL option for **each individual** eligible package that is sent to your address. The opt-in is authorized through the Informed Delivery Package dashboard. **Are retail customers able to override USPS eSOL if they want to obtain a physical signature from the recipient?** - Yes. Retail Systems Software provide retail customers an option to override USPS eSOL by simply selecting Retail Override from the Customer Display Unit. When an item is overridden, the Mobile Delivery Device will alert the mail carrier to obtain a physical signature from the recipient. **How many times can I apply USPS eSOL on items whose delivery attempt failed?** - Customers are only able to reschedule a redelivery request once; therefore, you have only one opportunity to apply your USPS eSOL on eSOL-eligible items that will be redelivered. And remember that you must apply your eSOL to an item before scheduling the redelivery. **What products are eligible for eSOL?** - USPS Electronic Signature Online™ is available for Priority Mail Express®, Signature Confirmation™, and insured items over $500. **Are other Extra Services that require signature upon delivery, such as Registered Mail and Certified Mail, eligible for eSOL?** - No. You can only apply your USPS eSOL to the three items mentioned above. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online#top) **Using USPS Electronic Signature Online™** **How do I provide an electronic signature for a package?** - To provide an electronic signature for an eligible item (Mailpiece or Package), scroll to the item and click “ _**Apply your USPS Electronic Signature Online**_”. | | | --- | | ![Screenshot of Informed Delivery Packages Tab. It lists 3 different packages that have all been accepted by USPS. Only a single package, with a tracking number of 9289 2901 0069 0309 0364 95, has had "USPS Electronic Signature Online Applied".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_dashboard_several_packages.jpg)![Another screenshot from the Informed Delivery Packages Tab. Only 1 item is listed. Emphasized by a red circle is "This package requires a signature. Apply your USPS Electronic Signature Online."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_dashboard_single_package.jpg) | When more information on the item displays, click “ _**Apply**_” in the “Your USPS Electronic Signature Online” section. You will receive confirmation that “ **Your USPS Electronic Online Signature has been applied.**” This authorizes your carrier to deliver your eligible item without you or anyone at the address to be present to sign. | | | | --- | --- | | **Click "Apply" to complete Electronic Signature** | **Electronic Signature confirmation** | | ![Screenshot of Package Details. It indicates the status of the item, its scan history, and "Your USPS Electronic Signature Online." The customer's digital signature is below. At the very bottom is the customer's name, the "Preferred Delivery Location" ("Garage or Other Location at Address"), and a blue "Apply" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_package_details.jpg) | ![Screenshot of Package Details. It indicates the status of the item, its scan history, and "Your USPS Electronic Signature Online." The customer's digital signature is below. At the very bottom is the customer's name, the "Preferred Delivery Location" ("Garage or Other Location at Address"), and, in green, the confirmation "Your USPS Electronic Online Signature has been applied."a blue "Apply" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/eSOL/eSOL-id_package_details_applied.jpg) | **I’m expecting an item and Informed Delivery indicated that I could apply my USPS Electronic Signature Online to the item. I later went back to Informed Delivery and no longer had the option to apply my USPS Electronic Signature Online. What happened to it?** - There is a point during shipment when the USPS eSOL option is no longer available for an item. When your item arrives at your local Post Office, as indicated by the " **Arrival at Unit**" scan, you cannot apply USPS eSOL to it. **When do I have to apply my USPS eSOL once I am alerted in the Informed Delivery dashboard with the message, "You need to apply your eSOL"?** - You must apply your USPS eSOL before an item is out for delivery. Once an item has an Out for Delivery scan, it is no longer eligible for USPS eSOL. **If I apply an eSignature to a package and then change my mind before the item is delivered, can I remove my eSignature?** - No. The request for an electronic signature is final once applied; you cannot cancel your eSignature. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS Electronic Signature Online® URL Name USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online [Informed Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFnGAM/informed-delivery) ## Related Articles - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) - [Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading USPS Electronic Signature Online®

## What does USPS® do with letters addressed to Santa Claus or God?

## What USPS does when postal districts receive letters addressed to Santa Claus or God.      - Mar 22, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006993

Customer Information

**Customers may be interested in what the U.S. Postal Service® does with letters addressed to Santa Claus or God.**

Different postal districts may handle them in various ways. **For example:**

- Letters may be sent to the district Consumer Affairs office.
- Local businesses and major corporations are recruited to volunteer their services to answer those letters in a non-committal manner.
- Some Post Offices™ participate in Operation Santa™  during the holidays to respond to letters.
- "Dear God" letters are sent to Mail Recovery offices or local churches.
