- [USPS Smart Lockers](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/) site
- [USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) FAQ article | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[What is Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Redelivery "What is it?")** | **[Where is Redelivery service available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#WhereIsRedeliveryAvailable "Where is Redelivery service available?")** | **[When can a Redelivery be scheduled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#scheduled "When can a Redelivery be scheduled and when will it take effect?")** | | **[Who can schedule and accept a Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Who "Who can schedule and accept a Redelivery?")** | [**Can I schedule a Redelivery request without a PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#without_3849) | **[How do I get started with Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#started "How do I get started?")** | | [**Why am I unable to schedule a redelivery?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#UnableToSchedule) | [**Redelivery error messages**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#error_messages) | [**Scan events related to Redelivery**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#new_scan_events) | | [**Other Redelivery Messages**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#error_messages) | [**What is the difference between Redelivery and USPS Delivery Instructions® Service?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Difference "What is the difference between Redelivery and USPS Delivery Instructions® Service?") | [**What type of Mail Receive a Redelivery Notice?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Mailpieces) | | [**How does Redelivery Handle Priority Mail Express?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Express) | [**How does Redelivery Handle an Ordinary Mailpiece (Item with No Extra Services)?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Ordinary) | [**How does Redelivery Handle Mail Requiring a Signature?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#Requiring) | **What is Redelivery?** If we missed you when we tried to deliver your mail, you can schedule a Redelivery online using a tracking number or the barcode number shown on the back of your PS Form 3849 - _We ReDeliver for You!_ Scheduling a Redelivery is free. If you received a [_PS Form 3849_\- _We ReDeliver for You!_](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)_,_USPS attempted a delivery to you where either: - The mail receptacle (mailbox, neighborhood cluster box, parcel locker, etc.) was full; and/or - There were no available parcel lockers (neighborhood cluster box or PO Box); and/or - The item(s) was too large to fit in the mail receptacle; and/or - A signature is required or postage and/or fees are required; and/or - The item(s) could not be left in a secure place. If the above points apply and your item is eligible for the [gopost® Parcel Locker Delivery option](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup), "Parcel Locker Eligible" option is checked. The gopost option is available only at limited ZIP Codes™. Also, you will receive a yellow _PS Form 3849-SD_ if the above applies and the delivery attempt occurred on a Sunday. **Where is Redelivery service available?** Redelivery service is available in many, but not all, areas nationwide. To check the availability of service in your area, enter your contact information (name, address, phone number, and email address) on our [Redelivery Service page](https://tools.usps.com/redelivery.htm) and click "Check Availability." **When can a redelivery be scheduled and when will it take effect?** ![The "About the missed delivery" section, highlighting which notice the PS Form 3849 is, the first attempt or the final notice. The next line indicates how long the Post Office will hold onto it.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Sept22_Missed_Delivery_Final_Notice.jpg)Redelivery items are delivered on the requested date (but you may NOT request a specific time). That requested date can be up to the day the item is scheduled for return to the sender. You can find the Return to Sender date on the front of PS Form 3849 if it is the final notice for an item. Under the "About the missed delivery" section, the "Return Date" will be written after the checked "Final Notice". You can also determine when to expect the final 3849 notice and the Return to Sender date at [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) However, keep in mind that: - The scheduling must be done with advance notice and NOT on the date the item will be returned to the sender. Unless otherwise noted, the return date is in **_calendar days_**, not Postal Business days. - If a Redelivery attempt is made on the last day before it is scheduled for return to the sender, _but cannot be delivered_, the item will be returned to the sender. - **Signing PS Form 3849 and leaving it for the mail carrier does _not_ meet the minimum requirements for [accountable mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail) to be delivered. If your item requires a signature, it must be signed for by the customer upon delivery.** **Note**: If there is a weather issue with a scheduled redelivery, the item will be redelivered the next business day. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#top "Back to Top") **Who can schedule and accept a redelivery?** | | | | --- | --- | | _**Residential Redelivery**_ | The addressee or the addressee's agent.
- An agent is a specific individual you, as the addressee, authorize to pick up your mail item either by:
- Filling out [PS Form 3801](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3801.pdf "Standing Delivery Order"), _Standing Delivery Order_
- Writing a letter to the postmaster and taking the form or the letter to your local post office. Your agent can then pick up mail on your behalf at the local Post Office by presenting [Acceptable Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). | | _**Business Redelivery**_ | Businesses will likely only see the form if they do not have a designated, safe delivery location and if their item requires a signature.
- An employee may schedule and accept a Redelivery as long as they can provide all of the necessary Redelivery information for the business, along with [Acceptable Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).
- An individual employee must sign for the pieces. The "business" cannot sign and carriers must be able to input the first initial and last name of the person signing for the delivery. | Regardless of who accepts the redelivery, remember that: - The item can only be redelivered to the original address listed on the mailpiece - The addressee or authorized agent must be present to sign for accountable mail (items that are insured for more than $500 and/or require a signature, for example). | | | --- | | **_Please Note_**:
**The carrier is not required to verify that the person accepting the package at the home or business is truly the "authorized agent." By being inside the home or business it is assumed they are associated with the person the item is for. However, if an agent comes to the Post Office™ to pick up an item, then identification (including a** **[valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)** **) would be required.** | **Can I schedule a Redelivery request without a PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice?** Yes, however, you **do need a tracking number or a barcode number** to schedule a Redelivery request [online](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#get_started_online) or through [other ways](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#started). If you need help locating your tracking number, see [How to find your tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number). | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | You check the status of your item(s) at [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) using its tracking number. Only packages with the following scan events are **eligible** for Redelivery. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Available for Redelivery or Pickup | Notice Left | Receptacle Blocked | | Receptacle Full / Item Oversized | No Secure Location Available | No Authorized Recipient Available | | Reminder to Schedule Redelivery | Available for Pickup
**(Customer Pickup and Returned to Sender Only)** | | | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#top "Back to Top") **How do I get started or modify an existing request?** | | | | --- | --- | | _**QR Code**_ | Scanning the Redelivery form's QR Code with an Internet-enabled smartphone is the quickest and most convenient way to select your Redelivery options. Directions for this method is available at [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice). | | **_Online_** | The online method is also quick and convenient. If you want to have same day redelivery, submit your request by 2am CST Monday through Saturday.
To start, you will need the USPS Tracking number on either version of PS Form 3849, _We ReDeliver for You!_ that was left for you.
You can set up a Redelivery one of two ways online:
1. Access [Redelivery online](https://usps.com/redelivery/ "Redelivery Tool")
2. Use [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS Tracking")
If using USPS Tracking, the Redelivery option will ONLY be displayed under "Available Actions" if your mail item is [eligible](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#eligible_scan_events) for the service.
Once you access [Redelivery](https://usps.com/redelivery/ "Redelivery Tool") or [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS Tracking") online, just follow the prompts to enter the required information.
**To modify your scheduled redelivery request online** at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®, you will need your Confirmation Number.
- Redeliveries musts be modified **no later than the day before** the scheduled Redelivery.
- Locate the confirmation email you received when you placed the Redelivery order and click the link to 'Edit this request'.
- You may also visit [Redelivery online](https://usps.com/redelivery/ "Online Redelivery Tool") and select 'Edit an existing redelivery request'.
- If you have an [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account, you may modify your Redelivery by signing in to your account and viewing your Activity History. Find the Redelivery in the list of previous orders, and click 'View Details', then follow the onscreen prompts.
**To _cancel_ your scheduled redelivery request online:**
- Redeliveries must be cancelled **no later than the day before** the scheduled redelivery.
- Locate the confirmation email you received when you placed the Redelivery order and click the link to 'Edit this request'.
- You may also visit [Redelivery online](https://usps.com/redelivery/ "Online Redelivery Tool") and select 'Edit an existing redelivery request'.
- If you have an [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account, you may modify your Redelivery by signing in to your account and viewing your Activity History. Find the Redelivery in the list of previous orders, and click 'View Details', then follow the onscreen prompts.
- Under "Select how you want to get your mail", change from "Carrier Redelivers" to "Customer will pick up". | | **_Online Confirmation_** | A confirmation page will be displayed with a confirmation number to verify your request has been accepted. The confirmation number will also be emailed to verify your request.
If you don't have access to your email, please be sure to print out the confirmation page or copy down your confirmation number for future reference. You will need your confirmation number to revise your request. | | **_Manually using PS Form 3849_** | | | | | --- | --- | | **Accountable mail** items must be **delivered to you or your authorized agent** or **picked up at your post office by you or your authorized agent** is not present to sign or pay, the accountable mail item will be returned to the post office. | If you decide to sign the **_We ReDeliver for You!_** Notice and give it to your letter carrier, please note the following:
- Uninsured and ordinary parcels cannot be left in an unprotected place, such as a porch or stairway, unless you authorize this via the signed PS Form 3849, or you use the "Carrier - Leave If No Response" endorsement.
- If a letter carrier decides s/he is **unable to leave** your mail in a secure location, s/he may elect not to leave the non-accountable mail item(s) and may instead leave another Redelivery notice.
- Accountable mail items must be delivered to you or your authorized agent or picked up at your post office by you or your authorized agent. | | Customers should **allow 2 days** for Redelivery (the letter carrier picks up the completed notice the first day, and redelivers the item the second day). | | For **_Non-Accountable Mail_** (mail that does not require a signature), fill out the **back** of the **_We ReDeliver for You!_** under option 3 for _PS Form 3849_: | ![null](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Sept22_Back_redelivery_options.jpg)
- Select the day you want the item redelivered, allowing at least 2 delivery days.
- Indicate where the item is to be left by checking the appropriate box.
- Sign and fill out the form under "Delivery Section." If you decide to sign, there are [some things for you to be aware of.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#scheduled) | | ![Image of the Delivery Section of the PS Form 3849 Form (Redelivery Notice). The "Delivery Section" is a table with two columns and three rows. The left column has the text "Signature", "Printed Name", and "Delivery Address" in the three rows. The right column is empty, allowing customers to fill that space.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Feb21_Back_delivery_section.jpg)
Place the completed form in your mailbox. | | **Note**: The manual option is not available:
- For Restricted Delivery or Adult Signature items
- On PS Form 3849-SD for Sunday delivery attempts | | Items that are eligible to be redelivered to a USPS Smart Locker | ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Sept22_parcel_locker_checked.jpg)
The "Parcel Locker Eligible" option will be checked on the PS Form 3849 Form if the "USPS Smart Locker" option is available when you request a Redelivery online. | | | **_By Phone_** | When contacting us by phone, you must have the tracking number from either version of _PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!_ that was left in your mail receptacle. If you do not have your tracking number, you may take a [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) to your local Post Office and pick up your mail item. You may also [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) to schedule a Redelivery via telephone. Automated information is available 24 hours per day, 7 days a week. If you do not have the Tracking number, the customer service rep can only send notification to your Post Office to redeliver the item. **Note**: If you want to have same day redelivery, submit your request by 2am CST Monday through Saturday. You may also [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) to schedule a Redelivery via telephone or **modify** an existing redelivery request. Automated information is available 24 hours per day, 7 days a week. You must have the Tracking number from either version of _PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!_ that was left in your mail receptacle. If you encounter problems completing a redelivery order online, follow these [technical support](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues) steps. | | | --- | | **Note**: The only Redelivery options available by phone are:
- Carrier redelivery
- Customer pickup
- Return to sender
Redelivery to "USPS Smart Locker" is **not** available by phone. | | | **_In person_** | To cancel or modify a **_scheduled_** redelivery:
- You may pick your item up at your local Post Office no later than the day before your redelivery was scheduled. Bring your Redelivery Notice (PS Form 3849) and a [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) to collect your item. This will cancel your scheduled Redelivery
**Can a customer have someone else pick up their mailpiece for them?**
Yes, this option is still available. The customer may designate someone they know to pick up their mailpiece at the local Post Office. The customer must sign the form in the appropriate section and write the name of the person who is picking the item up on their behalf. This option is not available for Registered Mail and mailpieces with Restricted Delivery. | | **_Informed Delivery_** | If you have signed up for [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/ "Informed Delivery"), you can use that service to manage your redeliveries. | | _**Online Redelivery Technical Support**_ | If you are unable to get past the Redelivery verification page, try to clear the cache/history on your browser and try the website again. Sometimes the cache will return an error message after an earlier Internet problem has been resolved. 
Also, check your browser's security and privacy settings. If the settings are too strict, it may prevent you from submitting personal information on the website.
If you are experiencing any other difficulty in using the [Redelivery Service](https://usps.com/redelivery/):
- Please send email to: [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/)
- Include your name, address, and phone number in case our technical support personnel need to ask additional questions. | **Why am I unable to schedule a redelivery?** If your item does not show any of these [scan events](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#eligible_scan_events), it is ineligible for Redelivery. Possible reasons preventing the scheduling of a redelivery request include: - The address entered for the tracking number does not match the original delivery address - A Redelivery request already exists for the package - The package was returned to sender and is no longer available for Redelivery [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#top "Back to Top") **Other Redelivery Messages** You may have tried to schedule or modify a Redelivery request and received an error message. Listed below are error messages and explanations as to why you are unable to create or modify your Redelivery request. | **USPS.com Redelivery**
**Error Messages** | **USPS.com Redelivery**
**Error Message Explanations** | | --- | --- | | This package has exceeded the number of allowed Redelivery attempts. See Post Office™ information below to pick up your package before the Return to Sender Date. | There is a maximum limit of one Redelivery request. | | Package is not eligible for Redelivery. | Reasons that a package is ineligible for Redelivery:
- The address entered for the tracking number does not match the original delivery address
- A Redelivery request already exists for the package
- The package was returned to sender and is no longer available for Redelivery
- See [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) for more information
- Refused (by recipient)
- Delivered
- Out for Redelivery | | The address entered for this tracking number does not match the original delivery address. | Redelivery cannot be requested because the original tracking number does not match the original address. | | A Redelivery request already exists for this package. | A Redelivery request already exists. User will have to go to Modify Redelivery Request to search for and edit an existing request. | | Sorry, you can no longer make changes to this Redelivery request. | The user must modify the Redelivery request before 2AM CST, the cut off for Redelivery requests. | | Sorry, this Redelivery request session has expired. Please schedule a new request. | Redelivery request session is closed/expired. User needs to restart a new request. | | A Return to Sender request cannot be canceled or modified. | Redelivery request submitted as Return to Sender. This request cannot be modified once the request is submitted. User can track the status of the package by going to USPS Tracking® feature. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#top "Back to Top") **Scan events related to Redelivery** When tracking your Redelivery items, the latest tracking information displayed may be one of the following: | Scan Event Type | Scan Event Description | | --- | --- | | Available For Redelivery or Pickup | Item is either available for pickup at a local Post Office, or the customer can schedule a Redelivery request | | Prepared for Redelivery | Item is being prepared for Redelivery for requested delivery date | | Out for Redelivery | Item is currently out for Redelivery | **What is the difference between Redelivery and USPS Delivery Instructions® Service?** Redelivery is an order you place to get your mail item delivered again in the event the first delivery attempt was not successful. So, Redelivery takes place **AFTER** the initial delivery attempt. The USPS Delivery Instructions® service lets you set up your delivery options for a mail item **BEFORE** it has been attempted or delivered. **Need more information? See the following related FAQs:** | | | | --- | --- | | [Authorizing Someone to Accept Your Redelivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) | [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) | | [Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office) | [How to find your tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number) | | [How Redelivery Service Handles Different Mail Types](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types) | | **What types of Mail Receive a Redelivery Notice?** **_Accountable mail_** requires a signature and/or payment of fees. Therefore, you will receive a Redelivery Notice for the following: - Adult Signature - Insured Mail (over $500) - Collect On Delivery (COD) - Registered Mail® items - Certified Mail® items - Signature Confirmation™ service - Restricted Delivery option - Domestic Priority Mail Express® service items ( [unless signature waiver is not required](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Waiver-of-Signature-What-is-Signature-Required)) - Return Receipt service (attached to any class of mail) _**Non-Accountable mail**_ can be delivered to an address without a signature, and/or when the recipient is not present, when the postal carrier believes it can be left securely. Non-accountable mail includes: - Perishable Items - USPS Tracking® items - Uninsured Packages - Insured Mail (up to $500) **Handling Redelivery Mail** | | | | --- | --- | | [**How does Redelivery Handle Priority Mail Express?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#how_does_handle_PME) | - If the letter carrier cannot deliver the item (for example, if no one is available to sign for the mailpiece or the sender either requested a signature or did not request "Waiver of Signature"), they will leave a [PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)(there are **no** automatic redelivery attempts made after the first attempt for Priority Mail Express®).
- If the customer does not schedule a Redelivery or does not visit their local Post Office™ to retrieve the item, a second _PS Form 3849_ will be left on the 3rd calendar day after the initial attempt.
**Note:** If you do not pick up the item from the Post Office or schedule a Redelivery by the close of business 5 calendar days from the first attempted delivery, the Priority Mail Express® item will be returned to the sender. | | [**How does Redelivery Handle an Ordinary Mailpiece (Item with No Extra Services)?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#extra_services) | - When someone is normally available to receive parcels, but an ordinary parcel (i.e., a parcel without any extra services) cannot be delivered on the carrier's first attempt, a second delivery attempt is made the next working day and no _PS Form 3849_ is left on the initial attempt.
- If the parcel cannot be delivered on the second attempt, _PS Form 3849_ is left at the address showing that the parcel is being held.
- If no one is available to receive the parcel and the carrier knows that someone at the address is not usually available to receive any parcels; _PS Form 3849_ is completed and left after the first attempt.
- When it is not known if someone is usually available to receive parcels, _PS Form 3849_ is left after the first attempt. Additional attempts are made only at the customer's request. | | [**How does Redelivery Handle Mail Requiring a Signature?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#accountable_mail) | If no one is available when the delivery is attempted the first time, the letter-carrier will leave a _PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!_
- This form notifies the addressee that an article is being held for them at the Post Office™ and will be available for pick-up the following business day.
- The addressee also has the option to have the item redelivered;**_it will not be done automatically_**.
- 5 days after the first notice is left, a second delivery notice will be left.
- This item will be held for a number of days based on the specific service (usually 15 days).
- If it is not claimed, the item will be returned to the sender. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics#top "Back to Top") Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Redelivery - The Basics URL Name Redelivery-The-Basics [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Redelivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGMGA2/redelivery) ## Related Articles - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Mailboxes - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailbox-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Domestic Refunds - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Refunds-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Redelivery - The Basics

metadata.title: Redelivery - The Basics
metadata.description: Information on how customer can request their items to be redeliver when it cannot be delivered the first time.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000012UPAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) ## USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics ## USPS Package Intercept® lets you redirect a domestic shipment that hasn’t been delivered or released for delivery. - Feb 10, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007877 Customer Information **What is USPS Package Intercept®?** **USPS Package Intercept®** is a [fee-based](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics#How_much_does_it_cost) service that enables the sender or their authorized agent to request that an item that has not been delivered or is not out for delivery to be either: - Held for Pickup at the delivery Post Office of the destination address or another Post Office - Returned to Sender - Redirected to a new delivery address ( **can only be requested by a commercial mailer from the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin)**) This service is for [eligible domestic letters, flats and parcels](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics#Eligiblemailclasses) that have a tracking barcode (excluding Collect on Delivery Hold for Pickup mailpieces) and tracking number such as a USPS® IMpb® (Intelligent Mail® Package Barcode). If the item does not bear a tracking barcode and number or is [ineligible for another reason](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics#ineligible_mailpieces), USPS Package Intercept service is not possible. If you are not sure the item qualifies for interception, you can [check the item's tracking number](https://retail-pi.usps.com/retailpi/actions/index.action). **Checking the tracking number, if eligible,  and logging in to your retail [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account allows you to submit a Package Intercept** request. **Mail Classes and Items Eligible for Package Intercept** Package Intercept service is available for the following mail classes: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Priority Mail Express® 
- Priority Mail®
- First-Class Mail® | - USPS Ground Advantage® - Retail
- USPS Ground Advantage® - Commercial
- Parcel Select® | - Media Mail®
- Library Mail
- Bound Printed Matter | | | | | --- | --- | | **Eligible for Package Intercept** | **Ineligible for Package Intercept** | | In addition, items must have all the following to be eligible:
- Have a USPS Tracking® barcode or Extra Services barcode
- Be addressed to a domestic recipient (APO/FPO/DPO addresses are excluded)
- Be addressed from a domestic sender
- Not measure more than 130 inches in length and girth combined
- **Note**: Only USPS Ground Advantage and Parcel Select [oversized-priced](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) items are greater than 108 inches, but not more than 130 inches, in combined length and girth. | A mailpiece is ineligible for Package Intercept if it is any of the following:
- Sent to or from International destinations
- Sent to or from APO/FPO/DPO destinations
- A domestic mailpiece requiring a customs declarations label
- A domestic mailpiece indicating surface-only transportation such as a mailpiece bearing hazardous materials markings such as "Limited Quantity," "Consumer Commodity" or "ORM-D"
- A domestic mailpiece without a tracking barcode (including those for extra services) | **Package Intercept: How it Works and Time Limits** Retail customers can request Package Intercept through [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/packageintercept/welcome.htm) while commercial customers can request the service through the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin). Upon request, items are intercepted at the delivery destination Post Office™ and then returned to the sender or Held for Pickup at a Post Office as Hold for Pickup (but not to a PO Box™). Commercial mailers may also redirect items to a new delivery address ( **can only be requested by a commercial mailer from the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin)**). USPS Package Intercept® is not a guaranteed service and certain conditions apply. | | | --- | | Commercially shipped packages typically contain a tracking number that begins with a "92" or "93" and **can only** be intercepted by a commercial mailer through the Business Customer Gateway. Package Intercept through [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), which is retail customers only access to the service, can't be used for tracking numbers beginning with a "92" or "93" prefix. | 1. Eligible you will need to verify the item is eligible for an intercept using either USPS Tracking® or the [eligibility checker](https://retail-pi.usps.com/retailpi/actions/index.action) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® for all tracking numbers. 2. Account whether you are the retail sender or their authorized agent, you must have a personal [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account to [login to USPS.com](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/RegistrationAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/) to request Package Intercept service. If you are the commercial mailer, you must have a USPS business account to login to the [USPS Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin) and request Package Intercept. - Retail customers can use a credit card, debit card or [other forms of acceptable payment](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted) to pay the Package Intercept fee and any applicable postage and fees for the new mailpiece. These forms of payment will be charged at the time of the intercept or otherwise will be collected from the recipient as postage due upon delivery. - Commercial customers must use the mailer's Enterprise Payment System (EPS) account to process payment of the Package Intercept fee and any applicable postage and fees. 3. Time Limits once a request is made: - USPS Package Intercept® requests are **active for 7 business days from the date of the request.** - The Post Office™ will make every attempt to locate the item during that time. 4. No guarantee that the item can be intercepted before delivery; but, if the item is eligible, the Post Office™ will make every attempt to locate the item. - As long as the item is not showing as out for delivery or delivered, USPS Package Intercept® can prevent delivery to the addressee if item is located. Based on your request, the item is redirected as either: - Return to sender - Hold for Pickup (but not to a PO Box) at the Delivery Post Office™ - An item to a new delivery address ( **can only be requested by a commercial mailer from the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin)**) - In addition to the Package Intercept fee, charges may be added for postage to a different delivery location and fees for requested extra services added to the item. - Some intercepted items are relabeled as new Priority Mail® or USPS Ground Advantage pieces. Once intercepted, they are redirected at the applicable commercial Mail postage rate. You pay all fees ONLY if an intercept was possible. Click the original mail class below for further information. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Priority Mail Express®** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Intercepted Item Mail Class and Size** | **Interception Instructions** | **Relabel and Postage** | | **Priority Mail Express®** | Redirected to sender | Not relabeled

Charged USPS Ground Advantage commercial postage price | | Oversized-priced pieces measure more than 108 inches in length and girth combined up to a maximum of no more than 130 inches in length and girth combined). | **Bound Printed Matter, Media Mail®, Library Mail** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Intercepted Item Mail Class and Size** | **Interception Instructions** | **Relabel and Postage** | | **Bound Printed Matter**

- Can only be requested by a commercial mailer from the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin) | Relabeled and handled as a new USPS Ground Advantage item
Charged USPS Ground Advantage commercial postage price | | Oversized-priced pieces measure more than 108 inches in length and girth combined up to a maximum of no more than 130 inches in length and girth combined). | | **Notes:** - **You cannot edit or cancel your USPS Package Intercept® request.** - **The Package Intercept fee, charged after USPS successfully intercepts the item, is nonrefundable.** **How do I Get Started?** | | | | --- | --- | | **Retail USPS Package Intercepts** | **Commercial USPS Package Intercepts** | | If you are the retail sender or their authorized agent and want to request a retail USPS Package Intercept®, you the must have a [USPS.com account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/RegistrationAction_input?app=UspsTools&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Ftools.usps.com%2Fgo%2FTrackConfirmAction%21input) and a credit card, debit card or [other forms of acceptable payment](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted)to pay the intercept fee and any applicable postage and fees. After [checking the item's tracking number](https://retail-pi.usps.com/retailpi/actions/index.action) to determine intercept eligibility, you can continue the request process and provide your payment method. If the interception is successful, the intercept fee and any applicable postage are charged. If the item is delivered before it can be intercepted, there is no charge. [Get Started Now >](https://www.usps.com/manage/package-intercept.htm) | If you are a commercial/USPS business account customer and have an Enterprise Payment System (EPS) account, you can request USPS Package Intercept® by registering online and submitting your request through the [USPS Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin). As a commercial customer, you can intercept items individually or in bulk. | **How Much does it Cost?** Any applicable postage and fees for sending the intercepted item to a different location or for purchasing an Extra Service will be charged at the time of intercept – if the intercept is possible – or otherwise will be collected at the time of delivery by a USPS carrier. The USPS Package Intercept fee applies for each mailpiece intercepted. No fee is charged until the item is found and your intercept request is processed. When the order summary page appears, you will see an "Estimated Total" for your USPS Package Intercept order. Fees are estimated based on whatever information is available on your package - generally, weight and dimensions. Once the package is intercepted, your credit card will be charged an _actual_ cost that may vary from the estimated total. Additional postage and fees may be applicable to redirect the item as requested by the mailer and will be charged to the requestor's method of payment, depending on whether a Residential or Commercial account was established, **after** a successful intercept. For current pricing please see: [Get USPS Package Intercept price information](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198). **Extra Postage Fee Exceptions** Items originally mailed as Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail®, First-Class Mail® and USPS Ground Advantage® being redirected to the sender are not relabeled and bear no additional postage cost. Again, the intercept fee is charged at the time of successful intercept, or collected by the USPS carrier at the time of delivery. See [above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics#package_intercept_relabeling) for additional information on what intercepted items are and are not relabeled. When requests for commercial intercepts are made through the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin), postage and fees are automatically deducted from the customer's Enterprise Payment System (EPS) account **after** a successful intercept. An EPS account is required to pay the USPS Package Intercept fee and any applicable postage and fees (such as Extra Services) for the intercepted item. **What are Other Options for Intercepted Mail?** When a USPS Package Intercept is requested, Extra Services can also be added to the intercepted item(s). The Package Intercept fee is adjusted with cost for the Extra Service. No matter the original eligible mail class, when the item is relabeled as Priority Mail®, it is assigned a new mailing barcode and may include applicable Extra Services. Customers can continue to track the status of the intercepted item using the original article tracking number. Customers can choose from the following services: - **Registered Mail®** (only when Redirected to Sender) - **Adult Signature Required** (when Redirected to a New Address or when Redirect to a Post Office for Hold for Pickup) - **Adult Signature Restricted Delivery** (when Redirected to a New Address or when Redirect to a Post Office for Hold for Pickup) - **Insurance** (if the original shipment included insurance and only for the original value stated) (when Redirected to a New Address or when Redirect to a Post Office for Hold for Pickup) - **Signature Confirmation™** (when Redirected to a New Address or when Redirect to a Post Office for Hold for Pickup) **Extra Services Exceptions** - Registered Mail items are returned with the original Registered Mail number, and are not re-labeled or re-packaged. - In addition, customers requesting intercept of Registered Mail pieces must write on the Registered Mail receipt "Withdrawn," sign the receipt, and surrender the receipt to the Post Office™. - **[Are there any situations where I might have to pay additional postage for an eBay®-generated label?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#pay_additional_postage)**
- [**Technical Support**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#technical_support)
- [**What is the refund policy for eBay® Online Postage?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#refund_policy)
- [**What are the acceptance restrictions for eBay® Online Postage?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#acceptance_restrictions) | [**How do USPS and eBay interact?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#usps_eBay_interaction)
- **[USPS® & eBay® partnership](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#usps_eBay_partnership)**
- **[USPS® and eBay® co-branded boxes](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#usps)**
- **[USPS® on eBay®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#uspss) (includes vehicle purchases)**
- **[eBay® Day](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#eBay_Day)** | **What are the Benefits of Using eBay **®**'s Online Postage?** **Using eBay® for Online Postage** Instead of surfing over to the Postal Service™ website, sellers on eBay® can do all their United States Postal Service® shipping from the auction site. eBay has introduced Online Postage, allowing sellers to print shipping labels and pay for the postage from their PayPal account: - There is no surcharge for printing Priority Mail Express® and Priority Mail® labels with the free electronic price USPS Tracking® service. - Labels can be printed with postage for the following USPS® international services: - Priority Mail Express International® - Priority Mail International® - First-Class Package International Service® - First-Class Mail International® - First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™ and Media Mail® labels are also available with no label processing fees. Sellers can print shipping labels, pay postage, and check the delivery status of their items right from eBay. And buyers can calculate shipping prices online. It's convenient one-stop shipping for eBay users. **How does eBay® Online Postage work?** 1. The eBay® seller posts an item for sale on eBay. 2. From the "View Item" page, prospective buyers can learn about the product and calculate shipping prices by entering their ZIP Code™. 3. When the item sells, the seller collects a shipping fee from the buyer. 4. The seller selects the appropriate shipping method, prints the appropriate shipping label, and pays the appropriate postage from his/her PayPal account. - PayPal is a service available through the [PayPal website](http://www.paypal.com/) ( [www.paypal.com](http://www.paypal.com/)). **Does eBay Online Postage provide mailing options?** In many locations around the country, sellers can also access Package Pickup from the eBay website to request next-day pickup of their Priority Mail® and Priority Mail Express® parcels for no extra charge, saving themselves a trip to the Post Office™. Anyone who has a PayPal account can use this service. Packages shipped through "online postage" on the eBay website use postage paid by PC Postage® indicia. The United States Postal Service's presence on eBay's Shipping Center also includes links to items such as: - [Calculating postage](https://postcalc.usps.com/) - [Checking delivery status of an item](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) - [Locate a Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) - [ZIP Code™ lookup tool](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm) - [Buying insurance](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Buy-Insurance-Online) **Does eBay® Online Postage offer insurance for shipped items?** eBay sellers who print labels and pay for postage online using eBay Online Postage are able to purchase insurance coverage of up to $5,000 to cover lost, missing contents, or damaged packages. **What are the advantages of using USPS® insurance with eBay® Online Postage? What domestic services can insurance be added to online?** USPS® insurance purchased online works the same way and for the same price as retail insurance. In addition to offering this option for domestic Priority Mail Express® and Priority Mail® items, eBay sellers will also be able to use eBay's Online Postage feature to purchase insurance for USPS Ground Advantage™ and Media Mail® pieces. - Up to $5,000 indemnity coverage is available - No extra forms are needed - Because online labels always include USPS Tracking® service, you can also check your delivery status online! Using eBay, you can add insurance online to the following domestic services: - Priority Mail Express service - Priority Mail service - USPS Ground Advantage™ service - Media Mail service **NOTE:** You cannot combine USPS insurance purchased online with insurance purchased at a Post Office facility. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#top) **How do I Use eBay®'s Online Postage Labels?** **What features and information are on eBay®-generated labels?** eBay generated labels are produced by Pitney Bowes, eBay's designated shipping label provider.  They show either a "P" for Priority Mail® or an "E" for Priority Mail Express®, and they may show either the exact postage paid or may simply state "US Postage Paid" along with the appropriate weight, zone, and any surcharge or extra service for the shipment.  The option not to display the postage value on the label is a feature that is very important to customers.  Either way, the postage has been collected from the shipper. **Are there any situations where I might have to pay additional postage for an eBay®-generated label?** - If the item does not match the description on the label, and the indicia says "US Postage Paid", the package should be handled as postage due. - Articles bearing online labels with short-paid postage will have the additional amount collected when the item is mailed, or the recipient will be requested to pay when the item is delivered. - See [Automated Package Verification Program](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program) for additional information. For more information about eBay online postage, visit [eBay's shipping website](http://pages.ebay.com/usps/home.html): ( [http://pages.ebay.com/usps/home.html](http://pages.ebay.com/usps/home.html)) ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ebay_postage.jpg) **Technical Support** Make certain you contact only the website you used. Please include your account number, tracking number(s), transaction number and date(s) and details about your system for technical questions. If you're printing eBay® / PayPal labels: 1. Go to the [PayPal website](http://www.paypal.com/) ( [www.paypal.com](http://www.paypal.com/)). 2. Send an email using the [Contact Us](http://www.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr?cmd=_contact-general) link found on the bottom of every PayPal page. If you're printing Click-N-Ship® labels: 1. Go to [http://www.usps.com/](http://www.usps.com/). 2. Send an email using the [Customer Service](https://www.usps.com/help/welcome.htm) link found in the upper left corner of every [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® page. **What is the refund policy for eBay® Online Postage?** Refund or questions on postage purchased through eBay with a PayPal account must be handled through the PayPal web site. - If you have a problem paying with your PayPal account, please refer to PayPal’s Customer Service at 1-888-221-1161 or [https://www.paypal.com/us/cgi-bin/helpscr?cmd=\_help&t=escalateTab](https://www.paypal.com/us/cgi-bin/helpscr?cmd=_help&t=escalateTab) - You should also go to PayPal's Customer Service if you have address shipping related questions and issues regarding voids. **What are the acceptance restrictions for eBay® Online Postage?** Since eBay®-generated parcels use PC Postage® indicia, the sender is "known" to the Postal Service™, so acceptance restrictions do not apply. - Mailers can give these packages to the letter-carrier, schedule a pickup (Package Pickup, Pickup on Demand, or drop them off at a Post Office™ facility. - Items to domestic destinations can be placed in a Collection Box receptacle (this is not allowable for international packages). Refer to What Can and Cannot be Deposited in a Collection Box? for what can be deposited. - Like all shipments bearing PC Postage indicia, the revenue is reallocated to the originating ZIP Code™. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#top) **How do USPS and eBay interact?** **USPS® & eBay® partnership** **For the Postal Service™**: - The U.S. Postal Service® is a preferred shipper for eBay members, handling more than 75% of all items shipped within the US market. This complete integration of services will provide the Postal Service with total access to the Internet's busiest website and will give every eBay customer the opportunity to pick the Postal Service to fulfill the purchase with quality and value. **For eBay**: - The partnership provides quality, access, and convenience. "Fast, affordable, and reliable shipping services are essential to eBay buyers and sellers alike," said Meg Whitman, former President and CEO of eBay. "We are very excited about this relationship because the Postal Service is committed to working with us to deliver more value to the eBay community." **USPS® and eBay® co-branded boxes** The eagle is flying high on co-branded USPS and eBay shipping supplies. The Priority Mail® boxes come in three sizes and all display the eBay and Postal Service™ logos. There’s co-branded tape, too! eBay already offers online USPS shipping solutions, which allow sellers to calculate prices, purchase postage, and print shipping labels from their computers. With this new service, co-branded Priority Mail boxes can be ordered for delivery directly to an eBay member’s home. The new shipping materials can be ordered exclusively through: - eBay’s website ( [www.eBay.com](http://www.ebay.com/)) **Note:** There will be a link from eBay to order the free co-branded boxes and tape. or - USPS-eBay Co-Branded Box website ( [http://uspssupplies.ebay.com/uspsweb/catalog](http://uspssupplies.ebay.com/uspsweb/catalog)) **USPS® on eBay®** The USPS sells or has sold under the following Seller Names. - USPS-al-pmsc - usps-ne-springfield - usps-orlando - ms-zip You may also go directly to the eBay [search by seller page](http://www.ebay.com/sch/ebayadvsearch?_sofindtype=2 "eBay Search: Advanced Search") ( [http://www.ebay.com/sch/ebayadvsearch?\_sofindtype=2](http://www.ebay.com/sch/ebayadvsearch?_sofindtype=2)). **Note**: A variety of surplus vehicles may be available for sale via the Internet, or locally, at any given time. These can include passenger, light, medium, and heavy duty trucks. Online sales can be accessed through eBay. From the eBay home page, select the search tool, then search by seller, using the following seller ID: - USPS-al-pmsc For questions about purchasing any other possible surplus items from the United States Postal Service®, contact your local Post Office™ facility. **eBay® Day** eBay Day is an event run by participating local Post Offices that will help customers learn the ins and outs of using eBay while educating them on how quick, easy, and convenient the Postal Service™ has made shipping. By utilizing Click-N-Ship® and Package Pickup, eBay is now easier than ever. Contact your local Post Office for more information. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/eBay#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title eBay® URL Name eBay [Postage Options](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG0GAM/postage-options)[PC Postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HH5GAM/pc-postage) ## Related Articles - [How to find your tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number) - [How do I Submit a Refund Request for Online Postage?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-submit-a-refund-request-for-online-postage) - [Automated Package Verification Program for Domestic Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [How Can I Buy Insurance Online?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Buy-Insurance-Online) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading eBay®

## How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?

## What to do when mail is undeliverable or misdelivered. Information and additional resources regarding endorsements that may prevent delivery, picking up mail early before it is delivered, postage due and mail sent to an individual at a business address.      - Feb 12, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006175

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**Undeliverable as Addressed Mail and other endorsements**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#undeliverable_as_addressed_mail) | [**How is 'Postage Due' mail handled?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#postage_due_handling) | [**Can I pick up mail before it is delivered?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#pick_up_before_delivery) |
| [**Mail sent to an individual at a business**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#mail_sent_individual_at_business) | [**How is undeliverable USPS Marketing Mail® handled?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#undeliverable_USPS_Marketing_Mail) | [**Reporting / returning misdelivered mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#report_return_misdelivered) |

**Undeliverable as Addressed Mail**

In order to be delivered by the United States Postal Service®, mailpieces must be addressed correctly, the address must be readable, and proper postage must be affixed

- If we are not able to deliver the mail as addressed, we will use the return address on the mailpiece and try to return it to the sender.
  - Refer to **507 Mailer Services \| Postal Explorer ( [usps.com](http://usps.com/)):** [https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#a\_1\_4](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#a_1_4) for a list of USPS endorsements for undeliverable as addressed (UAA) mailer such as "Electronic Service Requested" and "Address Service Requested".

- If the mailpiece is addressed incorrectly and has no return address, the mailpiece will either be handled by the local Post Office™ or sent to the Mail Recovery Center.
  - If your mailpiece has not arrived within 7 days from its date of mailing, you can:
    - Go to[Find Missing Mail](https://www.usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) for additional information/ guidance on next steps.
    - Submit a Missing Mail search request at [MissingMail.USPS.com](https://missingmail.usps.com/ "USPS Missing Mail Application")
    - [File a Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm "File a Claim") online at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) (for insured items meeting the appropriate timeframe requirements).

- If a domestic mailpiece is undeliverable and includes a foreign return address, the mailpiece will be handled in accordance with [current dead mail procedures](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#a_1_9).

Please note that with the filing of a search request, the United States Postal Service has guidelines on items or merchandise that can be returned or recovered. If the item(s) is located and it meets guidelines for recovery, it will be returned to you.

**Why was my mailpiece returned for a missing apartment number?**

Apartment complexes often have hundreds of residents and can experience a high turnover rate for tenants. With this in mind, the Postal Service encourages the use of apartment numbers and requires apartment management to provide a current alphabetical directory if the complex has fifteen (15) or more mail receptacles to ensure accurate delivery. As a matter of policy, mail will be delivered if any one of the following elements is present:

- Apartment number is included on the envelope.
- Recipient's name appears on or inside mail receptacle.
- Recipient's name appears on an alphabetical directory furnished by the apartment management.
- The mailperson recognizes the information and remembers the correct apartment number.

**Where can I find information on Endorsements?**

Ancillary Service Endorsements allow the sender to obtain the addressee's new (forwarding) address (provided the appropriate endorsement is used and if the addressee filed a Change-of-Address Order with the Postal Service) or the reason for non-delivery. Information on the use of **Undeliverable as Addressed** (UAA) endorsements can be found at [Ancillary Service Endorsements](https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality/ancillary-service-endorsements) on [USPS.com®](http://www.usps.com/). For information on the definitions of endorsements that are applied to an undeliverable mailpiece by the letter carrier, go to the [Domestic Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#1218184 "Chart of  USPS Endorsements for Mail Undeliverable as Addressed"). Examples of endorsements include Change Service Requested, Address Service Requested, Return Service Requested and Temp-Return Service Requested. Some endorsements require payment of an address-correction fee (PS Form 3547).

**How is 'Postage Due' mail handled?**

Mail is periodically checked during processing to ensure it has proper postage. If a mailpiece does not have proper postage, it may be returned, held awaiting postage payment([unpaid postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Counterfeit-Postage-and-Unpaid-Postage)), or delivered according to the following scenarios:

- If the item is without postage, or it appears postage fell off; it is endorsed " _Returned for Postage Mail_" and sent to the return address on the mailpiece.

- If the item is shortpaid, it will be delivered postage due and the letter carrier will endorse it to show the amount of the postage due.
  - The recipient must pay in cash for postage due mail before the mail is delivered
  - Depending on location, letter carriers may or may not be able to collect Postage Due directly from a customer or a customer's mailbox. For further information on how to pay for the Postage Due, please contact your local Post Office™ facility.
  - Rural carriers are not required to go to the door with a postage due letter. Policy stipulates that rural carriers go only as far as the mailbox and blow the horn. The recipient must come out to the mailbox to pay and receive the letter.
    - **Note**: If the shortpaid item cannot be delivered as addressed (including if the recipient refuses to pay for and accept it), it is endorsed "Returned for Additional Postage" and sent to the return address on the mailpiece.

A returned shortpaid mailpiece can have the necessary additional postage affixed (leaving the "postage due" message intact) to the original piece and does not have to be placed in a new envelope or wrapper.

**Please Note:** The USPS® makes a special effort when the mail piece has the appearance of a greeting card. Rather than being routed to the Mail Recovery Center because of no postage or below minimum size, greeting cards without a return address will generally be delivered with postage due during the month of December. (This is for domestic First-Class Mail® only. Foreign greeting cards without postage go back to the mailer for proper postage.)

**Note:** Automated Package Verification (APV) streamlines how we check postage on certain **packages with labels generated from Click-N-Ship® and PC Postage applications** ( [PC Postage applications include Click-N-Ship, Endicia, Stamps.com, eBay, PayPal, EasyPost and Pitney Bowes®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-PC-Postag-ePostage)). Please see " [Automated Package Verification Program](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program)" for more information.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#Top)

**Can I pick up mail before it is delivered?**

Unfortunately mail cannot be picked up before it's delivered. The United States Postal Service system is set up to deliver mail, it is not able to retain mail so that you can come in and pick it up.

There are services (which charge additional fees) that would allow you to pickup your mail on a regular basis at the Post Office. These are:

- **Purchase a Post Office Box** \- Mail is considered delivered when it reaches the box.

[How do I apply for a Post Office Box?](https://poboxes.usps.com/poboxonline/search/landingPage.do)
- **Caller services** \- Businesses can have their employees pick up their mail.

[What are Caller and Reserve Box Services?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Caller-and-Reserve-Box-Services)
- Some packages with a tracking number may qualify for Delivery Instructions, see [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) for more information. Letter mail is not eligible for Delivery Instructions.

**Mail sent to an individual at a business**

Mail is delivered to an organization (either governmental or nongovernmental) if it is addressed to:

- The organization itself (i.e. business, government agency, etc...).
- An individual (by name or title) at the address of the organization.
- A former official, employee, agent, etc... at the address of the organization.

Mail that is addressed to a company is delivered to the company, including mail endorsed "Personal and Confidential" for a specific individual. Postal Service™ responsibility for the mail ends at that point and disposition of such mail is in accordance with each company's policy. This also applies to mail addressed in this manner to former employees.

If there are disputes, mail is delivered in accordance with the wishes of the company's president, chief executive officer, or equivalent officer.

**How is undeliverable USPS Marketing Mail® handled?**

_USPS Marketing Mail_ items are not automatically forwarded or returned if they cannot be delivered (including cases where an intended recipient has moved). USPS Marketing Mail items may include catalogs, flyers, circulars, and advertising mail.

Undeliverable USPS Marketing Mail items are handled according to the endorsements on the item (endorsements are placed on the mailpiece in advance by the sender).  If the USPS Marketing Mail item does not have an endorsement, the mailpiece is disposed of by USPS.

For more information, visit the [https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm "USPS.com - Advertise by Mail").

**Reporting / returning misdelivered mail**

Despite our best efforts, occasionally mail is misdelivered, or is delivered to an old location for an individual. If you are receiving mail for the previous resident and do not know their address, simply return the mail piece back to the mailstream (by leaving in a Collection Box or other mail receptacle) with the notation **"Not at this address"** marked on the envelope.

For _Priority Mail Express®_ items only, you can obtain the number of the Priority Mail Express Reporting Unit by calling:

**1-800-ASK-USPS** ( **1-800-275-8777**)

For other misdelivered mail items (such as letters):

- If the mailpiece is delivered to the wrong location:

  - Don't erase or mark over the information, or write any type of endorsement on the mailpiece.
  - Place the item back in the mailbox or hand the item back to your mailperson.

- If the mailpiece is delivered to the correct location but the recipient on the mailpiece does not reside at the address:

  - Write " **Not at this address**" on mailpiece.
  - Don't erase or mark over the address.
  - Provide the mailpiece to your mailperson or drop into a Collection Box receptacle.

Destroying mail that was not intended for you may be prohibited by US laws.

- Willfully destroying mail is an act that may be punishable by the Federal Government.

If you have any questions about the legality of doing this, please contact your local law enforcement.

**Misdelivered Redelivery Notice**

If you receive a ["We ReDeliver for You" PS 3849 form](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) that is for a different address or for someone that does not live at your address (and the correct forwarding address is unknown):

1. Check the "Other" box on the Notice (located on the front of the form, close to the bottom)
2. Write in "Refused"
3. Return the form to your mailbox or hand it to your carrier.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled#Top)

## Outgoing Mail Pickup

## Guidelines for when customers hand outgoing, prepaid mail, to a carrier; often referred to as collection service.      - Oct 23, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007109

Customer Information

Collection Service often refers to prepaid mail deposited in a collection box. However, it is also considered collection service when customers hand their outgoing, prepaid mail to a carrier or other designated employee while that employee is performing normal delivery and collection duties. Letter carriers can pick up mail as they make their deliveries if the flag is up. However, carriers are not required to check mailboxes for outgoing mail that do not have flags up if they do not have deliveries for that address that day.

Outgoing mail may be handed to the letter carrier if:

- Correct postage has been applied to the item
- It falls within the size and weight restrictions of the class.
  - **Note**: Mail items _bearing only postage stamps as payment_ that are over 10 ounces in weight or over 1/2-inch in thickness must be given to an employee at a Post Office™ location. These items cannot be be picked up by a letter carrier or deposited in a collection box.
- The customer does not need a stamped or dated receipt for the item

The pickup amount may be limited by equipment and vehicle limitations. All mail picked up by letter carriers must comply with normal weight, size and mailing restrictions. If a customer has too much mail for the letter carrier to pick it all up, the customer may:

- [Schedule a Pickup](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm) (not available in all markets)
- Drop it off at a local Post Office™ facility

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000012KjAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials) ## What Does USPS® Classify as Hazardous Materials? ## Categorization of hazardous materials and mailabililty of those hazardous classes. - Feb 6, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006986 Customer Information [**Items possibly containing hazardous materials**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#items_possibly_containing_hazardous_materials) [**Items containing mercury or explosives**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#items_containing_mercury) [**Items containing alcohol**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#items_containing_alcohol) [**Items containing perfume**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#items_containing_perfume) You can find information on common hazardous, restricted and perishable materials in the summarized [USPS.com Shipping Restrictions page](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm), and the detailed [Publication 52, _Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_](http://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/welcome.htm) which also contains the [Transportation Requirements in section 327.1](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_008.htm). These guides identify many items by their common names and can help you determine what may be mailed and if special packaging [Packaging Instruction 3A](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52apxc_010.htm#ep999843)) or other restrictions apply. The guide is completely searchable by keyword and links to more detailed information. One of these guides is the " [Hazardous Materials Table: Postal Service Mailability Guide](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52apxa.htm)," based on the online DOT Hazardous Materials Table. The information is modified to identify the mailability of each hazardous material (such as different acids and batteries) based on Postal Service mailing regulations. A guide on making Hazardous Material shipping safer can be found in the [HAZMAT Shipping Safety Guide \| USPS Delivers](https://www.uspsdelivers.com/hazmat-shipping-safety/). **Notes:** - This table identifies the mailability of hazardous materials for domestic mail only. - Almost no hazardous materials are permitted in international mail. - For information on mailability in international mail, see [Chapter 6](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_001.htm) and [Exhibit 621.1](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_003.htm#ep266316). The Hazardous Materials Table also lists the hazard class of a hazardous material. The mailability of hazardous materials by hazard class is further summarized for postal purposes by the " [DOT Hazard Classes and Postal Mailability](http://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_010.htm#ep1018757)" table. If you are a **commercial mailer** and have questions about the mailability of an item, contact the [Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailing-Shipping-Solutions-Center-Hours) for Mailing Requirements. | | | --- | | **If a person knowingly mails items or materials that are dangerous or injurious to life, health, or property, they may face a civil penalty of at least $250, but not more than $100,000 per violation, the costs of any cleanup associated with each violation, and damages. They may also face criminal penalties.** | **Items possibly containing hazardous materials** Hazardous materials include any matter having a clear potential for causing harm to the mail, persons, or property involved in moving the mail. However, many commonly used items contain materials that are considered hazardous by U.S. Postal Service standards. Some fuel-containing items commonly found in the mail are: - Chainsaws - Lawn trimmers and edgers - Small motors and engines - Used fuel tanks (motorcycles, lawn mowers) - Small generators - Camp stoves - Gas lanterns or lamps - Model cars or aircraft **Each of the items listed is potentially mailable but only under one of the following conditions:** - The item has never contained fuel, or - The fuel and all vapors have been completely purged from the item. **Note:** If the item emits a fuel odor or if any residue is present on the outside or inside of the item, then the item is not completely purged. Items containing fuel or fuel vapors are not mailable under any circumstances. Simply removing the fuel from an item does not make the item acceptable for mailing. Fuel tanks containing only vapors can have a greater explosion risk and thus become more hazardous than tanks that are full or partially full of fuel. The fuel must be removed and all vapors must be purged to allow these items to be safely mailed. **Presenting parcels and/or potentially hazardous materials to the Post Office™** - At acceptance, Postal employees will ask, "Does this parcel contain anything liquid, fragile, perishable, or potentially hazardous?" - Postal employees will look out for parcels with items that could potentially contain fuel and advise mailers of the mailing limitations. - When accepting parcels, Postal employees will look for oil or fuel stains on the outside and fuel or petroleum odors. - If a mailpiece has markings denoting a flammable or combustible material, Postal employees will verify the contents with the mailer and with available references that all mailability requirements have been met. - While handling parcels, if Postal employees encounter a fuel odor, find liquid stains, or hear liquid sounds, they will move the parcel to the HAZMAT storage area or notify their supervisor. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#top) **Items containing mercury or explosives** **Can I mail explosives?** **No**, you can't mail explosives. All explosives, including fireworks, live ammunition, or gun powder, are completely prohibited in the mail stream. Common examples of fireworks, which are all prohibited in the mailstream, are smoke bombs, firecrackers, bottle-rockets, roman candles, pyrotechnic wheels, black powder, gender reveal smoke, and smokeless powder. For more information, see _Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Pub 52), [Section 341](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_020.htm), 341.21a | | | --- | | The following videos are available on explosives in the mail on the [USPS TV YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/user/uspstv/custom):
- [Fireworks](https://youtu.be/yych96gTOPg?si=6iwbTuw6HaU4EcTG)
- [Live Ammunition](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=i7NXmJocZuA) | **Can I mail items containing mercury?** **No**, you can't mail metallic mercury. Metallic mercury, which is a liquid at room temperature, and devices containing metallic mercury are always prohibited in the mail stream. This includes antique items such as mercury thermometers, mercury barometers, mercury blood pressure monitors and similar devices. Compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs), which contain small amounts of mercury in vapor form, are mailable domestically, but not internationally. The mercury contained in CFLs and other devices like fluorescent tubes and similar consumer devices generally exists in a vaporized state and will never be visible to the naked eye. | | | --- | | **If a person knowingly mails items or materials that are dangerous or injurious to life, health, or property, which includes metallic mercury, they may face a CIVIL PENALTY of at least $250, but not more than $100,000 per violation; the costs of any cleanup associated with each violation, and damages. They may also face criminal penalties.** | Mercury is capable of causing harmful effects on the nervous, digestive, and immune systems, lungs, and kidneys when exposed to it. Mercury can be adsorbed into the skin or inhaled through the vapor it emits. Therefore, these kinds of spills represent a tremendous safety risk to our employees and customers. If a mercury spill occurred on an aircraft in flight, the effects of such a spill could be catastrophic. It is for these reasons that elemental mercury is prohibited from the mail ( _Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Pub 52) [Section 348.21](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_027.htm#ep899998) and [Appendix A](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52apxa.htm)). | | | --- | | See the 1-minute [video on mailing mercury](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fUArRT747Qs) on the [USPS TV YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/user/uspstv/custom). | Common items containing mercury | | | | --- | --- | | ![Drops of liquid mercury (silver in color) poured from a clear, narrow vial on a black surface](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_liquid_mercury.png) **Liquid mercury:** Often encountered in a wide range of volumes. Reasons for sending include uses for gold reclamation/refinement, preparation of dental amalgams, and element collections. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**
**International[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#international_apo_fpo_dpo)**:
**Domestic Ground**:
**Domestic Air**: | **Prohibited**
**Prohibited**
**Prohibited** | Prohibited as per [_Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Publication 52) Section 348.21e](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_027.htm#ep899998). | | ![A barometer with mercury, a silver liquid, contained in a glass column located in the center of the barometer.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_mercury_barometer.png)**Mercury barometer:** These devices often contain significant amounts of mercury that are not well contained. They have been responsible for several leaks. **Note**: Fully digital versions are mailable without restrictions. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**
