The mercury is silver is in color.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_mercury_thermometer.png)**Mercury thermometer:** These items may contain significant amounts of mercury in breakable containment (glass). **Note**: Modern versions (i.e., digital and those using red liquid, which is alcohol to which red coloring has been added) are mailable without restrictions. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**

The manometer has an upright column of glass that contains liquid mercury.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_mercury_sphygmomanometer.png)**Mercury sphygmomanometer (blood pressure gauge):** These devices may contain significant amounts of mercury in breakable containment (glass). **Note**: Fully digital versions are mailable without restrictions. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**

**Prohibited** | Prohibited as per [_Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Publication 52) Section 348.21e](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_027.htm#ep899998). | | ![Part of an image of a mercury switch. The switch in the image is a small, clear container with a drop of liquid mercury and two metal electrodes nearby. Note that the image was flipped 90 degrees counter clockwise.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_mercury_switch.png)**Mercury switch:** May exist in a small enough size to get below the 100 mg. limit; however, this would be the exception rather than the rule. If you can see the mercury, it is not mailable. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**

A pool of mercury fills the lower portion of the tube.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/hazmat/6.29.23_mercury_relay.png)**Mercury relay:** May exist in a small enough size to get below the 100mg. limit; however, this would be the exception rather than the rule. If you can see the mercury, it is not mailable. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**

If broken, there will be no visible mercury and the vapors will readily disperse. | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailability**
**International[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials#international_apo_fpo_dpo)**:
**Domestic Ground**:
**Domestic Air**: | **Prohibited**
**Mailable**
**Mailable** | Mailable as per [_Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Publication 52) Section 348.22g](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_027.htm#ep900018). See [_Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Publication 52) Packaging Instruction 8C](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52apxc_028.htm#ep1000760) for additional information. | | \*International includes APO/FPO/DPO. | ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **Items containing alcohol** **Can I mail alcohol?** Common examples, including beer, wine, whiskey, vodka, brandy, rum, moonshine, and other liquors, are **all prohibited in the mailstream**. For more information, refer to [_Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Pub 52) Section 42](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c4_005.htm). ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **Items containing perfume** **Can I mail perfume?** Perfume, an ethanol-based flammable liquid, may **only be shipped domestically via surface transportation using USPS Ground Advantage®** unless the mailer has [prior written authorization from Product Classification to ship via domestic air transportation](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_022.htm#ep911758). Single or multiple containers of perfume are permitted in a single mailpiece, but the total volume cannot exceed the allowable quantity of 16 ounces. | | | --- | | **Note**: Shipping perfume to all International and APO/FPO/DPO destinations is prohibited. | **How do I prepare and ship packages containing perfume?** The package must include enough absorbent/cushioning material to prevent containers with perfume from breaking and to absorb any potential leakage. Those primary perfume containers must be packed in securely sealed secondary packaging (e.g., a sealable plastic bag). The primary container(s), secondary packaging and cushioning material must be placed in strong outer packaging (e.g., a cardboard box).The outer package must display the DOT Limited Quantity square on point marking designating surface transportation only. For more information, refer to [_Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail_ (Pub 52) Section 343.3](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_022.htm). ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What Does USPS® Classify as Hazardous Materials? URL Name What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Preparing Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGqGAM/preparing-mail-packages) ## Related Articles - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [What is the USPS Mail Recovery Center?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-USPS-Mail-Recovery-Center) - [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Does Your Mailpiece Require Ground Transportation or Contain Live Animals or Hazardous Materials?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Does-Your-Mailpiece-Require-Ground-Transportation-or-Contain-Live-Animals-or-Hazardous-Materials) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading What Does USPS® Classify as Hazardous Materials?

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000yqbAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) ## Where is my package? Tracking Status Help ## Learn about USPS® package tracking statuses and find out what to do when you believe your package is late, missing, or misdelivered. - Feb 3, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006077 Customer Information USPS® uses many package tracking status scans. Here are the most common statuses, what they mean, and what you can do when you see them. We invite all customers to sign up for [Informed Delivery_®_](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) notifications which offers a digital preview of your mail and allows you to manage your expected packages. Check the latest [tracking status of your package here](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input?_gl=1*oe3cz9*_gcl_au*MTgyMDQ4Mzc3NC4xNjg5ODc1NzE1*_ga*MTkwOTc2MDUxMi4xNjU4MjQ1NTAz*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY5MzQ5NDU5Mi4xMjguMC4xNjkzNDk0NTkyLjAuMC4w). Other sources of information: - [Domestic Claims](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) \- How to file a claim - [Missing Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) \- How to submit a Missing Mail search request - [Delayed Mail and Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) \- Delivery standards for all mail classes and when to contact Customer Service **Click the tracking status that you want more information about** **DELIVERED / DELIVERY ATTEMPTED** **[Delivered](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#DELIVERED)** **[No Access to Delivery Location](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#NO_ACCESS)** **[Insufficient Address](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#INSUFFICIENT_ADDRESS)** **[No Such Number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#NO_SUCH_NUMBER)** **[Reminder to Schedule Redelivery of your Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#REMINDER_TO_SCHEUDLE_REDELIVERY)** **[Redelivery Scheduled](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#REDELIVERY_SCHEDULED)** **[Notice Left (No Authorized Recipient Available](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#NO_AUTHORIZED_RECIPIENT))** **[Notice Left (No Secure Location Available)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#NO_SECURE_LOCATION_AVAILABLE)** **OUT FOR DELIVERY** **[Out for Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#OUT_FOR_DELIVERY)** [**Available for Pickup**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#AVAILABLE_FOR_PICKUP) **[Held at Post Office, At Customer Request](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#HELD_AT_POST_OFFICE_AT_CUSTOMER_REQUEST)** **[Awaiting Delivery Scan](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#AWAITING_DELIVERY_SCAN)** **[Delivery Status Not Updated](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#DELIVERY_STATUS_NOT_UPDATED)** **MOVING THROUGH NETWORK** **[In Transit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#IN_TRANSIT)** **[Arrived at USPS Facility](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#ARRIVE_AT_USPS_FACILITY)** **[Departed USPS Facility](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#PROCESSED_THROUGH_USPS_FACILITY)** **[Forwarded](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#FORWARDED)** **[Forwarded Processed](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#FORWARDED_PROCESSED)** **PREPARING FOR DELIVERY** **[Arrived at Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#ARRIVAL_AT_UNIT)** **ACCEPTANCE / PRESHIPMENT** **[Arrived Shipping Partner Facility, USPS Awaiting Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#ARRIVED_SHIPPING_PARTNER_FACILITY)** **[Departed Shipping Partner Facility, USPS Awaiting Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#DEPARTED_SHIPPING_PARTNER_FACILITY)** **[Picked Up by Shipping Partner, USPS Awaiting Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#PICKED_UP_BY_SHIPPING_PARTNER)** **[On its Way to USPS](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#ON_ITS_WAY_TO_USPS)** **[Pre-Shipment Info Sent to USPS, USPS Awaiting Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#PRESHIPMENT_INFO_SENT)** **[Shipping Label Created, USPS Awaiting Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#SHIPPING_LABEL_CREATED)** **[USPS In Possession of Item](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#USPS_IN_POSSESSION_OF_ITEM)** **[Shipment Received, Package Acceptance Pending](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#SHIPMENT_RECEIVED_ACCEPTANCE_PENDING)** | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Delivered** | | | **What It Means:** The package has been delivered with a recorded date, time and delivery location. If your package shows a delivered status but you can't find it, your carrier may have placed it in a safer location. Please check:
- Garage
- With others in your household
- Mailbox - Packages may come separately from your regular mail; check again later in the day
- Porch
- Areas protected from weather
- Areas out of street view, such as under a mat or near a back entrance
- Exterior doors and any locations where the package could be placed
If you have a multi-unit community mailbox and there is a key in your regular mail compartment, your package is in a parcel locker. Check the key tag for the corresponding locker number and open that locker. If you live in an apartment/condo, contact your building's management office to see if the package was left with them.
**What You Can Do:** If you still can't find your package and it has been more than one postal business day since the 'delivered' scan, visit the [email us](https://emailus.usps.com/s/?_gl=1*17cm4h7*_ga*MTYwNzY1MDU0LjE3Mzc4MTMwNTI.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3Njg0OTk5NDEkbzI2MSRnMSR0MTc2ODQ5OTk0OSRqNTIkbDAkaDA.) page on [usps.com](http://usps.com/) to submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™ for eligible items. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **In Transit** | **Arrived at USPS Facility** | **Departed USPS Facility** | | | **What It Means:** The package is currently being processed or transported to your delivering Post Office™. These types of tracking scans may appear multiple times from the same facility and/or on different days as it is traveling.
**What You Can Do:** The package is on its way and we are working to get it to you as soon as possible. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. Additional tracking information will display when the package arrives at unit and is out for delivery. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Out for Delivery** | | | **What It Means:** The package has been prepared for delivery at your local Post Office™ and will be delivered by a carrier today. Not all packages are delivered by the same carrier who delivers your regular mail and may arrive at a different time or later in the day.
**What You Can Do:** Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. If the package hasn't been delivered within 24 hours of this status, use your tracking number and submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™. | | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Arrived, Departed or Picked Up by Shipping Partner Facility, USPS Awaiting Item** | **On its Way to USPS** | | | **What It Means:**USPS does not have possession of the package. Your package was picked up by a shipping partner or arrived at or departed their facility, and they will be transferring it to USPS for processing and delivery.
**What You Can Do:** Please contact the shipper if you have questions about the status. The package is on its way, but we do not yet have it. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. Additional tracking information will display and a delivery date will be provided when USPS receives the package. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#top) | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Insufficient Address** | **No Such Number** | | | **What It Means:** The package is being returned to the sender. Some part of the delivery address is missing, incomplete, or incorrect and the package can't be delivered.
**What You Can Do:** When the sender receives the returned package, confirm that the delivery address is correct and that it is written completely and legibly on the package or label. The sender will need to reship the item with new postage. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **No Access to Delivery Location** | | | **What It Means:** Your carrier could not get to your mailbox, porch, garage, etc. Common reasons are: a vehicle blocking your mailbox, loose dog or other animal interference, address is in a gated community or locked building we could not get into, public utility work, or emergency responders.
**What You Can Do:**_No action is needed at this time._ We will automatically reattempt delivery the next business day. Check the area for anything that might block delivery access to your home or mailbox. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. If it's been more than 24 hours since the No Access status, use your tracking number and submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Notice Left (No Authorized Recipient Available)** | | | **What It Means:** The package requires a signature and no one was available to sign for it. The carrier left a [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice). There will be no automatic second delivery attempt.
**What You Can Do:** You have three options:
- [Schedule a Redelivery](https://tools.usps.com/redelivery.htm?_gl=1*1r9wzk9*_gcl_au*MTgyMDI2NjQ5Ny4xNjg5ODc0NDg4*_ga*NDM3ODkyOTE4LjE2ODIwNTg0OTI.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY5MDU4NTIwNi4xODEuMC4xNjkwNTg1MjA2LjAuMC4w) for a date when someone will be at home to sign for it; use the package's tracking number or the barcode number on the Redelivery notice. If delivery to a [USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) is available, it will be indicated on the Redelivery notice.
- Pick it up from the Post Office listed on the notice. An [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) can pick it up for you. You must show [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).
- Request the package to be [returned to sender using the Redelivery application](https://redelivery.usps.com/).
If you don't claim the package within the [time limit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery), we will return it to the sender. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Available for Pickup** | | | **What It Means:** The package cannot be delivered. It is available for pickup by you or your [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office) at your local Post Office™ during business hours. **A redelivery cannot be scheduled for this package**. Use [Postal Locator](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) to find your local Post Office.
**What You Can Do:** Pick up your package from your local Post Office™ beginning the **next business day** or request the package to be [returned to sender](https://redelivery.usps.com/). If picking up, you must show [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). If you don't claim the package within the [time limit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery), we will return it to the sender. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#top) | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Shipping Label Created, USPS Awaiting Item** | **Pre-Shipment, USPS Awaiting Item** | | | **What It Means:** A shipping label has been created with a USPS Tracking® number. The package has not yet been given to USPS. When the package is received, it will be processed and transported to your delivering Post Office™ and a delivery date will be provided.
**What You Can Do:** Please contact the shipper with any questions. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Arrived at Post Office** | | | **What It Means:** The package has arrived at your Post Office and is being prepared for delivery. The package should arrive today or the next postal business day.
**What You Can Do:** Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. If there has been no change in tracking status within 24 hours of arriving at your local Post Office™ AND no delivery has occurred, use your tracking number and submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **USPS in Possession of Item** | | | **What It Means:** USPS has received or picked up the package and will begin processing for transport to your delivering Post Office. It is on its way.
**What You Can Do:** _No action is needed at this time._ The package is on its way and we are working to get it to you as soon as possible. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. Additional tracking information will display when the package arrives at unit and is out for delivery. | | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Forwarded** | **Forwarded Processed** | | | **What It Means:** The package is being forwarded to a different address. This is due to a change of address request or because the package is at the wrong Post Office for delivery.
**What You Can Do:** The package is on its way and we are working to get it to you as soon as possible. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#top) | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Redelivery Scheduled** | **Reminder to Schedule Redelivery of your Item** | | | **What It Means:** A redelivery was scheduled, or you have received a reminder to schedule a redelivery before the package is returned to the sender.
**What You Can Do:** Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey.
If you have not yet scheduled a redelivery:
- [Schedule a Redelivery](https://tools.usps.com/redelivery.htm?_gl=1*1r9wzk9*_gcl_au*MTgyMDI2NjQ5Ny4xNjg5ODc0NDg4*_ga*NDM3ODkyOTE4LjE2ODIwNTg0OTI.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY5MDU4NTIwNi4xODEuMC4xNjkwNTg1MjA2LjAuMC4w) using the package's tracking number or the barcode number on the [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice). If delivery to a [USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) is available, it will be indicated on the Redelivery notice.
- Pick it up from the Post Office listed on the notice. An [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) can pick it up for you. You must show [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).
- Request the package to be [returned to sender using the Redelivery application](https://redelivery.usps.com/).
If you don't claim the package within the [time limit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery), we will return it to the sender. | | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Delivery Status Not Updated** | **Awaiting Delivery Scan** | | | **What It Means:** A Delivered status was not recorded by 9pm local time on the date of the Out for Delivery status and we now anticipate delivery of your package on the next business day.
**What You Can Do:** If it has been over 24 hours since this status, use your tracking number and submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Notice Left (No Secure Location Available)** | | | **What It Means:** The carrier did not see a secure place to leave the package and returned it to the Post Office. There will be no automatic second delivery attempt for the package.
**What You Can Do:** You have three options:
- [Schedule a Redelivery](https://tools.usps.com/redelivery.htm?_gl=1*1r9wzk9*_gcl_au*MTgyMDI2NjQ5Ny4xNjg5ODc0NDg4*_ga*NDM3ODkyOTE4LjE2ODIwNTg0OTI.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY5MDU4NTIwNi4xODEuMC4xNjkwNTg1MjA2LjAuMC4w) for a date when someone will be at home to sign for it; use the package's tracking number or the barcode number on the [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice). If delivery to a [USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) is available, it will be indicated on the Redelivery notice.
- Pick it up from the Post Office listed on the notice. An [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) can pick it up for you. You must show [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).
- Request the package to be [returned to sender using the Redelivery application](https://redelivery.usps.com/).
If you don't claim the package within the [time limit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery), we will return it to the sender. | | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Held at Post Office, At Customer Request** | | | **What It Means:** The package is being held at your local Post Office. This is due to a USPS Hold Mail® request, Hold for Pickup request or USPS Delivery Instructions® request. **A redelivery cannot be scheduled for this package.**
**What You Can Do:**
- Packages held as part of a valid [USPS Hold Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) request will be handled according to the instructions in the request (carrier delivery or customer pickup).
- If there is no USPS Hold Mail request for your address, you must pick up your package from your local Post Office™. Use [Postal Locator](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) to find your local Post Office. You must show [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). If you don't claim the package within the [time limit](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery), we will return it to the sender.
If you did not make a request, use your tracking number and submit a [service request](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to your local Post Office™. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#top) | | | --- | | | | | --- | | **Shipment Received, Package Acceptance Pending** | | | **What It Means:** A postal facility has received a container or pallet of multiple packages, but processing has not started. Once the container is opened, each individual package will receive a scan indicating postal acceptance.
**What You Can Do:** The package is on its way and we are working to get this to you as soon as possible. Please sign up to [receive text or email tracking updates](https://www.usps.com/tracking) to follow the package on its journey. Additional tracking information will display when the package arrives at unit and is out for delivery. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Where is my package? ## How do I reset my password or change my username on USPS.com®?

## Features of a usps.com account, resetting passwords and who to contact for additional assistance.      - Jun 16, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006225

Customer Information

**I forgot the username on my [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account. How do I log in?**

Go to the [USPS.com account sign-in page](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/) and select "Forgot your username?". Enter the email address associated with your account. If an account is detected for the submitted email address, you will receive an email with your username.

If you do not remember the email address tied to your account or no longer have access to that email address, you will need to [create a new user account online](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/RegistrationAction_input).

**Note:** You must use an email address that is different than the one tied to your original account.

**Can I change the username tied to my [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account?**

No. Unfortunately, [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) _**usernames cannot be changed**_ once they have been created. If you would like a new username, please [create a new user account online](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/RegistrationAction_input).

**How can I log in to my [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account if I forgot my password or if [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) says my account is disabled?**

If you are unable to log in to [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) or if [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) states that your account is disabled, then additional actions may be needed for you to access your account.

- If you forgot your password, you can [reset your password](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-reset-my-password-or-change-my-username-on-USPS-com#resetpassword).
- If your account is temporarily disabled, you can wait until the end of that time period and attempt logging in to your account again.
- If your account has been disabled, [contact the USPS Help Desk](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-reset-my-password-or-change-my-username-on-USPS-com#contactus) to reset your password and enable your account.

**To reset your password:**

1. Proceed to [USPS.com](http://www.usps.com/) and click "Sign In" located in the top right hand corner of the page.
2. Click the "I forgot my Password" link.
3. Enter your username (case sensitive).
4. Click the "Continue" button.
5. Select how you want to reset your password:
   - By answering previously selected security questions.
   - By having a temporary password sent to your validated mobile phone number (available only if you have opted-in for this method in the profile of your user account
6. If you decide to answer the security questions:
   - Enter the answer to your security questions (answers are _not_ case sensitive). Once correctly answered, a temporary password will be emailed to you.
   - Once you have received your new password, please go to [USPS.com](http://www.usps.com/) and sign in. After you sign in, you will be prompted to create a new password.

     - _**NOTE:** You can copy and paste the temporary password from the email to the Temporary Password field. [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) will ignore the extra space that may appear before and/or after the copied text._
7. If you decided to have your temporary password sent to your mobile phone:
   - A temporary password (Account Recovery Code) will be sent to you through text message. After entering that code into [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), you will be prompted to create a new password.

**If you continue to encounter an error**, please either:

- Email us at [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/s/my-online-account-inquiry) click on " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" and select from the topics the reason for your email. Please include the following information in your email:


  - Subject line: [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) Password Reset
  - Your full name
  - Your full address
  - Your username (or what you believe it to be)
  - Your full address
  - The email address you registered with
- Or call us at 1-800-344-7779

For additional information, see [USPS.com® Account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account).

## Authorizing Someone to Accept Your Redelivery

## Customer can authorize someone else to accept their item.      - Feb 4, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006179

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [**Who can schedule and accept a Redelivery?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#accept_redelivery) | [**What are the Guidelines for Authorized Agents?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#Guidelines) |
| [**Is the Letter Carrier Required to Verify an Authorized Agent?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#Carrier_required) | [**Can a customer have someone else pickup their mail piece?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#who_can_schedule_accetp) |

**Who can schedule and accept a Redelivery?**

The addressee, a responsible member of the addressee's family, the addressee’s agent, or an employee may schedule and accept a Redelivery as long as they can provide all of the necessary Redelivery information.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#top)

**What are the Guidelines for Authorized Agents?**

The term "addressee’s agent" refers to someone authorized to represent the addressee. An addressee agent is usually a friend of the family who’s authorized to pick up a package.

- The item can only be redelivered to the address originally stated on the mailpiece.
- On a Redelivery, the addressee (or an authorized agent) must be present to sign for an Accountable Mailpiece (For example, an item with Signature Confirmation).

Businesses can notify their local Post Office of all agents who are authorized to pickup and sign for Accountable Mail. Other [acceptable forms of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) for these authorized agents are company ID, uniforms with a company name, or a company vehicle. However, without a letter on file, the Post Office will verify identification of any individual it does not recognize.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#top)

**Is the Letter Carrier Required to Verify an Authorized Agent?**

The carrier is not required to verify that the person accepting the package at the home or business is truly the "authorized agent." By being inside the home or business it is assumed they are associated with the person the item is for. However, if an agent comes to the Postal Office™ to pickup an item, with [acceptable forms of identification (including a valid photo ID)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) would be required.

**Can a customer have someone else pickup their mail piece?**

If picking up mail for someone else, the customer needs either:

- to have been named by the recipient on the online Schedule a Redelivery application when selecting the "Customer Pickup" option.
  - Pickup requires the PS Form 3849 to be signed by the recipient and to have the name of the customer picking up the package printed on the back.
  - Customer picking up the package requires [acceptable forms of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) that matches the name printed on the PS Form 3849
- written authorization (and their own [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)).

  - Authorization can be written directly on the delivery notice (on the back of PS Form 3849) or on plain paper (simply a note on paper saying that "a name" has permission to pick up mail for "my name", and then it needs to be signed) from the addressee.
- to have the Redelivery Confirmation email or text message if the addressee selected the “USPS Smart Parcel Locker” Redelivery option. The person picking up the item does not need to be an Authorized Agent. See [USPS® Smart Parcel Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) or more information.

If a person has the same last name and address of another person that mail is addressed to, they cannot pick up accountable mail on the other person's behalf unless a standing order is in place. Or, if a customer is sending someone else to pick up an accountable mail item, they can follow the instructions on the back of the PS Form 3849.

**Note**: If you wish to make long term arrangements designating someone else to pick up your mail on a regular basis, a [Standing Delivery Order](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Standing-Delivery-Order-PS-Form-3801) on file is considered permanent authorization (unless revoked). This authorization also applies to signing for Accountable Mail including Registered Mail®. You can see a copy of the PS Form 3801 Standing Delivery Order at [https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3801.pdf](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3801.pdf)

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery#top)

## How to Apply for USPS Jobs

## How to search and apply for job with USPS, and links to resources.      - Feb 6, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008362

Customer Information

USPS is improving the job application experience to make it easier and more streamlined. Depending on the position you're applying for, you may need to use one of two systems:

- USPS Careers
- eCareer

Please visit [How to apply - Careers - About.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/) at [about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/) for more information on which application system to use, what to expect during the application process, and other helpful tips and guidance on searching and applying for USPS jobs.

**Note:** The two application systems do not share an account / profile. If you have an account in eCareer, you must create a new account in USPS Careers to use that system.

**Navigating the USPS Careers Website**

The USPS Careers website, [How to apply - Careers - About.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/), has three main sections to help you explore job opportunities and understand the application process:

- [Career Opportunities](https://about.usps.com/careers/career-opportunities/ "Career Opportunities") \- Learn about available job types, programs for students and recent graduates, and opportunities for veterans and their families at [about.usps.com/careers/career-opportunities/](https://about.usps.com/careers/career-opportunities/)
- [Working at USPS](https://about.usps.com/careers/working-usps/) \- Find details on employment requirements, pay and benefits, and career development at [about.usps.com/careers/working-usps/](https://about.usps.com/careers/working-usps/)
- [How to Apply](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/) \- Get step-by-step instructions on the application process, exams, and additional help for those using the eCareer system to apply at [about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/).

**Exams**

Not every job requires an exam or assessment. If an exam is required, it will be listed on the job posting under "Examination Requirements". You'll be guided through the exam / assessment during the application process or notified via email if an exam / assessment is required. For a full listing of exams and the jobs that require them, refer to [Postal exams - Careers - About.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/exams.htm#tableotherexams.) at [about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/exams.htm#tableotherexams](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/exams.htm#tableotherexams.)

**Finding your Application Status**

If you applied through USPS Careers:

- Login to USPS Careers ([jobs.usps.com](https://jobs.usps.com/)) with your USPS Careers credentials, then select **Job Applications** to view your application status.

If you applied through eCareer:

- [Login to eCareer here](https://wp1-ext.usps.gov/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/hrrcf_a_startpage_ext_cand?sap-client=010&sap-language=EN&_gl=1*kar80k*_gcl_au*MTQyNTg3MDQ4LjE3NjQwMjA4MDU.*_ga*OTE2NzMxNzA1LjE3MDE4OTQ4MTA.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*czE3NjU5MjE4MTkkbzE1NiRnMSR0MTc2NTkyNDQyMSRqNTYkbDAkaDA.)with your eCareer credentials, then select the **My Applications** link within the **Job Applications** section of your candidate profile to view your application status.
- For additional information on the eCareer system, including helpful videos, please see [Create a profile & apply - Careers - About.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/create-profile-and-apply.htm#create) at [about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/create-profile-and-apply.htm#create](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/create-profile-and-apply.htm#create)

**Lost or Forgotten Username / Password for USPS Careers or eCareer System**

- For USPS Careers ([jobs.usps.com](https://jobs.usps.com/)), use the **Forgot Password** link on [jobs.usps.com/applytohire/Login](https://jobs.usps.com/applytohire/Login)
- For eCareer, use the Forgot Username or Forgot Password links on [about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/create-profile-and-apply.htm#create](https://about.usps.com/careers/how-to-apply/create-profile-and-apply.htm#create)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000VobAAE%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft) ## Mail Theft ## What options are available involving mail theft? - May 2, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007940 Customer Information **Please follow the steps below****if you suspect theft of mail and / or packages.** | | | | --- | --- | | **Report Mail Theft** | | **Packages with Tracking** | **Letter Mail and / or Packages without Tracking** | | To submit a report for theft of a package **with a Tracking Number**, select " [Where is my package?](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry)" to complete the form. | To submit a report for theft of **letter mail or a package without a Tracking Number**, select " [Where is my mail?](https://emailus.usps.com/s/daily-mail-delivery-inquiry)" to complete the form. | You may also visit the U.S. Postal Inspection Service website for [additional tips to protect your mail](https://www.uspis.gov/tips-prevention/mail-theft/). You may report an incident of **Vandalism**\\* to your personal mailbox in the following manner: | | | | --- | --- | | **Personal Mailbox Vandalism** | | **No evidence of Mail Theft** | **Evidence of Mail Theft** | | 1. Notify your local police.
2. Place your mail on [hold](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=HOLDMAIL&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fholdmail.usps.com%2Fholdmail%2F) if needed, until you repair or replace your box.
3. Contact your [Local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) if you need further assistance. | 1. Notify your local police.
2. Place your mail on [hold](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=HOLDMAIL&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fholdmail.usps.com%2Fholdmail%2F) if needed, until you repair or replace your box.
3. Report mail theft by following the instructions under " [Report Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft#reportmailtheft)", above. | \* **IMPORTANT**: If the vandalism or a fire is in progress, please call your local Police Department or 911 to notify them. ## What is the Latest Collection Time at a Post Office™?

## If a mailpiece is dropped off at a 24-hour postal location before the last pickup time listed at that location, it would be postmarked with the date it was dropped off.      - Jan 27, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007492

Customer Information

If a mailpiece is dropped off at a 24-hour postal location before the last pickup time listed at that location, a [postmark](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) with the date it was accepted can be applied manually by an employee upon request.

If a mailpiece is deposited in a Collection Box receptacle before the last pickup time, it will be collected _but not guaranteed a postmark_ that same day.

**Why Hasn't My Mail been Picked Up at the Time Noted on a Collection Box?**

Collections should be made within approximately 30 minutes of the posted collection time. However, occasional transportation issues (severe weather, blocked traffic, etc.) may result in a later collection time. If it is necessary to change the collection time a new collection schedule will be posted on the collection box.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000z57AAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office) ## Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office ## Customer may pickup their item from the local Post Office. - Jan 20, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006184 Customer Information | | | --- | | For detailed information on the "USPS Smart Parcel Locker" Redelivery option, see the article **[USPS® Smart Parcel Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker)**. | | | | | --- | --- | | **[**Where can the customer go to pick up mail that is being held?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#mail_being_held)** | **[**Where do I pickup Accountable Mail?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#accountable_mail)** | | **[**What does the customer need to present if picking up their mail at their local Post Office™?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#What_does_customer_need)** | **[**Can a customer have someone else pickup their held mail?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#someone_else)** | **Where can the customer go to pick up mail that is being held?** Customers may go to their local Post Office pickup location **_based on the information on the back of their_ _[Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)_**. Customers may go to the Post Office to pickup the item on or after the date and time indicated on the PS Form 3849 delivery notice. _**When an item is looked up via the [USPS Tracking® feature](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)**_, the Post Office® information may not be provided immediately after an attempted delivery. The Post Office name, location, and hours of operations will be available when the package is returned back to the Post Office. **Where do I pick Up Accountable Mail?** If you have received a [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) and would like to pick the item up, the pickup location is typically printed on the notice. If it is not, there are two ways to find the location: Online or by phone. | | | | --- | --- | | **Online**
**Look up the tracking number or barcode number with the [USPS Tracking® feature](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)** | The Post Office® information may not be provided in your tracking history immediately after an attempted delivery. The Post Office name, location, and hours of operations will be made available automatically with USPS Tracking when the package is returned back to the Post Office at the end of the delivery day. 
- **To locate the local Post Office where your items are held for pickup:**
1. Use the [PO Locator](https://gcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftools.usps.com%2Flocations%2F%3F_gl%3D1*15l0qaa*_ga*MjQwMTE2MTk4LjE3NjU0NTkxMjY.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3Njg5MDQyNjIkbzE1JGcxJHQxNzY4OTA0Njc1JGo2MCRsMCRoMA..&data=05%7C02%7CJoel.G.Bauer%40usps.gov%7C20d3929f6ed44a640c3708de580ebfc7%7Cf9aa5788eb334a498ad076101910cac3%7C0%7C0%7C639045017567267638%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=nZlYJY8aX4FLlbQOoufon1KV3FIzsh7E%2FM7LR1k7dOU%3D&reserved=0) tool on USPS.com.
2. Click on the **Filter & Sort** option.
3. Click on " **Pickup Services**" in the associated dropdown menu.
4. Select the option “ **Pickup Notice Left Mail**”.   
5. **Enter the full delivery address** above and click " **Search**". | | **By phone**
**Call 1-800-ASK-USPS®** | Customer Service representatives are available as follows:
- Monday thru Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. EST
- Saturday: 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. EST
- Sundays and Holidays: Closed \[ [USPS® Holiday Service Schedule](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Holiday-Service-Schedule)\]
- **Please Note**: Automated information is available 24 hours per day, 7 days a week | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#top) **What does the customer need to present if picking up their mail at their local Post Office™?** You must present [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) if you choose to pick up your mail item at the Post Office™ facility indicated on the notice. You should also bring the PS Form 3849 left by the carrier, if available. Find out what forms of identification are [acceptable at the Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). **Can a customer have someone else pickup their held mail?** - If picking up mail for someone else, the customer needs written authorization (and their own [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)). Authorization can be written directly on the delivery notice (on the back of PS Form 3849) or on plain paper (simply a note on paper saying that "a name" has permission to pick up mail for "my name", and then it needs to be signed) from the addressee. - If a person has the same last name and address of another person that mail is addressed to, they cannot pick up accountable mail on the other person's behalf unless a [standing order](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3801.pdf "Standing Delivery Order") is in place. Or, if a customer is sending someone else to pick up an accountable mail item, they can follow the instructions on the back of the PS Form 3849. - A [Standing Delivery Order](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3801.pdf "Standing Delivery Order") on file is considered permanent authorization (unless revoked). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office URL Name Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Redelivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGMGA2/redelivery) ## Related Articles - [Post Office Boxes Online (POBOL) - General Information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Post-Office-Boxes-Online-POBOL-General-Information) - [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000K6nAAE%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing) ## USPS® In-Person Identity Proofing ## USPS® offers "In-Person Proofing" (IPP) for individuals registering to obtain access to USPS services or services provided by participating federal agencies. IPP allows registrants to securely verify their identities in-person when their identity cannot be verified online. - Feb 6, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008073 Customer Information | | | --- | | **Note:** In-Person Identity Verification Services or In-Person Proofing is a step or process connected to USPS® services like [Change of Address](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics), [USPS Hold Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics), and [Informed Delivery®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics). See their FAQs or USPS pages for specific IPP information. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**How does it work?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing#how_does_it_work) | [**Current Participating Federal Agencies**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing#current_participating_federal_agencies) | [**Acceptable Forms of Identification for In-Person Proofing Process**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing#acceptable_forms_identification_ipp) | USPS® offers In-Person Identity Verification Services, also known as "In-Person Proofing" (IPP) for individuals registering to obtain access to USPS services or services provided by participating federal agencies at over 18,000 Post Offices™ nationwide. In-Person Proofing (IPP) allows you as the registrant to securely verify your identity in-person when your identity cannot be verified online. ## **How does it work?** You as the registrant attempt to create an account online with USPS or a participating federal agency and your identity verification fails. You will be given the option to verify your identity in-person at a USPS retail location. If the in-person option is selected, you will receive an email with an enrollment barcode, a list of the 10 closest participating USPS locations, and instructions on which identification documents to bring with you. Upon arrival at a USPS retail location, a Retail Associate scans your barcode to begin the process. You must present them your original and non-expired forms of identification. The Retail Associate evaluates your identity documents to ensure that you, the Registrant, are the person you claim to be. Our IPP solution leverages both technical tools and manual evaluation (leveraging the expertise of our trained Retail Associates) to determine the legitimacy of the ID documents provided. In addition to validating identity, we will take additional steps to prevent fraudulent activity. Your personal information is stored consistent with the [USPS Privacy Policy](https://about.usps.com/who/legal/privacy-policy/full-privacy-policy.htm). | | | --- | | **Note:**
- No appointment is needed (walk-in appointments are acceptable).
- No fee is collected from the registrant.
- USPS does not have a process to identity proof through trusted referees or people you select to act on your behalf. | You will receive a confirmation email from the participating agency detailing the results of the In-Person Proofing session and the next steps. If you receive an error and cannot complete the In-Person Proofing process, then the Retail Associate can cancel the transaction until you are able to return. **If you have questions, please reference your confirmation email from the participating federal agency.** ## **Current Participating Federal Agencies** - **U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) / Login.gov**: Participating federal agencies use GSA's [Login.gov](http://login.gov/) to protect their users. [Login.gov](http://login.gov/) has partnered with the U.S. Postal Service in a first-of-its-kind agreement to make in-person identity verification available at over 18,000 Post Office locations nationwide. Over 99% of the U.S. population has a participating Post Office within 10 miles of their home. When attempting to sign into a participating agency website, you will be prompted to sign in or create an account with [Login.gov](http://login.gov/) before you can access your profile with the agency. - **Department of Labor (DOL)**: The DOL In-Person Proofing (IPP) serves as an additional means of conducting the identity verification required in administering the Unemployment Insurance benefits by state governments' unemployment agencies. IPP service allows USPS to become the trusted go-to provider for customers who are seeking to use the above government agency services and are unable to verify their identity online. ## **Acceptable Forms of Identification for In-Person Proofing Process** Our In-Person Proofing Services support two levels of Identity Assurance: Basic and Enhanced. The level chosen by USPS or the participating federal agency will determine which acceptable forms of identification are required and will fully be outlined in any enrollment emails. | | | | | | --- | **✔** | | **Passport Card** | **PRIMARY / STRONG** | **✔** | **✔** | | **Alien Registration Card** | **PRIMARY** | **✔** | | | **Certification of Naturalization** | **PRIMARY** | **✔** | | | **Government Identification Card** | **PRIMARY** | **✔** | | | **Recognized Corporate Identification Card / Badge** | **PRIMARY** | **✔** | | | **University Identification Card** | **PRIMARY** | **✔** | | | **Deed of Trust** | **SECONDARY / FAIR** | **✔** | **✔** | | **Home Insurance Policy** | **SECONDARY / FAIR** | **✔** | **✔** | | **Mortgage Statement** | **SECONDARY / FAIR** | **✔** | **✔** | | **Vehicle Insurance Policy** | **SECONDARY / FAIR** | **✔** | **✔** | | 1 **Basic IPP (GSA / Login.gov, Department of Labor)**: Participants are required to bring 1 form of primary identification and potentially 1 secondary form of identification to confirm the applicants name and address.

**Note:** Applicants that cannot provide the acceptable forms of identification required for in-person proofing will be unable to complete the identity verification process. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS® In-Person Identity Proofing URL Name USPS-In-Person-Identity-Proofing [Contacts & Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG8GAM/contacts-help)[Basic USPS Information](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHFGA2/basic-usps-information) ## Related Articles - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [What is the USPS Mail Recovery Center?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-USPS-Mail-Recovery-Center) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## What Forms of Payment are Accepted?

## A list of Products/Services that cannot be paid using a credit card with additional payment options like checks or debit cards.      - Jan 16, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007121

Customer Information

**Forms of Payment on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)**

**Accepted:**

- VISA®
- MasterCard®
- Discover®
- American Express™
- Debit cards are accepted if a PIN is not required
- PayPal™ (limited to Click-N-Ship® and The Postal Store®)
- Apple Pay
- Prepaid Cards (limited, see below for exclusions)

**Not Accepted:**

- Checks, Money Orders, and Traveler's/Cashier's Check
- Virtual MasterCard®
  - The Virtual MasterCard is distributed by PayPal. It does not have a 3-digit verification number as there is no physical card, making this form of payment unsatisfactory
- Gift cards
- Click to Pay (Discontinued in November 2025)
- Prepaid cards for:
  - Premium Forwarding Service (PFS)
  - Change of Address / Movers Guide Online Verification
  - Labels API Transactions
  - Stored / Saved Cards (cannot add or modify a prepaid card as a saved payment method on any USPS application)

**Forms of Payment at Retail**

- U.S. currency and coins (See [Penny Rounding Policy](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted#rounding))
- Checks made payable to _U.S. Postal Service_ or to _Postmaster_(title only) for the exact amount of the transaction. ([see check requirements](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted#checkrequirements))
- Money Orders (postal and non-postal) when presented with valid photo ID.
- Traveler's/Cashier's Check only when at least 50% of the face value is used to purchase Postal Service products or services.
- Credit Cards ([see credit card exclusions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted#ccexclusions))
- Debit Cards
  - Debit cards are accepted for all services, except passport fees paid to the Department of State (debit cards may be used to pay the USPS® processing fee). Cash back allowed in $10 increments up to $50. If a "checking" or "debit" card has a VISA or MasterCard logo on it, then it should be treated like a regular credit card.
- Contactless Payments, such as Apple Pay, Google Pay, etc. (subject to [credit card exclusions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted#ccexclusions))

**Credit Card Exclusions / Cannot be Paid with a Credit Card**

- Collect on Delivery (COD)
- Money Orders
- Passport fees payable to U.S. Department of State (only the Postal Acceptance Fee payable to "Postmaster" can be paid with a credit card)
  - Please note that a separate fee must be paid to the U.S. Department of State with specific methods of payment. Please visit [passport fees and payment methods](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/fees.html "passport fees and payment methods") on the Department of State's website.
- Payment for bulk mailings
- Trust accounts (pre-canceled stamps, permit imprints, periodicals, Business Reply Mail®)
- Credit cards are not acceptable for purchases made by phone, except on the Stamps by Phone™ line
- See [USPS® Credit Card Signature Policy](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Credit-Card-Signature-Policy)

**Check Requirements**

- Personal or Business checks must be made payable to _U.S. Postal Service_ or to _Postmaster_(title only) for the exact amount of purchase.
- For Collect on Delivery (COD) packages:
  - Senders may use checks payable to _U.S. Postal Service_ or to _Postmaster_ to purchase COD services at the time of mailing.
  - Recipients picking up COD packages may use checks made payable to the _sender to_ pay the COD amount due to them.
- The Post Office™ will only accept checks when photo ID is presented. Only the following are acceptable photo IDs:
  - Valid state-issued driver's license
  - State-issued non-driver identification
  - Passport
  - Military ID
  - Other U.S. government-issued identification \[Must have the customer's picture and signature. See [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)\]
  - Checks are not accepted just because the customer is well-known.
- Checks cannot be accepted if they are written for more than the exact amount of purchase.
- Checks cannot be accepted for the purchase of money orders.
- Personal checks are accepted at all postal facilities.
- Business checks are accepted at all postal facilities.

**Penny Rounding Policy**

**Effective 12/12/2025** due to the discontinuation of penny production, change due on all cash transactions will be rounded to the nearest nickel if pennies are unavailable. Pennies will still be accepted.

How rounding works:

- If the total cash purchase amount is less than 5¢, it will be rounded up to 5¢ unless paid with exact change.
- Amounts ending in 1¢, 2¢, 6¢, or 7¢ will be rounded down.
- Amounts ending in 3¢, 4¢, 8¢, or 9¢ will be rounded up.

|     |
| --- |
| Please note: The rounding policy only applies to cash transactions. Credit / debit card and check transactions are not affected. |
