Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000xrJAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) ## Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards ## How to calculate package size and weight, and definition of dimensional weight pricing. - Jan 6, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008134 Customer Information Shape, weight, and size affect the price for large packages. If you have a large, lightweight package, instead of paying the normal postage price, you may be charged the [balloon price](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#balloon_price), [oversized price](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#oversized_price), or [dimensional weight price](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#dimensional_weight) depending on the class of mail and additional factors. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Prices & Size**
- [**Determining Parcel Size & Weight**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#determining_parcel_size_weight)
- [**Dimensional Weight Price**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#dimensional_weight)
- [**Nonmachinable Price**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#nonmachinable_price)
- [**Oversized Price**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#oversized_price) | **Fees**
- [**Dimension Noncompliance Fee**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#dimension_noncompliance_fee)
- [**Nonmachinable Surcharge**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#nonmachinable_surcharge)
- [**Nonstandard Fee (Length Fee and Cubic Fee)**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#length_cubic_fee)
- [**Package Quality Noncompliance Fee**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#impb_noncompliance_fee) | **Charges**
- [**Oversize / Overweight Item Fee**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#overweight_oversize_item_fee) | **Determining Parcel Size & Weight** **Size Definitions** | | | | --- | --- | | - **Length** is always the longest dimension and is measured in inches.
- **Height** is the side that is perpendicular to the length and is measured in inches.
- **Width** is the remaining side that is also perpendicular to the length and is measured in inches.
- **Girth** is the distance around the thickest part of the remaining non-length sides and is measured in inches.

- For rectangular packages, _Girth_ would be _Height_ multiplied by two plus _Width_ multiplied by two.
- For cylindrical packages, _Girth_ would be the circumference of the cylinder.
- **Volume** or **Cubic volume** is determined for a rectangular item by measuring the Length, Height and Width in inches, rounding off each measurement to the nearest whole inch and multiplying the Length by the Height by the Width. If the item is nonrectangular like a tube, then the volume is calculated as just described then that the result is multiplied by an adjustment factor of 0.785.
**NOTE:** When looking up the price for a parcel in the shape of a tube using the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/), first select the "Large Package" shape, click the "Nonrectangular" button, and enter the appropriate dimensions. | ![Image of a shipping tube/cylinder and a shipping box. For the tube, a single arrow representing length runs from the top and bottom of the cylinder while a circular arrow representing girth wraps around its circumference. The shipping box, shaped like a cube, has a single arrow representing length running along the edge of the box from the left corner to the right corner. A set of arrows representing girth runs along the top, bottom, left, and right edge of a single face of the box.](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123Images/domestic-parcels.png) | **Maximum Size** - Parcels going to a domestic location may not measure more than 108 inches in length and girth combined. - USPS Ground Advantage® mailpieces are an exception; they may not measure more than 130 inches in length and girth combined. USPS Ground Advantage items that are between 108 and 130 inches will be charged at the [oversized price](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#oversized_price). - Lower maximum size standards may apply to mail addressed to some APOs, FPOs, and DPOs. - The maximum size of a parcel bound for an international destination varies based on country. This can be determined through the Postage Calculator. - Oversize charge - Any item exceeding the Postal Service maximum size limit of 130 inches in length and girth is nonmailable. Oversize items identified in the postal network will be assessed an [oversize item charge](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#overweight_oversize_item_fee) payable before release of the item, unless the item is discovered at the same facility it was entered. Postage and fees refunds **are not** available. **Package Weight** The U.S. Postal Service™ recommends that you use a scale to determine the weight of your package. Scales can be easily purchased online at [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/home). Please keep in mind that if the package weighs more than the weight you entered, it will require additional postage when it is mailed or delivered. A convenient alternative is Priority Mail Express® Flat Rate Envelopes and Priority Mail Flat Rate® Envelopes and Boxes. For one low price, you can mail any amount of material to a U.S. destination, of any weight up to 70 lbs that will easily fit in the flat rate envelope or box. - **Domestic packages may not weigh more than 70 pounds.** - Lower maximum weight standards may apply to mail addressed to some APOs, FPOs, and DPOs. - The maximum weight of **international packages** varies based on country. This can be determined through the [Postage Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/). - **Overweight charge** \- Any item exceeding the 70-pound weight limit is nonmailable. Overweight items will be assessed an [overweight item charge](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#overweight_oversize_item_fee) payable before release of the item, unless it is discovered at the same facility it was entered. Postage and fees refunds **are not** available. # [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards\#top) **Dimensional Weight Price** The _dimensional weight price_ may be applied to parcels of the mail classes listed below, but use and calculation of that price differs based on those mail classes. - **Dimensional Weight Price for Low-Density Parcels to Zones 1-9** \- For the domestic mail classes listed below, postage for parcels addressed for delivery to [Zones 1-9](https://postalpro.usps.com/operations/zone-charts?_gl=1*1nuyrxx*_ga*MTkxNDgxMDA5Ni4xNjQ4NTYzNDI0*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY0ODU3NDU5MS4zLjEuMTY0ODU3Njk1Mi4w) and that exceed one cubic foot (1,728 cubic inches) is based on either the actual weight or the _[calculated dimensional weight](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/123.htm#a_1_3)_, whichever is greater. The dimensional weight price may apply to: - Priority Mail Express® parcels - Priority Mail® parcels - USPS Ground Advantage® (excludes Limited Overland Routes parcels) parcels - Parcel Select® parcels (Parcel Select no longer has price by Zone, but can still be charged by the dimensional weight) - **Note**: If the dimensional weight exceeds 70 pounds, postage is the 70-pound price | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Nonmachinable Price** A _nonmachinable_ mailpiece is a mailpiece that must be sorted outside of the standard, automated mail process. Because it is more expensive to process these mailpieces, the _nonmachinable price_ is applied to items of the mail classes listed below. | | | | --- | --- | | - USPS Marketing Mail® letters
- USPS Marketing Mail Nonprofit letters | - First-Class Mail® letters (Commercial) | For letters of the above mail classes, the _nonmachinable price_ is applied when the letter has any one of the [criteria listed here](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/201.htm#ep1042571). | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Oversized Price** The _oversized price_ is used on parcels of the mail classes listed below that are greater than 108 inches, but not more than 130 inches, in combined length and girth. Weight is not a factor as long as it does not exceed 70 pounds (no package can exceed 70 pounds). This is for: | | | | --- | --- | | - USPS Ground Advantage® parcels
- USPS Ground Advantage® \- Limited Overland Routes parcels
- Parcel Return Service parcels | - Parcel Select parcels 
- USPS Connect™ Local | | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Length Fee, Cubic Fee and Characteristic Fee (Nonstandard Fee)** The _length fee_ is a nonstandard fee charged when the parcel length is either 22 inches up to 30 inches or more than 30 inches. The _cubic fee_, or _volume fee_, is a nonstandard fee charged when the parcel cubic volume is more than 2 cubic feet (3,456 cubic inches). The _characteristic fee_ is a nonstandard fee charged when the parcel contains any of the [nonstandard characteristics](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/201.htm#a_7_6_2). These can be charged for: | | | | --- | --- | | - Priority Mail Express (Retail and Commercial1)
- Priority Mail (Retail2 and Commercial1, 2)
- USPS Ground Advantage® (Retail3, Limited Overland Routes (LOR)3 and Commercial1, 3) | - Parcel Select Destination Entry3
- USPS Connect Local3 | A single mailpiece can be charged both the length fee and the cubic fee or both the characteristic fee and the cubic fee. A single mailpiece that can be charged both the length fee and the characteristic fee is only charged one fee, whichever one is the higher amount. 1 Except for Return service 2 Except for Flat Rate Packaging 3 Except for Oversized Price Parcels | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Dimension Noncompliance Fee** The _dimension noncompliance fee_ is charged when commercial customers shipping a package do not provide the package's accurate dimensions or extra service code (ESC) on the Shipping Services file manifests or other approved electronic documentation when required. This is applicable to packages that are greater than 22 inches in length or 18 inches in width or 15 inches in height and/or that requires a characteristic ESC. This fee applies to the following mail classes: | | | | --- | --- | | - Priority Mail Express (Commercial)
- Priority Mail (Commercial)
- Parcel Select Destination Entry | - USPS Ground Advantage (Commercial)
- USPS Connect™ Local | | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Nonmachinable Surcharge** A _nonmachinable_ mailpiece is a mailpiece that must be sorted outside of the standard, automated mail process. Because it is more expensive to process these mailpieces, the _nonmachinable surcharge_ is charged. It is applied to First-Class Mail letters (Retail) and First-Class Mail International® letters (Retail) that meet one or more of the nonmachinable characteristics for [domestic](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/101.htm#a_1_2) or [international](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368116) letters. | | | --- | | Use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) to determine the price of a specific item or see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for a comprehensive list of prices. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Package Quality Noncompliance Fee** The _Package Quality Noncompliance Fee_ (formerly the IMpb Noncompliance Fee) is a fee on packages shipped with the commercial mail classes and USPS Return services listed below. Mailers who ship those packages must meet the established thresholds for [Compliance Quality](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/204.htm#ep1105279)., categorized by address quality, manifest quality (shipping services file) and barcode quality. Failure to meet any of the thresholds results in the assessment of the Package Quality Noncompliance Fee. | | | | --- | --- | | - Priority Mail Express® (Commercial)
- Priority Mail® (Commercial)
- Priority Mail PMOD and Cubic (Commercial)
- USPS Ground Advantage® (Commercial)
- USPS Ground Advantage® Cubic (Commercial)
- Parcel Select® (Commercial)
- USPS Connect™ Local (Commercial)
- Packages containing HAZMAT | - Priority Mail Express Return
- Priority Mail Return®
- USPS Ground Advantage Return
- Parcel Return Service (PRS) | | | | --- | | See [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm) for the Package Quality Noncompliance Fee for the appropriate mail class. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) **Oversize / Overweight Item Fee** The Postal Service™ maximum mailpiece weight limit is 70 pounds and the maximum dimension (length plus girth) is 130 inches. Any item exceeding either of those measurements is nonmailable. If found in the postal network, the item either: - Must be secured for pick-up by the mailer or addressee who will be charged the _[oversize / overweight item fee](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1064717)_ of $200. - No fee is charged if the overweight / oversize item is discovered and picked up at the same facility where it was entered. - Or unless authorized, will be considered abandoned and disposed of at the discretion of the Postal Service if not paid for and picked up within 14 calendar days. Postage and fees refunds **are not** available. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards URL Name Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Preparing Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGqGAM/preparing-mail-packages) ## Related Articles - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup) - [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) - [What are the size and material standards for a window envelope?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-size-and-material-standards-for-a-window-envelope) - [PO Box™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? [Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FRequirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6t0000000bmQdAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles) ## Requirements for city delivery mail receptacles ## Installation of mailboxes must meet specific standards, which can be obtained from the local Post Office™ facility or Developers and Builders Guide - Oct 7, 2020 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006171 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**Curbside mailboxes**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#curbside_mailboxes) | [**Door slots**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#door_slots) | | [**Apartment mail receptacles**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#apartment_mail_receptacles) | [**Are businesses required to have mail receptacles or door slots?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#businesses_required_mail_receptacles_door_slot) | | **To establish new mail service or install new boxes:**
**[Developers and Builders Guide](https://about.usps.com/handbooks/po632.pdf)** | | **Curbside mailboxes** **Installation policies** Installation of curbside mailboxes must meet specific construction standards, which can be obtained from the local Post Office™ facility. For United States Postal Service® STD-7, Mailboxes, City and Rural Curbside, you may write to: MERRIFIELD VA 22082-8101 8403 LEE HWY DELIVERY & CUSTOMER SVCS EQUIPMENT USPOSTAL SERVICE - ENGINEERING The following are a few of the regulations that apply when installing a curbside mailbox: - **Important**: Before installing, moving or replacing your mailbox or mailbox support, you will need to contact your Postmaster or mailperson from your local Post Office™. - All mailboxes must be approved by the Postal Service™. - Custom made mailboxes will be approved by the Postmaster if they meet established standards. - Name put on box should be at least one inch high. - Generally, the boxes should be installed with the bottom of the box at a vertical height of between 41-45 inches from the road surface. - A mailbox with a lock must have a slot large enough to accommodate customer's daily mail volume. - Advertising on a mailbox or the supporting post of the mailbox is prohibited. A receptacle for newspaper delivery by private companies may be attached to the post of a curbside mailbox used by the Postal Service if the receptacle: 1. Does not touch the mailbox or use any part of the mailbox for support. 2. Does not interfere with the delivery of mail, obstruct the view of the mailbox flag, or present a hazard to mailperson or vehicle. 3. Does not extend beyond the front of the mailbox when the box door is closed. 4. Does not display advertising, except the publication title. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#top) **Delivery notes** If you have a curbside mailbox or a mailbox on the outside of your house, USPS regulations govern what can and cannot be placed in them. Generally speaking, only mail that has been sent through the USPS® may be placed in these types of receptacles. Conversely, USPS regulations do not govern what can be placed in a mail slot on your door. This means that if a local business wants to put a flyer in the mail slot, they can do so. **Address identification** Every curbside mailbox must bear the following address information: - A box number, if used, inscribed in contrasting color in neat letters and numerals at least 1 inch high on the side of the box visible to the carrier’s regular approach or on the door if boxes are grouped. - A house number if street names and house numbers have been assigned by local authorities and the postmaster authorizes their use as a postal address. - If the box is on a different street from the customer’s residence, the street name and house number must be inscribed on the box. The mailbox may bear the owner’s name. **Clear approach** - Customers must keep the approach to their mailboxes clear of obstructions to allow safe access for delivery. If USPS employees are impeded in reaching a mail receptacle, the postmaster may withdraw delivery service. **Purchasing a mailbox** The United States Postal Service DOES NOT sell mailboxes. You may be able to purchase approved mailboxes at local department stores, on the internet, or at other locations. Mailbox manufacturers must meet all applicable Postal Service mailbox standards. **Door slots** An alternate to curbside mailboxes is mail delivery using a Door Slot on the front door of a house. The following construction standards apply for a Door Slot: 1. The clear rectangular opening in the outside slot plate must be at least 1-1/2 inches wide and 7 inches in length. 2. The slot must have a flap, hinged at the top if placed horizontally, or hinged on the side away from the hinge side of the door if placed vertically. 3. When an inside hood is used to provide greater privacy, the hooded portion must not be below the bottom line of the slot in the outside plate if placed horizontally, or beyond the side line of the slot in the outside plate nearest the hinge edge of the door if placed vertically. 4. The hood at its greatest projection must not be less than 2-1/16 inches beyond the inside face of the door. 5. The bottom of the slot must be at least 30 inches above the finished floor line. **Apartment mail receptacles** Apartment house mail receptacles must be approved by the USPS. When apartment buildings are substantially renovated or remodeled to provide additional apartments, or a material change is made in the location of boxes, obsolete receptacles must be replaced by currently approved receptacles. **Are businesses required to have mail receptacles or door slots?** Mail receptacles or door slots are not required at businesses and offices that are open and have someone on hand to receive the mail when the carrier calls. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Requirements for city delivery mail receptacles URL Name Requirements-for-City-Delivery-Mail-Receptacles [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Mailboxes](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGLGA2/mailboxes) ## Related Articles - [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) - [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [No mail delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## USPS Label Delivery Service

## USPS is offering USPS Label Delivery, which delivers return/outbound labels to residential and business addresses for a fee charged per label.      - Jan 17, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008071

Customer Information

Use the new Click-N-Ship® experience to have labels delivered directly to the door of residential and business customers. USPS Label Delivery benefits consumers who have limited options to print or obtain a return or outbound label. Labels are available for Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage®, and Parcel Select®. Visit [https://cnsb.usps.com](https://cnsb.usps.com/) to get started.

|     |
| --- |
| For instructions on using USPS Label Delivery Service and its fee, see the [appropriate section on the page _Label Broker & Label Delivery Service \| USPS_](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm#labeldelivery). |

**Request a Refund**

If your USPS Label Delivery labels were delivered but not used or not delivered, you can request a refund through your [Click-N-Ship® Shipping History](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics). The option to request a refund is available for 30 days after label purchase.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| If the labels were NOT delivered | You are refunded both the label delivery fee and the label postage |
| If the labels were delivered and the customer decided not to use them | You are refunded the label postage, but not the label delivery fee |

## First Class Package International Service® (FCPIS)

## First-Class Package International Service (FCPIS) are small parcels under 4 lbs. and up to $400 in value.      - Dec 31, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007512

Customer Information

First-Class Package International Service® (FCPIS®) is USPS®'s most affordable international service for small packages. You can send anything up to 4 lbs to more than 180 countries, but you cannot send items that exceed $400 in value. Delivery time, which is not guaranteed, varies by destination.

**[https://www.usps.com/international/mail-shipping-services.htm](http://www.usps.com/international/mail-shipping-services.htm)**

At the sender's option, extra services may be added on a country-specific basis.

**General Information**

**Packages (small packets) must be within the following dimensions:**

- Minimum length: 6 inches
- Minimum height: 4 inches
- Maximum length: 24 inches. Length is the longest dimension
- Maximum length, height, and depth (thickness) combined: 36 inches
- The weight limit is 4 pounds.

**Rolls must be within the following dimensions:**

- Minimum length: 8¼ inches
- Minimum length plus twice the diameter combined: 12 inches
- Maximum length: 36 inches
- Maximum length plus twice the diameter combined: 42 inches
- The weight limit is 4 pounds

**What are Some Additional Requirements for First-Class Package International Service®?**

First-Class Package International Service packages (small packets) and rolls, which because of their size, shape, or configuration might be mistaken for another category of international mail, should be marked "LETTER-POST" on the address side of the mailpiece.

- Requires PS Form 2976, _Customs Declaration CN 22_— _Sender's Declaration._

**What Extra Services are Available for First-Class Package International Service?**

- Certificate of Mailing (unregistered mail items only).
- Package Pickup
- Pickup on Demand
- No insurance available

**How is the Price of First-Class Package International Service® Packages Determined?**

First-Class Package International Service® postage prices vary depending on the size/weight of the mailpiece and the destination country. In order to calculate the price of a Package, go to the [International Postage Price Calculator](http://ircalc.usps.gov/). For additional information on pricing, go to [International Mailpiece Quick Reference Chart](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mailpiece-Quick-Reference-Chart).

**What Payment Methods are Eligible for Postage of First-Class Package International Service® Items?**

First-Class Package International Service items may be paid with:

- Postage stamps
- Postage validation imprinter (PVI) labels
- Postage meter stamps
- Information-based indicia (IBI)
- Click-N-Ship®
- PC Postage service
- Permit imprint

**Items Eligible for Deposit or Pickup**

First-Class Package International Service items bearing a computer-generated customs form with customs data that has been electronically transmitted (e.g., using Click-N-Ship for Business service, an authorized PC Postage vendor, or the USPS Web Tools system) may be deposited through any of the following methods, provided postage is paid by a means other than the use of postage stamps:

- In a private mailbox bearing a return address that matches the address at the point of pick up, when the customer or business is known to reside or do business at that location
- Using Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand
- At a Postal Service retail counter
- Into a Postal Service lobby drop
- In a collection box
- At a Contract Postal Unit (CPU)
- At a USPS-Approved Shipper location

**Items Not Eligible for Deposit or Pickup**

Customers must present the following First-Class Package International Service items requiring a customs form to an employee at a Post Office retail service counter. The Postal Service will return improperly presented items to the sender for proper entry and acceptance.

- Any item bearing a customs form and paid with only postage stamps
- Any item bearing a handwritten customs form
- Any item bearing a customs form that was not computer-generated
- Any item for which the customs data was not electronically transmitted

The Basics ## Basic Information concerning Postage Stamps - Sep 16, 2022 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007122 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[How can I buy stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#howcanibuystamps)** | **[What are postage stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#whatarepostagestamps)** | **[What are Forever® stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#foreverstamps)** | | **[What are Global Forever® stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#globalforeverstamps)** | **[What are semipostal stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#semipostalstamps)** | **[What are NetStamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#netstamps)** | | **[What is the Stamps to Go® program](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#stampstogo)** | **[What should I know about U.S. postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#aboutpstalstamps)** | **[What are Duck Stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Duck-Stamps)** | **How Can I Buy Stamps?** | | | | --- | --- | | **In Person at a USPS® Location** | Visit [Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) for Post Office® locations or approved Postal providers | | **The Postal Store® Online** | Visit [The Postal Store](https://store.usps.com/store/home) [®](https://store.usps.com/store/) to order stamps or other USPS products online. | | **Stamps By Phone™** | Please call 1-844-737-7826 for questions about stamps or orders placed online through [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/), or to place an order for stamps by phone. 
**Hours of Operation:**
- Monday - Friday 7 AM - 3:30 PM CT
- Closed Saturdays, Sundays and [Postal Holidays](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/events/) | | **Stamps By Mail®** | - Stamps By Mail® allows you to order stamps through the mail using PS Form 3227-A. Stamp orders are fulfilled by your local Post Office and delivered by your letter carrier. PS Forms 3227-A are available at your local Post Office® location
- Stamps will be delivered 2-3 days after the order has been received.
- Payment: PS Form 3227-A and payment (check or money order made payable to "USPS” or “United States Postal Service") must be sent to the address on the form envelope prior to delivery. | | **Stamps By Fax** | - Although the Stamps by Fax program is not currently available at all postal facilities, no additional fee is charged for delivery of the stamps
- Contact your local Post Office location to inquire if the Stamps by Fax option is available
- Stamps will be delivered to you a few days after the order has been received. Please note that Stamps by Fax orders may take additional processing time during the month of December
- Payment:
- Payment methods for Stamps by Fax are the same as those of any Post Office location
- The stamps will not be sent until payment is received. Credit cards are normally the fastest method of payment | [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What are Postage Stamps?** A small adhesive piece of paper of specified value issued by a postal authority to be affixed to a letter or a parcel to indicate the amount of postage paid. Postage stamps are the most commonly used stamps and have a fixed amount of postage noted on them. Also, postage stamps are sold in a number of different forms. The most common ways to purchase postage stamps include: - Booklets - Sheets - Coils - Self-service kiosk stamp “Sheetlets” The following is a list of the most common types of postage stamps: | | | | --- | --- | | First Class Mail® **One-Ounce Price Forever Stamp**
The Forever Stamp will always be equal in value to the current First-Class Mail® one-ounce price. | ![The U.S. Flag 2019 stamp features a photograph of a U.S. flag, one of several on the flagpoles near the end of Chicago’s Navy Pier, waving in a May breeze. Under the photograph is the text "FOREVER / USA" with "FOREVER" crossed out.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2018/pb22509/html/images/info_010_1.jpg) | | First Class Mail® **Additional Ounce** Forever Stamps
The words "Additional Ounce" on this stamp indicates its usage value. Like a Forever® Stamp, these stamps will always be valid for the rate printed on it. | ![The Uncle Sam’s Hat additional ounce rate stamp features eight graphic top hats in Uncle Sam’s signature style, with red and white vertical stripes above a blue band with a white star and a gray brim. Beneath each hat is an oval shape representing a face, each in a different shade, meant to suggest the ethnic and racial diversity of the United States. Under the images are the text "2017 ADDITIONAL OUNCE USA" with "ADDITIONAL OUNCE" crossed out.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2017/pb22459/html/images/info2_006_1.jpg) | | First-Class Mail® **Presorted Stamps**
First-Class Mail® Presorted Stamps are available for customers holding a Mailing Permit and Business Mail Entry (BME) induction. Presort Stamps are available for Marketing Mail and First-Class Mail. | ![The nondenominated Presorted First-Class Mail Star Quilts stamps, in two designs, are a detail from a photograph of one of the two quilts, highlighting the intricate work involved in creating the Lone Star design. Both quilts feature colorful eight-point stars. The text "USA PRESORTED FIRST-CLASS 2016" is at the bottom of each stamp, with "PRESORTED FIRST-CLASS" crossed out.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2016/pb22443/html/images/info_005_1.jpg) | | **Postcard Stamps**
The word "Postcard" on the stamp indicates their usage value. Like a Forever® Stamp, these stamps will always be valid for the rate printed on it. | ![The Coral Reefs postcards stamps are in four designs. Four new postcard stamps celebrate the beauty and wonder of coral reefs. Each stamp depicts a type of stony coral, along with associated reef fish, in a highly stylized manner: Elkhorn coral, shown with two French angelfish; Brain coral, with a spotted moray eel; Staghorn coral, with bluestriped grunts; and Pillar coral, with a coney grouper and neon gobies. Each stamp has the name of their respective coral and the text "POSTCARD USA 2019" with "POSTCARD" crossed out.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2019/pb22514/html/images/info_005_1.jpg) | | **Denomination / Definitive Stamps**
Stamps that are valued at amounts varying from 1-cent to Priority Mail Express, with the value printed on the face of the stamp. 1-cent and 3-cent stamps are commonly used to add postage to older stamps when the value of the stamp is no longer sufficient to meet current postage rates. | | **Priority Mail Express® Stamps** | ![The $26.35 Grand Island Ice Caves Priority Mail Express® stamp features a colorful illustration of how one of these ever-changing ice caves might appear from the inside looking out toward the west at sunset.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2019/pb22535/html/images/info_009_1.jpg) | | **Priority Mail® Stamps** | ![The $7.75 Big Bend Priority Mail® stamp in one design depicts the Rio Grande flowing between the sheer limestone cliffs of Santa Elena Canyon in Big Bend National Park.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2019/pb22535/html/images/info_008_1.jpg) | | **Non-Denomination Stamps**
Stamps that do not have a visible face value. | | **Stamped envelopes and cards** | ![The Azulillo stamped card Forever® priced at 38 cents (34-cent postage + 4-cent surcharge) features an illustration of a Chilean blue crocus (Tecophilaea cyanocrocus) from pre-existing artwork by famed illustrator and designer Dugald Stermer (1936-2011). His penciled calligraphy under the flower indicates one of its common names, azulillo, which loosely translated from Spanish means “little blue thing,” with its botanical name above.](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2017/pb22471/html/images/info_005_1.jpg) | [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What are Forever® Stamps?** The Forever® Stamp, [as referenced above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#forever), is a special non-denominated stamp that was issued to save customers time and money, especially when First-Class Mail® prices change. The Forever Stamp always represents the current price of a one (1) ounce First-Class Mail postage. If you purchase a single Forever Stamp today, it will cost you the [same price as a First-Class Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail) ® [stamp](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail). All commemorative stamps, which celebrate distinguished artists, athletes, historic milestones, natural wonders, and more, are issued as Forever Stamps. The Postal Store has a [wide selection of Forever Stamps](https://store.usps.com/store/browse/subcategory.jsp?categoryId=forever-stamps&categoryNavIds=buy-stamps%3Astamps-by-type%3Aforever-stamps "USPS Postal Store - Forever Stamps") available for purchase. [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What are Global Forever® Stamps?** | | | | --- | --- | | A Global Forever stamp’s value is linked to the First-Class Mail International single piece 1-ounce letter price that is in effect on the day of mailing regardless of when the stamp was purchased and regardless of how prices may change in the future. Global Forever stamps can be used to mail a postcard or 1-ounce letter-size mailpiece anywhere in the world.  In addition, mailers may use a Global Forever stamp as postage for any type of single-piece international or domestic mailpiece.
- **Note:** The Global Forever stamp does not cover the [nonmachinable letter surcharge](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368116).
Global Forever stamps are round and always bear the words “Global Forever”.
The Global Forever Stamp cover up to 2 ounce letters when used to send to Canada. All other countries require Additional Ounce postage to a max of 3.5 ounces. 
- **Note:**U.S. postage cannot be used in another country. You must use the proper postage based on the country where the item is being mailed from. | ![Global Forever Stamp image](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2018/pb22499/html/images/info_003_1.jpg) | [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What are semipostal stamps?** | | | | --- | --- | | The Semipostal Stamps Program are Fundraising Stamps that are congressionally mandated for research of causes that are considered to be “in the national public interest and appropriate”. The stamp helps provide funding for a particular cause, such as Breast Cancer, Alzheimer’s, or vanishing species.
Semipostal stamps are [priced](http://edd/pls/posweb/ProdItem.SemiPostalCalc) at a minimum of 15% above postage (known as the differential), with no maximum. Prices are adjusted in increments of five cents. The price of a semipostal stamp pays for the First-Class™ single-piece postage rate in effect at the time of purchase plus a [tax-deductible](http://edd/docs/IRS%20Letter.pdf "Click for IRS letter") amount to fund causes that the Postal Service determines to be appropriate and of national public interest. By law, revenue from sales (minus postage and the reasonable costs of the Postal Service) is to be transferred to a selected executive agency or agencies.
For more information on the [Semipostal Stamp Program](http://about.usps.com/corporate-social-responsibility/semipostals.htm "Click for USPS Fundraising Stamps page"), click that link or see the [Domestic Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1167030 "Click for Domestic Mail Manual") or the [Code of Federal Regulations, Title 39, Part 551](http://www.gpo.gov/fdsys/pkg/CFR-2012-title39-vol1/pdf/CFR-2012-title39-vol1-part551.pdf "Click for Code of Federal Regulations, Title 39, Part 551"). | ![User-added image](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2019/pb22533/html/images/info_006_1.jpg) | [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What are NetStamps?** NetStamps are used like regular stamps and never expire. NetStamps are Denomination stamps with the value printed on the face of the stamps. For additional information, visit the [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/) website at [https://store.stamps.com](https://store.stamps.com/). [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What is the Stamps to Go® Program?** Stamps to Go® is an on-line ordering system for companies that sell stamps at cashier checkout counters, bank teller stations, department stores, pharmacies, grocery stores, catalog companies and more. For information on Stamps to Go, please visit [www.uspsstampstogo.com](http://www.uspsstampstogo.com/), or call **1-800-667-5410** to get started. [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) **What should I know about U.S. Postal Stamps?** - **Do U.S. Postal stamps expire?** No, U.S. postage never expires; you can use existing stamps indefinitely. All postage stamps issued by the United States since 1860 are valid for postage from any point in the United States or from any other place where U.S. Mail service operates. You may use any Denomination stamp(s) to equal the total postage affixed. Total postage affixed must equal at least the postage charge for the class of the mail. - **Can I exchange my older stamps for newly priced stamps after a price increase?** The USPS does not exchange stamps solely because of new postage prices. As mentioned above, postal stamps do not expire and can be used indefinitely. In the event of a price increase, customers can purchase Denomination Stamps to make up the price for postage or use Forever Stamps. - **Can I purchase 1-cent or 3-cent stamps to makeup the increase in postage?** Yes. You can use Denomination stamps of that value. See “ [How Can I Buy Stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#howcanibuystamps)”. - **How much of a stamp can be torn or damaged and still be considered valid?** Stamps which are mutilated or defaced are not valid for U.S domestic or U.S. originated international mail. However, a minor tear at the corner of a stamp does not render a stamp invalid. This standard needs to be applied in a common sense fashion allowing for the possibility of minor tears in stamps when they are removed from their host sheet or booklet. However, if more than a minor portion of a stamp has been torn and is missing, the stamp should be exchanged for another stamp of equal value provided the remaining portion of the stamp is in substantially whole condition and its denomination is evident. If less than 50% of a stamp is presented for exchange, postal officials may use their discretion in considering the exchange. - **Is it possible to reuse stamps?** No, a used stamp cannot be reused. If the stamp was already used to send mail through the USPS®, it cannot be used to mail a different mailpiece even if the stamp was not canceled, or defaced by the Postal Service™ to make a stamp unusable. Stamps that have not yet been used, but have been attached to an envelope may still be used. However, you may not cut an unused stamp from an envelope and use tape to apply to a new envelope. The tape will prevent the stamp from being canceled. A returned domestic or international shortpaid mailpiece can have the necessary additional postage affixed to the original piece and does not have to be placed in a new envelope or wrapper. A returned undeliverable as addressed or refused by the addressee mailpiece may not be re-mailed unless it is placed in a new envelope or wrapper with the correct address and new postage. - **Can stamps be sold at a higher price?** The Postal Service™ sells stamps at face value to everyone. We have no control over the pricing policies of private entrepreneurs, companies or agencies who resell our products. Customers may avoid paying more than the stated value for First-Class Mail® postage by purchasing stamps at a local Post Office® location, through Stamps By Mail®, phone, online, self-service kiosks and retail outlets that are involved with our consignment programs or from one of the many postal stores. - **How can I find the value of an old stamp?** We recommend contacting a Stamp Dealer in your area to discuss the value of any stamp. The USPS only recognizes stamps at face value, no matter how old or valuable they are. For example, the USPS printed a 24-cent stamp featuring an ancient World War I fighting plane - a JN-4, or "Jenny." It was misprinted with the plane upside down. These stamps are worth thousands of dollars to collectors; however the USPS only recognizes the Jenny at face value - 24 cents. - **What should I do if I find defective stamps?** The United States Postal Service wants to ensure quality and consistency with all its products. We ask that if you have defective stamp of any kind you report these to us. Samples of reported defective stamps, along with envelope samples, if available, should be sent to: - STAMP ACQUISITION, STAMP SERVICES ATTENTION: QUALITY ASSURANCE 475 L'ENFANT PLAZA SW WASHINGTON DC 20260-2436 [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Postage Stamps - The Basics URL Name Postage-Stamps-The-Basics [Postal Store](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFzGAM/postal-store)[Postage & Stamps](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HH0GAM/postage-stamps) ## Related Articles - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Mailboxes - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailbox-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Postage Stamps - The Basics

## How can I restrict mail delivery?

## List of different combinations of Restricted Delivery and other extra services along with methods to purchase Restricted Delivery service for your mailpiece.      - Nov 13, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006245

Customer Information

Restricted Delivery service is no longer available as a separate service. However, the mailer can still direct delivery of mail only to the addressee or the addressee's authorized agent through some combined services.

**Restricted Delivery Extra Services**

The following Extra Services combinations can restrict delivery to a specific individual and provide other services.

- Certified Mail Restricted Delivery

- Certified Mail Adult Signature Restricted Delivery

- [Collect on Delivery (COD) Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available)

- [Insurance (over $500.00) Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance)

- [Registered Mail® Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail)

- [Signature Confirmation™ Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) (expanded from online only, to include retail and commercial channels.)

- [Adult Signature Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery)


**International Restricted Delivery**

Restricted delivery service is no longer available — it was discontinued effective January 27, 2013.

**How Can I Obtain Restricted Delivery Service?**

At the time of mailing, you can:

- Request Restricted Delivery service at the time of mailing by asking the USPS clerk or by marking the mail "Restricted Delivery."
- A firm mailer must enter the proper fee in the correct column of the firm sheet and place the required endorsement on the mail.
- The correct block on the appropriate Extra Service form (for example, [PS Form 153, _Signature Confirmation_,](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps153.pdf) for Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery) must be checked to show that restricted delivery is also required.

After mailing, you can request Restricted Delivery by:

- Notifying the mailing Post Office in writing.
- You must identify the article, including item number and addressee, and pay the service fee and communication costs required to affect restricted delivery.

USPS® failure to provide the service because delivery was made before the delivery office received the request is not grounds for a refund of the fee or communication costs.

## How to Report New Construction and Street Address Information to USPS

## New construction street addresses are created by the local governments and reported to USPS for inclusion in delivery routes.      - Oct 29, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006804

Customer Information

**The Postal Service does not assign addresses or designate an address type as Residential, Business or Commercial. Land use and zoning information must be found through your local government.**

Street addresses are created by local government entities, such as **:**

1. City
2. County
3. Town
4. Township
5. Borough
6. Village
7. Parrish

Government-created street addresses are formatted per USPS guidelines and are comprised of the recipient's name, a street address or PO Box number (including apartment or suite number if applicable), city, state, and ZIP Code™. For more information, see [ZIP Code™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/ZIP-Code-The-Basics).

Once the address has been assigned by the local government, they are responsible for reporting it to USPS Address Management for future inclusion in delivery routes.

**To verify your address has been successfully reported to USPS**, allow 5-7 business days after the local government has assigned the address then check the [USPS.com ZIP Code Lookup Tool](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm). If your address has been added to the USPS database, you will see a match and your assigned 9-digit ZIP Code.

If your address is not found, you have two options to report your address directly to USPS.

- Report the new construction address directly to your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm).
- Complete the [online Growth Management Assistance Request form](https://prodpx-promotool.usps.com/promoweb/campaignView.do?campaignId=fy24deliverygrowth).

If you are moving to an address that has been built in the past 6 months, see [Change of Address - The Basics > Requesting a COA to a New Construction](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) for additional information.

|     |
| --- |
| Please note that companies and businesses that use USPS for their address database may need additional time to update their own systems after the Postal Service completes address verification. |

**Developers and Builders**

Are you a developer or building looking to establish new mail delivery? Please visit[Delivery Growth Management - What we do - About.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/what/business-services/delivery-growth-management/welcome.htm)and complete the[online Growth Management Assistance Request form](https://prodpx-promotool.usps.com/promoweb/campaignView.do?campaignId=fy24deliverygrowth)to get in contact with a USPS Growth Coordinator.

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Adult-Signature-Required-and-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery-Services# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Adult-Signature-Required-and-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery-Services?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FAdult-Signature-Required-and-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery-Services) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000wVRAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Adult-Signature-Required-and-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery-Services) ## Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Services ## Description of Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery services, including how each service is used, basic features and restrictions, and other general information. - Dec 8, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008355 Customer Information | | | --- | | **Adult Signature Required & Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Services** | The United States Postal Service offers several options for obtaining a signature upon delivery. Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery are available for purchase as [extra services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) on most mail types to ensure delivered items are signed for by eligible adults, 21 years of age and above. Adult Signature Required Vs. Adult Signature Restricted Delivery **What is Adult Signature Required?** - _**Adult Signature Required**_ is a service that ensures that the recipient of the mail items is at least 21 years of age or above. The recipient or a resident of the recipient's address is required to show a form of photo identification to the delivery employee for age confirmation. This ensures that the sender of the mailpiece did not give the item to an underage person. **What is Adult Signature Restricted Delivery?** - _**Adult Signature Restricted Delivery**_ ensures delivery of a package addressed to an adult 21 years of age or older. This service **requires the signature of the _addressee only_**, who must be 21 years of age or older. - **Note:**Prior to delivery, the recipient is required to show a government issued photo identification for proof of age and, in some cases, identity. Adult Signature Required & Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Features and Restrictions What Does an Adult Signature Label Look Like? - Customers will be provided the signature and name of the recipient, along with the date, time and location of the delivery. **Note:** - No record of an Adult Signature Required and/or Adult Signature Restricted Delivery service item is maintained at the office of mailing. | | | | --- | --- | | **Image of Adult Signature Required Label** | **Image of Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Label** | | ![Adult Signature required label. White rectangle label with a "P" in the upper left hand corner. The section labeled "Postage Area" is to the right of it. Below the "P" and "Postage Area" is written "USPS Priority Mail®". The section below has the return address (JOHN DOE; 1111 UNIVERSAL WAY; FAIRFAX VA 22030-1234) in the upper left hand corner and the delivery address (JOHN DOE; 3333 Main Street; Fairfax VA 22030-1234) in the lower, middle part of that section. The last section below has "USPS ADULT SIGNATURE" written in. A red circle is used to emphasized that wording. Below is a barcode and the barcode number.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/Adult_sign_restricted.jpg) | ![Adult Signature Restricted Delivery](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/Adult_sign_reqd.jpg) | Adult Signature Services Eligibility by Mail Class **Note:** Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery services are **not** **available at retail locations. [Click here for additional restrictions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Adult-Signature-Required-and-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery-Services#Restrictions)**. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Eligible Types of Mail:** | **Adult Signature Required** | **Adult Signature Restricted Delivery** | | **Priority Mail Express®** | Yes | Yes | | **Priority Mail®** | Yes | Yes | | **First-Class Mail** | Yes | Yes | | **Parcel Select®** (Nonpresort) | Yes | No | | **USPS Ground Advantage® - Retail** | No | Yes | | **USPS Ground Advantage® - Commercial** | Yes | Yes | | **USPS Marketing Mail™** | No | No | | **Periodicals** | No | No | | **Eligible Extra Services:** | **Adult Signature Required** | **Adult Signature Restricted Delivery** | | **Insurance** | Yes | Yes | | **Return Receipt**(using PS Form 3811) for Priority Mail Express® and Priority Mail items | Yes | Yes | | **Certified Mail®**(as [Certified Mail Adult Signature Restricted Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Certified-Mail) \- not available with Priority Mail Express or USPS Ground Advantage-Retail or commercial items) | No | Yes | | **Available Hold For pickup Options:** | **Adult Signature Required** | **Adult Signature Restricted Delivery** | | **Priority Mail Express® - Retail** | No | Yes | | **Priority Mail Express® - Commercial** | Yes | Yes | | **Priority Mail®** (Using Click-N-Ship® and commercial prices) | Yes | Yes | | **Parcel Select Barcoded** (Nonpresort) | Yes | No | | **USPS Ground Advantage-Retail** (using Click-N-Ship) | No | Yes | | **USPS Ground Advantage** | No | Yes | | **Available Fee and Payment Methods** | **Adult Signature Required** | **Adult Signature Restricted Delivery** | | **Click-N-Ship®** | Yes | Yes | | **USPS®-approved PC Postage®** | Yes | Yes | | **Permit Imprint,** if the customer electronically submits postage statements and mailing documentation. | Yes | Yes | | **Information-Based Indicia** (IBI) postage meter. | Yes | Yes | | **Restrictions:**
- **Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery are not available at retail locations.**
- **Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery are only available to customers who pay for postage and applicable fees using any of available payment methods listed above.**
- **Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery services do not expedite mailing or provide a guaranteed delivery time.**
- **These services must be added at the time of postage purchase.**
- **Postage cannot** **be paid using pre-canceled stamps.**
- **For current fee information, go to [http://pe.usps.com](http://pe.usps.com/)  and click on _Price List (Notice 123)_.**
**Note: Mail sent to the following locations cannot use Adult Signature Required:**
- Restricted APO/FPO/DPO destinations
- The Department of State
- Certain ZIP Codes of Palau, Micronesia, and the Marshall Islands:
- 96939
- 96940
- 96941
- 96942
- 96943
- 96944
- 96960
- 96970 | Adult Signature Required & Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Service Delivery Confirmation Options **What information is available when you purchase Adult Signature Required Service?** You can request the following information: - Recipient's signature. - Recipient's printed first initial and last name. - Date, time and location of the delivery. - [USPS Tracking®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) provides end-to-end item tracking and status. It is automatically included with all USPS domestic shipping products – except commercial USPS Marketing Mail® parcels. **How can I confirm the delivery of an Adult Signature Required/ Adult Signature Restricted Delivery item?** - **Package tracking** information can be retrieved through [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action) available on [USPS.com](http://www.usps.com/) ®. - **A Proof of Delivery (POD) letter**, which has a copy of the signature, can be obtained by email at no additional expense by going to the [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") website, entering the article barcode number of the mailpiece, and requesting a POD via email. - Email requests are processed 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. - **[Return Receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics)** purchased at mailing, if applicable, provides a proof of delivery letter. Mailed receipts are sent as First-Class Mail® items and should arrive in 2-3 business days. - **[Bulk proof of delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt)** is available to authorized commercial mailers. Through bulk proof of delivery, you can obtain signature data in a signature extract file format. - Customers interested in participating in Bulk Proof of Delivery service can refer to the [Postal Pro page on the Bulk Proof of Delivery Program](https://postalpro.usps.com/shipping/bulk-proof-delivery-program) for more details. - **Note**: The recipient's signature is **not** viewable online. You may also Track your **Adult Signature Required/ Adult Signature Restricted Delivery item**: - **By text**: Send a text to 28777 (2USPS) with your tracking number as the content of the message. Standard message and data rates may apply. See [Text Tracking FAQs](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm) for more information. - **By smartphone**: Download the USPS Mobile® app for iPhone® and Android™. | | | | --- | --- | | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/apple_app_store.png)](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/usps-mobile/id339597578) | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/google_play.png)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.usps) | - **By phone** at **1-800-222-1811**. - Phone hours of operation are: - Monday thru Friday:  8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. ET - Saturday: 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET - Sundays:  **Closed** - Holidays:  **Closed** \[ [Click here for Postal Holidays](http://about.usps.com/news/events-calendar/postal-holidays.htm)\] **How long is the delivery information available?** - Information via USPS Tracking is available for 45 days after date of delivery before it is archived. - Archived information can be restored through USPS Tracking as well. - USPS® maintains all delivery information, which includes the recipient's signature and proof of delivery, for up to 2 years after the date of delivery. Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Services

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000xPtAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds) ## International Mail Postage Refunds ## Information on eligibility for and requesting refunds for International Mail postage and extra service fees. - Dec 31, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006973 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Postage and Extra Service Fee Refunds** | | **[Priority Mail Express International® w/Money Back Guarantee](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#pmei_mbg)** | **[Priority Mail Express International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#pmei)** | **[Priority Mail International®, First-Class Package International Service®, and First-Class Mail International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#fcmi_fcpis_pmi)** | | **[**International Registered Mail®**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#intl_registered)** | **[International Return Receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#intl_return_receipt)** | | | **Important Notes:**
- Postage refunds refer to the fees paid for postage and extra services. **For more information on insured items, filing a claim and the international mail inquiry process**, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics).
- For directions to refund **unused Click-N-Ship® labels**, see [Request a USPS Refund: International](https://www.usps.com/help/international-refunds.htm). Submitting a refund request for **used international Click-N-Ship labels** is the same process as International postage refunds described below. | **Is Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee Service Eligible for Refunds?** Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI) with Money-Back Guarantee items, which is only available to [select countries](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC), are eligible for a postage refund if your shipment either: - Was not delivered or did not have a delivery attempt by the guaranteed delivery date or - Suffered total loss, damage, or loss of contents Either condition must be confirmed through the [International inquiry process](http://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm), which is also the first step in the process to file a claim. **If you are requesting a refund and you sent the mailpiece _from_ the United States**, submit an [International inquiry](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) within 30 days of the date of mailing. The investigation will determine your eligibility for a postage refund. If you need additional guidance, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). **Please note:** Local Post Offices™ will not issue any forms or postage refunds for PMEI with Money-Back Guarantee. | | | --- | | Postage refunds will **not** be made for any of the reasons listed in [International Mail Manual 942.52](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc9_011.htm#ep986000). | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#top) # **Is Priority Mail Express International® Service Eligible for Refunds?** Priority Mail Express International® service (PMEI) items are eligible for a postage refund **when the item has suffered total loss, damage, or loss of contents**, which has been confirmed through the [International inquiry process](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm). **If you are requesting a refund and you sent the mailpiece _from_ the United States**, initiate the [International inquiry process](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) within 90 days of the date of mailing. The investigation will determine your eligibility for a postage refund. If you need additional guidance, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). | | | --- | | Postage refunds will **not** be made for the following:
- Delayed PMEI items
- When the item contained prohibited matter. For more information on prohibited matter, see:
- [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Matter: International Mail](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_001.htm)
- [“Prohibitions and Restrictions” in the Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm)
- When the item has been seized or confiscated by customs or any other government agency of the destination country | **Note:** - PMEI is **not** the guaranteed service Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee which offers date certain service with a money back guarantee to select locations. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#top) # **Is International Registered Mail® Service Eligible for Refunds?** No, the extra service fee for international Registered Mail is not reimbursed. Items mailed with the service are eligible for both indemnity and postage refund **when it has not been delivered or has been delivered with contents totally damaged or totally missing**. The condition has to be confirmed through the [International inquiry process](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm). If you need additional guidance, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#top) # **Is an International Return Receipt Eligible for a Refund?** Yes. International return receipt fees are eligible for refunds **when the U.S. sender either does not receive a return receipt or receives an improperly completed return receipt**. **To request a refund of the return receipt extra service fee**, you **can either go to the post office where you purchased the return receipt or** initiate the [International inquiry process](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) for the return receipt. The investigation will determine your eligibility for an extra service fee refund. If you need additional guidance, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). **Note**: Return Receipts are not guaranteed; some countries do not provide the service. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds#top) # **Are Priority Mail International®, First-Class Package International Service®, and First-Class Mail International® Services Eligible for Refunds?** You may request a refund on First-Class Mail International®, First-Class Package International Service®, and Priority Mail International® items when postage, [extra service](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm) fees, or other return charges have been paid on: - Items for which full service was not rendered - Items for which you overpaid postage - Undeliverable-as-addressed items for which you paid incorrect return charges **If you are requesting a refund and you sent the mailpiece _from_ the United States**, submit the following to the postmaster of the Post Office from which the items were mailed within 60 days of the mailing date: - Duplicate applications on PS Form 3533, _Application and Voucher for Refund of Postage, Fees, and Services_. PS Form 3533 may be picked up at your local Post Office - When available, the envelope or wrapper, or the portion with such details as the names and addresses of the sender and addressee, canceled postage, and postal markings - Any other evidence of postage paid, or charges for which refund is desired **Note:** The Postmaster determines if you have met the refund eligibility requirements. Click below for more information: - [Full Refund](http://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/604.htm#9_2_3 "Refunds") - [No Refund](http://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/604.htm#9_2_4 "No Refund") **If you are requesting a refund and the mailpiece was sent _to_ the United States**, the sender should contact the foreign country's postal service about postage refund options.
