Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000003LvYAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Proof-of-Delivery) ## What is Proof of Delivery? ## Proof of Delivery (POD) is an e-mailed response letter containing the delivery information, the recipient's name, the tracking number, an image of the recipient's signature, and the printed name and address. - Jul 12, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007102 Customer Information **What is a Proof of Delivery?** A Proof of Delivery (POD) is an e-mailed response letter containing the delivery information, the recipient's name, the tracking number, an image of the recipient's signature, and the printed name and address. Other information includes: - Delivery location attribute (e.g., Front Door / Porch). - Product of mail. - Extra services. - Package weight. Proof of Delivery is only available for items where a Signature Confirmation™ service has been purchased or is included in the mail class: - Signature Confirmation™ Service - Restricted Delivery - Adult Signature Required - Priority Mail Express® - Registered Mail® A USPS Proof of Delivery is available to the 50 states, Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands, but is specifically NOT available to the other U.S. Territories and Possessions. **What is a Bulk Proof of Delivery?** Bulk Proof of Delivery is available to authorized commercial mailers when using a Signature Confirmation™ service, such as Adult Signature Required Service, Registered Mail®, or Restricted Delivery. Customers receiving Bulk Proof of Delivery may obtain signature data through a signature extract file format. Customers interested in participating in Bulk Proof of Delivery service can refer to the [Postal Pro page on the Bulk Proof of Delivery Program](https://postalpro.usps.com/shipping/bulk-proof-delivery-program) for more details. **How do I request a Proof of Delivery?** You can request a Proof of Delivery for applicable mailpieces by using the [USPS Tracking® Tool](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action) at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®. Select Proof of Delivery and follow the provided directions to receive a Proof of Delivery email either with or without a delivery address. If an item was delivered internationally, USPS does not have a POD for it. If such an item required a signature and if it is the policy of that country’s postal service, a POD for that foreign delivery can possibly be requested using the research process available through an USPS international agent. [Call the Customer Care Center](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm#kilo) and provide your item's USPS Tracking Number. Note that you may see the POD request option before the item is delivered. You can submit the request, but you won’t receive the POD until shortly after the item has been delivered. Access to Proof of Delivery is available for applicable mailpieces as long as the delivery information for such mailpieces is available. **What does a Proof of Delivery letter look like?** | | | | --- | --- | | **SAMPLE Proof of Delivery Letter**
(current version) | **SAMPLE Proof of Delivery Letter Priority Mail Express**
(previous version; businesses will continue to receive the version below until they convert to the new version) | | ![Sample Proof of Delivery Letter; This letter provides information on a delivered item. After referencing the Tracking Number, the letter is broken down into 4 different sections: Item details, Shipment Details, Destination delivery address, and Recipient signature.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/proof_of_delivery_letter_july_2018.jpg) | ![Sample Proof of Delivery Letter with a USPS letterhead. The letter includes details on the date of request, date and location of item delivery, and recipient. The letter also has the recipient's signature and address.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PODletter.jpg) | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Proof-of-Delivery#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is Proof of Delivery? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000v1VAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Restricted-Delivery) ## What is Restricted Delivery? ## Selecting Restricted Delivery allows the mailer to direct delivery only to the addressee or the addressee's authorized agent. - Nov 21, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000004840 Customer Information | | | --- | | Restricted Delivery service is no longer available as a separate service. However, the mailer can still direct delivery of mail only to the addressee or the addressee's authorized agent through some combined services.
See [What Domestic Mail Extra Services are Available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) for more information on those combined services. | _**Restricted Delivery**_ permits the mailer to direct delivery only to the addressee or the addressee's _**authorized agent.**_ - The United States Postal Service® may require proof of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) from the recipient (or agent). **Restricted delivery does** ****NOT** provide:** - Insurance against loss or damage. - Tracking or tracing. - Expedited delivery. - Guaranteed delivery time. - Mailing or Signature receipt (unless an additional service is purchased). **Are there any exceptions to who can receive Restricted Delivery mail?** **Specific common exceptions (allowing others to sign for Restricted Delivery mail) include:** - Mail with more than one name, for example: one person **OR** another (e.g., "John Doe **OR** Sally Doe"),**EITHER** party (or authorized agent of either) may sign the receipt. - **Note:**  Mail sent to two or more persons jointly (e.g., "John Doe AND Sally Doe") must be signed by BOTH recipients and / or their authorized agents. - Mail for famous personalities and executives of large organizations is normally delivered to an agent authorized to sign for such mail. - Mail for government officials may be delivered to a person authorized by the recipient or by regulations or procedures of the agency or organization to receive the recipient's mail. - Mail for officials of military organizations by name and title is delivered to the mail clerk as designated by the Department of Defense. - Mail for an inmate of a city, state, or federal penal institution, in cases where a personal signature cannot be obtained, is delivered to the warden or designee. - Mail for minors or persons under guardianship may be delivered to their parents or guardians. - Mail sent to one individual in care of another may be signed for by either person. **How can the recipient/addressee authorize another person to receive their Restricted Delivery Mail?** To authorize regular delivery of Restricted Delivery mail to an agent, the recipient should send a letter to the Postmaster or complete PS Form 3801 (Standing Delivery Order) at the local Post Office™ location. If no letter or standing delivery order is on file, the recipient may "authorize" a person to receive the mailpiece by completing the Attempted Delivery Notice, PS Form 3849. Images of the two forms can be found at "What Postal Forms are Associated with Restricted Delivery?" Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000vj3AAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) ## International Inquiries - The Basics ## International Inquiry is a request for an investigation submitted by a customer to determine if an item mailed to a foreign country has been delivered or received in good condition. An Inquiry is the first step to filing an International Claim. - Nov 17, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006068 Customer Information | | | --- | | **To begin an international inquiry, which could result in an international claim, please visit****[File an International Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm)**. **For more information on the processes, continue using this article.** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**Learning about international inquiries and claims**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#learning_about_international_inquiries_and_claims) | [**Starting an international inquiry**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#starting_an_international_inquiry) | [**After initiating an international inquiry**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#after_initiating_an_international_inquiry) | Did you send an item from the US to a foreign country and it was missing contents, damaged, or lost or was it received outside the guaranteed delivery date? Or did you, in the US, receive an item from a foreign country in a similar condition or state? You or the sender in a foreign country may be able to file an international claim for the insured value of the item or request a refund of the postage or extra services fee(s) from the country of origin. **_Before the claim process can begin_, the international inquiry process must first be initiated at the country of origin within a certain time frame and completed.** 1. An **international inquiry** is filed by a customer to see if an item sent using an international service has been received in good condition. If you are just starting out, you most likely have only filed an inquiry. 2. If the results of your inquiry determine the item was lost, missing contents or damaged, then your case will be sent to the **claim process**. A claim is a request for an indemnity payment that resulted from loss, missing content, or damage to insured items. Your next steps depend on: 1.   1. **Whether the item was shipped to or from the United States** 2.   2. [**The mail class of the item**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#table_mail_classes) 3.   3. [**What has happened to the item**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#scenario_table_top) 4.   4. [**When the filing period for an international inquiry begins and expires for a mail class**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#after_mailing_how_long_should_I_wait_to_file_an_international_inquiry) Use the [Scenario table](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#scenario_table_top) below to find out what you should do. | **Note**: If you do not know the mail class of the item: - Enter the item's tracking number into [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) to find out or - Match your tracking number with the sample numbers listed below: | SAMPLE NUMBER | MAIL TYPE OR SERVICE | | --- | --- | | EC 000 000 000 US | [Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International) | | EC 000 000 000 US | [Priority Mail Express International with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC) | | CP 000 000 000 US | [Priority Mail International® (PMI)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International) | | RA 000 000 000 US | [Registered Mail® Service International](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International) | | LX 000 000 000 US | [First-Class Mail International® Service (FCMI)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International) | | LX 000 000 000 US | [First Class Package International Service® (FCPIS)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Package-International-Service-FCPIS) | | _X_ and _0_ are in place to represent different possible letters and numbers. | | | **SCENARIO** | **YOU ARE THE** | **NEXT STEPS** | | --- | --- | --- | | The item:
- Is damaged, lost, or missing contents
- Is not insured and does not have indemnity (First Class Mail International Service or First Class Package International Service) | US Sender | We apologize, but USPS does not accept inquiries for uninsured items and items without indemnity. | | Non-US Sender | The non-US sender should find out what can be done through their foreign postal administration. | | The item:
- Is damaged, lost, or missing contents
- Is not insured and does not have indemnity (First Class Mail International Service or First Class Package International Service)
- Has been Returned to Sender | US Sender | We apologize, but an inquiry cannot be created for uninsured items and items without indemnity. | | Non-US Sender | The non-US sender should find out what can be done through their foreign postal administration. | | The item:
- Is damaged, lost, or missing contents
- Is insured and/or has indemnity | US Sender | The US sender will need to create an inquiry through USPS | | Non-US Sender | The sender of the item will need to create an inquiry with their foreign postal administration. 
The US recipient will take the item (all its contents and shipping material) to a Post Office to report. | | The item:
- Is damaged, lost, or missing contents
- Is insured and/or has indemnity
- Has been Returned to Sender | US Sender | The US sender will need to create an inquiry through USPS and take the item (all its contents and shipping material) to a Post Office for damage verification. | | Non-US Sender | The non-US sender should find out what can be done through their foreign postal administration. | | The item was delivered late | US Sender | If a Priority Mail Express International® item with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC) was delivered after the Guaranteed Delivery Date (GDD), create an inquiry.
PMEI, PMI, Registered Mail, FCMI, FCPIS, and M-bag items do not have a guaranteed date of delivery. | | Non-US Sender | If the item has a guaranteed delivery date, the non-US Sender must initiate an inquiry with their postal administration. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) # **Learning about International Inquiries and Claims** | | | --- | | - To create an international inquiry online, go to [https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) and look under the "How It Works" or "How to File a Claim" sections.
- For further information and details, continue. | | | | | --- | --- | | **[Why do I have to submit an international inquiry before I can file a claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#GettingStarted)** | **[Is there a charge for filing an inquiry on international mail items?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#GettingStarted)** | | **[After mailing, how long should I wait to file an international inquiry?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#WhenToFile)** | | **Why do I have to submit an international inquiry before I can file a claim?** The Universal Postal Union, which governs the exchange of international mail, requires that an inquiry be submitted before a claim can be processed. The postal administration of the destination country can investigate to determine if the item was delivered and whether it was damaged. **Is there a charge for filing an inquiry on International Mail?** No, there is no charge. **After mailing, how long should I wait to file an international inquiry?** Please allow sufficient time for the item to be delivered before initiating an inquiry. See the [Filing Period Table](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm#filingperiods) for information about when to file an inquiry based on the international service used. You will also want to [check the status](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) of your package before filing an inquiry. | ****MAIL TYPE OR SERVICE**** | ****WHEN TO FILE (FROM MAILING DATE)**** | | --- | --- | | ****No Sooner Than**** | ****No Later Than**** | ****Restrictions**** | | **Priority Mail Express International with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC)** | 3 days | 30 days | - 3-30 days to submit an inquiry for postage refund for late delivery
- 3-90 days to file an inquiry for loss, damaged, or missing contents | | **Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI)** | 3 days | 90 days | Online inquiries can only be initiated by the US sender. | | **Priority Mail International® (PMI) for all countries except Canada** | 7 days | 6 months | Online inquiries can only be initiated by the US sender | | **Priority Mail International® (PMI) for Canada** | 10 days | 6 months | Online inquiries can only be initiated by the US sender | | **Registered Mail® Service International** | 7 days | 6 months | Online inquiries can only be initiated by the US sender | | **First Class Mail International® (FCMI)** | Not eligible | Not eligible | Inquiries are not accepted for letters sent without Registered Mail service | | **First Class Package International Service® (FCPIS)** | Not eligible | Not eligible | Inquiries cannot be processed online for parcels sent without Registered Mail service | | **M-bag** | Not eligible | Not eligible | Inquiries are not accepted for M-bag items | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) # **Starting an international inquiry** | | | --- | | - To create an international inquiry online, go to [https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) and look under the “How It Works” or “How to File a Claim” sections.
- For further information and details, continue. | If you used the [Scenario Table](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#scenario_table_top) above, then you have already: 1.    I. Determined what the US sender or non-US sender should do 2.   II. [**Determined the mail class of your item**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#table_mail_classes) 3.  III. [**Determined the status of your item (missing contents, damaged, lost, received late, etc.)**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#scenario_table_top) 4.  IV. **[Checked the filing period to determine if your international item is still eligible for an inquiry](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#after_mailing_how_long_should_I_wait_to_file_an_international_inquiry)** Before you start your international inquiry, you will also need to: 1.   V. **[Gather your documents](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#gather_documents)** 2.  VI. **[Review filing options and file the inquiry](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#filing_an_international_inquiry)** **Gather your documents** The Postal Service asks that you provide information regarding the mailing, including but not limited to the following: | REQUIRED DOCUMENTS TO FILE | | --- | | Regardless of mail class, you'll need to have the following information about your package:
- The USPS Tracking number that appears on the receipt 
- The sender’s name, mailing address, e-mail address, and telephone number 
- The addressee’s name and mailing address
- The addressee’s e-mail address and telephone number, if available
- The date of the mailing, weight, postage paid, and additional fees, if applicable
- A description of the contents 
- Evidence of Mailing
- Evidence of Value | | See [File an International Claim](http://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm#documents) for further information. | **Filing an international inquiry / claim** If you meet the above requirements, there are two options to file an inquiry: [Online or By Phone](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#FilingOptions). | ****FILING INTERNATIONAL INQUIRIES OPTIONS**** | | --- | | ****Online**** | ****By Phone**** | | To initiate an online inquiry at [https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm), you must:
- Be the US sender
- Be a registered [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account holder or [create a USPS.com account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) before you file the inquiry. | To initiate an inquiry by phone you must:
- Be the US sender
- Call the USPS Customer Care Center 1-800-222-1811 within the [time limits listed by service](http://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm#filingperiods).
- **Hours of Operation**

Monday – Friday: 8 AM – 8:30 PM ET

Saturday: 8 AM – 6 PM ET

Federal Holidays: CLOSED | | **Processing an inquiry**
Once you provide the relevant mailing information and/or documentation online or by telephone, the Postal Service® will correspond with the appropriate foreign post and advise you of the results of the inquiry. If initiated online, the Postal Service will update the online inquiry.
[To check the status of an inquiry, click here](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm). | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) # **After initiating an international inquiry** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[How do I check the status of my international inquiry?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#check_status_inquiry)** | **[How do I check the status of my claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#check_status_claim)** | **[Who receives the claim payment?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#who_receives_the_claim_payment)** | | [**How do I appeal a claim that was denied?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#appeal_claim_that_was_denied) | [**If my claim is denied a second time, what is the next step?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#claim_denied_second_time) | [**Return to Sender package not received or received damaged or missing contents**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#ReopenRTS) | | [****Lost item recovered after a claim has been paid, and reimbursing USPS****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#lost_item_recovered_after_paid_claim) | | | | ****Online**** | ****By Phone**** | | --- | --- | | **Online claims are recommended for convenience and speedier processing:** - You can submit your inquiry online 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year - Check your inquiry or claim status online at your convenience - Processing time is significantly less for inquiries submitted online - No need to complete paper claim forms, in most instances, claims are processed automatically online When there is a determination that an item is eligible for a claim, the International Research Group will change the claim status to “Claim Sent for Review” and notify the customer by email. For damage or loss claims, you will not receive a paper claims form by mail; the process is completed online. The only exception is for PMEI postage refunds. The indemnity process, if applicable, is completed online automatically, however a PS Form 3533 will be sent to you for the postage refund portion only if it qualifies. You will need to take the PS Form 3533 to your local Post Office for the postage refund to be processed. No further action will be required from you once the inquiry is sent to Claims. Claims will review the investigation to determine if the claim will be approved or denied. You can also [review the status of your inquiry](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=OIC&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Finternationalclaims.usps.com%2FINQUIRY%2Finquiry%2FSubmit542.do%3Fmethod%3DdoSearchOLINo) by logging in to [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and viewing your “Inquiry History”. If the inquiry is: - **Approved**, you will receive an email of its approval. A check will be mailed to the address you provided. - **Determined not to be eligible for a claim**, an email will be sent with a status update specific to the results of your inquiry. | | | --- | | **For Return to Sender packages:**
If your online inquiry is closed due to the package being Returned to Sender, you will receive an email with instructions. If the item has not been returned to you within 30 days from the date of your email, or the package has been returned to you damaged, and you would like to file a claim, please sign into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account. Click on HELP > Filing a Claim > International Shipments > Inquiry History > Click on the Case Number > Select Reopen Case and follow the prompts. 
**If the item was returned to you damaged or with missing contents,** take the package to any Post Office as soon as possible. The item, the mailing container, wrapping, packaging, and any other contents, should be presented to the Post Office representative. | | After the investigation of the inquiry has been completed and it has been determined that you are **eligible for a claim**, a packet will be mailed.
The packet will include a letter of instruction on how to complete and submit the claim. Once the claim packet is received by the Accounting Service Center, the claim process will begin. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) **How do I check the status of my international inquiry?** | | | | --- | --- | | You can check the **status of an international inquiry _submitted online_** by: | Logging into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® registered account by clicking on the following link: [https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm).
- At a minimum, you will need the following information:
- [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® log-in credentials
- Tracking number | | You can check the **status of an international inquiry _submitted by phone_** by: | Calling 1-800-222-1811 and speaking with a Customer Care Center agent.
- At a minimum, you will need to provide the following information:
- Your name
- Case Number or [Tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number) | **How do I check the status of my claim?** | | | | --- | --- | | You can check the **status of a claim _submitted online_** by: | Logging into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® registered account by clicking on the following link: [https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm).
- At a minimum, you will need the following information:
- [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® log-in credentials
- Tracking number | | You can check the **status of a claim _submitted by phone_** by: | Contacting the Accounting Helpdesk at 1-866-974-2733.
- Hours of operation are Monday - Friday, 8:00 am to 8:00 pm ET (7:00 am to 7:00 pm CT).
- At a minimum, you will need to provide the following information:
- Your name
- Tracking number
- Original mailing date | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) **Who receives the claim payment?** Claims are paid as follows: | | | --- | | **Priority Mail Express International® claims**
Paid by the country of origin to the sender. Payments to US senders will be made by the U.S. Postal Service.
* * * | | **International Registered Mail® items claims**
Paid by the country of origin to the sender. Payments to US senders will be made by the U.S. Postal Service.
If the Registered Mail item is not delivered or has been delivered with contents totally missing or contents totally damaged, indemnity plus postage is paid.
**Note**: The sender may waive the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the addressee. Payment in such cases will be made by the destination postal administration.
* * * | | **Priority Mail International® items claims**
Paid by the country of origin to the sender. Payments to US senders will be made by the U.S. Postal Service.
**Note**: The sender may waive the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the addressee. Payment in such cases will be made by the destination postal administration.
* * * | | **Claims for items delivered in damaged condition or with missing contents**
Paid by the country of origin to the sender. Payments to US senders will be made by the U.S. Postal Service.
**Note**: The sender may waive the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the addressee. Payment in such cases will be made by the destination postal administration.
* * * | | **Claims for items _mailed from foreign countries_ that are lost or that are delivered in damaged condition or with missing contents**
Paid by the origin postal administration to the sender.
**Note**: The sender may waive the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the U.S. addressee. In such cases, the U.S. Postal Service pays the U.S. addressee.
* * * | For information about refunds for postage and fees, see [International Mail Postage Refunds](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) **How do I appeal a claim that was denied?** If your claim is denied, you may appeal the decision if either: - You are in [dispute of the denial](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#dispute_of_denial) or - You believe the [payment is insufficient](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#Insufficient_payment) | | **If claim was filed ONLINE** | **If claim was filed BY PHONE** | | --- | --- | --- | | **In dispute of the denial** | To start an appeal:
1. Go to the [Inquiry History page](https://internationalclaims.usps.com/INQUIRY/inquiry/Submit542.do?method=doSearchOLINo).
2. Select the inquiry.
3. Enter additional information you want considered and resubmit. | For a denied claim, you may appeal a decision by filing a written appeal within 60 days of the date of the original decision to the following address:
**ACCOUNTING SERVICES**
**INTERNATIONAL CLAIM APPEALS**
**PO BOX 80146**
**ST. LOUIS, MO 63180-0146**
In your appeal, please:
- Explain why your case should be reviewed.
- Make sure that proof of value, evidence of mailing and insurance coverage for the article have been submitted. | | **Insufficient payment** | For a check sent in an amount less than the insured amount, you may submit a check protest appeal by filing a written appeal within 60 days of the date of the check to the following address:
**ACCOUNTING SERVICES**
**CHECK PROTEST**
**PO BOX 80141**
**ST. LOUIS, MO 63180-0141** | **If my claim is denied a second time, what is the next step?** If your claim was filed online, your second appeal will be filed online in the same manner your first appeal was filed. If your claim was filed by phone, you may file a final appeal to the Consumer Advocate in writing within 60 days of the date of the denial. In your appeal, please explain why your case should be reviewed. Also, make sure that proof of value, evidence of mailing, and insurance coverage for the article has been submitted. Submit the appeal to the following address: **CONSUMER ADVOCATE** **INTERNATIONAL CLAIMS APPEALS** **475 L’ENFANT PLAZA SW** **WASHINGTON, DC 20260-2200** Complete details about the international inquiry and international claims processes, including appeals, can be found in the [International Mail Manual: Chapter 9 Inquiries, Indemnities, and Refunds](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc9_001.htm "9 Inquiries, Indemnities, and Refunds | Postal Explorer"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) **Lost item recovered after a claim has been paid, and reimbursing USPS** If a lost item is recovered after payment of a claim, the payee may accept the item and reimburse USPS for the full amount paid if the article is undamaged. If the article is damaged, has depreciated, or has missing contents, the payee may accept it and reimburse USPS in an amount set by the Consumer Advocate, USPS Headquarters. To begin the process of reimbursing payment: 1. **Please contact the Accounting Help Desk:** - Email [helpdeskaccounting-st.louis@usps.gov](mailto:helpdeskaccounting-st.louismo@usps.gov?subject=International%20Claim). - Call **1-866-974-2733** - **You will need to specify "International"** - Hours of operation are Monday - Friday, from 8:00am to 8:00pm Eastern Time (7:00am to 7:00pm Central Time) 2. **Provide the following required information:** - Your name - Tracking number (For more information, see [How to find your tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number)) - Original mail date 3. **Follow the guidance that is provided by the Accounting Help Desk:** - Useful addresses to know for: | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailing reimbursements to USPS:** | **Questions and concerns regarding recovered articles thought to be damaged, depreciated or missing content**: | | Eagan Disbursing Accounting Services
2825 Lone Oak Parkway
Eagan, MN 55121-9640 | Consumer Advocate
U.S. Postal Service
475 L'Enfant Plaza SW
Washington, DC 20260-2200 | 4. **Note:** Customers should keep a photo copy of the check and letter for their records. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title International Inquiries - The Basics URL Name International-Inquiries-The-Basics [International](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFwGAM/international)[Claim Inquiries](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGdGAM/claim-inquiries) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading International Inquiries - The Basics

metadata.description: International Inquiry is a request for an investigation submitted by a customer to determine if an item mailed to a foreign country has been delivered or received in good condition. An Inquiry is the first step to filing an International Claim.
metadata.title: International Inquiries - The Basics

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000tcPAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label) ## How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label? ## Instructions for printing domestic and international labels, and types of printers and paper to use. - Nov 13, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007274 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**Printing a domestic Click-N-Ship® label**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#printing) | [**Printing an APO/FPO/DPO, US territories/possessions, or Freely Associated States shipping label**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#apo) | [**Printing an international shipping label**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#Printing_International_Labels) | | [**Printing a "2-Up" label**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#twoup) | [**What kind of printer and paper should I use to print Click-N-Ship® labels?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#paper) | [**Printing duplicates of the same label**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#duplabel) | **Printing a domestic Click-N-Ship® label** To print a domestic shipping label in 4 easy steps, follow the guidelines below: 1. Enter the Label Information (Return and Delivery address, Package Details, optional Package Value, and Shipping Date) and proceed to the **_Select a Service Type_** section. Domestic labels must include a domestic return address; **foreign return addresses are not allowed on domestic mail and packages.** Use the _**Batch**_ option if you need to create multiple labels using the same mailpiece weight, service option, and return information. After all the label information has been entered, proceed to the **_Select Service Type_** section. - **Note**: The Batch option is not available for the following: - USPS Connect™ - Military Addresses (includes all APO/FPO/DPO addresses) - US Territories or Possessions (includes American Samoa, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands) - Freely Associated States (includes Marshall Islands, Micronesia, and Palau) - International Addresses 2. Select the desired _**Service**_ and any Extra Services for your shipment by clicking on the corresponding radio button and then **click** the **_Add to Cart & Start New Label_** button to create another label or **click** the _**Add to Cart**_ button to review your _Shipping Cart_. 3. Review the label information on the _**Shipping Cart**_ page. Edit any information that is incorrect, choose to create another label, delete the label, add to SCAN Form, or **click** the _**Next: Billing Information**_ button to purchase your labels _._ Select your payment method, enter the required information and **click** the _**Next: Pay and Print**_ button. 4. From the Payment Confirmation page you can print your label(s) at that time or at a Post Office® location through the [Label Broker® feature](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#Label_Broker), save your label(s) as a PDF file, create another label, or schedule a pickup (Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand). Saving your label allows you to print your label later. Labels can also be printed from the Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date. | | | --- | | **No access to a printer?**
Check out the [Label Broker®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) feature with the Click-N-Ship application. You can pay for your shipping label using the Click-N-Ship application and select the “Print Labels Later at the Post Office” option. A QR code will be emailed to you to take to a Post Office™ location with your package. The Retail employee at the Post Office location will scan the code, print the label, affix it to the package and induct it into the system for processing. See the article [Label Broker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) for more information. | **Notes:** - It is important that you mail your package on the Shipping Date you selected during the label generation process as an electronic record is automatically created for that label on that specific date. Labels with "stale" Shipping Dates (i.e. mailed after the Shipping Date printed on the label) may be returned to you. - If you are a first-time user, you may see a pop-up screen instructing you to install Adobe Reader software. This download must be installed for the label to print. The link to download the latest Adobe Reader version is available on the Payment Confirmation page. - With Priority Mail® service and select services, you may receive [USPS Tracking®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) at no additional charge; [Signature Confirmation™](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Signature-Confirmation) may be available at a lower price. Delivery information on your shipment is obtained by accessing your paid online labels from your Shipping History or by using our [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) tool at [USPS.com®](https://www.usps.com/welcome.htm). - Labels must be fully placed on the address side of the package and not folded over package edges. Use clear shipping tape to securely affix labels on all four sides. Do not tape over the "Postal Use" area on Priority Mail Express® labels or the barcodes. - The Click-N-Ship® Priority Mail Express® label will only print one copy that is attached to the mailpiece. - It is recommended that you use United States Postal Service® packaging or stickers when using Click-N-Ship® labels to clearly indicate the type of service you have purchased. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#top) **Printing an APO/FPO/DPO, US territories/possessions, or Freely Associated States shipping label** To print an APO/FPO/DPO, US Territories/Possessions, Freely Associated States label in 5 easy steps, follow the guidelines below: 1. Enter the Label Information (Return and Delivery address, Package Details, optional Package Value, and Shipping Date) and proceed to the **_Select a Service Type_** section. 2. Select the desired **_Service_** and Extra Services for your shipment by clicking on the corresponding radio button and then **click** the **_Continue_** button. 3. Enter the **customs** information (provide as much detail as possible and any relevant information about the contents of your shipment) for your item and then **click** the **_Continue_** button. 4. Review the label information on the **_Shipping Cart_** page. Edit any information that is incorrect, choose to create another label; or **click** the **Next: Billing Information** button to purchase your labels. Select your payment method, enter the required information and **click** the **Next: Pay and Print** button. 5. From the Payment Confirmation page you can print your label(s), save your label as a PDF file, create another label, or schedule a pickup. Saving your label allows you to print your label later. Labels can also be printed or reprinted from the Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date. **Notes:** - It is important that you mail your package on the Shipping Date you selected during the label generation process as an electronic record is automatically created for that label on that specific date. Labels with "stale" Shipping Dates (i.e. mailed after the Shipping Date printed on the label) may be returned to you. - If you are a first-time user, you may see a pop-up screen instructing you to install Adobe Reader software. This download must be installed for the label to print. The link to download the latest Adobe Reader version is available on the Payment Confirmation page. **Printing an international shipping label** To print an international shipping label in 5 easy steps, follow the guidelines below: 1. Enter the Label Information (Return and Delivery address, Package Details, Package Value, and Shipping Date) and proceed to the **_Select a Service Type_** section. 2. Select the desired _**Service**_ and Extra Services for your shipment by clicking on the corresponding radio button and then **click** the **_Continue_** button. 3. Enter the customs information for your item (provide as much detail as possible and any relevant information about the contents of your shipment) and then **click** the **_Continue_** button. 4. Review the label information on the _**Shipping Cart**_ page. Edit any information that is incorrect, choose to create another label, or **click** the _**Next: Billing Information**_ _button._ Select your payment method, enter the required information and **click** the **_Next: Pay and Print_** button. 5. From the Payment Confirmation page, you can print your label(s), save your label as a PDF file, create another label, or schedule a pickup. Saving your label allows you to print your label later. Labels can also be printed or reprinted from the Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date. **Note:** - It is important that you mail your package on the Shipping Date you selected during the label generation process as an electronic record is automatically created for that label on that specific date. Labels with "stale" Shipping Dates (i.e. mailed after the Shipping Date printed on the label) may be returned to you. For Priority Mail Express International and Priority Mail International® services, if there are multiple copies of your electronically generated customs form, they must be inserted in the plastic envelope required for online international services. These plastic envelopes, _PS 2976-E, Customs Declaration Envelope_, the smaller PS 2976-ES, _Small Customs Declaration Envelope_, and shipping supplies are available free of charge at your local Post Office™ location, from the Packing Supply Center, or online at [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/home). - **Note:** The Packing Supply Center can be reached via phone at **1-800-610-8734, option 1 (Spanish speaking customers, select option 2).** When printing certain labels, including PS Form 2976, an envelope (PS Form 2976-E/ES) is not required. You need only apply the label directly to the package or envelope. Here are examples of products that include PS Form 2976: - Certain Priority Mail International® labels - First-Class Package International Service® labels - Military / US Possessions, Territories, and Freely Associated States (PTFAS) labels If you are a first-time user, you may see a pop-up screen instructing you to install Adobe Reader software. This download must be installed for the label to print. The link to download the latest Adobe Reader version is available on the Payment Confirmation page. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#top) **Printing a "2-Up" label** This feature only applies to labels that do not include a customs form. Check the radio button next to the print option. _Print labels without receipt (2 per page)_ to print two (2) upright (2-Up) labels per piece of paper rather than one (1) label and the receipt. - Labels will print as two (2) 4 inch by 6½ inch Click-N-Ship® Domestic labels on one piece of self-adhesive 8½ inches by 11 inches medium paper. - Customers will have access to all of the label information online through the following options: - To access the information that would typically be printed on a receipt, customers should review the information provided on the Print Confirmation page (printer friendly option as well), or - Select the _**Shipping History**_ tab on the Click-N-Ship® toolbar to review all of the information regarding all shipments going back as far as six (6) months. **What kind of printer and paper should I use to print Click-N-Ship® labels?** You can use a laser printer or a high quality ink jet printer to print your Click-N-Ship® labels. For ink jet printers, 600 dots-per-inch (DPI) is recommended. Our scanning equipment is not capable of reliably reading labels generated using low quality Inkjet or dot matrix printers. **Note**: Only white paper should be used to print your labels, or you can buy self-adhesive labels. **What if I accidentally printed a second (duplicate) label?** Since you were only charged once for postage, you should discard the duplicate label. The postage is linked to the label number, which is unique and can be used only once. The label number will appear in a duplicate postage list in the United States Postal Service® database that is monitored by the Postal Inspection Service. If a customer intentionally uses a duplicate label, they will be liable for fraud. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000tXZAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics) ## Ordering Online - The Basics ## Customers have the convenience of ordering online from The Postal Store® by logging into their USPS.com® account. In addition, they can access other services such as Click-N-Ship®, scheduling a pickup, printing shipping labels, and PO Boxes® Online. - Nov 13, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006967 Customer Information **Note:** You can use your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account to access other services in addition to buying items from The Postal Store®, such as Click-N-Ship®, scheduling a pickup, printing shipping labels, and PO Boxes® Online. See below for [information on submitting international Postal Store orders](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics#international_postal_store_orders). See below for [information on checking out as a guest from The Postal Store (Guest Checkout)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics#guest_checkout). | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | # **Ordering Online at the Postal Store** | | **Description** | **Online Action** | **Process** | | **Ordering Stamps and Retail Products** | [https://store.usps.com/store](https://store.usps.com/store/results/shipping-supplies/_/N-7d0v8v?No=18&Nrpp=18&) | For questions regarding stamps or retail product orders you've already placed online through [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/), either:
- **Email:** [sfsdelivery.confirmation@usps.gov](mailto:sfsdelivery.confirmation@usps.gov)
- **Phone**: 1-844-737-7826

- **Note**: Stamp orders can be placed by phone.
**Hours of Operation:**
- Monday - Friday 7 AM - 3:30 PM CT
- Closed Saturdays, Sundays and [Postal Holidays](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Holiday-Service-Schedule) | | **Ordering Mailing Labels and Shipping Supplies**
(Priority Mail®, Priority Mail Express®, Click-N-Ship®, etc) | [https://store.usps.com/store](https://store.usps.com/store/results/shipping-supplies/_/N-7d0v8v?No=18&Nrpp=18&) | Call to order free shipping boxes, envelopes, and postal forms, or to report a problem with your shipping supply order.
- 1-800-610-8734
**Hours of Operation**
- Monday - Friday 7 AM - 11 PM ET
- Saturday 7 AM - 6 PM ET | | **What Credit Cards are accepted at The Postal Store?** | The following credit cards are accepted at The Postal Store®:
- Visa®
- MasterCard®
- Discover®
- American Express™
- Debit cards are accepted if a PIN is not required | Other Payment Options available for The Postal Store® include:
- [PayPal](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-PayPal-to-pay-for-Postal-Store-orders)
- [Apple Pay](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted) | | **Cancel Subscription Order** | Log into your [USPS.com account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/welcome.htm).
There is no option to delete individual installments of your subscription.
**Note**: You are not able to make modifications to individual installments within your Stamp Subscription. If you want to cancel a specific installment, you must cancel the entire order. | Select **"My Profile"** by hovering over your username at the top right.
Select **"Stamp Subscriptions"** from the left navigation.
To delete the entire order, press the **"Cancel"** button next to the original order.
It will not show as cancelled if you purchased another item in this order, but it will be cancelled as long as you follow the process. | | **Create an Account at the Postal Store**
For information on checking out as a guest, see the Guest Checkout section below. | Go to [www.usps.com](http://www.usps.com/) and select **"Register/Sign In"**, then " **Sign up Now.**" | Experiencing problems? Please [contact us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm). | | **Make changes to my billing or subscription address** | Log into your [USPS.com account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/welcome.htm). | 1. Select **"My Profile"** by hovering over your username at the top right.
2. Select **"Stamp Subscriptions"** from the left navigation.
3. Select **"Modify"** in the original order. | | **Can I change the credit card used for subscription?** | **Yes**, you can change the credit card to which the subscription is billed after the first order has processed. | To change your credit card information for future orders:
1. Go to The Postal Store main page, [https://store.usps.com](https://store.usps.com/)
2. Log into your account
3. Mouse over your name and click "My Profile"
4. After you sign in, select "Saved Payment Info".
5. Your credit card information will display.
6. Select "Edit" to proceed with making changes to your credit card information. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics#top) * * * **How to submit international Postal Store orders** The Postal Store® does not allow users to select a shipping address outside of the U.S., U.S. Territories, and APOs/FPOs. However, customers can place international orders by mailing in the USA Philatelic Order Form. How to place an order using an international shipping address: [Download the Philatelic Order Form](https://www.usps.com/stamp-collecting/assets/pdf/philatelic_order_form.pdf), which is located at the bottom of [The Postal Store homepage](https://store.usps.com/store/home) in the "For Collectors" section. To complete the form, follow the steps below: 1. Print the order form 2. Enter the 6-digit item number in the "ITEM NUMBER" space on the form for each item. 3. Enter the quantity of each item in the "QTY" space. 4. Enter the price of each item in the "PRICE" space. 5. Enter the Total amount in the "TOTAL" space. 6. Mail the form to the Stamp Fulfillment Services address below: STAMP FULFILLMENT SERVICES PO BOX 7247 PHILADELPHIA PA 19101-7097 * * * **Guest Checkout on The Postal Store** | | | | --- | --- | | The option to check out as a guest will be presented to you on the "Choose a Secure Checkout Method" page. That appears once you click _Check Out Now_ on the Shopping Cart page, provided your cart is [guest checkout eligible](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics#guest_checkout_eligible).
Once the order is placed you will have the option to register. However **,** the just completed transaction **will not be a part of your Activity History** in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account. | ![Partial screenshot of the "Choose a Secure Checkout Method" USPS.com page. Appears after clicking "Check Out Now". The image has a section to "Sign In to Your Account" and a section to "Continue as a Guest", which is highlighted in the image. Under "Continue as a Guest", the section has: "You'll have an opportunity to create an account once you complete checkout." Followed by a "Check Out as Guest" button. And it ends with "*Guest checkout orders are one-time transactions that will not show up on newly created accounts."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/postal_store/account_signin_guest_checkout_option_highlight.jpg) | If you have an item or items ineligible for Guest Checkout in your cart, you will need to either: - Sign in to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account or create an account in order to complete your purchase or - Remove all ineligible items from the cart if you still wish to check out as a guest. Complete the checkout process by clicking the _Check Out Now_ button, then clicking the _Check Out as Guest_ button. | # **Products Eligible and Ineligible for Guest Checkout** | | --- | | **Eligible products include:** | **Ineligible products include:** | | - Retail Products, including Officially Licensed Products
- Stamps
- Philatelic Items/Collectibles
- Cards & Envelopes | - Pre-Orders
- Back-Orders
- Orders totaling $250 or more
- Personalized Stamped Envelopes
- Personalized Stamped Cards
- Priority Mail Forever Prepaid Flat Rate Boxes and Envelopes
- Stamp Subscriptions
- Paid/Free Shipping Supplies | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Online-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Ordering Online - The Basics URL Name Ordering-Online-The-Basics [Postal Store](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFzGAM/postal-store)[Ordering Online](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGzGAM/ordering-online) ## Related Articles - [Mailboxes - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailbox-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center

## Contact information for the Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center (MSSC). The MSSC provides centralized support for commercial mailing and shipping questions and access to trained and professional Mailing Requirements staff.      - Feb 16, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007470

Customer Information

The [USPS Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center (MSSC)](https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions) is open to provide centralized support for commercial mailing and shipping questions. The MSSC is a commercial Help Desk that provides customers with assistance and solutions on mailing requirements and mailpiece design. These assistance and solutions ensure compatibility with automated mail processing and compliance with:

- Mailing standards
- Automation readability
- _PostalOne!_ .
- Enterprise Payment System Accounts (including ACH or W-9 form requests)

**Contact Information:**

- **Website:** [https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions](https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions)
- **Phone:** 1-877-672-0007
- **Email:** [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov); Inquiries submitted via email should expect initial contact within 24 hours

**Hours of Operation:**

- Monday through Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. Eastern Time (7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. Central Time)
- Saturdays: Closed
- Sundays: Closed
- Holidays: Closed

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000q6fAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services) ## What are Package Services? ## Package Services provide economical ground shipping options for packages which may contain merchandise, catalogs, printed and other media material. - Oct 10, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007474 Customer Information | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **[**Package Services overview**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#overview)** | **[**What mailing options are available as Package Services?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#mailing_options)** | **[**What are the benefits of using Package Services?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#benefits)** | **[**Estimated delivery time for Package Services**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#delivery)** | **Package Services overview** Package Services are a Domestic product previously known as Fourth-Class Mail. Package Services provide economical ground shipping options for packages which may contain merchandise, catalogs, printed and other media material. Personal correspondence is not to be included in Package Services items. **Note**: Package Services are not sealed against postal inspection. The mailing of articles at any of the options listed as Package Services prices constitutes consent by the mailer to postal inspection of the contents. **Contents not found in compliance are subject to First-Class Mail® pricing**. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#top) ****What mailing options are available as Package Services?**** Package Services consist of 3 mailing options: - [Media Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Library-Mail-Service-and-Media-Mail-Service)\- Books, sheet music, printed educational material, film, videocassettes, and computer prerecorded media such as CD-ROMs. Advertising restrictions apply. This product is available at the Retail counter and BME (Bulk Mail Entry Unit) with authorization. - [Library Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Library-Mail-Service-and-Media-Mail-Service) \- Items sent to or from or exchanged between academic institutions (schools), public libraries, museums, and other authorized organizations. Books, sound recordings, and academic theses. This product is not available at the Retail Counter and requires BME (Bulk Mail Entry Unit) authorization. - [Bound Printed Matter Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Bound-Printed-Matter-Service) \- Consists of permanently bound sheets of which at least 90% are printed with advertising, promotional, directory, or editorial matter (or a combination of such matter). This product is not available at the Retail counter. BME (Bulk Mail Entry Unit) requires authorization. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#top) **What are the Benefits of Using Package Services?** Package Service shipping options offer a variety of economy shipping solutions for customers who are making their shipping decision based on cost savings without sacrificing value and reliability. - Package Services are categorized into one of three mail processing categories: machinable, irregular, or nonmachinable. These categories are based on the physical dimensions of the piece, regardless of the placement (orientation) of the delivery address on the piece. - No mailpiece may weigh more than 70 pounds. Bound Printed Matter may not weigh more than 15 pounds. - Delivery to every address in the US including PO Boxes and military addresses. - Saturday and residential delivery is available at no extra cost. - Extra Services, such as Insurance, USPS Tracking® and Signature Confirmation™ are available for an additional fee. - Media Mail, Library Mail and Bound Printed Matter prices are based on weight only. - Package Service items can be up to 108" in combined length and girth. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#top) **Estimated delivery time for Package Services:** - The general time standard is typically 2-8 business days in the 48 contiguous states. Package Services is not a guaranteed service; delivery by a certain date or time is not available. - Estimated delivery time between Alaska / Hawaii / Puerto Rico / APO-FPO’s and the contiguous 48 states is calculated by entering the origin and destination information into the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/) located in [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/) ®. - To determine the estimated delivery time refer to: [https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm) | | | --- | | **IMPORTANT:**  The timeliness of service to destinations outside the contiguous U.S. may be affected by the limited availability of transportation. Due to the variability of transportation, Package Services packages may take up to three to five weeks to reach Hawaii, Alaska, Puerto Rico and any U.S. Territories.
- This includes ZIP Codes™ with prefixes of 967 and 969 and those with the following prefixes: 995, 996, 997, 998, 999, 968, 006, 007, 008, and 009. | | Transit times to Military APO/FPO/DPO will vary depending on operational conditions and the unit of the addressee. Get more information on MPO transit times [here](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6eq0000000hD3AAI/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2Fafl__afl_Article_Feedback__c%2Fa95eq0000000kajAAA%2Fview). | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What are Package Services? [Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FRefuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000rYzAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists) ## Refuse unwanted mail and remove name from mailing lists ## Rules regarding refusal of mail and who to contact to remove your name from mailing lists. - Oct 29, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006162 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [****Is Mail Always Delivered as Addressed and Can it be Refused / Returned?****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists#is_mail_always_delivered_as_addressed) | [**What are my Mail Refusal Options?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists#what_are_my_mail_refusal_options) | [**How do I remove my name from mailing lists?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists#how_do_I_remove_my_name) | ****Is Mail Always Delivered as Addressed and Can it be Refused / Returned?**** The United States Postal Service® will deliver the mail as addressed, but it does not have to be accepted. **Without a specific reason to the contrary, mail sent to an address will be delivered.** Mail is delivered to residential or business addresses even if the [name on the mailpiece is different than the known residents.](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6eq0000000frBAAQ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2Fafl__afl_Article_Feedback__c%2Fa95eq0000000mFxAAI%2Fview) Mail addressed to a PO Box™ is delivered _only_ to recipients previously identified on _PS Form 1093:_ - This is filled out when the box was opened and names can be added later with the box holder’s permission. - Adults must show two forms of ID at the local Post Office™ facility to be added. - Minors can be added by their parent or guardian (with proper identification). If a person alleging to be the addressee of certain mail item is unknown to the delivery employee, the mail may be withheld pending [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) of the claimant. An addressee may request the postmaster, in writing, to withhold from delivery (for a period not to exceed 2 years) any foreign letter or publication material with a specified name or address on the outside. If the addressee wants to add extra services, such as certified to a mailpiece being returned to sender, they MUST pay return postage to mail the item back to the sender in addition to paying for the extra service. Mail addressed to a person or persons or business for which a Change of Address has been filed will, of course, be forwarded to the new delivery address. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists#top) **What are my Mail Refusal Options?** **Not everyone accepts every piece of mail that is delivered to their home, whether it is unsolicited advertising or other pieces. When that happens, Postal standards offer mail refusal options.** _Domestic Mail Manual (DMM)_, Sect. 604.8.1.2 and Sect. 507.1.8.2 provide two instances where mail can be refused when it is offered for delivery, or, after delivery. You may mark **“Refused”** and return it unopened within a reasonable time. If a mailpiece has been opened, even if it is been resealed, it CANNOT be marked **“Refused”** and returned. If someone wants to return that mailpiece to the sender, they _have to put it in a **new envelope or wrapper**_ with a correct address and new postage. There is some mail that CANNOT be refused after it has been delivered. Once again, two groups fit this category: - Pieces sent as Registered Mail, Insured, Certified Mail, and Collect on Delivery (COD). Postage must be paid by the customer for this mail to be returned. - Response mail to a sales promotion, solicitation, announcement, or other advertisement that was not refused when offered to the recipient. **\*Note**:  Customers may refuse **Accountable Mail** prior to signing for and receiving it. Simply check the box marked “Refused” on the back of the delivery notice, sign your name by the “X” and place it back in your mailbox. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refuse-unwanted-mail-and-remove-name-from-mailing-lists#top) **How do I remove my name from mailing lists?** Anyone who wants to reduce the amount of prospect marketing mail they receive may register for the mail suppression service called DMAchoice provided by the Association of National Advertisers (ANA), which is independent of the United States Postal Service. To **“Remove”** your name from common mailing lists, you may register, along with a nominal [processing fee](https://www.dmachoice.org/register.php): - **Visit** [www.dmachoice.org](https://www.dmachoice.org/register.php) **for online registration.** - **For mail-in registration** submit check or money order payable to ANA no cash, certified or registered mail: | | | --- | | **DMAchoice**
**Consumer Preferences**
**P.O. Box 900**
**Cos Cob, CT 06807** | This service will not eliminate all promotional mail. It does not apply to promotional mail from companies or organizations that consumers have an existing business relationship. It also does not apply to local retailers, restaurants, and political organizations. You should expect to see a major reduction in the overall volume of prospect promotional offers received. Please note that ANA is not the source of consumer names for marketing purposes. This opt-in mail suppression service is a tool to assist consumers in managing their marketing offers. The ANA has [additional information](https://www.ana.net/accountability) for consumers about compliance tools and best practices, you can also [file a complaint](https://www.ana.net/content/show/id/accountability-file-complaint), or [email ethics@ana.net](mailto:ethics@ana.net). In addition, the three major credit-reporting services (Equifax, Experian & TransUnion) also offer a name removal option for prescreened offers at: 1-888-5-OPT-OUT (1-888-567-8688) or visit [www.optoutprescreen.com](http://www.optoutprescreen.com/). ## What are Duck Stamps?

## The Bird Hunting Stamp is a license (permit) in the form of a stamp allowing the purchaser to hunt migratory birds.      - Jul 13, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007113

Customer Information

****What is the Duck Stamp for Hunting?****

Many state and federal laws regulate the hunting of migratory birds. The Bird Hunting Stamp (also known as a "Duck Stamp") is a license (permit) in the form of a stamp allowing the purchaser to hunt migratory birds. It is:

- Valid only for the current hunting season (check your local hunting information for details, including valid dates and legal hunting areas).
- Sold at many (but not all) postal facilities. The Duck Stamp is also available at locations other than Postal Facilities.
- **NOT** be used for postage.
- Often purchased by collectors (including expired stamps from prior years).
- Nonredeemable and all sales are final.

Some states require additional permits for hunting birds, as well as specific species of bird, locations, etc. You should contact local authorities for additional information. The Migratory-Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp is required with a valid hunting license in order to hunt migratory birds and ducks.

Ninety-eight cents of every dollar from the sales of Duck Stamps goes to the purchase of wetlands.

**Note**: You must have the actual signed Federal Duck Stamp on your person while hunting any migratory waterfowl. It is illegal to use a receipt for the purchase of the Federal Duck Stamp to hunt. Even in states where electronic licensing is used, you must have a signed Federal Duck Stamp.

****Who do I Contact for Further Information?****

- Regulations and hunting Information may be obtained from your local State Wildlife Departments. Information on the[Federal Duck Stamp Program](http://www.fws.gov/birds/get-involved/duck-stamp.php)can be found on the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service webpage.
- Information on permits can be found at [The U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service Permits](http://www.fws.gov/permits/).

****Where Can I Purchase a Duck Stamp?****

The U.S. Postal Service® carries the last three years of issued stamps. Duck Stamps may be purchased through any of the following way:

- Online through [The](https://store.usps.com/store/home)[Postal Store](https://store.usps.com/store/home)® on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®.
1. Under the ‘ _Collectors Zone, Product Type’_, select ‘ [Federal Duck Stamps](https://store.usps.com/store/results/federal-duck-stamps/collector-s-zone/_/N-zrkw3iZ1j49vgd)’ on the left hand menu.
2. **Important**: Make sure you verify the stamp(s) listed are the current year, and not a stamp for prior years (purchased as a collectible).
- By visiting a local Post Office™ location by using [PO Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm).
