Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000Uu9AAE%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Address-information-requests-forms) ## Address information requests forms ## Completing Address Information Requests for a Process Servers and Government agencies. - May 27, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006976 Customer Information **Release of address information for specific customers** Upon request, the names and addresses of specifically identified Postal Service customers will be made available only as follows: 1. General. Disclosures must be limited to the address of the specific individual about whom information is requested, not other family members or individuals whose name may appear on the change of address form. The address of an individual may be withheld to protect the individual’s personal safety. If an individual has filed for a protective order, the address may not be disclosed except pursuant to a court order and on the advice of counsel. 2. To persons serving legal process or another government agency, as [shown in Handbook AS 353](https://about.usps.com/policy/as353_pol.pdf), [section 5-2 Exhibit 5-2a](https://about.usps.com/policy/as353_pol.pdf#page=88). This includes persons empowered by law, the attorney for a party on whose behalf service is to be made, or a party who is acting pro se (the term pro se means that a party is self–represented, and is not represented by an attorney). A copy of PS Form 3575, Change-of-Address Order, or PS Form 1093, Application for Post Office Box or Caller Service, is not supplied to the requester. The USPS does not have a standard form for use when requesting address information. Requesters are encouraged to use the standard format shown in [Exhibit 5-2b](https://about.usps.com/policy/as353_pol.pdf#page=91) below. If the requester uses the standard format on its own letterhead, the standard format must be used in its entirety, and the warning statement and certification must appear immediately before the signature block. If the request lacks any of the required information or a proper signature, the custodian must return it to the requester specifying the deficiency. Requests via facsimile from process servers are acceptable. Each request must specify all of the following information: - A certification that the name or address is needed and will be used solely for service of legal process in connection with actual or prospective litigation. - A citation to the statute or regulation that empowers the requester to serve process, if the requester is anyone other than a party acting pro se or the attorney for a party for whom service will be made. - The names of all known parties to the litigation. - The court in which the case has been or will be commenced. - The docket or other identifying number, if one has been issued. - The capacity in which the individual is to be served (e.g., defendant or witness). **Note:** There is no fee for this service. **Address Information Request Format - Process Server** [Exhibit 5-2b](https://about.usps.com/policy/as353_pol.pdf#page=91) **Change of Address or Boxholder Request Format — Process Servers** | | | --- | | To: Postmaster Date\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ | | \\_\\_\\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ | | City, State, ZIP Code | | **REQUEST FOR CHANGE OF ADDRESS OR BOXHOLDER INFORMATION**
**NEEDED FOR SERVICE OF LEGAL PROCESS** | | Please furnish the new address or the name and street address (if a boxholder) for the following: | | Name:\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ | | Address:\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ | | **Note:** Only one request may be made per completed form. The name and last known address are required for change of address information. The name, if known, and Post Office box address are required for boxholder information. | | The following information is provided in accordance with 39 CFR 265.14(d). ## Delivery of mail with multiple addresses or recipients

## How dual-addressed mail is delivered.  Mail addressed either to two people or to a business that is now two or more entities may be in dispute if one party has filed a Change of Address (COA) to a different address.  It may also be in dispute if two or more parties have filed conflicting COA requests.      - May 27, 2020 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006972

Customer Information

**How is mail with two (2) addresses handled?**

The intended delivery address should always be placed on the line immediately above the city, state, and ZIP Code™ line. If necessary, the additional address should be placed on a line above the intended delivery address line.

When a mailpiece consists of both a street address and a Post Office™ Box address on two (2) lines, the United States Postal Service® priority will be to attempt delivery to the address immediately above the city, state, and ZIP Code.  The ZIP Code on the mailpiece should correspond to this address.

If a street address and a PO Box address are placed on the same line, the US Postal Service will attempt delivery to the PO Box address. In this example, the ZIP Code on the mailpiece should correspond to the PO Box address.

In the following example, the mail would be delivered to the PO Box address:

JOHN W DOE

222 WALL ST STE 500

PO BOX 985

NEW YORK NY 21212-2222

**How is disputed joint-addressed mail delivered?**

Mail is generally delivered or forwarded as addressed. Mail addressed either to two people or to a business that is now two or more entities may be in dispute if one party has filed a Change of Address (COA) to a different address. It may also be in dispute if two or more parties have filed conflicting COA requests. Mail in dispute is handled based on the following guidelines:

- If conflicting COAs are filed (two parties submit two different COAs) for the same mail and they cannot agree who should receive the mail, the mail may be delivered to a third party agreed to by the disputing parties.
- If the disputing parties (whether family or business) are unable to select a third party receiver, the United States Postal Service® may retain or return mail until the dispute has been settled.

For more information on resolving a dispute, please [contact your local Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action).

## How do I get Started with Bulk Mailing?

## Customers can prepare and send mail by themselves, with assistance of local and national business mail support vendors with the understanding of prepping and sending mail.      - Sep 5, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007650

Customer Information

You can prepare and send your mail yourself or with the help of a mail service provider. There are local and national business mail support vendors with the understanding of prepping and sending mail.

- USPS Direct Mail Services
  - Click2Mail ( [http://www.click2mail.com/](http://www.click2mail.com/))
  - Premium Postcards ( [http://www.premiumpostcard.com](http://www.premiumpostcard.com/))
  - CardStore ( [http://www.cardstore.com/](http://www.cardstore.com/))
- Local Mail Service Providers
  - You can find a local provider through: [https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action)
- **Presort software** is special computer software that presorts your addresses, figures out how many mailpieces will qualify for which postage prices, and prints out a completed postage statement. For more information, please refer to the National Customer Support Center at 1-800-238-3150.

**To prepare your mail yourself:**

For complete information on fees and other business mail resources, please visit[http://pe.usps.com/businessmail101](http://pe.usps.com/businessmail101)

- Choose how you want to pay for your postage.
  - Postage Meter
  - Permit Imprint
  - Precanceled Stamps
- You can obtain a permit by either applying online through the[Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/), if applicable, or completing[PS Form 3615, Mailing Permit Application and Customer Profile](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3615.pdf), available from your local business mail entry unit or Post Office®.
- Take your completed Form 3615 to your local Business Mail Entry Unit (BMEU) or Post Office. You will need 2 forms of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) (one must contain a photograph) as well as the completed form. You will not be required to pay the annual fee when you submit your form, but may instead wait until you send your first mailing.
- The Postal Service will give you a permit number and a receipt. You’ll need your permit number for each mailing you complete.
- When you’re ready to send your first mailing, be sure to bring the following:
  - All of your mail (count your containers).
  - Complete and send your online postage statement electronically, or bring your signed and dated postage statement(s).
  - Supporting documentation (your tally sheets or printouts from presort software)
  - Check or cash to cover additional postage due (if your advance deposit account will not cover the cost of your mailing, or if you've put [precanceled stamp](https://pe.usps.com/BusinessMail101?ViewName=PrecanceledStamps) or [metered or PC Postage](https://pe.usps.com/BusinessMail101?ViewName=PostageTips) indicia stamps on your mail). For precanceled stamps or metered mailings, you can affix a meter strip to your postage statement to pay for the additional postage.
  - A check for your annual mailing fee (if you haven't already paid it).

**What the USPS will do when you arrive with your business mail shipment**

- The acceptance clerk checks to make sure that you’ve prepared your mail properly.
- Eligibility of contents \[for USPS Marketing Mail™ (formerly Standard Mail®), the clerk will actually open one of your mailpieces to check that the contents qualify for the rate you’re claiming\]
- Markings and endorsements
- Sortation
- Postage payment.
  - If you’re paying with permit imprint, the clerk will check to see that there’s enough money in your advance deposit account to cover the cost of your mailing.
  - In some instances, there may be problems with your mail. If your mail is not presorted correctly, the clerk will point the problems out to you and tell you how to fix the sortation.
  - If there are problems with the characteristics of your mailpiece, you might have to pay additional fees or surcharges.

**Accurate Addressing**

- One way you can better ensure your mailpieces arrive at the correct destination is by buying or renting Mailing Lists.
  - **The Postal Service does not keep a database of residential or business customers and does not sell address lists**. However, the Postal Service has products and services that help mailers check and standardize their addresses (for instance, whether 123 MAIN is a ST, AVE, or DR) and keep their lists up to date (for example, providing ancillary services to let mailers know if addressees have moved).
  - There are companies that collect and sell information about consumers and businesses. You can buy or rent a mailing list from those companies. Look in the yellow pages under "lists" or "mailing lists" or do an online search for list providers in your area.
  - Just because you buy or rent a list does not mean that the addresses are correct, checked, updated, or "certified." To make sure that you are getting a list with accurate addresses, ask about the National Deliverability Index (NDI) of the list. The NDI rates the percentage of addresses in a list that are deliverable. Use the NDI to target the address elements you need to get the delivery —- and postage price —- you want. For example, if you are targeting a specific 5-digit ZIP Code®, look for an NDI of 100% for address element.

- The Postal Service does offer ways to check the accuracy of your list. Vendors also offer USPS-approved products services for address list maintenance.
  - Use[www.usps.com](http://www.usps.com/)to check the ZIP Codes in your list
  - Address Information Systems (AIS) Database Products- Products to enhance address standardization
    - AIS products range from providing accurate ZIP Codes to providing mailers the ability to sort their mailings in approximate carrier-casing sequence.
    - Products are available on cartridge, CD-ROM and DVD. Customers who want to copy, in part or whole, or redistribute any AIS product must pay a licensee fee, which constitutes permission to duplicate a USPS® product.
  - Address Management Products - Tools for addressing software solutions
    - Products range from identifying incorrect addresses to providing and updating delivery sequence address information by carrier route.
    - Must qualify for some of these products
  - Address Management Services - Evaluation, grading and certification services.
  - Address Management Publications - Publications related to Address Management products and services.
  - Addressing Standards for Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
  - ZIP Code® Lookup and Address Information
  - Contact Address Management

## What is Open and Distribute Service?

## General information about Open and Distribute Service.      - Jul 13, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007484

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[**General Information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#general_information)** | **[**Barcodes, Container Tags and Labels**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#barcodes_tags_labels)** | **[**Postage and Payments**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#postage_payments)** |
| **[**Mail Classes, Guarantees, and Extra Services**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#mail_class_guarantees_extra_services)** | [****Mailing and Delivery****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#mailing_delivery) |  |

**General Information**

**What is Open and Distribute Service?**

**Open and Distribute Service** provides an easy alternative to mailer drop shipments for customers who want to expedite their mailings of certain mail classes to domestic destination postal facilities. The shipment receives Priority Mail Express and Priority Mail service between the acceptance BMEU or Associate Post Office® location with acceptance function and the destination facility. The enclosed mail is processed and provided the appropriate service from the mail processing facility to the destination delivery unit.

**Are any forms required for Open and Distribute Service?**

**Yes.** A PS Form 3152-E is required for Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute® (EMOD) containers and a _PS Form 3152_ is required for Priority Mail Open and Distribute® (PMOD) containers. A postage statement is necessary for postage payment for both the Priority Mail Express and Priority Mail Open and Distribute containers.

**Barcodes, Container Tags, and Labels**

**Is a barcode required for the container tag or label?**

**Yes.** An Open and Distribute barcode must be used on every container. The service barcode label must be placed either on the tag, the tray box, the Flat Rate box or envelope or the pallet. When the service barcode is scanned, it will provide the customer with date, ZIP Code™, the time that the container was accepted at the BMEU or Associate Post Office® location with acceptance function and the time that the container was received at the destination facility.

**Types of Tags or Labels**

- **PRIORITY MAIL EXPRESS OPEN AND DISTRIBUTE®**
  - **Blue Tag 257 (or Blue Tag 257-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute sacks or tray boxes going to Destination Delivery Units (DDUs) and contains an address label with a service barcode.
  - **Blue Label 257S (or Blue Label 257S-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute flat rate envelopes going to Destination Delivery Units (DDUs). An address label with a service barcode is affixed to the flat rate envelope.
  - **Yellow Tag 267 (o Yellow Tag 267-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute sacks or tray boxes going to all mail processing facilities and contains an address label with a service barcode.

- **PRIORITY MAIL OPEN AND DISTRIBUTE®**
  - **Pink Tag 190 (or Pink Tag 190-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Open and Distribute sacks, pallets, or tray boxes going to Destination Delivery Units and contains an address label with a service barcode.
  - **Pink Label 190S (or Pink Label 190S-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Open and Distribute flat rate envelopes and boxes going to Destination Delivery Units. An address label with a service barcode is affixed to the flat rate envelope of box.
  - **Green Tag 161 (or Green Tag 161-EVS)** is used on Priority Mail Open and Distribute sacks, pallets, or tray boxes going to all Destination Mail Processing Facilities and contains an address label with a service barcode.
- **Note**: The eVS / USPS® Ship version of the tags and labels will not require a postmark or signature during the acceptance and verification process. Instead, the eVS tags / USPS® Ship and labels will bear the marking "APPROVED eVS MAILER" in the space normally designated for postmark and signature.

**How can customers order the tags, labels and tray boxes?**

Tags, labels and tray boxes can be ordered by contacting the Expedited Package Supply Center at **1-800-610-8734.**

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#top)

**Postage and Payments**

**How is postage calculated for Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute® or Priority Mail Open and Distribute®?**

- Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute Postage is based on the zone and weight of the contents of the Open and Distribute shipment, excluding the external container's tare weight.
- Except as provided above, Priority Mail Open and Distribute is based on the weight of the contents of the Open and Distribute shipment, excluding the container's tare weight.
  - The minimum weight required for Priority Mail Open and Distribute sacks is 5 pounds, except for Open and Distribute sacks that contain qualified trays (trays prepared under the standards for the applicable class of mail).
- **Note**:

  - The maximum weight for each container is 70 pounds.
  - If applicable to the class of mail, zone pricing is based on the zone of the accepting Post Office® location to the zone of the container's destination facility, not the enclosed letter's destination Post Office® location.

**How is postage paid for the mailing?**

Postage on the enclosed mail may be paid with any method permitted for the mail class except for ordinary postage stamps requiring cancellation. Mail requiring cancellation may not be enclosed. Evidence of payment for the Open and Distribute container must be affixed to each individual tag, label, or the Open and Distribute tray box, with a permit imprint through a manifest mailing system or via meter postage. The mailing must be accepted at the BMEU or an acceptance location designated by Postmaster. Postage cannot be purchased at the Post Office® location.

**Mail Classes, Guarantees, and Extra Services**

**Is the mail enclosed in the container required to meet corresponding eligibility standards?**

**Yes**. Mail enclosed in the container is required to meet all corresponding eligibility and preparation standards for the mail class for which postage has been paid. These standards do not extend to hazardous materials: mail enclosed in a Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail Open-and-Distribute container may not contain hazardous materials.

**Does Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute® have a service guarantee?**

**Yes**. Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute has a service guarantee by end of day of the scheduled delivery day.

**Can extra services be added to the Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute® or the Priority Mail Open and Distribute® shipment?**

**No**. There are no authorized extra services for the Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail Open and Distribute container.

**How does a mailer determine where the shipment will be processed?**

Mailers are directed to the Facility Access and Shipment Tracking System (FAST) at [https://fast.usps.com/fast](https://fast.usps.com/fast "USPS - Facility Access and Shipment Tracking (FAST) - Welcome to FAST"). For facility address information, click on "Reports", "Mail Direction Search", "Drop Entry Point View."

**Mailing and Delivery**

**Where Can Open and Distribute Shipments be Presented?**

Mailers are required to present Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute® or Priority Mail Open and Distribute® shipments at a Business Mail Entry Unit or acceptance location designated by Postmaster.

**What are the approved destination facilities that Open and Distribute can be mailed to?**

Priority Mail Express Open and Distribute and Priority Mail Open and Distribute can be mailed to a Destination Delivery Unit (DDU), Sectional Center Facility (SCF), Area Distribution Center (ADC), Network Distribution Center (NDC) or, Auxiliary Service Facility (ASF).

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service#top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000001RIYAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/ZIP-Code-The-Basics) ## ZIP Code™ - The Basics ## ZIP Codes™ are an integral part of making sure the mailpiece is processed quickly and efficiently. Not having a ZIP Code™ on your mail causes unnecessary delays because it will require manual handling. - Sep 28, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007258 Customer Information **Need to look up a ZIP Code™?**Try the[ZIP Code**™** lookup tool](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/). **Note:** The [ZIP Code](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm) [**™** lookup tool](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm) will display the address in USPS standardized format. This may look different than the street address information that you entered. It is recommended to use the USPS standardized street address format when addressing mailpieces to ensure that they are processed smoothly by mail sorting equipment without any delays. | General questions about ZIP Codes or ZIP+4 | | :-: | | **Why are ZIP Codes™ necessary?** | Mailpieces with a ZIP Code are processed quickly and efficiently. Alleviates possible delays due to manual handling. | | **What are the parts of a ZIP™ Code?** | The nine digits of a ZIP+4 code (e.g., **12345-6789)** may be grouped as follows: \[ **_123_**\] \[ **_45_**\] \[ **_67_**\] \[ **_89_**\]
- \[ **_123_**\] :  Sectional Center or Large City
- \[ **_45_**\] : Post Office facility or Delivery Area
- \[ \- \] : The required "dash" or "hyphen" separates the first five digits from the last four digits; the +4
- \[ **_67_**\] : Sector or Several Blocks
- \[ **_89_**\] : Segment or One Side of a Street | | **Is the hyphen in a ZIP+4® ZIP Code™ number required?** | Yes, when using a ZIP+4 ZIP Code, the number must consist of five digits, a hyphen (or dash), and four digits. Improperly labeled mailpieces may progress slower through the mail system and may be returned to sender if the United States Postal Service® is unable to deliver the item. | | **ZIP Code History** | Visit: website [Publication 100 - The United States Postal Service - An American History 1775 - 2006  for _History of the United States Postal Service®_](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub100.pdf) | | **Are ZIP Codes™ 5-digit or ZIP+4 required?** | Required in the return address of all mail on which postage is paid with precanceled stamps or company permit imprint, and in the sender's return address on Periodicals mail when "Address Service Requested" is specified. 
Official mail (penalty mail) also requires a ZIP Code™ in the return address. | | **Can I access a database or directory of all ZIP Code™, ZIP+4 and Post Office™?** | This valuable information is now available in the _**Address Information System Viewer (AIS)**_. The AIS Viewer is an interactive application designed to provide the ability to retrieve, view, and print accurate and current ZIP Code information for all 50 states on request.
More information on the AIS View, go to [https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality/ais-viewer](https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality/ais-viewer "Address Information System (AIS) Viewer | PostalPro") or call the National Customer Support Center at 1-800-238-3150 and select “Option 6” followed by “Option 2” to speak with a customer service representative. | | **What is a ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® Status / Type?**
**Note:** City names should be completely and legibly written out to help the Postal Service process and deliver items more efficiently. | " **Status**" Definitions:
- **Acceptable**: Status of Acceptable (Default) means an adequate city name has been entered or received based on information entered. City names that are abbreviated will frequently be listed as unacceptable because abbreviations are not an acceptable city name format.   
- **Not Acceptable** (or Unacceptable): Means an inadequate city name has been entered or received based on information entered.
- **Unique**: Assigned to a company, government agency, or entity with sufficient mail volume, based on average daily volume of letter size mail received, availability of ZIP Code numbers in the postal area, and USPS cost-benefit analysis. 
**"** **Type**" Definition:
**Standard**: It is the established and usual ZIP Code used in a specified area. | | **How do I get a Unique ZIP+4®?**
The ZIP+4® Code assigned by the Postal Service™ is unique for the category of Reply Mail you use.  This unique ZIP+4 code enables Reply Mail to be sorted on postal automated equipment by specific size and weight (i.e., cards, 1oz. letters, 2 oz. letters, etc.). | 1. Register your company and authorized users using Customer Registration.
2. Select the “Add ZIP+4 Code” option within the online Business Reply Mail (BRM) tool (accessible after clicking "Automated Business Reply Mail" upon logging on to the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin)).
3. Select the media type that you intend to use.
4. Provide complete delivery information for the BRM mailpieces that will be returned.
5. Submit your request.
6. The standardized delivery information (with the assigned unique ZIP+4®) will be sent to you for use on your mail piece. | | **Will a ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® for a PO Box™ change?** | ZIP Codes for PO Boxes within the same city usually do not change. Please note that the ZIP+4 Code will likely include the actual PO Box number in the +4 part of the ZIP Code.
For further information on how ZIP Codes are assigned to PO Boxes, please contact your local Post Office™. | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/ZIP-Code-The-Basics#top) | Frequently asked questions | | :-: | | **Concern** | **Action** | **Solution** | | **Street name is misspelled in the ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® Look-up tool on usps.com.** | How can this problem be fixed? | Contact your local Post Office and request their assistance in resolving the problem. All information in our national ZIP Code database is maintained by the Address Management System (AMS) Office and request the street name to be corrected. | | **The ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® where I live changed recently.**
Due to an increase in population or to the improve postal operations, the US Postal Service® will occasionally add a new ZIP Code or change ZIP Code boundaries. | **ZIP Code lookup on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) not showing this change?** | The reason why a change isn’t showing may be the national site hasn’t been updated yet by the local Address Management System (AMS) office.  Any changes, corrections, or updates to the national database will be reflected in the ZIP Code lookup tool on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) within 30 to 60 days. | | **Where can I find an international/foreign ZIP Code™(Postal Code)?** | The U.S. Postal Service® does not keep information on Foreign Postal Codes (similar to the ZIP Code used in the USA). | For the Postal Service to deliver mail to a foreign country, we only need the name of the destination country. For postal code information for a foreign country, it is recommended that you contact the country in question or look for further information about that country's postal codes on the internet. | | **Why will the ZIP Code™ Look-up tool not acknowledge the address that I entered?** | Address may have been entered incorrectly, is an invalid address, or it may be a new construction address or a new housing development.  Verify that the address you have is correct. | Enter it in the [ZIP Code™ lookup tool](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm).
If you still do not get a response and the address is a valid mailing address, contact the [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) and request their assistance in resolving the problem. | | **ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® does not match City/State.** | In cases where mail has a street / city / state of one place but the ZIP Code™ area does not match, it is difficult to determine which Post Office™ is the actual office of destination. | The U.S Postal Service® dispatches mail sent in this manner to the Post Office indicated by the ZIP Code for possible delivery.
If undeliverable there, **it is forwarded** to the Post Office as stated by city and state. | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/ZIP-Code-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title ZIP Code™ - The Basics URL Name ZIP-Code-The-Basics [ZIP Codes](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG6GAM/zip-codes) ## Related Articles - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading ZIP Code™ - The Basics

## What Options Do I Have Regarding Unwanted / Unsolicited Mail?

## Regulation About Unwanted and Unsolicited Mail.      - Aug 13, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007685

Customer Information

**Do I Have to Return Unsolicited Merchandise Received in the Mail?**

If a company sends you a gift in the mail, but you did not request it, **the item is yours**, and you are under **no obligation to pay anything** (regardless of the mail class).

You, the consumer, may only legally be sent two types of merchandise through the mail without your consent or agreement:

- Free samples which are clearly and conspicuously marked as such.
- Merchandise mailed by a charitable organization that is soliciting contributions.

And in these two cases, you can consider the merchandise a gift if you wish. In all other situations, **it is illegal to send merchandise to someone, unless that person has previously purchased or requested it.**

If you do not wish to pay for **unsolicited** merchandise or make a donation to a charity sending such an item, you may do one of three things (in each case, by law, you have no obligation to the sender):

- If you have not opened the mailpiece, you may mark it **"Return to Sender,"** and the United States Postal Service will return it with no additional charge to you.
- If you open the mailpiece and do not like what you find, you may throw it away.
- If you open the mailpiece and like what you find, you may keep it for free. In this instance, "finders-keepers" applies unconditionally.
  - Furthermore, it is illegal for a company that sends you unordered merchandise to follow the mailing with a bill or dunning communication.

If you are aware of violations of the federal law prohibiting the mailing of unordered merchandise, or if you have personally had difficulty with such items--especially if you are sent statements insisting on payment for the merchandise--you should contact you local postmaster or the nearest Postal Inspector.

**Note**: These rules are codified in Title 39, United States Code, Sect. 300.

- **Receiving unsolicited merchandise through the mail may be an indication of a larger mailing scam commonly known as a "Brushing Scam". For additional information on identifying and reporting Brushing Scams and other common mailing scams please see [Scams & Scheme Alerts](https://usps.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/Scams-Scheme-Alerts).**

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Options-Do-I-Have-Regarding-Unwanted-Unsolicited-Mail#top)

**How Do I Stop Receipt of Unsolicited "Obscene" Mail?**

You may file [PS Form 1500](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps1500.pdf "PS Form 1500") at a local Post Office to prevent receipt of unwanted obscene materials in the mail or to stop receipt of "obscene" materials in the mail. The Post Office offers two programs to help you protect yourself (and your eligible minor children).

- **Option 1: Application for Prohibitory Order**(requires the objectionable mailpiece)

Under the Pandering Advertisements Statute, 39 USC 3008, **if you are the addressee of an advertisement, and consider the matter (product or service) that it offers for sale to be "erotically arousing or sexually provocative," you can obtain a Prohibitory Order against the mailer.** You apply for the order by submitting the entire advertising mailpiece (the original, NOT a photocopy) with a properly completed application. Minor children under 19 years old and residing with you may be included in the application.

  - This Statute provides a **deterrent to continued mailings by a specific mailer** advertising a product or service you consider erotically arousing or sexually provocative.
  - You are required to save all materials, including the envelope and the entire mailpiece and then attach the entire opened mailpiece to PS Form 1500.
- **Option 2: Application for Listing** (does not require a mailpiece)

Under the Sexually Oriented Advertisements Statute, 39 USC 3010, the United States Postal Service maintains a list of persons who have informed us they do not wish to receive sexually oriented advertisements in their mail. When informing the Postal Service, such persons may also have their minor children under 19 years of age, who are residing with them or are under their care, custody, or supervision, included on the list.

  - Provides a **deterrent to the mailing of ANY unsolicited sexually oriented advertisements** to protected persons.

**Note:**  If you have any further questions regarding this process, please contact the Local Post Office.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Options-Do-I-Have-Regarding-Unwanted-Unsolicited-Mail#top)

## Change of Address Refund Request

## How can a customer request a refund for the Change of Address authentication fee.      - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006167

Customer Information

- The purpose of the $1.25 credit card authentication fee is to verify the customer's identity, which allows the Change of Address to be submitted online
- There are **No Refunds** for the $1.25 authentication fee
- For more information call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)
  - **If a third party website attempted to submit a Change of Address on your behalf, contact that company. Effective 5/31/23, the Postal Service no longer accepts third party COA submissions.**

**Note**: If you are charged more than $1.25, please refer back to the website where you entered your COA or dispute the charge with your credit card company.

## Self-Service Kiosks

## Self-service kiosks provide customers a convenient alternative to the full-service counter.      - Jul 30, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007104

Customer Information

USPS Self-Service Kiosks offer many of the services available at a Post Office™ location retail counter. You can:

- Buy stamps
- Weigh packages
- Print and pay for shipping labels
- Look up a ZIP Code™
- Renew and pay PO Box™ renewal fees.
- Schedule a first-time passport application appointment at a local Post Office™ facility that accepts passport appointments.
- [Print a Label Broker Label](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm#kiosk)
- And more!

**To locate a self-service kiosk:**

- Go to the [Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/locations/home.htm?locationType=SSK "USPS.com Post Office Locator") on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®
- Enter your city, state or ZIP Code™
- Select _Search_

## Response to FOIA (Freedom of Information Act) request

## Answers to a few questions on submitting a FOIA request and links to pages with additional information on FOIA requests.      - Jul 23, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007837

Customer Information

The Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) is a federal law that provides the public with a right to access government records. FOIA provides that any person may ask any federal agency for records and that the agency must provide those records, unless an exemption applies or the records fall outside the coverage of the FOIA.

**For information about filing a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, please visit** [**https://about.usps.com/who/legal/foia/welcome.htm.**](https://about.usps.com/who/legal/foia/welcome.htm)

**When Can I Expect a Response From My FOIA Request?**

The FOIA states that an agency will respond in writing within 20 working days of receipt of a request. If a request is sent to the wrong facility, the number of days it takes to receive a response does not begin until the request is received by the custodian of the records. There are unusual circumstances in which the agency may extend the response period for another ten working days. These include requests that require a search for records from a facility other than the one processing the request, requests that require the search for and review of a large volume of records, and requests that require consultation with another agency or with other agency components having an interest in disclosure.

**Under What Circumstances May I Receive Expedited Processing for a FOIA Request?**

A FOIA request will receive expedited processing under two conditions only.

1. In one instance, the requestor must demonstrate that the failure to expeditiously obtain the records could reasonably be expected to pose an imminent threat to the life or physical safety of an individual.

2. In the other instance, the requester must demonstrate that he or she is primarily engaged in disseminating information to the public and that the requested information is urgently needed to inform the public about actual or alleged government activity.

**When Can Records Be Withheld From a FOIA Request?**

The FOIA requires the release of any requested agency record unless it is protected from disclosure by an exemption in the Act. It is the policy of the United States Postal Service to make its official records available to the public to the maximum extent consistent with the public interest. If records or parts of records are withheld, you have a right to appeal the denial of your request by writing to the Postal Service's General Counsel within 90 days of the date of the denial letter. The letter of appeal should include statements concerning the denial, the reasons why it is believed to be erroneous, and the relief sought, along with copies of the original request, the denial letter, and any other related correspondence. Submit the appeal letter to:

GENERAL COUNSEL

U.S. POSTAL SERVICE

475 L'ENFANT PLAZA SW, ROOM 6004

WASHINGTON DC 20260-1100

**Do you have more questions on the Freedom of Information Act or on how to submit a FOIA request?**

- [Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) FAQs](https://about.usps.com/who-we-are/foia/faq.htm) may have the question and answer that you're looking for.
- More detailed information can be found on the [Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) page on https://about.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/who-we-are/foia/welcome.htm).

## Parcel Select®

## Parcel Select® Service is a shipping solution designed for high volume shippers to enter packages for regional delivery through the United States Postal Service’s network.      - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006806

Customer Information

Parcel Select® service has the following features:

- 2-8 day delivery standard.
- Destination Delivery Entry prices for high volume shippers.
- USPS Tracking® included at no additional cost.
- No fuel, residential or Saturday surcharges.
- Delivery to every US address, including PO boxes and military addresses.
- Packages can weigh up to 70lbs and measure up to 130 inches in combined length and girth (distance around the thickest part).

**Prices for Parcel Select®**

Shape, weight, and size affect the price of packages classified under Parcel Select. If you have a package with volume exceeding 1 cubic foot (1,728 cubic inches) postage may be based on the actual weight or the [dimensional weight](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards), whichever is greater. Dimensional weight pricing applies to Parcel Select Destination Entry packages for all entries:

- Destination Delivery Unit (DDU)
- Destination Hub (DHub)
- Destination Sectional Center Facility (DSCF)
- USPS Connect™ Local

For very large packages, [oversized pricing](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) may apply if the total length and girth is over 108 inches. Parcels over 130 inches in combined length and girth (distance around the thickest part) are not shippable. See [Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) for additional information.

For postage prices, [click here](http://www.usps.com/tools/calculatepostage/welcome.htm?from=home&page=0061calculatepostage) to visit the Postage Price Calculator on USPS.com® or use [The Price List](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c139 "The Price List Notice 123").

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Select#top)

## What is a Permit Imprint?

## Description of a Permit Imprint.      - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007581

Customer Information

_**Permit Imprints**_ are a way to pay for business (also known as discount or bulk) postage. It is one of three business mail payment options, the other two being meters and precanceled stamps. Permit imprints are the only postage option that can ONLY be used for business mailing.

**Who should use Permit Imprint?**

Permit Imprint service is best for customers who ship large volumes of mail. It is suited for mailers who are able to deposit their mail at the same location, or few locations, because an application, which requires a [fee for select mail classes](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198 "Permit Imprint Fees and Return Services Fees"), is needed at each location. Permit Imprints can be used for the following classes of business mailings:

- Standard
- Package Services
- International Mail

**Benefits of Permit Imprint**

- Avoid meter maintenance or leasing fees (pay, when applicable to the mail class, the [one-time permit imprint application fee and a separate annual mailing fee](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198 "Permit Imprint Fees and Return Services Fees"))
- Print your imprint with minimal cost displayed on your mail piece
- Pay through your advance deposit account at the time of mailing
- Save time by eliminating the need to affix postage to each mail piece

**Restrictions of Permit Imprint**

- All mailpieces must weigh the same and be presorted (exception: single-piece First-Class Mail®)
- Must mail every 2 years to keep the permit active
- Mailpieces must be taken to the Post Office™ where you hold your permit
- To qualify for the permit imprint discount, you must mail a minimum number of pieces:
  - 50 pieces for Parcel Select items
  - 200 pieces (or 50 lbs.) for USPS Marketing Mail™ items
  - 300 pieces for Presorted Bound Printed Matter
  - 500 pieces for Presorted First-Class Mail items (exception: 200 pieces for single-piece First-Class Mail®)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000dSnAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter) ## What is a postage meter? ## Description of a postage meter, how it works and how to use it for business mailings, leasing a meter, and postage refund eligibility. - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007603 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**What is a postage meter?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#what_is_a_postage_meter) | [**How do I use a postage meter for business mailings?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_business_mailings) | | [**How do I lease a postage meter?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#lease_a_postage_meter) | [**Is postage meter postage eligible for a refund?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund) | **What is a postage meter?** Postage meters are postage printing machines or systems leased by authorized providers for use in your home or office. Meters print postage directly onto your mail or onto a meter tape, which you affix to your mail. Postage meters can be used for all mail services except periodicals. - Metered mail must have the actual date of mailing. Metered mail does not require a postmark as the postmark information (city, state, ZIP Code, and date) is included within the meter strip. - To use a postage meter, you must apply for a permit in the city of mailing by completing [PS Form 3615 USPS Mailing Permit Application](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3615.pdf?_gl=1*1jts4q3*_gcl_au*OTMyMjg1MjIuMTY4MjAwNjE1NA..*_ga*MTkwOTc2MDUxMi4xNjU4MjQ1NTAz*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY4NzQ0NTIwNy40OC4xLjE2ODc0NDY3NjguMC4wLjA.) See our [Postage Options page](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm) for a list of authorized meter vendors and other postage options. **What can you send with a postage meter?** **Your postage meter provides you with a convenient, easy way to print postage for all mail services except periodicals:** - [First-Class Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/first-class-mail.htm) \- Our standard service, fast and reliable. - [Priority Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm) (including Flat Rates) - Fast and affordable service that averages 2-3 days for delivery. - [Priority Mail Express®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm)  (including Flat Rates) - Delivered to most locations overnight. - [Media Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm) and Library Mail - A variety of ways to ship packages. - [USPS Ground Advantage®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Ground-Advantage) Retail and Commercial - [International Mail](https://www.usps.com/international/preparing-international-shipments.htm) \- Global delivery services for mailing or shipping. - [Presorted First-Class Mail Services](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm) \- Send 500 pieces or more and receive discounts. - [Presorted USPS Marketing Mail® (including Nonprofit)](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) **Can I pay for Extra Services with my postage meter?** **Yes,** you can add the necessary additional postage for most Extra Services (such as USPS Tracking® and Certified Mail®). However, you still may need to complete and take the necessary forms to the Post Office™ location to ensure you receive the service that you pay for. For example, Registered Mail® items and insurance require you to present your letter or parcel to a Post Office clerk. Likewise, all presorted mailings must be accompanied by a postage statement and brought to a Post Office clerk. **Postage Meters & Bulk Mailing** Although you can use the same postage meter for all of your mail, you need to be aware of a few important details to use it for bulk mailings: - You must [apply for a permit](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm) for bulk mailings. - There are special markings required for bulk mailings that can be applied with your Meter stamp. For more information, please consult [Using Postage Meters for Bulk Mail](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm). **Who should use postage meters?** Meter services are designed for customers who ship medium to high volumes of letters/flats or a mixture of letters and small packages. Many standard meters accommodate packages 2-5lbs, however more advanced meters are available that accommodate larger packages. Meters allow you to print postage and track costs from the home or office. **Benefits** - Paying postage with a postage meter makes you eligible for the applicable discounted Commercial Base rate of postage for PMEI, PMI, or FCPIS®. (Items with stamps for postage or brought to a retail counter for postage must pay the retail rate of postage.) - Eligible for the [lower Metered Mail price](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c037) for 1, 2, 3, and 3.5 oz mail. - Reduced number of trips to the Post Office™ due to printing postage from home/office. - Provide exact postage with built in scale. Note: Not all meters can handle large packages. Work with a meter manufacturer to select the meter that's best for your mailing needs. - Professional look on mail pieces. - Track overall mailing costs - Postage meters deduct postage costs from one, pre-funded account. - Postage calculations are current – Postage meters can download price changes in minutes. - Send per-piece or large volume mailings – the same meter can be used if your business sends per-piece and large volume mailings. **Postage Meter Options** - Postage meters come in all sizes. Some specialized meters fold, stuff, weigh, and meter postage onto envelopes. Some meters are small and require each mailpiece to be hand-fed. A meter manufacturer can help you decide which meter is right for your mailing needs. **Note:**Customers wanting information on upgrading their postage meter should contact their meter provider. **Common issues that arise with the use of a postage meter:** - **Stale Meter Date** A meter stamp must bear the current date. If the postage bears a previous or future date, the meter stamp is considered "stale." - The meter date must be updated daily. Mail must be metered with the actual date of mailing. - Mail deposited after the day's last scheduled collection may bear the actual date of deposit or the date of the next scheduled collection. - A customer who has mail with stale meter dates, but correct postage, may run the mail piece through again to produce meter indicia with the correct date but $0.00 postage. - This correction may be run on the reverse side of the envelope (upper right corner). For flats and parcels the correction should be made next to the existing meter stamp. **If you have questions regarding operation or meter settings, please ask your vendor for assistance.** **Note:** The United States Postal Service® examines metered mail to detect irregularities in preparation and dating. If stale dated metered mail is identified by a postal employee (machines do not catch stale meter dates) the mail piece(s) will be canceled with the correct date. If the mailer submits a large amount of mail with stale dates, the USPS will return the mail to the mailer for correction before it will be accepted. - **Depositing Mail with Metered Postage** Metered Priority Mail Express® Flat Rate service, Priority Mail® Flat Rate service, single-piece price First-Class Mail® and First-Class Package Service-Retail®, single-piece price Media Mail® and single-piece price Library Mail with metered or PC Postage indicia may be deposited at any postal facility, preferably within the area of the customer's local Post Office™. All mail not specified above must be deposited at the authorized mailing office (for presort price mail) or at a postal facility within the ZIP Code™ on which the price was based. - **Correcting Mistakes** **You cannot simply erase errors made once you have printed metered mail postage. You need to correct them, depending on the type of the error:** | | | | --- | --- | | _**Postage printed on the back (versus the front) of a letter**_ | - Do NOT attempt to mail the incorrect item.
- Print a new meter stamp with the correct information (your account will be charged for the second item).
- Apply for a [Refund](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund). | | _**Only portion of the postage printed or postage not legible**_ | - Do NOT attempt to mail the incorrect item.
- Print a new meter stamp (your account will be charged for the second item).
- Apply for a [Refund](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund). | | _**Too much postage on the item**_ | - If you mail an item for which you have overpaid postage, you will not be able to apply for a [refund](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund); otherwise:
- Print a new meter stamp with the correct information (your account will be charged for the second item).
- Apply for a [Refund](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund). | | _**Too little postage on the item**_ | - Create a new meter stamp for the difference in the postage (amount due minus amount printed).
- For a letter, print this on the back (upper right corner).
- For a flat or parcel, add the new meter strip on the front underneath the insufficient postage strip.
- Do NOT include a date on the new meter stamp. | | _**Incorrect date on the meter**_ | - Create a new meter stamp of $0.00 (but with the correct date).
- For a letter, print this on the back. (upper right corner).
- For a flat or parcel, add the new meter strip on the front under the incorrectly dated meter stamp. | | _**_**I**_ ncorrect address or Any Other Reason for which you choose not to mail the metered mail**_ | - Do NOT attempt to mail the incorrect item.
- Print a new meter stamp with the correct information (your account will be charged for the second item).
- Apply for a [Refund](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#postage_meter_postage_eligible_refund). | - **Adding Postage** - Customers should contact their meter provider to add postage to their meter. The United States Postal Service no longer permits the use of manual postage meters. All approved postage meters are remotely reset by dial up or Internet connection with the meter provider's electronic resetting system. - All postage meters can be electronically reset. Your resetting procedures will be set up by your meter manufacturer or dealer when you initially set up your postage meter account. - For additional meter strips/tape/labels please contact your meter provider. - **T** **echnical Problems** - Please contact your meter manufacturer. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#top) **How do I use a postage meter for business mailings?** - A permit is required to obtain price incentives for work sharing on business mailings. - Customers must apply for a permit in the city from which they are mailing. - There is an annual presort mailing fee (by class of mail) for business mailings – Check with your Local Post Office™ for details. - Metered presorted business mail must be taken to the Post Office where you hold a permit and cannot be deposited in a collection box or handed to your letter carrier. - There are special markings required for business mailings that can be applied with your Meter stamp. **To get started:** 1. **Contact your postage meter manufacturer.** - In many cases, the meter manufacturer will take care of your meter application for you. Before you begin, contact a meter manufacturer for pricing and available services. - Tell the meter manufacturer that you'll be doing business mailings and if you are authorized to mail at reduced nonprofit prices. Your meter manufacturer will install your meter for you and will "pre-program" the meter with an amount of postage that you purchase. 2. **If...** - **If you already hold a mailing permit**(meaning you've already filled out a PS Form 3615) to pay using a meter or permit imprint, you will only need to amend your existing form and do not need to complete a second one. Contact the Post Office where you hold your permit to change your postage options. \[Skip to [step 4](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#step_4)\] - **If you do not hold a mailing permit**, fill out PS Form 3615, _Mailing Permit Application and Customer Profile_. This application gives you permission to mail at commercial prices using your postage meter. Copies are available from your local post office or can be downloaded at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/). Check the box that says "Notification to Present Metered Mail in Bulk." If you haven't done so, you must contact your meter manufacturer to get the right rate marking "slug" for your meter. 3. Turn the completed PS Form 3615 in at your main Post Office. There is no application fee to get a permit to use your postage meter for business mailings. You will have to pay your annual mailing permit fee at the time of your first mailing. 4. The Post Office will process your application and give you a permit number to use meter stamps for commercial mailings. You will get a receipt with a permit number on it. You'll use this number when you fill out your postage statement. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#top) **How do I lease a postage meter?** The United States Postal Service approves providers and the postage meters they offer. Visit our [Postage Options page](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm) for a list of approved vendors you can contact. The USPS® only regulates manufacturers that provide postage meters. We are not allowed to supply them, nor can we recommend which provider you should use. | | **Note:**Vendors set service or leasing fees. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Postage-meter#top) **Is postage meter postage eligible for a refund?** **Yes, it is eligible for a refund if it is complete, legible, valid, unused and printed on unmailed envelopes, wrappers, or labels made in accordance with** [DMM 604.9.3.3](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#ep1081087 "Domestic Mail Manual 604.9.3.3 - Postage Payment Methods and Refunds") **and** [DMM 604.9.3.4](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#ep1179637 "Domestic Mail Manual 604.9.3.4 - Postage Payment Methods and Refunds"). **This includes:** - Postage printed on the back (versus the front) of a letter - Only a portion of the postage printed or postage isn't legible - Too much postage was printed on the item - Not enough postage was printed on the item - Incorrect date on the meter - Incorrect information (street name, number, city, etc) The refund request must include the unused metered indicia, proof (such as a copy of the lease or contract) that the person submitting the refund request is the authorized user of the postage meter, and a completed PS Form 3533. The request must be submitted to the local Post Office™ within 60 days from the date shown on the indicia. If the indicia is undated, [additional standards](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#ep1179637 "DMM 604.9.3.4 - Unused, Undated Meter Indicia") are applied and the request must be submitted to the manager of the business mail entry at the USPS district overseeing the mailer's Post Office. For all indicia except those produced by PC Postage systems, you will need to submit the indicia to the licensing Post Office. When the total face value of the indicia is $500 or less, the amount refunded is 90% of the face value. If the total face value is more than $500, the amount refunded is the total face value reduced by $50 per hour for the USPS time to process the refund, with a minimum charge of $50. If a request is denied, the authorized user may appeal within 30 days of the ruling to the [Manager of the Pricing and Classification Service Center](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/608.htm#ep1256210 "Manager, Pricing and Classification Center"). ## United States Postal Inspection Service

## The Postal Inspection Service is the primary law enforcement arm of the United States Postal Service, empowered by Congress to investigate matters relating to the Postal Service.      - Oct 2, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007982

Customer Information

The Postal Inspection Service is the primary law enforcement arm of the United States Postal Service, empowered by Congress to investigate postal offenses and civil matters relating to the Postal Service, including:

- Safeguarding the billions of mailpieces, the people who move the mail, and the customers who use it.
- Investigating mail theft crimes.
- If a fraudster uses the U.S. Mail to further their scheme, they fall under the jurisdiction of the U.S. Postal Inspection Service.
- Enforcing over 200 federal laws involving the misuse of the U.S. Mail for criminal activity, including identity crimes.
  - Overlapping jurisdiction often requires collaboration with other federal, state, and local authorities. Postal Inspectors have developed close working relationships with other agencies that facilitate such cooperation.

**Consumer Protection Campaigns**

**ALERT:** National Consumer Protection Week 2024 - Beware of investment scams!

During **National Consumer Protection Week (NCPW), March 3-9**, the U.S. Postal Inspection Service wants the public to beware of Investment Scams. Investment opportunities or “get rich quick” schemes are a favorite of fraudsters. Whether they sell bogus securities or commodities, oil wells, rare coins, or cybercurrency, these fraudulent promoters try to convince potential victims to invest their hard-earned money. Click [here](https://www.uspis.gov/ncpw2024) for more information about NCPW.

The Postal Inspection Service regards consumer education as one of its top weapons in fighting fraudsters and has developed and partnered in several comprehensive fraud initiatives with consumer-protection agencies and other stakeholders to educate consumers.

**For more information on the United States Postal Inspection Service, please visit:** [https://postalinspectors.uspis.gov/](https://postalinspectors.uspis.gov/)

## USPS® Printer Directory FAQs

## The Difference Between Online Experts and Local Printers.      - Nov 2, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007687

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[General Questions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#General)** | **[Location Permissions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#Location)** | **[Search Results](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#Search)** |
| **[Map](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#Map)** | **[Other](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#Other)** | **[EDDM](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#EDDM)** |

****General Questions****

**What is the difference between Online Experts and Local Printers?**

USPS® assumes no liability for the results of any contact with any company listed in either directory or list. USPS does not endorse these third-party vendors. Private vendors may apply non-USPS charges.

- Online Experts – these are companies whose representatives provide printing and mailing services online. Each Online Expert has a contract with USPS® as an affiliate provider.
  - USPS® third-party Online Experts help businesses create memorable, actionable mail pieces. These experienced vendors offer phone or chat support for strategizing, formatting, designing, printing, preparing, and sending your mailing. They can advise you on the best direct mailing marketing items for your business, from postcards, reply cards, greeting cards, to newsletters, customized stamps, and more.
- Local Printers – these are companies near you that provide printing and mailing services. These companies do not have affiliate contracts with USPS.

**Do I need to log into my [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account to use the USPS® Printer Directory tool?**

No, you do not need to have a [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account in order to use the USPS® Printer Directory tool. All functionalities of the USPS® Printer Directory tool can be accessed without signing in with a user account.

**What happens if I type a street address into the USPS® Printer Directory tool and nothing appears?**

You may have typed an incorrect address containing spelling or numerical errors, or there may be no printers in the Directory within 25 miles of that address.

If this happens, try searching by a city, state or ZIP Code™.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#top)

****Location Permissions****

**How does the USPS® Printer Directory tool use my current location?**

This functionality only works if you enable your specific browser’s settings. The application uses several pieces of data to pinpoint your location including, but not limited to, your IP address, GPS location, cell towers, and Wi-Fi connection (tablet and mobile).

**I pressed “Use Current Location” and it is not displaying my location. What do I do?**

Check your location permissions settings; they may be disabled. Try typing your location in the search box to find surrounding locations.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#top)

****Search Results****

**What is the default order of my search results?**

Search results are ordered by distance of nearest to farthest based on your search criteria. If you want to modify the order of your search results, you can sort your results by name alphabetically (A-Z or Z-A). Once you execute a search, you can filter results by services or order results by distance or name. You can also adjust the radius of your search.

**How do I filter results?**

Once you execute a search, you can filter results by services or order results by distance or name. You can also adjust the radius of your search.

**How do I report inaccuracies about printer listings?**

Report all inaccuracies to:[_printerdirectory@usps.gov_](mailto:printerdirectory@usps.gov) _._

**What are the definitions of the services provided by each Printer location?**

You can find quick definitions of the services in the filter results drop down. See below for additional information.

- Printing – Print mail pieces (with or without addresses).
- Graphic Design – Provide templates and consultation for mail piece design.
- Direct Mail – Delivery of marketing mail to recipients.
- Every Door Direct Mail® (EDDM®) – Regular preparation of EDDM materials including design, preparation, and drop off your mailings by ZIP Code™ and carrier route; create direct mail campaigns targeting customers by age, income, or household size.
- Mailing lists – Provide targeted mailing lists.
- Prep and entry – Provide sorting, counting, bundling and addressing services.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#top)

****Map****

**How do I use the map?**

After inputting an address, city, state, and/or ZIP Code™, search results will appear and the location of resulting vendors will be plotted on the map. To adjust magnification or zoom, go to filter results and adjust the radius of the search area. To move about the map, drag the map to a new location using your cursor and then click “Search Here?” button to update your search results.

Filtering results will display corresponding pins on the map. The numbers on the map pins correspond to the numbered vendor listings in your results, ordered based on proximity to the provided address. The numbering will remain the same even if the vendors are sorted by various criteria (alphabetically, distance).

**How do I see more of the results on the map?**

Try expanding the radius on the map by selecting a larger radius in the filter results drop-down.

****Other****

**How can I have my company listed on the USPS® Printer Directory tool?**

You can apply to have your business listed in the USPS® Printer Directory by signing in to your[Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin "USPS Business Customer Gateway")account and navigating to “Printer Directory” within the Mailing Services tab. For all inquiries, send an email to[_printerdirectory@usps.gov_](mailto:printerdirectory@usps.gov) _._

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#top)

****EDDM****

**Why has the Online Tool been updated to include a link (“Find a Printer”) to the USPS® Printer Directory tool?**

The EDDM tool previously had a link (“Find an Expert”) that allowed users to search for Online Experts who provide a variety of mailing services. This link has been updated (“Find a Printer”) to now allow users to search for both Online Experts and Local Printers. Now customers can search for Printers in their vicinity or contact Online Experts who are always available to offer services to assist with designing, printing, and preparing mail pieces.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Printer-Directory-FAQs#top)

## Every Door Direct Mail Online Tool BMEU Nonprofit FAQs

## FAQs for Every Door Direct Mail Online Tool BMEU Nonprofit      - Nov 2, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007684

Customer Information

**Does the Every Door Direct Mail Online Tool offer Nonprofit pricing for Business Mail Entry Unit customers?**

Yes.  As of May 31, 2015, eligible BMEU customers with Nonprofit Authorizations tied to their Customer Registration Identifiers (CRIDs) are able to create Nonprofit orders within the Every Door Direct Mail Online Tool.

**Who is eligible to use Every Door Direct Mail® BMEU Nonprofit pricing?**

BMEU customers with a Nonprofit Authorization tied to one of their Customer Registration Identifiers (CRIDs) are able to use Every Door Direct Mail® Nonprofit pricing.  BMEU Customers with Nonprofit Authorization are eligible if their order is for a mailing that satisfies the requirements for Nonprofit pricing in accordance with Publication 417, _Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail Eligibility_.  Retail users are unable to use Nonprofit pricing.

**I am preparing an Every Door Direct mailing for an authorized Nonprofit organization, how do I access the Nonprofit pricing?**

To access Nonprofit pricing in the Every Door Direct Mail® Online Tool, log into the Online Tool using a BMEU Account with a valid Nonprofit Authorization tied to your Customer Registration Identifier (CRID).  Once you have logged in and search for routes, you will be presented with a Nonprofit/For-Profit toggle above the “Entry type” dropdown. If you select Nonprofit pricing, you will be prompted to select a valid CRID that has been authorized for Nonprofit use before you can complete the order.

**Where can I find additional information on using the Every Door Direct Mail® Online Tool?**

Please see the [Every Door Direct Mail User Guide](https://www.usps.com/business/pdf/every-door-direct-mail-user-guide.pdf) for instructions on using the Online Tool.

**How Do I Get Started with Every Door Direct Mail® Service?**

You can learn more about Every Door Direct Mail® service by visiting [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/).  Select “Advertise with Mail” under the “Business” dropdown in the blue header near the top of the page. Then, scroll down the page to “Advertise with Every Door Direct Mail” and click on “Learn More” to get more information on the program.  You may also click on “Get Started” to access our free online mapping tool and begin selecting the areas/routes you wish to reach to create your order.  Demographic information based on the 2010 U.S. Census Report such as age, household income and size for selected routes is also available within the tool. Please refer to the [Every Door Direct Mail User Guide](https://www.usps.com/business/pdf/every-door-direct-mail-user-guide.pdf) for more information. If you still have questions, you can speak to your local Postmaster or call customer service at 877-747-6249.

## What is the Picture Permit Authorization Process?

## Description of the authorization process for a Picture Permit.      - Jan 27, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007604

Customer Information

**Step 1:**  The mailer goes to:[https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm)to review the guidelines and required elements and completes the online registration.

**Step 2:** Once registered, the mailer will receive a link to complete the online USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia application, PS Form 3615-A which requires permit indicia and other information.  Submit the signed PS Form 3615-A to[_picturepermit@usps.com_](mailto:picturepermit@usps.com).

**Step 3:** The Program Management Office (PMO) will confirm the application has been approved via email. The mailer will then need to submit a PDF copy of an addressed mailpiece showing envelope/postcard dimensions, IMb, clear zone and proposed Picture Permit design to:  [picturepermit _@usps.com_](mailto:picturepermit@usps.com).

**Step 4:  Engineering Evaluation Process**

**4.1 (Option 1) Engineering Evaluation Requirements:**Letter Mail conforming to DMM 604.5.4, Picture Permit Imprint Indicia (PPI), shall undergo the standard engineering evaluation process which takes approximately 45 business days.  Mailers shall provide 500 production samples which will replicate what will be printed and produced in the live mailstream. Each sample mailpiece must include a complete and valid destination address. Do not use the same address for all mailpieces; the 500 samples must contain a variety of valid addresses.

**4.2 (Option 2) Engineering Evaluation Requirements\* :**Letter Mail conforming to DMM 604.5.4, Picture Permit Imprint Indicia (PPII), and all of the following restrictions, may be subject to a reduced Engineering evaluation period of  approximately 10 business days.  After approval for alternative engineering evaluation, mailers shall provide 50 production samples _or_ samples of sufficient quality to accurately represent the mailpiece including complete address printed on each sample.  Letter Mail not conforming to all of these requirements shall undergo the standard engineering evaluation process.

- Color image shall not contain embedded text or numbers.
- Color image shall not have a drop shadow on any edge.
- PPII area (including .50” left and bottom clear zone) to be on white background (no reverse print).
- PPII shall be positioned in upper right-hand corner with “clear zones” as shown in Fig. B
- Overall width of longest text line shall not be less than width of image as shown in Fig. C
- All mail class text to conform to the approved template provided by USPS.
- No disk mailers, reusable envelopes, perforated folded self-mailers, windowed envelopes.
- Does not exhibit any of the non-machinable criteria listed in DMM 101.1.2.

**\*** For more details please review the Picture Permit Technical Requirements document

_**Please mail samples to:**_

**U.S. Postal Service**

**Attn: Picture Permit Program Office**

**475 L’Enfant Plaza SW, RM 2P916**

**Washington, DC 20260-5621**

USPS® Engineering will then test the designed mailpieces. Once the mailer passes these tests, the PMO will request a mailing schedule, including dates and volumes.

**What information about Picture Permit is available online?**

**The Picture Permit website will provide the following information:**

- Registration Link
- Picture Permit Product Requirements
- Picture Permit Technical Requirements
- Fact Sheet
- FAQ
- Program Management Office (PMO) email address

**What is the website address?**

**The website address is:**[https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm).

**How will I know if my application is approved?**

The Picture Permit PMO will email your company with authorization to move to the next step.

**How do I contact the Picture Permit Program Office?**

**By Mail****:**

**UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICE®**

**ATTN:  PICTURE PERMIT PROGRAM OFFICE**

**475 L’ENFANT PLAZA, SW ROOM 2P916**

**WASHINGTON, DC 20260-5621**

**By Email:**

[_picturepermit@usps.gov_](mailto:picturepermit@usps.gov)

**Information and resources will also be posted online at:**

[https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm)
