Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000002JZYAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-am-I-Restricted-from-Mailing) ## What am I Restricted from Mailing? ## General Information about mailing Restricted Items. - Jan 3, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007588 Customer Information **Domestic Shipping Prohibitions & Restrictions** What you may ship domestically is regulated by U.S. and USPS® guidelines. Here, you’ll find important tips and information on items that are prohibited (completely forbidden) and restricted (only allowed under certain conditions). If you think your item may be prohibited or restricted or have more questions, see [Shipping Restrictions & HAZMAT - What Can You Send in the Mail?](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm) Hazardous, restricted, or perishable materials mailed to, from, and between overseas Military and Diplomatic Post Offices are subject to the conditions of [International Mail Manual 130](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc1_010.htm), the standards in [Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail](http://pe.usps.gov/text/pub52/welcome.htm), and conditions prescribed by the Department of Defense (DOD), as listed in the "Overseas Military/Diplomatic Mail" section of the latest issue of the [_Postal Bulletin_](https://about.usps.com/resources/postal-bulletin.htm). Further information on items restricted from mailing can be found at [What Does USPS® Classify as Hazardous Materials?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials) | | | | --- | --- | | Videos are available on shipping prohibitions and restrictions of the following items on the [USPS TV YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/user/uspstv/custom): | | - [Fireworks](https://youtu.be/yych96gTOPg?si=6iwbTuw6HaU4EcTG)
- [Lithium batteries](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_5EIjOwbZ_U) | - [Live Ammunition](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=i7NXmJocZuA)
- [Mercury](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wriu99Z01r8) | **International Shipping Restrictions** Regarding international mail, restrictions for international mailpieces (outside of postcards and letters with no enclosures) vary by country. For general information on internationally restricted and prohibited items as well as some mailer guidelines, see [International Shipping Restrictions - What You Can Mail Internationally](https://www.usps.com/international/shipping-restrictions.htm). You can visit the USPS [Index of Countries and Localities](http://pe.usps.gov/text/Imm/immctry.htm) to find a comprehensive listing of prohibitions, restrictions, and size and weight limits for a particular country. ## What is Customized MarketMail®?

## Learn more about the design elements and benefits of Customized Market Mail.      - Nov 8, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007594

Customer Information

**Design Elements**

**Materials**

- Customized MarketMail® pieces can be constructed of virtually any material, including paper, cardstock, cardboard, foam, rubber, plastic, etc.
- Pieces may not contain sharp edges that could harm USPS® employees or customers and must be sufficiently flexible to be inserted into smaller Post Office™ boxes and mail boxes.

**Difference from other die-cut mail**

- Customized MarketMail service allows you to avoid enclosing non-rectangular, die-cut pieces in an envelope or box. This eliminates the costs incurred designing a container for your message while differentiating your message and making it immediately visible.

**Production**

- The Postal Service™ does not produce Customized MarketMail® (CMM) pieces.
- Virtually any commercial printer capable of manufacturing die-cuts is capable of manufacturing and printing creative, high-impact CMM pieces. Check with your current printer, creative agency or mail house to see if they can have your CMM piece manufactured for you.
- A list of printers who have indicated they are capable of producing CMM pieces is listed on the Customized MarketMail website ( [https://www.usps.com/customizedmarketmail/\_pdf/cmm-prospective-manufacturers-list.pdf](https://www.usps.com/customizedmarketmail/_pdf/cmm-prospective-manufacturers-list.pdf)).

**Size and Weight Standards**

- Weight no more than 3.3 ounces
- 3 1/2 to 12 inches high
- 5 to 15 inches in length; the length is the longest dimension of the mailpiece.
- Pieces can be perfectly square or round, with the same height and length.
- Pieces need not be uniform in thickness, but must be no less than .007 inches at the thinnest point and no more than 3/4 inches at the thickest point.

**Benefits of Customized MarketMail®**

**Deliver a highly targeted message that differentiates your product!**

- **Achieve business objectives** \-  Customized MarketMail service is suitable for a variety of objectives -- launching new products, announcing events, generating brand awareness or driving business to your store or website.

- **Make an impact** - Dazzle your customers with never-before-seen Direct Mail creativity and help establish a strong brand identity.

- **Think outside the envelope**\- Provide the attention-getting power of a dimensional piece without hiding it in an envelope.

- **Overcome the clutter** \- Push your creative limits to deliver a marketing experience that attracts customer attention and encourages a response.

- **Shape it up** - Experiment with non-rectangular, unique shapes such as circular, oblong, triangular, square... anything goes!

- **Cut it out****-** Design your Customized MarketMail pieces to include die-cuts, voids or holes, and benefit from the ability to mail them without an envelope.

- **Get flexible**\- Customized MarketMail service allows you to use a variety of flexible materials to create high-impact mail piece of non-uniform thickness.

- **Showcase your business** \- Shape your mailpiece to immediately demonstrate your product or service, for example cars, shoes, computers or pizza.

- **Stick around** \- The more unique the mail piece, the more likely consumers are to retain and share it with friends and family, thereby extending the life of your message.

## Click-N-Ship® - Managing the Address Book

## Features and functionality of the Click-N-Ship® Address Book.      - Nov 6, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007592

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Adding to the Address Book](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#adding)** | [**Creating an Address Group/Sub Group**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#creating) | [**Importing an existing Address Book**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#importing) |
| [**Deleting from the Address Book**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#deleting) | [**Exporting from the Address Book**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#exporting) | [**Editing the Address Book**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#editing) |

Your Click-N-Ship® _Address Book_ can store up to 5,000 addresses. You can import any number of new addresses until your Address Book reaches that limit.

The Click-N-Ship Address Book will allow you to add contacts to new or existing Address Groups and Subgroups. As your needs change, the Address Book functionality is flexible enough to change along with it. There is a limit of 500 addresses per group.

To get started, click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar (Create Label, Preferences, Shipping History, Address Book, Shipping Cart tabs). You can also click on the contact name to display the details of your address. From here, you will be able to edit your contact information in addition to adding or removing your contact from groups or subgroups.

**Adding to the Address Book**

Click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar. Follow these steps to add an address:

1. Click _Quick Add_ or _Add A Contact_ at the bottom right of the address book.
2. Enter contact name.
3. Enter contact details (example provided in the address book). _Add A Contact_ allows for more detailed information and additional addresses to be entered for that contact.
4. Click _Save_ or _Cancel_.

**Creating an Address Group/Sub** **Group**

A Click-N-Ship® _Address Group_ is any number of addresses you would like grouped together. For example, personal or family addresses can be combined into an Address Group named Family Members, or business addresses can be combined into a group of similar characteristics like Top Ten Customers. By utilizing the Address Group functionality, you can quickly and easily find addresses of family members or your best customers. You can add up to 5,000 total contacts with a maximum of 500 per group.

A Click-N-Ship® Address Book _Subgroup_ is any collection of addresses you would like grouped together under a parent Address Group. For example, the Family Members group can have the subgroups of Parents, Cousins, etc., or the Top Ten Customers group can have subgroups by geographical areas. By utilizing the Address Book Subgroup functionality, you can quickly and easily find addresses of family members or your best customers in an even more detailed level. You can add up to 500 subgroups with a maximum of 5,000 total contacts.

To create an Address Group, perform the following steps:

1. Click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar.
2. Click the “Add a Group” button.
3. Select “Add a New Group”. Enter the name of the New Address Group you want to create.  Click on the “Save” button, and your New Address Group will be created.

To add a saved address to a Group, perform the following steps:

1. Click in the checkbox of any address(es) you want to move to a particular group.
2. Click _Add to A Group_.
3. Select the group you wish to add the contact(s) to.
4. Click _Save Changes_, and your address(es) will be saved to the desired group, or click _Cancel_ to return to the Address Book without saving the changes.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#Top)

**Importing an existing Address Book**

Before you import an address book from another application into your Click-N-Ship® address book, you will need to create a **.csv file**. A .csv file is a comma-separated value file used to import and export addresses in a standard format. Before creating your .csv file, it is strongly recommended that you review the example (sample.csv) provided for you on the Address Book Import page (note the column headings).

**To import a contact to the CNS Address Book:**

1. Click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar.
2. The Click-N-Ship Address Summary Page will be displayed. Click on the **Import** button.
3. Click **Browse**.

   - Only .csv files are compatible for importing
4. Browse your computer and select the contact file
   - **Note**: Customers will need to ensure they have a header row in the .csv for a successful import
5. Select a group name to import to, or create a new group.
6. Click **Continue**
7. Match the USPS address book fields (listed on the left) with the appropriate column names from the file that was uploaded (listed in the drop-down menu on right)
8. Click **Continue**
9. Verify the information that was uploaded and click **Continue**
   - During an import, customers may see any of the following error messages:
     - Import limits exceeded
     - You reached the maximum number of groups for your Address Book
     - \# of # row imported successfully

**Notes on exporting/importing your business or personal addresses:**

1. Export your address book from another application as a .csv file. Follow the instructions for exporting found in your application. Make sure you note where the exported .csv file is saved on your computer.
2. Review the sample .csv file to determine the headings that will need to be included in the .csv file you are about to import into your address book. Make note of required fields: i.e. Full Name, Address 1, City, and State.
3. Make any necessary adjustments; add headings, delete empty columns, add Reference numbers, or provide your own Short Name to your .csv file prior to importing.
4. In the Click-N-Ship Import Address Book page, select the saved .csv file using the Browse button and import the file into your Click-N-Ship® Address Book.
5. If you have already created an Address Group, you can select the Address Group you would want the imported address to be placed from the New Import Group drop-down menu. If you have not created an Address Group, import the addresses into the "Unassigned Group" and assign the addresses to a group at a later time using the Manage Address Group. You can also add a new group by typing in a new group name in the available field.
6. After successfully importing your file, you will be taken to the Address Book Summary page.
7. From the Address Book Summary page, it is recommended that you take a few minutes and review the addresses just imported.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#Top)

**Exporting from the Address Book**

You may export your address book by group(s), individual selections, by letter or all.

- To export your address book by group, click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar. Follow these steps to export your Address Book:

1. Select which group(s) you’d like to export.
2. Click “Export Group” button.
3. A dialogue box will display to ask what you want to do with the file you selected to export. Options may be to “Save”, “Open”, or “Cancel.” Once you make your selection, the export is complete.

- To export your address book by All or by select individual(s), click on "All" to display all names in the address book:

1. To export the entire address book, click the checkbox below "All" next to the Name column; all names in the address book will be checked. You may de-select any address you don't wish to export by clicking the checkbox next to their name. To export specific addresses only, uncheck the box below "All" and click the checkbox next to the names of the address you wish to export.
2. Click the “Export” button.
3. A dialogue box will display to ask what you want to do with the file you selected to export. Options may be to “Save”, “Open”, or “Cancel.” Once you make your selection, the export is complete.

- To export your address book by letter:

1. Click on the letter of the last names you'd like to export. Only the names of contacts for that letter will display.
2. Click the checkbox next to the names to export, or click the checkbox under "All" to select all names for that letter.
3. Click the "Export" button.
4. A dialog box will display to ask what you want to do with the file you selected to export. Options may be to "Save", "Open", or "Cancel". Once you make your selection, the export is complete.

**Deleting an address or an address group from the Address Book**

Click on the _Address Book tab_ in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar. Follow these steps to delete an address from an Address Book Group:

1. Select the address in which you want to remove from a group
2. Click on the contact’s name to display the contact information
3. Click “Edit” Button
4. At the bottom of the contact form field details, de-select any group in which you want to remove from this address.
5. Click “Save Contact”.

**Note:** When deleting an address from an Address Group, the address will not be deleted from your Address Book; it will just be removed from that specific Address Group.

To delete an entire Address Book Group, click on the _Address Book tab_ in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar. Follow these steps to delete a group:

1. Click the “Delete” link next to the name of the group in which you want to delete.
2. Select the option you prefer: (a) delete only the group and keep the group’s contacts; or, (b) delete both the group AND ALL contacts within it.
3. Click on the “Delete” button.

**Editing the Address Book**

Click on the _Address Book_ tab in the Click-N-Ship® toolbar. Select an existing address by clicking on the name. The contact’s information will then be displayed in the lower panel. Click “Edit” button to display all form fields for the contact and the address will be ready for modification. After you are done modifying your contact, click “Save Contact” to save changes or click “Cancel” to cancel the modifications that were made.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-Managing-the-Address-Book#Top)

## What is USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia?

## Description of the Picture Permit Imprint Indicia.      - Jun 13, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007583

Customer Information

**USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia**

The United States Postal Service® introduced USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia program in June 2012. This service is available for mailings of automation First-Class Mail® letters and postcards and USPS Marketing Mail™ letters.

**What is Picture Permit Indicia?**

Picture Permit Indicia (Picture Permit) provides commercial mailers the opportunity to include corporate logos, brand images, or trademarks in the permit indicia space of First-Class Mail®  automation letters and postcards, and all USPS Marketing Mail letters. The addition of images to the permit imprint indicia has the potential of increasing open rates and enhancing the value of the mail.

**Why does the U.S. Postal Service® offer Picture Permit?**

The U.S. Postal Service is responding to requests from customers to use their corporate logos, brand images or trademarks in the permit indicia space and to improve the open rates of their mailpieces. In creating Picture Permit, the U.S. Postal Service® has developed guidelines and requirements for the use of mailers’ proprietary images in the permit indicia space of the mailpiece.

**How will my company benefit from using Picture Permit?**

Picture Permit could boost the visual impact and open rate of your mailpiece, which will improve the effectiveness of the overall mailing.

**Who can participate in Picture Permit?**

Commercial mailers who plan to use a new or existing mailing permit to send First-Class Mail Full-Service IMb automation letters and postcards or USPS Marketing Mail.

**How do I apply?**

Follow the instructions provided on the program’s website: [https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm).

## How is Military Mail Processed?

## Military Mail is processed similar to domestic mail. All military locations, regardless of where they may be located around the globe, are assigned a ZIP Code™ referred to as an APO/FPO/DPO number.      - Sep 2, 2020 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007112

Customer Information

## **The price of Military Mail postage is the same as domestic postage.  A First-Class® Mail stamp can deliver a letter to a U.S. military service member located halfway around the world.**

The United States Postal Service processes APO (Air/Army Post Office), FPO (Fleet Post Office), and DPO (Diplomatic Post Office) mail similar to domestic mail.  All military locations, regardless of where they may be located around the globe, are assigned a ZIP Code™ number referred to as an APO/FPO/DPO number.

Mail processing for APO/FPO/DPO mail differs in many ways from standard domestic mail. USPS places this mail on international commercial passenger service and international cargo service aircraft from various cities. APO/FPO/DPO mail is charged and handled by USPS as Domestic mail, but the service is considered International Mail with applicable rules for what may be safely mailed internationally (which differs significantly from domestic rules).

When the plane lands overseas, the mail is transferred to the Military. The mail is then handled by Military Postal Service representatives and transported to the Military Post Office for distribution to the service member's unit for delivery to the recipient. These unique transportation and handling requirements for Military Mail, including the necessity of some mail to go to remote and war-zone locations, results in extending the standard delivery time for each class of mail.

## USPS Share Mail® - The Basics

## General Information about Share Mail®.      - Nov 2, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007582

Customer Information

# ****What is USPS Share Mail®?****

Share Mail® is a customized direct mail product that works like a word of mouth marketing tool. Mailers and customers can communicate and relay messages to each other through a postcard or letter sent from a USPS commercial mailer.

# **How does it work?**

As the commercial mailer, you decide the message to send as you design and prepare your card or letter.

The Share Mail item is enclosed in a direct mail piece that is mailed out to customer lists and customers can then mail the enclosed postcard or letter to a friend or neighbor.

Mailers who put unique barcodes on their Share Mail letters and postcards can also acquire tracking information about when and where the piece was sent.

# **Does it cost anything?**

Yes, as the mailer, you pay postage to cover the mailpiece circulating throughout the USPS mail system. Because Share Mail pays the advanced postage, your customers can address your note and drop it in the mailbox. Visit [https://postalpro.usps.com/mailing/share-mail](https://postalpro.usps.com/mailing/share-mail "Share Mail | PostalPro") for all Share Mail information, including FAQs, and layout guidelines.

# **Can Share Mail items be sent internationally? Are there Share Mail packages?**

Share Mail items can only be sent to domestic addresses (includes APO/FPO/DPO addresses). Share Mail is also limited to First-Class Mail® letters or postcards that weigh no more than 1 ounce.

# **I’m a business owner – how do I get started with Share Mail?**

Participants and/or Mail Service Providers (MSPs) can fill out a registration form on the [Business Customer Gateway (BCG)](https://gateway.usps.com/ "USPS Business Customer Gateway"). After signing in (or registering for the first time), click on Mail Services to access the Share Mail section.

For all Share Mail information, including FAQs, and layout guidelines, visit [https://postalpro.usps.com/mailing/share-mail](https://postalpro.usps.com/mailing/share-mail "Share Mail | PostalPro").

## What is Priority Mail Express Military® Service (PMEMS)?

## Provides the Department of Defense personnel stationed overseas mailing privileges and expedited delivery service to or from the United States.      - May 22, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007117

Customer Information

Priority Mail Express Military Service (PMEMS) is available between the United States and designated APOs, FPOs, and DPOs to provide Department of Defense personnel stationed overseas, and others entitled to APO/FPO/ DPO mailing privileges, an expedited delivery service to or from the United States.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**Availability**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS#availability) | [**Sunday/Holiday Fee for PMEMS**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS#sunday_holiday_fee) | [**Service Guarantee**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS#service_guarantee) |

# **Availability**

- Delivery time is typically 3 to 5 business days depending on origin and destination; however, the scheduled delivery date is determined at the time of mailing.
- Priority Mail Express® service standards for pieces shipped “TO” an APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Code™ will be “Business Days”.
  - As a result, Priority Mail Express pieces mailed “TO” an APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Code will never have an expected delivery day of Saturday, Sunday, or on a holiday.
- Priority Mail Express is not available between APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Codes.
- PMEMS may not be available at or between all Post Offices or at all times of deposit. A PMEMS directory, showing detailed local information about PMEMS, is available at Post Offices.

**Sunday/Holiday Fee for PMEMS**

- The Sunday/Holiday fee is not available to any Priority Mail Express piece being shipped “TO” an APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Code as the PMEMS service standard is in "Business Days".
- The Sunday/Holiday fee would apply, when applicable, for any Priority Mail Express piece being shipped “FROM” an APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Code.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS#top)

# **Service Guarantee**

**To APO/FPO/DPO**

Items mailed **to** APO/FPO and DPO destinations (from the United States) are available for delivery at the destination APO/FPO or DPO Post Office by 6pm on the designated delivery day unless the designated delivery day is a weekend or holiday; in such cases, the item is available for delivery on the next business day.

**From APO/FPO/DPO**

Items mailed **from** APO/FPO and DPO locations (going to the United States) are delivered to an addressee within the delivery area of the destination Post Office by 6pm on the designated delivery day.

|     |
| --- |
| **For additional information on Military Mail, go to**[How Do I Address Military Mail?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Do-I-Address-Military-Mail). |

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-Military-Service-PMEMS#top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000N6HAAU%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Hold-for-Pickup-Service) ## Hold for Pickup Service ## When Hold for Pickup is requested at the time of mailing for an eligible item, a recipient can pick it up from a designated Post Office. - Feb 28, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008085 Customer Information Hold for Pickup is a service that allows mailpieces to be held at a designated Post Office location for pick up by a specified addressee or designee rather than have a carrier attempt delivery. Mailers can request this service when they purchase postage for [eligible items](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Hold-for-Pickup-Service#eligible_items) with [select methods](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Hold-for-Pickup-Service#eligible_postage_payment_methods). The addressee or designee must provide an [acceptable primary form of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) to pick up the item from the Post Office. Items are held at the pickup location for a limited amount of time: - If the item has not been picked up within 5 days (3 days for Priority Mail Express®), the Post Office makes another notification attempt - If not picked up within 15 days (5 days for Priority Mail Express), the item is returned to sender If the shipper provides an email address or text-capable phone number with their shipping manifest, the recipient will automatically be signed up to receive email or SMS text tracking notifications from USPS®. **Eligible Postage Payment Methods, Mail Classes and Mail Services** There is no fee for Hold for Pickup service. It is available for eligible items when postage is paid through: - USPS Click-N-Ship® - USPS-approved PC Postage products - Permit imprint - USPS-approved Information-Based Indicia (IBI) postage meters when also using an authorized Hold For Pickup label - Enterprise Payment System (EPS) account when used with a Package Intercept request - Accepted payment methods at retail Post Office locations (for Collect on Delivery Hold for Pickup only) Hold for Pickup service is available for the following mail classes and services: - At retail Post Office locations: - Priority Mail Express® with or without Collect on Delivery service (COD) - Priority Mail® with COD service - First-Class Mail® with COD service - Library Mail with COD service - Media Mail® with COD service - Using USPS Click-N-Ship: - Priority Mail Express - Priority Mail - USPS Ground Advantage Service® - Commercial mailings of: - Priority Mail Express (excluding Priority Mail Express Military® Service and Priority Mail Express Open and Deposit®) - Priority Mail - USPS Ground Advantage Service - Commercial - Bound Printed Matter parcels Hold for Pickup service is **not** eligible for: - Commercial mailings of First-Class Mail letter-size and flat-size pieces - Mail addressed to APO/FPO and DPO destinations such as Priority Mail Express Military Service - Priority Mail Express items containing cremated remains Items mailed with Hold for Pickup service requested will have the service listed on the package label. | | | --- | | **SAMPLE Label Hold for Pickup Labels** | | ![Label 11F - Priority Mail Express Hold for Pickup Label, MAY 2021](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/11-f-may-2021.png)![Image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/nonretail_manual_priority_mail_COD_HFPU_Label.jpg)

## What do I Need to Know When Packaging Military Mail?

## General packing information.      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007103

Customer Information

**Please keep the following in mind to ensure that packages are delivered promptly:**

- Extreme Temperatures:  Desert temperatures typically exceed 100 degrees.
- Place the mailing address inside the parcel on a separate piece of paper.
- Use a box that is constructed to conform to the contents you are sending.
- Ensure the weight of the items in the box are evenly distributed.
- Add packing material on the inside if any portion of the box sags or is easily depressed.
- Use packaging tape for wrapping the outside of the box. Do not wrap with string.
- Cross out or cover excess markings or printing to prevent your box from being missent.
- Enclose all personal hygiene items, such as deodorant, lotion, or shampoo, in a sealable bag.
- Place all perishable items, such as cookies, chips, etc., in a plastic container.
- Do not mail liquids of any kind, especially carbonated drinks, which can explode during transit.

## What is Official Mail (Penalty Mail)?

## Description of Official Mail, also known as Penalty Mail.      - Oct 30, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006795

Customer Information

**Official Mail (also known as _Penalty Mail)_ is sent by U.S. government agencies, relating solely to the business of the U.S. government, which is authorized by law to be carried in the mail without prepayment of postage.**

- For this standard, _agencies_ are departments, agencies, corporations, establishments, commissions, committees, and all officers and authorities of the U.S. government authorized to use penalty mail.
- These agencies must reimburse the United States Postal Service the equivalent amount of postage and fees due for the penalty mail service they receive.
- Penalty mail stamped stationery (plain stamped envelopes, personalized envelopes, and stamped cards) and penalty mail adhesive stamps are available in various denominations
- Metered postage can also be used in conjunction with Official Mail.
- "Penalty for Private Use $300" refers to the use of penalty envelopes, cards, meter stamps, etc. by any private person, concern, or organization or for matter not relating exclusively to the business of the United States government.

![Official Mail Stamp Image](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6t0000000bmQt&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UIvn)

**Franked Mail (also referred to as Congressional Mail) is official mail sent without postage prepayment by members and members-elect of Congress (which includes the Vice-President of the United States) and other authorized individuals.**

**Congressional Franked Mail is identified by a specified marking or the written or printed facsimile signature (or frank) of a member of Congress in the upper right corner of the envelope or label. This may be followed by “M.C.” standing for member of Congress or “U.S.S.” standing for U.S. Senate. These markings are in place of a postage stamp.**

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6t0000000bmPdAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Security) ## Mail Security ## USPS policies and procedures to ensure the safety and security of customers, employees, and the mail. - Dec 5, 2019 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007855 Customer Information United States Postal Service® facilities, including the Mail Transport Equipment Service Centers (MTESC), are following established procedures to inspect all mail transport equipment and notify the appropriate agencies as necessary: - In instances where there is a confirmed exposure, the equipment is decontaminated. - In instances where the material is unknown but suspect, Postal Service personnel treat the scene as a HAZMAT instance, isolating equipment until a determination is made of the nature of the suspect material. **Where Can I Find Information on Recent Mail Security?** The United States Postal Service® website, [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®, contains up to date information regarding the security of the mail. This information can be accessed through the following areas: - [Securing the Mail](http://about.usps.com/what-we-are-doing/securing-the-mail/welcome.htm): This is the Mail Facility/Service Updates page of the special section on the United States Postal Service® website dedicated to security of the mail. Historical information on issues related to mail security, including closures and impacts resulting from anthrax contamination, can also be found here. - [Postal Newsroom](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/): The latest news about USPS, including news regarding the security of the mail. **What Happens When a Mailing-Related Facility Closes?** When a Processing Plant is forced to close due to suspicious mail, mail is held on site. Postal Service™ personnel will not be allowed into the facility until cleared by the Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and/or appropriate local health officials. Since each instance is unique in nature, no specific timetable for re-opening the facility can be determined in advance of the actual situation. **Is Mail X-Rayed?** Some of the mail that is sent through the United States Postal Service will pass through an X-Ray machine. There are no specific guidelines as to what may or may not be x-rayed, though mail sent to or through larger cities is likely to pass through an X-Ray machine. If you have additional questions about X-Raying of items, contact your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction_input). **Note**: Marking a mailpiece "Do Not X-Ray" will not forgo the X-Raying of a mailpiece, and may result in the item being considered suspicious. **Is Mail Ever Irradiated or Sanitized?** The only mail currently being irradiated is mail sent to federal government agencies in Washington, DC, for ZIP Codes™ beginning with **202, 203, 204** and **205**. Mail to these Zones is accumulated at a site near the Brentwood Road facility, and then shipped to New Jersey to be irradiated. This process in New Jersey takes approximately 24 hours. Mail can take three (3) to five (5) days longer to reach its destination than it did prior to irradiation, but is generally not more than three (3) days. Mail returned from New Jersey is aired out for a short period before it is processed at a postal location designated for sorting all Government mail. Once the United States Postal Service® provides mail to Congress and other affected government groups, some have their own mail rooms that do additional security checking before the mail is sorted and actually given to the recipients. Customers may send either Priority Mail® or Priority Mail Express™ items for expedited handling. This mail is not irradiated unless it is sent to the White House. Mail may arrive in a plastic bag(gie) with a letter explaining the sanitization process (if the mail was sanitized). If not sanitized, the Postal Service™ "re-wraps" mail that has been harmed so that it can reach its destination. We use bags because it helped us ensure the letter would go with the mailpiece. For sanitized mail customers, the Postal Service™ is exploring various methods and hasn't yet made a final decision on whether to bag or not bag sanitized mail before it reaches its destination. The Postal Service™ process for sanitizing the mail is a three-tier approach: - Immediate action - Using available off-site facilities to decontaminate. - Short-term action - Identifying key postal locations for the centralized processing and sanitization of mail. - Long-term action - Identifying and building into existing processes sanitization equipment that is completely safe for employees and customers. **What are the Effects of Irradiation?** Irradiation can have a negative effect on some plant, food, medical, and electronic devices. But most mail is not currently being irradiated. Only mail to federal government agencies in Washington, DC whose ZIP Code™ ranges begin with 202, 203, 204 and 205 are being irradiated. While some people have reported mild symptoms after handling irradiated mail, substantial biological and medical testing has found no link between irradiated mail and those symptoms. Because the irradiation process can dry out paper and "yellow" the mail, there may be more paper dust or roughness associated with this mail. The irradiation process does not create any harmful radiation. The process was developed in consultation with scientists and experts both within and outside the federal government, led by the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy. **Who Do I Call for USPS Law Enforcement?** The United States Postal Service has two law enforcement agencies with distinct areas of investigative of responsibilities to serve the needs of stakeholders, postal employees, and the American public. | **Office of Inspector General** | **Postal Inspection Service** | | --- | --- | | - Theft, delay, or destruction of mail by employees or contractors
- Injury compensation fraud
- Embezzlements and financial crimes
- Contract Fraud
- Kickbacks
- Computer Crimes
- Narcotics
- Employee misconduct
- Internal affairs and executive investigations
- Whistleblower reprisals
Contact 888-USPS-OIG (888-877-7644)
[http://www.uspsoig.gov](http://www.uspsoig.gov/ "Home Page | USPS Office of Inspector General") | - Security of employees, facilities, and equipment
- Revenue and postage fraud
- International mail security
- Violent Crimes
- Threats and assaults of employees
- Burglaries and robberies
- Mail theft by non-employees
- Dangerous mail bombs
- Mail fraud 
- Identity theft
- Narcotics in the mail
- Child pornography and obscenity
Contact 877-876-2455

## How do I Submit a Refund Request for Online Postage?

## Refund and payment options for online PC Postage® vendors.      - Jul 9, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007858

Customer Information

For all indicia except those produced by PC Postage® systems, you will need to submit the images. In most cases, customers will pay a monthly fee to use this service. Vendors can offer different or specialized services.

- **_[Endicia.com](http://endicia.com/)_** and **_[Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/)_** offer customers the ability to print postage-paid labels for any single-piece (letters, envelopes, packages) of domestic or international mail class with address verification, discounted USPS Tracking® and Signature Confirmation™, and insurance.  For certain classes of mail, postage amounts are hidden on the labels.

- eBay and PayPal® users can print labels with postage using eBay's online postage solution.

**Note:** Regardless whether postage is paid online or not, a customer must have the ability to add additional postage if necessary by using stamps, meter, etc.

- _**Pitney Bowes pbSmart Postage**_ offers customers the ability to print package, large envelope and envelope postage from their computer.

**How does Information Based Indicia compare to PC Postage® products?**

PC Postage is the U.S. Postal Service™ product brand and trademark used to refer to products that are developed by commercial companies to print postage (IBI) using a personal computer. Information Based Indicia (IBI) are the digital postage markings printed by digital postage evidencing systems, PC Postage products, or digital postage meters.  IBI consist of human-readable and machine-readable information.  It is distinguished by the use of a two-dimensional barcode to encode security and mail processing information.

**What payment options are available for PC Postage®?**

PC Postage vendors typically charge a monthly fee for their service and most require the use of a credit/debit card for that service fee. Customers can pay for postage using a credit or debit card. If using a commercial PC Postage® product, it is also suggested that customers set up an Automated Clearing House (ACH) account as a direct debit account with their bank.

The actual payment process is set up by the PC Postage vendor.

**Can I get a refund for a PC Postage® label that does not print?**

Yes, **PC Postage®** that is unused is eligible for a refund if it is complete and legible. Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days from the date shown on the indicia and should be submitted to the system provider for processing. The provider may charge a fee for processing refund requests.

Please allow up to two billing cycles for your refund to be posted to your credit card statement. Processing requests can take up to 14 days. Posting to your account is dependent upon your financial institution. Check your Shipping History to see the status of your refunds.

**Who should use PC Postage®?**

PC Postage is designed for customers who need a more robust solution than Click-N-Ship. It’s designed for those who send both letters/flats and packages and want fast, easy access to postage.  Typical users are home-based businesses and individuals who want the convenience of printing postage from their office. PC Postage is ideal for people who:

- Ship higher mail volumes
- Ship multiple packages frequently
- Ship letters & packages

PC Postage® products and services are currently authorized for use on all mail services, with the exception of Periodicals and Bound Printed Matter. Eligible services include:

- Domestic [First-Class Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail) items
- Priority Mail® mailpieces
- Priority Mail Express® items
- USPS Ground Advantage™
- International Mail and Parcels
- Presorted First-Class Mail
- Extra Services (such as USPS Tracking® and Certified Mail™ services)

Customers may often use free shipping supplies, Package Pickup, and USPS Tracking® services.

If you are searching for a mailing option to ship USPS Marketing Mail™, you will want to consider meter or permit imprint instead.

## USPS / Postmaster General Statements

## Official USPS statements and links to other organizational news.      - Apr 27, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007853

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **USPS Statements** |
| [**USPS Newsroom**](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/) | [**USPS Accelerates Key Network Infrastructure Investments**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Postmaster-General-Statements#infrastructure_investment) |
| [**USPS 10-Year Plan**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Postmaster-General-Statements#usps_10yr_plan) | [**USPS Announces Organizational Changes**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Postmaster-General-Statements#usps_restructure) |

**USPS Accelerates Key Network Infrastructure Investments to Meet Customers’ Evolving Mailing and Shipping Needs Ahead of 2021 Holiday Season**

**4/27/2021**

As part of its 10-year plan to achieve financial sustainability and service excellence, the Postal Service announced key network infrastructure investments to meet the evolving mailing and shipping needs of American public and business customers ahead of the 2021 holiday season. [See here for complete statement.](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/national-releases/2021/0427-usps-accelerates-key-network-infrastructure-investments-ahead-of-2021-holiday-season.htm)

**U.S. Postal Service Unveils 10-Year Plan to Achieve Financial Sustainability and Service Excellence**

**3/23/2021**

The United States Postal Service released its 10-year Plan, ‘Delivering for America’, to return the organization to financial sustainability and achieve service excellence while maintaining universal six-day mail delivery and expanding seven-day package delivery. [See here for complete statement.](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/national-releases/2021/0323-usps-unveils-10-year-plan-to-achieve-financial-sustainability-and-service-excellence.htm)

**U.S. Postal Service Announced Next Phase of Organizational Changes**

**3/3/2021**

Postmaster General and CEO Louis DeJoy provided details of the next phase of organizational changes he first announced in August 2020, designed to improve efficiency, drive success and better serve Postal Service customers. [See here for complete statement.](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/national-releases/2021/0303-usps-announces-next-phase-of-organizational-changes-begun-in-august-2020.htm)

## What is the Carrier Release Program?

## How senders authorize a carrier to leave parcels if no one is available to receive it at time of delivery.      - May 27, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006981

Customer Information

The Carrier Release Program allows senders of parcels to authorize carriers to leave parcels in a safe location protected from weather if no one is available at the location to receive it or when the addressee has filed a written order to allow a carrier to leave the parcel. This request can also be made for flats too large for mail receptacles.

A parcel may be left only when it bears the **”CARRIER─LEAVE IF NO RESPONSE“**(written clearly and legibly on the mail piece) endorsement. It must appear directly to the left of the postage area (preferred) or directly below the return address. A minimum 1/4 inch clear space must appear between any other printing and the carrier release endorsement.

If an ancillary service endorsement is used, the carrier release endorsement must be separated from the ancillary service endorsement by the equivalent of one blank line.

A carrier release endorsement may be used on only:

- Uninsured parcels
- Parcels which do not require a signature
- Expedited Packaging Supplies Parcels that are uninsured and do not require a signature

## What is Bound Printed Matter Service?

## Consists of permanently bound sheets of which at least 90% are printed with advertising, promotional, directory, or editorial matter (or a combination of such matter).      - Apr 29, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007830

Customer Information

**Bound Printed Matter** is a cost effective way to mail single-piece or volume presort permanently-bound sheets of advertising, promotional, directory or editorial material such as catalogs, books and other printed material using PS Form 8042 (Bound Printed Matter Documentation Form) and a permit account or a penalty permit account. BPM is no longer accepted at retail offices but must be deposited as business mail at the Business Mail Entry Unit where the permit imprint was issued.

**Features**

- Cost efficient way to send small and large packages containing permanently-bound sheets of directory, advertising, or editorial matter, such as catalogs or phone books.
- Permanently-bound sheets should be secured with staples, spiral binding, glue, or stitching.
- Pieces can weigh up to 15 lbs.
- Only available for Permit mailings.

**For additional information, including pricing, go to** [https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm).

## What is Single Point Delivery?

## Single Point Delivery is provided for large companies, buildings, schools, hotels, etc. Delivery service is provided to a single location or receptacle designated by the management of the development for the receipt of mail which will be distributed by its employees.      - Mar 13, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006966

Customer Information

**Single Point Delivery****is provided for large companies, buildings, schools, hotels, etc.  Delivery service is provided to a single location or receptacle designated by the management of the development for the receipt of mail which will be distributed by its employees to the recipient(s).**

**Forwarding Mail from Single Point Delivery:**

- A customer cannot make a Change of Address (COA) away from a Single Point Delivery address.
- Customers will need to work with the mail administrator of the facility to have their mail forwarded.

**Delivery of Accountable Mail:**

- Delivery is to the single point mail delivery location and the person to whom the restricted mail piece is addressed would have to sign for it. Hotels and motels often have an agreement allowing them to act as agents for those to whom mail is addressed to at their location.

**Single Point Delivery and Elevators:**

- If there is an elevator and if the offices are open to receive mail, or if door slots are provided, delivery is authorized to all floors of office buildings. If there is no elevator, delivery is provided to the first floor, and to the second floor if it is occupied primarily by business offices and if the service is requested.

**Single Point Delivery and Multiple Locations:**

- Delivery service may be provided to one or more central points for the direct receipt of mail by postal customers within the area. The requirements for such central delivery are as follows.
  - The local Postal Service managers must approve the mailbox sites and equipment.
  - Customers must not be required to travel an unreasonable distance to obtain their mail.

**Single Point Delivery and Educational Facilities:**

- If the customer's mail is sent to a College or University mail will be handled in the following manner:
  - **Administration Buildings** \- Mail is delivered to principal administration buildings. Mail undeliverable as addressed or not addressed to a specific building is delivered to the main administration building office for further handling. At larger universities, deliver to the different departments, colleges, faculty buildings, and principal campus structures, such as the Chemistry Building, Engineering Building, and so forth, provided that mail is thus addressed and the volume warrants. Delivery is not to be made to individual administration offices.
  - **Dormitories or Residence Halls** \- Mail is delivered to dormitory buildings and residence halls when addressed to a specific building. Mail is delivered in bulk to a designated representative of the school or property, who then becomes responsible for further distribution to students and residents. Postal Service personnel do not distribute mail into apartment-type mailboxes for dormitories or residence halls. A dormitory building or residence hall often consists of single or multi-room units that may share or have access to centrally located kitchens, bathrooms, showers, or social or common areas. Whether located on or off campus, and regardless of private ownership, such buildings are nevertheless dormitory buildings or residence halls and the building owner or operator is responsible for the final delivery of student mail.

Where no affiliation with the school is established, the Postal Service determines the proper mode of delivery to be established based upon the totality of the circumstances, and may require that designees from the property be identified to accept mail for each location prior to initiating delivery. In making such a determination based upon the totality of the circumstances, the Postal Service weighs the following, among other considerations:

a.  The nature of the leases offered by the property (e.g., whether the leases’ starting month corresponds to academic semesters or other school-related schedules and whether the lease term is for a period of less than one year).

b.  Whether the leases are generally for a bedroom within a multi-bedroom unit or for the entire unit and the scope of the lessee’s liability in case of default.

c.  The Postal Service’s treatment of similarly situated properties.

d.  The property’s proximity to the school.

e.  External information furnished by the school and/or by local government, including any pertinent zoning classification.

f.  Whether the property is primarily marketed to students.

g. The residency turnover rate.

h.  Amenities catering to student populations.

i.  Any other relevant factor.

- **Married Student Housing** \- Apartments and housing units for married students ordinarily are complete quarters consisting of a living room, kitchen-dinette, bedroom, and bath. Whether located on or off campus and regardless of ownership, the apartment mail receptacle requirements apply.
- **Fraternity and Sorority Buildings**\- Deliver mail in bulk to a common mailbox or to a representative of the organization if addressed to a specific building.

## What is Firm Holdout Service?

## Service for customers who receive 50 or more pieces on the first stop on a Carrier Route.      - May 13, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006995

Customer Information

# **What is Firm Holdout Service?**

**Firm Holdout** is a service for customers who receive 50 or more pieces on the first stop on a Carrier Route.

- These customers may pickup their mail at the local Post Office facility once a day.
- There is no additional charge for this service.

## How Redelivery Service Handles Different Mail Types

## Scheduling redelivery with different types of mail.      - Nov 1, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006173

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [**How does Redelivery Handle Priority Mail Express?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#how_does_handle_PME) | [**How does Redelivery Handle an Ordinary Mailpiece (Item with No Extra Services)?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#extra_services) |
| [**How does Redelivery Handle Accountable Mail (Items Requiring a Signature)?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#accountable_mail) | [**What type of Accountable and Non-Accountable Mail Mailpieces Receive a Redelivery Notice?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#non_acountable_mail) |

**How does Redelivery Handle Priority Mail Express?**

If the letter carrier cannot deliver the item (for example, if no one is available to sign for the mailpiece or the sender either requested a signature or did not request “Waiver of Signature”), they will leave a [PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)(there are **no** automatic redelivery attempts made after the first attempt for Priority Mail Express®). If the customer does not schedule a Redelivery of any type, a second _PS Form 3849_ will be left on the 3rd calendar day after the initial attempt.

**Note:** If you do not schedule a Redelivery by the close of business 5 calendar days from the first attempted delivery, the Priority Mail Express® item will be returned to the sender.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#top)

**How does Redelivery Handle an Ordinary Mailpiece (Item with No Extra Services)?**

When someone is normally available to receive parcels, but an ordinary parcel (i.e., a parcel without any extra services) cannot be delivered on the carrier's first attempt, a second delivery attempt is made the next working day and no _PS Form 3849_ is left on the initial attempt. If the parcel cannot be delivered on the second attempt, _PS Form 3849_ is left at the address showing that the parcel is being held.

If no one is available to receive the parcel and the carrier knows that someone at the address is not usually available to receive any parcels; _PS Form 3849_ is completed and left after the first attempt. When it is not known if someone is usually available to receive parcels, _PS Form 3849_ is left after the first attempt. Additional attempts are made only at the customer's request.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#top)

**How does Redelivery Handle Accountable Mail (Items Requiring a Signature)?**

If no one is available when the delivery is attempted the first time, the letter-carrier will leave a _PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You!_

- This form notifies the addressee that an article is being held for them at the Post Office™ and will be available for pick-up the following business day.
- The addressee also has the option to have the item redelivered; **_it will not be done automatically_**.
- 5 days after the first notice is left, a second delivery notice will be left.
- This item will be held for a number of days based on the specific service (usually 15 days).
- If it is not claimed, the item will be returned to the sender.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#top)

**What type of Accountable and Non-Accountable Mail Mailpieces Receive a Redelivery Notice?**

**_Accountable mail_** requires a signature and/or payment of fees. Therefore, you will receive a Redelivery Notice for the following:

- Adult Signature
- Insured Mail (over $500)
- Collect On Delivery (COD)
- Registered Mail® items
- Certified Mail® items
- Signature Confirmation™ service
- Restricted Delivery option
- Domestic Priority Mail Express® service items ( [unless signature waiver is not required](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Waiver-of-Signature-What-is-Signature-Required))
- Return Receipt service (attached to any class of mail)

_**Non-Accountable mail**_ can be delivered to an address without a signature, and/or when the recipient is not present, when the postal carrier believes it can be left securely. Non-accountable mail includes:

- Perishable Items
- USPS Tracking® items
- Uninsured Packages
- Insured Mail (up to $500)

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Redelivery-Service-Handles-Different-Mail-Types#top)

## General Guidelines and Policies for Rural Delivery

## Rural stations and branches are established, and rural delivery is provided, according to USPS policies and procedures, the characteristics of the area to be served, and the methods needed to provide adequate service.      - Nov 14, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006172

Customer Information

**How are USPS® Rural Stations, Branches, and Delivery Service Established?**

Rural stations and branches are established, and rural delivery is provided, according to USPS policies and procedures, the characteristics of the area to be served, and the methods needed to provide adequate service. Requests or petitions to establish, change, or extend rural delivery service, signed by the heads of families wanting this service, must be given to the postmaster of the Post Office from which delivery service is desired, or from which the route operates, as applicable.

Any and all specific questions regarding Rural Delivery are handled by the particular postal unit involved with that route and with the rural carrier delivering to that route.

**General Guidelines**

- Mailbox must be in the rural carrier's line of travel.
- Subject to state laws, the mailbox must be placed on the right hand side of the road, in the direction of travel of the rural carrier, in all cases where traffic conditions are dangerous for the rural carrier to drive to the left to reach the box, or where doing so would violate traffic laws and regulations.
- Requests / petitions for extension of route must be given to the postmaster of the delivery unit.
- On a rural route, more than one (1) family, but not more than five (5) families may use the same mailbox, using the address of the receptacle’s owner and the “care of” address format. A written notice of agreement signed by those who use such a box is filed with the postmaster at the delivery unit.
- Rural carriers are required to stop at a mailbox with the signal flag raised, indicating mail to be picked up, even if there is no mail for delivery.
- Any questions regarding hardship cases or change from rural to door delivery must be referred to the delivery unit.
- For Accountable mail items and oversized parcels (not postage due), the carrier must try to gain the attention of the recipient, which includes honking their horn, in order to get them to come to the vehicle. If the customer doesn't come out and the house/delivery point is within 1/2 mile of the line of travel AND has a passable road, the carrier should attempt to deliver it. Otherwise, the carrier will leave PS Form 3849 and the customer will have to make arrangements to obtain the item.
- Rural carriers are not required to go to the door with a Postage Due letter - only to the mailbox and blow the horn. The customer must come out to the mailbox to pay and/or receive the letter.
- Rural carriers operate a Post Office™ on Wheels and can do the following:
  - Sell stamps
  - Accept/Deliver mailable matter including USPS Ground Advantage™, Priority Mail® and Priority Mail Express® (Registered Mail®, Certified Mail®, Insured & Collect on Delivery \[COD\]).
  - Payment for services and stamps can be made in cash or by check payable to Postmaster, similar to paying fees in a Post Office.
  - Sell money orders, after filing PS Form 6387 - Rural Money Order Transaction Application. Only cash will be accepted for money orders.

**Establishment**

Rural stations and branches are established, and rural delivery is provided, according to:

- USPS policies and procedures
- the characteristics of the area to be served
- the methods needed to provide adequate service.

Requests or petitions to establish, change, or extend rural delivery service, signed by the heads of families wanting this service, must be given to the Postmaster of the Post Office from which delivery service is desired, or from which the route operates, as applicable.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/General-Guidelines-and-Policies-for-Rural-Delivery#top);

## What are Caller and Reserve Box Services?

## Caller Service is usually used by large businesses and institutions, but also available to residential customers.      - Apr 2, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006168

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [**What is Caller Service?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Caller-and-Reserve-Box-Services#what_is_caller_service) | [**What is Reserve Service?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Caller-and-Reserve-Box-Services#what_is_reserve_service) |

**What is Caller Service?**

_Caller Service_ is available for a fee if you require more than standard free carrier service, have more than five PO Boxes at one location, or receive more mail than can be delivered to the largest PO Box. It is available to residential customers as well as businesses. Caller Service is usually used by large businesses and institutions with many separate departments (e.g., banks, universities, prisons, and mortgage companies), including some with unique ZIP codes like the IRS, which has addresses that are only used for taxpayer letters or payments mailed through the USPS.

- _Early Morning Mail_: You can generally pickup your mail in the morning and take care of business earlier in the day. You may still pickup your mail later in the day, if you prefer. Pick up must be arranged with the Post Office. Mail availability via Caller Service will most likely be during low activity times at the Post Office.
- _Security:_ Checks, dividend payments, and other valuable correspondence are secure.
- _Separate Business Address_: You can separate your business mail from your personal mail. That makes it easier to keep track of checks, orders, responses and important correspondence.
- _Accessibility_: You can easily retrieve your mail during Post Office™ operating hours.
- _Travel Convenience_: You can go away on business or vacation trips without worrying about your mail – no need to request a Hold Mail while you are gone. Your mail can pile up fast - we strongly recommend you advise your local Post Office BEFORE you head out of town, especially if you will be away for more than 5 days.

Caller Service is associated with a virtual or PO Box number and requires incoming mail be addressed to that virtual or PO Box number (not a street address). However, if you have a street address, you may still get free delivery by the Carrier.

With Caller Service, you pick up your mail at the Post Office call window or loading dock during the time period designated by your Local Post Office. Anyone authorized to pick up the mail at the call window or loading dock must be listed on the Caller Service application and must bring appropriate **[Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)** when picking up the mail.

**Note:** Caller Service is cancelled with a written notice, failure to pay the required fees, or by submitting a permanent Change of Address (COA). (The postmaster may also terminate service if any standards are violated.)

**Enterprise Post Office Boxes Online** (ePOBOL) allows customers to open, close, and pay for PO Boxes, Caller Service Boxes, and Reserve Boxes online using the Enterprise System (EPS). ePOBOL allows for a centrally managed, secure, online solution for customers with PO Boxes, Caller and Reserve Services. Customers without an EPS account are not able to apply for or renew Caller and Reserve Services at a retail counter.

For more information on Caller Service and other business mail services, please visit  [https://www.usps.com/business/manage-mail.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/manage-mail.htm). For detailed information on fees, see the “Other Services” section of [Notice 123, _USPS Price List_](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Caller-and-Reserve-Box-Services#top)

**What is Reserve Service?**

_Reserve Service_ is a service that can only be used **in conjunction with** a caller box number and is often reserved for “future use”. This means you can use it, for example, when running high volume promotional mailings for business purposes, or when you want to distinguish between types of mail you receive at your caller number address.

Reserve Service is also beneficial when your mail volume starts to grow and you don’t want to have to change your PO Box or virtual Caller Service number.

**Remember:** To order Reserve Service, you **must** have an active caller service number.

**How much do these services cost?**

Caller Service fees are paid on an annual basis. If using the service with a PO Box (POB), add the cost of your POB to the total, yearly fee. Reserve numbers must be paid on an annual basis and expire on December 31. Check with your local Post Office on fees and setting up Reserve Service to meet your mailing needs.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Caller-and-Reserve-Box-Services#top)

## Registered Mail® - The Basics

## General information on Registered Mail service, the mail classes and extra services that are compatible with it, and other characteristics of the service.      - May 14, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006061

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[What are the characteristics of Registered Mail and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#characteristics)** | **[What domestic mail classes and services can be sent as Registered Mail®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#domestic)** | **[Preparing Registered Mail Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#domestic)** |
| **[What is the estimated delivery time for Registered Mail and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#estimated)** | **[Tracking Registered Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#tracking)** | **[Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#Extra)** |
| **[Registered Mail Forms](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#forms)** | **[Is Registered Mail® Eligible for a Refund?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#Refund)** |  |

**What are the characteristics of Registered Mail and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery?**

The most secure United States Postal Service® mail service (protected by safes, cages, sealed containers, locks, and keys).

- Must be presented to a retail employee or rural carrier.
- Requires a signature upon delivery.
- Mailers using Registered Mail Restricted Delivery can direct delivery only to the addressee (or addressee's authorized agent).
- Delivery information provides delivery status or attempted delivery status when the item reaches its destination. [Tracking](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#tracking) is not provided as the item is en route to its destination.
- Registered Mail can be sent to military locations. However, delivery information is not available for items destined to an APO / FPO / DPO.
- A system of receipts is provided to monitor movement of mail from the point of acceptance to delivery. These receipts are not readily available and can only be accessed by initiating a [claims](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) form, which cannot be completed by phone.

**What domestic mail classes and services can be sent as Registered Mail®?**

Domestic Registered Mail is only available for Mail Classes:

- First-Class Mail®
- USPS Ground Advantage® - Commercial
- USPS Ground Advantage® - Retail
- Priority Mail®

**Preparing Registered Mail Packages**

**USPS Assistance with Preparing Registered Mail Packages**

USPS employees are not permitted to help customers prepare or seal mail to be registered. Any envelope or package that appears to have been opened and resealed, or otherwise improperly prepared, may not be registered.

Accepting Registered Mail items for mailing and shipping can be done by retail associates at any post office, station, or branch. Rural carriers can also accept Registered Mail, provided the required postage and fees are prepaid.

**Sending Cash**

Commercial customers shipping cash amounts of over $500 are required to use Registered Mail. In addition, commercial mailers cannot use USPS packaging to ship cash no matter the amount.

Non-commercial customers mailing cash amounts up to and over $500, and commercial customers mailing cash amounts up to $500, can continue to use services other than Registered Mail. However, only Registered Mail includes insurance payable at full value up to the limit of $50,000. See “ [What Extra Services are available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#Extra)” for additional information.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#top)

**What is the estimated delivery time for Registered Mail and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery?**

- The expected delivery date provided is based on the class of mail the item was mailed with.
- Registered Mail is kept highly secured and is processed manually, which naturally slows the speed at which it travels.
- **Registered Mail is not recommended if speed of delivery is important.**

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#top)

**Tracking Registered Mail**

Registered Mail (domestic and international) service and Registered Mail Restricted Delivery service alone cannot be tracked through the mailstream. Updated scans are not provided as the piece is processed from facility to facility. However, those mail services provide delivery information when a scan shows either a delivery status or information on an attempted delivery, which can be retrieved in [several ways](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Proof-of-Delivery).

**What Extra Services are available?**

**Insurance:** You must declare the full value of the mailpiece, when presenting it for mailing. Insurance matching the declared value of the item(s) is included up to $50,000.

- Articles with a declared value of more than $50,000.00 can be registered, but compensation for loss or damage is limited to $50,000.00.
  - To determine the price of items over $50,000.00, customers should contact their local Post Office™.

Registered Mail may only be sent without postal insurance, if the item(s) has no declared value. **Go to**: [What Domestic Mail Extra Services are Available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available)

**Note:** Mail travels at the speed of the **mail class** the additional service is added to. Due to the higher level of security placed on this service, it is possible that the Registered Mail process could slow the speed at which a mailpiece travels.

**Registered Mail Forms**

- [PS Form 3806 (Receipt for Registered Mail](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3806.pdf) [)](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3806.pdf)
- [PS Form 200-N (Registered Mail Label 200-N)](http://store.usps.com/store/product/shipping-supplies/registered-mail-label-200n-P_LABEL200N) \- Used when a mailer-generated shipping label bearing an IMpb or IMmb (such as a PC Postage label) is also affixed on the same item.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#top)

## **Is Registered Mail® Eligible for a Refund?**

The Registered Mail® fee is not eligible for a refund after the Postal Service™ accepts the item. However, if the article is lost and a claim is made, the postage will be refunded, but not the Registered Mail fee.

​​​​​​

**The declared value of an item must be determined using the table below:**

| **Item** | **Maximum Replacement Price** |
| --- | --- |
| **Merchandise** | Market value or cost |
| **Money / Cash / Currency** | Full value |
| **Nonvaluables** \- Matter not having intrinsic value such as letters, files, records, etc. | No value or replacement cost if postal insurance coverage desired [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#table_note) |
| **Negotiable Instruments (Documents)** \- Instruments payable to bearer, including stock certificates and money orders endorsed in blank. | Market value (value based on value at time of mailing). |
| **Nonnegotiable Instruments (Documents)**\- Registered bonds, checks, money orders, and drafts made out to specific recipients, deeds, wills, and similar documents. Stock certificates are considered nonnegotiable unless endorsed in blank. | No value or replacement cost if postal insurance coverage desired [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#table_note) |
| **Jewelry, Gems, Precious Metals** | Market value or cost |
| \*A mailer who does not know replacement costs should contact a person or firm familiar with such documents and determine replacement costs before mailing the articles. |

**International Registered Mail Service Information**

Information about International Registered Mail Service can be found at[Registered Mail® International](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International).

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics#top)

## Credit Card Fraud

## Available resources if someone fraudulently applies for a credit card using your name.      - Aug 13, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007984

Customer Information

**What you can do if someone fraudulently applies for a credit card in your name.**

1. If a fraudulent credit card application was completed in your name, you should immediately report the fraud to the issuing credit card company's fraud department.
2. Next, contact the consumer fraud victim assistance office at one of the three major credit-reporting services (Equifax, Experian, or Trans Union). You have the right to ask them to place a fraud alert in your file to inform potential creditors and others that you may be a victim of identity theft.

**TIP**: Ask **1** of the **3** credit reporting companies to put a fraud alert on your credit report. They must tell the other 2 companies – make sure to confirm that this will be done. A fraud alert is free, but you must provide proof of your identity.

- [Equifax](http://www.equifax.com/CreditReportAssistance/ "Equifax") \- 800-525-6285
- [Experian](https://www.experian.com/consumer/cac/InvalidateSession.do?code=SECURITYALERT "Experian") \- 888-397-3742
- [Trans Union](http://www.transunion.com/fraud-victim-resource/place-fraud-alert "Trans Union") \- 800-680-7289

A fraud alert can make it more difficult for someone to get credit in your name, because it tells creditors to follow certain procedures to protect you. However, it  may also delay your ability to obtain credit.

- You may have additional rights available to you under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, including the right to receive a free credit report.For additional information, visit the [Federal Trade Commission's website](http://www.consumer.ftc.gov/articles/0216-protecting-against-credit-card-fraud).
- You should also fill out and submit the [Identity Theft Complaint Form](https://ehome.uspis.gov/mailtheft/idtheft.aspx) online, to lodge a complaint with the U.S. Postal Inspection Service.

See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Identity Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft), and [Mail Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud).
