Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000005ULAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost) ## gopost® ## A convenient way to receive and ship packages. How to register for an account and links to additional gopost resources. - Oct 2, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007973 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**How do I order an item and have it shipped to gopost®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#5) | [**How do I ship a package from gopost®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#9) | [**What types of packages can be received or shipped via gopost®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#10) | | [**How do I register and get authenticated for gopost®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#14) | **[gopost® Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup)** | **[USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker)** | **What is gopost®?** gopost® is a convenient way to receive and ship packages, only from the United States Postal Service®. gopost® units are automated, secured, self-service parcel lockers placed in convenient locations where you can pick up or ship packages at your convenience. And at no additional cost to you. For more information on what to do when you receive a notice of a missed package delivery, see [gopost® Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup). **When can you use gopost®?** Anytime. You can access gopost units 24 hours a day, 7 days a week so you can pick up or ship out packages when it suits your schedule without having to go to the Post Office™—as long as the gopost unit is operational and available to the public. **Where are gopost® units?** gopost units are located at convenient locations to accommodate your busy lifestyle. The Postal Service is installing units near certain Post Offices™, grocery stores, pharmacies, transportation hubs, shopping centers and more. [Find gopost locations](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action "find gopost"). **How does gopost® work?** Each gopost unit contains dozens of individual parcel lockers that can be used to receive or ship packages. Once you [register](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#14) for gopost, you will receive an account number/access card and PIN so you can access the unit. You will not be assigned a specific parcel locker; rather, packages are placed in available lockers that may accommodate the size of your package. If you use the gopost unit as your shipping address, your packages will be delivered there. You can also use the gopost unit to ship your prepaid packages. Once you register and begin using gopost units, you will receive communication through email or text notifications. To help ensure you receive emails properly, please check your mail filter so that emails from the U.S. Postal Service, [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and gopost are allowed. **How do I order an item and have it shipped to gopost®?** Follow these steps when ordering an item: 1. Determine the gopost address of the unit where you would like to pick-up your shipment. The gopost address contains the gopost unit number followed by the word "gopost". [Find gopost locations](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action "find gopost"). 2. Place and complete your order from your desired merchant (e.g., via online, telephone). If the merchant offers a choice, select the U.S. Postal Service as your shipper. If not, we are partnering with key shipping competitors to assure your packages are delivered successfully to our gopost units in most instances. When asked for the shipping address, use your name, gopost account number and the address of the gopost unit (as shown in example below) where you want to receive your shipment. You must include your gopost account number so that we can notify you when the package arrives. Do not include the gopost location street address number and street name in the address. ![here's how to address a package for gopost: your name; your gopost unit number, e.g. 4505 gopost, your gopost account number, e.g. XXXXXXX; City, State, ZIP Code TM, e.g. Anywhere, VA 12345-2345](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/receiving_mail/gopost/addressing_package_gopost.png) When USPS® delivers your package to a gopost unit, you will receive an email or text notification that you have a package to pick up. Simply visit the gopost unit to pick up your package. If a properly-sized parcel locker is not immediately available for your package, you will be notified that it will soon be available for pick-up at the gopost unit. Your package will be given the highest delivery priority for the next available locker of the proper size. **Remember:** You must be [registered](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#14) to use the gopost service. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#top) **How will I know my package has been delivered to a gopost® unit?** When your package is delivered to the gopost unit, you will receive an email and/or text message (you specify preference). **How do I pick up my package after I am notified that it has been delivered?** To pick up a package at a gopost unit: 1. Scan your gopost card and enter your PIN on the gopost user screen; the screen will display the parcel locker where your package was delivered and the parcel locker will light up and automatically unlock.\* 2. Open the locker and remove your package. The locker will shut and lock automatically only after it is empty. 3. If you requested a receipt, collect the paper receipt from the unit. Once you have removed your package, you will automatically receive an electronic receipt via email or text.(\*). If your package requires a signature, you will be instructed to sign your name directly on the screen using your finger before the locker will unlock. \*You have a maximum of 15 days to pick up your package at a gopost unit (exception: 5 days for Priority Mail Express®). After 15 days, the package will be returned to the sender. International packages will be removed from the unit after 15 days and taken to a nearby post office. You will be notified of the post office's address. After 15 days at the post office, the package will be returned to sender. **Can I use gopost® to receive packages from friends and family?** Yes. Just make sure you ask the sender to use the correct address format including your account number. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#top) **How do I ship a package?** Your package must have a label that indicates that postage has been prepaid with a barcode that enables tracking. Acceptable labels include: Click-N-Ship®, PC Postage®, and merchant-paid Return Services. The gopost units cannot currently dispense postage. To ship a package: 1. Visit any convenient gopost unit to send a pre-paid barcoded package. [Find gopost locations](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action "find gopost"). 2. Scan your gopost card and enter your PIN on the gopost screen. 3. Select "Ship Packages". 4. Scan the USPS® barcode label on the front of the package using the unit's barcode scanner and follow instructions on the screen to complete your transaction. 5. If you requested a receipt, collect your paper receipt from the unit. You will automatically receive an electronic receipt via email or text. **What types of packages can be received or shipped via gopost®?** While we prefer you choose the U.S. Postal Service for your shipping needs, we understand that many online merchants do not offer a clear choice of shippers. For receiving packages using gopost, we are partnering with key shipping competitors to enable your online orders to be delivered to your preferred gopost unit address. For shipping packages using gopost, USPS package services are required. USPS® package mail classes allowed include: 1. Any Priority Mail® mailpiece 2. Any Priority Mail Express® mailpiece 3. Other package types that are at least 3/4" thick Postal Service mailing regulations apply. Our gopost units also accept your Return Merchandise Labels to make your returns even easier. There are three parcel locker sizes at each gopost unit to accommodate various size packages: 1. Large: up to 12"W x 15"D x 18-1/2"H (maximum size) 2. Medium: up to 12"W x 15"D x 6-3/4"H 3. Small: up to 12"W x 15"D x 3"H Our National Address Management Service recognizes gopost addresses and formats as acceptable for addressing packages. If the sender will not accept the gopost unit address, please contact the sender and discuss this with them. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#top) **What type of domestic packages _cannot_ be received or shipped via gopost® at this time?** 1. Letters or flat-sized envelopes less than 3/4" thick (other than Priority or Priority Mail Express) 2. Packages larger than the maximum size (12"W x 15"D x 18-1/2"H) 3. Packages received/shipped with the following Extra Services: | | | | --- | --- | | - Certified Mail®
- Return Receipt (hardcopy "green card")
- Collect On Delivery (COD)
- Registered® Mail | - Adult Signature
- Restricted Delivery
- Special Handling | Prohibited items may be returned to sender. For packages too large for gopost, USPS will hold the package at a nearby Post Office and will send you the Post Office's address via email/text. **What type of international packages can be received or shipped via gopost®?** Inbound packages that do not require customs fees can be received via the gopost unit. International Extra Services that cannot be included are Restricted Delivery, Registered Mail, and Return Receipt. Outbound international and APO/FPO packages cannot be shipped via the gopost unit at this time. **Do I have to use gopost® for all my packages?** You are not _required_ to use gopost for all your packages. You can use gopost for specific incoming packages or for as many incoming packages as you wish. You have control over where you want to receive your packages based on the address you provide the shipper—home, work or gopost. **How do I register and get authenticated for gopost®?** To register and get authenticated for gopost, follow these quick and easy steps: - Go to [gopost.usps.com](https://gopost.usps.com/go) [/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go) and click the **Register** button. To register for gopost, you need to sign in or sign up for a [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account (it's free). - Enter all required information for [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and gopost - including a Personal Identification Number (PIN) and your preferred authentication option. (For details, see [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#authentication_options).) - You'll receive an email confirming that you are pre-registered for gopost with your gopost account number. You should receive your gopost card and packet in the mail within 7-10 days. The next step for completing your registration is authentication. You may select one of two options: - **Authenticate at gopost unit:** This option allows you to start using gopost immediately to **receive** packages - even before you receive your gopost card and packet in the mail. To authenticate your account at a gopost unit, simply scan your Driver's License or state-issued ID card on the screen display at a gopost unit. (You can do this when you log in to pick up your first package.) Once your ID is accepted, you will receive an email confirming that you have a fully activated account and you may then use gopost to ship **and** receive packages. For more information, see [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#18). - **Authenticate at Post Office** If you select this option, you must wait until you have received the gopost packet in the mail. Once you authenticate at a Post Office, you will receive an email confirming that your account is fully activated for sending or receiving packages. To authenticate your account at a Post Office, take the completed Authentication Sheet and gopost card you received in the mail to a location listed at [https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action). Show a government-issued picture ID. We will then send you an email confirming that you have a fully activated account. Your new gopost account and card is for you only. Like a credit or debit card, only you should use your gopost card and assigned PIN. Other adults (18 or over) interested in using gopost may register free of charge for their own account, card, and PIN. **NOTE:** For gopost, you must register as an individual even if you are a business user; gopost accounts are issued in the names of individuals, not companies. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#top) **How do I make changes to my gopost® account?** To make changes to your gopost account, go to [gopost.usps.com](https://gopost.usps.com/go) [/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go) and click **Register or edit your account**. Once you sign in, you make changes to your account information. **Can I order a replacement gopost® card?** Yes. To order a replacement card, go to [gopost.usps.com/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go) and click **Register or edit your account**. Once you sign in, you can select "Replace Lost Card" under **Special Requests** and request a new gopost card. **What are the gopost® registration requirements?** When you register for gopost, you specify notification and authentication options and are required to select a Personal Identification Number (PIN). The USPS requires customer authentication to meet security requirements. **How do I authenticate my account at the gopost® unit?** If you would like to start receiving packages through gopost right away and you prefer to authenticate at the gopost unit, select this option during the registration process at [gopost.usps.com/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go). If you select this option, you'll need to provide your Driver's License or state-issued ID number during the online registration process and your gopost account will be temporarily authorized. To complete the authentication step, simply scan the barcode on the back of your ID at the gopost unit. If the information matches what you provided online, you will receive an email that your temporary account has been fully activated and you may send and receive packages once your account is activated. **Important:** Your temporary gopost unit account authorization allows you to log into the gopost unit up to three (3) times to pick up packages that you have sent to the unit. After that, if you have not received confirmation that your account is activated, your account will be suspended and you must authenticate your account at a Post Office location listed at [https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLAction!locations.action](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLAction!locations.action). **For information or assistance:** For the most up-to-date information and usage terms, you may also call 1-USPS-MY GOPOST (1-877-769-4676) [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title gopost® URL Name gopost [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Mailboxes](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGLGA2/mailboxes) ## Related Articles - [gopost® Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup) - [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) - [I Received a Vacant Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/I-Received-a-Vacant-Notice) - [Commercial Mail Receiving Agency (CMRA)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Commercial-Mail-Receiving-Agency-CMRA) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading gopost®

The Basics ## New option for USPS Tracking service that extends the length of time you can access the tracking history of a domestic package. - Jan 20, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006092 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[What is USPS Tracking Plus®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#description)** | **[What are the benefits of USPS Tracking Plus®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#benefits)** | **[What products are eligible for USPS Tracking Plus®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products)** | | [**Do I need a USPS.com® Account to purchase this service**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#usps_account) | [**When can I purchase this service**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#purchase_timeframe) | [**How do I purchase this service**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#how_to_purchase) | | [**Can I purchase this service for multiple packages at once**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#multiple_packages) | [**Can I get a refund**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#refund) | [**Can I receive a report of my tracking history**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#report) | | [**Can I purchase this service at my local Post Office® retail counter**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#purchase_at_counter) | [**Is it available for International mail pieces**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#international) | [**Can the service be extended after the retention period**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#extension) | # **What is USPS Tracking Plus®?** USPS Tracking Plus® enables you to extend your access to the tracking history for your package on USPS.com. Currently, [access to tracking information for non-signature items is limited to 120 days. Tracking information for signature items is available for 2 years](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics). USPS Tracking Plus® provides options to extend access to the tracking history for anywhere from an additional 6 months up to 10 years for a [small fee](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus). With USPS Tracking Plus®, in addition to keeping your tracking records online for up to 10 years, you can also request a USPS Tracking Plus® Statement via email at any time during that period. | **Available Retention Timelines for USPS Tracking Plus® History** | | --- | | **Packages without a Signature** | **Packages with a Signature** | **Fee** | | 6 months | -- | See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) | | 1 year | -- | | 3 years | 3 years | | 5 years | 5 years | | 7 years | 7 years | | 10 years [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#retention_timeline-note-1) | 10 years [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#retention_timeline-note-1) | | \*Available for Domestic Products Only | # **What are the benefits of USPS Tracking Plus®?** By purchasing this service, you will have access to your online tracking history for up to ten years for Domestic products and seven years for International products. Along with the extended access, your USPS Tracking Plus® Statement may be used as: - USPS evidence of delivery to resolve disputes and claims - Critical evidence of mailing and or delivery / delivery attempt for legal and court proceedings - Official authentic source of data that legal and financial sources may accept Small businesses, law offices, ecommerce marketplace, financial institutions, and pharmaceutical providers can benefit from extended access to tracking data for [Proof of Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Proof-of-Delivery) to resolve disputes and claims. # **What products are eligible for USPS Tracking Plus®?** | Domestic Products | International Outbound Products | | --- | --- | | - Priority Mail Express® Service
- Priority Mail® Service
- USPS Ground Advantage®
- Parcel Select® Service
- Bound Printed Matter
- Library Mail
- Media Mail®
- First-Class Mail® (Letters and Flats) [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products_table_note-1)
- USPS Marketing Mail® parcels [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products_table_note-1)
- USPS Marketing Mail® Nonprofit parcels [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products_table_note-1) | - Priority Mail Express International®
- Priority Mail International®
- First-Class Package International Service® (where Electronic USPS Delivery Confirmation® International Service is available) | | \*With purchase of a trackable extra service | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#top) # **Do I need a USPS.com® Account to purchase this service?** Yes, a USPS.com account is required to complete your purchase. If you do not have an account, you will need to [create an account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=UspsTools&appURL=https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) to process your order. See [USPS.com® Account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) for more information. # **When can I purchase USPS Tracking Plus®?** You may purchase USPS Tracking Plus® from the first day your tracking history is available on USPS.com up until the last day. [Depending on the product](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics), the last day may be anywhere between 120 days for non-signature items up to 2 years for signature items. # **How do I purchase USPS Tracking Plus®?** After you enter an [eligible tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products) into the [USPS Tracking® website](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input), the “USPS Tracking Plus® Available” feature will display on your status page above the status bar. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **1** | Below the status bar, click " _**USPS Tracking Plus®**_" to expand the section to view your current online tracking history expiration date and your extension options. | ![USPS Tracking results page screenshot. The status of the item is Delivered. The "drawers" near the bottom that can be clicked and opened are labeled: "Text & Email Updates", "Tracking History", "USPS Tracking Plus™", and "Product Information".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_1_USPS_Tracking_Plus_Available.jpg)
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **2** | Select the length of time you would like to extend access for your package by clicking on one of the retention options listed. | ![USPS Tracking results page screenshot. The status of the item is Delivered. The "drawers" near the bottom that can be clicked and opened are labeled: "Text & Email Updates", "Tracking History", "USPS Tracking Plus", and "Product Information". The "USPS Tracking Plus" drawer is opened with a message, a red circle around the 6 time/price options, and a button labeled "Select for Purchase".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_2_USPS_options.selection.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and are subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **3** | Click “ _**Confirm Selection**_” button. After your purchase, your online tracking availability date will update to reflect your NEW expiration date.
- **Note:** You can purchase extended history only once and will not have an opportunity to modify your selection at a later date. | ![USPS Tracking results page screenshot. The status of the item is Delivered. The "drawers" near the bottom that can be clicked and opened are labeled: "Text & Email Updates", "Tracking History", "USPS Tracking Plus", and "Product Information". The "USPS Tracking Plus" drawer is opened with a message, the 6 time/price options, and a red circle around a button labeled "Select for Purchase".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_3_USPS_options.selection.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **4** | After you confirm selection, you will be directed to the USPS.com online account page to finalize your order. If you do not have an account, you will need to create an account to process your order. See [USPS.com® Account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) for more information. | ![A screenshot of the USPS.com Account login page. Textboxes for "Username" and "Password" are in the left half side of the screen, in the center. "Sign In" button is below."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/1.24.21_No_4_USPS_account_login.jpg)
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **5** | Once you login, your name and email information will be prepopulated. Select “ **_Add to Cart_**” to proceed with your order. **Note**: If you currently have any items in your cart, you must complete the purchase or they will be automatically removed from the cart. | ![USPS Tracking results page screenshot. The status of the item is Delivered. The "drawers" near the bottom that can be clicked and opened are labeled: "Text & Email Updates", "Tracking History", "USPS Tracking Plus", and "Product Information". The "USPS Tracking Plus" drawer is opened, and after a time/price was selected, the first paragraph references the timing of the tracking extension, fee, tracking number, the new date of the tracking history availability. The next section has text boxes to enter "First Name", "Last Name", and "Email Address". The next section is the Privacy Act Statement. The final section is the "Add to Cart" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/1.24.21_No_5_USPS_Another_Package.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **6** | Your shopping cart will display your order summary: _Tracking number, Price, Extended period._ | ![Shopping Cart screenshot. The left "Item" section/column has "USPS Tracking Plus; 6 months Extended History; Tracking Number; email address". The next column has a clickable "Remove" option. The third column has "Price", the fourth column is "QTY" (quantity); and the fifth column is "Total". The section to the right is the "Order Summary" which includes the total and the "Checkout" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_6_USPS_Tracking_Shopping_Cart_a.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **7** | If you are ready to check out, click the Check Out Now box. You will be sent to the order screen to complete the order. | ![Shopping Cart screenshot. The left "Item" section/column has "USPS Tracking Plus; 6 months Extended History; Tracking Number; email address". The next column has a clickable "Remove" option. The third column has "Price", the fourth column is "QTY" (quantity); and the fifth column is "Total". The section to the right is the "Order Summary" which includes the total and the "Checkout" button.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_6_USPS_Tracking_Shopping_Cart.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **8** | Upon successful completion of your purchase, your USPS Tracking Plus® statement will be sent to the email address provided. After your purchase, you can request additional USPS Tracking Plus® Statements on the USPS Tracking web page throughout your extension period. | [![Screenshot of a confirmation page that "Your Order Has Been Placed!" After the "Success" message is the "Order Number". The next lines are "A confirmation email was sent to xxxx@xxxx.gov". "Your Premium Tracking Statement will be sent to the email address provided." "You can review your order by clicking on the order number now or going to the Orders section." "Return to Tracking to purchase Premium Tracking for another tracking number." Below is a section indicating "Status", "Order Placed", "Bill To", and what the "Payment" was. The final section below is the Shopping Cart information (Item, Price, QTY, and Total).](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_7_USPS_Tracking_Order_Placed.jpg)![Screenshot of a confirmation page that "Your Order Has Been Placed!" After the "Success" message is the "Order Number". The next lines are "A confirmation email was sent to xxxx@xxxx.gov". "Your Premium Tracking Statement will be sent to the email address provided." "You can review your order by clicking on the order number now or going to the Orders section." "Return to Tracking to purchase Premium Tracking for another tracking number." Below is a section indicating "Status", "Order Placed", "Bill To", and what the "Payment" was. The final section below is the Shopping Cart information (Item, Price, QTY, and Total).](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_7_USPS_Tracking_Order_Placed_Part2.jpg)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_No_7_USPS_Tracking_Order_Placed.jpg)
**Note**: Listed prices are up-to-date as of 1/25/2022 and subject to price change. See [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_USPS%20Tracking%20Plus) for current prices.
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#top) # **Can I purchase USPS Tracking Plus® for multiple packages at once?** Yes. You can purchase USPS Tracking Plus® for more than one package at a time. To do so, press the "Save & Continue" button to add more USPS Tracking Plus selections to your cart until you are ready to complete your purchase. # **Can I get a refund for USPS Tracking Plus®?** No. All orders are final and are not eligible for a refund. # **Can I receive a report of my tracking history?** Yes. A statement of tracking, the USPS Tracking Plus® Statement (see below), will be provided for each purchase via email. After your purchase, you can request additional reports on the USPS Tracking web page throughout your extension period. **Note:** If your tracking statement was prepared before the item reached its final destination, the tracking history will continue to refresh and populate status updates as the item travels through the USPS network. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Image of USPS Tracking Plus® Statement for a Non-Signature Product** | ![Screenshot of USPS Premium Tracking Statement of an item that did not require a signature. Upper righthand corner has "Premium Tracking Statement As of 10/10/2019". Below is where the Tracking Number is listed. The rest of the page is a table listing "Date & Time", "Status of Item", and "Location". Under the table is the Note "If this report was sent before the item reached its final destination, the tracking history will continue to refresh and populate status updates as the item travels through the USPS® network.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_tracking_plus_tracking_statement_nonsignature_v2.jpg)
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | | **Image of USPS Tracking Plus® Statement for a Signature Product** | **Page 1**
![Screenshot of USPS Premium Tracking Statement of an item that does require a signature. Upper righthand corner has "Premium Tracking Statement As of February 5, 2020". Below is where the Tracking Number is listed. The rest of the page is a table listing "Date & Time", "Status of Item", and "Location".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_tracking_plus_tracking_statement_signature_pg1_v2.jpg)
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | **Page 2**
![Screenshot of the last page of a USPS Premium Tracking Statement of an item that does require a signature. Upper righthand corner has "Premium Tracking Statement As of February 5, 2020". Below is where the Tracking Number is listed. A row indicates the "Signature" section which is a box with the words "Signature", "Address", the recipient's signature, and the address. The last portion of the page has the following: "Note: Scanned image may reflect a different destination address due to delivery instructions on file. If the Premium Tracking statement was prepared before the item reached its final destination, the tracking history will continue to refresh and populate status updates as the item travels through the USPS® network. Return to USPS.com to prepare a new Premium Tracking Statement.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tracking/3.8.22_tracking_plus_tracking_statement_signature_pg2_v2.jpg)
**Note**: Click image to make larger. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#top) # **Can I purchase USPS Tracking Plus® at my local Post Office® retail counter?** USPS Tracking Plus® service is only available online through the USPS Tracking webpage. # **Is USPS Tracking Plus® available for International mail pieces?** USPS Tracking Plus® is available for [International outbound packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics#eligible_products). # **Can USPS Tracking Plus® be extended after the retention period?** No, the service cannot be extended but the Archive feature allows access to tracking history if the retention period has already expired. ## Are Retail products purchased at a Post Office™ eligible for a refund?

## If Retail products purchased at a local Post Office facility are eligible for a refund.      - Dec 16, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000005306

Customer Information

Retail products purchased at a local Post Office™ facility may be eligible for a refund. If you have questions about your retail transaction or would like to request a refund for a retail item purchased, you may take the retail item and the receipt to the post office where you made your purchase and they will assist you. However, there is no guarantee that a refund will be issued.

Some retail products such as shipping products, tapes, greeting cards or gift cards purchased at a Post Office™ are final and are not eligible for a refund.

## How do I apply to Use Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail?

## Information about applying for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail?      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007677

Customer Information

**Application Process**

A single authorization is required to mail at the Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail (formerly called Standard Mail) prices throughout the United States. Once an organization is approved to mail at the Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, it may make mailings at those prices at any PostalOne! entry location.  Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail must be deposited at a Post Office facility where the organization producing the mailing has an approved mailing permit.

Mailers will continue to be responsible for the payment of applicable permit imprint or annual mailing fees at all entry locations.

In order to apply for authorization to mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, please complete the following steps:

1. Complete _PS Form 3624 - Application to Mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail Prices_, available on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ( [http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3624.pdf](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3624.pdf)).

2. You will need to provide evidence that the organization is Nonprofit. Acceptable evidence includes:
   - An Internal Revenue Service (IRS) letter of exemption from payment of federal income tax.
   - If an IRS exemption letter is not available, a complete financial statement from an independent auditor (such as a certified public accountant) substantiating that the organization is nonprofit. A statement from a member of the organization is not sufficient (do not submit State tax exemption information).
3. You will need to provide documents describing the organization's primary purpose, such as:
   - Organizing instruments that state the purpose for which the group is organized, such as the constitution, articles of incorporation, articles of association, or trust indenture. The organizing instrument, including all amendments to the original, should bear the seal, certification, or signature of the Secretary of State or other appropriate state official. If one or more of these documents are not sealed, certified, or signed by state officials, an officer or other person authorized to sign for the applicant should submit a written declaration certifying that the documents are complete and accurate copies of the originals.
   - Materials showing how the organization actually operated during the previous 6 to 12 months and how it will operate in the future. Bulletins, financial statements, membership forms, publications produced by the organization, minutes of meetings, or a list of its activities may be used.
4. The application must be signed by someone in authority in the organization, such as the president or treasurer. It must not be signed by a printer or mailing agent.

5. Submit the completed _PS Form 3624_ to the Post Office where Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail mailings will be deposited. If the application is approved, the authorization will apply nationwide.

**Using an Agent**

As an organization authorized to mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, you can use a commercial firm to prepare and present your mailing for you at those prices. The issue is whether the arrangement is a legitimate "principal-agent" relationship.

- If you are paying a fee to the mailing house to prepare your mailing, and the mailing house has no other interest in the mailing, it should be eligible for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices.
- However, if the mailing house shares in the advertising revenues, places its own advertisement(s) in the mailpiece at no cost, or at a reduced price, or will not receive any fees for its services (reimbursement for expenses), the mailpiece may not be eligible for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices. Under this arrangement, the mailing house now has a vested interest in the mailing and is no longer functioning in an agent capacity.

**Maintaining Nonprofit Status**

The Postal Service™ will allow nonprofits to use professional fundraising organizations without jeopardizing their eligibility to mail Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail. This decision will allow nonprofits to increase the potential pool of charitable donors when the economy is weak and public resources are being diverted.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-apply-to-Use-Nonprofit-USPS-Marketing-Mail#top)

## Refunds for Premium Forwarding Service Residential®

## Premium Forwarding Service-Residential cancellation and refund policies.      - Feb 7, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007743

Customer Information

**Are refunds permitted for PFS Residential?**

The Premium Forwarding Service Residential® (PFS-Residential®) enrollment fee is nonrefundable even if the customer terminates service early.

Refunds may be available for those customers whose shipments were not processed in a given week, were charged, and did not receive the shipment. All refund requests are reviewed for eligibility.

**NOTE**: The following are not eligible for a refund, **at any time**:

- Enrollment fee
- Weekly shipping fees, **unless** pre-paid at Retail and unused
- Delayed shipments

**PFS-Residential® at Retail**

**Refund Request for Service Termination**: If you pay for the service at Retail, you prepaid the weekly shipping charge. If you request that your Premium Forwarding Service Residential® ends early (cancel the PFS-Residential®), the Postal Service can refund the portion of the weekly shipment charge that was prepaid, but has not been used.

For example, a customer who terminates PFS-Residential® early (e.g., a customer prepays for 10 weeks but returns to a primary address after 8 weeks) may request a refund for any unused weekly shipment charges from the Post Office™ facility serving the primary address.

To request a refund, start by contacting your primary Post Office™ facility (the Post Office where you ordered PFS-Residential®) and fill out PS Form 3533, _Application for Refund of Fees, Products and Withdrawal of Customer Accounts, Modified With Barcode_, and present a valid form of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).

PFS-Residential® shipments are placed in the mail each Wednesday, so you must cancel your request by close of business (COB) on Tuesday to receive any prepaid fee refund.

If you request a refund for early termination, you will be issued a no-fee money order right away.

**_Remember_: ONLY the portion of the weekly shipment charge that has not been used is refundable.**

**Refund Request for Service Issue**: Contact your Local Post Office™ facility to file a service complaint and request a PFS refund. You will need to fill out Form 3533 and present a valid form of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).

If you request a refund due to a service issue, your request will be processed in 2-3 business days. Processing may take longer if additional information is needed. Once your request is processed, you will be notified via phone or email if your service-related refund request was approved or denied.

If approved, you will be mailed a no-fee money order to your primary address. The refund covers any shipping fees, only.(Enrollment fees are not refundable) Handling fees are not refundable.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refunds-for-Premium-Forwarding-Service-Residential#back_to_top)

**PFS-Residential® Online**

**Early Service Termination**: If you enroll in PFS-Residential® using the Online application, the credit card designated to process the weekly shipment fee is charged weekly. You can stop the PFS-Residential® shipment and future weekly shipment charges by going online and updating your information.

**Timing**: If the service is not stopped before the weekly fee is charged, that fee is not refundable. In order to cancel without getting charged for the upcoming week, you must cancel your PFS-Residential® enrollment by Monday at 11:59pm CST. If you do not cancel the PFS-Residential® enrollment before Monday at 11:59pm, CST, your credit card will be charged the weekly shipment fee and there are **no refunds** for this fee.

**Refund Request for Service Issues**: You can submit a refund request for PFS-Residential® created online by following directions on the [Global Refund Page](https://uprt.usps.com/pfs/pfsRefund.html "Global Refund Page").

To request a Premium Forward Service Residential® (PFS-Residential®) refund, you must provide, at a minimum:

- The Order Number for the weekly shipment(s) you wish to refund;
- The reason for the refund request; and
- The specific PFS-Residential® shipment you are requesting the refund for (please, include dates).

[Request a PFS Refund](https://uprt.usps.com/pfs/pfsRefund.html "Request a PFS Refund")

Your request will be processed in 2-3 business days. Once your request is processed, you will be notified via email if it was approved or denied. Processing may take longer if additional information is needed. If approved, please allow up to 2 billing cycles from notification for a refund to appear on the credit card associated with the account.

**Checking Your Online PFS Refund Status:** If you submitted a refund request for the PFS-Residential® order made online, you can check the status of your order at your convenience by viewing the order in your [Activity History](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=ATG&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fstore.usps.com%2Fstore%2Fmyaccount%2FmyOrders.jsp%3Fselpage%3DMY%2BORDERS).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Refunds-for-Premium-Forwarding-Service-Residential#back_to_top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000001o7YAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS) ## Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS) ## The Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS) was created as an optional program to facilitate and simplify the declaration and payment of the value added tax (VAT) for goods sold from a distance by sellers from either the European Union (EU) or from a non- EU country or territory. - Sep 2, 2021 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007501 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[What is the Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS)?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#Import_One-Stop_Shop)** | [**What does this mean for me as a USPS® customer?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#USPS_customer) | [**What does the IOSS VAT Identification Number look like?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#IOSS_VAT_Identification_Number) | | [**What do I need to do as a USPS® customer?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#need_to_do) | [**What does USPS® do with this information?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#information) | [**Additional Information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#additional_information) | # What is the Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS)? On July 1, 2021, the value added tax (VAT) exemption for the importation of goods not exceeding 22 euros (EUR) was removed. As a result, all goods imported to the European Union (EU) are subject to VAT. [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#note-1) The Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS) was created as an optional program to facilitate and simplify the declaration and payment of VAT for goods sold from a distance by sellers from either the EU or from a non- EU country or territory. The IOSS allows suppliers and electronic interfaces selling imported goods to buyers in the EU to collect, declare and pay the VAT to the tax authorities, instead of making the buyer pay the VAT the moment the goods are imported into the EU as it was previously the case (for products over 22 EUR). [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#note-1) 1[https://ec.europa.eu/taxation\_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce/ioss\_en](https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce/ioss_en) [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) # What does this mean for me as a USPS® customer? **Senders using the IOSS program to pay for VAT should provide the IOSS VAT Identification Number ( [described in further detail below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#IOSS_VAT_Identification_Number)) for each mail item sent to the EU in the electronic data (Shipping Services File)** sent to the USPS®. It is highly recommended that electronic data be provisioned at least two (2) hours in advance of mail induction, to ensure export compliance processing. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) # What does the IOSS VAT Identification Number look like? The IOSS VAT Identification Number contains a total of twelve (12) alphanumeric characters in the following format of **IMxxxyyyyyyz** where: - **xxx** is the 3-digit ISO numeric code of the Member State of identification, - **yyyyyy** is the 6-digit number assigned by the Member State, and - **z** is a check digit It is important to note the following: - The Member State of identification (xxx) is identified using a three (3) digit ISO numeric code ( [provided below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#ISO_numeric_code)). - The three (3) digit ISO numeric code is followed by seven (7) other numerical digits: the six (6) digit number assigned by the Member State (yyyyyy) and the check digit (z). - Based on the above, the total length of the IOS VAT Identification Number is strictly limited to twelve (12) digits (characters): **IM + ten (10) numerical digits** | | | | | | | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **ISO 3166-1 Country Codes** [2](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#note_2) | Finland | 246 | Ireland | 372 | Malta | 470 | Slovakia | 703 | | Austria | 040 | Cyprus | 196 | France | 250 | Italy | 380 | Netherland | 528 | Slovenia | 705 | | Belgium | 056 | Czechia | 203 | Germany | 276 | Latvia | 428 | Poland | 616 | Spain | 724 | | Bulgaria | 100 | Denmark | 208 | Greece | 300 | Lithuania | 440 | Portugal | 620 | Sweden | 752 | | Croatia | 191 | Estonia | 233 | Hungary | 348 | Luxembourg | 442 | Romania | 642 | N. Ireland | 900 | 2Three (3) digit ISO numeric codes per ISO 3166-1 for EU Member States and Northern Ireland. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) # What do I need to do as a USPS® customer? The USPS® understands some customers might currently be providing the IOSS VAT Identification Number in the “Sender Customs Reference Number” field in the Shipping Services File (SSF). Moving forward, in order to improve both data quality and accuracy, we request customers using the “Sender Customs Reference Number” leverage the “Importers Reference” fields (“Importers Reference Type” & “Importers Reference”) in its place. Customers currently leveraging the “Sender Customs Reference Number” may continue to do so until they can make the required system changes. **Instructions**: Per the request above, senders should provide the IOSS VAT Identification Number in the “Importers Reference” fields. - The “Importers Reference Type” field (SSF v1.7, record position #1029 – 1029 \| SSF v2.0, record position #25) should be populated with ‘2,’ indicating that you will be providing a VAT number. - ➞ Note: This field allows for a max of one (1) alphanumeric character. Options to populate this field are: ‘1’ = Tax Code, ‘2’ = VAT no., ‘3’ = Importer Code, or ‘ ’ = Space - The “Importers Reference” field (SSF v1.7, record position #1030 – 1069 \| SSF v2.0, record position #26) should be populated with the IOSS VAT Identification Number. - ➞ Note: This field allows for a max of forty (40) alphanumeric characters [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) # What does USPS® do with this information? When customers provide USPS® with the requested information (IOSS VAT Identification Number) in the SSF (via version 1.7 or 2.0), we will then ensure the data is mapped and transmitted to the corresponding ITMATT field. The USPS® understands some customers might currently be providing the IOSS number in the “Sender Customs Reference Number” field instead of the “Importers Reference” field as specified above. Until all customers have fully migrated to solely using the “Importers Reference” field, the following rules will apply: - If information (IOSS VAT Identification Number) is provided in the “Importers Reference,” we will accept & transmit the data via the corresponding ITMATT field - If information is provided in the “Sender Customs Reference Number,” we will temporarily accept, map, & transmit the data via the corresponding ITMATT field, and - If information is provided in both fields (“Importers Reference” & “Sender Customs Reference Number”), the data provided in the “Importers Reference” will take precedence: we will accept both, yet solely transmit the information (data) provided in the “Importers Reference” field [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) | Additional Information | | --- | | For additional information on upcoming changes to EU VAT regulations for e-commerce, please see the following links: | | - [VAT for e-commerce FAQs](https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce_en)
- [Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS) FAQs](https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce/ioss_en) | - [IOSS FAQs for Online Sellers](https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce/online-sellers_en)
- [IOSS FAQs for Electronic Interfaces](https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/business/vat/vat-e-commerce/online-electronic-interfaces_en) | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Import One-Stop Shop (IOSS) URL Name Import-One-Stop-Shop-IOSS [International](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFwGAM/international)[Preparing International Shipments](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGeGAM/preparing-international-shipments) ## Related Articles - [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics) - [Click-N-Ship® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics) - [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) - [Money Orders - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) - [Ordering Free Shipping Supplies](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Ordering-Free-Shipping-Supplies) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## What is Periodical Class Mail?

## Magazines, newspapers or other publications that has the primary purpose of transmitting information considered to be Periodical Class Mail.      - Oct 18, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007654

Customer Information

**General Information**

_**Periodical Class Mail**_is a class of mail consisting of magazines, newspapers or other publications that has the primary purpose of transmitting information. **ONLY** newspaper and periodical publications meeting mailability standards as well as the general and applicable specific standards may be authorized to mail at the Periodical class.

The United States Postal Service® does NOT guarantee delivery of Periodicals within a specified time. Periodicals must be issued regularly on a stated frequency and publishers must have an established list of subscribers or requesters (along with meeting other criteria).

- The customer must also submit a listing of the publishers, corresponding titles, and publication numbers with the application for the qualifying Periodicals publications distributed by the applicant.
- _**PS Form 3500**_ must be completed and filed at the Post Office™ serving the known office of publication to apply for Periodicals Mailing Privileges.

**Restrictions**

**The following restrictions apply to Periodical Class mailings:**

- Periodicals must be issued regularly on a stated frequency
- The continuity of the periodical must show from issue to issue.
- The content of the periodical may consist of original or reprinted articles on one topic or many topics, listings, photographs, illustrations, graphs, a combination of advertising and nonadvertising matter, comic strips, legal notices, editorial material, cartoons, or other subject matter.
- Periodicals must be published at least 4 times per year.
- A Mail piece must have less than 75% advertising.
- Mailers must have an established list of subscribers or requesters

**Application Process**

_**PS Form 3500**_( [http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3500.pdf](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3500.pdf)) must be completed and filed at the Post Office™ to apply for Periodicals Mailing Privileges. Information on the application process can be found at " [How to Apply for Periodicals Privileges](https://pe.usps.com/businessmail101?ViewName=PeriodicalPrivileges "How to Apply for Periodicals Privileges | Postal Explorer")."

Examples of information the customer will need to provide on the form:

- Title of publication
- Name of publisher
- Frequency of issue
- Subscription price
- Organization type (fraternal, trade union, church or organization, institution of learning)

**Note:**Additional information is required if you are attempting to mail as a non-profit organization or if you are attempting to apply for a lower periodical price (agriculture or classroom price), etc.

The customer must pay a fee when applying to mail Periodicals. Mailers must pay Periodicals postage by advance deposit account at the original or additional mailing offices. Periodicals may NOT be paid by permit imprint, meter stamp, postage stamp, or precanceled stamps. Price Incentives for Periodicals are available if customers pre-sort mailings.

Additional information on [Periodicals](https://pe.usps.com/businessmail101?ViewName=Periodicals "Periodicals | Postal Explorer") is available.

## What is the Periodicals Ride-Along Program?

## United States Postal Service® offers a special low price for the inclusion of a single, high-impact advertisement, such as a product sample or multimedia CD, with major Periodical subscriptions such as magazines, newspapers and more.      - Oct 18, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007665

Customer Information

**General Information**

Leap directly into your customers' hands through the _Ride-Along Program_, one of the most creative, price-effective and high-impact marketing mediums available today. Only the United States Postal Service® offers a special low price for the inclusion of a single, high-impact advertisement, such as a product sample or multimedia CD, with major Periodical subscriptions such as magazines, newspapers and more.

Burst through the clutter and provide a unique brand experience.  Why compete for your customers' attention when you can seize it? Ride-Along advertisements provide a memorable message that can't be missed.

**What Can the Program Do for You?**

- Grab your customers' attention. Your Ride-Along piece will be one of the first things your customers see as they open their mailboxes.
- Make your message stand out. Send a product sample, a multimedia piece and much more.
- Enhance the value of your message. Your customers will associate the high esteem they have for their Periodical subscriptions with your Ride-Along piece.
- Get personal. Customize specific Ride-Along messages by industry or subscriber interests.
- Pinpoint your market. The Ride-Along program allows you to target right down to the individual subscriber level.
- Market regionally. Select specific geographic locations to reach specific customers with the appropriate message.
- Execute extensive testing. Targeting flexibility allows you to test various market demographics and psychographics so you can perfect your advertising message.
- Emphasize your message. Use a Ride-Along piece to reinforce your print advertising within the host Periodical.
- Stand out and get results with the Ride-Along program.
- The Ride-Along program makes it easy for you to achieve maximum impact for your advertising dollars.
- Get noticed first. Postal Service™ standards allow only one Ride-Along piece at the Ride-Along price per Periodical, so your advertisement takes the front seat.
- Add new dimension to your message. Here's your chance to turn flat print advertising into something more tangible. Include anything from multimedia CDs to catalogs to product samples.
- Send just about anything. The options are countless, as long as the Ride-Along piece weighs less than 3.3 oz., meets Postal Service USPS Marketing Mail™ (formerly Standard Mail®) criteria and does not cover the publication title or customer name and delivery information.
- Don't miss your chance to ride in the front seat straight to your target!
- Currently, many of the nation's leading periodicals offer the Ride-Along program as part of their standard price card for print advertisers. To find out how you can supplement your next advertisement with a Ride-Along piece, contact the advertising representative for the publication of your choice.

**Examples of Periodical Ride-Along Pieces**

- Attach a Ride-Along piece to the outside of a publication, insert it within the publication or enclose it within a polyvinyl bag that contains the Ride-Along piece and the publication.
- Samples are a great way to encourage customer interaction with your product. Be it high-impact multimedia software, like a CD-ROM or diskette, or samples of skin care products, cosmetics, food or catalogs, you are enhancing the customers likelihood to see, try and ultimately buy your product.

The Ride-Along program is restricted to mail delivered within the United States

**Additional Information and Assistance**

For additional information on the Periodicals Ride-Along Program, please refer to article [Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center Hours](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailing-Shipping-Solutions-Center-Hours) for Mailing Requirements .

## Shipping Products Permit

## Description of the universal “Shipping Products” permit for commercial shipping of outbound items and returns at one or more Post Office locations and its benefits. There is information for new users and owners of existing permits.      - Jan 21, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007649

Customer Information

The “Shipping Products” permit replaces both the “Return Services” permit and the need for a separate permit imprint for outbound Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail, USPS Ground Advantage™- Commercial, Parcel Select, Bound Printed Matter Parcels, Media Mail, and Library Mail. The impacted Return Services include Business Reply Mail (parcels only), USPS Returns (Scan Based Payment), and Parcel Return Service.

This one universal “Shipping Products” permit can be **used for both outbound and returns at one or more Post Office locations**.

Mailers will not have to maintain at least one outbound piece annually to have the fees waived for the Shipping Products permit. Also, the Postal Service eliminated the application fee for the new Shipping Products permit regardless of the number of pieces sent or received under that permit. Further information on the fees for outbound items and returns can be found at " [Fees Associated with Permit Imprint and Return Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Fees-Associated-with-Permit-Imprint-and-Return-Services)".

**Benefits**

- Eliminates the need to pay any application, annual, or account maintenance fees for Business Reply Mail parcels and for outbound and returns.
  - In addition, there will be no permit application fees for additional entry points.
- Many products can be mailed or returned under a Shipping Products permit:
  - Outbound shipments of Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail, USPS Ground Advantage™- Commercial, Parcel Select, Bound Printed Matter Parcels, Media Mail, and Library Mail.
  - Return shipments of Parcel Return Service and Business Reply Mail parcels.

**New Mailers**

New permit customers will be provided with a single Shipping Products permit number for use with the applicable outbound and return services and will be expected to include the “Shipping Products” Permit number marking on their labels. New Shipping Products permit customers using the permit for returns will be able to select any of the following return products: BRM Parcels, USPS Returns, or PRS.

**Existing Permits**

The annual maintenance or mailing fees will be waived for several existing permits. Effective January 22, 2017, the annual mailing fees for Parcel Select destination entry, Bound Printed Matter destination entry Parcels, presorted Media Mail, and Library Mail are eliminated. Additionally, the annual account maintenance fees for Return Services permits (USPS Returns and Parcel Return Service) are eliminated.

When the Shipping Products permit is implemented, permit holders using the applicable products and returns services can continue to use their existing Permit number for their related shipping and return labels. It is expected that these customers will migrate to a single “Shipping Products” Permit number within a two-year period.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-Permit#top)

## What is Signature Confirmation™?

## What you can receive from Signature Confirmation™ service and a link to the form for the service.      - Oct 17, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007319

Customer Information

**What will I receive with Signature Confirmation™ Service?**

You will be provided the following information:

- Signature and name of the recipient
- Date, time and location of the delivery
- Signature Confirmation™ requires that the recipient (or a responsible person at the residence) be present to sign for the item.

**Notes:**

- It does **not** provide USPS Tracking® of mail items.
- It does **not** expedite mailing, or provide a guaranteed delivery time.
- Must be purchased at the time of mailing.
- No record of a Signature Confirmation service item is maintained at the office of mailing.

Image of [PS Form 153](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps153.pdf), _Signature Confirmation Receipt_

**Items eligible for Signature Confirmation™ Service**

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **USPS Ground Advantage™** | **Priority Mail® service items** |
| **Parcel Select®** | **Package Services** |

**How Much Does Signature Confirmation™ Cost?**

Fees vary for Signature Confirmation Service, depending on how it was purchased (Retail vs. Electronic). For detailed fee information, go to the _[Price List - Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/cpim/ftp/manuals/dmm300/Notice123.pdf)_ or to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) and click on "Show Details" under Signature Confirmation.

Postage may be paid with ordinary postage stamps, meter stamps or permit imprints.

**Retail vs. Electronic price for Signature Confirmation Service**

- The **retail fee** can be purchased at your local Post Office™. The retail label, PS Form 153, is hot pink with a detachable receipt. To see an image of PS Form 153, [click here](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps153.pdf). Or, you can print online [Shipping Labels](http://www.usps.com/shipping/label.htm) for Priority Mail service and qualify for the lower-fee electronic option. High-volume shippers can also print their own labels or purchase preprinted labels from the United States Postal Service®.

- The **electronic fee** is for mailers who purchase postage online (for example, using Click-N-Ship®) or are capable of sending and receiving electronic files of their shipments.


Whichever fee you choose, you will have access to the same Signature Confirmation features and benefits. To receive a copy of the signature, track your mail item on [USPS.com](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input?_gl=1*d24xnf*_gcl_au*MTAzMTgxMjAxNC4xNzI2NTg2NTAy*_ga*ODg4MTgyODI0LjE3MjY1MTE3NzA.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcyODQ4OTAzNi41Ni4xLjE3Mjg0OTE3MjguMC4wLjA.) then select Proof of Delivery. Follow the provided directions to receive an email with the delivery details.

## Additional Resources

## USPS® knows the importance of connecting with your customers and making informed decisions to grow your business, so we have provided our small business customers, in-depth articles, educational resources, and research all in one place.      - Jan 11, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007441

Customer Information

Use the following resources as inspiration to take your business to the next level.

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **[USPS Delivers®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Additional-Resources#USPSdelivers)** | **[SBA.gov](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Additional-Resources#SBAhelp)** | **[Chamber of Commerce](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Additional-Resources#ChamberCommercehelp)** | **[Webinars](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Additional-Resources#Webinars)** |

## What is USPS Delivers®?

[USPS Delivers](https://www.uspsdelivers.com/) is a resource of shipping and marketing content full of expert business knowledge and insights. We hope it inspires you to take your business to the next level.

## How can the [SBA.gov](http://sba.gov/) help you?

The U.S. [Small Business Administration](https://www.sba.gov/) helps Americans start, build and grow businesses. The SBA was created in 1953 as an independent agency of the federal government to aid, counsel, assist and protect the interests of small business concerns, to preserve free competitive enterprise and to maintain and strengthen the overall economy of our nation.

## How can the Chamber of Commerce help you?

The [Chamber of Commerce](https://www.chamberofcommerce.com/) is one of the largest and most trusted online business directories. Chamber specializes in helping small businesses grow their business on the web while facilitating the connectivity between local businesses and more than 7,000 area Chambers of Commerce worldwide.

What are Webinars?

A collection of seminars conducted over the internet and tailored for small businesses.

- [Stories of Resilience: A Conversation with Mark Cuban](https://www.salesforce.com/form/sflive/stories-of-resilience/?d=7010M0000022CcvQAE): Hear Mark Cuban talk about how your small business can succeed when times get tough.

See [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) for additional shipping information and resources.

## What Are The Benefits of Using PC Postage®?

## Ability to print personalized postage by uploading images to the custom postage provider, and the provider creates the postage and sends it back to the customer by mail.      - Sep 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007266

Customer Information

**Note:**These benefits are common to PC Postage but should be verified with your PC Postage vendor.

**What are some benefits and features of PC Postage®?**

Available additional features include printing variable prices, integration with scales and related online applications, and postage expense reports. Other benefits of PC Postage include:

- Reduced number of trips to the Post Office™.
- Ability to print mail and package labels from one location.
- Online Price Incentives for Expedited and International labels.
- Personalized postage.
- SCAN Forms (PS Form 5630 [)](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6BY00000001wfYAA/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FKnowledge__kav%2Fka6BY00000000juYAA%2Fview#psform_5630_image)

**What is Personalized Postage?**

Personalized postage, or Custom Postage, is postage that has a customer supplied image on the label. Customers upload images to the custom postage provider, and the provider in return creates the postage and sends it back to the customer by mail.

**What are the price incentives of using PC Postage®?**

Commercial Base Prices are available for International Shipping Services when postage is paid through an authorized PC Postage vendor or customers using IBI postage metering systems and transmit customs forms electronically.

**Discounts (excluding international Flat Rate items) are as follows:**

- Priority Mail Express International®:  up to 8% below retail prices.
- Priority Mail International®:  up to 5% below retail prices.

**Discounts for domestic services through PC Postage are:**

- Priority Mail Express®:  Save 5% below retail prices.
- Priority Mail®: Save up to 11% below retail prices (dependent on weight and distance).

Discount pricing applies only to the postage portion of the mailpiece. They do not apply to any other charges or fees, such as Pickup on Demand service, additional merchandise insurance coverage, or shipments made under a customized agreement.

**What is a SCAN Form?**

A Shipment Confirmation Acceptance Notice (SCAN) Form (PS Form 5630 [)](https://usps.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka6BY00000001wfYAA/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FKnowledge__kav%2Fka6BY00000000juYAA%2Fview#psform_5630_image) allows customers to see that the shipment containing their package has been received by USPS and the package is pending acceptance. The SCAN form is designed to better meet customer needs regarding tracking and visibility.

The SCAN Form features a master barcode that represents all of the packages in a shipment; it is scanned when the shipment is received by the U.S. Postal Service®.  This single scan enters all of the associated packages into the Postal Service USPS Tracking® database as “Shipment Accepted” and allows both the sender and the recipient to see when the package entered the mailstream.
