## To use PC Postage an account with the vendor is required. By visiting the PC Postage vendor’s website, customers can select the service plan that best fits their business needs.      - Nov 20, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007259

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**What types of products and services can I use PC Postage® for?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#types_products_services) | [**Can I get Extra Services with my PC Postage® product?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#extra_services) | [**How do I begin using PC Postage®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#begin_using_PC_Postage) |
| [**What are my PC’s system requirements for using PC Postage®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#system_requirements) | [**What are my system requirements if my Mac® does not have OSX?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#MAC_system_requirements) | [**What security measures are in place for PC Postage®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#security_measures) |

PC Postage vendors typically charge a monthly fee for their service. Most vendors require the use of a credit/debit card for the service fee. Postage is also paid via a debit/credit card. The actual payment process is set up by the PC Postage vendor.

If using a commercial PC Postage® product, it is also suggested that customers set up an Automated Clearing House account as a direct debit account with their bank.

**What types of products and services can I use PC Postage® for?**

PC Postage products and services are currently authorized for use on all mail services, with **the exception of Periodicals and Bound Printed Matter.**

**Eligible services include:**

- Domestic First-Class Mail® items
- Priority Mail® service mailpieces
- Priority Mail Express® service items
- USPS Ground Advantage™
- USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail
- International Mail and Parcels
- Presorted First-Class Mail discount services
- Extra Services (such as USPS Tracking® and Certified Mail® services)

**Note**: Not all authorized products support all types of mail. Please check with the providers for products that meet your needs.

**Can I get Extra Services with my PC Postage® product?**

Yes, you can add the necessary additional postage for most Extra Services (such as USPS Tracking and Certified Mail services). However, you still may need to complete and take the necessary forms to the Post Office™ location to ensure you receive the service that you pay for. For example, Registered Mail® items and insurance require you to present your letter or parcel to a Post Office clerk.

In addition, you can also use USPS shipping and packaging supplies, Package Pickup (for specific mail classes), USPS Tracking, and Confirmation Services.

- **Combined Signature Confirmation and Restricted Delivery**
When purchased through PC Postage or Click-N-Ship, Signature Confirmation and Restricted Delivery services can be combined without any additional services. This is applicable to parcels of the following mail classes:

  - Priority Mail®
  - USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail
  - USPS Ground Advantage™
  - Package Services
    - Bound Printed Matter
    - Library Mail
    - Media Mail

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#top)

**How do I begin using PC Postage®?**

An account with the PC Postage vendor of your choice is required. You should visit the PC Postage vendor’s website in order to select the service plan that best fits your business needs. An Internet connection and printer will be required to start using the solution.

To select the right vendor, customers should consider factors such as the volume for both mailing and shipping, the need for hidden postage, and address management solutions. You can find more information on several vendors through the links below; Click-N-Ship and other approved providers can be found at [https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm):

- [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/) ( [http://www.stamps.com/](http://www.stamps.com/))
- [Endicia.com](http://endicia.com/) ( [http://www.endicia.com/](http://www.endicia.com/))
- Pitney Bowes ( [https://www.pitneybowes.com/us/shipping/software-and-systems/sendpro-online.html](https://www.pitneybowes.com/us/shipping/software-and-systems/sendpro-online.html))
- EasyPost ( [https://www.easypost.com/](https://www.easypost.com/))

**What are my PC’s system requirements for using PC Postage®?**

All you need is a PC running Windows or MAC OSX, an internet connection, and a printer. Optional equipment includes a scale and a label printer. Some PC Postage vendors offer free or reduced prices on optional equipment.

**What are my system requirements if my Mac® does not have OSX?**

System requirements vary to use PC Postage products on a Mac. Check with [authorized providers](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm) for the specific requirements you need.

**What security measures are in place for PC Postage®?**

PC Postage products and services are designed to enhance customer convenience while ensuring security.

To ensure that personal information is confidential, the provider uses industry standard encryption software, such as Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) or equivalent technology. The URL in the browser will update to "HTTPS" (instead of "HTTP") when this security feature is invoked. The browser may also display a lock symbol on its bottom task bar line to indicate a secure transmission.

Also, to ensure that personal information remains confidential, data is sent via a Virtual Private Network (VPN). Once received by the Postal Service™ system, personal information is stored in a password-protected database accessible only by authorized personnel

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Can-I-Use-PC-Postage#top)

See also [What Are The Benefits of Using PC Postage®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Are-The-Benefits-of-Using-PC-Postage)

## What Else Should I Know When Using PC Postage® for Shipping?

## PC Postage products are ideal for packages and overnight mail.      - Nov 6, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007262

Customer Information

**Can I Use PC Postage® with packages and overnight mail?**

Yes. PC Postage products and services are ideally suited for use with [packages](https://www.usps.com/ship/online-shipping.htm), [Priority Mail Express](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm) ®service, and [Priority Mail](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm) ®service. Scales are available with many PC Postage products to help you calculate the exact postage required.

**I printed a PC Postage® Service Label for a package; however, the USPS® states that I have insufficient funds on my item. Why is this?**

At the time of creating your PC Postage service Shipping Label, you are asked to estimate the weight of the item. If your estimate was lower than the actual weight and your item is short paid, the United States Postal Service is required to charge the additional fees.  This charge can be at the time of mailing or the delivery recipient may be required to pay the additional fees.

**Can my PC Postage® account ever be closed against my wishes?**

Accounts for printing PC Postage® service Shipping Labels are closed due to use of invalid labels. For example, the use of a label that you had indicated did not print properly and thus were not charged the appropriate fees. The USPS® monitors this type of fraudulent activity. If a customer feels their account should not be closed, they can reach or email USPS at: [https://www.usps.com/help/welcome.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/welcome.htm).

**An account may be terminated with intentional misuse of online postage.**

## Is PC Postage Eligible for a Refund?

## PC Postage that is unused is eligible for a refund if it is complete and legible. The PC Postage vendor will explain the refund process.      - Nov 6, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007276

Customer Information

**PC Postage** that is unused is eligible for a refund if it is complete and legible. Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days from the date shown on the indicia and should be submitted to the system provider for processing. The PC Postage vendor will explain the refund process. The provider may charge a fee for processing refund requests.

Please allow up to two billing cycles for your refund to be posted to your credit card statement. Processing requests can take up to 14 days. Posting to your account is dependent upon your financial institution. Check your Shipping History to see the status of your refunds.

## How Do I Get Started Using Direct Mail?

## Steps needed to start using Direct Mail.      - Apr 29, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006897

Customer Information

**Customer Options for Sending Direct Mail:**

1. Completing the mailing themselves - Customers should complete the mailing themselves when they are concerned with price and have the time to manage the process themselves.
2. Using a Mail Service Provider - Customers should use a [Mail Service Provider](https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm)(under "Find a Provider") when they are attempting to save money on an infrequent mailing or save time completing a mailing. Mail Service Providers can complete the entire process of creating and mailing your pieces for an additional fee.

**How to Complete the Direct Mailing Yourself**

1. Decide what Direct Mail offer you want to make
   - Coupons
   - Flyers
   - Notification of an upcoming sale
   - Gather information from customers
2. Create Your Mailing List
   - Create/ Update Your Own Mailing List
     - You can use your own customer list as the original mailing list
     - You should standardize a large mailing list’s addresses to save on costs of mail that does not reach your customer.
   - Purchase a Mailing List from a Vendor
     - Customer can search online for “Mailing List Providers” or check local phone directory
     - Customer can use NetPost direct mail options to obtain a mailing list and then create a mail piece to be sent to that mailing list
3. Create Your Mail Piece
   - Direct Mail Piece Types
     - Postcards – give your message immediate attention
     - Letters/ Flyers – allow you to tell your story
     - Booklets – Share information with your customers
     - Certified Mail
     - Reply Mail
   - Designing and Printing Your Direct Mail
     - Customers can design and print their mail pieces from Direct Mail Providers or at local design / print shops
     - Expert assistance is available by contacting a Mailpiece Design Analyst: [http://pe.usps.com/MailpieceDesign/Index?ViewName=MDASupportContact](http://pe.usps.com/MailpieceDesign/Index?ViewName=MDASupportContact "MDA Support Center-Contact Info")
     - For more tips on creating your Mailpiece, go to [https://www.usps.com/business/vendors.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/vendors.htm)
     - For printing options, [www.printing.org](http://www.printing.org/) allows you to search for local options
4. Select Postage and Send out Direct Mail
   - How to Decide Between First-Class Mail® and USPS Marketing Mail™ (formerly called Standard Mail®)
     - _USPS Marketing Mail_
       - Frequent mass mailing
       - Slower delivery
       - Less time sensitive
     - _First-Class Mail_
       - Smaller mailing
       - Speedy delivery
       - Mailing infrequently
   - How to Work with USPS®
     - _Mailing between 50-200 mail pieces/week_
       - _**PC Postage**_ \- Contact vendor to subscribe. You can print directly from your computer, hide mailing costs, and use addresses from Outlook. There are monthly fees and you must have a computer, printer, and internet access.
       - _**Meter**_ \- Contact vendor to subscribe, You can print directly from your computer, track costs, and create exact postage with the built in scale. You must lease meter through vendor and have a prepaid account.
     - _Mailing at commercial volumes_ (over 200 pieces for USPS Marketing Mail / over 500 pieces for First-Class Mail) and mails frequently. Note that USPS Marketing Mail has content restrictions.
       - _**Permit Imprint**_ \- Allows you to have a personal design. There is no meter maintenance and no cost displayed on the mail piece.  You pay at the time of mailing. However, there is a one time application fee, and you can only send presorted First-Class Mail, First-Class Mail International®, or USPS Marketing Mail.
       - _**Meter**_ \- There is no application fee. You can print directly from your computer, track costs, and create exact postage with the built in scale. You must lease meter through vendor and have a prepaid account.
       - _**Precancelled Stamps**_ \- There is no application fee and you can pay a portion of the postage through stamps and then the remainder when they mail.  You would need to provide a return address on the mail piece.
     - _High volume mailing but will only complete this mailing one time_
       - Recommend a [Mail Service Provider](https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm) (under "Find a Provider").

**Obtaining Help from Mail Service Provider**

You can obtain help from a Mail Service Provider by visiting [https://www.usps.com/business/vendors.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/vendors.htm)(under "Find a Provider"). Mail Service Providers offer a full range of services, including:

- Designing
- Writing
- Printing
- Sending the direct mailpiece

If you can want to purchase a mailing list, you should be advised to search online for “Mailing List Providers” or check your local phone directory.

## Merchant Returns Application

## Information on what the Merchant Returns Application is, its functionality, and how to troubleshoot issues with it.      - Sep 9, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006869

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[What is the Merchant Returns Application?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_What_is_the)** | **[Scan Based Payment (SBP)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Scan_Based_Payment)** | **[PO Locator](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Post_Office_(PO))** |
| **[Schedule Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Schedule_Package_Pickup_1)** | **[Track Your Package](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Track_Your_Package)** | **[Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) - Customers](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_FAQs_%E2%80%93_Customers)** |
| **[Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) - Merchants](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_FAQs_%E2%80%93_Merchants)** | **[Accessing the Merchant Returns Application](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Accessing_the_Merchant)** | **[Problems/Issues](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Problems/Issues)** |

# **What is the Merchant Returns Application?**

The **Merchant Returns Application** is an easy-to-use label generation tool for customers. Either on their own or with the help of Call Center agents, customers can quickly and easily:

1. **Generate a ready-to-use return label.**
2. **Print the label**, or have the label emailed to them for later printing.
3. **Schedule a Package Pickup:** Customers will be able to schedule a package pickup directly on the Merchant Returns web page, or by scanning a QR code at the bottom of the label with their iOS or Android mobile device.
4. **Locate the Nearest Post Office:** Customers will be directed to the PO Locator on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) if they wish to locate the nearest Post Office to their location, or they can scan a QR code at the bottom of the printed label with their iOS or Android mobile device to locate a Post Office.

Benefits of the **Merchant Returns Application** include:

- Flexible Label Printing – Customers can generate labels on-demand
- Free package tracking for customers and merchants
- Free billing reports for merchants
- Free package pickup at residence
- Unmatched consumer access and visibility

Customers can access and use the **Merchant Returns Application** in the following ways:

- A link on the merchant website directs consumers to the USPS Returns customer facing page to create labels.
- The merchant call center agents use the USPS Returns call center facing page to generate and send labels to customers.
- Merchants integrate with the USPS Merchant Returns API (Application Programming Interface) in order to generate labels.

A customer using the customer self-service page or the call center will be asked to provide their address, shipping information, and choice in label delivery (printable PDF pop-up window or email message). Customers using the API will have the same easy-to-use label generation experience with the merchant’s custom look, specifically tailored to fit the merchant’s needs and products.

**Print and Deliver Return Label Service (formerly, Call Tag Return Service)**

The Postal Service is introducing a return tag (label) option for permit holders to electronically request an applicable USPS® return label for their permit, to be generated and delivered by USPS to their customer (end-user). The customer using the return label can return their package without paying a fee or going to a retail postal unit for mailing. The permit holder can purchase insurance coverage for up to $200 at the time of the label request for any USPS Returns® product (Priority Mail® Return service, USPS Ground Advantage™ service).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **Scan Based Payment (SBP)**

**Scan Based Payment**, or **SBP**, is one of two return products available to merchants using the Merchant Returns Application. Intended for merchants with a high volume of annual returns (>10,000 returns a year), SBP gives merchants the ability to pay a single shipping rate for all of their customers’ returns.  The single rate is determined using the following sampling method:

1. Sampled pieces are weighed and rated to determine an accurate price per piece.
2. A statistically valid sample represents the merchant’s overall shipping profile.
3. Multiple sampling methods ensure random sampling from the total volume.
4. Sampling can occur at any point in the shipping process.

All SBP mail is tracked using this system, even though only a portion of the mail is weighed and rated as part of the sampling process. The final price per piece is an average rate based on the weight and distance traveled of the sampled pieces.

**Benefits to SBP**

- Merchants are charged automatically each day based on package activity. As a result, no manifesting is needed.
- Rates are adjusted monthly based on updated sampling results.
- The USPS visibility tool allows merchants to view all charges, sampling results, and other payment-related reports.
- Merchants may pre-print labels for insertion into outbound packages (Merchants are not charged for the label unless the package is returned).
- Elimination of delays in time-in-transit caused by manual rating.
- Merchants have the option of enabling _proximity routing_.

**Proximity Routing**

Proximity Routing allows SBP merchants with multiple locations to automatically address their customers’ return labels to the closest returns location.  This feature could help reduce shipping and processing costs for merchants, bringing down the price per piece.

**Mail Services Available**

SBP Merchants have access to the following four service types:

- Priority Mail® Return Service
- USPS Ground Advantage™ Return Service
- Parcel Return Service (PRS) - Full Network

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

**Post Office (PO) Locator**

Customers can drop off the labeled package at any USPS Post Office or designated drop box. Once the label has been generated, the customer will be provided a link to the PO Locator on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) where they can view a list of Post Offices and drop boxes close to the address entered during the label generation.

Additionally, customers can scan a QR code at the bottom of the printed return label using the camera on their iOS or Android mobile device. The device must have USPS Mobile®, Informed Delivery®, or another QR scanning application installed.

When customers scan the QR code on the label, they will be presented with two options:

- Schedule a Pickup: Customer can specify a date and time for a free package pickup for their return shipment
- Find a Post Office: Customer will be presented with the 10 closest Post Offices, with distance and hours of operation

To find a Post Office near you, visit the[PO Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm)website.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **Schedule Package Pickup**

With the USPS Package Pickup service, a customer can request from the Merchant Returns web page that a USPS mail carrier pick up the package for free. Customers who choose this option will be asked to provide the following:

- Their phone number
- The weight of the package
- The location where the mail carrier can expect to find the package (i.e. “Front Door”)

Once this information has been submitted, customers will receive the following:

- Date of Pickup
- Confirmation Number

Additionally, customers can scan a QR code at the bottom of the printed return label using the camera on their iOS or Android mobile device. The device must have USPS Mobile®, Informed Delivery®, or another QR scanning application installed.

When customers scan the QR code on the label, they will be presented with two options:

- Schedule a Pickup: Customer can specify a date and time for a free package pickup for their return shipment
- Find a Post Office: Customer will be presented with the 10 closest Post Offices, with distance and hours of operation

For more information, visit the [Schedule a Package Pickup](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm) website.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **Track Your Package**

Mail pieces are scanned for visibility and designated as delivered or en route. Customers will be provided a tracking number after the generation of a label that can be used to track the package’s progress. The tracking number provided will be a hyperlink. By clicking it, customers can access the [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action) page which can be used to track the package’s progress.

- If your scan indicates that your Parcel Return Service item has been “Picked Up and Processed by Agent” and has a delivery address of ZIP Code of 56935, or 56950, then your returned item had been consolidated at a USPS facility for pick up by a third party provider who completes shipping back to the original mailer’s distribution center.
- If checking for the status of your item, please check with the company that you are returning the item to and inquire with them about its status. That company can contact their vendor.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **FAQs – Customers**

- **Will I be charged for the labels printed?**

> No, you will not be charged for the labels. The fee for the labels will be handled by the merchants to whom the packages are being returned.

- **Can I choose the sizes of the labels printed?**

> Yes. Depending on the return service chosen by your merchant, you can choose to have your returns labels printed as 4X6, 4X4, or 3X6 inch labels so as to better fit the size of your package.

- **What is the RMA, Invoice Number, or Order Number?**

> The RMA, Invoice Number, or Order Number is package information that a merchant may ask you to provide when returning a package. This information is not for USPS’ benefit and is solely for use by the company receiving your return – it can be displayed on the label to make it easier for the company to keep track of their returns inventory.

- **Is it necessary that I provide an RMA, Invoice Number, or Order Number?**

> Whether or not an RMA, Invoice Number, or Order Number is required is entirely up to the company that the return package is going to. You should check to see if the company has provided you with an RMA number, as it may be a necessary part of your company’s return system.

- **Can I purchase insurance for the package I am returning?**

> If you would like to purchase insurance, you must contact the company you are making the return to. They will be able to inform you whether or not you can purchase insurance through the Merchant Returns Application and if you or the company will be billed for the insurance.

- **Can I generate the same label multiple times and use if for different returns?**

> No. It is important that every package has a different label. This is because every label contains a unique barcode and tracking number that is used for tracking purposes. There might be complications with processing your packages if you use the same label multiple times.

- **Can I print multiple labels at one time?**

> No, you cannot generate multiple labels at one time. You must access the link provided by your merchant and complete the form again in order to acquire another label. Keep in mind that you cannot print the same label multiple times and use it for different returns.

- **Is there someone I can call to help me generate my label?**

> Maybe. Some companies utilize the call center service offered by the Merchant Returns application. You can reach out to the company accepting the return to determine if they offer call center service.

- **Is the personal information I provided stored by USPS?**

> No, information considered as Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is not held by USPS. Only your ZIP Code and state are stored on the Merchant Returns application.

- **What should I do with the label once it has been generated?**

> Once it has been generated, you simply attach it to the largest side of your package. Place the label so that it does not fold over to another side. Do not place tape over any barcodes on the label or any part of the label where postage and fee information will be recorded. You can then choose to drop the package at your local Post Office or in a collection box, leave it with your letter carrier, or schedule a Package Pickup.

- **How can I locate the nearest Post Office?**

> Customers wanting to find the nearest Post Office or package drop-box can use the [Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/). You need only enter your City and State or your ZIP code.
>
> If you use the self-service option to create your label, you will be provided with a button that will direct you to a list of the Post Offices closest to the address you previously provided. For more information, please see the [PO Locator](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Post_Office_(PO)) section above.

- **Can I arrange to have my package picked up from my home or office through the Internet?**

> Yes. If you would like to have your package picked up, you can request it by using the USPS free Package Pickup service. You can schedule a pickup directly after generating your label or on the [Schedule a Pickup website](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm). For more information, please see the [Schedule Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Schedule_Package_Pickup) section above.

- **Can I arrange to have my package picked up from my home or office over the phone?**

> Maybe. If the merchant to which you are returning your package provides you with a number for their Call Center, then a call center agent will have the ability to schedule a package pickup for you over the phone. If your merchant has not provided you with a number for a Call Center, you can schedule a pickup directly after generating your label or on the [Schedule a Pickup website](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm). For more information, please see the [Schedule Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Schedule_Package_Pickup) section above.

- **How much does it cost to have my package picked up from my home or office?**

> Nothing. Package Pickup is a free service offered by USPS.

- **How can I track my package once it has been shipped?**

> Each label will contain a tracking number that can be used to track each package after it has shipped. You can track your package by accessing the [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action) page on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/). The only information you need to track the package is the tracking number which will be printed on the label. For more information, please see the [Package Tracking](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Track_Your_Package) section above.

- **How long will it take for my returned package to reach its destination?**

> Depending on the service type, it should take between 2-8 business days for the package to reach its destination. The estimated time of delivery for each service offered through the Merchant Returns Application is as follows:

- Bound Printed Matter: **2-8 Day Delivery**
- USPS Ground Advantage™ Return Service: **2-5 Day Delivery**
- Library Mail: **2-8 Day Delivery**
- Media Mail: **2-8 Day Delivery**
- Priority Mail® Return Service: **2-3 Day Delivery**
- Parcel Return Service (PRS) - Full Network: **2-8 Day Delivery**

> For more information on these and other USPS shipping services, please visit the [Shipping for Business website](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) at USPS.com.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **FAQs – Merchants**

- **Will I be charged for every label printed?**

> No, you will only be charged for a label once the customer has shipped a package with the label. The mere generating and printing of labels comes free of charge for both merchant and customer. Reference the [SBP](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Scan_Based_Payment) section above for information on the sampling process used to determine shipping rates.

- **Will my customers have to pay for every label they print?**

> No. You, the merchant, will be billed for your customers’ labels when those labels have shipped. The mere printing of labels does not cost anything. Please reference the [SBP](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_Scan_Based_Payment) section above for information on the sampling process used to determine rates.

- **Can I choose the sizes of the labels?**

> Yes. During the onboarding process, you can choose to have the returns labels printed in 4X6, 4X4, or 3X6 inch formats. You also have the option of printing a message or special instructions (250 character max) on the return labels.

- **How can I change the service types and/or label sizes I offer my customers?**

> Service types and label sizes can only be changed by a USPS administrator. USPS administrators can make any required updates to your label preferences from the merchant profile that was created when you where onboarded onto the Merchant Returns Application.

- **Can I purchase insurance for the packages being returned?**

> When you register your business with the Merchant Returns Application, you will be able to choose whether or not you want your customers to have the option of insuring their returns packages.

- **How do I know which mail service types to offer my customers?**

> If you are an **SBP** merchant, you have the ability to offer your customers the following four return services: Priority Mail® Return Service, USPS Ground Advantage™ Return Service, and Parcel Return Service (PRS) - Full Network. A description of each service has been provided below to help you decide which services will suit your business needs:

- _Priority Mail® Return Service_ is a great choice for shipping packages up to 70 pounds, offering the fastest delivery time of any return. **2-3 Day Delivery**
- _USPS Ground Advantage Service_ is a fast and affordable service for envelopes and packages. **2-5 Day Delivery**
- _Parcel Return Service - Full Network_ is a dedicated return service for businesses that generate 500,000 or more returns a year. PRS - Full Network is a workshare program to pass on savings to high volume shippers. **2-8 Day Delivery**

For more information on these and other USPS shipping services, please visit the [Shipping for Business website](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) at USPS.com.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **Accessing the Merchant Returns Application**

For more information on equipping your business’ website with the Merchant Returns API, please review the [Merchant Returns API User’s Guide](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/2722).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

# **Problems/Issues**

For technical assistance, you can call the Internet Customer Care Center (ICCC) at 1-800-344-7779. Once you have dialed the number, select Option 3 for technical support. Support hours are:

- Monday thru Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. ET
- Saturday: 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET
- Sundays: **Closed**
- Holidays: **Closed**\[ [Click here for Postal Holidays](http://about.usps.com/news/events-calendar/postal-holidays.htm)\]

(ICCC can also be reached via email us at [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/))

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Merchant-Returns-Application#_top)

## How Do I Initiate Contact With USPS to Start a Business Relationship?

## Information about Contacting USPS to start a Business      - Feb 11, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006887

Customer Information

- **How can I make the Postal Service™ aware of the products or services I can provide?**
One way is to register your business with the Postal Service using the new on-line supplier portal registration system on the internet [How to Become a USPS Supplier](http://about.usps.com/suppliers/becoming.htm) ( [http://about.usps.com/suppliers/becoming.htm](http://about.usps.com/suppliers/becoming.htm)). The information you enter is secure and only made available to our buyers.


- **Who do I contact if I want to offer my business services (contract work, offer to sell goods and services, etc.) to the U.S. Postal Service®?**
If you would like to offer your business services as a contractor to the U.S. Postal Service, please see USPS [Publication 131](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub131/welcome.htm) ( [http://about.usps.com/publications/pub131/welcome.htm](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub131/welcome.htm)), which discusses the Postal Service's Unsolicited Proposal Program.


- **If I have an opportunity, idea, or innovation to establish an alliance or partnership with the Postal Service, what should I do?**
Please visit the Postal Service's Innovations website at [https://about.usps.com/transforming-business/innovations.htm](https://about.usps.com/transforming-business/innovations.htm) and submit your proposal there. Please ensure that your email address is correct, and your email program allows you to receive emails from [innovations@usps.gov](mailto:innovations@usps.gov) (if you have an email filtering program in place).


- **How do I start a Business Relationship with the United States Postal Service (including Vendors / Suppliers)?**
The United States Postal Service works with various companies who are able to meet the needs of the Postal Service and postal customers.


  - If you are a vendor wishing to investigate becoming a supplier for the United States Postal Service, please visit the:
    - ( [http://about.usps.com/suppliers/becoming.htm](http://about.usps.com/suppliers/becoming.htm)).
  - For information about other cooperative business relationships (including licensing United States Postal Service Art and Other Intellectual Property), visit:
    - ( [http://about.usps.com/licensing/commercial-program.htm](http://about.usps.com/licensing/commercial-program.htm))

The _**Mover's Guide**_ and the _**Welcome Kit**_ are sent to postal customers who have moved. The United States Postal Service formed a strategic alliance with MYMOVE (a privately held marketing solutions firm) to provide these products free to the Postal Service or postal customers. Advertising is used to cover all government expenses and generate revenue. Please note that Address Management is the contact for these products.

If your business is interested in working with MYMOVE in regards to the Mover's Guide (including the online edition) or the Welcome Kit, you can:

  - Visit the MYMOVE website at: [https://mymove.com/partner-with-us/](https://mymove.com/partner-with-us/).

## How do I know my mailpiece with Signature Confirmation® was delivered?

## Signature Confirmation provides the mailer with information about the date and time an article was delivered or an attempt was made for delivery.      - Nov 5, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006255

Customer Information

**Signature Confirmation Service Delivery Status**

- Delivery status information will remain available for one (1) year after the date of delivery. The information can be accessed through the following (you will need the Signature Confirmation service tracking number):
  - At [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") available on [USPS.coms®](http://www.usps.com/ "United States Postal Service")
  - By Phone at **1-800-222-1811 –** Hours of Operation are:
    - Monday thru Friday:  8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. ET
    - Saturday:  8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET
    - Sundays:  **Closed**
    - Holidays:  **Closed**\[ [Click here for Postal Holidays](http://about.usps.com/news/events-calendar/postal-holidays.htm "When are Postal Holidays?")\]

- You can request that a copy of the signature be emailed (at no additional expense):  See "[What is Proof of Delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Proof-of-Delivery)".
  -  Email requests are processed 24 hours a day, 365 days a year.

**Note**: The recipient's signature is not viewable online

**Note**: If you are requesting multiple records, you must list and request each one individually by confirmation number

## What are the Limits for Insuring Cash and Checks?

## What you need to know before attempting to insure mailpieces containing cash and/or checks.      - Apr 29, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006228

Customer Information

****Money (currency / cash) that is sent through the mail can be insured with the following limits:****

- For Registered Mail®, cash can be insured up to $50,000. For amounts over $50,000, the full value must be declared but the maximum insured value is $50,000.
- **For all other mail classes and services not Registered Mail**, the maximum indemnity for negotiable items, currency, or bullion is $15.00. Negotiable items are defined as instruments that can be converted to cash without resort to forgery.

|     |
| --- |
| **Note:** Commercial cash transactions of over $500 being sent through the mail must be sent as Registered Mail. This requirement only applies to commercial customers. There is no impact to residential customers. See [Registered Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics) for more information. |

**When a claim is filed, money is replaced at the insured value as indicated above only:**

1. After [proof of value](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) is shown (if the actual value was less than the insured amount, then **only** the actual value is paid).
   - **Important**: If you are sending a significant amount of cash, you need to verify with your local Post Office what is considered valid proof of value **before** mailing the item. You should also verify that it is packaged securely enough to protect a high value item from being harmed during regular mail processing.
2. After the claim process is completed.

**Please Note**: Checks are **not** considered cash and cannot be insured other than for document reconstruction expense.

## What does insurance cost?

## Information on the coverage, pricing determination, and limits of insurance for mailpieces.      - Jul 9, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006246

Customer Information

**General Information**

- Insurance pricing is based on the dollar amount of coverage being purchased
- There is no difference in the price of insurance purchased online or at a Post Office™
- Insurance purchased online is limited to $5,000 for certain mail classes. You can purchase Insurance at any Post Office facility in amounts up to $5,000.

Priority Mail Express® Insurance

- Priority Mail Express Insurance (for merchandise) is provided automatically up to $100. Documents are insured against loss, damage, or rifling at no additional cost to mailer.
- Coverage is limited to the actual value of the shipment.
- Additional coverage may be purchased at any Post Office in increments up to $5,000.

- Priority Mail® Insurance
  - Priority Mail® Insurance (for merchandise) is provided automatically up to $100.
  - Additional coverage may be purchased at any Post Office in increments up to $5,000.

For detailed information on insurance fees, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm).

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000002ASYAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-Format-and-Sequence-of-Information-for-the-Recipient-s-Address) ## What is the Format and Sequence of Information for the Recipient's Address? ## Description of the format and sequence of a recipient's address. - Dec 14, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000005011 Customer Information **What is the Format and Sequence of Information for the Recipient’s Address?** We recommend that you write addresses in all uppercase letters. If possible, addresses should have no more than five lines. Addresses must be written in ink or typewritten. Pencil is not acceptable. The full address should be typed or legibly written in English - using Roman letters and Arabic numeral - and should be placed lengthwise on one side of the item. An address in a foreign language is permitted if the names of the city, province, and country are also indicated in English. The last line of the address block area must include only the complete country name (no abbreviations) written in uppercase letters. Foreign postal codes, if used, should be placed on the line above the destination country. Some countries prefer that the postal code follow the name, while others prefer that it precede the city or town name. - **LINE 1:** NAME OF ADDRESSEE - **LINE 2:**STREET ADDRESS OR POST OFFICE BOX NUMBER - **LINE 3:**CITY OR TOWN NAME, OTHER PRINCIPAL SUBDIVISION (i.e., PROVINCE, STATE, COUNTY, ETC.) AND POSTAL CODE (IF KNOWN) - (**Note:**  in some countries, the postal code may precede the city or town name) - **LINE 4:**COUNTRY NAME (UPPERCASE LETTERS IN ENGLISH) | | | --- | | **For additional information,please check out the following articles:**
- [Where Can I Find New Construction and Street Address Information?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-Can-I-Find-New-Construction-and-Street-Address-Information)
- [Addressing Mailpieces](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Addressing-Mailpieces)

## What is Insurance?

## General information on insuring mailpieces.      - Sep 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004863

Customer Information

_**Insurance provides**_coverage of up to $5,000 for merchandise that is lost, damaged, or with missing contents in the custody of the United States Postal Service®. (Registered Mail® with insurance has a liability limit of $50,000.) Customers can purchase insurance at a local Post Office™ or online. Online labels also include USPS Tracking® service, so customers can check their delivery status online.

**Insurance does NOT provide:**

- Expedited delivery
- Guaranteed delivery time

**Insurance may include scan information if it was purchased for a mail class that includes USPS Tracking®.**

**Insurance may only be purchased at one source for each insured mailpiece. For example:**

- Insurance purchased online cannot be supplemented with insurance purchased at a Post Office® or self-service kiosk.
- Insurance purchased at a self-service kiosk cannot be supplemented with insurance purchased online or at a retail Post Office.

**Note**: A certain amount of insurance coverage may be included in some mail classes purchased in certain conditions. This includes, but is not limited to:

- Priority Mail Express®
- Priority Mail®
- USPS Ground Advantage®
- Priority Mail Express International®

**Insurance may be combined with:**

- Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery (not at retail)
- USPS Tracking®
- Return Receipt (if insured for more than $500, Form 3811 only)
- Signature Confirmation™ and Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery (if insured for less than or equal to $500)
- Hold for Pickup
- Restricted Delivery
  - _**Insurance Restricted Delivery**_provides all the services that Insurance provides. In addition, customers opting for Insurance Restricted Delivery service can direct delivery of mail insured for more than $500 **only** to the addressee (or addressee’s authorized agent).

**Insurance Forms:**

- If Insurance is less than $500, use [**PS Form 3813**](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3813.pdf) (a signature is not required).

- If Insurance is greater than $500, use [**PS Form 3813-P**](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3813p.pdf) (a signature is required at delivery).

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance#Back_to_Top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000001hkYAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-some-Examples-of-How-to-Format-Different-Types-of-Addresses) ## What are some Examples of How to Format Different Types of Addresses? ## Examples of different addressing formats. - Oct 27, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000004866 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | **Address Types** | **Examples** | | **Alpha Prefix** | Some addresses in New Mexico and Wisconsin have a letter prefix as a directional and may also include another letter within the street number of the address. This numbering system is similar to grid numbering but spaces are omitted between the letters and the numbers.
**W310S478 MAPLE AVE**
**WAUKESHA, WI 53188** | | **Grid Numbering** | Many addresses in the state of Utah (as well as a limited number in some other states) are numbered using a grid. This grid provides both a pre- and post-directional abbreviation.
**204 E 5600 S**
**MURRAY, UT 84107** | | **Hyphenated Addresses** | Some Hawaii and New York addresses contain hyphenated street numbers. The hyphen should be included.
**99-040 KAUHALE ST**
**AIEA, HI 96701** | | **Individual** | **MR JAMES F JONES**
**4417 BROOKS ST NE**
**WASHINGTON DC 20019-4649** | | **Numbered Streets** | **ROBIN MONET**
**12 1ST ST NW**
**HAMPTON IA 50441-1902**
**Note:** Using numerals as above is the official postal formatting for this address, instead of "Twelve First" ST NW | | **Rural Route** | **HE BROWN**
**RR 3 BOX 9**
**CANTON OH 44730-9521** | | **Highway Contract** | **BG LIGHT CO**
**HC 72 BOX 293 A**
**DULUTH MN 55811-9702** | | **\*Post Office Box** | **MISS JANICE SMITH**
**PO BOX 34**
**DULUTH MN 55803-0034**
\*A Post Office Box rented from a commercial mail receiving agency (CMRA) (such as the UPS Store) would be addressed with the street number of the store with a box number next to it. You would not include the name of the CMRA. For example:
**MISS JANICE SMITH**
**5100 WYOMING ST BOX 34**
**Duluth, MN 55803-0034** | | **Attention Line** | MR S ONEILL
SEAN ONEILL INC
4321 MAPLE ST
OAKTON MD 12345-6789
**_ATTN_ MR S ONEILL**
**SEAN ONEILL INC**
**4321 MAPLE ST**
**OAKTON MD 12345-6789** | | **Military** | \*Mail addressed to an APO / FPO / DPO address should not include the country name.
**SSGT KEVIN BEASLEY**
**UNIT 2050 BOX 4190**
**APO AP 96522-1215**
Refer to: [How Do I Address Military Mail?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Do-I-Address-Military-Mail) for additional information. | | **International** | **MS HELEN SAUNDERS**
**1010 CLEAR ST**
**OTTAWA ON K1A 0B1**
**CANADA** | | **Punctuation** | With the exception of the hyphen in the ZIP+4 Code, punctuation may be omitted in the delivery address block. | | | | --- | --- | | Acceptable | Preferred | | MR. WALTER W **.** WITHERSPOON JR **.**
MDM ENTERPRISES **,** INC **.**
1401 S **.** MAIN ST **.**
PLUMMER’S LANDING **,** KY 41081 **-** 1411 | **MR WALTER W WITHERSPOON JR**
**MDM ENTERPRISES INC**
**1401 S MAIN ST**
**PLUMMER’S LANDING KY 41081-1411** | | | **Spelling of Cities** | Spell city names in their entirety. When abbreviations must be used due to labeling constraints, use only the [approved abbreviations](https://pe.usps.gov/text/pub28/28apc_002.htm). | | | | --- | --- | | Acceptable | Preferred | | W STOCKBRIDGE

NEWBERRY SPGS | WEST STOCKBRIDGE

NEWBERRY SPRINGS | **Note**: Normally, the abbreviations follow the logic of using existing abbreviations, such as for suffix or directional words | | **Urbanization Name (Puerto Rico)** | The term "urbanization" is commonly used in Puerto Rican urban areas; urbanization denotes an area, sector or development and precedes the name of the area. Customers may provide you with an urbanization name, along with a street address. Simply enter the street name in the regular area as usual
The urbanization name goes in the "Customer Urbanization" field of the Application pane.
**Note**: This information is not necessary for checking an address in the "Change of Address" database.
**URB FLAMINGO HILLS**
**123 CALLE MAIN**
**ANYTOWN PR 00957-1234**
In this case, the urbanization (URB) would be: FLAMINGO HILLS
**See** [Puerto Rico Addresses Format](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub28/28c2_041.htm) for details | | **Pacific Northwest addresses** | Pacific Northwest addresses often include a post-directional abbreviation such as SW, NW, etc.
**123 Main Street SW**
**98106-1234** | | **CMRA (Private Mail Boxes)** | **See** [DMM Section 508.1.8.2](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/508.htm#1_8_2) for details | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What are some Examples of How to Format Different Types of Addresses? ## What are the USPS® abbreviations for U.S. states and territories?

## Color coded chart of all U.S. states and territories.      - Nov 14, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004862

Customer Information

**USPS® abbreviations for U.S. states and territories**

|     |     |     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **State** | **Ab.** | **State** | **Ab.** | **State** | **Ab.** | **State** | **Ab.** |
| **Alabama** | **AL** | **Idaho** | **ID** | **Montana** | **MT** | ****Puerto Rico**** | ****PR**** |
| **Alaska** | **AK** | **Illinois** | **IL** | **Nebraska** | **NE** | **Rhode Island** | **RI** |
| **American Samoa** | **AS** | **Indiana** | **IN** | **Nevada** | **NV** | **South Carolina** | **SC** |
| **Arizona** | **AZ** | **Iowa** | **IA** | **New Hampshire** | **NH** | **South Dakota** | **SD** |
| **Arkansas** | **AR** | **Kansas** | **KS** | **New Jersey** | **NJ** | **Tennessee** | **TN** |
| **California** | **CA** | **Kentucky** | **KY** | **New Mexico** | **NM** | **Texas** | **TX** |
| **Colorado** | **CO** | **Louisiana** | **LA** | **New York** | **NY** | **Utah** | **UT** |
| **Connecticut** | **CT** | **Maine** | **ME** | **North Carolina** | **NC** | **Vermont** | **VT** |
| **Delaware** | **DE** | ****Marshall Islands**** | ****MH**** | **North Dakota** | **ND** | **Virginia** | **VA** |
| ****District of Columbia**** | ****DC**** | **Maryland** | **MD** | ****Northern MarianaIs.**** | ****MP**** | ****Virgin Islands**** | ****VI**** |
| ****Federated States of Micronesia**** | ****FM**** | **Massachusetts** | **MA** | **Ohio** | **OH** | **Washington** | **WA** |
| **Florida** | **FL** | **Michigan** | **MI** | **Oklahoma** | **OK** | **West Virginia** | **WV** |
| **Georgia** | **GA** | **Minnesota** | **MN** | **Oregon** | **OR** | **Wisconsin** | **WI** |
| ****Guam**** | ****GU**** | **Mississippi** | **MS** | ****Palau**** | ****PW**** | **Wyoming** | **WY** |
| **Hawaii** | **HI** | **Missouri** | **MO** | **Pennsylvania** | **PA** |  |  |

**Color Key**:

- **Black**\- One of the 48 contiguous states. Sometimes referred to as the "lower 48" or the "mainland."
- **Blue -** Non-contiguous states. Alaska and Hawaii are US states, but are not physically connected to other states.
- **Green**\- Territory or possession of the United States.
- **Red**\- U.S. Capital.

## How do I Address a Postcard?

## Information on how a postcard is addressed.      - Apr 30, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004854

Customer Information

The address side of a postcard is the side bearing the destination address and postage. The address side may contain a message area.

Miscellaneous graphics such as symbols, logos, or characters that appear on the address side of cards not containing a message are generally acceptable provided the items are not intended to convey a message.

To receive the postcard price the message area on the address side must be divided vertically or horizontally and meet the following additional standards:

- **Vertically divided cards**
  - The address side of the card must be divided into a right portion and a left portion, with or without a vertical rule. The _left portion is the message area_.
  - The destination address, postage, and any United States Postal Service marking or endorsement must appear in the right portion. The right portion must be at least 2-1/8 inches wide (measured from the right edge of the card, top to bottom inclusive).
- **Horizontally divided cards**
  - The address side of the card must be divided into an upper portion and a lower portion, with or without a horizontal rule. The _portion of the address side that does not contain the destination address is the message area_.
  - The address, postage, and any Postal Service marking or endorsement must appear within the portion containing the destination address. As an alternative, when the destination address appears within the lower portion, it is permissible for the postage, return address, and any USPS marking or endorsement to appear in the upper portion. The portion bearing the destination address must be at least 1-1/2 inches high.

**Example of a vertically-divided card:**

![Postcard](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6BY00000002Tj&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UJ6H)

**Example of a horizontally-divided card:**

![](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6BY00000002Tj&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000LsP8c)

## How Can I Buy Insurance Online?

## Purchasing insurance on line through Click-N-Ship includes USPS Tracking service and up to $5,000 indemnity coverage against lost, rifled , or damaged articles.      - Jul 8, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004857

Customer Information

**Buying Insurance Online**

Indemnity coverage is available online, including through Click-N-Ship®, for up to $5,000 for lost, rifled, or damaged articles.

- No extra forms are needed.
- Because online labels always include USPS Tracking® service, you can also check your delivery status online!

**Note:** You cannot combine United States Postal Service® insurance purchased online with insurance purchased from a different source.

**Eligible Items**

Depending on the online postage solution (PC Postage, Click-N-Ship®, eBay®, etc.), insurance is available with the following domestic mail classes: Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™, USPS Ground Advantage-Commercial, Media Mail® and First-Class Mail® parcels.

The availability of international insurance purchased online is determined by the [different destination countries](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/ab_001.htm). Insurance is not available with Priority Mail International Flat Rate Envelopes or Priority Mail International® Small Flat Rate Boxes.

**Insurance Fees – Online vs. Post Office™**

Insurance fees online are the same as at Post Office facilities and are based on the amount of coverage needed. You can purchase insurance at any Post Office facility or online in amounts up to $5,000 depending on the mail class

## Should I Use Tape or Write On Envelopes and Boxes?

## Details concerning writing and tape on envelopes and boxes.      - Oct 18, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004852

Customer Information

**See [Domestic Mail Manual (DMM) Section 125](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/125.htm?q=125&h=125&t=H&s=R&p=1&c=DMM) for guidelines on mail preparation of Priority Mail® flat rate packaging.**

**Can tape be used to close, reinforce or enlarge Flat Rate envelopes / boxes?**

When mailing using Flat Rate Priority Mail® service or Priority Mail Express® service (both domestic and international), the contents of the flat rate envelope must be confined within the envelope with the adhesive provided on the flap as the primary means of closure. Contents must also be confined within the Priority Mail Flat Rate box secured with tape.

- The envelope or box flaps must be able to close within the normal folds.
- Tape may be applied to reinforce the flap of the envelope and to secure the box.
- The design /shape of the envelope or the box may not be enlarged by any means.

**Note:** The design of the container may not be enlarged by opening the sides, and the container may not be reconstructed in any way.

**Mail was returned to me because there was tape on the stamp. Why?**

When stamps are covered, the canceling machines cannot imprint directly on the stamp; therefore, they are not acceptable for mailing. The machines must also be able to read the ultra-violet ink on stamps to detect them on the envelope. If your stamp had only been partially covered, perhaps at the top and bottom, the stamp may have been acceptable for processing.

**Tape on shipping label barcodes – is it okay?**

No. Barcodes with tape over them cannot be scanned by our equipment.

**Can I Just Fold a Piece of Paper into an Envelope?**

Paper can be folded and sent through the mail if it meets certain requirements

- Must be prepared from paper with a minimum basis weight of 16 pounds (If formed of a single folded sheet, the self-mailer must be prepared from paper with a minimum basis weight of 28 pounds)
- Must be prepared with the folded edge parallel to the longest dimension and the delivery information of the mailpiece
- Can be sealed with Tab, Wafer Seal, Cellophane Tape (in some instances), or Permanent Glue
- Customers can also reference the [Quick Service Guide 201b](http://pe.usps.com/text/qsg300/q201b.htm), Commercial Letters and Postcards Using Tabs, Wafer Seals, and Glue Strips.

**Note**: The postcard price is not available to folded pieces unless they are a double postcard with postage on each half.

**Can I Write on the Outside of an Envelope?**

- In general it is ok to write things on the outside of an envelope such as "Past Due," "Open Immediately," "Dated Materials," etc.
- However it is illegal for these types of mailings to copycat government mailings.  Any mailing that is not from a government agency, but copycats official government mailings, without clearly showing their non-affiliation with government agencies may be in violation of the Deceptive Mailings Prevention Act of 1990. Many of these mailings will go further to make the mailing look even more authentic. Messages such as "Important Notice," Official Business," or "Open Immediately" are often hand-stamped or printed on the envelopes.
- If you are uncertain about a mailing, carefully read the material inside the envelope. If the mailer is not being totally deceptive, it should become clear whether the mailing is a deceptive government look-alike mailing from a private organization not connected with any government agency or program.
- If you have received an unsolicited government look-alike mailing from an entity with no governmental connection, approval, or endorsement, and the required disclaimer is not present, please send that mailing with a brief transmittal letter to a Postal Inspector, care of the your local postmaster.

## What are the size and material standards for a window envelope?

## Size and window location and material requirements for window envelopes.      - Nov 9, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004849

Customer Information

**Size of a Window Envelope**

**Window envelopes are available with these window sizes:**

- The standard window is 1-1/8 inches high and 4-1/2 inches wide, and is placed 5/8 inch from the bottom edge of the envelope.
- In size 6-3/4 envelopes, the window is 7/8 inch from the left edge.
- In size 10 envelopes, the window is 1 - 1/16 inch from the left edge.
- On double window envelopes, the second window (designed for return information) is 1/2 inch from the top and left edges, and measures 3/4 inch high and 2-1/2 inches wide.

**Other window sizes and locations can be produced, if the window is:**

- Not more than 1-1/2 inches high.
- Not more than 5 inches wide.
- At least 1/2 inch from the bottom edge of the envelope.
- At least 3/8 inch from any envelope edge.

****Window Cover Material****

- Window cover material:
  - Must be made of a non-tinted clear or transparent material (e.g., cellophane or polystyrene).
  - Must permit information, as viewed through the window material, to meet the print-contrast ratio standards to ensure efficient processing.
- Glassine may be used for window cover material.
- All edges of the window cover material must be glued securely to the envelope.

In general, windowed envelopes must allow compliance with general United States Postal Service® guidelines.

If you have any questions about valid envelope window sizes, refer to the Mailing Requirements area of your [local Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action)  facility.

****Inserts or Mailpieces Going Into Window Envelopes****

Mailers using window envelopes (both letter-size and flat-size) should design inserts and address element placement to ensure successful processing of window envelopes on automated equipment.  One critical element that must be considered in the design stage is adequate clear space (blank space) between the address block and all four edges of the window.  The mailpiece should be designed so that when it is inserted into the window envelope the address block will appear in the window no matter how it is shifted or moved within the envelope.

****What are the Clear Space Requirements for a Windows Envelopes’ Address Block?****

**The requirements for clear space around the address block are as follows:**

- For non-automation mail \- except letter-size mail bearing a barcode in the address block and submitted at Enhanced Carrier Route high density and saturation prices - a clear space of at least 1/8 inch is required between the address block elements and the top, bottom, left and right edges of the window.

- For automation mail and letter size mail submitted at Enhanced Carrier Route high density and saturation prices bearing a barcode in the address block:
  - A clear space of 1/8 inch is required between the address block (including barcode) and the left and right edges of the window.
  - A clear space of 1/25 inch is required between the top and bottom of the barcode and any address information line and (depending on placement within the address block) the top or bottom edge of the window.

**Note**: Any letter-size or flat-size submitted at automation prices or for letter-size mail claimed at Enhanced Carrier Route high density and saturation prices must also meet the barcoding standards for letters and flats in [DMM™ 202.5.1](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/202.htm) [.](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/202.htm)

- For letter-size automation price mail and letter-size mail submitted at Enhanced Carrier Route high density and saturation prices bearing a barcode in the barcode clear zone a clear space of 1/8 inch is recommended between the address block elements and the top, bottom, left and right edges of the window.

**Coins in Window Envelopes**

Coins or tokens visible through a window envelope must be affixed to one of the components in the mailpiece, covered in a second wrapping, and streamlined within the envelope. The coin or token cannot interfere with the address or the barcode.  For more information refer to the Customer Support Ruling (CSR) [PS-328.](https://pe.usps.com/text/CSR/PS-328.htm)

## What are the Best Tips for Addressing Mail?

## Tips for addressing mail.      - Nov 14, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004842

Customer Information

There are things that everyone can do to help the United States Postal Service® process and transport items more efficiently. Whether it is mail for holidays or special occasions, or regular letters and packages, these helpful tips may come in handy:

- Type or neatly write the complete address of the recipient.
- Use a complete return address.
- Provide complete address information. Always use the apartment or suite number and correct directions such as _N, W, SW_ and _AVE, BLVD_ and _ST_.
- Write the city, state, and ZIP Code information on the same line. Check to make sure you are always using the correct ZIP Code numbers.
- Do not write phone numbers or fax numbers as part of the delivery address.

By following basic guidelines, you can help ensure your card or letter will reach its destination quickly and efficiently. - Dec 15, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008202 Customer Information By following these basic guidelines, you can help ensure that your card or letter will reach its destination quickly and efficiently. ![Image of a properly addressed mail piece](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6BY00000002Ac&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UJGU) 01. Always use a return address - if the letter is undeliverable, it can be returned. 02. Endorsements can be placed in one of four locations on the envelope. In this example the endorsement is placed below the return address). 03. Name and/or "Attention:" line. Print or type delivery address clearly in center of envelope (Use 10, 12, 14 pts for best readability). 04. First and foremost, the address should be clear and legible: - Use all CAPS, spaces between words, no punctuation, and left justify the margin. - The color contrast between the address and the mailpiece background must be kept at a reasonable degree. A brilliant colored background or reverse printing is not permitted for automation discounts. 05. Always use applicable directionals in the address (e.g., "N" for north and "S" for south). 06. Always use applicable abbreviated locators in address (e.g., APT, ST, AVE). This helps direct the mail efficiently. 07. Always include applicable RM (Room), FL (Floor), STE (Suite), or APT (Apartment) # on same line as (and after) street address (preferred), or on the line above the street address, not below. 08. Use two letter state abbreviations in address block. 09. Use a ZIP+4® ZIP Code™ (if known) on last line following city and state. Otherwise, use the ZIP Code. 10. This is the barcode area; if you do not have a program that calculates and prints the correct barcode, then leave this area blank. 11. Postage is placed in upper right corner. **Are the return address and ZIP Code™ always needed?** If the sender's name is not included in the return address, another clear designation (apartment, suite, or room number) is required to ensure proper handling of ancillary services and/or return of the piece. ZIP Codes™ (5-digit or ZIP+4®) are required in the return address of all mail on which postage is paid with precanceled stamps or company permit imprint, and in the sender's return address on Periodicals mail when "Address Service Requested" is specified. Official mail (penalty mail) also requires a ZIP Code™ in the return address. | | | --- | | **Effective December 14, 2023**, the Postal Service is no longer accepting items that are shipped domestically and have a foreign return address. Domestic shipments can only have a domestic return address.
- If a domestic item is undeliverable, and includes a foreign return address, USPS will handle the mail in accordance with the current USPS dead mail procedures.
- A foreign address can only be used when shipping an item internationally, along with the appropriate customs form. | A return address tells the USPS where the sender wants the mail returned if it is undeliverable and is required on certain types of mail. For letter mail prepared as upgradable mail, the return address must not be in the OCR read area. Mail qualifying for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail™ prices must have the name and return address of the authorized nonprofit organization either on the outside of the mail piece or in a prominent location on the material being mailed. For a list of items that that require return addresses, see the [Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/602.htm#ep1078971). ZIP Codes™ are an integral part of making sure your mailpiece is processed quickly and efficiently. Not having a ZIP Code™ on your mail causes unnecessary delay because it will require manual look-up. Finding a ZIP Code™ is quick and easy from the [USPS.com home page](https://www.usps.com/) by clicking on "Quick Tools" and clicking " [Look Up a ZIP Code™](https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm)". ## USPS 2025 Service Standards Changes

## Starting 4/1/25, the United States Postal Service® will revise the service standards for First-Class Mail®, Periodicals, USPS Marketing Mail, and Package Services. The second and final phase will be implemented 7/1/25.      - Jun 30, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008244

Customer Information

Detailed information regarding service standards changes and specifications for downloadable files with the new standards can be accessed on PostalPro ([Service Standards \| PostalPro](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/13819)).

Please see [Delivering for America: Our ten-year plan highlights - about.usps.com](https://about.usps.com/what/strategic-plans/delivering-for-america/details.htm#fcps) for Press Releases, fact sheet and FAQs related to the second and final phase of the 2025 service standards changes effective 7/1/25.

To calculate the service standard for your mail or package, please see [https://postcalc.usps.com/ServiceCommitments/Index](https://postcalc.usps.com/ServiceCommitments/Index).

## Where Can I Learn about International Mailing Restrictions?

## Where to find information concerning International mailing restrictions.      - Jun 23, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006809

Customer Information

The [International Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) lists the specific restrictions for each country in the [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.gov/text/Imm/Immctry.htm) section. The following restrictions appear for all countries and are prohibited in all Priority Mail Express International® shipments: coins, banknotes; currency notes (paper money); securities of any kind payable to bearer; traveler’s checks; platinum, gold, and silver (manufactured or not); precious stones, jewelry, (including watches); and other valuable articles.

- Mailing (or exportation) from the United States of any rough diamond (regardless of size, source, or value) is prohibited unless the rough diamond has been controlled through the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme. See [IMM 560](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc5_022.htm) for the complete mailing standards of these items.

- For a current list of Priority Mail Express International and Priority Mail International Merchandise Insurance Limits (in U.S. Dollars) see [Priority Mail Express International Insurance](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc3_005.htm#ep447094 "Priority Mail International/Priority Mail Express International Insurance")

- For [What US Possessions, US Territories, and Freely Associated States are Considered Domestic?](https://usps.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/What-US-Possessions-US-Territories-and-Freely-Associated-States-are-Considered-Domestic),see the [current Postal Bulletin](http://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/welcome.htm "Postal Bulletin Archives") for currently prohibited or restricted items. The Mail Products screen contains a section called "Restrictions Apply" towards the bottom. Clicking on it will display all of the specific conditions for mailing a package to the country you had specified.

- Mail sent to APO/FPO/DPO addresses are domestic, even if the address is located overseas. However,[restrictions](https://postcalc.usps.com/Restrictions.aspx "APO/FPO/DPO Overseas Military and Diplomatic Mail") do apply to those overseas addresses.

- **Note**: All domestic mailing/shipping restrictions apply to international mail, such as size, weight, potentially hazardous contents. For additional information on domestic shipping restrictions, please see [Domestic Shipping Prohibitions, Restrictions and Hazmat](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm "Domestic Shipping Prohibitions, Restrictions and Hazmat").
