Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000n7BAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail) ## What is Priority Mail®? ## Priority Mail® provides the customer with an option of sending mail based on weight and dimension or via Flat Rate envelopes or boxes in a fast and affordable way. In most instances, delivery is made within 2 or 3 business days. - Oct 21, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006793 Customer Information **Priority Mail _®_** provides a fast and affordable way to deliver mail to every address in the U.S. In most instances, delivery is made within 2 or 3 business days. Priority Mail provides the customer with an option of sending mail based on weight and dimension or via USPS®-produced Flat Rate envelopes or boxes. All First-Class Mail® items over 13 ounces are sent at the Priority Mail service price. A customer can also request that mail content less than 13 ounces be sent at the Priority Mail price (to add an Extra Service that cannot be used on First Class Mail, for example). **Priority Mail Features** - Delivered six (6) days a week with no extra charge for Saturday delivery. - Often travels on the same transportation network as Priority Mail Express® service items. - Domestic Priority Mail includes up to $100 of insurance. - You can mail Priority Mail items from any local Post Office™ facility. You can also hand your prepaid packages to your letter carrier at the time of your regular mail delivery for no extra charge. - USPS Tracking® is provided for Priority Mail items at no additional charge. - Package Pickup™ and Pickup on Demand® services are available. - Forwarded or returned free of charge. - You may purchase [Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) such as Insurance and Return Receipt. ****Estimated Delivery Time**** - Priority Mail may arrive in 2 business days or 3 business days based on where you're shipping your package from and where it's being sent. (The scheduled delivery day can be found when [postage is calculated](http://postcalc.usps.gov/ "USPS.com - Postage Price Calculator")) This is not a guaranteed service. - Date of delivery is **not guaranteed**. Delivery by a specific date at a specific time is not available. - The first Postal business day counted is the day after the item was mailed. - Priority Mail® service, through USPS Tracking®, can be tracked at no additional charge. This also allows you to find the delivery status of the mailpiece. **Size and Weight Standards** - Priority Mail Letters: - Minimum Size = 3-1/2 inches x 5 inches. - Maximum Size = 11-1/2 inches x 6-1/8 inches. - Priority Mail Packages: - Maximum Length + Girth = 108 inches. - Maximum Weight = 70 pounds (lower weight limits apply to Commercial Cubic priced items and packages sent to APO/FPO/DPO destinations, depending on the ). - All letters and large envelopes over 13 ounces are sent at the Priority Mail® service price. Mail content less than 13 ounces may be sent as Priority Mail at the request of the sender. - Priority Mail® packages larger than 1 cubic foot and traveling to destinations within Zones 5-8 are subject to dimensional-weight pricing. **Postage Pricing** Priority Mail prices are determined by how the mailpiece is prepared for mailing. Items mailed or shipped in USPS-produced [Priority Mail Flat Rate Envelopes and Flat Rate Boxes pay a single price](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm#flatrate) not dependent on the mailpiece's weight (up to 70 lbs) or destination in the United States. When a Priority Mail item is mailed or shipped in an envelope or box either not produced by USPS or produced by USPS, but is not Flat Rate, the postage price is charged based on zone (the shipping origin and destination) and weight. Any fraction of a pound is rounded up to the next whole pound. For example, if a piece weighs 1.2 pounds, the weight (postage) increment is 2 pounds. The minimum postage amount per addressed piece is the 1-pound price. For detailed information on Priority Mail prices, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm). Other prices, fees, or charges can apply to Priority Mail packages. See [Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) for more information. **Commercial Pricing Availability** Priority Mail® Commercial Prices are available to commercial customers at a discount from retail prices who: - Pay postage with USPS-approved IBI postage meters - Meet specific criteria regarding how they print their IBI postage and electronically transmit required information to the Postal Service™ In addition, Priority Mail® Commercial Prices are available to both personal and business customers when purchasing postage through the [Click-N-Ship® application](https://cnsb.usps.com/). **Is Priority Mail Service Eligible for a Refund?** Priority Mail service without Extra Service is not eligible for a refund. Priority Mail with the following Extra Services may be eligible for a refund of the Extra Service fee only, if Extra Service was not fulfilled. Priority Mail postage is not eligible for a refund. | | | | --- | --- | | - Adult Signature
- Adult Signature Restricted Delivery
- Certificate of Mailing (Form 3817)
- Certified Mail®
- Certified Mail Restricted Delivery
- Collect on Delivery
- Collect on Delivery Restricted Delivery | - Insurance
- Insurance Restricted Delivery
- Registered Mail®
- Registered Mail Restricted Delivery
- Signature Confirmation™
- Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is Priority Mail®? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000014zFAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailing-Your-Tax-Return) ## Mailing Your Tax Return ## Information regarding tax forms and any special hours associated with the tax return filing deadline. - Mar 3, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007835 Customer Information # **Federal Tax Filing Deadline** The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) considers a tax return filed on time if it is addressed correctly, has enough postage, and is postmarked by the due date. - **April 15, 2026:** Federal tax return filing deadline for tax year 2025. - **Need a postmark?** If you want to ensure that your mailpiece receives a postmark and that the postmark date aligns with the date of mailing, go to any USPS retail location and ask for a manual postmark at the counter. It will be applied free of charge. - **For taxpayers affected by recent natural disasters**: See the IRS " [Tax Relief in Disaster Situations](https://www.irs.gov/newsroom/tax-relief-in-disaster-situations)" page for tax filing and payment deadline extensions. **Where can I find mailing options for sending my taxes?** See Mailing Options & Services below or visit [https://www.usps.com/taxes/](https://www.usps.com/taxes/ "Mailing Your Tax Return | USPS") for additional mailing information. **Mailing Options & Services** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | These mailing services apply a postmark to your return. If your return is postmarked by the IRS deadline date, it is considered on time. | | [Priority Mail Express®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm)
- 1−3 business day delivery
- [USPS Tracking®](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) included
- Eligible for Click-N-Ship® | [Priority Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm)
- 2−3 business day delivery
- [USPS Tracking®](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) included
- Eligible for Click-N-Ship®
- Eligible for [Certificate of Mailing](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) or [Registered Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics) | [First-Class Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/first-class-mail.htm)
- 1−5 business day delivery
- Extra services available
- Eligible for [Certificate of Mailing](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm#certificateofmailing) or [Registered Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics) | **Proof of Mailing & Proof of Delivery or Delivery Attempt** USPS has [extra services](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) that can be purchased and provide proof of mailing and proof of delivery or delivery attempt. **We highly recommend keeping your mailing receipt or Certificate of Mailing.** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Service** | **Proof of Mailing** | **Proof of Delivery or Delivery Attempt** | | **Certificate of Mailing**
This official record proves the date and time your mail was presented to USPS for mailing.
\*Ineligible for a refund | **Yes** | **No** | | **USPS Tracking®**
Provides tracking updates as an item travels to its destination, including the date and time of delivery or attempted delivery. Tracking information can be for non-signature items for 120 days and 2 years for signature items.
With the USPS Tracking Plus® service, you can buy extended tracking history for up to 10 years.
For additional information, please see: [USPS Tracking Plus® \- The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-Plus-The-Basics) | **No** | **No** | | **Certified Mail****®**
Prove you sent it. See when it was delivered or that a delivery attempt was made, and get the signature of the person who accepts the mailing when combined with Return Receipt.
For refund eligibility, please see: [Certified Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certified-Mail-The-Basics) | **Yes** | **Yes** | | **Return Receipt**
Can only be purchased for First-Class Mail and Priority Mail if combined with either Certified Mail, Collect on Delivery, insurance over $500.00, or Registered Mail.
For additional information and refund eligibility, please see: [Return Receipt - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics) | **No** | **Yes** | [![Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailing-Your-Tax-Return#top) **Where can I find retail hours for Tax Day?** Visit [https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) to find USPS locations and their retail hours. **My tax refund is late – what can I do?** If you haven't received your refund, please [visit the IRS Refunds page](https://www.irs.gov/refunds) to check the status. The IRS is currently processing all mailed tax returns in the order received. If you mailed a tax return and haven't heard from them, please check expected wait times for [current IRS operations and services.](https://www.irs.gov/help/processing-status-for-tax-forms "Processing status for tax forms") **Are tax forms available at my local Post Office®?** No, Post Offices do not have tax forms available for customers. However, you can view, download, and print specific tax forms and publications at the ["Forms, Instructions & Publications" page of the IRS website](https://www.irs.gov/forms-instructions "Forms & Instructions | Internal Revenue Service"). You may also acquire tax forms [through the mail](https://www.irs.gov/forms-pubs/forms-and-publications-by-us-mail "Forms and Publications by U.S. Mail | Internal Revenue Service"). If you meet the qualifications, you can also begin to e-file your taxes online. Your local government offices may have tax forms available for pickup. **My return shows it was delivered to a different ZIP CODE** **™** **than the one I mailed it to?** You may see your tax return delivered to a ZIP Code that is different than the one you addressed it to. **This is normal and does not indicate that your return was delivered to the wrong location.** The IRS has multiple delivery locations and courier services across the country to expedite pickup and delivery of mailed tax returns. | | | --- | | **Delivery Questions** | **I sent my tax return with a trackable service. Why do I not see that it was delivered?** Even with the assistance of technology, combined with our employee's best efforts, items will be delivered but unfortunately may not receive a delivered scan. We know this is disappointing, and we ask that you please accept our apology for any inconvenience. If your tax documents did not receive a delivered scan, we recommend the following steps: - If you sent a payment, please check with your financial institution to verify if your check/money order/cashier's check has been cashed. - Contact the agency to see if their records indicate receipt of your mailing. We also highly recommend keeping your mailing receipt. The agency may request proof of mailing, such as your mailing receipt or Certificate of Mailing. - **Your return is considered filed on time if the envelope is properly addressed, has enough postage, is postmarked, and is deposited in the mail by the due date.** See [https://www.irs.gov/taxtopics/tc301](https://www.irs.gov/taxtopics/tc301) for more information. - Check the status of your refund at [https://www.irs.gov/refunds](https://www.irs.gov/refunds). **I've moved. Will my W2 forward to my new address?** Mailpieces sent from businesses, financial institutions or government agencies that use [ancillary endorsements](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#a_1_5) may not forward to your new address. Endorsements are instructions from mailers to USPS that tell us what to do with mail that cannot be delivered as addressed. Common examples could be driver's licenses or bank statements. Please contact the sender of the mailpiece you are looking for to ask whether it will forward or can be resent. **Additional IRS Assistance** - **Visit the IRS website**( [http://www.irs.gov/](http://www.irs.gov/ "Internal Revenue Service homepage")) where you can find the mailing addresses for IRS offices. - [IRS Phone Numbers](https://www.irs.gov/help/telephone-assistance "Let us help you") - [Where to File Paper Tax Returns](https://www.irs.gov/filing/where-to-file-paper-tax-returns-with-or-without-a-payment "Where to file paper tax returns with or without a payment") - Visit the [Forms, Instructions & Publications page](https://www.irs.gov/forms-instructions "Forms, Instructions & Publications") to download products and forms - Or call **1-800-829-3676** (1-800-TAX-FORM) to place an order [![Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mailing-Your-Tax-Return#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Mailing Your Tax Return URL Name Mailing-Your-Tax-Return [Contacts & Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG8GAM/contacts-help)[Seasonal Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHGGA2/seasonal-services)[Hot Topics](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHTGA2/hot-topics) ## Related Articles - [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) - [Return to Sender Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-to-Sender-Mail) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [Certified Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certified-Mail-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Mailing Your Tax Return

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FU-S-Passports-The-Basics) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000012xRAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) ## U.S. Passports - The Basics ## Information on applying for a first-time passport, fees, photo requirements and contacting Department of State. - Mar 2, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007488 Customer Information **IMPORTANT NOTES** | | | --- | | Passport customers can choose either routine processing or optional expedited processing for an additional $60. Check the [Department of State's website](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports.html#inner-content) for the current processing times for the services.
The Department of State suggests you:
- Plan ahead and apply early.
- Send your application to us via trackable mail, so you can track your application before it enters the Department's system. This can be done whether applying at an acceptance facility or by mail.
- Pay an extra $22.05 for 1-3 day delivery for the return of your completed passport.
To learn more about current passport operations, visit the [Department of State's website](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/traveladvisories/ea/passport-covid-19.html) or contact the National Passport Information Center at 1-877-487-2778. | | | | --- | | # Life-or-Death Emergencies
Life-or-death emergencies are serious illnesses, injuries, or deaths in your immediate family that require you to travel outside the United States **within 72 hours (3 days)**. If you're traveling in more than 72 hours, learn more about how to apply [here](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/get-fast/emergencies.html#inner-content).
Call us to make an appointment for emergency passport service in case of a life-or-death emergency **within 72 hours (3 days)**.
You must provide:
- A [passport application with supporting documents](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/how-to-apply.html "How to Apply for a Passport page").
- Proof of the life-or-death emergency such as a death certificate, a statement from a mortuary, or a signed letter from a hospital or medical professional. Documents should be translated or in English.
- Proof of international travel (itinerary).
**How to Contact Us for assistance during a Life or Death Emergency ONLY**
For non-emergencies, see [normal business hours and contact information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_contact_the_State_Department)
From the United States:
- 1-877-487-2778/ 1-888-874-7793 (TTY/TDD) Monday-Friday, 8:00 am to 8:00 pm Eastern Time, except federal holidays.
- 202-647-4000 outside of the hours listed above
From outside the United States:
Contact the nearest [U.S. Embassy or Consulate](http://www.usembassy.gov/ "Find your Nearest Embassy or Consulate"). | **Select a link to access the frequently asked questions (FAQs).** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**What is the role of the United States Postal Service with passports?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#What_is_the_United_States_Postal_Service_role) | [**What does a U.S. passport book or card look like?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#What_does_a_US_Passport_book_look_like) | [**How do I renew a passport?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_renew_a_passport) | | [**How can I check my passport renewal application status with the Department of State?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#check_status) | **[How do I contact Department of State for passport information?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_contact_the_State_Department "How do I contact Department of State for Passport information?")** | **[How do I apply for a first-time passport with USPS®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_apply_for_a_first-time_passport)** | | **[Where can I find information on passport photos?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Where_can_I_find_Info_on_passport_photos)** | **[How do I pay passport fees at a local Post Office™ facility?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_pay_passport_fees_at_a_local_Post_Office)** | **[How do I schedule a passport appointment?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_schedule_a_passport_appointment)** | | **[How can I find information on lost passports, errors, fees, expedited processing and refunds?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_can_I_find%20information_on_lost_passports)** | **[What about applying in person and minors?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#What_about_applying_in_person_and_minors)** | **[What else do I need to know when applying for a Passport with the USPS?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#What_else_do_I_need_to_know "What else do I need to know when applying for a Passport with the USPS®?")** | | **[Need more information? Check out these FAQs.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Need_more_Info)** | | | ## **What is the role of the United States Postal Service with passports?** - The Postal Service will: - accept a customer's FIRST TIME passport application - take authorized passport photos - review the application and forward the application to the U.S. Department of State for processing - The Postal Service does **_NOT_** issue or create passports. - Customers **_CANNOT_** pick up passports from a Post Office™ facility; they are **mailed to the customer from the U.S. Department of State.** - For those wishing to **renew** a passport, **replace** a lost or stolen passport, or provide a **name change**, please visit the Department of State's website [https://travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs") for more information. **Can other companies assist me with passports?** Courier companies, also known as "passport expeditors," are private companies, some of which are allowed to submit passport applications on behalf of customers. Please visit the Department of State's website [https://travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/hurry/courier-and-expeditor-companies.html) for more information. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## **What does a U.S. passport book or card look like?** - All travelers are required to present a passport or other document that denotes identity and citizenship when entering the United States. The U.S. passport book is a full service passport document. Therefore, it can be used for all forms of travel (air, land, and sea) to and from any destination outside of the United States. When using international air travel, a passport book is required regardless of the foreign destination. - The U.S. Department of State began issuing U.S. passport cards in July 2008. The passport card is the wallet-size travel document that can only be used to re-enter the United States at land border-crossings and sea ports-of-entry from Canada, Mexico, the Caribbean, and Bermuda. The card provides a less expensive, smaller, and convenient alternative to the passport book for those who travel frequently to these destinations by land or by sea. **Note - the passport card cannot be used for international travel by air.** **![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PASSPORT/Passport%20Book.jpg)** **Why can't I use the passport card to fly to Canada, Mexico, the Caribbean, or Bermuda?** - The passport card was designed for specific needs of northern and southern U.S. border communities with residents that cross the border frequently by land. The passport book is the only document approved for international travel by air. ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PASSPORT/Passport%20Card.jpg) **Can I apply for the passport card and passport book at the same time using the same application?** - Yes, simply check the appropriate box at the top of the form to indicate that you would like to apply for both products. If you already have a passport book and are eligible to use Form DS-82, you may apply for your new passport card by mail using Form DS-82. You may renew your passport book at the same time using the same form. - For specific information about passport requirements (including specific information about travel to and from Canada, Mexico and the Caribbean) please visit [https://travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs"). Other contact information can be found by [clicking here](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_contact_the_State_Department "clicking here"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) **How do I renew a passport?** - For those wishing to renew a passport [online](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports.html "information for online passport renewal") or by mail, replace a lost or stolen passport, or provide a name change, please visit the Department of State's website at [https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports.html](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs") for more information. The Department of State encourages those renewing a Passport Application by mail to use a service option that includes tracking. We recommend the following services: - [Priority Mail Express®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm "Priority Mail Express®") service (for 1-3 day delivery requests to most U.S. locations) - [Priority Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm "Priority Mail®") service [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) **How can I check my passport renewal application status with the Department of State?** - Call the National Passport Information Center at 1-877-487-2778 or you can access the [Online Passport Status System](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/after/status.html "Online Passport Status System") to check the status of your application. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) **How do I contact the Department of State for passport information?** - You may contact the Department of State's National Passport Information Center (NPIC) for detailed information on U.S. Passports (i.e. expedited services, refunds, or documents such as birth certificates, etc. to be returned to you) through any of the following methods: - By website [https://travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs") - By phone - 1-877-487-2778 (1-877-4-USA-PPT) - 1-888-874-7793 (TDD/TTY) - Available: - Customer Service Representatives are available Monday-Friday from 8:00am to 10:00pm Eastern Time, or Saturday-Sunday from 10:00am to 3:00pm Eastern Time - Excluding federal holidays - Automated passport information is available 24/7 - By email: [NPIC@state.gov](mailto:NPIC@state.gov), most email inquiries are answered within 24 hours; however, the status of a passport will not be communicated via email. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## **How do I apply for a first-time passport with USPS®?** You can apply for a first time passport at Post Office™ locations throughout the U.S. | | | --- | | ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PASSPORT/Step%201_Passport%20Application.png)**Complete Passport Application Form DS-11 Online** | | Passport Applications should be completed and printed prior to appointment. (U.S. Passport Application forms are not provided by USPS and can be found at [pptform.state.gov](https://pptform.state.gov/ "pptform.state.gov").)
After completing [Passport Application Form DS-11](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/apply-in-person.html) online, you can apply for a first time passport at Post Office™ locations throughout the U.S.
To locate a Post Office™ facility where you can apply for a passport or have your passport photo taken for an additional fee, [click here for our Post Office™ Locator tool](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action?address=&radius=&locationTypeQ=passport&tAddress=&tAddress1Ams=&tAddress2Ams=&tCityAms=&tStateAms=&tZipAms=&tCarrierRouteAms=&sWithin=&sServices=&hoursSelected=&collectionBoxSelected=&collectionHoursSelected=&servicesSelectedBusinessOffice=&servicesSelectedOffice1=&servicesSelectedPassportOffice=&servicesSelectedOffice2=&servicesSelectedOffice3=&approvedProvidersSelected=&allLocationsSelected1=&allLocationsSelected2 "USPS Postal Locator"). To schedule a passport appointment, go to " [How do I schedule a passport appointment?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#How_do_I_schedule_a_passport_appointment)" for more information. | | ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PASSPORT/Step%202_Proof%20of%20ID.png)**Bring Required Documents** | | In order to apply for a first time passport there are a number of required documents you must have including a completed [Passport Application Form DS-11](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/apply-in-person.html "Apply in Person"). Customers should visit [https://travel.state.gov/content/travel.html](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel.html "Travel") for more information.
1. **PROOF OF U.S. CITIZENSHIP:**

Evidence of U.S. citizenship **AND a photocopy** of the front (and back, if there is printed information) must be submitted with your passport application. The photocopy must be on 8.5" x 11" paper, black and white ink, legible, and clear.

2. **PROOF OF IDENTITY:**
1. One must be an original document (not a photocopy) proving U.S. citizenship, such as a certified U.S. birth certificate or a certificate of naturalization or citizenship
- Photocopy of U.S. citizenship must be on 8.5" x 11" white paper and have a copy of the front and back side (if there is writing on both sides) photocopy cannot be double sided.
2. One must be a current photo identification document such as a valid driver's license, government ID, or military ID
- Photocopy must be on 8.5" x 11" white paper and have a copy of the front and back side (if there is writing on both sides) photocopy cannot be double sided.
- The same ID document must also be presented if you are applying in person.
3. You must present your original identification **AND submit a photocopy** of the front and back with your passport application. | | ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/PASSPORT/Step%203_Photo%20Reqs.png)
**PASSPORT PHOTOS:**
- On a plain white or off-white background
- 2 x 2 inches in size
- In color
- Taken within the last 6 months reflecting your current appearance
- If eyeglasses are worn, they must be removed in photo
- More information on [passport photos requirements and examples](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/photos.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs") as established by the Department of State.
For a $15 fee, your passport photo may be taken at many of the Post Office facilities that accept passport applications. Please check with your local Post Office™ facility to make sure it offers photo service and whether an appointment is necessary if you need passport photos. | | Photo Source: U.S. Department of State 2017 [https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/photos.html](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/photos.html "Passport Photos") | **My passport has been damaged. Can I continue to use this passport?** - If your passport has been significantly damaged, especially the book cover or the page displaying your personal data and photo, you will need to apply for a new passport. Conditions that may constitute damage requiring you to replace your passport include water damage, a significant tear, unofficial markings on the data page, missing visa pages (torn out), a hole punch, or other injuries. Normal wear of a U.S. passport is expected and likely does not constitute "damage". For instance, normal wear includes the bend of a passport after being carried in your back pocket or fanning of the visa pages after extensive opening and closing. If you need to replace your damaged passport, you will need to submit the following in person: - The damaged passport - A signed statement explaining the damaged or mutilated condition of your passport book and/or card - [Form DS-11](https://eforms.state.gov/Forms/ds11_pdf.PDF) - All documents required by Form DS-11 including citizenship documentation (i.e., birth certificate) **How long does it take to get a passport?** - Processing times can vary depending on the time of year, and occasional unforeseen circumstances such as natural disasters. During busier times, such as the summer travel season, the Department of State encourages customers to expedite their applications if traveling in less than 6 weeks. You can refer to the Department of State's website for the [most up to date processing times](https://travel.state.gov/content/passports/en/passports/information/processing-times.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs"). - Processing times begin the day the Department of State receives your application at a passport agency or center, not the day you mail your application or apply for a passport at a local acceptance facility. **What is an Acceptable Form of Identification?** **Photo Identification** (For a list of all acceptable identification IDs please visit [https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/identification.html](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/identification.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs"). You must present a physical, government-issued photo identification document (ID) when submitting [Form DS-11](https://eforms.state.gov/Forms/ds11_pdf.PDF "DS-11 Passport Form") if you are: - Over age 18 - The parent or guardian identifying an applicant under age 18 You must present one physical, primary ID which includes your photo. We cannot accept digital IDs. If you cannot present one of the primary photo IDs below, please present a combination of at least two secondary IDs. **Bring the ID and a photocopy of the ID when you apply** Submit a photocopy of the front and back of each ID that you present when you apply. - Photocopy must be on white, 8.5'' x 11" standard paper, black and white, and single sided. - Do not decrease the image size (although you may enlarge it). ### _**Acceptable Primary IDs:**_(submit one) - Valid or expired, undamaged U.S. passport book or passport card - In-state, fully valid driver's license or enhanced driver's license with photo - Certificate of Naturalization - Certificate of Citizenship - Government employee ID (city, county, state, or federal) - U.S. military or military dependent ID - Current (valid) foreign passport - Matricula Consular (Mexican Consular ID) - commonly used by a parent of a U.S. citizen child applicant - U.S. Permanent Resident Card (Green Card) - commonly used by a parent of a U.S. citizen child applicant - Trusted Traveler IDs (including valid Global Entry, FAST, SENTRI, and NEXUS cards) - Enhanced Tribal Cards and Native American tribal photo IDs - Other documents: In-state, fully valid learner's permit with photo, In-state, fully valid non-driver ID with photo, and temporary driver's license with photo. Note: you may be asked to present an additional ID when presenting one of these 3 documents. **Notice on Digital Identification Documents**: Some states now issue digital ID documents (also known as mobile driver's licenses or mobile IDs). We cannot accept these digital IDs when you apply for your U.S. passport. You must continue to submit your physical, photo ID and a photocopy of the ID. ### _**Acceptable Secondary IDs:**_(submit at least two from the list below) You must present one physical, primary ID which includes your photo. We cannot accept digital IDs. **If (and only if) you cannot present one of the primary photo IDs listed above, you should present a combination of at least two secondary IDs listed below.** - Out-of-state driver's license or enhanced driver's license with photo - Learner's or temporary driver's permit (without a photo) - In-state, fully valid non-driver ID (without a photo) - Out-of-state, non-driver ID - Temporary driver's license (without a photo) - Social Security card - Voter registration card - Employee work ID - Student ID - School yearbook with identifiable photograph - Selective Service (draft) card - Medicare or other health card - Expired driver's license - Form DS-71, for an Identifying Witness (only available at your local [Acceptance Facility or a Passport Agency](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/need-passport/apply-in-person.html)). ## How do I pay passport fees at a local Post Office™ facility? - Two separate payments are required for passport services. One is an application fee which is provided to the Department of State; the second fee is an acceptance fee that is paid directly to the Postal Service: - Application Fee and any Additional Services Fees payable to "U.S. Department of State." - Checks (personal, certified, cashier's, traveler's) and money orders. - Credit and debit cards CANNOT be accepted. - Please visit [https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/fees.html](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/fees.html "U.S. Department of State Bureau of Consular Affairs") for the U.S. Department of State. - $35 Acceptance Fee paid separately to USPS® with checks/money orders made payable to "Postmaster". - Cash - Money orders - Personal checks - Debit or credit cards [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## How do I schedule a passport appointment? The USPS Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler® feature allows for scheduling a passport application appointment every 15 minutes during passport hours of operation. To schedule a passport appointment: - **Online:** Please visit the [USPS Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler](https://www.usps.com/scheduler "USPS Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler") ( [www.usps.com/scheduler](http://www.usps.com/scheduler)) and click "Schedule an Appointment". - **By Phone:** Customers may call 1-800-275-8777 and schedule passport appointments through the IVR by saying "passport appointment" when prompted with the question "How can I help you?" at the beginning of the call. Appointments for up to 6 applicants may be scheduled through the IVR. - **At a Post Office location**: Use the self-service kiosk. - **Note**: Passport **renewals** cannot be made using RCAS or on the kiosk. **Please note: USPS employees do not have access rights to create appointments online in the Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler** **application on behalf of customers.** You can learn more by visiting the FAQ - [USPS Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Retail-Customer-Appointment-Scheduler). You can find your local Post Office™ facility's passport hours of operation through the [Postal Locator](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction "USPS Postal Locator"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## How can I find information on lost passports, errors, fees, expedited processing and refunds? - [Report a lost or stolen passport](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/after/lost-stolen.html "Report a lost or stolen passport") immediately to protect yourself from identity theft. - Detailed information on [how to change or correct information in your passport](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/change-correct.html "Change or Correct a Passport") can be obtained by visiting the U.S. Department of State's website. - **Expedited processing** - For more information to [Get a Passport in a Hurry](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/hurry.html "Get a Passport in a Hurry"), locate a [Regional Passport Agency](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/where-to-apply/passport-agencies.html "Passport Agencies"), or to make an appointment contact the [National Passport Information Center](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/contact-us/passports.html "Contact Us"). - Service Commitment - if you pay for Expedited Service and do not receive your passport within 2-3 weeks after applying (assuming two-way Priority Mail Express® service is chosen), you may [submit a refund request](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/hurry/refund.html "Refund of Expedite Passport Fee") with the U.S. Department of State for the Expedited Service Fee. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## What about applying in person and minors? - You must apply in person using Form DS-11 if at least **one** of the following is true: - You are applying for your **first** U.S. passport or - You are under age 16 - Your previous U.S. passport was issued when you were **under age 16** or - Your previous U.S. passport was **lost, stolen, or damaged** or - Your previous U.S. passport was issued **more than 15 years ago** or - Your name has changed since your U.S. passport was issued and you are **unable to legally document your name change** - Please note that additional requirements exist for minor children under 16 years of age and for minor children 16-17 years old: - **Minor Children Under 16 years of age** - Must apply in person with both parent(s)/guardian(s). - Parent(s)/Guardian(s) must submit evidence of their relationship to the minor applicant. - When applying for a minor under age 16, **both** parent(s)/guardian(s) must present acceptable identification at the time of application. - **Both** parent(s)/guardian(s) must provide consent authorizing passport issuance for a minor under age 16. If you **cannot** submit [primary identification](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/identification.html#headerandtext_1 "Apply in Person"), then you will need to submit documents that meet [secondary identification requirements](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/requirements/identification.html#headerandtext_2 "Frequently Asked Questions"). For more information on passports for minors (under age 16) please visit [Department of State's FAQ for minors](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/faqs.html#Minors "Frequently Asked Questions"). - **Minor Children 16 and 17 years old** - Must appear **in person** at the time of application - Have a parent or guardian present photo identification if the minor does not have identification of his or her own - Establish parental consent (You may be requested to provide written parental consent for a child age 16 or 17 if it is not implied on the application) - **_If none of the above statements apply to you, you may be eligible to [Renew by Mail](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/renew-by-mail.html "Renew by Mail") using Form DS-82._** [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## What else do I need to know when applying for a passport with USPS®? - **Do I have to provide my Social Security Number on my passport application?** Yes. The FAST Act (Public Law 114-94) requires you to provide your Social Security Number (SSN), if you have one, when you apply for a U.S. passport or renewal of a U.S. passport. If you do not have an SSN, enter zeros in box #5 of your passport form. The Social Security Administration can issue you an SSN for future use. If you fail to provide the information, you will encounter a delay in processing and/or denial of your passport application. You will also be subject to a $500 penalty enforced by the IRS pursuant to Section 6039E of the Internal Revenue Code (26 U.S.C. 6039E). - **Will I receive my new passport and documents back in one envelope?** - **Passport Book Only:** You may receive your newly issued passport and your citizenship documents in two separate mailings. - **Passport Card Only:** You may receive your newly issued passport card and your citizenship documents in two separate mailings. - **Passport Book and Card:** You may receive three separate mailings; one with your citizenship documents, one with your newly issued passport book, and one with your newly issued passport card. - **If you do not receive a second mailing** within 10 business days of receiving the first, please contact the [National Passport Information Center](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/contact-us/passports.html "National Passport Information Center") by email at [NPIC@state.gov](mailto:NPIC@state.gov) or by phone at 1-877-487-2778; 1-888-874-7793 (TDD/TTY). - **Non-Refundable Fees** The passport application fee and the execution fee are non-refundable. They are collected and retained by law even if a passport is not issued. - **Service Commitment** If you pay for Expedited Service and do not receive your passport within 2-3 weeks after applying (assuming two-way Priority Mail Express® service is chosen), you may submit a [refund request](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/apply-renew-passport/hurry/refund.html "Refund of Expedite Passport Fee") with the U.S. Department of State for the Expedited Service Fee. - **Mailing Requirements** All Acceptance Facilities must use USPS Priority Mail Flat Rate envelopes (EP14F) **OR** USPS Priority Mail Express Flat Rate envelopes (EP13F) when mailing passport applications to the Department of State. No other form of trackable mail will be allowed for mailing passport applications. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) ## Need more information? Check out these FAQs: - [What Forms of Payment are Accepted?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted) - [USPS® Credit Card Signature Policy](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Credit-Card-Signature-Policy) [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics#Top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title U.S. Passports - The Basics URL Name U-S-Passports-The-Basics [Passports](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFpGAM/passports) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading U.S. Passports - The Basics

metadata.title: U.S. Passports - The Basics

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000015NRAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates) ## Mail Service Alerts and Updates ## How to find information on suspensions and limitations of mailing and shipping services. This includes Priority Mail Express® embargoes and quarantines. - Mar 4, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006996 Customer Information Mail service alerts and updates are provided on USPS.com® to inform the public and the mailing industry when service is suspended or embargoed at Post Office™ facilities for any reason, including natural disasters such as floods or fires and quarantines. Information is updated frequently and updates are made in real time. To view postal facility service alerts and updates, go to the [USPS Service Alerts page on USPS.com](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/) at [https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/). * * * | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Information on other disruptions can be found below: | | | | --- | --- | | [**Military & Diplomatic Mail (APO/FPO/DPO) Service Disruptions and Suspensions**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#dpo_mail_service_disruptions) | [**International Mail Service Suspensions**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#temporary_international_service_suspensions) | **Military & Diplomatic Mail (APO/FPO/DPO) Service Disruption and Suspensions** Mail addressed to military and diplomatic post offices (APO/FPO/DPO) overseas is subject to certain conditions or restrictions of mailing regarding content, preparation, and handling. - To find the latest condition and restrictions for a specific location, enter its ZIP Code™ on the [APO/FPO/DPO Restrictions page](https://postcalc.usps.com/militaryrestrictions). - To check if mailing services to an APO/FPO/DPO destination has been suspended, go to the [USPS Service Alerts page on USPS.com](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/ "https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/") at [https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/ "https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/"). - For a complete list of restrictions to all APO/FPO/DPO destinations, see the most recent [_Postal Bulletin_'s](https://about.usps.com/resources/postal-bulletin.htm) pull-out section (Pull-Out Information > Other Information > Overseas Military/Diplomatic Mail). | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Military Post Offices Temporarily Suspended** **Effective 3/4/26**, all Mailing Services to the Military ZIP Codes listed below are temporarily suspended until further notice. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - 09303
- 09307
- 09309
- 09315
- 09321
- 09343
- 09365 | - 09801
- 09803
- 09807
- 09811
- 09815
- 09819
- 09829 | - 09832
- 09853
- 09858
- 09877
- 09898
- 09903 | | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#top) **International Mail Service Suspensions** The Postal Service™ receives notices that various postal operators are no longer able to process or deliver international mail or services originating from the United States (U.S.) due to unavailable transportation, logistical impacts resulting from the conflict in the Middle East, and other unrelated service disruptions. As a result, the Postal Service is currently unable to accept items destined for affected countries at any Post Office® or postal facility location. **List updated March 4, 2026 and effective until further notice.** **Customers are asked to refrain from entering items addressed to any of the below countries into the USPS system effective immediately.** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Afghanistan
- Algeria
- Armenia
- Bahrain
- Belarus
- Bhutan[¹](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#suspensions_1)
- Cuba
- Djibouti
- Haiti | - Iran
- Iraq
- Israel
- Kuwait
- Libya
- Madagascar
- Niue[²](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#suspensions2)
- Pakistan
- Qatar | - Russia
- Seychelles
- South Sudan[³](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#suspensions_3)
- Sudan
- Syria
- Turkmenistan[4](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#suspensions_4)
- United Arab Emirates
- Venezuela
- Yemen | | **Note: Canada Post has announced a shutdown for the duration of a national strike by the Canadian Union of Postal Workers.**
- USPS will continue to pick up, accept, and transport mail and packages to Canada, while monitoring the situation closely.
- **Customers are asked to refrain from mailing perishable items or time sensitive mail until further notice**.
- **Effective October 24, 2025**, USPS resumed the guarantee on Priority Mail Express International (PMEI) items. | | ¹ Bhutan's service disruption affects Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI), Priority Mail International® (PMI), First-Class Package International Service® (FCPIS®), International Priority Airmail® (IPA®), and M-Bag® items. Customers are asked to refrain from mailing those items to this country.
² Niue's service disruption affects Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI). Customers are asked to refrain from mailing PMEI items to this country.
³ South Sudan's service disruption affects Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI), Priority Mail International® (PMI), First-Class Package International Service® (FCPIS®), International Priority Airmail® (IPA®) Packets and M-bags, and Airmail M-Bag® items. Customers are asked to refrain from mailing those items to this country.
4 Turkmenistan's service disruption affects Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI), Priority Mail International® (PMI), First-Class Package International Service® (FCPIS®), International Priority Airmail® (IPA®) Packets and M-bags, and Airmail M-Bag® items. Customers are asked to refrain from mailing those items to this country. | | International shipping can be impacted and suspended to countries due to multiple service disruptions. According to [_Domestic Mail Manual_ 604.9.2.3](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#a_9_2_3), customers are entitled to a full refund of their postage costs when service to the country of destination is suspended. | These service disruptions affect Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI), Priority Mail International® (PMI), First-Class Mail International® (FCMI), First-Class Package International Service® (FCPIS®), International Priority Airmail® (IPA®), International Surface Air Lift® (ISAL®), and M-Bag® items. Unless otherwise noted, service suspensions to a particular country do not affect delivery of military and diplomatic mail. For military and diplomatic mail suspensions, look in the [above section](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#dpo_mail_service_disruptions). | | | | --- | --- | | **For already deposited items:** | Postal Service employees endorse them "Mail Service Suspended — Return to Sender" and then place them in the mail stream for return. | | **For any returned item bearing a customs form:** | The Postal Service will, upon request, refund postage and fees on mail returned due to the suspension of service. | | **For all other returned items not bearing a customs declarations form:** | Either:
- The Postal Service will, upon request, refund postage and fees on mail returned due to the suspension of service,
- Or the sender may re-mail them with the existing postage once service has been restored. When remailing under this option, customers must cross out the markings "Mail Service Suspended — Return to Sender." | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates#top) | | * * * - The Postal Service maintains steady communications with mailers during natural disasters or other events that require emergency responses and advises residential customers and business mailers with regard to postal facility disruptions that may impact delivery in an affected area via its USPS Service Alerts webpage at: [https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/). | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Mail Service Alerts and Updates URL Name Mail-Service-Alerts-and-Updates [Contacts & Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG8GAM/contacts-help)[Basic USPS Information](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHFGA2/basic-usps-information)[Hot Topics](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHTGA2/hot-topics) ## Related Articles - [Scams & Scheme Alerts](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Scams-Scheme-Alerts) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [What is Library Mail Service?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Library-Mail-Service-and-Media-Mail-Service) - [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) - [No mail delivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/No-Mail-Delivery) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Mail Service Alerts and Updates
