Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000015LpAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup) ## Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand® ## Benefits of Package Pickup (free) or Pickup on Demand® (for a fee), how they work, how to schedule, what types of mail are eligible for pickup, limitations and postage options. - Mar 4, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006808 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[General information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#generalinfo)** | **[Scheduling a pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#scheduling)** | **[How does it work?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#work)** | | **[Where do I leave item(s) for pickup?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#leaveitems)** | **[What is eligible for pickup?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#eligibility)** | **[Confirmation notices](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#confirmation)** | | **[Limitations on what can be picked up](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#limitations)** | **[Can I modify or cancel my request?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#cancellations)** | **[Package pickup postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#postage)** | | **[Additional information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#additionalinformation)** | **[Pickup on Demand refunds](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#pickup)** | [**Pickup on Demand fees**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#PODemandFees) | **General Information** **The Postal Service has three different options for pickup of outgoing mail.** - **Pickup on Demand®** is perfect for when you need to have mail picked up on a specific date in a specific timeframe. A pickup fee is charged per trip, regardless of the number of items scheduled for pickup. - **Package Pickup**allows you to schedule a free pickup for the next delivery day or a designated day. - **Collection Service**refers to prepaid mail deposited in a collection box or prepaid mail customers hand directly to a letter carrier or other postal employee. **How does it work?** **Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand allow you to request a package pickup:** - Package Pickup is a free (regardless of the number of items scheduled for pickup), next delivery day (or designated delivery day) service. - Pickup on Demand allows you to schedule a specific time of day for a package pickup, for a [fee](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198). - You can schedule pickups Monday - Saturday until 4:59 AM local time on the day of the pickup. After 5:00 AM local time, same-day pickup is not available. - Package Pickup can occur at any time on the scheduled date. Pickup on Demand typically occurs within two hours of the scheduled pickup time on the schedule date. - You can [edit or cancel](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#cancellations) a scheduled pickup request until 4:59 AM local on the day of the pickup. - Individual items cannot exceed 70 pounds or 130 inches in total length and girth. **Schedule your Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand [here](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm?_gl=1*42ulqf*_gcl_au*OTMyMjg1MjIuMTY4MjAwNjE1NA..*_ga*MTkwOTc2MDUxMi4xNjU4MjQ1NTAz*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY4NzQ2MzQzNS41MC4wLjE2ODc0NjM0MzUuMC4wLjA.).** | | | --- | | Mailpieces weighing more than 10oz. or more than 1/2-inch in thickness bearing postage stamps for postage are not valid for Package Pickup and must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail counter at a Post Office™ location. | **[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top)** **What is eligible for pickup?** **Your letter carrier can pick up packages with prepaid postage and/or return merchandise using the following services:** - **Priority Mail Express® -** the service commitment varies based on the time the item is mailed, where it is deposited, and the destination. - Due to the different cutoff times for each Post Office™ facility as well as different letter carrier schedules: - If you want the Priority Mail Express guarantee for a specific mailing, the item(s) must be presented to an acceptance employee at an actual Post Office location. - You will need to contact your local Post Office for guarantee details in your area (including estimations on whether or not Priority Mail Express items picked up using Package Pickup or otherwise given to a letter carrier will meet the cutoff time for that day). - **Priority Mail®** - **USPS Ground Advantage®** - **Returns** - **International** - Priority Mail Express International® - Priority Mail International® - First-Class Package International Service® | | | --- | | - International packages can only be picked up if the shipping label, customs forms and postage payment was completed online. Packages with hand-written customs forms must be taken to a Post Office™ retail location for mailing.
- APO/FPO/DPO addressed mail is generally handled as if it were a Domestic Mail item; [however a customs form may be required](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#limitations).
- Packages of other mail classes, such as First-Class Mail® items, may also be picked up, as long as they are combined with one of the aforementioned premium products. | **Limitations on what can be picked up** - Hazardous materials must meet all packaging, labeling, and other United States Postal Service® mailability standards in the [Domestic Mail Manual (DMM®)](https://pe.usps.gov/DMM300/Index) or [International Mail Manual (IMM®)](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) as applicable. This includes items that contain anything fragile without proper packaging or liquid items. - Size limitations: - 70 pounds - 130 inches in total length and girth - The following items must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail counter at a Post Office™ location: - Mailpieces weighing more than 10 oz. or more than 1/2-inch in thickness bearing postage stamps for postage must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail counter at a Post Office™ location. - International mailpieces that require a customs declaration form and bear postage stamps must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail service counter at a Post Office location. - International mailpieces, including mail destined to APO/FPO/DPO addresses, with a customs declaration form that was not completed online (e.g., using Click-N-Ship® application) must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail service counter at a Post Office location. - For more detailed information about what can be mailed, customers should reference the [Domestic Mail Manual (DMM®)](https://pe.usps.gov/DMM300/Index), which is available online at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® ( [pe.usps.com](https://pe.usps.gov/)). **[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top)** **Package pickup postage** Sufficient postage must be properly applied to your mailpiece(s) before the pickup. You can purchase postage online using the Click-N-Ship®, PC Postage®, or Postage Meters. Postage stamps may be used on an item weighing 10 oz or less or 1/2-inch in thickness or less. However, mailpieces weighing more than 10 oz or more than 1/2-inch in thickness and bearing postage stamps for postage must be presented by the customer to an employee at a retail counter at a Post Office location. - **What are the valid methods to prepay postage?** **Postage can be prepaid with:** - Postage stamps (provided the item weighs 10 oz or less and 1/2-inch in thickness or less) - Postage meter imprint - PC Postage® products - Online [Click-N-Ship®](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=GSS&appURL=https://cns.usps.com/labelInformation.shtml) labels with postage (Pickup can be scheduled in the Click-N-Ship application) - Prepaid Priority Mail® Flat Rate envelope - Return Items – like Parcel Return Service – are prepaid - **Does the letter carrier have to verify postage?** **No.** The letter carrier is not required to verify postage, however, offices will engage in standard revenue protection practices if packages appear to be underpaid. **[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top)** **Scheduling a pickup** - **Can I request pickup on a holiday or weekend?** You can submit your request on a holiday or weekend. However, the pickup will occur on the next scheduled delivery day. For example, requests made on Saturday afternoons will be accepted for Monday pickup, unless Monday is a postal holiday. - **How far in advance can I schedule a pickup?** You can schedule your Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand up to 6 months in advance. Your request can be extended as long as your future dates are within a 6 month time frame. - **When I scheduled a pickup, why was my address modified from what I entered?** Addresses are compared to the current database of the United States Postal Service® and arranged in a standard format. This format helps us process and deliver your mail more efficiently. Although no system can be up-to-date 100% of the time, this system is very accurate and is updated regularly to include new delivery addresses. - **Why is my login required for future and repeat requests?** Your login is required so you can store and maintain your schedule information. - **Can I arrange for a scheduled pickup (daily, weekly, monthly)?** **Yes**. You can sign in to request a pickup for any number of days during the next year. - **How can I extend my scheduled Package Pickup request?** You can extend your scheduled Package Pickup request by logging into the Package Pickup website. You will be able to extend your pickup up to 6 months from the current date. - **What if the average package volume or weight fluctuates in my pickup?** If you have a scheduled pickup request, you can login to modify your average package volume and weight. Once you login, select the edit link, and follow the screen prompts to modify the package volume or weight. - **What if my package volume or weight increases for a specific date in my pickup request?** If it is the same day as the scheduled pickup, you must call your local Post Office™. If it is before the scheduled day of pickup, you can login to edit the package details for a specific date. Once you login, select the edit link and the specific date that you would like to modify. You will need to follow the screen prompts to modify the package volume or weight. To edit a next day pickup, select the edit cancel button at [Package Pickup](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm). **[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top)** **Where do I leave item(s) for pickup?** When you request a pickup, you will be required to select the package location. Possible locations may include: In/At Mailbox, On the Porch, Front Door, Back Door, Side Door, Knock on Door/Ring Bell, Mail Room, Office, Reception, Other. **Notes:**  Depending on the area, some additional restrictions may apply: - For residents of multi-story buildings, please bring your package(s) to a ground level, secured location for pickup. - Letter Carriers may use their discretion when picking up packages. - Hazards such as snow, ice, obstructed pathways and [dogs](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-can-cause-my-mail-not-to-be-delivered) may prevent the letter carrier from collecting packages. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top) **Confirmation Notices** - **How do I know if my mailpiece was successfully picked up?** You will receive a confirmation email or if no email address is provided, your letter carrier will leave a pickup confirmation notice at the pickup location after the pickup has occurred. The email or notice will confirm that the mailpiece was successfully picked up. If the mailpiece was not collected, the notice will detail why. Contact your local [Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) if you do not receive a pickup confirmation. - **Do I need to keep my confirmation notice?** The email or notice simply confirming that your mail was picked up. However, if you purchased any confirmation service(s) you will want to retain the confirmation notice as you would a receipt. **Can I modify or cancel my pickup request?** Note: You must modify or cancel before 5:00 am local time the day of the scheduled pickup. - **How do I modify a scheduled pickup?** 1. Navigate to the Schedule a Pickup application under "Quick Tools" or "Send" 2. Click "Modify an Existing Pickup" 3. Enter your confirmation number and email address or phone number 4. Click "Edit Pickup" 5. Edit the information necessary for modification. You can modify the pickup date, location, number of packages and total weight. 6. Check the agreement box 7. Click "Save" 8. A confirmation page will be displayed with the update and confirmation number. 9. An email will be sent to you with the updated change confirmation - **How do I cancel a scheduled pickup?** 1. Navigate to the Schedule a Pickup application under "Quick Tools" or "Send" 2. Click "Modify an Existing Pickup" 3. Enter your confirmation number and email address or phone number 4. Click "Edit Pickup" 5. Scroll down and click "Cancel this Scheduled Pickup" 6. Click "Yes" to confirm you want to cancel the pickup 7. A confirmation page will be displayed with the cancellation and confirmation number 8. An email will be sent to you with the cancellation confirmation - **How do I modify a scheduled pickup from my Activity History/Account?** - **Option 1** 1. Sign in to your account 2. Click "Activity History" from the left navigation 3. Find the last scheduled pickup of your request from the list of previous orders and click "View Details" 4. Click "Edit" and the system will take you to the Schedule a Pickup page with your scheduled pickup(s) for that request. 5. Select the pickup(s) you wish to change and select "Edit Pickups" 6. Edit the information necessary for modification. You can modify the pickup date, location, number of packages and total weight. 7. Check the agreement box 8. Click "Save" 9. A confirmation page will be displayed with the update and confirmation number(s). Your confirmation number(s) will also be located in the details of your Activity History - **Option 2** 1. Sign in to your account 2. Click "Package Pickup Requests" from the left navigation 3. Select the desired pickup location from the dropdown list 4. Select the pickup request you wish modify and click "View Details" 5. Select the pickup(s) you wish to change and select "Edit Pickups" 6. Edit the information necessary for modification. You can modify the pickup date, location, number of packages and total weight. 7. Check the agreement box 8. Click "Save" 9. A confirmation page will be displayed with the update and confirmation number(s). Your confirmation number(s) will also be located in the details of your Activity History - **How do I cancel a scheduled pickup from my Activity History/Account?** - **Option 1** 1. Sign in to your account 2. Click "Activity History" from the left navigation 3. Find the last scheduled pickup of your request from the list of previous orders and click "View Details" 4. Click "Edit" and the system will take you to the Schedule a Pickup page with your scheduled pickup(s) for that request 5. Select the pickup(s) you wish to cancel and click "Cancel Pickups" 6. Click "Yes" to confirm you want to cancel the pickup(s) 7. A confirmation page will be displayed with the cancellation and confirmation number(s). Your confirmation number(s) will also be located in the details of your Activity History - **Option 2** 1. Sign in to your account 2. Click "Package Pickup Requests" from the left navigation 3. Select the desired pickup location from the dropdown list 4. Select the pickup request you wish to cancel and click "View Details" 5. Select the pickup(s) you wish to cancel and click "Cancel Pickups" 6. Click "Yes" to confirm you want to cancel the pickup(s) 7. A confirmation page will be displayed with the cancellation and confirmation number(s). Your confirmation number(s) will also be located in the details of your Activity History. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top) **Additional Information** - **Can I track or confirm the status of delivery for picked up mailpieces online?** **Yes.** Delivery status is available for Priority Mail Express® service items or packages that include USPS Tracking® or Signature Confirmation™ services. Once your mailpieces are entered into our system, you can visit [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®. - **Can I remove a package from my scheduled pickup list?** **Yes.** Go to the pickup webpage ( [https://www.usps.com/pickup](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm)) and follow the prompts. After signing in, you will see a list of your scheduled Package Pickups and you have the ability to EDIT the specific information for each pickup. You can also CANCEL or ADD a scheduled pickup. - **How do I find the fees for Pickup on Demand?** Fees are listed in [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198), under Domestic – Other Services and Fees. See also [What Forms of Payment are Accepted?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted). - **Do I need to be present for pickup?** No, as long as you are shipping postage-paid items within the United States that have been properly prepared for pickup and left in a safe location for the letter carrier. - **For questions about your Package Pickup or Pickup on Demand request, (including confirmation notices not received) contact your local** **[Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action)** **facility.** [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand® URL Name What-is-Package-Pickup [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Pickup Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGtGAM/pickup-services) ## Related Articles - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Outgoing Mail Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Outgoing-Mail-Pickup) - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand®

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000015IbAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) ## Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail ## Guidelines and restrictions for Military Mail and Diplomatic Mail and how to submit inquiries for late mail. - Mar 4, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008054 Customer Information **For helpful information and additional resources for sending military and diplomatic mail, including pricing, shipping restrictions and customs forms, please see [https://www.usps.com/ship/apo-fpo-dpo.htm?pov=international](https://www.usps.com/ship/apo-fpo-dpo.htm?pov=international).** | | | | --- | --- | | [**Military APO/FPO/DPO Mail Transit Times**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#military_mail_transit_times) | [**Tracking & **Additional Information****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#additional_information) | | [****Delayed Military Mailpiece Information****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#delayed_military_mailpiece_information) | [**Extra Services**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#extra_services) | Military APO/FPO/DPO Mail has unique transportation and handling requirements; it is transported outside of the United States to overseas locations, including remote and war-zone locations. Normal delivery time for Military Mail varies depending on mail class, destination and military activity; please take that into consideration. Scheduled delivery times are the same for mailing to and from an APO/FPO/DPO. | Military APO/FPO/DPO Mail Transit Times | | --- | | **Europe/Atlantic**
APO/FPO/DPO AE ZIP™ Codes beginning with 090-092 and 094-099 | Express Mail® Military Service | 3 Days | | First-Class Mail®
Priority Mail® | 7-9 Days | | Parcel Select [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface)
USPS Ground Advantage® [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface) | 30-45 Days | | **Iraq/Afghanistan/Middle East**
APO/FPO/DPO AE ZIP™ Codes beginning with 093 | Express Mail® Military Service | N/A | | First-Class Mail®
Priority Mail® | 7-13 Days | | Parcel Select [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface)
USPS Ground Advantage® [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface) | 20-24 Days | | **Japan/Korea/Pacific Islands/Far East**
APO/FPO/DPO AP ZIP™ Codes beginning with 962-966 | Express Mail® Military Service | 3 Days | | First-Class Mail®
Priority Mail® | 7-9 Days | | Parcel Select [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface)
USPS Ground Advantage® [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface) | 30-45 Days | | **Central America/South America/Caribbean**
APO/FPO/DPO AP ZIP™ Codes beginning with 340 | Express Mail® Military Service | N/A | | First-Class Mail®
Priority Mail® | 7-9 Days | | Parcel Select [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface)
USPS Ground Advantage® [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface) | 18-21 Days | | **Africa** | Express Mail® Military Service | N/A | | First-Class Mail®
Priority Mail® | 15-18 Days | | Parcel Select [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface)
USPS Ground Advantage® [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#surface) | N/A | | **For more information on Military Mail transit times, see** [**Publication 640**](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub640.pdf). Normal delivery time for Military Mail varies depending on mail class, destination and military activity; please take that into consideration.
\* This class of mail may receive surface transportation by ship to certain locations. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#top) **Delayed Military Mailpiece Information** Transit times will vary depending on operational conditions and the unit of the addressee. Those in established bases should continue to receive regular service, while those in forward areas or engaged in operations may experience longer arrival times due to logistical constraints. When it appears that the difficulty has occurred after the mail has left the jurisdiction of the United States Postal Service® (for example: if outside the USPS delivery standards for the item based on the [chart above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#military_mail_transit_times), refer to [Delayed mail and packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages)), USPS may refer the matter to the Military Postal Service Agency. MPSA is the Department of Defense liaison agency with the Postal Service that has overall responsibility for postal operations at military locations overseas (including items scanned as delivered when the customer states the item was not received). | | | --- | | **For additional information and assistance with your Military Mail item:**
1. **Track your item [here](http://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)**
2. **If sufficient time has passed** [**based on Military Mail Transit Times**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#military_mail_transit_times), click [here](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) to contact the United States Postal Service by email
3. Click [here](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm) to file a domestic insurance claim (only if the item was insured; APO/FPO mail is considered domestic)
4. Click [here](https://www.usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm) to submit a Missing Mail Search Request **if you have completed the first 3 steps, or have been advised to by a USPS representative** | **Extra Services** In general, articles mailed to Military Post Offices (MPOs) are eligible for extra services, but extra services may not be available to all MPO's, refer to [What Domestic Mail Extra Services are Available?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available) MPOs include Army Post Offices (APOs), Fleet Post Offices (FPOs), and Diplomatic Post Offices (DPOs). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail URL Name Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail [Military & Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFtGAM/military-diplomatic-mail) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? [Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Collection-Box# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Collection-Box?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FWhat-is-a-Collection-Box) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq000000157JAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Collection-Box) ## What is a Collection Box? ## Broad, basics-like information on USPS Collection Boxes. - Mar 3, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007468 Customer Information Collection Boxes are mail receptacles where customers can deposit outgoing mail. USPS® collection boxes are typically blue, freestanding units, located at post offices and other strategic locations. See below for a list of types and locations of mail collection boxes. **Types of Collection Boxes** | | | | --- | --- | | **Name** | **Description** | | **Snorkel Collection Box Receptacle** | A United States Postal Service® collection box that is placed at the curbside and is fitted with a chute to receive mail deposited by motorists. This is also referred to as a courtesy box or motorist mail chute. | | **Priority Mail Express® Collection Box Receptacle** | A specially marked white collection box receptacle for the public deposit of properly prepared and prepaid Priority Mail Express items. | | **Mail chute** | A glass-fronted tube with a mail slot on each floor of a tall building. Letters are dropped for collection through the chute into a box on the ground floor. | | **Lobby Drop or Wall Drop** | A slot or opening where customers deposit mail. | | **Package Drop Units (PDUs)** | A new form of collection receptacle that the Postal Service™ has installed to better assist business/volume mailer with their shipping needs using prepaid postage. The PDUs are only for acceptance of packages meeting proper restrictions and for which the postage has been prepaid using one of the following: Click-N-Ship® service, PC Postage, Postage Meter, USPS Returns® service, and Parcel Return Service. | | **Standard Collection Box Receptacle (Blue Box)** | A blue painted street box with the USPS® logo used by the public to deposit mail. | | **Cluster Box** | A centralized unit of more than eight individually locked compartments sized to accommodate the delivery of magazines, merchandise sample and several days accumulation of mail. In addition, collection mail may be deposited in a designated compartment. | | **Rack** | Rack collection boxes are cooperative mailing racks that may be installed in office buildings. | | **Firm** | Generally in High Rise buildings - businesses i.e., Sears Tower, John Hancock bldg. | | **Hub / Depots** | Not physical boxes but points on a collection route | | **Receiving Box** | Business type, not available to the public. | | **Other** | A pick up/scan that is not available to the public. | **What can be mailed in a collection box?** Because of transportation security regulations, not all mail and packages may be placed in United States Postal Service® collection box receptacles. - Mail deposited outside of the guidelines below will be returned to sender. - Competitor items (including FedEx and UPS) placed in USPS collection boxes will be picked up by that competitor at their convenience. - Service guarantees or commitments for Priority Mail Express items will not take effect until the item has been picked up from the collection box and receives a valid USPS acceptance/mail processing scan event. **The following exhibit explains additional restrictions on deposit of certain types of mail in collection boxes.** | | | | --- | --- | | **COLLECTION BOX CONTENTS** | | _**The following mail items are allowed in a USPS Collection Box**_ | _**The following mail items are NOT allowed in a USPS Collection Box**_ | | Domestic mail (including Priority Mail Express®) weighing 10 oz. or less and measuring 1/2-inch in thickness or less (including items with online postage) | Domestic stamped mail (including Priority Mail Express®) weighing over 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness | | Domestic mail (including Priority Mail Express®) with metered postage or PC Postage | | | Domestic Business Reply Mail (BRM) – ALL | | | Priority Mail Express International® and Priority Mail International items that were prepared (completed shipping label; customs declaration form generated and data transmitted electronically; and postage paid) online. | Any international mail with a customs declaration that was not completed and submitted online, OR international mail requiring a customs declaration that bears postage stamps. | | International mail (stamped or metered) – Meeting all of the following:
1. Weighs 10 oz. or less
2. Is 1/2-inch in thickness or less
3. Does not require a customs form | International stamped or metered mail weighing over 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness. | | Priority Mail Express International® items weighing 16 oz. or less, paid with online postage or postage meters or information-based indicia (IBI), and bearing an electronically generated PS Form 2976-B with data transmitted electronically to USPS. | Priority Mail Express International® items that don't have PS Form 2976-B. | | PrePaid Priority Mail Flat-Rate Envelope (with postage printed directly on the envelope) | | | Official mail\* (penalty by a government agency\*\*) except for mail requiring a customs form\*\*\* | | | Free Matter for the Blind – ALL | | | APO/FPO/DPO mail weighing 10 oz. or less and measuring 1/2-inch in thickness bearing only postage stamps and not requiring a customs form
**Note**:  Priority Mail Express to APO/FPO/DPO must bear a PS Form 2976-B, regardless of the weight, value, or contents | APO/FPO/DPO mail weighing over 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness and bearing only postage stamps as postage | | APO/FPO/DPO mail paid with methods other than postage stamps, weighing less than 16 oz. and not requiring a customs form
**Note:** Priority Mail Express to APO/FPO/DPO must bear a PS Form 2976-B, regardless of the weight, value, or contents | | **Notes:** - \*The term penalty mail refers to official mail, sent by U.S. government agencies, relating solely to the business of the U.S. government, which is authorized by law to be carried in the mail without prepayment of postage. - \\*\\* Government Agency = Federal, State, or Local government agency. - \*\*\*All APO/FPO/DPO and international mail 16 oz or over must have customs forms affixed. For mail less than 16 oz. see [Individual Country Listing](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_001.htm) for customs form requirements. - [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics) has further information on where mailpieces can be deposited based on the methods of customs declaration. - Mail deposited incorrectly will be returned to sender. - All mailpieces that are not acceptable for deposit in a collection box must be taken to the Post Office for processing. **Can Packages with Online Shipping Labels be Deposited in a Collection Box?** A mailpiece may be deposited in a Collection Box with online Shipping Labels with PC Postage® service affixed if it meets the following criteria: - Items with postage meter strips can be deposited in collection boxes when: - The correct postage, fees, and labels are affixed - Return information is on the mail piece - Items weighing 10 oz. or less and measuring 1/2-inch in thickness or less with postage stamps can be deposited in collection boxes when: - The correct postage, fees, and labels are affixed - Return information is on the mailpiece **Note:**  For APO/FPO/DPO and international requirements see the exhibit on the previous page. Mailpieces bearing only postage stamps for postage and either weighing more than 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness must be brought to an employee at a retail service counter in a Post Office. **Can Mail with Signature Confirmation or USPS Tracking® Be Deposited in a Collection Box?** **To use a Collection Box for a mailpiece with Signature Confirmation or USPS Tracking, it must meet the following:** - If the postage for the item is from a postage meter strip (including an online Shipping Label with PC Postage service), the item must: - Have the correct postage and fees affixed - Include the return information on the mailpiece - If the postage for the item is from postage stamps, the item must: - Weigh 10 oz or less - Be 1/2-inch in thickness or less - Have the correct postage and fees affixed - Include the return information on the mailpiece **Note:**  Mailpieces bearing only postage stamps for postage and either weighing more than 10 oz or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness must be brought to an employee at a retail counter in a Post Office. **How to find collection box pickup times** - Collection, or pickup, times for the collection box are posted on the label. Please note that Pickup Times may vary depending on the day of the week. Occasional changes in transportation may result in adjustments to collection schedules. And in some cases, such as the day before a holiday, normal collection times may not apply. Note that mailpieces are not necessarily postmarked on the day they are collected. If you want to ensure that your mailpiece receives a postmark and that the postmark date aligns with the date of mailing, go to any USPS retail location during retail service hours and ask for a manual postmark at the counter. It will be applied free of charge. - QR (Quick Response) Codes are also on Collection Times label. Scan these QR Codes with a mobile device to open [USPS Mobile®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile) and find the nearest Post Office location with the address, hours of operations, and distance. - To search for alternate collection boxes and drop off locations click [here](https://tools.usps.com/locations/?_gl=1*1fqdsm7*_up*MQ..*_gs*MQ..*_ga*MTYwNzY1MDU0LjE3Mzc4MTMwNTI.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3NTY0OTgzMDYkbzE1MSRnMCR0MTc1NjQ5ODMwNiRqNjAkbDAkaDA.&gclid=EAIaIQobChMI0PrgiuqwjwMVFQutBh3JXiLWEAAYASAAEgIAOPD_BwE&gclsrc=aw.ds&gbraid=0AAAAADpMVX-AiSho599etifp8srV1FT8r). | | | | --- | --- | | **Collection Times Label for a Standard Collection Box** | ![Label titled United States Postal Service Collection Times. A QR Code is at the center of the label with the message "Scan here" above it. Space is available on the label to insert times for "Monday-Friday" and "Saturday". Space is also for the location of the "Last collection in this area is at:" and the times for "Monday-Friday" and Saturday". Primary messaging on the label is the following: "To find the nearest Post Office™ location with the address, hours of operations, and distance, scan the QR Code. To report QR Code issues, go to usps.com. On the day before a holiday, normal collection times may not apply. Check with your local postmaster to verify collection times."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/collection_box_label_lab66-QR.jpg) | | **Collection Times Label for a Priority Mail Express Collection Box** | ![Label titled United States Postal Service Priority Mail Express Collection Times. A QR Code is at the center of the label with the message "Scan here" above it. Space is available on the label to insert times for "Monday-Friday" and "Saturday". Space is also for the location of the "Last collection in this area is at:" and the times for "Monday-Friday" and Saturday". Primary messaging on the label is the following: "e will deliver Priority Mail Express items dropped in this box within 1 or 2 delivery days. Delivery days depend on the date and time you drop the item and the destination ZIP Code™. To find the nearest Post Office™ location with the address, hours of operations, and distance, scan the QR Code. To report QR Code issues, go to usps.com."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/collection_box_label_lab67-QR.jpg) | **Can Flyers be placed on Collection Boxes?** No. USPS® Collection Boxes are the property of the United States Postal Service®. Customers are not allowed to affix anything to them, including fliers, signs about missing animals, and advertisements. **Collection Box Installation and Removal** - The number of street collection boxes installed, their location, and the frequency of collection service depend primarily on the mailing patterns and the volume of mail generated by the individual community. As these and other local conditions change, collection service is modified and adapted accordingly. For decisions on specific collection boxes, Post Offices™ will apply the applicable Postal Operations Manual guidance. - The Postal Service reviews collection box density every year on a routine basis to identify redundant/seldom used collection boxes as First-Class Mail volume continues to decline. Based on the density testing, boxes are identified for potential removal and notices are placed on boxes to give customers an opportunity to comment before the removal decision is made. This process is one of the many ways the Postal Service makes adjustments to our infrastructure to match our resources to declining mail volumes. - If you have any questions or concerns regarding collection boxes, please contact the postmaster or manager of your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm). ## USPS APIs

## Information about USPS APIs for developers, how to contact us for support, mapping spreadsheets to migrate Web Tools, developer guides, and more.      - Mar 3, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008359

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
|  | The Web Tools API platform was retired on **January 25th, 2026**. Web Tools API integrators **must** **migrate**to the new USPS APIs cloud-based environment. |

**What is an API?**

An API, or Application Programming Interface, is a set of rules that allow different software applications to communicate with each other. An API acts as a go-between, or messenger, enabling one piece of software to request services or data from another without needing to understand the internal workings of that other system. APIs provide a way for programmers to interact with and use the functionality of other applications within their own code.

The USPS API cloud-based environment is designed to provide you with the highest quality service and the most advanced technologies. USPS APIs provide:

- **Improved performance and stability** \- Cloud infrastructure offers better uptime and optimized processing speeds.
- **Scalability and flexibility** \- Easily scale API usage as your business grows.
- **Reduced maintenance and overhead** \- No need for on-prem hardware maintenance, reducing costs and complexity.
- **Enhanced security and compliance** \- Cloud-based security ensures adherence to USPS standards.

**Getting Started**

Please see our **[Developer Portal](https://developers.usps.com/)**to learn more about USPS APIs, including the API catalog, information on [Getting Started](https://developers.usps.com/getting-started) and FAQs.

|     |
| --- |
| **Need additional assistance?** Please email us at [https://emailus.usps.com/s/usps-APIs](https://emailus.usps.com/s/usps-APIs) for additional support. |

• | **The Postal Service™ does not fulfill requests for mail-in ballots. If your state does not automatically send a ballot to all registered voters, you will need to contact your state and local election officials to request a ballot.** If you opt to vote through the U.S. Mail®, make sure you're familiar with your local jurisdiction's requirements. Contact your local election officials if you have questions. **Requesting Ballots** - Some jurisdictions, including states with mostly all-mail elections, generally do not require eligible voters to request a ballot in order to receive one. - In jurisdictions that require eligible voters to request a ballot in order to receive one through the mail, we recommend that domestic, nonmilitary voters request their ballot as early as their jurisdiction allows. **Mailing Completed Ballots** You should always check to make sure you understand your jurisdiction's requirements and recommendations on mailing your completed ballot. States and other jurisdictions establish the rules and deadlines for submitting ballots. In most jurisdictions, mail-in ballots must be **received on or before Election Day**. Some jurisdictions will accept ballots as timely if they were **mailed by Election Day** and **received within a specified number of days later**. For domestic, non-military voters who choose to use the mail to return their completed ballot: - The Postal Service recommends mailing your completed ballot before Election Day, and at least 1 week before the deadline by which their completed ballot must be received by their local election office. - Some jurisdictions may recommend allowing even more time for mailing completed ballots. **Find answers to your questions about voter registration and other information about voting in your area:** | | | | --- | --- | | - **[Find My State or Local Election Office Website - USAGov](https://www.usa.gov/election-office)**
- [Absentee and Early Voting - USAGov](https://www.usa.gov/absentee-voting)
- [Vote.org](https://www.vote.org/)
- [Department of State - Absentee Voting Information for U.S. Citizens Abroad](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/international-travel/while-abroad/voting.html)
- [National Voter Registration Forms](https://www.eac.gov/voters/national-mail-voter-registration-form) | - [Election Assistance Commission](http://www.eac.gov/)
- [Election Center](https://www.electioncenter.org/)
- [Federal Voting Assistance Program](https://www.fvap.gov/)
- [National Association of State Election Directors](http://www.nased.org/)
- [National Association of Secretaries of State](http://www.nass.org/) | **For additional information and FAQs for voters, click [https://usps.com/votinginfo](https://usps.com/votinginfo)** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[When should I mail my voted ballot?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#when_to_mail)** | **[When is my ballot due?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#ballot_due)** | [**How much postage do I need to mail my ballot?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#postage_needed_to_mail_ballot) | | **[Is my ballot safe in the mail?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#safety)** | **[Does my mail-in ballot need a postmark, and how do I get one?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#doesballotneedpostmark)** | **[Ballots for Members of the Uniformed Services, Overseas Voters, and their Families](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#ballots_members_uniformed_overseas)** | | **[Election Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail)** | **[Preparing and Sending Political Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail)** | **[Election Mail - YouTube](https://youtu.be/K3ZGwxQFZXA?si=xhb7C1-uCDqgI9NL "A discussion with the Postal Service’s Executive Director for Election and Government Mail Services, Adrienne Marshall. This is brought to you by \"Mailin’ It!\", the Official USPS Podcast.")** | ## **When should I mail my voted ballot?** If you are eligible to vote by mail, you should plan ahead to give yourself enough time to complete and return your ballot by your [state’s deadline](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#ballot_due). For domestic, nonmilitary voters who choose to use the mail to return a completed ballot, we recommend that you mail your completed ballot _before_ Election Day, and at least one week before the deadline by which your completed ballot must be received by your local election office. Some states may recommend allowing even more time for mailing completed ballots. You should always check to make sure you understand your state's requirements and recommendations on mailing your completed ballot. You can look for information about your state's requirements and recommendations here: [Find My State or Local Election Office Website \| USAGov](https://www.usa.gov/election-office). | | | --- | | Note that mailpieces are typically postmarked by machines at processing facilities. Some mailpieces may not reach those processing machines and may not receive a postmark on the same day they are collected by the carrier or dropped off at a retail unit. This may occur either as part of the regular process or due to unexpected circumstances. If you want to ensure that your mailpiece receives a postmark and that the postmark date aligns with the date of mailing, bring it to a USPS retail location and ask for a manual postmark at the counter. The postmark will be applied free of charge. | ## **When is my ballot due?** Ballot return deadlines vary on a state-by-state basis. Please contact your local election official or you can look for information about your state's requirements and recommendations here: [Find My State or Local Election Office Website \| USAGov](https://www.usa.gov/election-office). ## **How much postage do I need to mail my ballot?** If you are a domestic, nonmilitary voter, you should make sure appropriate postage is affixed to your return ballot envelope unless your state or local election officials provide you with a prepaid return envelope. To help voters, the Postal Service requires election officials to inform voters of the amount of First-Class postage required to return their ballots if the voter decides to return their completed ballot by mail. If a return ballot is entered into the mailstream with insufficient or unpaid postage, it is the Postal Service's policy not to delay the delivery of completed absentee balloting materials, including mail-in ballots. We are proactively working with state and local election officials on mailing requirements, including postage payment. In cases where a ballot enters the mailstream without the proper postage, the Postal Service will deliver the ballot and thereafter attempt to collect postage from the appropriate Board of Elections. | | | --- | | See [Election Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail) for additional information, including a video, on how USPS works with state and local election officials. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#top) **Is my ballot safe in the mail?** Yes. The Postal Service has a long and proud history of supporting our country's electoral system and we take seriously our clear and defined role to process, transport, and deliver the nation's Election Mail, including ballots. The U.S. Mail remains a safe, secure, efficient and effective means for citizens and campaigns to participate in the electoral process, when policymakers choose to use the mail as part of their election systems or when voters choose to use our services to participate in an election. Additionally, the United States Postal Inspection Service® (USPIS®), as the federal law enforcement and security arm of the Postal Service, is responsible for defending the nation's mail system from illegal or dangerous use. The Postal Inspection Service continues to employ its technical capabilities and specialized personnel to protect the critical infrastructure of our processing and distribution networks, as well as the employees who will be delivering to voters across the country. Utilizing proven fraud detection and loss prevention strategies, Postal Inspectors will continue to actively protect and defend the nation’s mail system. In doing so, the Postal Inspection Service will be working closely with our law enforcement partners, including the Department of Justice (DOJ), the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the United States Postal Service Office of the Inspector General (USPS OIG), and local and state law enforcement to respond to and investigate election crimes involving the U.S. Mail. ## **Does my mail-in ballot need a postmark, and how do I get one?** Each state establishes the rules and requirements for ballot submission, submission deadlines and the role, if any, that postmarks play in determining whether a ballot was timely. You can look for information about your state's requirements here: [https://www.usa.gov/state-election-office](https://www.usa.gov/state-election-office). If you are a nonmilitary voter located in the United States and you choose to use the mail to return your completed ballot, our general recommendation is that as a common sense measure, you mail your completed ballot before Election Day, and at least one week prior to the deadline by which your ballot must be received by your election office. Some states may recommend allowing even more time for mailing completed ballots. In the normal course of operations, the Postal Service does not postmark every piece of mail in the system. However, the Postal Service tries to ensure that every return ballot mailed by voters receives a postmark, whether the return ballot is mailed with postage pre-paid by elections officials or with a stamp affixed by the voter. Also, while our postmarking practices have not changed, we have made adjustments to our transportation operations that will result in some mailpieces not arriving at our originating processing facilities on the same day that they are mailed. Because postmarks are generally applied at those processing facilities, this means that the date on the postmarks applied at those facilities may not necessarily match the date of mailing. **You can ensure that your ballot-return envelope receives a postmark and that the postmark date aligns with the date of mailing by bringing your mailpiece to the counter at a Postal Service retail location and requesting a manual postmark, which will be applied free of charge upon accepting custody of your mailpiece.** | | | --- | | **Our message to voters is to plan and mail early.**
The Postal service recommends that voters mail their completed ballot before election day, and at least one week before the deadline by which their completed ballot must be received by their election official. Some states may recommend allowing even more time for mailing completed ballots. | **If you wish to ensure that a postmark aligning with the date of mailing is applied to your return ballot**, you can visit a Postal Service retail office and request a manual postmark from a retail associate when dropping off the ballot. Be sure to check collection times posted on collection boxes and at Post Office™ locations and retail facilities. Also be mindful of when mail is delivered and picked up at your residential mailbox. Note that depositing mailpieces in collection boxes or your residential mailbox does not ensure that it will be postmarked on the same date. Mailpieces are typically postmarked by machines at processing facilities. Certain mailpieces may not reach those processing machines, and thus will not receive a postmark on the same day they are collected by the carrier or dropped off at a retail unit. This may occur either as part of the regular process or due to unexpected circumstances. If a customer wants to ensure that a mailpiece receives a postmark that aligns with the date on which the Postal Service first accepted possession, the customer may take the mailpiece to a Post Office, station, or branch and request a manual (local) postmark at the retail counter when tendering their mailpiece. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#top) ## **Ballots for Members of the Uniformed Services, Overseas Voters, and their Families** **When can balloting materials be sent free of postage for members of the uniformed service, their families, and overseas voters?** Balloting materials may be sent through the mail without prepayment of postage to enable individuals in the categories below to apply for registration and vote by absentee ballot (in elections for Federal office) when absent from the place of voting residence and otherwise eligible to vote as an absentee: - Members of the Armed Forces in active service and their spouses and dependents | | | --- | | The Armed Forces includes the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, and the commissioned corps of the Public Health Service and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. | - Members of the U.S. Merchant Marine and their spouses and dependents - U.S. citizens residing outside the United States **Are there requirements for the deposit of mailpieces prepared as free absentee ballots for qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families?** Yes. Balloting materials, including return absentee ballots, to or from the individuals identified above must be deposited at the following locations to be mailable without the prepayment of postage: - U.S. Post Offices - Army and Air Force Post Offices / Fleet Post Offices (APO/FPO) - Diplomatic Post Offices (DPO) - American Embassies or American Consulates - If balloting materials are deposited at other locations, postage must be paid. Envelopes containing these balloting materials must have the appropriate indicia to deposit without prepayment of postage: - The words "Official Absentee Balloting Material — First-Class Mail" (or similar wording required by state law) must be printed across the face of the envelope in a rectangular box. - Immediately below, the words "No Postage Necessary in the U.S. Mail — DMM 703.8.0" must be printed. - In the upper right corner of the envelope, in a rectangular box, the words "U.S. Postage Paid, 39 USC 3406" must be printed. - Lastly, an appropriate inscription or blank spaces for the return address of the sender must be shown in the upper left corner. - For more information, please see [_Domestic Mail Manual_ (DMM) 703.8.0](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#a_8_8). Another option is available for absent overseas uniformed services voters returning their absentee ballots. They may use Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD with Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA)-authorized APO/FPO/DPO addresses to mail from military APO or FPO locations, DPO locations, or Department of State (DOS) locations without the prepayment of postage. The label includes “Waiver of Signature” and “Guaranteed by End of Day” endorsements so ballots can be delivered the day they arrive at the destination Post Office. See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Voting-by-Mail#priority_mail_express_label_11-DOD) and [_Domestic Mail Manual_ (DMM) 703.8.9](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#a_8_9) for more information on Label 11-DOD. All other types of ballots and balloting materials that do not meet the above requirements must bear postage using a permit imprint, postage meter, adhesive stamp, or other acceptable method of postage payment prior to mailing. **When I see the indicia that reads, "U.S. Postage Paid, 39 USC 3406," does that mean the mailpiece only has 39-cent postage?** No. That statement refers to Section 3406 of Title 39 of the United States Code, which is a federal statute requiring the Postal Service to carry certain balloting materials covered under the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA) free of postage. These mailpieces are not short-paid, and no additional postage is needed. **Can absentee ballots for state or local elections be mailed free?** No. The postage-free mailings for absentee balloting materials for uniformed services and overseas voters apply only to elections for Federal office. This includes any general election of electors for President and Vice President, or of senators and representatives in Congress, and other general, primary, and special elections. Combined Federal and non-Federal balloting materials also may be sent postage free under this rule. **Where can I find more information about military and overseas voting by qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families?** The [Federal Voting Assistance Voting Program (FVAP) (www.fvap.gov)](http://www.fvap.gov/) is dedicated to providing voting assistance for uniformed service members, their families and citizens living outside the United States. Contact the FVAP by visiting their website at [www.fvap.gov](https://www.fvap.gov/) or calling 1-800-438-VOTE (8683). **Where can I find the latest information for handling absentee ballots addressed to military and overseas absentee ballots?** The latest information can be found in the "Military & Overseas" tab on [www.usps.com/electionmail](https://www.usps.com/electionmail). **Where can I find more information about balloting material for uniformed services and overseas voters?** More information about the standards applicable to absentee balloting materials for uniformed services and overseas voters can be found in [DMM Section 703.8.0](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#ep1174014) or can be found by contacting your local Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA). **Can my absent overseas and uniformed services ballot be tracked?** | | | | --- | --- | | Yes, absent overseas uniformed services voters may return absentee ballots from overseas APO/FPO military locations, DPO locations, or Department of State (DOS) locations by using Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD (see image to the right) which provides the ability to track the absentee ballots all the way to delivery. Items with Label 11-DOD must be scanned for tracking and accounting purposes. | ![Image of a sample envelope addressed to a non-existent Registrar of Voters. Above this sample address is "OFFICIAL ABSENTEE VOTER BALLOT (To be Opened only by Election Canvassing Board)". The Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD is affixed across the top right corner. The label contains a barcode with a tracking number below it. The label states "ONLY FOR USE WITH ABSENTEE BALLOTS FROM THE MILITARY POSTAL SYSTEM". The label indicates that: "Postage Paid for by the Department of Defense", that there is a "WAIVER OF SIGNATURE", and that item delivery is "Guaranteed By End of Day".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/election_mail/label_11_dod_absentee.jpg)_Image of a sample envelope addressed to a non-existent Registrar of Voters._
_Priority Mail Express Label 11-DOD is affixed across the top right._ | **Are there special procedures in place to handle APO/FPO/DPO absentee ballots?** Yes, special procedures for those ballots are in effect between September 1, 2024, and November 30, 2024. The U.S. Postal Service and Military Postal Service Agency have joined forces to expedite the delivery of absentee ballots to/from overseas qualifying uniformed services voters, overseas voters, and their families. | | | --- | | If you are eligible to vote by mail and choose to do so, you should plan ahead to give yourself enough time to complete and return your ballot by your state’s deadlines. You can look for information about your state’s requirements and recommendations here:
- [Federal Voting Assistance Program: Find your state election website](https://www.usa.gov/election-office) | Details of other [special procedures](http://about.usps.com/kits/kit600.pdf#page=7 "Special Procedures: APO/FPO/DPO Absentee Ballots") are available. ## What is PC Postage®?

## PC Postage allows customers to print labels and postage from their computer.      - Feb 28, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007264

Customer Information

**PC Postage® (Information Based Indicia) Description**

Vendors for PC Postage (or Information-Based Indicia) such as [Stamps.com](http://www.stamps.com/welcome/), [Endicia.com](http://www.endicia.com/), [EasyPost](https://www.easypost.com/ "The Simple Shipping API - EasyPost"), and [Pitney Bowes](http://www.pitneybowes.com/us/shipping/software-and-systems/sendpro-online.html) are postage solutions for customers who send high volumes of mail and/or packages. Customers can print labels and postage from their computer. In most cases, customers will pay a monthly fee to use this service.

- [Endicia.com](http://endicia.com/) and [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/) offer customers the ability to print postage-paid labels for any single-piece (letters, envelopes, packages) of domestic or international mail class with address verification, discounted USPS Tracking® and Signature Confirmation™ as well as insurance. For certain classes of mail, postage amounts are hidden on the labels.

- Pitney Bowes offers customers the ability to print package, large envelopes and envelope postage from their computer.

PC Postage does not require a postmark as the postmark information (city, state, ZIP Code and date) is already included within PC Postage.

**Note**: Regardless whether postage is paid online or not, a customer must have the ability to add additional postage if necessary by using stamps, meter, etc.

**What is Custom Postage?**

Custom Postage is postage that has a customer supplied image on the label. Customers upload images to the custom postage provider, and the provider in return creates the postage and sends it back to the customer by mail

**Who should use PC Postage®?**

PC Postage is designed for customers who need a more robust solution than Click-N-Ship®. It's designed for those who send both letters/flats and packages and want fast, easy access to postage. Typical users are home based businesses and individuals who want the convenience of printing postage from their office.

- Ship higher mail volumes
- Ship multiple packages frequently
- Ship letters & packages
- PC Postage products and services are currently authorized for use on all mail services, with the exception of First Class Package Service Commercial parcels, periodicals, and Bound Printed Matter.

**Eligible services include:**

- Domestic First-Class Mail® items
- Priority Mail® items
- Priority Mail Express® items
- USPS Ground Advantage® items
- International Mail and Parcels
- Presorted First-Class Mail®
- Extra Services (such as USPS Tracking® and Certified Mail® services)
- Customers may often use free shipping supplies, Package Pickup, USPS Tracking Services, and Track and Trace.
- Customers who ship USPS Marketing Mail® will want to consider a meter or permit imprint instead

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-PC-Postag-ePostage#Back_to_Top)

**Benefits**

Available additional features include printing variable prices, integration with scales and related online applications, and postage expense reports.

- Limit trips to the Post Office™
- Ability to print mail and package labels from one location
- Online Price Incentives for Expedited and International labels
- Personalized postage

**Price Incentives**

- Priority Mail Express – Save up to 5% off retail prices on all Priority Mail Express shipments
- Priority Mail – Save up to 11% off retail prices on all Priority Mail shipments, depending on weight and distance
- International Shipping – Save up to 5% on Priority Mail International shipments and up to 8% for Priority Mail Express International (does not include Flat Rate items)
- First-Class Mail® Letters – Savings available when entered at retail as [Metered Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail)

**Note:** These benefits are common to PC Postage but should be verified with your PC Postage vendor.

**Payment**

You can pay for postage using a credit card. If using a commercial PC Postage® product, it is also suggested that you set up an Automated Clearing House account as a direct debit account with your bank.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-PC-Postag-ePostage#Back_to_Top)
