Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000yiXAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) ## Domestic Claims - The Basics ## To learn about, or file, an insurance claim for loss or damage of domestic items. - Jan 17, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006089 Customer Information To learn about, or file a domestic claim for loss or damage, please visit [File a Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm) or select the links below. If you think your package or mail is lost or your mailpiece is missing contents, please visit [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) for more information. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Who can file a claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Who_may_file_domestic_claim)** | **[How to file a claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#How_to_file_claim)** | **[When to file a claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#timeframe_to_file_claim)** | | **[Saving an online claim](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#save_online_claim)** | **[Claims and payment questions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#claim_payment_questions)** | **[Postage and insurance reimbursement](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#postage_insurance_reimbursement)** | | **[Proof of value](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#What_is_Proof_of_Value)** | **[Proof of damage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#what_is_proof_of_damage)** | **[Proof of insurance](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#What_is_Proof_of_Insurance)** | | **[How do I appeal a denied claim?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Appeal_Denied_Claim)** | **[Web browser issues](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Web_Browser_Issues)** | **[What is covered if insurance is not purchased?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#whatiscovered)** | | **[How are some products covered by insurance?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#productscovered)** | [**Eligible Items**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Eligible) | [**Ineligible Items**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Ineligible) | **Who can file a domestic claim?** Either the person sending the mailpiece ( **Mailer**) or the person receiving the mailpiece ( **Recipient** or **Addressee**) may file a claim for a damaged or missing article. **_Notes:_** - If the Mailer files an insurance claim **_for a damaged article_** still in the possession of the Recipient, the Recipient is required to retain the damaged article and container (including packaging, wrapping, and any other contents received) until the claim is fully resolved. **Upon written request by the Postal Service™**, the Recipient must turn the materials over to a Post Office™ for inspection, retention, and disposition in accordance with the claim's decision. - If the Mailer and the Addressee both claim insurance, any payment due is made to the party who submits the claim first and whose claim is approved for payment. **How to file a claim?** There are two (2) options to file a domestic insurance claim: | **Options for Filing Domestic Insurance Claims** | | --- | | **Online** | **By Mail** | | **You may [File a Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm) online, which is the preferred method.** Follow steps 1-3 under "How to File a Claim" to make sure you have everything you need before you get started.
**Proof of value and proof of insurance is required.**
**Notes:**
- You can add up to 10 items as long as they're included in the same tracking number.
- Uploading proof / files

**How do I upload files?**



Click the "Upload Images/Documents" link and then select the applicable file. File formats accepted are .gif, .jpg, .pdf or .png and must be 2MB or less.



- **Note**: "Documents" may be the text to click and no pointer may appear over "Upload Images".

**What kind of files do I upload?**
You may include files that show proof of value. If your item was lost or completely damaged, the files should provide full proof of value. If your item is to be repaired, the files should include an estimate for repair costs. You can also upload files that show proof of insurance and, for damage claims, evidence of damage.
**Can I upload multiple files as Proof of Insurance, Proof of Value or Proof of Damage?**
Yes. Clicking "Upload Images/Documents" again after uploading a file as Proof brings up a new window allowing you to select a new file for uploading.
**To receive electronic claim status notifications:**
- File your claim online.
- Select the checkbox associated with the electronic claim status notification method to opt-in to that communication method.
- **Notes**:

- You can opt out at any time by navigating to the Claim History screen.
- You will still receive mailed correspondence regardless of the electronic claim status notification decision. | If you're unable to file a claim online, you may have a Domestic Claim form mailed to you.
1. Call the USPS National Materials Customer Service center during their hours of operation to request a Domestic Claim form.
2. Complete the form and mail it, along with proof of value and proof of insurance, to the address on the form.
**National Materials Customer Service** 1-800-332-0317
**Hours of Operation**
Monday - Friday 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM Central Time
Saturday/Sunday - **CLOSED**
Federal Holidays - **CLOSED** | ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **When to file a claim?** **File claims as follows:** 1. _Damaged or Missing Contents:_ customers should file a claim immediately but must file no later than 60 days from the date of mailing. 2. _Lost Articles:_ customers must file a claim within the time limits in the chart below. | MAIL TYPE OR SERVICE | WHEN TO FILE (FROM MAILING DATE) | | --- | --- | | No Sooner Than | No Later Than | | Priority Mail Express® | 7 days | 60 days | | Priority Mail Express COD | 15 days | 60 days | | Registered Mail® | 15 days | 60 days | | Registered COD | 15 days | 60 days | | Priority Mail® | 15 days\* | 60 days | | USPS Ground Advantage® | 15 days\* | 60 days | | Insured Mail | 15 days\* | 60 days | | COD | 15 days | 60 days | | APO/FPO Priority Mail Express Military® Service | 21 days | 180 days | | APO/FPO/DPO Insured Mail and Registered Mail (Priority Mail, First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage - Retail®, SAM, or PAL) | 45 days | 1 year | | APO/FPO/DPO Insured Mail (Surface only) | 75 days | 1 year | | \*Bulk Claims submitted via the Business Customer Gateway (BCG) should be filed no sooner than 15 days. | _Source DMM 609.1.4_ **Note**: A decision regarding most claims is usually communicated within 5-10 days. Claims requiring in-depth processing, such as high claim amounts, can take up to 30 days. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **How to save an online claim before submitting** If you are not ready to submit your online claim, or are not finished with it yet, you can click the "Save Claim" button which can be found at the bottom of each page. This will save the data you entered and will not need to be reentered. - To access your saved claims, go to [https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm). - Sign in using your account name and password. - Select the "Saved Claims" link. - To access an individual claim, you may search using the claim nickname provided when placing the claim, or the tracking number. Saved claims will be deleted after 3 days, if not submitted. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **Claim and payment questions** **All required claim documents** must be completed and submitted. A decision regarding your claim is usually communicated within 5-10 days (Claims requiring in-depth processing, such as high claim amounts, can take up to 30 days). Allow 7-10 business days to receive approved claims payments in the mail. - **If your** **claim was filed online,** you may check the status by [opting in to receive status notifications](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#electronic_claim_status_notifications) or [signing in to your usps.com account](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=OIC&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fonlineclaims.usps.com%2FOICWeb%2F). The following is needed when checking the status of your claim online. - [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® log-in credentials - Claims nickname provided when placing the claim or tracking number. - **To check the status of a mailed claim, a payment that was mailed more than 10 business days ago, or for questions about a received payment,** **Please contact the Accounting Help Desk:** - Email [helpdeskaccounting-st.louis@usps.gov](mailto:helpdeskaccounting-st.louismo@usps.gov?subject=Domestic%20Claim). - Call **1-866-974-2733** - You will need to specify if you are calling about a "Domestic Claim" or "International Claim" - Hours of operation are Monday - Friday, from 8:00am to 8:00pm Eastern Time (7:00am to 7:00pm Central Time). **Provide the following required information:** - Your name - Tracking number (For more information, see [What is proof of insurance?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#What_is_Proof_of_Insurance)) - Original mail date **For more information about what happens after you file a claim**, review the section titled, "After You File - Next Steps" at [https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm). **Postage and insurance reimbursement** Postage reimbursements will be included with the claim payment if the item was lost or not delivered. Insurance fees cover the cost of the insurance and are not reimbursed. **Lost item recovered after a claim has been paid, and reimbursing USPS** **®** If a lost Registered Mail®, insured, Collect on Delivery, or Priority Mail Express® article is recovered after payment of a claim, the payee may accept the article and reimburse the USPS for the full amount paid if the article is undamaged. If the article is damaged, has depreciated, or has missing contents, the payee may accept it and reimburse the USPS in an amount set by the Consumer Advocate, USPS Headquarters. To begin the process of reimbursing payment: 1. **Please contact the Accounting Help Desk:** - Email [helpdeskaccounting-st.louis@usps.gov](mailto:helpdeskaccounting-st.louismo@usps.gov?subject=Domestic%20Claim). - Call **1-866-974-2733** - **You will need to specify if you are calling about a "Domestic Claim" or "International Claim"** - Hours of operation are Monday - Friday, from 8:00am to 8:00pm Eastern Time (7:00am to 7:00pm Central Time) 2. **Provide the following required information:** - Your name - Tracking number (For more information, see [What is proof of insurance?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#What_is_Proof_of_Insurance)) - Original mail date 3. **Follow the guidance that is provided by the Accounting Help Desk:** - Useful addresses to know for: | | | | --- | --- | | **Mailing reimbursements to USPS:** | **Questions and concerns regarding recovered articles thought to be damaged, depreciated or missing content**: | | Eagan Disbursing Accounting Services
2825 Lone Oak Parkway
Eagan, MN 55121-9640 | Consumer Advocate
U.S. Postal Service
475 L'Enfant Plaza SW
Washington, DC 20260-2200 | 4. **Note:** Customers should keep a photo copy of the check and letter for their records. **What is proof of insurance?** Proof of insurance includes: - The original mailing receipt issued at the time of mailing - The outer packaging showing the names and addresses of the sender and the addressee and the proper label showing that the article was sent insured - A printed electronic online label record or a computer printout from the application used to print the label and purchase the insurance ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **What is proof of damage?** Photos that clearly show the extent of damage will help with your case. For damaged claims, you'll also need to provide an estimate of the repair costs from a reputable dealer. If you received something damaged, please hang onto the original packaging and the damaged item until your claim is settled. You may be asked to take them to your local Post Office™ for inspection later. Please do not reship the package. If a claim is filed because some or all of the contents are missing or damaged, the addressee must retain the mailing container, including any damaged articles, all packaging, and any contents received. Upon written request by the USPS®, the addressee must make this proof available to the local Post Office® for inspection, retention, and disposition in accordance with the claims decision. Failure to do so will result in denial of the claim. The Postal Service employee completes **PS Form 3831, _Receipt for Article(s) Damaged in Mails_** and it is provided to the customer. The Post Office retains the item until the claims process is completed and determines full, partial, or denied payment and whether to dispose of or return the damaged item. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **What is proof of value?** Proof of value proves an item's value or any required repair costs. Either the mailer or the addressee must submit acceptable proof to establish the cost or value of the article at the time it was mailed. Proof of value should be submitted electronically or attached to the claim form; otherwise, the claim cannot be processed. Other proof may be requested to help determine an accurate value. Examples are: 1. A sales receipt, paid invoice or bill of sale, or statement of value from a reputable dealer. 2. Paid repair bills; if the claim is for partial damage, estimates of repair costs or appraisals from a reputable dealer. Repair costs may not exceed the original purchase price. 3. Receipt or invoice for the costs incurred to buy a surety bond required to reissue a lost item. 4. Receipt or invoice of costs incurred for the reconstruction of non-negotiable documents. 5. A copy of a credit card statement or other documentation indicating the amount paid. 6. For Internet transactions conducted through a Web-based payment network that offers payment services through a stored value account, provide a computer printout of the online transaction identifying the purchaser and seller, price paid, date of transaction, description of item purchased, and assurance that the transaction status is completed. The printout must clearly identify the Web-based payment network provider through which the Internet transaction was conducted. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **How do I appeal a denied domestic claim?** In your appeal, please explain why your case should be reviewed and provide any supporting documentation that can help your case. Make sure that proof of value, evidence of mailing and insurance coverage for the article is submitted with your appeal. | | | | --- | --- | | **How do I appeal a denied domestic claim?** | | **Online** | **By Mail** | | For a claim filed online, you may file an **online appeal** **within 30 days** of the date of the original decision by signing in to your online [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® [account](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=OIC&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fonlineclaims.usps.com%2FOICWeb%2Fclaims_history) then:
1. Go to Claim History
2. Select the claim to appeal (if more than one)
3. Click Submit an Appeal | For a claim filed by mail, you may file a **written appeal within 30 days** of the date of the original decision to the following address:
DOMESTIC CLAIMS APPEALS
ACCOUNTING SERVICES
US POSTAL SERVICES
PO BOX 80141
ST. LOUIS, MO 63180-0141 | ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **If my claim is denied a second time, what is the next step?** If your claim is denied a second time, you may file a final appeal to the Consumer Advocate within 30 days of the date of the second denial. If filed online, you may submit online. In your appeal, please explain why your case should be reviewed. Also, make sure that proof of value, evidence of mailing and insurance coverage for the article has been provided. If you did not file online, you may mail your final appeal to the following address: CONSUMER ADVOCATE DOMESTIC CLAIMS APPEALS 475 L'ENFANT PLAZA SW WASHINGTON, DC 20260-2200 - For more details on payable claims, see the [Domestic Mail Manual 609](http://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/609.htm#1097212). **Web browser issues** If you are having trouble submitting your online claim, please try clearing your browser cache, history, cookies or ensuring encryption. You may also visit [USPS.com Website Support - Browser and Download Issues](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues) for detailed steps. If after following these steps and you are still experiencing issues, please [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/) and click " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" as the reason for your email. Include your name, a brief description of the issue and steps taken to resolve, and an agent will respond via email. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **What is covered if insurance is not purchased at the time of mailing?** If insurance is not purchased at the time of mailing, the United States Postal Service® is unable to honor any requests to be compensated for lost, missing, and/or damaged item(s). The Postal Service™ is not held liable for damage which occurs during the processing or handling of mail matter under Title 28, Section 2680(b) of the U.S. Code, except for Priority Mail Express®, Priority Mail®, Registered Mail®, Insured, or Collect on Delivery (COD). The USPS® liability is restricted to lost, damaged, and/or missing content claims for the following products: - Insured Mail (includes any mail class purchased with Insurance, i.e. USPS Ground Advantage® or Priority Mail®) - Registered Mail - COD - Priority Mail Express® (at any value) The liability amount is limited to no more than the insurance value stated and paid for at the time of mailing. Claims without a mailing receipt can be filed, but payment may be limited to $100 for Insured Mail, Registered Mail, and Priority Mail Express®, $50 for COD Mail, and up to $100 for Priority Mail (dependent on payment method). **Notes**: - The USPS is not legally authorized to compensate for loss or damage of uninsured articles. ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **How are some products covered by insurance?** - **Airline Tickets:** You may only file for the price of filing a Lost Ticket Report with the airlines, not the full price of the lost ticket. It is your responsibility to file a Lost Ticket Report with the appropriate airline. - **Gift cards:** Indemnity is **not** paid for gift cards and similar items like lottery tickets, sweepstakes tickets, and contest entries. - **Adult birds:**If the number of birds per parcel follows the container manufacturer limits and each bird weighs more than 6 ounces, eligible birds are mail able via Priority Mail Express™ in biologically secure containers approved by the manager of Mailing Standards. Under the applicable standards, indemnity claims may only be paid for lost, damaged, or missing contents (not for death of the birds in transit if there is no visible damage to the mailing container). - Eligible birds are disease-free adult birds that weigh no more than 25 pounds. Mailers must also comply with [all applicable governmental laws and regulations and state, municipal, and local ordinances, and USPS guidelines](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#1064912) [.](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#1064912) - **Money (currency / cash):**Money that is sent through the mail can be insured up to its value, within the stated limits: - Except for Registered Mail, the maximum indemnity for negotiable items (defined as instruments that can be converted to cash without resort to forgery – such as money orders), currency, or bullion, is $15.00 - For Registered Mail items, including money, the maximum insured value is $50,000 - An item worth more than this amount must still be declared at full value, but the maximum insurable limit is $50,000. Ask your local Post Office for additional information (including pricing) for items valued above $50,000. - **Important:** Checks (including cashier's checks) are **not** considered cash and cannot be insured other than for document reconstruction costs. [Coverage for document reconstruction](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics#Eligible) is available for items mailed in Priority Mail Express mailpieces. - **Collectibles:**Collectibles can be insured based on the sales receipt, invoice, bill of sale, or statement of value from a reputable dealer. - When a claim is filed as cash, the amount of indemnity is only $15.00. - Please refer to section [609.4 of the Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/609.htm#1097212) or ask your local Post Office if you have additional questions about insuring collectibles. For additional information on the payment of claims, go to [section 609 of the DMM](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/609.htm#1097212). ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **ELIGIBLE Items** - _**Priority Mail Express®:**_ - Up to $100 insurance coverage included in price of postage with the following limits: - $100 for merchandise - $100 for document reconstruction - $15 for cash - Additional insurance coverage may be purchased for up to $5,000 for merchandise only - _**Priority Mail® and USPS Ground Advantage®:**_ - Up to $100 insurance coverage included in price of postage - Insurance coverage is for merchandise only - Additional insurance coverage may be purchased for up to $5,000 for merchandise only - **_First-Class Mail®_** _**:**_ available for merchandise only (i.e. you cannot insure correspondence). - Insured mail travels at the speed of the mail class it is purchased with - USPS Marketing Mail™ _(bulk insurance only)_ - _Package Services (Library Mail, Media Mail®, and Bound Printed Matter) and Parcel Select® pieces_. - _Official government mail endorsed "Postage and Fees Paid"_ **Please Note:** Insured mail must be mailed from a physical Post Office™ location unless purchased online ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) **INELIGIBLE Items** - Parcels containing matter offered for sale, addressed to prospective purchasers who have not ordered or authorized their sending. If such matter is mailed, payment is not made for loss, missing contents, or damage. - Coverage is not provided for consequential losses, delay, spoilage of perishable items or prohibited articles. - Articles so fragile that they cannot be carried safely in the mail regardless of packaging. - Articles not adequately prepared to withstand normal handling in the mail. As a rule, any mailable package should be insurable. - Mail not bearing the complete names and addresses of the sender and addressee. - USPS Marketing Mail letters, flats, and non-flat machinables. - First-Class Mail® and Priority Mail correspondence. - Gift cards and similar items like lottery tickets, sweepstakes tickets, and contest entries. **Additional Services** - When an item is insured for more $500, the following services can be purchased: - Insurance Restricted Delivery (instead of Insurance, alone) - Return Receipt - Electronic Return Receipt - Adult Signature Required and Adult Signature Restricted Delivery are available for insured Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail, and Parcel Select Ground. - USPS Tracking® - Hold for Pickup - Signature Confirmation™ (for items insured for $500 or less) - Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery (for items insured for $500 or more) **Payment** - The fee and postage may be paid with postage stamps, meter stamps, or permit imprints. **Things to Keep in Mind When Purchasing Insurance:** - Because NO record is kept by the mailing Post Office facility, the sender needs to keep the receipt for making a claim. - Insurance may be purchased for perishable items. However, the claim will **not** be paid if the item is spoiled. - Postage (but not the insurance fee) may be refunded in a claim if the insured article is lost. - Cash: When a claim is filed, money is replaced at the insured value - After proof of value is shown (if the actual value was less than the insured amount, then **only** the actual value is paid) and - After the claim process is completed - **Important:** Customers sending a significant amount of cash should verify with the local office what is considered valid proof of value **before** mailing the item. Customers should also verify that it is packaged securely enough to protect a high value item from being harmed during regular mail processing. - More information on [insuring cash and checks](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Limits-for-Insuring-Cash-and-Checks) is available ![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Domestic Claims - The Basics URL Name Domestic-Claims-The-Basics [Missing, Late Or Damaged Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFqGAM/missing-late-or-damaged-mail-packages)[Claims](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGHGA2/claims) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Domestic Claims - The Basics

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FInformed-Delivery-The-Basics) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000yCHAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) ## Informed Delivery® - The Basics ## Informed Delivery® is a free and optional notification feature that gives residential, business and eligible PO Box™ consumers the ability to digitally preview their letter-sized mail and manage their packages scheduled to arrive soon. - Jan 8, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007106 Customer Information | | | --- | | **Click the arrows or topics below to learn more or visit [Informed Delivery®](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action).**
For help with Informed Delivery Multifactor Authentication, please see [Multifactor Authentication (MFA)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Multifactor-Authentication-MFA) | **Overview** **What is Informed Delivery®?** Informed Delivery is a free and optional online notification feature that gives residential, business and eligible PO Box™ consumers the ability to digitally preview their letter-sized mail and manage their packages scheduled to arrive soon. The United States Postal Service® (USPS) digitally images the front of letter-sized mail that runs through our automated mail sorting equipment. USPS uses those images to provide digital notifications in advance of the delivery of physical mail. **Who is eligible for Informed Delivery®?** Informed Delivery is available to most residential and PO Box™ addresses, and many business addresses. Visit [informeddelivery.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) to check if your address is eligible and sign up. | | | --- | | **Note:** In rare instances, you may live in an eligible ZIP Code location, but can't sign up because your individual mailbox is not uniquely coded; this coding determines which images are provided to each Informed Delivery user and ensures that users can only view mail destined for their address. While most addresses are uniquely coded, not all are, especially in high density areas (e.g., apartment buildings or condos). If your address is not uniquely coded, you will not be able to sign up for Informed Delivery. 
Visit [informeddelivery.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) to sign up and check if your address is eligible. | **What will Informed Delivery show me?** **Incoming Mailpieces:** You will receive daily emails to the email address associated with your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account containing images of the address side of up to 10 pieces of incoming letter mail. You can also view these images on your Informed Delivery online dashboard, where there is no limit on the number of images that will be shown. | | | --- | | **Note:** Some mailpieces (e.g., catalogs or magazines) are not imaged by our automated equipment and will not appear in your Informed Delivery email or dashboard. If a scan is detected on these types of mailpieces, you will see a message stating, "There is one or more mailpieces for which we do not currently have an image that is included in today's mail." | **Incoming and Outbound Packages:** You will also receive status updates for packages arriving at your address and packages you have sent at the bottom of your Informed Delivery Daily Digest email and within your Informed Delivery dashboard. **Informed Delivery Interactive Content:** Mailers and shippers can supplement their physical mailpiece or package with a custom image and call-to-action known as an interactive campaign. From your Daily Digest email or dashboard, you can click the image (color image below the grayscale image) or the "Learn More" button to access the mailer or shipper's digital content. **H****ow are my Informed Delivery notifications delivered?** Informed Delivery notifications are delivered via Daily Digest email, on your online Informed Delivery dashboard, and on the USPS Informed Delivery® Mobile application. Informed Delivery can only be sent to one email address per account. However, multiple accounts can have Informed Delivery for the same address. | | | | --- | --- | | **Notification Source** | **Description** | | **Email** | - Check your email opt-in settings to make sure email notifications are turned on.
- You receive emails to the email address in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account containing images of the address side of up to 10 pieces of incoming letter mail that is arriving soon. The email also provides you the status of incoming and outbound packages.
- Emails are sent once daily, typically before 9:00AM local time, Monday through Saturday. Notifications are not sent on days when there is no mail to be delivered, on Sundays, or federal holidays. | | **Online** | - Access your [Informed Delivery online dashboard](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) to track and manage incoming mail and packages.
- From your dashboard, you can update your Informed Delivery settings, including your Daily Digest email preferences, email address, SMS (text) tracking notifications, and enroll in USPS Electronic Signature Online®. | | **Mobile App** | - Manage your incoming mail and packages while on the go with the USPS Informed Delivery Mobile App ( [Apple App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/id6478885776) \| [Google Play Store](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.usps.id45833)).
- The app features all the core Informed Delivery features along with expanded functionality, mobile capabilities, and an intuitive interface. | | **To change or opt out of any Informed Delivery notification:**
- **Sign in to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account**
- **Click on "Informed Delivery"**
- **Click on "Settings"**
- **Toggle on or off Informed Delivery desired email notifications and/or package tracking notifications** | **Signing Up / Welcome Letter** **How do I sign up for Informed Delivery®?** **To sign up for Informed Delivery with a new account:** 1. **Visit** [https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome](https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome) 2. **Create your account:** Select "Sign Up for Free" 3. **Verify your identity:** Protect your privacy and personal information by verifying your identity and address 4. **Watch for updates:** Look for mail and package updates via email, the online dashboard, or the USPS Informed Delivery® Mobile app **Existing Personal Account Customers:** 1. **Visit** [https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome](https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome) 2. Select "Sign In" 3. Enter your email address, then enter your username and password 4. The system will verify your mailing address is eligible for Informed Delivery and will guide you through the process of enrolling in Informed Delivery 5. If you are unable to verify your identity online, the system will present options to "Request Code by Mail" or "Verify at Post Office" via in-person verification at a local Post Office 6. Click on "Informed Delivery" 7. Select the checkbox to opt-in for Informed Delivery and agree to the Terms and Conditions 8. If your identity cannot be verified online, the system will automatically present options to either "Request Invitation Code by Mail" or "Verify at Post Office" via in-person verification at a local Post Office **Request Code:** 1. Click "Request Code by Mail" 2. Check the box "I acknowledge that I warrant and represent that I am eligible to receive mail at this address indicated in my profile" 3. Click "Request Code" 4. You should receive your code in the mail within 3-7 postal business days - Once the code is received, sign into [https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome](https://reg.usps.com/informeddelivery/welcome). The system will determine a code was requested and will provide a form for entering the code. An alternate method is to sign into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) personal account and go to Account Preferences > Informed Delivery > "Click here if you have already received an invitation code" and enter your code. - Code expires 90 days from the date of issue or after 5 successful uses. **Verify at Post Office:** 1. Click "Find Post Office" 2. You will be presented with a list of nearby participating Post Offices, or you can search for a Post Office by ZIP Code™ 3. Select "Continue to In-Person Verification". You will be redirected to a page that contains detailed instructions and receive an email with further instructions **Existing Business Account Customers:** 1. Sign into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) business account 2. From your account's Preferences page, Click on "Informed Delivery" 3. Select the checkbox to opt-in to Informed Delivery and agree to the Terms and Conditions 4. Request and Informed Delivery Verification Code by checking the box next to disclaimer, "By checking this box and accepting, I acknowledge that I warrant and represent that I am eligible to receive mail at this address indicated in my profile." 5. Your code will be received by mail within 3-7 postal business days 6. When the code is received sign into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) business account, go to your account's Preferences page and under Informed Delivery enter the code to finalize the verification process. Any profile updates for your account will invalidate the code. You will have to request a new code via mail after completing all profile updates for your account. 7. The codes expires 30 days after the date of issue, after 1 successful use, or if the associated [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) profile address is edited. **What if I can't verify my identity online?** If you are unable to verify your identity online, the system will provide options to Request a Code by Mail or Verify at a Post Office. If you want to verify your identity in person at a USPS retail location, please see above section titled ["Verify at Post Office"](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics#verifyatpostoffice). Bring all required documentation and forms of [acceptable forms of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification?r=343&ui-knowledge-components-aura-actions.KnowledgeArticleVersionCreateDraftFromOnlineAction.createDraftFromOnlineArticle=1) to the facility you select. If you do not follow the steps above immediately after failing the identity verification process, for security purposes you will need to wait 72 hours before you may attempt online identity verification again. _**Note:** Most post office retail locations offer in-person identity proofing. In-person Identity Proofing will not be offered if the closest post office is more than 50 miles away from the address on the account._ **Welcome Letter** A Welcome Letter is sent as a part of our mail-based verification of new Informed Delivery accounts. After signing up for Informed Delivery® you will receive a [Welcome Letter](http://www.usps.com/c360/images/informed_delivery/SCR3-4_Page%202.pdf) in the mail. - If you recognize the account information listed in the letter, **_no further action is required_**. If the account was not created by you (or someone in your household), or you do not recognize the masked username or email address, **_deactivate it by following the instructions in the letter_** - Visit https://reg.usps.com/idremove. - Enter the alphanumeric unsubscribe code provided in the letter. The code is case-sensitive and must be entered exactly as it appears in the letter. - Select a reason for unsubscribing in the drop-down menu. - Click the "Submit" button. - Upon your submission of this code, the account associated with the unsubscribe code is unenrolled from Informed Delivery and access is disabled. - The unsubscribe code expires 90 days after the date of issue, after successful use or if the associated [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) profile address is edited. **Dashboard** Your [Informed Delivery® dashboard](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action) displays serves as your central hub for Informed Delivery. It automatically displays mail images for a **seven-day period**, and package information for **15 days after each package has been delivered**. **Labels manually added to the dashboard are displayed for 30 days**. View scan history, leave delivery instructions, or schedule a redelivery. Outbound packages can only be viewed on the dashboard by manually inputting the tracking number. There is no limit to the number of packages that appear on your dashboard. You can also use the Informed Delivery dashboard to **update your Informed Delivery settings. From the settings page:** - Update your Daily Digest email preferences and email address - Update your SMS (text) tracking notifications and mobile phone number - Update package tracking notifications - Enroll in [USPS Electronic Signature Online®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online) To explore the features of the dashboard, select the " **_Packages_**" tab to see a list of your incoming and delivered packages. Then select the **_blue arrow on the right side of the package entry_** to: - View the tracking scan history - Leave [USPS Delivery Instructions®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) if the package is eligible - Schedule a redelivery if the package is eligible - Add a nickname to the package - Remove the package from the dashboard If you wish to add additional packages to your dashboard that are not currently displayed, click "Add USPS Tracking® Number or Traceable Indicia ID+" and enter the tracking number. **Daily Digest & Mail Delivery Notifications / Issues Receiving Notifications** **Daily Digest Notifications** As an Informed Delivery user, you can opt-in to receive daily emails to the email address in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account containing images of the address side of up to 10 pieces of incoming letter mail as well as the status of incoming and outbound packages. While only the first 10 pieces of mail appear in your Daily Digest email, the remaining mail images can be viewed on your dashboard for a 7-day period. The Daily Digest email will not always show all packages destined to your address that day (e.g., items scanned into our system after the email has been sent). Outbound packages will only display on your Informed Delivery email if your account return address is included on the package and the package is within 3 days of its expected delivery date. There is a limit of 20 outbound packages that can be displayed on your email each day. **Mail Delivery Notifications** The Mail Delivery Notification feature sends an email notification alerting you that your daily letter mail has been delivered to your mailbox. These notifications rely on data from our letter carrier's scanning devices and are sent within approximately 30 minutes of your mail being delivered. These emails are separate from your Informed Delivery Daily Digest emails, and will not appear in your Informed Delivery dashboard. To opt in to receive Mail Delivery Notification emails, or to manage other Informed Delivery notifications, log into your Informed Delivery account and go to "Settings". You can opt out at any time in Informed Delivery settings, or by clicking on the unsubscribe link at the bottom of the Mail Delivery Notification email. If your Mail Delivery Notification is not accurate, you may complete the survey found at the bottom of the email. You may also email [USPSInformedDeliveryQuestions@usps.gov](mailto:USPSInformedDeliveryQuestions@usps.gov). Please note the following: - Notifications are not available for PO Box addresses - APO/FPO/DPO ZIP Codes are not eligible for MDN. - Notifications do not guarantee that a specific piece of mail seen in your Daily Digest was delivered that day - Notifications can only be sent by email - Notifications will only be sent if you have mail content in your Daily Digest email; mail could still be delivered to your home even if you did not receive a Daily Digest email or have any mail displayed on your Informed Delivery dashboard that day. **Issues Receiving Notifications** | | | | --- | --- | | **Issue** | **Solution** | | I did not receive mail that was in today's Daily Digest. | Not all mail seen in the Daily Digest will be received the same day.
If you have additional questions, please see the Privacy & Security Concerns section below. | | My mail is on Hold. | If you have submitted a [USPS Hold Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics?r=343&ui-knowledge-components-aura-actions.KnowledgeArticleVersionCreateDraftFromOnlineAction.createDraftFromOnlineArticle=1) request:
- **Your physical mail will be held**
- You will still receive Informed Delivery notifications
- You will still receive Mail Delivery notifications | | I'm not receiving email notifications. | You should begin to receive notifications within three business days, but in rare instances it can take up to 7 days for activation to complete. - Check your email spam filter settings and your deleted items or junk email folders. You should also check the email address in your USPS.com profile for accuracy | | | --- | | **Note:** We recommend adding USPSInformeddelivery@email.informeddelivery.usps.com to email contacts as a trusted source to help prevent your email spam filter from blocking Informed Delivery email digests. | - Verify that the email address in your USPS.com profile is accurate - Verify that you have chosen to opt-in for Daily Digest Email notifications - Login to your account at [informeddelivery.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) - From your dashboard, go to "Settings" - Select "Yes" to turn on Informed Delivery email notifications - If you would like email and/or text notifications about your incoming packages, enter your mobile phone number in the "Settings" tab on your dashboard under the Package Notification Options section | | Some of the mail I receive do not show up in my Informed Delivery® notification. | - Only mail processed on USPS automated equipment is included in the Informed Delivery Notification. Not all mail (e.g., magazines and catalogues) is processed this way, so they cannot appear in the notifications.
- There are also instances where automated equipment is moved or shut down temporarily for maintenance. In some rare cases, pieces of mail may overlap when the image is taken. Any of these issues may result in images not matching the actual mail delivered that day. | | I can't log into my dashboard. | - You could encounter this issue if you signed up for Informed Delivery through an Official Internet Change of Address (COA), or if a USPS employee helped sign you up using a mobile enrollment app. During initial sign up, you should have received an email from us. It contains a one-time link that can be used to complete the sign up process (complete the account registration process by selecting a username and password). Completing these steps will grant you full access to your Informed Delivery dashboard.
- If the link in the email has expired or if you can no longer locate the email, you must create a new account on USPS.com in order to sign up. | | Why am I receiving notifications for my roommate's mail? | Individuals who share a residence and mailbox (e.g., roommates, families, etc.) will receive the images for all mail delivered to that household and notifications for all incoming packages. There is no way to separate your mailpiece images or package notifications from others' if a physical mailbox is shared. | | I updated my account profile and lost Informed Delivery. | If you update your name, email or address on your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) residential or business account profile you will be opted out of Informed Delivery. You will need to opt back in to Informed Delivery and may be required to complete identity Proofing again either online, in-person or via an Invitation Code by mail. Any Invitation Codes requested prior to the account profile update should not be used. You will need to request a new Invitation Code if you choose to use that Identity Proofing method. | **Viewing Package Details / Fraudulent Package Notifications** **Within your Daily Digest Email:** You can view the delivery status of both incoming packages (i.e., packages arriving at your address) and outbound packages (i.e., package you have sent) at the bottom of your Informed Delivery email. the Daily Digest email will not always show all packages destined to your address that day (e.g., items scanned into our system after the email has been sent). Outbound packages will only display on your Informed Delivery email if your account return address is included on the package and the package is within three days of its expected delivery date. **On your Informed Delivery Dashboard:** On the Informed Delivery dashboard, you can select "Packages" to see a list of your incoming and delivered packages. Click on the blue arrow on the right side of the package entry to expand the field and see the scan history, leave delivery instructions, or schedule redelivery. Outbound packages can only be viewed on the dashboard by manually inputting the tracking number. If you wish to add additional packages to your dashboard that are not currently displayed, click "Add USPS Tracking® Number or Traceable Indicia ID+" and enter the tracking number. **Will I see images of packages?** No. Package information will be presented as status updates, not images. **What does the color coding indicate for packages?** Packages are sorted into four categories: | | | | --- | --- | | **Green** | Packages that have been delivered are displayed in green text for 15 days after delivery.[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics#Manually_added_labels) | | **Blue** | Packages currently in transit are displayed in blue text. | | **Gray** | Packages for which USPS cannot yet accurately predict a delivery date are displayed in gray text. This may occur when USPS has received pre-shipment information and is pending determination of a delivery date. | | **Red** | Packages for which action is required are displayed in red text. For instance, a package requiring redelivery appears in red. | **\*Note:** Package labels manually added to the dashboard will remain visible for 30 days. **Package Tracking Emails** Informed Delivery customers are automatically opted-in to email notifications from USPS Tracking® when there is an update on your package's delivery status ("Expected Delivery Updates"). They provide enhanced package visibility and security, keeping you informed on your package's status. You can opt-in to several additional package notifications by going to your Informed Delivery Settings page: - Day of Delivery - Package Delivered - Available for Pickup - Delivery Exception updates - Package In-Transit updates Package tracking email notifications will be sent to the email address used to enroll in Informed Delivery. If you have updated your email address for Informed Delivery Daily Digest emails, this will not automatically update the email address for package tracking notifications. To update the email address for package tracking notifications, you must manually enter the email address within Informed Delivery Settings under "Package Notification Options". To opt out of the automatic tracking emails, log into your Informed Delivery account, go to "Settings" and uncheck the box "Expected Delivery Updates". You can also choose to receive text notifications in addition to or instead of email notifications. **Packages Destined to USPS Smart Lockers** If you have a package coming to you that is destined for delivery to a USPS Smart Locker, you will see the following notification in your Daily Digest email: - The email address associated with the package must match the email address for your Informed Delivery account. - Tracking information beginning when the USPS Smart Locker package is within 3 days of delivery. - Indication that the USPS Smart Locker package has been delivered to a locker. - A one-time use QR Code to open the locker. - The QR Code will be displayed in the Daily Digest until the package is picked up from the locker - The QR Code will expire after 5 calendar days. After 5 days, your package will be removed from the Smart Locker and held at the Post Office retail counter for retrieval during their normal business hours. For additional information about USPS Smart Lockers, please visit  [USPS Smart Locker (uspssmartpackagelockers.com)](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq) **Fraudulent Package Notifications** Fraudulent package alerts provide Informed Delivery customers notification that a package being shipped to you has been determined to have counterfeit or fraudulent postage. These are similar alerts to what is seen on the USPS Tracking page on USPS.com. Please see our FAQ [Counterfeit Postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Counterfeit-Postage-and-Unpaid-Postage) for additional information on items with counterfeit postage. **Change of Address** **How to Change Informed Delivery to a New Address** After you submit an Official Change of Address (online at _[moversguide.usps.com](https://moversguide.usps.com/mgo/disclaimer?referral=MG82),_ in-person at a Post Office™, or through your mail carrier), you will receive an email and a physical letter with a 9-digit alpha-numeric code requesting confirmation of your change of address. If USPS determines that there is a _[usps.com](https://www.usps.com/) ®_ account that corresponds to the information in the change of address submission, access to Informed Delivery® will be temporarily suspended. If your new address is within a participating ZIP Code™ location and the feature is available at your new address, you will be able to resume notification emails once you confirm your change of address by following the instructions in your Change of Address validation letter. **Receiving Notifications for Old Address** It is likely that your [_usps.com_](https://www.usps.com/) ® profile is not updated with your current address. The best way to prevent this issue is to submit an Official Change of Address online at [_moversguide.usps.com_](https://moversguide.usps.com/ "Official Change of Address Form - US Postal Service (USPS)") _,_ in person at a Post Office™, or through your carrier. This will allow you to easily transfer your Informed Delivery® account to your new address if the United States Postal Service® can match your Change of Address and online _[usps.com](http://usps.com/)_ account details. If you have already moved and did not submit an Official Change of Address, please update your address in your [_usps.com_](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=ATG&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Fstore.usps.com%2Fstore%2Fmyaccount%2Fprofile.jsp "usps.com") profile. Once you update your address, the Informed Delivery notifications from your old address will be discontinued. **Informed Delivery Access Suspended** When the Postal Service detects that a permanent Official Change of Address has a corresponding [_usps.com_](https://www.usps.com/) account, access to your Informed Delivery account will be temporarily suspended. Access is suspended to prevent unauthorized access to your mail or package information until your permanent change of address is confirmed. You will be able to easily resume access at the address where you currently reside by following instructions in the change of address confirmation letter(s). **Change of Address Validation Codes** When the Postal Service® detects that an Official Change of Address submission has a corresponding _[usps.com](https://www.usps.com/) ®_ account, access to Informed Delivery for the online account will be temporarily suspended until the change of address is confirmed. Two confirmation letters containing unique validation codes will be mailed to each address listed on your official change of address submission (these are referred to as your "old address" and "new address" on your Official Change of Address form). The letters should be received within 3-5 business days of when the services were suspended. The Official Change of Address process is associated with a physical move of an individual or family, the Postal Service® needs to ensure that you are able to receive mail at the address where you intend to use Informed Delivery®. Email cannot be used to verify your physical residence. - If you have already moved to your new address, visit [_reg.usps.com/restoreaccess_](https://reg.usps.com/restoreaccess) to enter the validation code listed on the physical letter that was mailed to your new address. This will automatically restore your access to Informed Delivery at your new home, if the feature is available at your new address. Only enter the validation code pertaining to the address where you reside, which is where you receive Informed Delivery notifications. If you received a letter at your "old address," and you are not the addressee, you may discard the letter for the address and no action is required. - If **you did not move** and need to restore your Informed Delivery access at your "old address", visit [_reg.usps.com/restoreaccess_](https://reg.usps.com/restoreaccess "reg.usps.com/restoreaccess") to enter the validation code listed on the letter that was mailed to you at your current address. - If you recently submitted an Official Change of Address and have completed your move, but still have access to Informed Delivery, you must edit the profile on your _[usps.com](http://usps.com/)_ account to match your current address. Your access to Informed Delivery will be discontinued at your old address and you will be able to complete identity verification at your new address to restore your access. - Validation codes are active for 60 days from the move date on your Change of Address. Informed Delivery® will remain suspended until one of the validation codes received at the old or new address is entered online. If your validation code expires, you have to go through the identity verification process for your current address on [_reg.usps.com/preferences_](https://reg.usps.com/preferences "USPS.com® - Edit Account Preferences") to restore your access to Informed Delivery, if available at your address. - Only enter the validation code for the address where you reside, which is where you will receive Informed Delivery® notifications. Only use the validation code associated with the old address if you do not actually move or if you did not request an Official Change of Address. - If you received a Change of Address validation code letter at your address that is not addressed to you or anyone in the household, no action is required from you. Your mail information and any online accounts you have with the Postal Service® are safe and have not been affected. **Get a new Change of Address validation code** If you misplaced your validation code or never received it, a second set of validation codes are automatically sent 30 days after the original Change of Address was submitted. A reminder email is sent after 15 days. If you do not receive the second set, you may submit a new Change of Address either online at [moversguide.usps.com](http://moversguide.usps.com/) or in person at a local post office, which will generate a new set of confirmation codes to be mailed to you. **Decided Not to Move** If you submit an Official Change of Address with the intent to move to a new address but do not actually move, enter the validation code in the letter you received at your current address ("old address" from your change of address submission) to restore your access to Informed Delivery®. You can always submit a new Change of Address if you plan to move again in the future. **Redelivery** Redelivery is only available after a failed delivery attempt. You will not see a redelivery option for your item if an initial delivery attempt has not happened. Additionally, once a delivery attempt is marked as final notice, there are set timelines for when the item will be returned to the sender. Your items will be returned to the sender on the date indicated in this set timeline unless you arrange a redelivery or come to pick them up at your local Post Office™ facility before this date. **Change or Cancel a Redelivery Request placed via Informed Delivery®** **To modify your redelivery request through your Activity History:** 1. Sign in to your account. 2. Hover over your name in the top navigation bar and click "My Profile." 3. Click "Activity History" from the left navigation. 4. Find the redelivery from the list of previous orders and click "View Details." 5. Within the "View Details" page, you can see the information on Redelivery and click "Edit" which will take you to the editable Redelivery page. You can modify the article number, type of mail, how and when you want to get your items, packages, and additional instructions. 6. After making updates, scroll to the bottom of the page and click the "Make Changes" button. Then select "Yes" to verify. 7. A confirmation page will be displayed to indicate your request has been updated and a confirmation email will be sent. The update will also be in your "Activity History." **Update Email Addresses & Passwords** | | | | --- | --- | | **Issue** | **Solution** | | How do I update my email address? | Log into your [USPS.com account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=UspsTools&appURL=https://tools.USPS.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) and edit the email address in your profile. | | I forgot my password. | Your Informed Delivery password/login is the same as for USPS.com. To reset your password:
1. Go to [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/ "United States Postal Service Homepage") and select "Register / Sign In" located in the top right-hand corner.
2. Select the "Forgot your password?" link.
3. Enter your username.
4. Select the "Continue" button.
5. Select "Answer secret questions" or "Send temporary password to validated mobile number" (if you have enabled mobile device account recovery) and follow the instructions. | | I forgot my username. | Your Informed Delivery® username is the same as for USPS.com. To recover your username:
1. Go to [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/ "United States Postal Service Homepage") and select "Register / Sign In" located in the top right-hand corner.
2. Select the "Forgot your username?" link.
3. Enter the email address associated with your username.
4. Select the "Continue" button.
5. If your username is eligible for retrieval, you will receive an email containing your username. | | For additional information, please see [USPS.com® Account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics#top) **Privacy & Security Concerns / Missing Mail** **I received a letter saying that someone at my home address signed up for Informed Delivery®.** This letter is sent as a part of our mail-based verification of new Informed Delivery® accounts. **If you or other adult members of your household do not recognize the masked username or email address referenced in the letter, please follow these steps:** 1. Visit [https://reg.usps.com/idremove](https://reg.usps.com/idremove). 2. Enter the alphanumeric unsubscribe code provided in the letter. The code is case-sensitive and must be entered exactly as it appears in the letter. 3. Select a reason for unsubscribing. 4. Click the "Submit" button. **Upon your submission of this code, the Informed Delivery access is disabled.** **How can I be sure that notification emails are from USPS® and not spam?** All emails from the U.S. Postal Service® originate from our mail system and the contents are strictly images of the mailpieces associated with your delivery address or notifications for packages arriving soon. Email messages will be sent from [USPSInformeddelivery@email.informeddelivery.usps.com](mailto:USPSInformeddelivery@email.informeddelivery.usps.com) to the email associated with an Informed Delivery user's _[usps.com](http://usps.com/)_ ® profile. **Informed Delivery Security** The U.S. Postal Service® takes the privacy of customers' mail very seriously and takes measures to ensure that all personal information is protected. Because Informed Delivery gives you increased visibility into both mail and packages being delivered to your address, USPS® follows industry best practices to verify your identity, including the use of a mail-based verification letter. - The scanned mail images are of the external markings, showing only the exterior, address side of letter-sized mailpieces. Package notifications do not include images, only information on the delivery status of the package. - Your mail is protected by the U.S. Postal Inspection Service®, whose sole mandate is to safeguard the entire U.S. Postal Service® system, including the employees who deliver and process the mail and millions of customers who use it. - The U.S. Postal Service adheres to the privacy requirements of the Privacy Act established by the federal government which controls when and how the USPS shares personal information and limits the conditions in which that information can be disclosed externally to outside parties. **Missing Mail / Did not receive a mailpiece shown in today's alert** Informed Delivery_®_ provides notifications for mailpieces arriving soon - not necessarily mailpieces arriving that same day. Occasionally, pieces may be delayed getting to your delivery office; please allow several days, up to one week, after receiving your notification for your mailpiece to arrive. | | | --- | | **What to do if your mailpiece or package has not arrived** | | - **Wait for delivery:** We ask that you allow several days, up to one week, after your Informed Delivery notification before reporting the mailpiece or package as missing.
- **Submit an inquiry:**
- I **f your letter mail** not been delivered after this period, please visit [emailus.usps.com](https://emailus.usps.com/s/) to report the issue. Click on the link under "Where is my mail?" and complete the required steps.
- **If your package** has not been delivered after this period, please visit [emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry](https://emailus.usps.com/s/package-inquiry) to report the issue.
- Please note: While most mailpieces are delivered as expected, some may not reach their destination. Reports of mail loss greatly concern us. Due to the high volume of mail moving through our network each day, it may not be possible to trace individual letter-sized mail to determine what may have happened
- **Contact the sender:** If you still have not received your item, we recommend contacting the sender to request a replacement or redelivery. | | **Persistent delivery issues should be reported to your** [local Post Office](https://emailus.usps.com/). | **Referrals, Reminders & Sharing Feature** **Referrals Feature** Refer your friends and family to Informed Delivery so they can experience the benefits. You can click the referral banner that appears at the bottom of the Daily Digest to refer a friend via text or email. Once selected, a pre-populated email or text message with a link to enroll in Informed Delivery will be generated. Reminders Feature The Informed Delivery Reminders feature allows you to view your Daily Digest and select the "Set a Reminder" button beside an eligible mailpiece. Select a date to receive a reminder email including the mailpiece image and any associated digital content. You will receive an email reminder on the selected day. **Social Sharing Feature** To share links to the digital content via text, email or social media, you can click the social media sharing icons that appear alongside digital content. Once you click an icon, a pre-populated message will appear and include the link associated to the digital content. The sender will manually enter the recipient's phone number or email address. When the recipient receives the email or text message, it will appear from the sender's phone number or email address. - Click the ![](https://www.usps.com/real-mail/images/icon-mail.png) icon to share digital content via email - Click the ![](https://www.usps.com/real-mail/images/icon-text.png) icon to share digital content via text **Informed Delivery Mobile App** The Informed Delivery Mobile app is a stand-alone app designed to enhance your Informed Delivery experience. With a user-friendly mobile format, you can manage your incoming mail and packages anytime, anywhere. Key Features - **Push Notifications:** Opt-in to receive notifications for your Daily Digest, package delivery updates, and more - straight to your mobile device. - **Intuitive Sign-In:** Experience a fast and secure sign-in process, allowing you to seamlessly navigate to your favorite Informed Delivery features in the app. - **Incoming Mail:** Stay informed of what's arriving in your mailbox by viewing and interacting with your incoming mail. The Informed Delivery app displays mail images for a seven-day period on the "Mail" tab. - **Packages:** Effortlessly track packages and seamlessly share your package statuses with others. View package information for 15 days after the last package tracking event (or for up to 30 days for labels manually added to the Informed Delivery dashboard). **Downloading and Signing in to the App / Biometric Sign-in** The app is available for download within the [Apple App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/id6478885776) and [Google Play Store](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.usps.id45833). If you are an existing Informed Delivery user, log in using your current username and password by selecting "Have an account? Sign In". If you are not an existing Informed Delivery user, you can create a [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account and enroll in the Informed Delivery feature directly within the mobile app by selecting "Create Account". **NOTE:** It takes several days for an account to finish setting up, so you may not see mail images or package data right away. You can choose to set up biometrics to sign in without entering your username and password every time. If you do not have biometric settings on your device set up already, you must go to your device settings and turn it on (e.g., Face ID, Touch ID, fingerprint, etc.). To turn on biometrics within the Informed Delivery mobile app: 1. Ensure your device biometrics are enabled. 2. Go to the "More" tab. 3. Select "Sign-In Preferences". 4. Select the toggle to turn on biometric sign-in. 5. When you see "Would you like to sign in faster using biometrics?", select the toggle next to Biometrics, Face ID, or Touch ID to turn on biometric sign-in. 6. Prove your identity with your biometric authentication to use biometric sign-in. You can turn off biometric authentication by going to the "More" tab, select "Sign-In Preferences" and toggle biometrics off or on. **App Settings and Notifications** To change your app settings, such as turning off notifications or camera access, navigate to the "More" tab in the app. from there, you can manage push notifications, sign-in preferences and app permissions. To receive notifications to your mobile device, select the "More" tab at the bottom of the screen, then select "App Permissions". Select "Notifications" to allow notifications and customize the type of notifications you see (banner, sounds, badges, etc.). You can choose when you'll receive notifications (e.g., Daily Digest notifications, package updates, etc.) by selecting "Manage Push Notifications" on the "More" tab. **Troubleshooting** | | | | --- | --- | | I don't see a mail image in my app | Select the "Mail" tab and scroll down to see if the mailpiece arrived on a different day. We also recommend logging out of the app by selecting "More", then selecting "Logout". Close the app and sign back in with your username and password. | | I don't see a package on my app | Sometimes a package that is in your Daily Digest email or Informed Delivery dashboard will not show in the mobile app. To find your packages more easily, navigate to the "Package" tab and filter for your packages by Expected Delivery Date, Delivery Status, or Package Type. Follow the steps in the next row to manually add the package to your app dashboard. | | How to manually add a package | You can manually add packages in by scanning the package barcode with your phone camera or by manually adding the tracking number:
**Camera Scan:**
1. Tap the "Home" tab.
2. Tap the barcode scan icon in the search bar.
3. Use your camera to scan the package barcode and add the package. You should now see the package in the "Package" tab.
1. NOTE: You can add more than one package by selecting "Multiple Mode". You will need to allow your device to access your camera. The mobile app will never store your pictures.
**Manually Add the Tracking Number:**
1. Tap the "Home tab.
2. Tap "Track a Package" in the search bar.
3. Enter your package tracking number.
4. Tap "Track". You should now see the package in the "Package" tab. | | Unable to install the app | Make sure your device internet or cellular service is turned on and you are signed into the app store used by your device (Apple or Google Play). | | Unable to update the app | Make sure your device internet or cellular service is turned on and you are signed into the app store used by your device (Apple of Google Play).  If you still cannot update, try deleting the app and reinstalling it on your device. | | The mobile app screen or a feature in the app is not working, or the app crashes | Please try logging out of the app. Tap "More", then "Logout". Close the app, re-open it and sign back in with your username and password. If you still experience an issue, [send us an email](https://emailus.usps.com/s/informed-delivery-inquiry?_gl=1*danha0*_ga*MTMxMzU1NjIwNi4xNzE0NTc1NzA2*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxNzYxMzUyNS41MS4xLjE3MTc2MTM5NjguMC4wLjA.) to tell us more about the issue, such as where the issue happened in the mobile app and what kind of mobile device you are using. | | Unable to log in to the app | To log in using the app:
1. Open the app and select “Sign in with Password.”
2. Enter your username and password.
3. Select “Sign In.”
If you follow the steps above and still can’t log in, close the app and try again or reset your username and password. If the problem continues, there may be a system error. In this case, close the app and try again later.
- If you forgot your password, select “Forgot your password?” on the sign-in screen. Enter your username for a temporary password to be sent to the email address associated with your account. 
- If you forgot your username, select “Forgot your username?” on the sign-in screen. Enter the email address associated with your account to have your username emailed to you.
**_Note:_** _Usernames cannot be changed._ | | I had the beta test app and can no longer access it | If you were a beta app user, follow the steps below to ensure you have the correct version of the mobile app.
**Apple:**
1. Visit the Apple App Store and download the official Informed Delivery® Mobile app to continue using the service.
**Android:**
No need to uninstall your current app, as it will update automatically. However, if you’re having issues accessing the app, please try reinstalling the app from the Google Play Store. | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Informed Delivery® - The Basics URL Name Informed-Delivery-The-Basics [Informed Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFnGAM/informed-delivery) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Informed Delivery® - The Basics

metadata.description: Informed Delivery® is a free and optional notification feature that gives residential, business and eligible PO Box™ consumers the ability to digitally preview their letter-sized mail and manage their packages scheduled to arrive soon.
metadata.title: Informed Delivery® - The Basics
