Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000013BxAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail) ## Preparing and Sending Political Mail ## Different types of Political Mail, how to prepare those mailings, and reporting problems with mailings. - Feb 18, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007854 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**Political Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#political_mail) | [**Political Mail – Using Tag 57**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#using_tag_57) | **[Assistance in Preparing Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#preparing_mail_assistance)** | | **[Election Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail)** | **[**Voting by Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Election-Mail)** | **[Election Mail - YouTube](https://youtu.be/K3ZGwxQFZXA?si=xhb7C1-uCDqgI9NL "A discussion with the Postal Service’s Executive Director for Election and Government Mail Services, Adrienne Marshall. This is brought to you by \"Mailin’ It!\", the Official USPS Podcast.")** | # **Political Mail** **What is Political Mail?** _Political Mail_ is any material mailed for campaign purposes by a registered political candidate, campaign committee, a committee of a political party or any material mailed by a political action committee or organization engaging in issues advocacy or voter mobilization. Political Mail may be sent for any public election - partisan or nonpartisan - for which there is a ballot. Political Mail is identified using red Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_. Political mail can also be identified by using Political Mail Service Type IDs (STIDs) in the Intelligent Mail barcode (IMb). Political Mail can be sent as First-Class Mail® or USPS Marketing Mail® service. Certain Political committees and State or Local Voting Registration Officials may qualify for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail® prices \[ [_Domestic Mail Manual_ (DMM) 703.1.3.1](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#ep1114977)\]. Additional resources for Political Mail include: - The [Political Mail page at USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/business/political-mail.htm) - The Political Mail page allows you to [alert an office of an upcoming mailing or report any issues with your mailing after it is processed](https://tools.usps.com/political-mail.htm). - [Assistance in Preparing Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#mailing_assistance) for assistance in dealing with issues preparing your mailing. - The [DeliverTheWin](http://www.deliverthewin.com/) website. It provides many Political Mail resources, including tools to help determine which USPS class of service to use and the best way to get return mail. Also available are guidelines for properly addressing mail, tips for ensuring mailing lists are up to date, and a link to request further assistance from the Postal Service. **What is the difference between a "Political Mailing Notice" and a "Political Mailing Problem"?** | | | | --- | --- | | A "Political Mailing Notice" **notifies USPS of a political mailing** so the postal entry facility is aware and can help to ensure timely processing of political mail volume. | "Political Mailing Problem" **reports an issue with a mailing that has already been entered at a USPS® facility**. | | If there is a specific issue with your political mailing, it is important to provide the requested details about the mailing so the problem can be appropriately investigated and resolved. Allow time for a thorough investigation to be completed
_**Prior to**_ reporting a “delivery delay” issue, please allow:
- Approximately 3-10 business days for processing & delivery of USPS Marketing Mail
- Approximately 2-5 business days processing & delivery of First-Class Mail | **What is the purpose of providing a "Political Mailing Notice" prior to entering a political campaign mailing?** The Postal Service understands that timely processing and delivery of Political Mail to customers in advance of upcoming elections, for political candidates and campaigns especially, is important. USPS has implemented an online interface so that political candidates/campaigns may notify USPS of upcoming mailings. The purpose of providing USPS notice prior to entering Political Mail at a USPS facility is to ensure the appropriate postal entry facilities are aware of incoming mail volume in order that such mail volume can be processed and delivered as quickly as possible. **How long does it take to receive a response to a "Political Mailing Problem"?** If you submit a Political Mailing Problem form: 1. You will receive a confirmation email shortly after submitting. 2. Next, if needed, a USPS employee will contact you within the following 24 hours to gather additional details on the issue you’re experiencing. Allow time for a thorough investigation to be completed. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#top) # **Political Mail – Using Tag 57** **What is Tag 57?** | | | | --- | --- | | Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_, is a red container tag that may be used to identify Political Mail upon entry into the mailstream. Tag 57 has the words "Political Campaign Mailing" printed in black and with six black stars above and below those words.
Tag 57 is only intended to identify:
- Political Campaign Mailing by a registered political candidate, campaign committee, and committee of a political party; or
- Political Message Mailing by a PAC, super-PAC, or other organization engaging in issue advocacy or voter mobilization. | ![Tag 57 is red, with the words "Political Campaign Mailing" printed in black and with six black stars above and below those words.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/tag_57.jpg)
_Click image for larger version_ | | The USPS _Postal Operations Manual defines_ 'a registered political candidate or party' as an "individual or organization recognized as such by the appropriate governmental election control authority". | **Who qualifies to use Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_?** Tag 57 is used by mailers to identify trays and sacks that contain Political Mail in anticipation of any public election – partisan or nonpartisan - for which there is a ballot. Tag 57 is used to identify Political Mail that was sent for political campaign purposes by a registered political candidate, campaign committee, committee of a political party, Political Action Committee (PAC), Super-PAC, or organization engaging in issue advocacy or voter mobilization. **Is the use of Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_, required on all containers of Political Mail?** No. The use of Tag 57 is optional; however, the Postal Service recommends doing so as it provides greater visibility to Political mailings as they enter Postal Service processing and distribution operations. _**NOTE:**_ When using Tag 57, it is not necessary to place a tag on every tray in the mailing. The tag can be placed on the container with trays of political mail, usually at the top and on all 4 sides. **Is Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_, reusable?** Yes. Once containers of Political Mail have been emptied, Tag 57 should be removed from the trays and sacks and returned to the office (i.e., Business Mail Entry Unit) to be reused on another Political Mail container. **How can I get a supply of Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_?** Post Offices™ maintain a sufficient supply of Tag 57 to meet their local needs. When needed, Post Office facilities can order red Tag 57 from the Material Distribution Center (MDC) using normal ordering procedures. **Can Tag 57, _Political Campaign Mailing_, be used for non-partisan Political Campaign Mail?** Yes. Qualified non-partisan political campaign mailings must receive the same handling and treatment as partisan political campaign mailings. - **Note**: Specific responsibilities for handling Political Mail are detailed in in the [Political Mail page at USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/business/political-mail.htm). Publication 632, _[State and Local Election Mail — User’s Guide](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub632.pdf)_, has some useful information as well. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#top) # **Assistance in Preparing Mail** **Who can assist me with designing my campaign mail piece?** A [Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA)](https://www.usps.com/political-mail/xls/district-sales-managers.xls) can answer questions about mailpiece design, give advice on evaluating pieces of mail for automation discounts, provide technical assistance on envelope standards, and help construct mailing plans. For assistance from an MDA, please email [MDA@usps.gov](mailto:MDA@usps.gov) or call 877-672-0007 between 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM Central Time. **Does the USPS provide mailing lists?** The U.S. Postal Service® does not make any addresses available. Vendors & Resources are available online at [https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/advertise-with-mail.htm) offers resources that include list brokers that can assist in purchasing list by demographic, geographic etc. **Can my campaign staffer put my flyers in every mailbox?** No. By law, customer mail receptacles, like private mailboxes, may be used only for matter bearing postage. Any materials placed upon, supported by, attached to, hung from, or inserted into a mail receptacle must have postage. Willfully depositing mailable material, on which no postage has been paid, in a mail receptacle with the intent to avoid paying postage is a criminal offense in violation of 18 United States Code § 1725 that is punishable by a fine. The Postal Service offers a service that allows campaigns and candidates to deliver mail to every address. Flyers can be converted to a mailpiece and submitted as “Every Door Direct Mail" (EDDM) mailpieces. For more information, visit [www.usps.gov](http://www.usps.gov/) \> Business > Using EDDM or [http://www.usps.com/business/every-door-direct-mail.htm](http://www.usps.com/business/every-door-direct-mail.htm). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Preparing and Sending Political Mail URL Name Preparing-and-Sending-Political-Mail [Contacts & Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HG8GAM/contacts-help)[Seasonal Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHGGA2/seasonal-services)[Hot Topics](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHTGA2/hot-topics) ## Related Articles - [Requirements and Standards for Sending Domestic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Requirements-and-Standards-for-Sending-Domestic-Mail) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [USPS Mailing Addresses for Consumers](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mailing-Addresses-for-Consumers) - [Types of First-Class Mail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Types-of-First-Class-Mail) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Preparing and Sending Political Mail

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq000000135VAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) ## USPS Tracking® - The Basics ## USPS Tracking® service provides end-to-end item tracking. This article provides in-depth information on how to use the service, what information the service provides, and more convenient ways to access your item's tracking information. - Feb 17, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007991 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**What is USPS Tracking®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#What_is_USPS_Tracking) | [**My mailpiece hasn't been delivered**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#My_mailpiece_hasnt_been_delivered) | [**Expected Delivery Window**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#Expected_Delivery_Window) | | [**How does USPS Tracking® work?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#How_does_USPS_Tracking_work) | [**Add tracking to my item**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#Add_tracking_to_my_item) | [**Access tracking information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#Access_tracking_information) | | [**Receive automatic notifications**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#Receive_automatic_notifications) | [**How can I use USPS Tracking®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#How_can_I_use_USPS_Tracking) | [**My item was received, but not scanned as delivered**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#item_not_scanned_as_delivered) | | **[How to find your tracking number](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number)** | **[How to Stop Text Messages for Tracking Notifications](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#StopText)** | | **What is USPS Tracking®?** USPS Tracking® service provides end-to-end item tracking and is available for most domestic mail products that are large enough and that are addressed to domestic locations, including to limited Army Post Offices (APOs), Fleet Post Offices (FPOs), Diplomatic Post Offices (DPO) destinations and to ZIP Codes in U.S. territories and possessions or Freely Associated States. Entering the tracking number associated with a mailpiece into the USPS Tracking page gives you the current status of an item along with other tracking information. That info includes, if available, delivery and/or attempted delivery information, including date and time of delivery as well as the delivery location of the item (mailbox, reception desk, neighbor, etc.). USPS Tracking does not expedite or guarantee delivery. There is no additional cost for the tracking mechanism on products that automatically include USPS Tracking. Basically, you pay for that product's postage, and tracking is included, unless you are shipping USPS Marketing Mail® parcels. Adding USPS Tracking to that commercial mailing service requires paying a fee. - _**What about International shipping products?**_ Tracking is included with many — but not all — international products; however, destination restrictions can apply. To learn more about tracking options for international products, visit the International Mail & Shipping page. - _**What about First-Class Mail® letters?**_ While end-to-end tracking is not available for First-Class Mail letters, you can purchase an Extra Service and get tracking information. Learn more about First-Class Mail options here. **What can I do if my mailpiece hasn't been delivered?** First, please check the delivery standard for the mail class of your domestic item. You can find the mail class by entering the USPS® Tracking number and looking up "Product Information." Then compare your mail class and progress to what is found in the Mail Delivery Standards chart located in [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages). The delivery standard chart indicates when your item should be delivered by. - If the status on the USPS Tracking page indicates your item is eligible for additional research, the appropriate next steps will be provided on the website. - If your item has a status of "Alert," a delay could have occurred because of weather-related and other natural disasters or events. In this case, please check USPS Service Alerts first. **Expected Delivery Window** When checking the status of your item, you may see a message about the Expected Delivery Window (ExD™). This window is a 2-hour slot of time when you can expect your package to arrive. Delivery within that time is not guaranteed, however, you may still receive your item by the end of the day if it does not arrive within the expected time slot. Check USPS Tracking® for updated information. [![Back to Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#top) **How does USPS Tracking® work?** The label of your trackable item is scanned several times throughout the mailstream, whether we possess it or one of our shipping partners does. Tracking can start as soon as the item's sender or our shipping partner notifies us electronically that we should expect a package. It continues with USPS accepting the package for transit and ends when the item is delivered or picked up by the recipient or the recipient's agent. Throughout this process, you can access this tracking information in a variety of ways through the USPS Tracking number associated with the item. **When is tracking information available?** Scanning information is updated throughout the day as scans become available. **What does this tracking information mean? What can I do?** To get a better idea of what a specific status is and what it means for you, check the Scan Event Status page. **How do I add tracking to my item?** **At a Post Office™**: Once you pay postage on your qualifying shipping product at the Post Office retail counter, USPS Tracking® is automatically activated. Use the tracking number on your mailing receipt to then access delivery information. See " [How do I access tracking information?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#Access_tracking_information)" **Online**: Create an [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® online account to print USPS Tracking® labels, purchase mailing/shipping services, buy stamps, etc. The tracking label prints both the label affixed to the product AND a receipt with the tracking number. Access your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account anytime to retrieve tracking information or status of your item. Remember that when you pair a USPS Tracking® product with a compatible Extra Service, you can receive proof of delivery, such as a signature. See [Domestic Extra Services](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) and [International Extra Services](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm) for details. Whichever option you choose — at the Post Office retail counter or online at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) — you'll have access to the same USPS Tracking® information. Just remember to hold on to your retail receipt! The Postal Service cannot track or locate an item without the tracking number. **What does a USPS Tracking® label look like?** | | | | --- | --- | | **USPS Tracking Retail Label**
The tracking number is the series of numbers under the barcode. | **Priority Mail® Label, Front**
For use by electronic option mailers; can be used at retail. | | ![Image of retail label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/USPSRetailTracking_111618_374px.jpg) | ![USPS Tracking preprinted label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/USPSPrePrintTracking.jpg) | Preprinted USPS Tracking labels have a peel-off portion at the bottom that can be retained for records and tracking. [![Back to Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#top) **How do I access tracking information?** The type of tracking and delivery information available is based on the class of mail or add-on services you purchased at the time of mailing. Whether you are the sender or recipient, you can track your item: - **Online**: Use USPS Tracking® on the United States Postal Service® website. - **By text**: Send a text to 28777 (2USPS) with your tracking number as the content of the message. Standard message and data rates may apply. See [Text Tracking FAQs](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm) for more information. - **By smartphone**: Download the USPS Mobile® app for iPhone® and Android™. | | | | --- | --- | | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/apple_app_store.png)](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/usps-mobile/id339597578) | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/google_play.png)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.usps) | - **By phone**: - To obtain tracking information, customers can go to [Contact USPS](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm#kilo) for the phone number and hours of operation. - For telecommunications device for the Deaf/Teletypewriter (TDD/TTY), call 1-800-877-8339. - Listen to automated information 24 hours/seven days per week. **Can I receive automatic notifications?** Yes. There are several ways that you can request receiving automatic notifications by email or text: - When you have the item's tracking number, enter it at the [USPS Tracking® website](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input). If available, click "Text & Email Updates" to expand that section and follow the directions. - The [USPS Text Tracking® FAQs page](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm) has detailed directions on requesting notifications by only sending a text message to 28777 (2USPS). - Retailers, merchants and other shippers and mailers can automatically sign up their customers for tracking email notifications. Although these emails come from USPS, they are sent at the request of shippers and mailers. Email and text update requests are active for a period of 2 weeks following the request. After that time, the request will be closed. **Once the item is delivered, you won't receive further notifications.** | | | --- | | An additional option is to sign up for [Informed Delivery®](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/). Instead of tracking emails, you receive Daily Digest emails that preview your mail and packages scheduled to arrive soon. Through Informed Delivery, you can also access "Package Notification Options", a feature similar to the above " [Text & Email Updates](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#text_email_updates)", as well as other features to manage your packages. | **I'm not receiving any notification emails or texts!** You may not receive any updates or status emails or texts if there are no new shipment updates or the email address entered was incorrect. To be certain that you are experiencing an issue, visit the USPS Tracking page and enter the tracking number. If no new event information is available, then there would be no messages for USPS to send. If there is new event information and you haven't received it, then try submitting another notification request. If you requested email notifications and are having problems, also try looking in your spam/junk mail folder. If enabled, the junk mail should be checked to ensure emails from the Postal Service are allowed. **How do I Stop USPS Text Tracking Messages?** To stop receiving USPS Text Tracking messages, reply "STOP" to 28777 (2USPS). NOTE: Replying "STOP" will end all active text tracking requests for any tracking number you previously requested tracking for, not just the most recent tracking number. See [Text Tracking FAQs](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm) for more information. [![Back to Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#top) **How can I use USPS Tracking®?** Once you enter the tracking number of an item(s) into the USPS Tracking® website, you can review the latest status of that item. Depending on the status, origin and destination, and the class and services of the item, you may be able to take a number of different actions if available. You can find the item's tracking history and see what kind of services and features are associated with it. You can request emails and/or text messages about your item or a Proof of Delivery email. You can also leave instructions for the carrier on where to leave your mailpiece if no one is available to accept it. **How long are records kept for tracking?** The records of mail classes and services, including access to package history and Proof of Delivery when available, are stored in the system for the life of the package data: | Mail Class and Services | Record Storage | | --- | --- | | USPS Tracking® Service | **120 days** | | Signature Confirmation™ Service | **1 year** | | Priority Mail Express® Service | **2 years** | | Certified Mail® Service | **2 years** | | Registered Mail® Service | **2 years** | | Numbered International Mail Services | **2 years** | | Adult Signature Required/Adult Signature Restricted Delivery Mail Services | **2 years** | [![Back to Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#top) **What if my item was received, but not scanned as delivered?** The absence of a delivery scan on a mailpiece does not necessarily indicate that the item was not delivered. It is possible the piece was delivered but the scan was not captured. If you think this has happened, you may wish to contact the sender or recipient to confirm delivery of the item. If there are no delivery scans in our system, and you know the package was delivered, the sender can request a refund for the USPS Tracking® fee (but not the postage). This is applicable only to USPS Marketing Mail® parcels. [![Back to Top](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS Tracking® - The Basics URL Name USPS-Tracking-The-Basics [USPS Tracking ®](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFmGAM/usps-tracking-)[Featured Tracking](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHOGA2/featured-tracking) ## Related Articles - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading USPS Tracking® - The Basics

## PO Box™ - The Basics

## How to apply, pay for, renew, close, transfer, refund a Post Office Box.      - Jan 5, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000005315

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |

**What is PO Box™ service?**

**Post Office (PO) Box information**

- PO Box™ service is a premium service offered for a fee to anyone who requires more than free home delivery or general delivery.
  - **Note:**  A PO Box may be available for no fee, in specific situations, for those who are not eligible for home delivery.
- PO Boxes are usually accessed by or lock combination (some older Post Offices™ may use combination locks).
- PO Box service is provided in 3-month (with an automatic recurring payment by credit card), 6-month (semi-annual), or 12-month (annual) increments.

**What are lobby hours and business hours?**

- PO Boxes are generally located in the lobby area of the Post Office.
- Select Post Offices have extended business hours and/or 24-hour lobby access.
- _**Lobby hours**_ are the hours when you may access your PO Box. Many Post Office lobbies are open 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, so be sure to check the lobby hours when selecting a PO Box online.
- _**Business hours**_ are the hours when Post Office employees are available to answer questions and to conduct transactions (PO Boxes, mailing, shipping, purchase stamps, etc.). To present your IDs and pick up your keys or lock combination, you need to visit the Post Office during business hours.
- For more information on your PO Box lobby hours, please go to [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm).

**How do I find the PO Box Access Hours for my local Post Office™?**

- Use Post Office Locator to [find the Post Office where your PO Box is located](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm). Look for the section on "PO Box Access" to see when you can check your mail. Look for "PO Box Delivery" to see the time your mail should be delivered by each day.

![Image of USPS Locations webpage with where to find the PO Box hours shown](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000xzN&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq000005Wuqb)

**Benefits of PO Box service:**

- You get your mail when and where you want it.
- You can search online for a PO Box at a location that's convenient for you.
- There are several ways that you can renew a PO Box:
  - Online with a credit or debit card.
  - At any self-service kiosk.
  - By mail with a check or money order.
  - In person at the Post Office™.
- Mail typically arrives faster at a PO Box than at a residential or business address.
- Your PO Box address can be used for selected contacts and transactions.
- PO Boxes are affordable.
- PO Boxes are good for businesses expanding to other cities.
- Additional services available at some Post Office locations include Street Addressing and Signature on File. See [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm) for more information and availability.

**How do I find the availability and prices of PO Boxes online?**

Available PO Boxes can be found online or at your [Local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm). To find a PO Box online:

1. Go to [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm).
2. Enter a valid street address, city, and state **or** a ZIP Code™ to find a PO Box at a location that's convenient for you.
3. Click 'Search' and select a Post Office location from the list or map, and then a box size and payment period. The search results include:
   - If parking is available
   - Lobby hours (when you can access your PO Box)
   - Business hours or 24 hour locations
   - The street address of the Post Office
   - Pricing, fees, and available box sizes at each Post Office

**How do I get technical help with PO Boxes Online?**

For assistance with PO Boxes Online, please visit Customer Service at [Help - USPS](https://www.usps.com/help/welcome.htm) or email the Internet Customer Care Center at [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/), click on " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" and select from the topics.

**How do I apply for a PO Box™?**

**Applying for a PO Box**

You may apply for a PO Box in person at a Post Office™ or online at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®. An application process for PO Box service is not completely approved until your identity and current permanent physical address where you reside or conduct business is verified.

There is only one (1) official (primary) box holder for a PO Box. This box holder may permit others to also use the box.

- Spouses may complete and sign one _PS Form 1093_, _Application for Post Office Box_. All applicants listed (including both spouses and other individuals listed, except for minors) must present two forms of acceptable identification. [One must be an acceptable primary of identification and the other an acceptable secondary form of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). A parent or guardian may receive delivery of a minor's mail by listing the name(s) of each minor on PS Form 1093.
- Other adult persons who receive mail in the PO Box of an individual box customer must be listed on PS Form 1093 and must present two forms of acceptable identification.
- Employees or members who receive mail in the PO Box of an organization who is a box customer must be listed on PS Form 1093. Each person must have verifiable identification and, upon request, present this identification to the United States Postal Service®.

**Important**: Only the official box holder may file a Change of Address (COA) order with USPS to close mail (temporarily or permanently) away from a PO Box, except residential customers who are eligible for a Group E (no-fee) box.

When any information required on PS Form 1093 changes, the primary box holder must update the application file at the local Post Office facility.

**How do I apply for a PO Box in person?**

To apply at a Post Office, you must complete all required information on PS Form 1093, Application for Post Office Box Service (to view and print the form, [click here](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps1093.pdf)). If you apply online, this form is generated during the application process. Bring a copy of the completed form along with two forms of acceptable U.S. identification ( [one must be an acceptable primary of identification and the other an acceptable secondary form of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)) for verification to a Post Office that provides window service. See [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).

The Post Office need not be the one where box service is desired. Please note that furnishing false information on the application or refusing to furnish required information is sufficient reason for denial of the application or discontinuance of service.

**How do I apply for a PO Box online?**

Go to [PO Boxes Online](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm)

1. Search for an available PO Box by entering an address or a ZIP Code™ under Reserve a New PO Box. Choose an available PO Box location by clicking on the location in the list or map.
2. From there, choose an available PO Box size, indicating a 3-month, 6-month, or 12-month payment preference and click 'Continue.' _If the box size is not available, you will be added to the wait list._
3. Complete the PO Box Online Application (PS Form 1093) on Step 2: Reserve a New PO Box and agree to the Terms & Conditions to proceed to enter payment information.
4. Select your payment method and enter your card information. If you wish to save the card, click in the box to the left of 'Save Credit Card to My Account.' You are allowed to save three cards to your account.
5. Next, read the refund policy and, if you accept the refund policy, click on the box to the left of 'I accept the refund policy' and click 'Continue.'
6. Review the Application and Payment Review page for accuracy. Submit your application.
7. Once the PO Box application has been submitted, customers can view a confirmation page with a summary of their order details along with detailed instructions how to activate their PO Box.
8. Customers then have 30 days to bring their printed PS Form 1093 and two forms of acceptable U.S. identification (see [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)) to the Post Office location where they have reserved their box online to receive their keys / box combination.
9. Customers that are eligible for a Free Group E PO Box should not apply for a PO Box online. These customers should visit their local Post Office to complete their application.

**What is the Wait List?**

If your preferred PO Box size is not immediately available, you may choose another box size, find a PO Box at a different location, or request to be added to the Wait List (online or at a Post Office). If you request to be added to the Wait List, your name will be placed on a waiting list for that box size at the Post Office you have requested.

**How will I be notified when a PO Box is available if I am on the Wait List?**

The Post Office will contact you by phone or by email to let you know that a box is available. You should respond promptly as there may be other customers on the wait list for the same box size. Be sure to take your completed application (PS Form 1093) and two forms of acceptable U.S. identification with you to the Post Office and check to make sure the information on the form is correct. If you applied online, an email will be sent to you notifying you when your box is available off the waitlist. You have two days to complete the payment online for this box. Then, you have 30 days to print the 1093, available from the "Manage Your Account" page, and bring it into the office, along with two forms of acceptable U.S. identification. One form of ID must contain a photograph. Your valid photo ID **must be current**, contain sufficient information to confirm that you are who you claim to be, contain your current address, and be traceable to you. For more information on acceptable forms of identification, see [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification).

**Can I remove my name from the Wait List?**

Yes, you may remove your name from the Wait List. Either contact the Post Office or, if you applied online, go to the Manage Your Account page, select the box that you have on the wait list, and click 'Remove from wait list.'

**How do I edit the information on my PO Box application (PS Form 1093)?**

When you are applying for a new PO Box, be sure to review all the information on the application form carefully and make sure it is accurate (online or on paper).

If you have already submitted your online application or are using your PO Box, you must complete an updated PS Form 1093, _Application for Post Office Box Service_, at the Post Office where your PO Box is located.

**How do I get keys or the lock combination to my PO Box?**

You will receive two keys (or a lock combination) for your PO Box at the Post Office where your box is located—after you have paid the applicable fee, submitted a completed PO Box application (PS Form 1093) and your two forms of acceptable U.S. ID are verified. If you need more keys, you may request and pay an additional fee for each key at the Post Office where your PO Box is located.

**Is my personal information stored online kept private and secure?**

Yes. To ensure that your personal information is confidential, the US Postal Service® uses industry-standard encryption software. Your data is sent over a secure connection between your computer and the USPS. Once received, your personal information is stored in a password-protected database that is accessible only by authorized USPS personnel.

**PO Box™ Privacy Act Statement:**

Your information will be used to provide PO Box service. Collection is authorized by 39 U.S.C. 401, 403, & 404.

_Providing the information is voluntary, but if not provided, we will be unable to fulfill your request for PO Box service. We do not disclose your information to third parties without your consent, except to facilitate the transaction, to act on your behalf or request, or as legally required. This includes the following limited circumstances: to a congressional office on your behalf; to financial entities regarding financial transaction issues; to a U.S. Postal Service auditor; to entities, including law enforcement, as required by law or in legal proceedings; to contractors and other entities aiding us to fulfill the service (service providers); to process servers; to domestic government agencies if needed as part of their duties; and to a foreign government agency for violations and alleged violations of law. Information concerning an individual box holder who has filed a protective court order with the postmaster will not be disclosed except pursuant to court order._

For more information regarding our privacy policies visit [http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/welcome.htm](http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/welcome.htm).

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**Will I receive an email confirmation when I sign up for an account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®** **?**

Yes. After successfully signing up for an account with [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/), you will receive an email confirmation of your new account.

**I have forgotten my [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) password. Can I get a new one?**

If you have forgotten your password, are unable to sign in to [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/), or if you receive a message that your account is locked, you need to reset your password. To reset your password:

1. Click the 'I forgot my password' link.
2. Enter your username.
3. Click the 'Continue' button. **Note**: If you press the 'Enter' key instead of selecting the 'Continue' button, you will return to the Home Page.
4. Enter the answer to your security question (case sensitive). Once you have correctly answered your security question, a temporary password will be sent to you via email.
5. Once you have received your new password, go to [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/welcome.htm) and sign in. After you sign in, you will be prompted to create a new password.

If you continue to encounter an error, please go to: [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/) and click " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" as the reason for your email or call **1-800-344-7779**. Please include the following information in your email:

- Your full name and business name (if applicable)
- Your PO Box address and ZIP Code™
- Your username (or what you believe it to be)
- The email address you used to sign up online
- Please make the subject line of your email " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) Password Reset"

**How do I manage my PO Box account online?**

To manage your PO Box account online, either sign up for an [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account or sign in to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account. From the PO Box landing page ( [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm)) you can:

- Search and Reserve a New PO Box (apply online for another PO Box)
- Set up your PO Box online account by clicking "Manage Account
- Add an existing open PO box to your account by clicking "Link" (closed PO Boxes cannot be linked)
- Renew a current PO Box you are managing online
- Select Manage Account to sign in and view all the PO Boxes you are managing online

**How do I view my previous online payments?**

To view the past three years of your online payment transactions, click "Manage Account" from the PO Box Landing page to get to your Manage Your Account page. From there, click "See Details" for the box that you'd like to view history for. The transaction history will appear at the bottom of the page.

**How many credit or debit cards am I able to store online?**

Up to three credit or debit cards may be saved in your PO Box account. Your saved cards are conveniently available for Click-N-Ship® and The Postal Store™ purchases.

**How many PO Boxes can I manage online?**

Currently, customers can manage as many PO Boxes as they would like online. For additional boxes or to upgrade to Caller Service, please visit your local Post Office.

**How do I edit my online registration information?**

To edit your registration information, sign in to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account and click "My Profile" from the header. You can edit your personal information, preferences, and saved payment information.

**Is there an age requirement to apply for a PO Box online?**

You must be 18 years or older to apply online for a PO Box. However, minors may not apply for a PO Box online. If you are under 18 years of age and want a PO Box, you may apply at a Post Office. See [Applying for a PO Box.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#howdoiapply) PO Box service may be provided to a minor (a person under 18 years of age) unless the minor's parent or guardian submits a written objection to the appropriate postmaster.

**Can I apply for a PO Box in another ZIP Code™?**

Yes, you can apply for a PO Box at a Post Office that is most convenient to your home or business. The Post Office geographically closest to you may not be in your ZIP Code.

**I am a business customer. What do I need to bring to the Post Office™?**

You need to apply for a PO Box like any other customer. The ID requirements are the same as those for residential and business customers. (See [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification))

**How can I qualify for a free PO Box?**

You may qualify for free (Group E) PO Box service if your physical / business location meets all of the following criteria:

1. The physical / business location is within the geographic delivery boundaries administered by a Post Office.
2. The physical / business location constitutes a potential letter carrier delivery point of service.
3. The United States Postal Service® chooses not to provide letter carrier delivery to the physical / business location.
4. The customer does not receive letter carrier delivery via an out-of-bounds delivery receptacle.

If you believe you meet all of these requirements, do not reserve a PO Box using the Post Office Boxes Online application. In these instances, please contact your local Post Office facility.

**Can I apply for a PO Box in a different city or state?**

If you are moving to another location, you may apply for a PO Box online or in person. In either case, to begin the verification process, you need to go to a Post Office where you currently live with a copy of your application and two acceptable forms of U.S. ID. The Postal Service™ has 3 days to verify your information. Once your information has been verified, an employee at the Post Office™ will call you to continue the application process. After you move, you will need to complete the verification process at your new address.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**How do I pay for my PO Box™?**

**What are PO Box fees and which can I pay online?**

When applying online for a new PO Box, you select a three-month, six-month, or 12-month payment period. Your rental fee, plus a refundable key deposit (for the two keys provided) are billed to your credit or debit card. Prices vary according to the size of the box and in which Post Office™ facility your box is located in. For a complete breakdown of our pricing, you can [learn more by clicking here](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/notice123.htm#_c202).

When renewing a PO Box, you may also select a three month, six-month, or 12-month payment period for the upcoming period.

If the Postal Service does not deliver to your home address, you may be eligible for a Free Group E PO Box. If this is the case, you should not apply for a PO Box online, and should visit your local Post Office to complete your application.

**What credit cards and other payment options are accepted for payment of a PO Box?**

- American Express
- Discover
- MasterCard
- VISA
- [Apple Pay](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Forms-of-Payment-are-Accepted)

**Can I use a credit card with an international billing address for PO Box fees?**

No. Credit cards with international billing addresses are not currently supported online.

**How do I edit a card linked to an automatic renewal?**

Sign in to your PO Box account by clicking "Manage Account" from the landing page. From your Manage Your Account page, click "See Details" for the box that you'd like to edit automatic payment for. Then click "Edit Payment Info" and "Use a Different Card" to edit your card information.

**What are my options for paying PO Box fees?**

Your options for making a payment are:

- **Pay online:**
  - Go to [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm) and click "Renew." This will direct you to a page where you can Sign In if you already have an account, or Register if you do not.

    - If you already **have** an account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), enter your Username and Password.
    - If you **do not have** an account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), click ' [Sign Up Now](https://reg.usps.com/login?app=POBO&appURL "USPS Registration")' (it's free). Once you create your account, you will be redirected back to PO Boxes online.
  - If you have never linked your PO Box to your online account, click "Link." Enter in your box information exactly as it was on your application to link your box to your online account. You can set up automatic renewal or pay one time for this box if you are 30 days before your next due date.
  - If you are already managing your box online, on your Manage Your Account page:
    1. Click "Renew Now" next to the box you'd like to pay for
    2. Review your information
    3. Enter your billing information, and then submit
- **Pay at a Self-Service Kiosk:**
  - You can find a self-service kiosk at [USPS.com® - Find Locations](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm).

- **Pay by mail:**
  - Send a check or money order payable to "U.S. Postal Service" to the Postmaster where your PO Box is located.

- **Pay in person:**
  - Visit the Post Office where your PO Box is located using cash, credit or debit card, check or money order payable to "U.S. Postal Service" or "Postmaster".

**Notes:**

- If we have not received your payment by the 10th day after the due date, your PO Box service will be terminated and all incoming mail will be returned to senders. To reopen your box, you would need to pay in person and, in addition to any unpaid box fees, you may be assessed a lock change or handling fee. To avoid this inconvenience and this extra cost, we encourage you to renew on time.
- If you have signed up for a 3-month payment period, you must enroll in automatic renewal. Your card will be charged every 3 months automatically.

**Will I be notified when my PO Box service fees is due?**

Yes. If you have provided us with your email address, you will receive email reminders as well as reminders in your PO Box.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**How do I renew a PO Box?**

**PO Box renewal**

Your payment options for renewing your PO Box are exactly the same as paying for a new PO Box.

- **What is Automatic Renewal?**
You can set up automatic renewal payments online to pay your annual or semi-annual PO Box fees automatically on the due date using the credit or debit card of your choice. To set up automatic payments, you must use a saved credit or debit card. If you have not saved a card, you can add one to your account.

  - **Note:**  If there is no card saved in your PO Box online account, your PO Box fees will not automatically be paid.
- **What happens if my PO Box renewal fee is late?**
If your renewal fee is late, your box will be closed and you will not be able to retrieve your mail. If your payment is more than 10 days late, you need to go to the Post Office where your PO Box is located to pay your renewal fee and a handling fee to cover our processing costs. You will not be able to pay online or at a self-service kiosk. After 10 days of nonpayment, your mail is treated as undeliverable mail and your PO Box is closed. Closed boxes may be made available for new customers.

**Notes:**

  - If payment is not received by the 10th day after the due date (a $27.00 fee is charged for late payments), PO Box service is terminated, incoming mail will be returned to the sender, and, in addition to any unpaid monthly PO Box fees, the customer will be charged a handling fee to reopen the box.
  - The online system is available to receive payments for PO Boxes that are past due up to the 10th day after the due date.
  - Box fees are due either on the 15th or the last day of the month (depending on what billing cycle the customer is on). Boxes that have already been added to an account have the 10 day grace period to pay their renewal fees online. Existing PO boxes may be added (linked) to an online account as long as the box is still open (including during the 10 day grace period).
- **How do I renew my PO Box?**
You have four options for renewing your PO Box:


  - **Online with a credit or debit card**
    Go to [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm) and click 'Renew' which will take you to the 'Manage Your Account' page. Then click Renew PO Box. This feature is available 30 days before your next payment is due.

  - **At a Self-Service Kiosk**
    Go to: [USPS.com® - Find Locations](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) \-\- select Self-Service Kiosks from the drop-down menu.

  - **At the Post Office where your box is located**
    **Note:** If your payment is more than 10 days late, this is your only option for renewing your PO Box.

  - **Renew by mail**
    Send a check or money order payable to "U.S. Postal Service" to the Postmaster where the PO Box is located. Address the mailpiece to:

    1. Postmaster

       Name of the City, State, ZIP Code

- **Does USPS design its systems to maintain my credit or debit card information securely?**

Yes. Your information is protected using powerful Secure Socket Layer (SSL) 128-bit encryption technology.

- **I already have a PO Box. How do I renew it online?**

To renew your PO Box online, navigate to [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm) and select "Renew". After signing into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account, you will be directed to the Manage Your Account page. Find the PO Box you want to make a payment on, and make your payment.


[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**How do I close a PO Box?**

There are two ways you can request to close a PO Box:

1. **If you manage your PO Box online**, you can close it by visiting PO Boxes Online at [http://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm).
2. **If you manage your PO Box at a local Post Office,** you must close your PO Box at the Post Office facility where your box is located.

To determine if your box is eligible for a refund, see [Refunds for PO Box rental fees](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#refund).

**Why was my PO Box closed?**

A PO Box may be closed if fees have not been paid by the due date. After the fees have become past due, access to the box is blocked. The boxholder has 10 days to pay past due fees and pickup held mail. After that ten days (ten days after the due date), the box is closed and your mail is returned as undeliverable.

A PO Box may also be closed if the official box holder has:

- Falsified the application for the box
- Physically abused the box
- Violated any regulation on the care or use of the box (including continuous overflow mail)
- Neglected to pick up his/her mail and the box is overflowing

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**Can a PO Box be transferred?**

PO Box service may be transferred, without payment of an additional fee, to any box of the same size and fee group at the same facility or a different facility as long as the PO Boxes have the same 5-digit ZIP Code. The customer will have to locate and rent the box they want.

To transfer service, submit a new application either to the facility where service is currently provided or to the facility where service is desired.

- PO Box service may be transferred no more than once in any semiannual payment period.
- _PS Form 3575_ must be completed and submitted when transferred.
- Customers must contact their local Post Office with specific questions (including whether or not a nearby office with a desired PO Box location has the same ZIP Code).

If you want PO Box service at a location where you can't transfer to, you will need to close your current PO Box service and start service at that new location.

**Death of a PO Box holder** If a box customer dies or disappears before the end of the period for which the box is issued the following options are available:

- As a temporary measure to retain mailing services to the PO Box, another person listed on the box, an appointed executor, or administrator for a deceased person may file a temporary change of address.
- If another person listed on the _PS Form 1093_ would like to become the permanent PO Box customer, they will need to file a new _PS Form 1093_ and present the [Required ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)before becoming the new primary box holder.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**What do I need to know about PO Box™ delivery?**

**General guidelines**

Private shipping companies or carriers, such as UPS, FedEx and Amazon, are not able to deliver to (place mail in) a PO Box. Only the United States Postal Service® is permitted to deliver mail to a PO Box.

If a mailpiece that is addressed to a PO Box must be signed for, or is too large to fit in the box, the Post Office™ will place one of the following in your box:

1. A key to a larger storage locker within the same facility
2. A notice that you can take to a window clerk to collect your item(s) – Redelivery is not permitted since you have to collect the item from a window clerk.
3. The yellow-orange PS Form 3908-S with a QR Code and USPS Code – A USPS Smart Locker located at the same post office as your PO Box contains your oversized package. Either scan the QR Code or enter the USPS code at the Smart Locker display to open the locker containing your package. More information on Smart Lockers is [available](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker).

![image.png](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000xzN&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMBY0000003kEt)

To learn more about Recipient Services (such as Post Office Box Services) provided by the United States Post Office®, please visit the [Domestic Mail Manual (DMM), section 508.4.0](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/508.htm#4_0).

**There is no mail delivery where I live. Can I apply for a PO Box online?**

You can search for an available PO Box online, but you need to apply for a PO Box at a Post Office and determine whether you qualify for a no-fee PO Box.

**Can all mail classes be delivered to a PO Box?**

All mail classes and services may be sent to a PO Box. If the item requires a signature, a notice will be left. If the second attempt is unsuccessful, the item will be returned to the sender after the required number of business days for it to be held have elapsed.

**Can I send domestic Priority Mail Express® to a PO Box?**

Domestic Priority Mail Express _**may**_ be sent to a PO Box. If the mailer sending a Priority Mail Express item instructs USPS to obtain a signature from the addressee upon delivery of the item, USPS will leave a delivery notice inside the PO Box that the customer must sign for the item. Otherwise, USPS will leave the Priority Mail Express item in the PO Box if it fits. If it does not fit, USPS will leave a notice inside the PO Box.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**What are the PO Box™ forwarding guidelines and restrictions?**

Mail may be forwarded [online](https://moversguide.usps.com/mgo/disclaimer?referral=MG82) from one PO Box to another PO Box or to a street address or by submitting a PS Form 3575, _Change of Address Order_. For information, visit our [FAQ - Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics).

Forwarding of mail for other persons receiving mail at the box is the responsibility of the official box holder.

- Submitting a "permanent" Change of Address (COA) request will automatically close the PO Box permanently (except residential Group E boxes).
- Turning in a "temporary" COA will not close the PO Box. A temporary COA forwards mail for 6-month intervals, not to exceed 1 year.
- Mail that is in a PO Box when a COA is filed is forwarded to the new address (either permanent or temporary).

**Change of Address orders may be filed as follows:**

- **Residential Customers**

Only the box customer listed on Form 1093 may file change of address orders.

- **Organizations**

Only the box holder customer or authorized representative of the organization listed on _PS Form 1093_, _Application for Post Office Box_ may file Change of Address (COA) orders.

  - **Note** **:** Forwarding of mail for other persons is the responsibility of the organization _._
- **Group E residential boxes only**

The box customer or any other persons listed on the PS Form 1093 may file an individual change of address order. Only the official box holder may file a change of address order for entire family.

The official box holder of a PO Box may manually forward mail received at the PO Box (but intended for someone else) by:

1. Crossing out the old address.
2. Writing the new address on the mail item.
3. **Important**: Only the address can be updated. The recipient's name must remain the same.

A PO Box may **not** be used primarily for the purpose of forwarding mail on to a different address.

Mail sent to a PO Box that is **closed** but has no COA request on file will be handled in the following ways:

- A postal clerk may file a COA on the box holder's behalf. Please contact the local Post Office facility where the box is located.
- Mail will be held in General Delivery until a COA is on file.
- Mail will not be returned to sender until the PO Box is declared vacant, except for Registered Mail®, Certified Mail®, Insured Mail, COD Mail, Signature Confirmation™ Services Mail, postage-due mail, and perishable mail items.

**Notes:**

- If you have additional questions regarding the forwarding of mail from your PO Box to another address, please contact your local Post Office.
- You may apply for a PO Box in a city/state other than the one in which you live, subject to availability and verification of identity and current physical address.
- After applying online or in person, you may go into any Post Office to complete the ID verification process. You will STILL need to pick up your keys or lock combination at the Post Office where you rented your PO Box. For this reason, the Postal Service recommends completing the ID verification and key/combination pickup processes at the same time.
- Customers that submit a Change of Address online or in person for a PO Box must inquire about refund eligibility at the post office where their PO Box is located. Refunds for eligible boxes closed due to Change of Address are not granted without an in person or written request.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

**Refunding PO Box Rental Fees**

**Managed In-Person**

Your PO Box™ is prepaid in advance for a three, six or twelve-month service period. If you decide to close your PO Box, you may be eligible for a refund according to the following guidelines:

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **PO Box Refund Policy – INACTIVE PO BOXES**<br>**(Managed In-Person; Applied, but never picked up keys or lock combination)** |
| For **3, 6, and 12** month boxes **never activated**:<br>- Customers will receive an automatic full refund after 30 days of inactivity.<br>- If customers reserved the PO Box online, customers will receive an automatic full refund after 30 days of inactivity.<br>  - Please note: automatic box closure and refunds due to inactivity will only apply to PO Boxes that were reserved online after the 6/25/2017 POBOL launch. |
| **PO Box Refund Policy – ACTIVE PO BOXES**<br>**(Managed In-Person; Applied and picked up keys or lock combination)** |
| **3 month** | **6 month** | **12 month** |
| Not eligible for a refund once activated, but can still be closed online. | Customers can request a partial refund<br>for active 6 or 12 month boxes online. |
| -- | Within the first three months, 50% of the payment is refunded. | Within the first three months, 75% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | After the beginning of the fourth month, none of the payment is refunded. | During months four through six, 50% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | -- | During months seven through nine, 25% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | -- | After the beginning of the 10th month, none of the payment is refunded. |

**Managed Online**

Your PO Box™ is prepaid in advance for a three, six or twelve-month service period. If you decide to close your PO Box, you may be eligible for a refund according to the following guidelines. To apply for a refund, go to [http://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm).

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **PO Box Refund Policy – INACTIVE PO BOXES**<br>**(Managed Online; Applied, but never picked up keys or lock combination)** |
| For **3, 6, and 12** month boxes **never activated**:<br>- Customers will receive an automatic full refund after 30 days of inactivity.<br>- If customers reserved the PO Box online, customers will receive an automatic full refund after 30 days of inactivity. <br>  - Please note: automatic box closure and refunds due to inactivity will only apply to PO Boxes that were reserved online after the 6/25/2017 POBOL launch. |
| **PO Box Refund Policy – ACTIVE PO BOXES**<br>**(Managed Online; Applied and picked up keys or lock combination)** |
| **3 month** | **6 month** | **12 month** |
| Not eligible for a refund once activated, but can still be closed online.<br>To close online, go to [http://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm). | Customers can request a partial refund for active 6 or 12 month boxes online.<br>To apply for a refund, go to [http://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm). |
| -- | Within the first three months, 50% of the payment is refunded. | Within the first three months, 75% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | After the beginning of the fourth month, none of the payment is refunded. | During months four through six, 50% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | -- | During months seven through nine, 25% of the payment is refunded. |
| -- | -- | After the beginning of the 10th month, none of the payment is refunded. |

**How do I request a refund for PO Box fees?**

- **If you are managing your PO Box online:**
  - Visit PO Boxes Online at [https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm "PO Boxes Online"). After you have signed into your account, find your box on the Manage Your Account page and select the link to "Close / Request a Refund".
  - If you have not completed the verification process within 30 days of your online application, you may also request a refund online.
    - If you require technical support for the PO Box Online application, contact the Technical Support team by email at [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/) (click on " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" and select from the topics) or by phone at 1-800-344-7779.
- **If you are managing your PO Box at a local Post Office:**
  - If you started using your PO Box, you must request a refund at the Post Office facility where your box is located.

**Canceling automatic payments**

- If you signed up for automatic payment renewal online, you can cancel automatic payments online up to the 15th of the month prior to the due date for your PO Box.
- After the 15th, you will not be able to cancel automatic payment online.
- **Automatic payments for 3-month boxes CANNOT be canceled.**

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics#top)

## What is USPS Marketing Mail®?

## General information about USPS Marketing Mail. USPS Marketing Mail is mail matter not required to be mailed as First-Class Mail® or Periodicals. With special authorization lower nonprofit prices are available for USPS Marketing Mail.      - Apr 5, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007680

Customer Information

**General Information**

_USPS Marketing Mail_ ® is mail matter that is not required to be mailed as First-Class Mail® or Periodicals. Lower nonprofit prices are available for USPS Marketing Mail, but require specific authorization.

**Mailers use USPS Marketing Mail to send:**

- Printed matter, flyers, circulars, advertising (including [Every Door Direct Mail® (EDDM)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Every-Door-Direct-Mail-EDDM) and [Every Door Direct Mail - Retail®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Every-Door-Direct-Mail-EDDM-Retail)).
- Newsletters, bulletins, and catalogs.
- Small parcels.

**USPS Marketing Mail Characteristics**

- USPS Marketing Mail is not forwarded or returned unless requested with an ancillary service endorsement.
- Forwarding and return services may result in additional fees or postage.
- Only the following extra services may be used with USPS Marketing Mail parcels. They are not included with Marketing Mail and must be purchased (see [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c191) for fees):

  - Electronic USPS Tracking®
  - Bulk Insurance (only on Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail machinable and irregular parcels)
  - Certificate of Mailing

**Qualifications for Sending Marketing Mail Items**

- All USPS Marketing Mail prices are bulk prices requiring a Business Mail Permit.
  - Minimum Weight: Starts at 1 ounce
  - Maximum Weight: less than 16 ounces
  - Minimum Quantity: 200 pieces or 50 pounds of mail
- In USPS Marketing Mail, there are prices for Letters, Flats/Large Envelopes, and Parcels. Per piece and per pound prices for bulk mailings are available at [Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c109).
- There is no single-piece USPS Marketing Mail postage.
- Unlike First-Class Mail, there is no separate USPS Marketing Mail postcard price (USPS Marketing Mail postcards are mailed as letters or flats depending on size).

**What is Non-Profit USPS Marketing Mail?**

_Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail_ is printed material eligible to be mailed as USPS Marketing Mail at significantly reduced nonprofit postage rates when sent by nonprofit organizations authorized by the Postal Service™.

See [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) for additional information and resources.

service**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics#Technical_Assistance) | | **Who can submit a USPS Hold Mail**®**request?** A USPS Hold Mail request may be submitted by anyone at an A third party may schedule a USPS Hold Mail request for a particular address as long as the customer can provide all information required to complete the request. **How can I submit a USPS Hold Mail request?** | | | | --- | --- | | **Online** | **In Person or In Writing** | | Please visit [USPS Hold Mail](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) [Service](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) to submit a request online. You will need to sign in to or create a USPS.com® account to begin an online USPS Hold Mail request.
You can enter an online [USPS Hold Mail](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) request up to 30 days in advance of the start date or as early as the next scheduled delivery day.
Online [USPS Hold Mail](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) requests submitted _before_ 2:00 A.M. Central Time can begin on the same Postal business day. Online USPS Hold Mail requests submitted _after_ 2:00 A.M. Central Time can begin on the next Postal business day at the earliest.
Postal business days are Monday through Saturday, with the exception of Postal holidays. | Please visit your local Post Office™ location and complete [PS Form 8076, Authorization to Hold Mail](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps8076.pdf).
