Please visit your local Post Office™ location and complete [PS Form 8076, Authorization to Hold Mail](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps8076.pdf). **The Customer Care Center is not able to submit requests for ineligible addresses.** | **How do I edit, change or cancel a USPS Hold Mail request?** | | | | --- | --- | | **Edit / change / cancel** | | Confirmation numbers for USPS Hold Mail requests are sent via email if submitted online or through the Customer Care Center, as long as you provide a valid email address. A direct link to the USPS Hold Mail application is provided in the email, allowing you to make changes to your request. If you submitted your Hold Mail at your local post office, you will not receive a confirmation number. 
Edit / Change / Cancel requests submitted _before_ 2:00 A.M. Central Time can begin on the same Postal business day. Requests submitted _after_ 2:00 A.M. Central Time can begin on the next Postal business day at the earliest.
Postal business days are Monday through Saturday, with the exception of Postal holidays. | | **With** **a confirmation number**, you can edit, change or cancel your hold mail request: | - By following the link provided in your confirmation email
- By calling the Customer Care Center | | **Without a confirmation number**, you can't edit, change or cancel your hold mail request:
**Note**: Without a confirmation number, an agent can only extend the USPS Hold Mail® request. | - Only by visiting your local Post Office™ location and presenting one of the **[Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)**.
- If you are unable to visit your local Post Office™ location (still out of town, on vacation, etc.), you will need to call your local Post Office. You may locate the phone number and address using **[Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm)** **.** | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics#Back_to_Top) **How long can I place mail on hold?** USPS Hold Mail® requests must be for a minimum of 3 days and a maximum of 30 days. If you need mail held longer than 30 days, please sign up for a **[USPS Forward Mail service](https://www.usps.com/manage/forward.htm)**. Also, at least 3 days must elapse between the end of one USPS Hold Mail period and the start of the next one. For example, if you’re at the end of a 30-day USPS Hold Mail period, you can ask for another one on the 31st day, but it won't go into effect until after 3 days. **Can I authorize someone else to pick up my USPS Hold Mail?** You have the option to authorize a third party to pick up your held mail by providing written permission to the appropriate Post Office™ location. The third party that you authorize will need to present [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). **How do I get mail when my USPS Hold Mail request ends?** Review the directions provided when the USPS Hold Mail request was filed. You can pick up your mail at your local Post Office™ location or your letter-carrier can deliver your mail on the ending date you specify. Please note the following: - You must present one of the [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) if you choose to pick up your mail. - If you pick up your accumulated mail in person, regular mail delivery will resume the next Postal business day. - If you choose to have your letter carrier deliver your accumulated mail, only the mail that will fit in your mail receptacle will be delivered if you are not home. If the accumulated mail exceeds your mailbox size, your letter carrier will leave a [PS Form 3849 - We ReDeliver for You!](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) and return the overflow mail to your local Post Office location for pickup. **Note:** There is a 10 day period in which to pick up mail at the end of a USPS Hold Mail® service request. If not picked up by this deadline, it will be returned to sender. If your delivery does not resume as requested please [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics#Back_to_Top) **Can I pick up USPS Hold Mail earlier than originally requested?** If you pick up your mail earlier than the date supplied on the USPS Hold Mail request, the 'Hold' is automatically cancelled and regular mail delivery will resume on the next Postal business day. **My USPS Hold Mail did not start or stop as requested?** You may [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) and an agent will document your concern for the local Post Office™ personnel to address. **How will a Change of Address (COA) affect USPS Hold Mail?** | | | | --- | --- | | **USPS Hold Mail with Active COA** | | **If the USPS Hold Mail request is for the new / current address:** | You may submit a normal USPS Hold Mail request. | | **If the USPS Hold Mail request is for the old / previous address AND you are now residing at that address:** | The forwarding order must be canceled by the customer before you can submit a USPS Hold Mail request.
- Please go into your local Post Office™ facility to cancel the existing Change of Address (COA).
- After the COA has been canceled, you may submit your USPS Hold Mail request. | | **If the USPS Hold Mail Request is for the old / previous address AND you do not reside at that address:** | - You may NOT submit a USPS Hold Mail request for an address where you do not live. | | **USPS Hold Mail with COA Submitted Later** | | If you submit a USPS Hold Mail request and later submit a COA, the COA overrides the USPS Hold Mail request (all held mail will be forwarded). | **Why can’t USPS® verify my identity online and how can I put my mail on hold?** Security and privacy are of high importance to USPS®. The current online USPS Hold Mail request process requires you to attempt identity verification. In some cases, individuals may be unable to complete online identity verification. If you are unable to verify your identity within the USPS Hold Mail application, you can request an identity verification code to be mailed to you. Follow the prompts in the USPS Hold Mail application to request the verification code. The letter will be sent via First-Class Mail® and will arrive within 3-5 business days. The code expires 90 days from the date of issue, after successful use, or if the address in your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account profile is edited prior to entering the verification code. You may also visit your local Post Office™ location and complete [PS Form 8076, Authorization to Hold Mail](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps8076.pdf). Requests made in person, or by calling 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777), must be accepted by close of business of your local Post Office™ location or the Customer Care Center for the request to be completed by the next delivery day. **Technical Assistance for Online USPS Hold Mail Service** - **If you are having technical difficulty submitting a USPS Hold Mail request online, please Contact Us** **.** [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics#Back_to_Top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics URL Name USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics [Holding Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFsGAM/holding-mail)[Hot Topics](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HHTGA2/hot-topics) ## Related Articles - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [Sending Military and Diplomatic Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Sending-Military-and-Diplomatic-Mail) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics

## Delivery to multi-story buildings

## Mail and package delivery, and redelivery, to multi-story or apartment buildings.      - Oct 22, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007111

Customer Information

Even in multi-story buildings or apartment buildings, the Postal Service™ will always make a first attempt to deliver a package before leaving a Redelivery Notice - without regard to the floor on which the office or residence is located.

If we missed you when we tried to deliver your mail, you can [schedule a redelivery](https://redelivery.usps.com/redelivery/) online using the peach-colored slip left by your carrier to look up your items.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000012ivAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) ## Label Broker® Feature ## The Label Broker® feature allows customers to go to a local Post Office® location and print out a shipping label by scanning a QR Code® or entering a code provided by a merchant or requested from the Click-N-Ship application. - Feb 12, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006815 Customer Information **What is the Label Broker® feature?** The Label Broker feature supports customers who would like to ship with USPS but do not have access to a printer or shipping labels. You can print pre-paid and No Postage Necessary (e.g. Returns) shipping labels at most Post Office® locations and at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/). For further information and locations, see " [Where are Label Broker printers and locations?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#printers_locations)" **If I want to print my label at a Post Office location, what do I need to bring?** You need to bring the the QR Code® or the 8-10 character Label Broker ID supplied by the Label Provider. Either can be used to print the prepaid label. Also, the Label Broker ID can be written on the package for mailing. **Can I print a Label Broker label at a Post Office location then take it home?** No. The Label Broker label is designed for the label to be affixed to the package at the Retail Window. **Can I add additional services to a Label Broker label?** No. **What If I did not receive a QR Code email?** 1. Check your email's spam or junk folders for the email containing the QR Code. 2. If the email can't be found, request the label provider to resend the email or provide the 8-10 character Label Broker ID, which can be used instead of the QR Code. - If the label provider is Click-N-Ship®, the Label Broker ID can be found on the Label Details page. **Where are Label Broker printers and locations?** Most automated USPS Post Office® locations can print a Label Broker label. To be certain if a specific location supports the Label Broker, you can: 1. Access USPS.com. 2. Click on either the [Locations or Find USPS Locations link](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action). 3. On the Find USPS Locations page: 1. Enter your City and State, or ZIP Code™. 2. Select Post Office as the Location Type(s). 3. Click Filter > Mailing Services and check either Label Broker® Retail and/or Label Broker® Self-Service. 4. Apply the Filters. 4. Click Search to see a list of Post Offices local to the location you entered that has Label Broker service available at the Post Office retail counter or for self-service. Labels may also be printed through [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and at Automated Parcel Drops. | | | --- | | See _[Label Broker & Label Delivery Service \| USPS](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm)_ for additional information on the feature and service. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#top) **Can I use the Label Broker feature with the Click-N-Ship® application?** Yes. The Label Broker feature, which allows customers to have a shipping label printed at a Post Office location, is a shipping option for Click-N-Ship (CNS) customers. | | | --- | | See below for instructions on making and paying for a shipping label using CNS and printing the label at a Post Office location:
- [With Label Broker at a Post Office](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm#cns)
- [With a Self-Service Kiosk](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm#kiosk) | | | | | --- | --- | | The Label Broker option "Print later at Post Office" appears as a selection under "Label Printing Format" after the checkout process is complete on the Click-N-Ship Payment Confirmation page (see screenshot to the right). When choosing the option, you will receive an [email containing a Label Broker ID® (QR Code followed by 8-10 characters)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/label_broker/cns_label_print_code.jpg). You will need to bring the the QR Code or the 8-10 character Label Broker ID along with the package to the Post Office location.
**Note**:  If you can't find the email:
- Be sure to check your spam or junk folders
- Go to the Click-N-Ship Label Details page, find the Label Broker ID, and use that to print postage at a Post Office. | [![Screenshot of the Click-N-Ship Payment Confirmation page. On the left side of the image is the "Print Your Labels" column. A yellow caution symbol indicates to the customer that they "will have until 11:59 PM Central Time of the Ship Date to print this label." The first two options after that are to print labels now with a receipt and to print labels now without a receipt. The third option, which is highlighted for this screenshot, is "Print labels later at the Post Office. A Label Broker ID code will be sent to you that you can use to print your labels at the Post Office. When choosing this option, USPS will send you an email containing a Label Broker ID code that can be scanned at participating USPS Retail locations. For more information visit Label Broker FAQs." A text field below is where the customer enters their email address. A button below is to "Send Code".](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq00000012iv&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OqsuY)](https://usps.file.force.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000lJt&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OqsuY)
_Click image for larger version_ | The Click-N-Ship Label Details page looks like the following: | | | | --- | --- | | If Label Broker has not been selected, the "Print at the Post Office" option will be available under "More Actions".
[![Screenshot of the Click-N-Ship Label Details page.](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq00000012iv&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OsIAE)](https://usps.file.force.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000lN7&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OsIAE)
_Click image for larger version_ | If Label Broker had been selected, you will see the message "LABEL STORED IN LABEL BROKER" in the Label History table at the bottom of the Label Details page.
[![Screenshot of the Click-N-Ship Label Details page.](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq00000012iv&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OqyVO)](https://usps.file.force.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000lN7&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000OqyVO)
_Click image for larger version_ | **What do I need to know about using the Label Broker feature in the Click-N-Ship® application?** - Any domestic CNS label is eligible as long as it doesn't require a customs form (e.g. military packages requiring customs forms are not eligible) - You will receive an email with the QR Code followed by 8-10 characters to be shown at [Label Broker printers and locations](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#printers_locations) - Either the QR Code or the 8-10 character Label Broker ID can be used to print the shipping label. You do not require both to print a label. - If you purchase multiple orders of labels using Label Broker, you can store up to 11 labels in a single QR code. However, if there are more than 11 labels for a single transaction, the Label Broker feature will not be shown as an option. - If you choose to print the label yourself during Click-N-Ship checkout and change your mind, you can go into the [CNS Shipping History Label Details and request a Label Broker code](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#with_click-n-ship) - If you choose Label Broker and change your mind after checkout, you can go into CNS Shipping History Details and select Print/Reprint under More Actions ([see screenshot above](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/label_broker/label_details_post_labelbroker_email.jpg)) - The Label Broker option will not appear in CNS at checkout if any of the labels in the cart are ineligible for Label Broker (requires a customs form); if you want to select the Label Broker option for eligible labels, you can go into the [CNS Shipping History Label Details and request a Label Broker code](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#with_click-n-ship) | | | --- | | See _[Label Broker & Label Delivery Service \| USPS](https://www.usps.com/ship/label-broker.htm)_ for additional information on the feature and service. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Label Broker® Feature URL Name Label-Broker [Business](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFxGAM/business)[Return Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGhGAM/return-services) ## Related Articles - [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label) - [Self-Service Kiosks](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Self-Service-Kiosks) - [USPS Label Delivery Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Label-Delivery-Service) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Label Broker® Feature

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000011jdAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC) ## Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC) ## General information about Priority Mail Express International With Money Back Guarantee. - Feb 12, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000005318 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | **[General Information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#GeneralInformation)** | **[Money Back Guarantee](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#MoneyBackGuarantee)** | | **[Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#ExtraServices)** | | **General Information** Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee (also called Priority Mail Express International® Service, Date Certain Delivery with Money Back Guarantee and PMEI-DC) is an enhanced service of [Priority Mail Express International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International) (PMEI) that offers a date-certain, postage-refund guarantee for pieces to [specific destinations](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#destinations). The item is guaranteed to be delivered on or before the Guaranteed Delivery Date (GDD). The GDD is the specific date a PMEI-DC item is scheduled to arrive at its destination. This date is calculated by the origin Postal Code of the item and its destination Postal Code and can be determined in advance by using the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/). Depending on the country you choose, the product you choose, and the size of the package, you may be able to calculate a guaranteed date of delivery in the place of a range of delivery days. If this service is available, a blue "Calculate Guaranteed Date" link will display under the Priority Mail Express International product name and details. Click that link to access the "Priority Mail Express International Guaranteed Delivery Lookup." This enhanced service currently only exists with the countries listed below (as well as the United States). | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Australia
- Canada
- China | - France (Does not include Corsica and Monaco)
- Hong Kong
- Japan | - South Korea
- Spain (Does not include Canary Islands) | | **Note**: All destinations in a country may NOT be available. To determine if the postal code is available for the PMEI-DC service, use the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/). | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#top) **Money Back Guarantee** PMEI-DC items are guaranteed to arrive on a specific date with a money back service guarantee on the postage paid if delivery or a delivery attempt did not occur by the Guaranteed Delivery Date. - To request a refund, go to [https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) to submit an international inquiry. - Additional conditions allowing and not allowing the guarantee are described in the [International Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc9_011.htm). - For more information on the inquiry process, see [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). | | | --- | | Guaranteed date-certain delivery with Priority Mail Express International is only available for the above [specific destination countries](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#destinations). This guaranteed date-certain delivery is **not eligible** for other international mail classes. | **Locating the GDD** You can locate the Guaranteed Delivery Date (GDD) on the following for your PMEI-DC item: | | | | --- | --- | | - Postage receipts
- Mailing/shipping labels | - Customs forms | You may have received one or more of the above depending on where the item was mailed from. | | | | --- | --- | | ****Where was the PMEI-DC item mailed?**** | ****What receipt should you have?**** | | **Local Post Office** | - [A postage receipt listing the GDD](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/international/retail_receipt_pmei-gdd.jpg)
- Sender's copy of PS Form 2976-B ( [2976-B may have the scheduled delivery date entered in the box](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#customs_form_2976b)) | | **Contract Postal Unit**
**(** **Mail deposited at a CPU after the last collection of the day will be processed on the following postal business day.)** | - Sender's copy of PS Form 2976-B with the scheduled amount of days | | **Click-N-Ship®**
**(The Click-N-Ship item must be mailed from the Post Office to get a Guaranteed Delivery Date)** | - A postage receipt printout listing the GDD
- Copy of online customs form | | **Collection Box**
**(** **Mail deposited in a Collection Box after the last collection of the day will be processed on the following postal business day.)** | - Copy of online customs form with the scheduled amount of days will be mailed to you after the contents of the Collection Box are scanned into the system | | **Additional information on PS Form 2976-B**
Look for the shaded box labeled **Scheduled Delivery Date** on the right side of the [PS Form 2976-B](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976b.pdf) (Priority Mail Express International Shipping Label and Customs Form). It will have either:
- The **Guaranteed Delivery Date or**
- The **number of scheduled postal business days** for the guaranteed delivery

- For example, if the delivery of an item is guaranteed to arrive in 5 postal business days, the clerk will mark "5" in this box.
- If number of days is entered, this number is **not calendar days**. This excludes the time that spans a weekend or holiday in the US or destination country. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#top) **Extra Services** **Insurance** Both PMEI-DC shipments and Priority Mail Express International (PMEI) shipments containing _merchandise_ are insured against loss, damage, or missing contents up to $200 at no additional charge. Shipments containing _nonnegotiable documents_ are insured against loss, damage, or missing contents up to $100 at no additional charge. - Additional _merchandise_ insurance coverage up to $5,000 may be purchased. Coverage for _documents_ beyond $100 is not available. - Coverage is limited to the actual value of the shipment. - Insurance availability varies by country, content, and value. See the [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) for availability and insurance limits. **Tracking** PMEI-DC items include USPS Tracking. See [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) for more information on the service. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC) URL Name Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC [International](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFwGAM/international)[International Mail Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGaGAM/international-mail-services) ## Related Articles - [Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services) - [Money Orders - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) - [What is Priority Mail Express International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International) - [Priority Mail Express® Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC)

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FClick-N-Ship-The-Basics) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq000000121NAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics) ## Click-N-Ship® - The Basics ## Information on requirements, use and benefits of Click-N-Ship®. Links to other FAQ articles with additional information regarding specific functions of Click-N-Ship. - Feb 4, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007500 Customer Information **Overview** Click-N-Ship® (CNS) is a convenient application for residential and small business customers to create postage labels online. Easily print domestic and international shipping labels. With just a few clicks, you can set shipping preferences and print postage labels from your home or office. All you need to get started is a [free USPS.com residential or business account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/&_gl=1*1stq85r*_ga*MTkwOTc2MDUxMi4xNjU4MjQ1NTAz*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTY2MjA0Nzc2Mi4yMS4xLjE2NjIwNDc4NzEuMC4wLjA.), computer and standard printer. If you do not have a printer available, check out our [Label Broker®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) option which allows to print your CNS labels at the Post Office™. To get started using Click-N-Ship, create a new or sign into an existing [USPS.com® business account or personal account](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=GSS&appURL=https://cnsb.usps.com) and visit [cnsb.usps.com](https://cnsb.usps.com/). After signing into your account, you will be able to print and pay for shipping labels, schedule a pickup, create an address book and more. **Benefits** - Save trips to the Post Office™ - Click-N-Ship allows you to print labels and pay for postage using your credit card from home or the office - Save time looking up recipients - Click-N-Ship has a built in Address Book that saves the information of the people to whom you ship - Maintain a record of your shipments - Shipping History provides a detailed history of both your domestic and international transactions and paid-online shipping labels - Easy Shipping - You can request next day pickup of your items at no charge - Notify your customers the package is on its way - Click-N-Ship allows you to send Email Ship Notifications to recipients of the mailpiece label number and shipping date. (domestic mail only) - USPS Tracking® (typically an additional charge) is included with the service if your Priority Mail shipment is sent using Click-N-Ship. Not available to all APO/FPO/DPO destination ZIP Codes. | | | --- | | For additional guidance, see the:
- _[Click-N-Ship Label Creation User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf)_
- [_Click-N-Ship Preferences User Guide, January 2026_](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Preferences.pdf)
- _[MFA for Click-N-Ship Customer User Guide, December 2025.pdf](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/MFA/12.3.25_MFA%20Customer%20User%20Guide.pdf "MFA for Click-N-Ship Customer User Guide")_ | Available Products - Domestic and International Services **Products available with Click-N-Ship®** First-Class Mail® Letter Priority Mail Express® Priority Mail® Priority Mail Cubic USPS Ground Advantage® USPS Ground Advantage Cubic First-Class Mail® Large Envelope Parcel Select® Priority Mail Express International® Priority Mail International® First-Class Package International Service® **For information on additional postage options, please [click here](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm).** Additional eligible and ineligible products **Additional products available only for [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) business account customers** USPS Connect® Local USPS Connect Regional USPS Connect Local Mail **For information on additional postage options, please [click here](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm).** **Ineligible for Click-N-Ship®** First-Class Mail International® [Package Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Package-Services) **See [PC Postage®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Should-I-Mail-Items-Using-PC-Postage) vendors for additional postage options.** New and Improved Landing Page The new and improved Click-N-Ship® Landing Page (homepage) provides the ability to quickly access key features such as creating new labels, searching for labels in their Shipping History, and other personalized services and resources available depending on the account type. The [Click-N-Ship Landing Page User Guide](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Landing%20Page.pdf) will help you navigate the new landing page step-by-step. **Creating and Printing Labels** The Click-N-Ship® application allows business and personal account customers to more efficiently create, print and pay for shipping and return labels. Creating Labels The _[Click-N-Ship Label Creation User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf)_ will help you get started creating labels. | | | --- | | **Note:** Business Account customers are now required to enter a package weight for Flat Rate packaging when creating your online label. | Click on a link below to open that section of the user guide. Below are the different label creation methods offered in Click-N-Ship: 1. [Creating a single domestic label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=4) (Classic View) 2. [Creating a single international label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=50) (Classic View) 3. [Creating a single domestic label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=79) (Quick-Flow View) 4. [Creating a single international label](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=113) (Quick-Flow View) 5. [Creating labels using a USB scale](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=233) 6. [Creating a batch of labels](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=140) 7. [Importing multiple labels (up to 1000) via file upload](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=149) 8. [Creating multiple labels in Label Manager by starting from your Address Book](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=174) 9. [Creating a single drop shipment](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=190) (Business Users Only) | | | --- | | **Note:** Your online label cannot be altered and must be used as it was printed.
- If you find an error in your label, print a new label with the correct address and request a refund.
- Any item with a manually-altered online label will be returned to you. | Naming and Searching Batches of Labels You can name batches of labels during the **Create a Batch** and **Import Labels** label creation processes. Naming a batch allows you to see the associated Batch Name and Batch Symbol throughout the entire multi-label creation and payment process once the batch has been saved to Label Manager. You can also Filter Search by Batch Name in Shipping History (Order History). Priority Mail Open & Distribute (PMOD) Labels Business users can now utilize a new Open and Distribute drop shipment feature in Click-N-Ship to create a container label with the PMOD Service via single label creation. To learn more about creating PMOD labels, refer to the [Single Label Creation User Guide](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf#page=213) or to the article [What is Open and Distribute Service?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Open-and-Distribute-Service). **Extra Services listed will vary depending on the service and package type selected. Extra Services offered could include:** - Additional Insurance - [USPS Label Delivery Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Label-Delivery-Service) - [Create a Return Label](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics#ReturnsLabels) - Hide Postage on Label - Signature Services: [Signature Confirmation](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Signature-Confirmation), [Adult Signature Restricted Delivery 21 or Older](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Adult-Signature-Restricted-Delivery), [Adult Signature 21 or Older Required](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Adult-Signature-Required) **Electronic Signature Online - Opt Out Option for Shippers** [USPS Electronic Signature Online®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Electronic-Signature-Online) (ESOL**®**) is a feature that allows recipients to electronically sign for certain items that require signature for delivery. USPS ESOL is only available to residential Informed Delivery® (ID) customers that opt in to this service. Shippers can opt out of the ESOL service option and require a physical signature, by following the steps below when creating a label: 1. Once on **Step 4: Select Extra Services** of the single label flow, refer to the **Signature Services** section and select **Signature Confirmation™**. ![Screenshot of Signature Confirmation options](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq000000121N&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq000003V9Jp) For eligible mail pieces: - If you do not want to allow for an ESOL, select the **Do Not Allow Electronic Signature** checkbox. - If you do want to allow for an ESOL, uncheck the **Do Not Allow Electronic Signature** checkbox. ![Screenshot of Electronic Signature Online Opt Out for Signature Confirmation on a Priority Mail Express](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq000000121N&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000ZZaFo) 2. To receive a copy of the signature, track your mail item on [USPS.com](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input?_gl=1*d24xnf*_gcl_au*MTAzMTgxMjAxNC4xNzI2NTg2NTAy*_ga*ODg4MTgyODI0LjE3MjY1MTE3NzA.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcyODQ4OTAzNi41Ni4xLjE3Mjg0OTE3MjguMC4wLjA.) then select Proof of Delivery. Follow the provided directions to receive an email with the delivery details after the item has been delivered. | | | --- | | For guidance on creating labels, including how to add extra services or shipping to an available [USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker), see _[Click-N-Ship Label Creation User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf)_
You can also automatically create shipping for customers by connecting your eCommerce systems to Click-N-Ship® Direct Connect. For additional guidance, see the [_Click-N-Ship®: Direct Connect User Guide, January 2026_](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Direct%20Connect.pdf). | Printing Labels Once you have created your Click-N-Ship label, it will show in your Label Cart. You will have the following options to print your label(s): - Print your labels at home Save the labels as a PDF and print now or later - Have your local Post Office print your labels by selecting the [Label Broker® Feature](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) | | | --- | | For additional guidance, see _[Click-N-Ship Label Cart User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Cart.pdf)_. Refer to the last section, titled, "All Actions within Payment Confirmation Page". | | | | --- | | **No access to a printer?**
Check out the [Label Broker®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) feature with the Click-N-Ship application. You can pay for your shipping label using the Click-N-Ship application and select the "Print Labels Later at the Post Office" option. A QR code will be emailed to you to take to a Post Office™ location with your package. The Retail employee at the Post Office location will scan the code, print the label, affix it to the package and induct it into the system for processing. See the article [Label Broker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) for more information. | Before you print What kind of printer and paper should I use to print CNS labels? - Laser printer. - High quality ink jet printer. For ink jet printers, 600 dots-per-inch (DPI) is recommended. - Self-adhesive labels recommended. - Plain white paper is acceptable. Do not tape over the "Postal Use" area on labels or cover barcodes. Tape makes it difficult for processing equipment to read the labels. - Online labels should print to be approximately 4" x 6". If the label is too small, you may need to adjust your desktop font size or display settings, as they may not be readable by the scanning equipment. Computer settings and required applications - First-time users may see a pop-up screen instructing you to install Adobe Reader software. This download must be installed for the label to print. The link to download the latest Adobe Reader version is available on the Payment Confirmation page. - To ensure proper printing of labels, please ensure that you have the 'block pop-ups' setting disabled from your browser and/or device. If this setting is not disabled, Click-N-Ship will notify you via a warning message via the Payment Confirmation page (see below): **Payment Confirmation Page (Desktop View)** ![Screenshot of the CNS Payment Confirmation Page, Desktop view. Focus is on a pale yellow message box with a caution symbol. The message is "We have detected that you have a pop-up blocker enabled, which may interfere with printing labels successfully. Please disable your pop-up blocker in your browser settings if you are having issue printing a label."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/7.15.25_CNS%20Pop-Up%20Notification_Desktop.jpg) **Payment Confirmation Page (Mobile View)** ![Screenshot of the CNS Payment Confirmation Page, Mobile view. Focus is on a pale yellow message box with a caution symbol. The message is "We have detected that you have a pop-up blocker enabled, which may interfere with printing labels successfully. Please disable your pop-up blocker in your browser settings if you are having issue printing a label."](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/7.15.25_CNS%20Pop-Up%20Notification_Mobile.jpg) **Warning Message:** "We have detected that you have a pop-up blocker enabled, which may interfere with printing labels successfully. Please disable your pop-up blocker in your browser settings if you are having issue printing a label." Printing two labels per page This feature only applies to labels that do not include a customs form. Check the radio button next to the print option, **Print labels without receipt (2 per page)** to print two (2) upright (2-Up) labels per piece of paper rather than one (1) label and the receipt. - Labels will print as two (2) 4 inch by 6½ inch Click-N-Ship® Domestic labels on one piece of self-adhesive 8½ inches by 11 inches medium paper. - Customers will have access to all of the label information online through the following options: - To access the information that would typically be printed on a receipt, customers should review the information provided on the Print Confirmation page (printer friendly option as well), or - Select the _**Shipping History**_ tab on the Click-N-Ship toolbar to review all of the information. Additional information - It is important that you mail your item on the Shipping Date you selected during the label generation process as an electronic record is automatically created for that label on that specific date. Labels with "stale" Shipping Dates (i.e. mailed after the Shipping Date printed on the label) may be returned to you. - It is recommended that you use United States Postal Service® packaging or stickers when using Click-N-Ship labels to clearly indicate the type of service you have purchased. Printing Labels for Domestic, International and APO/FPO/DPO Destinations Printing domestic Click-N-Ship labels - With Priority Mail® service and select services, you may receive [USPS Tracking®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) at no additional charge; [Signature Confirmation™](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Signature-Confirmation) may be available at a lower price. Delivery information is found in your Shipping History or by using our [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) tool at [USPS.com®](https://www.usps.com/welcome.htm). - The Click-N-Ship® Priority Mail Express® label will only print one copy that is attached to the package. - See [above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics#printing_two_labels_per_page) for information on printing 2 labels on a page. This is only available when a customs form is not required. Printing international or military (APO/FPO/DPO) Click-N-Ship labels requiring customs forms Printing an international label is much like printing a domestic label, however there are special considerations due to custom forms requirements. You will have the following options to print your label(s): - Print your labels at home - Save the labels as a PDF and print now or later - Have your local Post Office print your labels by selecting the [Label Broker® Feature](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker) | | | --- | | For additional guidance, see _[Click-N-Ship Label Cart User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Cart.pdf)_. Refer to the last section, titled, "All Actions within Payment Confirmation Page". | **BEFORE YOU PRINT:** - For Priority Mail Express International® and Priority Mail International® services, if there are multiple copies of your electronically generated customs form, they must be inserted in the plastic envelope required for online international services. These plastic envelopes, _PS 2976-E, Customs Declaration Envelope_, the smaller PS 2976-ES, _Small Customs Declaration Envelope_, and shipping supplies are available free of charge at your local Post Office™ location, from the Packing Supply Center, or online at [The Postal Store®](https://store.usps.com/store/home). - **Note:** The Packing Supply Center can be reached via phone at **1-800-610-8734.** - When printing certain labels, including PS Form 2976, an envelope (PS Form 2976-E/ES) is not required. You need only apply the label directly to the package or envelope. Here are examples of products that include PS Form 2976: - Certain Priority Mail International® labels - First-Class Package International Service® labels - Military / US Possessions, Territories, and Freely Associated States (PTFAS) labels - As described [above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics#printing_two_labels_per_page), more than 2 labels **cannot** be printed on a single page if your label requires a customs form to be printed out. - **NOTE**: Letters & Flats stamps can only be printed using the 6 x 4 print option. You cannot print a SCAN form or use the USPS Label Broker print option for your stamps from the Payment Confirmation page. Affixing Labels to Packages and to Letters & Flats (Large Envelopes) Packages Labels must be fully placed on the address side of the package and not folded over package edges. Use clear shipping tape to securely affix labels on all four sides. Do not tape over the "Postal Use" area on Priority Mail Express® labels or the barcodes. Do not bend, fold, or overlap labels. Letters and Flats (Large Envelopes) Make sure the addresses are parallel to the longest side of the letter or flat. Place the label on the upper right corner of the letter or flat. For letters, make sure the bottom of the delivery address is at least 5/8" from the bottom of the envelope. | | | --- | | **Example of Label Placement on Envelope**
![Diagram of an envelope with "SAMPLE - DO NOT MAIL" written diagnollay across the envelope. At the upper right-hand corner of the envelope is the USPS Eagle logo, a QR Code, and the text "08/21/2025 U.S. POSTAGE $2.17 LFP". Above the delivery address just below the center of the envelope is a barcode composed of a series of lines.](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq000000121N&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMeq00000ZXBmw) | - As described [above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics#printing_two_labels_per_page), more than 2 labels **cannot** be printed on a single page if your label requires a customs form to be printed out. What if I accidentally printed a second (duplicate) label? Since you were only charged once for postage, you should discard the duplicate label. The postage is linked to the label number, which is unique and can be used only once. The label number will appear in a duplicate postage list in the United States Postal Service® database that is monitored by the Postal Inspection Service. If a customer intentionally uses a duplicate label, they will be liable for fraud. Creating Return Labels Business Shippers can generate return labels for outgoing shipments to their customers. Creating a return label is free and you, the shipper, will only be charged fees once the return label has been used. A returns label cannot be created without an associated outgoing shipping label purchased through Click-N-Ship**®**. Once a customer uses the Return Label, you will receive an email notifying you that a charge was made and the label will appear in your Shipping Cart. You will need to log into your Click-N-Ship account to pay for the used Return Label. Return labels must be used within 30 days of the ship date of the label used to ship the item(s) to the recipient. | | | | --- | --- | | For additional guidance, see the_[Click-N-Ship Label Creation User Guide, January 2026.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20Label%20Creation.pdf)_ Refer to the section titled, "Create a Return Label".

The remaining labels you do not wish to refund should be scanned individually. For more information on Click-N-Ship SCAN forms, please see [Click-N-Ship® SCAN Form](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Click-N-Ship-The-Basics#click-n-ship_scan_form). Refund timeframe Click-N-Ship® labels are eligible for refunds within 30 days of the label creation date. To request a refund, log in to Click-N-Ship and view your Shipping History. Labels are eligible for refunds if: - A barcode scan event does not show up in our system - A refund has not been previously requested or processed for the label If it has been more than 30 days from the label creation date, but less than 60 days, you can initiate a refund by e-mailing the Click-N-Ship Help Desk at [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/s/click-n-ship-inquiry). Include the following information in your e-mail: - Username - Click-N-Ship account number - Label number - Transaction number and date Requests for refunds will not be granted after sixty (60) calendar days (2 credit card billing cycles) have elapsed from the date the label was created. The refund request will be verified and processed, and your original payment method will be credited if approved. Posting to your account is dependent upon your financial institution. You will receive an email notification after you request your refund and once your refund request has been approved or denied. - Refund requests for Click-N-Ship labels will process 14 days from the date of the refund request. Refunds at the Post Office - Priority Mail Express Service Failure and Signature Confirmation **Priority Mail Express® service refund** Click-N-Ship customers are eligible for a service related refund if Priority Mail Express was purchased and is listed on the Label. - You may go to a Post Office™ to request a service related refund. - You may request a service refund for your Priority Mail Express® label by viewing the Label Details for that label and clicking "Request a Service Refund" or by visiting the [Service Refund](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm) application and entering in the label number for the label you want to request a service refund for. - The service commitment for Priority Mail Express varies based on the time the item is mailed, where it is deposited and the destination. **Signature Confirmation™ refund** - The refund process for Signature Confirmation™ fees for online labels can be requested at your local Post Office™. - A Refund for a Signature Confirmation™ service for online labels can be requested through Click-N-Ship by viewing the Label Details for that label and clicking "Request a Service Refund" or by visiting the [Service Refund](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm) application and entering in the label number for the label you want to request a service refund for. How to request a refund online There are two ways to request a refund through the desktop site: either from Shipping History within the Order Details page or from Shipping History within the Label Details page. Refunds can also be made from your mobile device. In both cases, you must first locate the specific label you would like to request a refund for on the Shipping History tab. Select **Get PDF**. | Click-N-Ship SCAN Form for Shipments with Multiple Packages A Shipment Confirmation Acceptance Notice (SCAN) Form (PS Form 5630) allows customers to see that the shipment containing their package has been received by USPS and the package is pending acceptance. - The SCAN Form features a master barcode that represents all the packages in a shipment; it is scanned when the shipment is received by the U.S. Postal Service®. This single scan enters all of the associated packages into the Postal Service USPS Tracking® database as "Shipment Accepted" and allows both the sender and the recipient to see when the package entered the mailstream. - All Click-N-Ship transactions with two or more labels will automatically receive a SCAN Form which can be printed from the Payment Confirmation Page. - The SCAN Form is designed to better meet customer needs regarding tracking and visibility. It is important to note that items with online postage labels will show only the "Shipment Accepted" event scan. - With this feature, mailers will have a complete online solution for shipping expedited packages - online labels with postage, free packaging, free USPS Tracking service, free Package Pickup service, and the ability to validate shipment acceptance. **Click-N-Ship users can create, manage, print and reprint SCAN Forms from the SCAN Forms tab.** - SCAN Forms must be printed by 11:59pm CST on the Ship Date. SCAN Forms can't be printed after the Ship Date. - Users will have up until 11:59pm CST of the label(s) Ship Date to create a new SCAN Form and can manually add labels with the same Ship Date and ship from ZIP Code. - Only new SCAN Forms after 1/19/25 will be able to be viewed. SCAN Forms created before 1/19/25 will not be available on the Recent SCAN Forms tab. **To proceed with creating the new SCAN Form, users will be required to select the checkbox indicating that "SCAN Form labels are nonrefundable if they are not shipped with the SCAN form and that no changes can be made to the SCAN Form after printing".** | | | --- | | For additional guidance on using SCAN Forms, see [Click-N-Ship SCAN Forms User Guide, January 2026](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/ClickNShip/Click-N-Ship%20-%20SCAN%20Forms.pdf).

Laser printer or high-quality ink jet printer using black ink - Labels can be printed on 8½" x 11" white paper - Labels must be fully placed on the address side of the package and not folded over package edges. Use clear shipping tape to securely affix labels on all four sides. Do not tape over the "Postal Use" area on Priority Mail Express® labels or the barcodes. - You can also purchase Self-Adhesive Labels. - For ink jet printers, 600 dots-per-inch (DPI) are recommended. - Our scanning equipment is not capable of reliably reading labels generated and printed using low quality Ink Jet or dot matrix printers. - **A printer is not required** if selecting the [Label Broker® option](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Label-Broker). - Mozilla Firefox - Google Chrome - Edge - Safari Customers may require a first-time only download of Adobe Acrobat Reader, available at [https://www.adobe.com](https://www.adobe.com/). Multifactor Authentication for Click-N-Ship Please see [Multifactor Authentication (MFA)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Multifactor-Authentication-MFA) for information on the Multifactor Authentication process. Please see [_MFA for CNS Customer User Guide_](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/MFA/12.3.25_MFA%20Customer%20User%20Guide.pdf "MFA for CNS Customer User Guide") for help setting up or modifying your MFA for Click-N-Ship. Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000oBJAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding) ## Extended Mail Forwarding ## Extended Mail Forwarding helps to ensure that customers receive all of their mail by allowing them to request an extension of their Change of Address request for a fee. - Sep 23, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006086 Customer Information [**What is Extended Mail Forwarding?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#what_is_Extended_Mail_Forwarding) [**Cost**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#cost) [**Availability**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#availability) [**Where to sign up**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#where_to_sign_up) [**Signing up after Change of Address**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#signing_up_after_COA) [**Changing or canceling a request**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#changing_or_canceling_a_request) [**Extending duration if signed up for 6 months**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#extending_duration_if_signed_up_for_6_months) [**Signing up after COA request expiration**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#signing_up_after_COA_request_expiration) [**Eligible mail classes**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#eligible_mail_classes) [**Contact for assistance**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#contact_for_assistance) # **What is Extended Mail Forwarding?** Extended Mail Forwarding provides customers the ability to extend the forwarding of their mail in 6-, 12-, or 18-month increments, not to exceed a total of 18 months of extended forwarding time. **Extended Mail Forwarding is only available for permanent Change of Address**. These extensions are in addition to the initial Change of Address forwarding time period of 12 months. # **How much does Extended Mail Forwarding cost?** When you first sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding, you will have the option to extend at the following price points: - $24.50 for six (6) months - $36.50 for twelve (12) months - $48.50 for eighteen (18) months If you only sign up for 6 or 12 months initially, you will have the option to purchase additional extensions in 6-month increments at $24.50 each, not to exceed 18 months total. # **Who can sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding?** Extended Mail Forwarding is available to all customers requesting a permanent, domestic Change of Address request. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#top) **Where and when can I sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding?** Customers can purchase Extended Mail Forwarding when submitting their initial Change of Address (COA) request at [moversguide.usps.com](https://moversguide.usps.com/) on USPS.com. You will receive an email confirmation of the Extended Mail Forwarding service purchase from [AddressChange@usps.gov](mailto:AddressChange@usps.gov). Customers with an existing, active permanent COA request can purchase Extended Mail Forwarding using the COA confirmation code and their new ZIP Code™: - At Post Office™ retail windows - Where available, at Self-Service Kiosks in retail lobbies - Via [managemymove.usps.com](http://managemymove.usps.com/) on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) See [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) for more information on locating the confirmation code. If the confirmation code is not available, visit a Post Office retail window for assistance. **Note**: An email will be sent to you at the 11th month mark of your COA request reminding you of your option to extend your forwarding. | | | --- | | ![Image of Extended Mail Forwarding button on Manage My Move](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/Extended_Mail_Forwarding/7.31.20_EMF%20Button.jpg)
_**Click image to increase size.**_ | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#top) **Will I receive a reminder to sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding if I do not sign up when I submit my COA request?** Yes. At the 11th month mark of your COA request, a reminder email will be sent to you with the offer for Extended Mail Forwarding. # **If I sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding, can I change or cancel the request? Do I get a refund if I cancel?** No, you **cannot change** your Extended Mail Forwarding request. Once your forwarding begins, your mail will be forwarded according to the duration you selected. You **can cancel** your COA and that will also cancel your Extended Mail Forwarding request. If canceled, you are not entitled to a refund. # **If I sign up for 6 months, but decide I need more time, can I extend the duration?** Yes, you can sign up for another 6 month interval at the price provided for 6 months. You can repeat this request until you have reached an 18 month extension. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#top) # **If my Change of Address request expires, can I still sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding?** No, once a Change of Address request has expired, you will not be allowed to sign up for Extended Mail Forwarding. **What classes of mail are included with Extended Mail Forwarding?** The following classes of mail will continue to be forwarded with Extended Mail Forwarding: - First-Class Mail® Service - USPS Ground Advantage® - Commercial items - Priority Mail® Service **If I have any issues signing up, who can I contact for assistance?** You can email [changeofaddress@usps.gov](mailto:changeofaddress@usps.gov). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Extended-Mail-Forwarding#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Extended Mail Forwarding URL Name Extended-Mail-Forwarding Moving, Change of Address and Forwarding Mail ## Related Articles - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Mail Forwarding Options](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Forwarding-Options) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [PO Box™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? [Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FOnline-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000012W1AAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services) ## Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services ## You can use the Service Refund application to request refunds for service failures related to Priority Mail Express® and extra services. - Feb 10, 2026 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006185 Customer Information **What are the Priority Mail Express® (PME) services that are available for online service refund requests?** You can use the Service Refund application to request refunds for the following PME service failures: | | | | --- | --- | | **Primary Product / Service** | **Extra Service / Value Added Service** | | Priority Mail Express® | - Collect on Delivery
- Sunday / Holiday Delivery
- Army Post Office (APO) / Fleet Post Office (FPO) / Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) PME Delivery | **What are the USPS Extra Service(s) that are available for online service refund requests?** You can request refunds for the following extra services: | | | | --- | --- | | **Primary Product/Service** | **Excluding** | | Certified Mail® | - Certified Mail Restricted Delivery | | Return Receipt and Return Receipt Electronic | | | Signature Confirmation™ | - Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery | | USPS Tracking®
(applies to USPS Marketing Mail® parcels only) | | **What other Postal services can I request a refund online for?** You can find information such as eligibility requirements and how to request a refund online for the services below. You will need to log into your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account to complete these refund processes. | | | | --- | --- | | - [Click-N-Ship®](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#cns)
- [EDDM Retail® orders not yet dropped off](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#eddm)
- [PO Boxes™](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics) | - [Premium Forwarding Service Residential®](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#pfs-r)
- [USPS Package Intercept®](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#intercept)
- [USPS Pickup On Demand®](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#pickup) | | Although you cannot request online refunds for the services below, information on requesting refunds by phone, at a Post Office™, or other means is linked to below:
- [Change of Address](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#coa)
- [EDDM Retail orders placed online & paid for at the Post Office](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#eddm)
- [EDDM Retail orders dropped at the Post Office](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#eddm)
- [PO Boxes if you are managing your PO Box at a local Post Office](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#poboxes)
- [Postal Store Returns & Exchanges](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#store) | **Can refund requests for Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI) be submitted online?** Yes. See [International Mail Postage Refunds](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Mail-Postage-Refunds) for more information on initiating PMEI inquiries and filing requests for refunds. **How can I access the USPS Online Refund application to request a PME or Extra Service refund?** The USPS Online Refund application is available to registered USPS.com customers. You may register online at [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/ "Welcome | USPS"). Once on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), navigate to the " [Help](https://www.usps.com/help/welcome.htm "Help | USPS")" section and select " [Request a Refund](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm "Request a Domestic Refund | USPS")". **May I submit multiple refund requests at a single time?** Yes. Consumers using [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) may submit up to five tracking numbers in a single refund request. **How do I enter multiple tracking numbers in the USPS Online Refunds application?** Each tracking number must be separated by: - a comma (,) or - semicolon (;) or - or press the ENTER key after each tracking number **How can commercial customers access the USPS Online Refund application to request a PME or Extra Service refund?** Commercial customers need to register through the Business Customer Gateway (BCG) at [https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin "USPS Business Customer Gateway"). **What do I need to request a refund online for PME or an Extra Service?** Commercial customers should log into their BCG account and navigate to the Online Refunds application. Within the application, they will need the Intelligent Mail® package barcode (IMpb®) tracking number in addition to the mailing date associated with the mailpiece they wish to file a refund request for. Consumer customers should log into their [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account and navigate to the Online Refunds application. They will need tracking numbers for which they wish to file a refund request for. The refund request must also meet the filing time limits required by the Postal Service. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services#top) **What is the time limit for requesting a PME service refund?** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Timeline by Mail Type or Service** | **When to File** | | **No Sooner Than** | **No Later Than** | | Priority Mail Express Delivery
Without an Extra Service | 2 days (after the date of mailing) | 30 days (after the date of mailing) | | Priority Mail Express® Delivery
With Extra Service(s) | 30 days (after the date of mailing) | 60 days (after the date of mailing) | **What is the time limit for requesting an Extra Service refund?** Refunds for Extra Service failures may be requested no sooner than 30 days from the date of mailing and no later than 60 days after the date of mailing. **When should I file for a refund for a PME piece that included an Extra Service?** You may file a refund request for a PME piece with an Extra Service associated no sooner than 30 days after the mailing date and no later than 60 days after the mailing date. **Why do I receive an error message when I submit my refund request for Certified Mail 30 days after the mailing date?** USPS is still working to complete its delivery obligation. You may resubmit your refund request in 30 days after the date that you first entered the tracking number into the Online Refunds application. **What does it mean when I receive an error message indicating that "two refund requests for one tracking number is not permitted"?** It means the tracking number you are using to submit has already been submitted for a refund request and therefore cannot be submitted again. **Submit all claims to a particular tracking number at one time.** **How will I know if my refund request submitted through BCG was received?** You will see a brief message indicating your file has been successfully uploaded. A confirmation email will be sent to the email address associated with your USPS BCG account providing you a confirmation number, the date of your request, and a hyperlink to connect you to BCG site for status view. **How will I know if my refund request submitted through [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) was received?** You will see a confirmation page indicating you have successfully submitted your refund request. A confirmation email will also be sent to the email address associated with your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account providing you a confirmation number, date of request, tracking numbers submitted, and a hyperlink to connect you to [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) for the status of your request. **How long does it take to process the payment once a request has been filed online?** Once all required documentation is received and the refund request is determined as eligible (which may take several days), the payment will be processed immediately. **How can I check the status of my refund request?** You may use the hyperlink provided in the confirmation email to log into your File Upload History page at any time to see the status of your refund request. You will be able to view the refund status shown below: | | | | --- | --- | | **Refund Request Status** | **Dispute Status** | | Under Review | Dispute Under Review | | Partially Approved | Dispute Approved | | Approved | Dispute Denied | | Denied | Dispute Paid | | Processing Payment | Dispute Paid | | Paid | Dispute Paid | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services#top) **Why does the Online Refunds application ask me to confirm my address when I submit a refund request?** The application is designed to have the consumer confirm that his/her mailing address is accurate because USPS wants to ensure the refund check is mailed to the correct address upon approval of your refund request. **Why am I asked to provide the mailing date of my items after I have submitted my refund request?** In some situations, USPS needs additional information, such as the mailing date from the USPS retail receipt or the electronic receipt, to determine a refund eligibility. **How to appeal a denied refund** If your request is denied, the reason for the denial will be indicated. The Online Refunds application will allow you to navigate to the dispute page of the application to appeal and provide additional documentation for further consideration **.** **Under what circumstances will I not be able to appeal a denial?** You will not be able to dispute under these circumstances: - Tracking number has been approved for a refund. - A refund request has already been submitted for the same tracking number. - If a proof of purchase such as a USPS retail receipt or electronic receipt cannot be provided. **If I file my service refund request online, what form of payment will I receive?** Payment for PME or Extra Service refunds will be provided in the following manner: - Consumers: Payment will be received in the form of a check. - Click-N-Ship® Customers: Payment will be received via the original form of payment (PayPal®[1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services#PayPal) or credit card). - Commercial Customers: Payment will be received in the form of a check. - USPS Corporate Account Customers: Payment will be received in the form of a check. **If I have paid for the postage at a USPS® Retail location and go back to file my service refund request, what form of payment will I receive?** Payment for PME or Extra Service refunds will be provided in the following manner after a completed PS Form 3533, _Application for Refund of Fees, Products and Withdrawal of Customer Accounts,_ is submitted and the refund request is approved: - If the refund is $25 or less, you will receive cash. - If the refund is over $25, you will be given a no-fee money order. - If the refund is over $1,000, a check will be mailed to you from the San Mateo Accounting Service Center. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Online-Refunds-for-Priority-Mail-Express-and-Extra-Services#top) 1PayPal® is a registered trademark of PayPal®, Inc. Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Online Refunds for Priority Mail Express® and Extra Services
