United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
Vietnam | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Electronic-USPS-Delivery-Confirmation-International#top) **What other standards apply to E-USPS DELCON INTL® service?** **The following additional standards apply to E-USPS DELCON INTL® service:** - E-USPS DELCON INTL® service may not be combined with any other international extra services. - No refunds, inquiries, or claims are offered, and indemnity is not provided. - Postage Payment Methods - Mailers may pay for E-USPS DELCON INTL® items by using postage validation imprinter (PVI) labels, postage meter imprint, USPS-approved PC Postage, Click-N-Ship postage, or permit imprint. - Price Eligibility - Only items paid with USPS-approved PC Postage (with the exception of Click-N-Ship) or permit imprint are eligible for the applicable Commercial Base or Commercial Plus price for the postage portion of the mail piece. - Electronically generated customs forms that are not generated with PC Postage or a permit imprint (for example, forms electronically generated by Webtools or Click-N-Ship service) are charged the retail price. ## Certificate of Mailing - The Basics

## Certificate of Mailing general information, fees, eligible mail classes and services, and links to sample forms for the service.      - Jan 9, 2026 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006232

Customer Information

**What is a Certificate of Mailing?**

A **_Certificate Of Mailing_** provides evidence that you sent an item when you say you did. This official record only shows the date your mail was accepted. It does not provide record of delivery or insure items against loss.

The Postal Service® does not keep copies of Certificate of Mailing receipts. It is therefore critical that you retain your receipt. No signature or receipt is obtained when Certificate of Mailing items are delivered to the recipient.

- **No insurance is provided** against loss or damage.
- May be used for domestic or international mail.
- Can be paid for by affixing postage stamps, meter stamps, or permit imprints.

**Does it cost anything?**

Fees vary, depending on whether the Certificate of Mailing is purchased with an Individual Article or an Individual Article with Firm Mailing Book. Fees are in addition to regular postage. For detailed information on fees, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm "USPS.com - Insurance & Extra Services"): (for International prices, go to [https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm "USPS.com - International Shipping Insurance and Extra Services")).

****What are the different Certificates of Mailing – what do they look like?****

**PS Form 3817**

You can view the _Certificate of Mailing_ form online at: [PS Form 3817 (PDF)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3817.pdf "PS Form 3817 - Certificate of Mailing"). This form is used for both domestic and international Certificate of Mailing when the shipment contains one or two items.

**PS Form 3665**

You can view the _Certificate of Mailing - Firm_ form online at: [PS Form 3665 (PDF)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3665.pdf "PS Form 3665 - Certificate of Mailing — Firm (Domestic)"). This form can be used for both domestic and international Certificate of Mailing when the shipment contains three or more items.

In addition to the existing Domestic products, it can be used for unregistered First-Class Mail International, First-Class Package International Service, Free Matter for the Blind, and Airmail M-Bags items.

Information on USPS approval for privately printed or computer-generated forms is included on the PS Form 3665. The approval process for these forms can be found in [DMM 503](http://pe.usps.com/cpim/ftp/manuals/dmm300/503.pdf "Domestic Mail Manual - Extra and Additional Services"), section 5.1.7, _Privately Printed Forms_.

Mailings of fewer than 50 pieces and less than 50 pounds of corresponding articles presented at one time must be presented for mailing to a retail Post Office™, station or branch.

Mailings of 50 pieces or 50 pounds and more presented at one time, hardcopies of PS Form 3665 must be presented to a Post Office Business Mail Entry Unit (BMEU) or authorized Detached-Mail Unit (DMU).

Electronically submitted form PS 3665 with mailings of three pieces or more must be presented to a Post Office BMEU.

**PS Form 3606**

You can view the _Certificate of Bulk Mailing - International_ form online at: [PS Form 3606 (PDF)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3606.pdf "PS Form 3606 - Certificate of Bulk Mailing - International"). This form is used to verify the number of identical-weight pieces in bulk **international** mailings.

**PS Form 3606-D**

You can view the _Certificate of Bulk Mailing - Domestic_ form online at: [PS Form 3606-D (PDF)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3606d.pdf "PS Form 3606 - Certificate of Bulk Mailing - Domestic"). This form is used to verify the number of identical-weight pieces in bulk **domestic** mailings.

For identical-weight mailings of at least 50 pieces or 50 pounds, hardcopies of PS Form 3606-D must be presented to a Post Office BMEU or authorized DMU.

Electronically submitted form PS 3606-D of any number of pieces or weigh in pounds must be presented to a Post Office BMEU.

**Firm Mailing Book**

PS Form 3877 is a multipage bound book that contains individual sheets with space to enter multiple article numbers for accountable mail such as Certified Mail, COD, Priority Mail Express® and insured mail pieces.

- **Note:** the use of the book eliminates the need to generate individual mailing receipts for each article. For listing Certificate of Mailing, please use PS Form 3665 or PS Form 3606-D. For more information on PS Form 3877, please reference [What is a Firm Mailing Book?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Firm-Mailing-Book)

**What Certificate of Mailing Forms Can be Submitted Electronically?**

**Certificates of Mailing can be submitted electronically through Postal Wizard (web-based) or Mail.dat (Presort software required). A [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/eAdmin/view/signin) account is required.**

- **Form 3665** (firm sheet) - Available for 3 or more pieces
- **Form 3606-D** \- Used to specify the number of identical-weight pieces being mailed. Cannot be used as Certificate of Mailing of individual mailpieces or itemized lists.
- **Form 3877 Firm book**\- A multi-page bound book that contains individual sheets with space for multiple article numbers for accountable mail such as Certified Mail, Priority Mail Express, and insured mail. The use of the book eliminates the need to generate individual mailing receipts.

Electronically submitted forms must be paid through an EPS account; charges will be automatically deducted once USPS has approved the mailing.

|     |
| --- |
| For additional information and user guides on electronic submission of bulk Certificates of Mailing please see [https://postalpro.usps.com/com](https://postalpro.usps.com/com). |

**What classes / services can be combined with a Certificate of Mailing?**

Available with

- First-Class Mail®
- Priority Mail® service
- Bound Printed Matter
- Library Mail
- Media Mail®

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000cuvAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Media-Mail-Book-Rate) ## What is Media Mail®? ## Media Mail is for shipping media items containing books, video and sound recordings, printed music, recorded computer-readable media (such as CDs and DVDs). - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006802 Customer Information **Features** - Media Mail® is an economy shipping solution for media items. - Available for sending small and large packages containing books, film, manuscripts, sound and video recordings, and computer media (such as CDs, DVDs, and diskettes). See the [_Domestic Mail Manual_](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/173.htm#ep1113509) for additional details on qualified items. - Media Mail cannot contain advertising. Books shipping as Media Mail may contain incidental book announcements. - Packages can weigh up to 70 lbs. and measure up to 108 inches in combined length and distance around the thickest part. - See [Business Shipping - Rates, Discounts, Services \| USPS](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) for commercial customer information and [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c156) for commercial prices. **Notes** - Video games, computer drives, and digital drives do not qualify for Media Mail prices. - Including items that do not qualify for Media Mail in a package can result in the shipper or recipient paying higher postage rates. - Media Mail is not sealed against postal inspection. Contents can be inspected by the Postal Service to insure pricing eligibility. | | | --- | | For additional information, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm#mediamail](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm#mediamail). For pricing information, see [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c156).
[Library Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Library-Mail-Service-and-Media-Mail-Service) is a related service for eligible organizations, including schools, museums, nonprofits, and other groups.
Businesses looking for information on Media Mail can go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm).
For the full list of Media Mail requirements and allowed items, see the _[Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/173.htm#ep1113500)_. | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is Media Mail®? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000xJRAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/SDR-Value) ## SDR Value ## Chart of SDR values and Registered indemnity rates dating back to 2012. - Dec 31, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007852 Customer Information SDR stands for Special Drawing Rights. This is an exchange rate used to help identify values for international mail in a generally accepted format. The SDR Value amount is entered on PS Form 2976-A (Customs Declaration and Dispatch Note). The retail clerk who accepts a package for which Form 2976-A is needed will be able to assist you in calculating the SDR Value for the item (including using any updated exchange rates that may have occurred). Detailed Information on the exchange rate is below: **INTERNATIONAL CLAIMS USD / SDR CONVERSION RATE CHART** **2026 Rates effective 1/1/2026** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | | **USD TO SDR CONVERSION RATE** | **SDR TO USD CONVERSION RATE** | | **2026** | $1 USD = 0.7463 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3400 USD | | **2025** | $1 USD = 0.7532 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3277 USD | | **2024** | $1 USD = 0.7526 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3288 USD | | **2023** | $1 USD = 0.7341 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3623 USD | | **2022** | $1 USD = 0.6998 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.4290 USD | | **2021** | $1 USD = 0.7242 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3808 USD | | **2020** | $1 USD = 0.7222 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3847 USD | | **2019** | $1 USD = 0.7032 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.4220 USD | | **2018** | $1 USD = 0.7097 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.4091 USD | | **2017** | $1 USD = 0.7159 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.3969 USD | | **2016** | $1 USD = 0.7049 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.4186 USD | | **2015** | $1 USD = 0.6509 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.5364 USD | | **2014** | $1 USD = 0.6580 SDR | 1SDR = $1.5197 USD | | **2013** | $1 USD = 0.6502 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.5379 USD | | **2012** | $1 USD = 0.6339 SDR | 1 SDR = $1.5776 USD | **REGISTERED INDEMNITY RATES** **2026 Rates effective 1/1/2026** | | | | --- | --- | | **2026** | **$40.20** | | **2025** | **$39.83** | | **2024** | **$39.86** | | **2023** | **$40.87** | | **2022** | **$42.87** | | **2021** | [**$41.42**](https://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/2021/pb22565/html/updt_002.htm) | | **2020** | **$41.54** | | **2019** | **$42.66** | | **2018** | **$42.27** | | **2017** | **$41.91** | | **2016** | **$42.56** | | **2015** | **$46.09** | | **2014** | **$45.59** | | **2013** | **$46.14** | | **2012** | **$47.33** | | | | --- | | **CALCULATIONS AND CONVERSIONS** | | The formula is **KG x 4.5 = 4.5KG + 40SDR + POSTAGE = Payable amount in SDRs**.
- Remember to always **convert your postage to SDRs**.
- **If no postage is provided no postage will be indemnified**.
- Compare **Value** vs **. Maximum Indemnity** allowed for the weight.
- If the **Value is more** than the Maximum Indemnity you must **pay the Maximum Indemnity plus postage**.
- If the **Maximum Indemnity is more** than the Value you will **pay the Value plus postage**. | | **To convert SDRs to U.S. Dollars**
- Multiply the Maximum Indemnity or the Value **X** the SDR rate is U.S. Dollars for year of mailing.

**For Example:  An item mailed in 2011 the SDR rate is U.S. Dollars = 1.5346**

You have an item that is 44.5 SDR **multiply** by **1.5346** to get the U.S. Dollar amount (which is $68.28). | | **To convert U.S. Dollars to SDRs**
- Multiply the U.S. Dollars times the SDR rate for the year of mailing.

**For Example: An item mailed in 2011 the U.S. Dollar amount in SDRs = .6516**

You have an item valued at $68.28 **multiply** by 0.6516 = 44.5 SDR | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/SDR-Value#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title SDR Value URL Name SDR-Value [International](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFwGAM/international)[Claim Inquiries](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGdGAM/claim-inquiries) ## Related Articles - [Registered Mail® International](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Registered Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics) - [What is the USPS Mail Recovery Center?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-USPS-Mail-Recovery-Center) - [Postage Stamps - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Postage-Stamps-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading SDR Value

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000xMfAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons) ## What is Free Matter for the Blind or Other Physically Handicapped Persons? ## Information on Free Matter for the Blind, and what items can be mailed using the service. - Dec 31, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007834 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | [**Domestic**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#domestic) | **[International](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#international)** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Domestic** | | [**What is Free Matter for the Blind and Other Physically Handicapped Persons?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#free_matter) | [****How do I become eligible for Free Matter for the Blind?****](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible_persons) | **[What Items Can Be Mailed as Free Matter for the Blind?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible)** | | **[What Items Cannot Be Mailed Using "Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped" Service?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#ineligible)** | [**How Should "Free Matter for the Blind" Be Packaged and Submitted to USPS®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#packaged) | | **What is Free Matter for the Blind and Other Physically Handicapped Persons?** Matter may be sent free of charge if mailed by or for the use of blind or other persons who cannot read or use conventionally printed materials due to a physical handicap. When such matter is mailed domestically, it is not considered any specific class of mail, such as Priority Mail®, Priority Mail Express®, etc. However, the standards for processing, delivering, and handling if undeliverable are the same used for First-Class Mail®. Domestic "Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped" must be mailed by or for blind or other persons who cannot read or use conventionally printed materials due to a physical handicap. That person must also be either an American citizen domiciled abroad or a resident of the United States. That includes territories, insular possessions, and the District of Columbia. The steps to register for the free matter privilege are [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible_persons). Other mailers and shippers that are eligible for mailing and shipping Free Matter include: - Commercial producers of those eligible items when mailed to an eligible person as described above. - Individuals, libraries, and other noncommercial organizations eligible to participate in the U.S. Library of Congress National Library Service for the Blind and Print Disabled (NLS) when mailed to persons and other organizations serving people meeting the eligibility requirement. These libraries and other noncommercial organizations can also mail and ship among themselves. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) **How do I become eligible for Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped?** The Postal Service® requires individuals claiming use of the free matter privilege to provide [evidence of eligibility](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/703.htm#ep1113985) to the Post Office that you're mailing from. Certification or evidence of being considered to be blind or having a physical handicap that causes a person to be unable to read or use conventionally printed material is provided by a [certifying authority](https://pe.usps.com/text/DMM300/703.htm#ep1113995), which includes doctors of medicine, ophthalmologists, professional staff of hospitals and public or private welfare agencies, and professional librarians. Eligibility is also met by being a certified participant in the Library of Congress National Library Service for the Blind and Physically Handicapped (NLS). An alternative is that the local postmaster may extend the free matter privilege to a customer based on their personal knowledge of the customer's eligibility. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) **What Items Can Be Mailed as Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped?** The following can be mailed domestic Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped can include: - Reading matter in braille or 14-point or larger sightsaving type and musical scores - Sound reproductions - Paper, records, tapes, and other material for the production of reading matter, musical scores, or sound reproductions - Reproducers or parts of them for sound reproductions - Braille writers, typewriters, educational or other materials or devices, or parts - Must be used for writing by, or designed or adapted for use of, a blind person or a person who has a physical impairment as described [above](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#individuals). Free matter must also be mailed under certain conditions. These conditions include: - The matter may be opened and inspected by the USPS. - Letters sent by blind or other physically handicapped persons must be in Braille, in 14-point or larger type, or in the form of sound recordings. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) **What Items Cannot Be Mailed Using "Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped" Service?** Domestic Free Matter for the Blind or Handicapped cannot include: - Advertising - Letters prepared by sighted individuals to be sent to a blind or other physically handicapped person or empty shipping materials for mailing potential free matter - Hand written letters, or letters printed or typed in font smaller than 14-point, may not be sent free - Any item with a charge, rental, subscription, or other fee that exceeds the actual price of the item [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) ****How Should "Free Matter for the Blind" Be Packaged and Submitted to USPS®?**** | | | | --- | --- | | Items should be packaged in wrappers, in rolls, between cardboard, or in bags, boxes, unsealed envelopes with the flaps tucked in, or containers. Dangerous fasteners may not be used. The articles may also be tied with string or twine in a manner that will permit them to be easily untied. | | Free matter is **not** protected against postal inspection. Packages should be sealed for processing with the understanding that it can be opened and inspected to ensure it meets the eligibility standards. Only envelopes should remain unsealed with the flaps tucked in. | | Free matter must be marked "Free Matter for the Blind" in the upper right corner of the address side of the mailpiece.
USPS recommends that your item bear a "Free Matter for the Blind" label on the address side of the package.
Post Offices do not provide this label. You can click on the image to the right to open the image in a new window by itself and print it out. If that produces a label that's too large for your purpose, try copying the image and pasting it into a word processing or image editing program where you can resize and print it. | ![International Free Matter for the Blind Label - Person walking with White Cane; Envelope floating to right of head](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/free_matter_for_the_blind_label_27123.jpg) | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) Customer Information 2 | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **International** | | [**What can be mailed as international "Free Matter for the Blind?"**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible_items) | [**What mail classes can be marked as "Free Matter for the Blind?"**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible_mail_classes) | [**What extra services can be applied to international "Free Matter for the Blind"?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#eligible_extra_services) | | [**Are any customs forms needed to send Free Matter?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#customs_forms) | [**Preparing International "Free Matter for the Blind" Packages**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#preparing_international_packages) | [**What are my deposit or pickup options for international Free Matter for the Blind?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#deposit_pickup_options) | **What can be mailed as international "Free Matter for the Blind?"** Eligible matter and items in international mail are limited to the following: - Letters bearing writing used by the blind and plates bearing the characters of writing used by the blind - Books, periodicals, literature, and other correspondence - Paper, records, tapes, musical scores, or sound recordings - Equipment or materials of any kind adapted to assist blind persons in overcoming the problems of blindness, including reproducers or parts of them for sound reproductions - Specially adapted CD's - Braille writing equipment, typewriters, educational or other materials or devices, or parts thereof, used for writing by, or designed or adapted for use of a blind person - White canes - Recording equipment | | | --- | | **International Free Matter for the Blind cannot include:**
- Items with the purpose of advertisement.
- Items intended for a profit-making transaction. There should be no charge, rental, subscription, or other fee that exceeds the actual price of the item.
**This list is not inclusive for international free matter, but it does provide guidance on determining ineligible items.** | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) **What mail classes can be marked as "Free Matter for the Blind?"** For International Mail, "Free Matter for the Blind" matter may be entered **free of postage** provided the item is: - Sent to or by: - An organization for the blind (an institution or association serving or officially representing blind persons) or - A blind person (person registered as blind or partially sighted or who meets the World Health Organization's definition of a blind person or a person with low vision) - **Note:** An "organization for the blind" means. - And sent using one of the following eligible international mail classes: | Mail Service | Maximum Weight Limit | | --- | --- | | First-Class Mail International® Letters | 3.5 ounces | | First-Class Mail International® Flats | 15.994 ounces | | First-Class Package International Service®
Priority Mail International® Flat Rate Envelopes
Priority Mail International® Small Flat Rate Boxes | 4 pounds | | Priority Mail International® Parcels
Priority Mail International® Medium Flat Rate Boxes
Priority Mail International® Large Flat Rate Boxes | 15 pounds | | **Note**: If a Priority Mail International parcel exceeds the 15-pound weight limit, the parcel will not be eligible as "Free Matter for the Blind." | **What extra services can be applied to international "Free Matter for the Blind"?** The only applicable Extra Services are: - Registered Mail service for First-Class Mail International items. - Free Matter for the Blind items, even when purchased with Registered Mail service, are **not** sealed against inspection. - Additional merchandise insurance service for Priority Mail International Flat Rate Envelopes and Small Flat Rate Boxes and Priority Mail International parcels up to 15 pounds. Extra Services must be paid by the mailer at the time of mailing. **Are any customs forms needed to send Free Matter?** When required by the mail class, a fully completed electronically generated PS Form 2976 or 2976-A must be affixed to each item. | | | --- | | See [USPS.com® - Customs Forms](https://cfo.usps.com/) to create a customs form or go to [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics) for additional assistance. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) **Preparing International "Free Matter for the Blind" Packages** | | | | --- | --- | | Free matter must be marked both "Free Matter for the Blind" and "Envois pour les Aveugles" (for international mail) in the upper right corner of the address side of the mailpiece. | | In addition to the "Free Matter for the Blind" marking, international items must also bear a copy of the label on the address side of the package.
Post Offices do not provide this label. You can click on the image to the right to open the image in a new window by itself and print it out. If that produces a label that's too large for your purpose, try copying the image and pasting it into a word processing or image editing program where you can resize and print it. | ![International Free Matter for the Blind Label - Person walking with White Cane; Envelope floating to right of head](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/free_matter_for_the_blind_label_27123.jpg) | For any further questions, please contact your local Post Office™ **What are my deposit or pickup options for international Free Matter for the Blind?** Eligible Free Matter for the Blind items bearing a computer-generated customs form with customs data that has been electronically transmitted may be deposited through any of the following methods, provided any extra service fees have been paid by a means other than the use of postage stamps: - In a private mailbox bearing a return address that matches the address at the point of pick up, when the customer or business is known to reside or do business at that location. - At a Postal Service retail counter. - Into a Postal Service lobby drop. - In a collection box. - At a Contract Postal Unit (CPU). - At a USPS Approved Shipper location. **Note**: Mailers can use the above methods to deposit a First-Class Mail International letter-size or flat-size item that both does not exceed 10 ounces in weight and does not exceed one-half inch in thickness. If either is exceeded, the item must be presented to an employee at a Post Office retail service counter. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons#top) Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is Free Matter for the Blind or Other Physically Handicapped Persons? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000xGDAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International) ## Registered Mail® International ## International Registered Mail is a service that provides additional protection and security in dispatch and transfer in the United States. In destination countries, Registered Mail items are handled according to their own internal procedures. - Dec 31, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007507 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[General information](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#General_information)** | **[Insurance or indemnity availability](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#Insurance_or_indemnity_availability)** | **[Eligibility for International Registered Mail service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#Eligibility)** | | **[How much does it cost?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#cost)** | **[Can International Registered Mail items be tracked?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#tracked)** | [**What do Registered Mail forms look like?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#Forms) | **General information** International Registered Mail is a service that provides additional protection and security in dispatch and transfer **in the United States**. Other features include: - A mailing receipt issued by the office of mailing and a delivery record maintained at the office of destination for each registered item. - The recipient's signature is not required in the destination country. - In the United States, Registered Mail service items are handled separately from all other mail and are kept in a secure area with restricted access. In destination countries, Registered Mail items are handled according to their own internal procedures. - When using Registered Mail service, the item must be [presented to a retail employee or a rural carrier](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics). - When [mail is registered internationally and is inbound for the United States](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc7_015.htm), it will be handled in the domestic First-Class Mail mailstream from the point that USPS obtains it until it reaches the local Postal Office responsible for mail delivery. The recipient must sign a delivery receipt at the time of delivery. - **Note**: You can check out the [Individual Country Listings in the IMM](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immctry.htm "Individual Country Listings in the IMM") and/or the [Postage Price Calculator](http://postcalc.usps.com/ "International Postage Price Calculator") for Registered Mail service availability and restrictions. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | International Registered Mail **does NOT** provide: | | - Expedited delivery | - Address information | - Guaranteed delivery time | **Is any insurance or indemnity available for International Registered Mail items?** Yes, indemnity is payable for loss, damage, or missing contents. The maximum indemnity limit for all destination countries changes each January and can be found in the [International Mail Manual (IMM) section 333.2](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc3_009.htm "International Mail Manual (IMM) section 333.2"). As of January 1, 2026, that limit is $40.20. **Regardless of the declared value of a registered item, claims are not paid above the indemnity limit, which USPS does not control.** Also, the extra service fee for international Registered Mail is not reimbursed even if the customer receives a claim payment. - **Note****:** Indemnity limits are much lower for Registered Mail service items than for insured mail. In order to begin an inquiry, which could result in an international claim, follow the steps in [File a USPS Claim: International](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm). Additional information on international inquiries can be found at [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics). | | | --- | | Indemnity may **not** be paid for any of the reasons listed in _[International Mail Manual 932](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc9_006.htm)_. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#top) **International Registered Mail service availability** International Registered Mail service can be purchased for First-Class Mail International items that contain documents only, including [Free Matter for the Blind](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons) items. | International Registered Mail Availability | | --- | | Available Mail Types | **Ineligible Mail Types** | | --- | --- | | First-Class Mail International® items
- Includes [Free Matter for the Blind](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons) items
**Note:**
[Return Receipts](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm) can be purchased for First-Class Mail International letters with Registered Mail service only; See the [Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immctry.htm) for country-specific eligibility. | Priority Mail Express International® items
Priority Mail International® items
M-bag service
First-Class Package International Service® items
- Includes Free Matter for the Blind items | Prohibitions and restrictions vary by destination country. See the [Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immctry.htm) for country-specific information. **How much does it cost?** The price for International Registered Mail is its fee in addition to postage for the mailpiece. See [Notice 123, _Price List_](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c432 "Notice 123, Price List") for the International Registered Mail fee). Regardless of the item's declared value, the maximum amount of indemnity payable for loss, damage, or missing contents is a set fee. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#top) **Can International Registered Mail be tracked?** No. Registered Mail service items cannot be tracked through the mailstream and will not provide updated scans. However, Registered Mail service **does** provide information with a scan showing the delivery status or attempted delivery information which can be retrieved in three ways: 1. Go to [www.usps.com](http://www.usps.com/). Select " [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)." By entering the tracking number shown on the mailing receipt into the lookup field, you can view the delivery status of the article. 2. By calling the USPS Tracking telephone number found at [Contact USPS](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm#kilo). 3. By bulk electronic file transfer for mailers who provide an electronic manifest to the United States Postal Service®. **What do Registered Mail forms and labels look like?** For the Registered Mail Receipt Form, see [PS Form 3806 (Receipt for Registered Mail)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3806.pdf). If mailing three or more Registered Mail items at one time, [PS Form 3877 (Firm Mailing Book For Accountable Mail)](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3877.pdf) may be used. All Registered Mail items of U.S. origin must bear Label 200-N or Label 200. See [IMM 334.13](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc3_010.htm#ep28233). When accepted at a Post Office™, the retail employee will affix the Registered Mail label below to the item: ![Image of red label with the text "Registered Mail" being the largest. The rest of the text as follows: UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICE; REGISTERED MAIL; FOR USE ONLY WITH IMpb SHIPPING LABEL; Label 200-N, January 2014; PSN 7690-17-000-0391](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/registered_mail_label_200-n.jpg) [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Registered Mail® International URL Name What-is-Registered-Mail-International [International](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFwGAM/international)[International Mail Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGaGAM/international-mail-services)[Insurance & Additional Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGcGAM/insurance-additional-services) ## Related Articles - [Registered Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Registered-Mail-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [International Inquiries - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? metadata.title: Registered Mail® International
metadata.description: International Registered Mail is a service that provides additional protection and security in dispatch and transfer in the United States. In destination countries, Registered Mail items are handled according to their own internal procedures.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000la1AAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) ## USPS Smart Package Locker™ ## Description of and guidelines for USPS Smart Lockers and extended hours access. - Aug 29, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000008056 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[What are USPS Smart Package™ Lockers?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#what_are_usps_smart_parcel_lockers)** | [**Where can I find USPS Smart Package™ Lockers?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#where_can_I_find_usps_smart_parcel_lockers) | **[How do USPS Smart Package™ Lockers work?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#how_do_usps_smart_parcel_lockers_work)** | | [**When can I retrieve a package from a USPS Smart Package Locker?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#when_can_I_retrieve_a_package_from_a_usps_smart_locker)
[**Extended Hours Access**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#EHA) | [**What if I can no longer pick up my package from a USPS Smart Package Locker?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#what_if_I_can_no_longer_pick_up_my_package) | [**Who do I contact if I need help with my USPS Smart Package Locker or the QR Code?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#who_do_I_contact_if_I_need_help_with_my_usps_smart_parcel_locker_picker) | ****What are USPS Smart Package™ Lockers?**** USPS® Smart Package Lockers are electronic, keyless parcel lockers that provide a safe, secure, and convenient package delivery and returns alternative for both package recipients and shippers. **Where can I find USPS Smart Package Lockers?** USPS Smart Package Lockers are currently being installed in Post Office lobby locations across the country. Smart Lockers will be placed in Post Office lobbies, giving many package recipients 24/7 access to retrieve their package. To see a list of active locker locations, please visit the [Locker Location Page](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/locker-location). **How do USPS Smart Package Lockers work?** Currently, Smart Lockers support direct-to-locker deliveries, redeliveries, and oversized PO Box™️ packages. These capabilities allow for customers to skip the retail line and pick up their packages directly from a Smart Locker. In the near future, updates will enable customers to utilize Smart Lockers for Returns as well. | | | --- | | **For more information on Smart Lockers and answers to FAQs, please visit:**
- **[https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/)**
- **[https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq)** | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#top) **When can I retrieve a package from a USPS Smart Package Locker?** You can retrieve your package after you receive the first email with a six-digit access code or a QR code. The code is generated when a postal employee places the package into a USPS Smart Package Locker. Packages can be picked up 24 hours a day, 7 days a week from  many USPS Smart Locker locations. The package is kept in the locker for 5 calendar days; you will be sent 2 reminder emails during that time. After 5 days, you will receive a final email that the code has expired and the package has been removed from the locker and [must be picked up at the Post Office retail window](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#what_if_I_can_no_longer_pick_up_my_package). **Extended Hours Access** Extended hours access is a free service that provides you with a safe and secure way to access lobbies to retrieve packages delivered to a Smart Locker that do not have 24-hour public access. A secure system with a QR code reader and keypad is placed at the lobby entrance, and grants access to the locked lobby after normal business hours using the same QR code you received to access your Smart Locker. Extended hours access is available at select Post Office™ locations. **What if I can no longer pick up my package from a USPS Smart Package Locker?** You have 5 calendar days to pick up your package from a locker or to have someone else do so on your behalf. | | | --- | | You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your QR Code. While sharing the email containing the code and instructions is permitted, make sure to think about your safety and privacy, and only share it with people that you trust. | If the package is not picked up by the end of the 5th calendar day, the item will be removed from the locker and taken to the Post Office retail window. You will receive an email notification stating that the package must be picked up at the retail window and you will need to provide an [acceptable form of identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). If you signed up for [text tracking notifications](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm), you will receive text messages as well. | | | --- | | **Note**: You have **up to 5** calendar days for Priority Mail Express® items. Priority Mail Express items are returned to sender 5 calendar days after the initial delivery attempt no matter when the "USPS Smart Package Locker" option is selected. | Once taken to the Post Office retail window: - Priority Mail Express items will be returned to sender - Most domestic items will be held an additional 10 days at the Post Office - International items will be held an additional 25 days - The package is not eligible for another redelivery - See [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) for a complete list of mail retention periods. If you or a friend are not able to pick up your package within the corresponding number of days, we'll automatically return it to the sender. **Packages Destined to USPS Smart Package Lockers for Informed Delivery Subscribers** Informed Delivery subscribers that have a package coming that is destined for delivery to a USPS Smart Package Locker will see the following notification in the Daily Digest email: - The email address associated with the package must match the email address for your Informed Delivery account. - Tracking information beginning when the package is within 3 days of delivery. - Indication that the package has been delivered to a locker. - A one-time use QR Code to open the locker. - The QR Code will be displayed in the Daily Digest until the package is picked up from the locker - The QR Code will expire after 5 calendar days. After 5 days, your package will be removed from the Smart Locker and held at the Post Office retail counter for retrieval during their normal business hours. For additional information about USPS Smart Package Lockers, please visit  [uspssmartpackagelockers.com](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq) **Who do I contact if I need help with my USPS Smart Package Locker or the QR Code?** If you didn't receive a QR Code, check your junk email to see if it was delivered. If your code is not working, a USPS employee at the locker location can assist you during normal business hours. Contact the [Post Office location by phone during its normal business hours](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm). | | | --- | | **For more information on Smart Lockers and answers to FAQs, please visit:**
- **[https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/)**
- **[https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq](https://www.uspssmartpackagelockers.com/faq)** | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS Smart Package Locker™ URL Name USPS-Smart-Package-Locker [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Redelivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGMGA2/redelivery) ## Related Articles - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [USPS Delivery Instructions™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Delivery-Instructions-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000jzdAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) ## What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery? ## Number of days that items are held at the local Post Office - Aug 22, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006169 Customer Information **The Postal Service™ is no longer extending the time before a mail piece will be returned to sender. The times in the** [table below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery#Return_Dates) **are in effect again.** - Check the "Final Notice" box on the peach-colored _PS Form 3849 - Delivery Notice/Reminder/Receipt_. If this box is marked, then this will be the last notice you will receive before your package or extra service mail item is returned to the original sender. - The carrier will not have the item with them when the "Final Notice" is left. - Your mailpieces or extra service mail items will be returned to the sender on the date indicated unless you arrange a redelivery or come to pick up your mail at your local Post Office™ facility before this date. - Articles with extra services are held for 15 days, with the exception of Collect on Delivery (COD), which is held for 10 days (unless fewer days are designated by mailer, or it includes Hold For Pickup service). ****Second/Final Notice Return Dates**** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Service** | **Second /Final Notice** | **Total Number of Days Held after Initial Delivery Attempt** | | **Special Services** | | Certified Mail™ | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Collect on Delivery (COD) | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 10 days (unless fewer days are designated by mailer) | | Insured Mail (including Priority Mail and Ground Advantage) | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Registered Mail®
(Domestic and Outbound (originating in the US) International Registered Mail) | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Return Receipt for Merchandise | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Signature Confirmation™ | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Adult Signature Required | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | USPS Tracking® | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | **Domestic Priority Mail Express** | | Priority Mail Express | 3 days after receipt of first notice | 5 days after first attempt | | Priority Mail Express Hold for Pickup | Not Applicable | 5 days after first attempt | | **International - Inbound / Foreign Origin Mail** | | Customs | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 30 days (unless fewer days are designated by mailer) | | Registered Mail International | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 30 Days | | Priority Mail Express International | 3 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | International Parcels (including Priority Mail International) | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 30 Days | | **Other Mail** | | Perishable Parcel\* | 5 days after receipt of first notice | Destroyed after 10 days | | Postage Due | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | | Post Office Box Parcel | 5 days after receipt of first notice | 15 days | \\* **If endorsed Postmaster: Perishable** - Unless otherwise noted, the return date is in calendar days, not Postal business days. If the return date falls on a non-Postal Business day, the item will be returned on the following Postal Business day. - Articles with extra services are held for 15 days, with the exception of COD which is held for 10 days (unless fewer days are designated by mailer, or it includes Hold For Pickup service) and Priority Mail Express which is held for 5 days, before being returned to the mailer. - All items are available for pickup at the local Post Office™ until close of business on the applicable return date.  The item is returned to the sender at close of business. - Accountable Mail is returned to the sender after 15 days (30 days for Customs). - Carriers will leave two notices (regardless of whether or not a customer schedules a redelivery): first and final. - If a customer schedules a redelivery after the first notice has been left by the carrier and is not home to accept the item, the carrier will leave a second/final notice. - If the customer schedules a redelivery after the second/final notice has been left by the carrier and is not home to accept the item, another notice will not be left by the carrier. ## Commercial Mail Receiving Agency (CMRA)

## A Commercial Mail Receiving Agency (CMRA) is a private business that rents Private Mail Boxes (PMBs) to customers and accepts mail from the United States Postal Service for distribution to their customers.      - Feb 19, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008136

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[General Guidelines](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Commercial-Mail-Receiving-Agency-CMRA#general)** | **[Customer Termination of Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Commercial-Mail-Receiving-Agency-CMRA#termination)** | **[Selling Postal Products](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Commercial-Mail-Receiving-Agency-CMRA#postalproducts)** |

A Commercial Mail Receiving Agency (CMRA) is a private business that accepts mail from the Postal Service™ on behalf of their customers and delivers it to a private mailbox (PMB), or re-mails it to another location of the customer's choosing with new postage. Customers are responsible for the expenses associated with the services provided by CMRA stores such as Mail Boxes Etc., PostNet, Postal Annex, Pak Mail, and others.

To learn about CMRA mail processing, see [DMM Section 508.1.8.3](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/508.htm#ep1045195) for details.

**General Guidelines**

- Customers apply for a PMB at a CMRA and will be required to fill out [PS Form 1583](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps1583.pdf).
- Two forms of ID (one with a photo) are required when applying for a Private Mail Box.
- The United States Postal Service delivers all of a CMRA customer’s mail to the CMRA, but is not responsible for putting the mail in the individual boxes.
- Contact the CMRA directly (not the Postal Service™) to resolve any delivery issues.
- You must go to the local Post Office to accept Registered Mail. A CMRA may accept other Accountable Mail.
- To receive Restricted Delivery at your CMRA, make sure to authorize the Postal Service by signing in box #5 on [PS Form 1583](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps1583.pdf). Mail can be forwarded to a CMRA, but not from a CMRA.
- It is Postal policy that only addressees can refuse mail. PS Form 1583 obligates the CMRA to accept its customer's mail.
- If you have your mail forwarded to a CMRA, it may cost you money to have your mail forwarded back.

**Customer Termination of Private Mail Box**

When a customer terminates their Private Mail Box (PMB) the CMRA may, _but is not required to_, notify the Post Office. CMRAs are required to provide the USPS with a quarterly report of customers who have terminated their relationships with the CMRA. This allows us to adequately monitor CMRA compliance and to determine potentially fraudulent activity.

The CMRA is responsible for forwarding their customers’ mail for 6 months following PMB termination. USPS cannot process a change of address request from a CMRA to another address.

**Selling Postal Products**

CMRAs may sell Postal products. However, prices on postal products through a CMRA may not be the same as what the Postal Service charges. This is a legal service provided by the CMRA (not the United States Postal Service) as a for-profit business transaction. All refunds for products purchased at a CMRA must be handled by the CMRA. **USPS can not issue refunds for postage or products purchased through a third-party vendor.**

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Commercial-Mail-Receiving-Agency-CMRA#top)

## Are Stamps and Other Postage Products Eligible for a Refund?

## General Information about Refund for Stamps and Other Postage Products.      - Aug 28, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007100

Customer Information

**Stamp Exchanges**

Stamps and other postage products purchased at a local Post Office™ location may (based on the decision of the local office) be exchanged for new postage (of the same value) under the following conditions:

- **USPS Fault** \- The local office was at fault because it sold damaged, defective, or otherwise unserviceable stamps. You may exchange these stamps for other stamps at full postage value.

- **Purchase Error** \- A customer bought the wrong denomination stamps (or the wrong kind, size, or denomination envelope), and the following conditions are met:
  - Only full panes of stamps, coils of stamps in the original sealed wrappers, full boxes of stamped envelopes, or original sealed packs of stamped cards may be exchanged.
  - A customer exchanging $250 or more of such stock must show proof of identification [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) (e.g., a valid federal or state identification card with a photo) and must present the stock for exchange to the postal unit from which his or her mail is delivered.

- **Condition and Quality** \- Stamps that are damaged or otherwise unusable for postage (because of humidity, moisture, or other causes) while in a customer's possession may be considered for exchange if the following conditions are met:
  - Unusable stamps accepted from a customer under these conditions must be those on sale at local offices within 12 months before the transaction.
  - Quantities of the same denomination over $10 must be returned in the same configuration as when bought, i.e., sheets, coils, booklets.
  - Each such transaction is limited to $100 worth of postage from each customer. However, the following exceptions apply to:
    - Unmutilated single and double stamped cards are limited to 85% of postage value plus full value of postage added.
    - Postage stamps (ordinary or precanceled when applicable) affixed to commercial envelopes and postcards are limited to 90% of postage value.
    - Unused precanceled stamps in full coils and in full sheets redeemed from precanceled permit holder are limited to 90% of postage value.
  - The stamps presented for exchange must be in substantially whole condition, with the denomination evident.
  - The stamps may be exchanged only for an equal number of stamps of the same denomination.

**Note:**  Stamps cannot be exchanged due to a price increase since they are still valid for the total postage required. Instead, you should purchase additional stamps to make up the difference between the old and new prices.

The following postage items (regardless of when purchased) are not exchangeable:

- Mutilated and defaced stamps
- Stamps cut from stamped cards, stamped envelopes
- Parts and pieces of stamped cards
- Stamped cards, stamped envelopes, and aerogrammes received for reply
- Adhesive stamps, unless one of the following conditions is met:
  - Mistakes were made in buying (and the conditions stated above are met)
  - The stamps were defective
  - The stamps were affixed to commercial envelopes and postcards
  - The postage stamps were converted an advance deposit for permit imprint mailings (request for such a conversion is approved or denied by the district manager)

The postmaster at the local office makes the final determination as to what postage items may actually be exchanged. For more information or for questions about refunds on other items purchased at a Post Office location, contact the local office directly.

## Return Receipt - The Basics

## General information, eligible mail classes, compatible extra services, and images associated with Return Receipt service (PS Form 3811) and what you should expect.      - Dec 3, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006076

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[How is Return Receipt used?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#return_receipt_use)** | **[What does a Return Receipt (Green Card) look like?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#Green_Card)** | **[How much does a Return Receipt cost?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#cost)** |
| **[What domestic and international mail classes can be used with Return Receipt?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#domestic_and_International_mail_classes)** | **[Compatible Extra Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#extra_services)** | **[After Mailing an Item, Can I Request a Return Receipt?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#return_receipt_after_mailing)** |
| **[Is Return Receipt Eligible for a Refund?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#Refund)** | **[What is Electronic Return Receipt?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt)** |  |

**How is Return Receipt used?**

**_Return Receipt_** provides the sender with proof of delivery (the recipient's signature along with information about the delivery address, if different, and date of and time of delivery).

- A Return Receipt may be purchased at the time of mailing. **A mailer can no longer purchase a Return Receipt After Mailing**, but can [request information from the delivery record](https://usps.my.salesforce.com/articles/FAQ/After-Mailing-an-Item-Can-I-Request-a-Return-Receipt) if the previously purchased Return Receipt did not arrive.
- A mailer purchasing return receipt service may choose to receive the return receipt by mail (by use of [PS Form 3811](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#Green_Card)) or email (by [Electronic Return Receipt?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt)).
- Proof of delivery includes the following information:
  - Date of Delivery.
  - Signature of Recipient (or Recipient's Authorized Agent).
  - Information about the recipient's actual delivery address, if different.
    - If provided by email, the Return Receipt includes a link to the USPS Tracking® prepopulated with tracking/delivery information on the mail item.
- If the correct fees, postage, and form are affixed, a mailpiece with Return Receipt may be mailed from the home, office, or dropped in a Collection Box receptacle. Aviation Mail Security restrictions apply.
- PS Form 3811, _Domestic Return Receipt_, includes a barcode and Related Tracking Number for [tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS Tracking") of the proof of delivery as it is being sent to the mailer after delivery of the mailpiece.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

**What does a Return Receipt (Green Card) look like?**

The hardcopy PS Form 3811, _Domestic Return Receipt_ (seen below), or any USPS-approved copy includes a barcode with a Tracking Number. This Tracking Number is **not** to track the mailpiece sent by the mailer. Entering this Related Tracking Number into [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS Tracking") provides tracking information on the hardcopy Return Receipt itself as it is being mailed to the original mailer who requested this service.

**NOTE:** A Return Receipt cannot be viewed online.

**Image of the front of PS Form 3811 _, Domestic Return Receipt:_**

**![PS Form 3811 Front image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/return_receipt_ps3811front_July20.png)**

**Image of the back of PS Form 3811, _Domestic Return Receipt:_**

**![PS Form 3811 Back Image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/return_receipt_ps3811back_July20.png)**

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

# **How much does a Return Receipt cost?**

For detailed information on fees, go to [Notice 123](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#2589669) or " [Insurance & Extra Services](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm)."

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

**What domestic mail classes can be used with Return Receipt?**

Return Receipt service (PS Form 3811) is available for:

- Priority Mail Express® (Form 3811 only)
- Priority Mail®, First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™-Commercial™, and Parcel Select (if purchased with Certified Mail, Collect on Delivery (COD), Insurance over $500, or Registered Mail®)
- USPS Marketing Mail™ (parcels only, excluding Marketing Parcels; if purchased with Bulk Insurance over $500)
- Parcel Select Lightweight (if purchased with Bulk Insurance over $500)
- Bound Printed Matter, Library Mail, Media Mail (if purchased with Collect on Delivery or Insurance over $500)

**What International mail classes can be used with Return Receipt/How are they used?**

- **First-Class Mail International:**
  - For First-Class Mail International items, return receipt service is available for

    purchase **only for items that use Registered Mail service to certain**

    **destinations** — see the [Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm) for availability.

- PS Form 2865 _, Return Receipt for International Mail_(Avis de Reception), is a pink card that is attached to a First-Class Mail International item when used in conjunction with international Registered Mail service at the time of mailing and that is removed and signed at the point of delivery and returned to the sender.
  - Return receipts are completed in the country of destination in accordance with its internal regulations, which may not require the addressee’s signature except under special circumstances. These receipts are returned to the sender by airmail.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

**The following extra services can be combined with Return Receipt service (PS Form 3811)**

- Certified Mail
- Certified Mail Restricted Delivery
- Certified Mail Adult Signature Required
- Certified Mail Adult Signature Restricted Delivery
- Restricted Delivery
- Collect on Delivery
- Collect on Delivery Restricted Delivery
- Registered Mail
- Registered Mail Restricted Delivery
- USPS Tracking
- Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery (Form 3811 only)
- Signature Confirmation (Form 3811 only)
- Insurance (when insured for more than $500, Form 3811 only)
- Adult Signature Requested (Form 3811 only)
- Adult Signature Restricted Delivery (Form 3811 only) (Priority Mail Express and Priority Mail only

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

# **After Mailing an Item, Can I Request a Return Receipt?**

Mailing an item and then purchasing a Return Receipt is not an option. You can purchase a [Return Receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics) at the time of mailing and receive a Return Receipt with delivery information either in the mail or by email.

**If you did purchase a Return Receipt service, but have not received the Return Receipt from the item's delivery**, you can still request information from the delivery record within 90 days of the date of purchasing Return Receipt. You must visit any Post Office, station or branch, complete [PS Form 3811-A, _Request for Delivery Information/Return Receipt_](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3811a.pdf "PDF of PS Form 3811-A, Request for Delivery Information - Return Receipt") and produce your receipt showing that the applicable return receipt fee was paid. This must be done within 90 days of the date of purchase.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

**Is Return Receipt Eligible for a Refund?**

# It may be eligible under certain conditions.

Mailers can use USPS Tracking® to [check the status of the delivery record after delivery of a mailpiece with a hardcopy Return Receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#return_receipt_use).

Return Receipt fees are refunded only if the USPS® fails to provide the recipient's signature (if not otherwise refused, unclaimed, or returned to sender). Visit [www.usps.com/help](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#extra) or your origin Post Office™ to request a refund, not less than **30** days, or more than 60 days from the date of mailing.

If you have purchased a Return Receipt at a retail Post Office™ location and have not received the return receipt (or receive an incomplete receipt), you may request a refund or replacement record either:

- After **30** calendar days from date of mailing.
- Any time after you know the item has been delivered.

The applicable fee is not charged at retail if the sender can produce their receipt showing the return receipt fee was paid.

If you have purchased an [electronic Return Receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt) and have not received your electronic return receipt via email within 3-5 days of your request, we suggest you visit [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® and check the delivery status of your item.

- If you discover that your item has been delivered, we suggest that you check your email or junk folder. Occasionally email that is not junk will be sent to the folder if it does not meet your criteria for acceptable email.
- As a final alternative, we suggest that you request your electronic Return Receipt again. To do this, enter the tracking number in USPS Tracking®, select "Return Receipt Electronic" under "Available Actions", and provide your email address.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics#top)

## Shipping Cremated Remains and Ashes

## USPS Priority Mail Express® and box kits for shipping Cremated Remains and ashes.      - Dec 1, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007442

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | **Effective 3/1/25, customers are required to use the USPS® Priority Mail Express® Cremated Remains box. See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#new_cremated_remains_packaging_requirement) for additional information.** |

[**Mailing Cremated Remains**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#mailing_cremated_remains)

[**Cremated Remains Kits**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#cremated_remains_kit)

[**Extra Services**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#extra_services)

[**Video - How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains with USPS®**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#video)

[**Cremated Remains Packaging Requirement**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#new_cremated_remains_packaging_requirement)

**Mailing Cremated Remains**

**How should Cremated Remains be mailed?**

- US Postal Service requires the use of postal branded Cremated Remains packaging for all cremated remains shipments (both human and animal). This includes remains shipped as jewelry, blown glass, or other artisan works of art.
- Cremated Remains must be shipped using [Priority Mail Express®](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm)  (or [Priority Mail Express International®](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-express-international.htm)) service. Priority Mail Express comes with USPS Tracking®. No other service is permitted.
- You can visit your [local Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) to have the package shipped.

**Do Cremated Remains have to be shipped using a Priority Mail Express box or the Cremated Remains Kit?**

- Yes, a Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE) is **required** for all cremated remains shipments. Follow the guidelines for shipping Cremated Remains listed in [Publication 139, _How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains_](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub139.pdf). The use of postal branded PME Cremated Remains packaging aids in the safety and security of cremated remains shipments and increases visibility in processing and the importance of the package contents.

**Can Cremated Remains be shipped internationally?**

- Cremated remains are permitted to be mailed to an international address _when the designating country does not prohibit_ the contents and when Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country.
- You can verify this by checking the Individual Country Listing in the Mailing Standards of the United States Postal Service, _[International Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immctry.htm)_ (IMM®).

**Where can I order a Cremated Remains Kit?**

- Kits **can be ordered** **free of charge** from the [Postal Store](https://store.usps.com/store/results?Ntt=cremated+remains&_requestid=57740) on USPS.com. Four types of kits are available for order. Orders are restricted to a maximum of 5 kits per order. You can visit your [local Post Office](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) to have the package shipped.
- Packaging **can be ordered through the Click-N-Ship® application** when creating a shipping label. A label must first be created and placed in the cart in order to see the option to add packaging supplies to the order.
- Businesses who ship a large volume of cremated remains (i.e., crematoriums, funeral homes, etc.) can place orders for pallet quantities of boxes through the Package Supply Center at 1-800-610-8734.

**Can I obtain a Cremated Remains kit from a local post office?**

- The kits may be available at retail locations behind the counter, though all retail locations may not have them stocked at all times. Supplies **ARE NOT** available on display on Retail lobbies. Customers are encouraged to order kits from The Postal Store.

**How do I pack and prepare the Cremated Remains for shipping?**

- For details on packaging, labeling and addressing see [Publication 139, _How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains_](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub139.pdf).

**Cremated Remains Kits**

**What is included in the Cremated Remains Kits?**

**Large Kit 1**

**Large Kit 2**

- A sturdy box 14-3/4"(L) x 10-1/4"(W) x 10"(H)
- A roll of Priority Mail Express packing tape

![Picture of items within Cremated Remains Human Kit 1: A USPS Priority Mail Express box with orange labels on each side that say "CREMATED REMAINS" and have a blue urn flush to the label's right. Two rolls of tape with "Priority Mail Express" is displayed. Note: kit includes a single roll of tape.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/cremated_remains/BOX_CREKIT1-Z0.jpg)

- A sturdy box 14-3/4"(L) x 10-1/4"(W) x 10"(H)
- A roll of Priority Mail Express packing tape
- Bubble Cushioning
- A self-sealing plastic bag
- A copy of [Publication 139, _How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains_](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub139.pdf)

![Picture of items within Cremated Remains Human Kit 2: A USPS Priority Mail Express box with orange labels on each side that say "CREMATED REMAINS" and have a blue urn flush to the label's right; a roll of "Priority Mail Express" tape; the Publication 139 a pamphlet (top 1/3 is light blue, bottom 2/3 is white, and thick black line is between the two); a self-sealing plastic bag with "Cremated Remains" typed on it; and a roll of bubble cushioning.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/cremated_remains/BOX_CREKIT2-Z0.jpg)

**Small Kit 1**

**Small Kit 2**

- A sturdy box 9"(L) x 7-1/4"(W) x 5"(H)
- A roll of Priority Mail Express packing tape

**NOTE:** Not available for order in Click-N-Ship until 5/27/25

![Small Cremated Remains Kit 1](https://www.usps.com/ecp/asset/images/BOXCREKIT1SM-Z0.jpg)

- A sturdy box 9"(L) x 7-1/4"(W) x 5"(H)
- A roll of Priority Mail Express packing tape
- Bubble Cushioning
- A self-sealing plastic bag
- A copy of [Publication 139, _How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains_](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub139.pdf)

**NOTE:** Not available for order in Click-N-Ship until 5/27/25

![Small Cremated Remains Kit 2](https://www.usps.com/ecp/asset/images/BOXCREKIT2SM-Z0.jpg)

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#top)

**Extra Services**

**Can I add extra services to my Cremated Remains package?**

- Yes. Extra services such as Return Receipt, Insurance, Signature Required, and Signature Waived are available for mailing Cremated Remains.

**Video - How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains with USPS®**

How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains - YouTube

[Photo image of USPS](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCY9TGayMuvIA2HMAClY5PYA?embeds_referring_euri=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2F)
