[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#top)

**Cremated Remains Packaging Requirement**

- Effective 3/1/25, US Postal Service is requiring the use of postal branded Cremated Remains packaging for all cremated remains shipments (both human and animal). This includes remains shipped as jewelry, blown glass, or other artisan works of art.

**Will Post Offices continue to supply the orange Label 139?**

- No, Post Offices are not continuing to supply the orange Label 139. Use of Label 139 has been discontinued. Everyone is required to follow the new guidelines implemented for shipping cremated remains using Priority Mail Express® (PME) Cremated Remains packaging.

**How do I secure postage for the Cremated Remains shipment?**

- Priority Mail Express postage is required for shipping Cremated Remains. To create a shipping label please visit the Click-N-Ship® application via [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® or use software available from an approved USPS PC Postage® provider. A list of the approved PC Postage providers may be found at [https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/postage-options.htm). When creating a label, please select the option to use your own package, Priority Mail Express as your service, and Cremated Remains Shipping as your extra service. You can also visit your local Post Office retail counter to purchase postage to have the package shipped. Stamps cannot be used to mail these packages.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes#top)

## What are Some Packaging and Shipping Tips for Mailing Parcels?

## Tips for packaging and shipping parcels.      - Nov 13, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004865

Customer Information

**Easy, convenient, multilingual and always at the fingertips**, [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) has a complete suite of tools to help with shipping gifts, including whether they contain lithium batteries or alkaline batteries:

- Military & Diplomatic Mail: [_https://www.usps.com/ship/apo-fpo-dpo.htm_](https://www.usps.com/ship/apo-fpo-dpo.htm)
- Preparing International Shipments: [_https://www.usps.com/international/preparing-international-shipments.htm_](https://www.usps.com/international/preparing-international-shipments.htm)
- Free Shipping Supplies: _[https://store.usps.com/store/results/free-shipping-supplies](https://store.usps.com/store/results/shipping-supplies/_/N-7d0v8v?_requestid=2737082)_
- Create Package Labels and Pay For Postage Online: _[https://cns.usps.com/labelInformation.shtml](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=GSS&appURL=https://cns.usps.com/labelInformation.shtml)_
- Fill Out Customs Forms Online: [_usps.com/international/customs-forms.htm_](https://www.usps.com/international/customs-forms.htm)
- Request Free Package Pickup: _[https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm)_

**Additional tips:**

- Remove batteries from toys and other electronic devices. Wrap and place them next to the item in the mailing box. Include new batteries in the manufacturer’s packaging.
  - USPS has a different policy for [lithium batteries](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Ship-Lithium-Batteries).
- Place a card inside the package that contains both the delivery and return addresses. This ensures the safe return of an item that could not be delivered should the mailing label become damaged or fall off.
- The address, return address, and postage should all be on one side of the parcel only. (Improperly addressed parcels may be returned postage due).
  - Always use a return address.
  - If the mailed item is a gift, include both “to” and “from” information on only one side of the package.
- Paper wrapping is not recommended; it can catch and tear off in mail processing equipment.
- Parcels (including fragile items) should be prepared to withstand normal mail processing and transportation.
  - Contents can be padded with newspaper, Styrofoam, bubble wrap, tissue and ink-free packing paper.
- Parcels that could harm people, equipment, or other mail are NOT acceptable for mailing.
- Fragile, liquid, and perishable items need to be identified on the outside of the package.
- Use tape that is designed for shipping - packaging tape or strapping tape is recommended for closures. Do not use gift wrapping, cellophane tape, duct tape and masking tape as these are not strong enough to withstand temperature fluctuations during transit. (Registered Mail must be sealed with glue, plain paper tape, or cloth tape; the tape must absorb ink and must visibly damage the envelope or wrapper if removed.)
- Items do NOT have to be placed in a box, however they should be prepared to withstand normal processing and transportation.
- Twine or cord is NOT recommended for use, as it tends to catch and bind in mail processing equipment.
- Reused boxes that have outside markings indicating hazardous materials are not acceptable for mailing.
  - To reuse boxes that previously contained alcohol, all markings associated with the prior contents must be completely removed or obliterated and leaks or stains from the alcohol cannot be on the packaging.
- It is acceptable to reuse boxes if all package markings and labels have been removed or completely marked out (Wrapping a parcel in paper or just crossing out a label is not sufficient).
  - However, don’t reuse mailing boxes; they can weaken in the shipping process.
- Mail pieces outside of the machinable guidelines are acceptable for mailing but have to be worked in slower sorting areas of mail processing (refer to [Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards), Nonmachinable Surcharge).
- To save time by skipping the Post Office visit, you can order free boxes online by visiting the Postal Store at [www.usps.com/shop](http://www.usps.com/shop) and utilize our Free Package Pickup service.
- Circular parcels are measured as rectangular and non-rectangular.
- [How Do I Keep My Mailed Item Cold or Refrigerated?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Do-I-Keep-My-Mailed-Item-Cold-or-Refrigerated)
- [What am I Restricted from Mailing?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-am-I-Restricted-from-Mailing)
- [What Does USPS® Classify as Hazardous Materials?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials)

**Note**: Please refer to the [Domestic Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/601.htm#wp1064962) and [International Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc1_013.htm), as well as [Publication 52, Hazardous, Restricted and Perishable Mail](http://pe.usps.gov/text/pub52/welcome.htm), for questions regarding the mailability of Hazardous Materials.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000uLZAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice) ## PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice ## PS Form 3849 is the Redelivery Notice left by carrier for a customer when mailpiece was attempted but not delivered. - Feb 25, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006180 Customer Information PS Form 3849 is the Redelivery Notice left for you when your mailpiece is undeliverable. This form provides you with information on the type of mailpiece that was attempted by the Carrier, why the mailpiece was not left, and which delivery options are available. Those delivery options can include requesting a carrier redelivery, picking up your mailpiece from the Post Office, and picking up your mailpiece from a USPS Smart Parcel Locker. **Why was the form revised for September 2022?** The PS Form **3849G** (gopost® Redelivery form) was retired from production and replaced with February 2021 version for all customers. That version allowed carriers to check "Parcel Locker Eligible" if a customer is eligible to have their items redelivered to a gopost® parcel locker. The September 2022 version updates that checkmark to "USPS® Smart Parcel Locker Eligible". For more information on using the form if "Parcel Locker Eligible" or “USPS® Parcel Locker Eligible” is checked, see either the [gopost® Package Pickup](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup) or the [USPS® Smart Parcel Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker) articles. - **Note**: Previous versions of PS Form **3849** are still in use by USPS and can be used by customers. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **PS Form 3849 Redelivery Form (Peach Color)** | **PS Form 3849-SD Sunday Delivery (Yellow Color)** | | **Front**
![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/Front_PS_Form_3849_Sept22_v2.jpg) | **Back**
![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/Back_PS_Form_3849_Sept22_v2.jpg) | **Front**
![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/3849SD_Front_Jan_2018_Yellow.jpg) | | _Click above images to make larger_ | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice#top) **What is a QR Code and how does it work?** A QR Code is a type of barcode, shaped like a square, when scanned it directs the user to additional information about the attempted mailpiece(s). When the QR Code on the PS Form 3849 is scanned, it will direct the user to the redelivery page to allow them to request a redelivery. **Are users required to download a QR Code reader/scanner app to their smart device?** Users have two options for accessing the QR code on the PS Form 3849: - iOS customers can access their mobile camera, hold it over the QR code and a message will appear to open " [qr.usps.com](http://qr.usps.com/)" in Safari. Upon agreeing to the message, the user will be redirected to the Redelivery application. - iOS and Android customers can also download the QR Code reader/scanner app to their smart device to scan the QR code on the PS Form 3849, and the user will be directed to the redelivery application. **Is a PS Form 3849 available to businesses?** Both business and residential customers are able to take advantage of the redesigned PS Form 3849. Businesses will likely only see the new form if they do not have a designated, safe delivery location and if their item requires a signature. **Can a customer have someone else pick up their mailpiece for them?** Yes, this option is still available. The customer may designate someone they know to pick up their mailpiece at the local Post Office. The customer must sign the form in the appropriate section and write the name of the person who is picking the item up on their behalf. This option is not available for Registered Mail® and mailpieces with Restricted Delivery. **Do I have to be present to sign for my mailpiece?** You or an [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) must be present to sign for accountable mail being delivered by a mail carrier. If delivery was attempted for your accountable mail item and you want to schedule a redelivery, signing a Redelivery Notice (PS Form 3849) and leaving it for the mail carrier does not meet the minimum requirements for redelivery. You or an authorized agent must be present to sign for accountable mail when the carrier is redelivering the item. See [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics)for additional information on scheduling a redelivery. **What types of services require the first initial and last name of the recipient to be manually entered?** Priority Mail Express®, Signature Confirmation, and Adult Signature all require the first initial and last name of the recipient to be manually entered by the carrier in the Mobile Delivery Device (MDD). If the MDD is not available a signature on the PS Form 3849 will be required. **Can a carrier sign for a mailpiece linked to a PS Form 3849?** Carriers may only sign for Priority Mail Express, provided a waiver of signature is indicated by the sender. **If a PS Form 3849 is for a business delivery, can the signature reflect the business name or must a "person" sign for the mailpiece(s)?** An individual employee must sign for the pieces. The "business" cannot sign and carriers must be able to input the first initial and last name of the person signing for the delivery. **Do all PS Form 3849s have barcodes?** Yes, all revised PS Form 3849s have barcodes along with barcode numbers. The barcode number can be entered into the USPS Tracking® and Schedule a Redelivery applications instead of a tracking number. If there was a failed delivery of more than one package in a single attempt, the barcode number is associated with all the tracking numbers in that attempt. Entering that barcode number into the above applications provides information on all the tracking numbers. **What is the difference between PS Form 3849-SD,** _**Sunday Delivery**_ **, and PS Form 3849,** _**We ReDeliver for You!**_ **?** PS Form 3849-SD, Sunday Delivery form is designed to notify customers that a package delivery attempt was made on Sunday and the package will be available for pickup on the next business day (excluding Holidays) at their local Post Office or can be scheduled for redelivery by scanning the QR Code on the form. Sunday Delivery forms are used by hub offices and do not include the local office information. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice URL Name PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Redelivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGMGA2/redelivery) ## Related Articles - [What are the Second and Final Notice and Return Dates for Redelivery?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery) - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) - [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) - [Where is my package? ## What Retail Services does the United States Postal Service® provide?

## List the consumer goods and services provided to customers through multiple channels of distribution. Retailers satisfy demand identified through a supply chain.      - Oct 23, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007098

Customer Information

**The United States Postal Service provides the following services:**

- Post Office™ Box rentals
- Selling shipping supplies (where available)
- Accepting debit / credit card payment (where available)
- Selling postage in any denomination
- Providing USPS® mailing information
- Weigh and classify postage of envelopes and parcels
- Providing assistance with dispense forms such as Priority Mail Express®, Certified Mail®, etc.,
- Confirmation services
- Providing receipts for purchases
- Change of Address (COA) service
- Money order sales
- Cash Postal money orders
- Cash U.S. Treasury Checks (for a fee)

**Other services may also be available depending upon the location. In addition, the USPS offers an alliance with The UPS Store:**

- There are currently over 800 locations participating in ten of the eleven postal areas.
- The UPS Store locations will enter into individual contracts with the Postal Service™ and offer additional services. (Mail classes, items, and stamps are currently offered at the more than 3,000 UPS store locations.)
- This initiative will provide greater convenience for our customers and encourage the sale of postal products and services.

## Restrictions for attaching flyers, posters, etc. to a mailbox

## Guidelines governing placing any item into a mail receptacle.      - Apr 7, 2020 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006998

Customer Information

**Can a flyer/envelope be put it in someone else's mailbox without being mailed? What if a stamp was placed on it?**

No. Per [Sect. 508.3.1.3 of the Domestic Mail Manual (DMM)](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/508.htm#1051823):

_"No part of a mail receptacle may be used to deliver any matter not bearing postage, **including items or matter placed upon, supported by, attached to, hung from, or inserted into a mail receptacle**. Any mailable matter not bearing postage and found as described above is subject to the same postage as would be paid if it were carried by mail."_

"Postage" is defined as: Payment for a delivery service that is affixed or imprinted to a mailpiece, usually in the form of a postage stamp, permit imprint, or meter impression.

Notes:

- USPS regulations do not govern what can be placed in a mail slot on your door. This means that if a local business wants to put a flyer in the mail slot, they can do so.
- For further questions (or to report occurrences) regarding flyers being placed into your mailbox without first going through the postal system, please refer to the [local Post Office.](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm?)

**Can I affix a flyer or poster to a United States Postal Service® Collection Box®?**

United States Postal Service Collection boxes are the property of the Postal Service. You are not allowed to affix anything to them, including flyers, signs about missing items or animals, and advertisements.

## Can I purchase domestic insurance coverage online?

## General information on the availability of insurance, including payment methods and how insured mailpieces can be deposited.      - Nov 3, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006223

Customer Information

**Availability**

- Indemnity coverage is available online via Click-N-Ship®, PC Postage, or customer-generated integrated barcodes up to $5,000. (Online labels also include USPS Tracking® service, so customers can check their delivery status online.)
- Insurance up to $100 is included with Priority Mail Express® service, Priority Mail® service, and USPS Ground Advantage™ items. Additional insurance can be purchased for either mail class.
- No extra forms are needed
- An item insured online can be mailed using the following methods:
  - Package Pickup
  - Dropped off at your local Post Office™
  - Dropped in a Collection Box
  - Placed in a mailbox or hand to your letter carrier

**Restrictions**

- Postal insurance may be purchased at only one source for each mailpiece; combining postal insurance from multiple sources is not permitted.

**Ineligible Items**

- See the [Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/503.htm#4_3_2) for other conditions that will result in an item not to be insured.

**Note**: For information on international insurance coverage, please see [What is Insurance (International)?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International)

## What is Priority Mail Express International®

## Priority Mail Express International® service is a reliable, high-speed mail service.      - Oct 5, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000005305

Customer Information

**_Priority Mail Express International®_** service is a reliable, high-speed mail service for correspondence, printed matter, and packages.  It is available to more than 180 countries and offers the following:

- Average delivery of 3 to 5 business days. (Average number of days may vary based upon origin and destination.)
- Can be tracked by:
  - Using the [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") page and entering the item's Tracking Number
  - Calling the phone number at [Contact USPS](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm#kilo) (under USPS Tracking). Hours of operation provided
- Customers can save time and ship from their home or office.
- Customers can go to " [Prepare International Shipments](https://www.usps.com/international/preparing-international-shipments.htm "Preparing International Shipments | USPS")" to do the following:
  - Order free packaging supplies.
  - Print shipping labels and customs forms all in one document (PS Form 2976-B, _Priority Mail Express International Shipping Label and Customs Form_, available starting July 2013).
  - Request free _Package Pickup™_ service.
  - Track shipments.
- Reciprocal services listed in the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/?country=10440 "Postage Price Calculator").
- Check the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/?country=10440 "Postage Price Calculator") for individual country limitations.
- Service is available from any Post Office™.
- In addition to cash, check, and credit/debit cards, customers may pay with stamps or postage meter strips.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International#top)

**Insurance**

Insurance against loss, damage, or missing contents is available at no additional cost to mailer (up to $100 for documents and up to $200 for merchandise).

- Additional coverage may be purchased at any Post Office™ or by using Click-N-Ship® for amounts up to $5,000.
- Insurance availability varies by country, content, and value. See the [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) for availability and insurance limits.
- Insurance for **merchandise** is provided automatically up to $200. The included insurance covers loss, damage, or missing contents at no additional cost to the mailer.

  - Coverage is limited both to the actual value of the shipment and by country of destination.
- Insurance, which provides document reconstruction for **nonnegotiable documents** (such as audit and business records), is provided automatically up to $100. Insurance covers loss, damage, or missing contents at no additional cost to the mailer.  Additional coverage beyond $100 is not available.
- Priority Mail Express International® items **are not** insured against delay in delivery. There is no indemnity payment in the event of a delay. However, with Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee Service, postage refunds for delay are considered.

  - Neither indemnity payments nor postage refunds will be made in the following situations involving Priority Mail Express International with Money-Back Guarantee Service:
    - Delivery was attempted but could not be made.
    - The delivery address or postal code was incomplete or inaccurate.
    - The item was detained or delayed by customs or any other government or law enforcement agency of the destination country.
    - The item had to be forwarded to another address.
    - Delay resulted from any defect or characteristic due to the nature of the shipment, even if known to USPS at acceptance.
    - The item was delayed by any circumstance beyond the control of USPS and the foreign postal administration, acts of God, _force majeure,_ terrorist activities, strikes, labor actions, war, insurrection, or civil disobedience.
    - The item was held by customs at origin or destination for more than 24 hours. If the item was held by customs for less than 24 hours, the guarantee may be adjusted to account for the delay by customs.
    - The item was not deposited at a designated Priority Mail Express facility.
- For detailed information on cost of insurance and coverage available, go to [International Insurance and Extra Services](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm "USPS.com - International Insurance and Extra Services").

**Delivery Delays**

Priority Mail Express International® items are not insured against delay in delivery (with the exception of [Priority Mail Express International® with Money-Back Guarantee (PMEI-DC)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-International-reg-with-Money-Back-Guarantee-PMEI-DC) service). Neither indemnity payments nor postage refunds will be made in case of delay of Priority Mail Express International service.

**NOTE:** Maximum size dimensions for Priority Mail Express International service vary by country and are noted in the [Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm). The maximum weight limit for Priority Mail Express International service is 70 pounds; however, size and weight limits apply depending on the destination country.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International#top)

**Please note the following when using Priority Mail Express International® Service**

Pickup On Demand® service is available for Priority Mail Express International Service; see  [What is Package Pickup](https://usps.my.salesforce.com/articles/FAQ/What-is-Package-Pickup?id=kA3t0000000CctN) and [What is Pickup on Demand](https://usps.my.salesforce.com/articles/FAQ/What-is-Pickup-on-Demand?id=kA3t0000000CcuB) for additional information or [Schedule a Pickup](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm) to request the service.

Priority Mail Express International items bearing a computer-generated customs form with customs data that has been electronically transmitted (e.g., using Click-N-Ship® service, an authorized PC Postage® vendor, or the USPS Web Tools® system), may be deposited through any of the following methods, provided postage is paid by a means other than the use of postage stamps:

- In a private mailbox bearing a return address that matches the address at the point of pick up, when the customer or business is known to reside or do business at that location.
- Through Pickup on Demand service.
- Through Package Pickup service.
- At a Postal Service™ retail counter.
- Into a Postal Service lobby drop.
- In a collection box.
- At a Contract Postal Unit (CPU).
- At a USPS Approved Shipper location.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International#top)

**Priority Mail Express International items not eligible for deposit or pickup:**

Customers must present the following Priority Mail Express International items requiring a customs form to an employee at a Post Office™ retail service counter. All deposit and pickup methods listed above are prohibited. If any of the following Priority Mail Express International items are not submitted to the USPS at a Post Office™ retail service counter, the Postal Service will return these improperly presented items to the sender:

- Any item bearing a customs form and paid with only postage stamps.
- Any item bearing a handwritten customs form.
- Any item bearing a customs form that was not computer-generated.
- Any item for which the customs data was not electronically transmitted.

Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI) labels typically begin with two alpha characters ("E" followed by any other letter), followed by nine numbers, and two more alpha characters.

Size and weight limits differ from country to country — see the [International Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/?country=10440) for countries that offer Priority Mail Express International (PMEI) or the [Price List (Notice 123)](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International#top)

**Priority Mail Express International® Flat Rate Service:**

Priority Mail Express International Flat Rate Boxes (and Priority Mail Express Flat Rate Boxes) are discontinued as of January 17, 2016. Customers can continue using the boxes listed below for shipping after that date, but will be charged the appropriate Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail Express International price based on weight and destination.

- The **top-loading box** (PME-FRB1) is 11 inches x 8-1/2 inches x 5-1/2 inches inside.
- The **side-loading box**(PME-FRB2) is 13-5/8 inches x 11-7/8 inches x 3-3/8 inches inside.

Priority Mail Express International Flat Rate Envelopes have a 4 pound weight maximum (some countries have lower weight restrictions) and eight destination-specific prices (to Canada and seven other geographical regions).  See [Priority Mail Express International](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-express-international.htm) for more information.

**Note:**  Domestic Priority Mail Express® Flat Rate Envelopes with prepaid postage may be used for a Priority Mail Express International item as long as appropriate additional postage is added before mailing. For details on pricing, see the [International Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/?country=10440 "USPS.com - International Postage Price Calculator") or go to [https://www.usps.com/international/mail-shipping-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/mail-shipping-services.htm "USPS.com - International Mail Services").

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International#top)

## Mail Fraud

## Identifying and providing information on preventing Mail Fraud.      - Apr 9, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007948

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **[What is Mail Fraud?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud#what_is_mail_fraud)** | **[Where Can I Find Information Preventing Mail Fraud?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud#preventing_mail_fraud)** |
| **[What Should I Do if I am the Victim of Mail Fraud?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud#I_am_the_victim)** | **[Websites Offering Information or Resources on Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud#websites)** |

**What is Mail Fraud?**

Any fraud that uses the U.S. Mail®—whether it originates in the mail, by phone, or online—is mail fraud.It’s a scheme to get money or something of value from you by offering a product, service, or investment opportunity that does not live up to its claims. Prosecutors must prove the claims were intentionally misrepresented and that **the mail was used** to carry out the scheme.

The U.S. Postal Inspection Service website provides alerts and information to the public on the most commonly reported [Mail Fraud schemes](https://www.uspis.gov/tips-prevention/mail-fraud/).

**Where Can I Find More Information on Preventing Mail Fraud?**

For a comprehensive listing and description of various fraud schemes please refer to a _Guide to Preventing Mail Fraud_:  [https://about.usps.com/publications/pub300a.pdf](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub300a.pdf "U.S. Postal Inspection Service Guide to Preventing Fraud").

**What Should I Do if I am the Victim of Mail Fraud?**

To report mail fraud to The U.S. Postal Inspection Service online, you may fill out and submit the [Mail Fraud Complaint Form](https://ehome.uspis.gov/fcsexternal/default.aspx) . You may also call the U.S. Postal Inspection Service hotline at **1-800-372-8347** to file a complaint about suspected mail fraud. See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud), and [Identity Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft).

**Websites Offering Information or Resources on Fraud**

- _U.S. Postal Inspection Service_: [https://www.uspis.gov/](https://www.uspis.gov/)
- _U.S. Postal Service Privacy Office_: [http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/privacy-policy-highlights.htm](http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/privacy-policy-highlights.htm "USPS Privacy Policy")
- _Federal Trade Commission_: [http://www.ftc.gov/](http://www.ftc.gov/) (or) [http://www.ftc.gov/bcp/consumer.shtm](http://www.ftc.gov/bcp/consumer.shtm)  (also offers brochures).
- _National Consumers League_: [http://www.fraud.org](http://www.fraud.org/)
- _Better Business Bureau_: [http://www.bbb.org/](http://www.bbb.org/)
- _FirstGov for Consumers_: [http://www.consumer.gov/](http://www.consumer.gov/)
- _Consumer Issue Information: [https://www.usa.gov/consumer](https://www.usa.gov/consumer)_
- _U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) Citizen and Consumer Resources: [https://www.gsa.gov/resources-for/how-gsa-helps-citizens-and-consumers](https://www.gsa.gov/resources-for/how-gsa-helps-citizens-and-consumers)_

See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud), and [Identity Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft).

download instructions for the app and the ability to perform popular USPS.com® functions via their internet-capable mobile device; allowing customers access to services practically anytime and anywhere. - Aug 7, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007747 Customer Information **To access the USPS Mobile® website, go to [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® with your internet-capable mobile device.** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**What is the USPS Mobile® website and how can I use it?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#What_is_USPSMOBILE) | [**Do I have to be a registered USPS.com® user to purchase stamps or create and print a shipping label via my smartphone?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Registered) | [**Why are only a portion of USPS® postage stamps available for purchase on smartphones?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Portion_stamps) | | [**USPS Mobile® App for Smart phone (iPhone® or Android®) Devices**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#USPS_Mobile_App) | [**Security and Restrictions**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Security) | [**Will I incur any charges for accessing the USPS Mobile® site?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Charges) | | [**How can I Provide Customer Feedback on USPS Mobile®?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Security) | | | **What is the USPS Mobile® website and how can I use it?** The USPS Mobile® website provides customers with the ability to perform popular [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) functions via their internet-capable mobile device **[†.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Notes_Usability)** By using the USPS Mobile website, customers can access key USPS® services practically anytime, anywhere. **The following services are available from the USPS Mobile site:** | | | | --- | --- | | **Buy Stamps** | Allows you to purchase from a selection of postage stamps available on The Postal Store®. | | **Ship Online** | Provides you the ability to create and print domestic Priority Mail Flat-Rate® shipping labels. | | **Tracking + Search** | 'Tracking' helps you easily check the status of your packages. Simply enter your package label number and 'Track' will provide you with latest delivery status information for your package. **\*** Terms can be entered into the same textbox to conduct a search of USPS.com. | | **Schedule Pickup** | Allows you to schedule a next-day package pickup. | | **Locations** | ‘Locations’ is designed to help you find locations near you.  After entering location information (i.e. ZIP Code™, address), ‘Locations’ will provide you with a list of the nearby Post Office™ locations along with the location hours and services provided. You can also specify what type of Postal Service™ location you are looking for such as a retail Post Office location, self-service kiosk, or a USPS collection box. | | **Prices** | Allows you to calculate a price. Once you enter the ZIP Code you are shipping from, the destination ZIP Code, and the shape and weight of the item, prices are displayed for your shipping options. | | **Zip Codes™** | ‘ZIP Codes’ will help you look up ZIP Code(s) for a specific city or address. You will be able to search by address, by city/state, or you may search for a city by ZIP Code. After you enter a city/state combination or an address, ‘ZIP Code’ will provide you with a list of the ZIP Code(s) for specific locations.  When you enter a ZIP Code after selecting “Search for a city by a ZIP Code”, the city for that ZIP Code is displayed. | | **Informed Delivery** ®: | Allows you access a dashboard that displays your incoming USPS® mail and packages in a central location to view status information and manage your packages. You can check the status of your packages at any time from a computer or web-enabled device. [Click here for more information on Informed Delivery.](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action) | | **Change Address** | Allows you to conveniently change your address from your mobile device. An identity validation fee is required. Please see the [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) for more information. | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **Do I have to be a registered [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® user to purchase stamps or create and print a shipping label via my smartphone?** Yes.  You must be registered on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) in order to make stamp or label purchases using a smartphone. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **Why are only a portion of USPS® postage stamps available for purchase on smartphones?** The stamps that are available to purchase via smartphones are the most popular stamps among Postal Store customers.  The stamps offered on mobile will change to coincide with new releases and seasonal demands. If you wish to purchase stamps other than those listed, you may simply click on “Postal Store desktop” to view and order from the full catalog of postage stamps and merchandise available on the Postal Store® page. **Notes:** **†** Usability and functionality may be limited on phones using WAP browsers. **\*** 'USPS Tracking' is available with certain delivery services. For more information see [_https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action_](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction!input.action). [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **USPS Mobile® App for Smartphone (iPhone® or Android®) Devices** | | | | --- | --- | | **What is USPS Mobile® app?** | The USPS Mobile® app is a downloadable application for mobile phone and tablet users.  It provides USPS Mobile customers with the ability to track packages, ship online, buy stamps, locate a Post Office™ facility, schedule a next-day pickup, find a ZIP Code™, and more on mobile devices. | | **How do customers download the USPS Mobile® app?** | The USPS Mobile **®** app for iPhone can be downloaded from the Apple® App Store® or Google Play Store®. | | **Is there a cost to download the USPS Mobile® app for iPhone® application?** | The USPS Mobile **®** app is free. **\*** | | **What are some of the unique features that the USPS Mobile® app offers?** | - Tracking
- Locations -- this includes:
- Approved Postal Provider locations
- Pickup service locations
- Specific hours for services such as passport services
- Schedule Pickup
- Prices
- ZIP Codes™
- Hold Mail
- Ship Online
- Buy Stamps
- Informed Delivery® 
- Change of Address
Customers may also scan barcodes (to track packages) or QR Codes. | | **Does USPS® plan to offer other mobile applications for other devices in the future?** | Yes. USPS will continue to enhance the USPS Mobile **®** experience for our customers by supporting more devices and adding new features and functions. Please visit [www.usps.com/mobile](http://www.usps.com/mobile) for the latest enhancements. | **Notes:** iPhone® is a registered trademark of Apple Inc.  \*\* Restrictions apply              Android is a trademark of Google Inc.  \*\* Restrictions apply [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **Security and Restrictions** **Can I be sure that the information I enter into the USPS Mobile® site is secure?** Please view detailed information about our privacy policy at [http://www.usps.com/privacyoffice](http://www.usps.com/privacyoffice). **Are there any restrictions for accessing the USPS Mobile® site?** The USPS Mobile site is only available on internet-enabled devices with a web browser. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **Will I incur any charges for accessing the USPS Mobile® site?** Standard service charges from your mobile carrier may apply. For more information, please check with your mobile service provider. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) **How can I Provide Customer Feedback on USPS Mobile®?** To ensure that we are providing the best customer experience, we encourage you to provide your feedback/comments about the USPS Mobile site. Please go to [https://usps.force.com/emailus/s/](https://usps.force.com/emailus/s/ "Email Us"), 1. Click on  " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" 2. Select "Other Technical Support On [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" 3. Select "Other" 4. Enter requested information [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Mobile#Back) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is USPS Mobile®? URL Name What-is-USPS-Mobile [USPS.com / Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFvGAM/uspscom-services)[USPS Mobile](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGWGA2/usps-mobile) ## Related Articles - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [What is the USPS Mail Recovery Center?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-USPS-Mail-Recovery-Center) - [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics) - [USPS Retail Customer Appointment Scheduler®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Retail-Customer-Appointment-Scheduler) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## Export License for International Shipment

## Steps to obtain export information and Internal Transaction Number (ITN) for customs forms      - Sep 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007453

Customer Information

**VALUE AT $2,500 OR LESS AND/OR NOT REQUIRING AN EXPORT LICENSE (AES Exemption)**

Most shipments require an AES Exemption code. To fill out the appropriate option(s) through an electronically generated customs form, enter one of the following codes in block 10 (PS Form 2976-A), complete block 13 and/or block 14 (PS Form 2976-R), or check the appropriate box (PS Form 2976):

- **_NOEEI 30.36_** \- for authorized shipments, regardless of value, destined to Canada that does not require an export license.
- **_NOEEI 30.37(h)_**\- for authorized shipments of gift parcels or humanitarian donations that does not require an export license.

  - These countries are Crimea, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, and Syria.
- **_NOEEI 30.37(a)_**\- for goods valued at $2,500 or less each and does not require an export license.

  - This AES Exemption may not be used for items sent to Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, or Syria.
- _**NOEEI 30.37(y)**_\- for shipments containing only published books, software, or similar media, made available for general distribution to foreign libraries or similar institutions, and not requiring an export license.

  - These countries are Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, and Syria.

**VALUE OVER $2,500 AND/OR REQUIRING AN EXPORT LICENSE (AES)**

Follow these steps to file required export information and obtain an Internal Transaction Number (ITN) to enter on either electronically generated or handwritten customs forms (mailers must use PS Form 2976-A or PS Form 2976-R and an appropriate shipping option when an ITN is required):

1. Log on to [https://aesdirect.census.gov](https://www.census.gov/foreign-trade/aes/aesdirect/transitiontoace.html) and follow the instructions for registering and completing the Automated Export System (AES) Certification Quiz.

**Note:** The downtime citation includes the word "AESDOWN", your AES filer ID and the date - for example AESDOWN123456789 09/30/2010. For further information, please call the Census Bureau's AES hotline at **1-800-549-0595, Option 1**.

2. For the "Port of Export" code for shipping through the Postal Service™, enter _8000_.

3. For the "Mode of Transport" enter _Mail_.

4. Leave the carrier as "SCAC/IATA", and leave the conveyance name fields blank.


After successfully filing the electronic export information, customers will receive an alphanumeric Internal Transaction Number (ITN). The ITN consists of the letters AES followed by the ITN. For example: AES X20080930987654

## Choosing a Customs form to use. - Oct 15, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007510 Customer Information | | | | --- | --- | | **[Customs declaration form usage by mail category](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#customs_declaration_form_by_mail_category)** | [**Using customs forms**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#using_customs_forms) | | [**What is used to contain customs forms?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#contain_customs_forms) | | # **Customs Declaration Form Usage by Mail Category** | **Type of Item** | **Declared Value, Weight, or Physical Characteristics** | **Is Customs Form Required?** | **Comment (if applicable)** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ****Priority Mail Express International® Items**** | | **All items** | **All values** | _PS Form 2976-B_ [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_1) | Endorsements vary by country and are specified in the [Individual Country Listings](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immctry.htm). | | ****Priority Mail International® Items**** | | **All items** | **All values** | _PS Form 2976-A_ [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_1) | All items mailed in USPS-produced Priority Mail International packaging (including Flat Rate Envelopes and Small Flat Rate Priced Boxes) or any other container bearing a Priority Mail sticker or marked with the words "Priority Mail" are considered to be within the scope of this requirement (other than those marked "Priority Mail Express" or "Priority Mail Express International"). | | **First-Class Mail International® Letters and Large Envelopes (Flats), including IPA\* Letters and Large Envelopes (Flats) and ISAL\* Letters and Large Envelopes (Flats)**
\*IPA - International Priority Airmail; ISAL - International Surface Air Lift | | **All First-Class Mail International** [letter-size](https://www.usps.com/ship/preparing-domestic-shipments.htm) and [flat-size](https://www.usps.com/ship/preparing-domestic-shipments.htm) items **containing only nondutiable documents** | **Under 15.994 ounces** | No customs form required in this instance [2](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_2) | See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#documents) for additional information concerning "documents."
Items containing merchandise are prohibited in First-Class Mail International. | | **All IPA and ISAL** [letter-size](https://www.usps.com/ship/preparing-domestic-shipments.htm) and [flat-size](https://www.usps.com/ship/preparing-domestic-shipments.htm) items **containing only nondutiable documents** | **Under 15.994 ounces** | No customs form required in this instance [2](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_2) | See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#documents) for additional information concerning "documents."
Items containing merchandise are prohibited in IPA/ISAL letters and large envelopes (flats). | | **16 ounces to 17.6 ounces** | _PS Form 2976_ [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_1) | | **All items** containing any **goods**, regardless of weight. | **Prohibited** | Prohibited | See [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#documents) for additional information concerning "documents" and merchandise.
Items containing merchandise are prohibited in First-Class Mail International and in IPA/ISAL letters and large envelopes (flats). | | **First-Class Package International Service® Packages (Small Packets), including IPA\* Packages (Small Packets) and ISAL\* Packages (Small Packets)**
\*IPA - International Priority Airmail; ISAL - International Surface Air Lift | | **All First-Class Package International Service** packages, as referenced in the article [First Class Package International Service® (FCPIS)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Package-International-Service-FCPIS), regardless of contents, and, for commercial mailers, IPA packages and ISAL packages, regardless of contents | **$400 or less** | _PS Form 2976_ [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_1) | Merchandise is permitted unless prohibited by the destination country. | | **Over $400** | Prohibited | Items over $400 must be mailed using Priority Mail Express International service or Priority Mail International service. | | **Free Matter for the Blind** | | **All items** | **Follow above requirements for relevant mail category, as appropriate** | Follow above requirements for relevant mail category, as appropriate | See [What is Free Matter for the Blind or Other Physically Handicapped Persons?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Free-Matter-for-the-Blind-or-Other-Physically-Handicapped-Persons) or [IMM 270](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc2_032.htm) for authorized mail classes and eligibility. | | **M-bags (Airmail, IPA\* Service, and ISAL\* Service)**
\*IPA - International Priority Airmail; ISAL - International Surface Air Lift | | **All M-bags** | **$400 or less** | _PS Form 2976 or 2976-A_ [1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#table_note_1) | A fully completed PS Form 2976 or 2976-A must be affixed to PS Tag 158, _M-bag Addressee Tag_. | | **Over $400** | Prohibited | N/A | | 1 All customs forms must be [electronically generated](https://cfo.usps.com/). All USPS-supplied hard copies of PS Form 2976, 2976-A, and 2976-B with a preprinted barcode and designed to be completed by hand are obsolete and prohibited from use.
2 Certain nonnegotiable documents controlled by export regulatory agencies may require customs documentation. See [510](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc5_001.htm#ep357926)- [540](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc5_021.htm#ep398331) and [Publication 699](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub699.pdf) for additional information. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#top) **Using customs forms** - Duties are another form of taxes for exported or imported goods. "Non-dutiable documents" are paper documents that have no duty assessed. They include: audit and business records, personal correspondence, circulars, pamphlets, advertisements, written instruments not intended to be resold, money orders, checks, and similar items that cannot be negotiated or converted into cash without forgery. - The above "Type of Item" column has several references to "documents." For this purpose, documents refer only to printed documents, not to electronic storage media or devices such as CDs, DVDs, or flash drives. - Packaging must be large enough to accommodate the applicable customs form(s), postage, and any applicable markings, and extra service labels on the address side of the item to be mailed. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#top) **What is used to contain customs forms?** Customs forms and other documents required for international mailing and shipping can be placed in a USPS-provided envelope with adhesive, allowing it to adhere to the mailpiece. - [PS Form 2976-E, Customs Declaration Envelope CP 91](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976e.pdf) \- An adhesive-backed, plastic envelope that is used to contain and protect customs form sets when mailing items. (You can either request Customs Envelope 2976-E online or obtain it at no charge at your local Post Office™.) - [PS Form 2976-ES, Small Customs Declaration Pouch](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976es.pdf) \- A smaller, adhesive-backed, plastic envelope that is used to contain and protect customs form sets when mailing items. Ideal when your mailpiece is a smaller envelope or box. (You can either request Customs Envelope 2976-ES online or obtain it at no charge at your local Post Office™.) [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Which Customs Form do I Use? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000ptlAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number) ## How to find your tracking number ## This FAQ explains the different ways you as a sender or recipient can find the tracking number of a mailpiece. Lost tracking numbers cannot be recovered. - Oct 8, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007829 Customer Information We apologize in advance that Care Center agents and local Post Offices™ are **not able to provide help or assistance recovering lost tracking numbers.** When an item does have a tracking number, it is generated and provided to the sender. If you are the recipient expecting an item, you can ask the sender for that number. | | | --- | | **Examples of USPS tracking number formats can be found on the [USPS Tracking page](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input), section “What does my Tracking number look like?”** | Please keep in mind that not everything has a tracking number. Postcards and First-Class Mail® letters without extra services fall into this category. We invite all customers to sign up for _Informed Delivery®_ notifications by USPS®. Most USPS® domestic packages tied to the address associated with your USPS.com® account will be automatically available on your Informed Delivery® emails and dashboard. If you are concerned that an **item is delayed or lost**, whether or not it has a tracking number or you have its tracking number, see the following articles: - [Delayed mail and packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Where can I find my tracking number?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#where_find_tracking_number)** | [**I didn’t receive a tracking number, but want one for my item**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#didnt_receive_tracking_number) | [**I lost or forgot my tracking number**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#forgot_my_tracking_number) | | [**I’m waiting for an item. Can you tell me what the tracking number is?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#waiting_for_an_item) | [**Can I use a redelivery number instead of a tracking number?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#redelivery_number_instead_of_tracking_number) | [**Where is the tracking number located on receipts?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#information_on_sample_receipts) | | [**How do I access tracking information?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#access_tracking_information) | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Question** | **Explanation** | **Reminders** | | **Where can I find my tracking number?** | You may locate your tracking number on your:
- [Sales or mailing receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#information_on_sample_receipts)
- Online label record, if you purchased postage online (through Click-N-Ship®, eBay, or other services)
- Or below the barcode placed on the mailed item (see article [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics), section “How does USPS Tracking® work?") | Remember that postcards, First-Class Mail® letters and flats, and some USPS Marketing Mail® items may not have tracking numbers. | | **I didn’t receive a tracking number, but want one for my item.** | It is possible that you did not receive a tracking number **[because your item does not come with one](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Domestic-Mail-Extra-Services-are-Available)**. Such items include:
- Postcards
- First-Class Mail letters and flats
- Periodicals
- Some USPS Marketing Mail parcels
**If the item should include a tracking number**:
- **Senders**: [Did you check your receipt, your email, or Click-N-Ship or another service used to purchase online postage?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#where_find_tracking_number)
- **Recipients**:

- Ask the sender for the tracking number.
- Use Informed Delivery if a current Informed Delivery subscriber. | When you ship an item, be sure to:
- Add [an extra service](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) to your First-Class Mail letter or flat to make it eligible for USPS Tracking
- Mail it as a Priority Mail® letter.
**Care Center agents and local Post Offices do not have a way to recover lost tracking numbers.** | | **I lost or forgot my tracking number** | **Local Post Offices and Care Center agents do not have a way to recover lost tracking numbers.**
If the recipient is a [**current** Informed Delivery subscriber](https://reg.usps.com/portal/login?app=RMIN&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Finformeddelivery.usps.com%2Fbox%2Fpages%2Fintro%2Fstart.action%3Frestart%3D1), they will be able to see the tracking number on their dashboard. | If you, the sender, misplaced or forgot the tracking number, we suggest using these resources to find that number.
- [Your mailing receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#information_on_sample_receipts)
- Your online label record, if you purchased postage online (through Click-N-Ship®, eBay, or other service)
- [Your sales receipt](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#information_on_sample_receipts)
- Below the barcode placed on the mailed item (see ["USPS Tracking® - The Basics", section "What does a USPS Tracking label look like?"](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics))
- Ask the recipient if they are a current Informed Delivery subscriber. If they are, they should be able to see the tracking number on their dashboard | | **I’m waiting for an item. Can you tell me what the tracking number is?** | Unfortunately, local Post Offices and Care Center agents do not have a way to find an item’s tracking number.
The easiest way to find the tracking number of an expected item is to ask the sender.
If you are a [**current** Informed Delivery subscriber](https://reg.usps.com/portal/login?app=RMIN&appURL=https%3A%2F%2Finformeddelivery.usps.com%2Fbox%2Fpages%2Fintro%2Fstart.action%3Frestart%3D1), you can find the tracking number of expected items in your dashboard. | If you are **not a current** Informed Delivery subscriber, you can [sign up for it](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action). For any item with USPS Tracking®, recipients are able to view the delivery status of items in Informed Delivery notifications. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#top) **Can I use a redelivery number instead of a tracking number?** If you received a PS Form 3849, _We ReDeliver for You!_ notice, you can use the barcode number on the notice to search on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® as you would use a tracking number to get information on the mailpiece(s). Note that **entering the Redelivery barcode number will return all tracking numbers associated with that delivery attempt.** | | | | --- | --- | | ![Section of the back of PS Form 3849, We ReDeliver for You! Notice. To draw attention a red square is drawn over a barcode that is labeled "USPS" to its left. Under the "VARIABLE DATA" barcode is the sample barcode number "1A2B 3C1A 2B3C 1A2B"](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/3849_Jan_2018_Barcode_Back.jpg)
_Click image to make larger_ | ![Section of the front of PS Form 3849, We ReDelivery for You! Notice. To draw attention, a red square was drawn over a QR Code. To the right of the code is the text "For redelivery, scan the QR code or go to usps.com/redelivery and enter the barcode number shown below: 1A2B 3C1A 2B3C 1A2B"](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/3849_Jan_2018_Barcode_Front.jpg)
_Click image to make larger_ | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#top) **Where is the tracking number located on receipts?** | | | | --- | --- | | **Sales Receipt** | **Mailing Receipt** | | **Printed retail receipt**
[![Retail Receipt for purchase of USPS Ground Advantage and Priority Mail services. Full receipt is not shown in article, but clicking the link opens a new tab that displays the full receipt. Highlighted in red squares near the top under the Date and Time are all the Tracking Numbers associated with services purchased with this transaction. Red square highlights are found further down in the Purchase Details section to point out the Tracking Number associated with each purchased product. Highlighted in a blue square near the top under the Date and Time and Tracking Numbers is a section dedicated to finding the Tracking Status of items by QR Code, by Text Message, and Online.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/10.8.25_retail_receipt_partial.jpg)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/10.8.25_retail_receipt_full.jpg) | **Return Receipt**
![PS Form 3811 Front image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/return_receipt_ps3811front_July20.png)
![PS Form 3811 Back Image](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/return_receipt_ps3811back_July20.png) | | **Printed receipt from a Self-Service Kiosk**
[![Domestic Express Mail Receipt from a Self-Service Kiosk. Pointed out are the Guaranteed Delivery Date ("Thursday, July 7, by 3:00pm if you mail it here before 5:05pm today") and the Service Details ("Zone-6 Express Mail with $100.00 insurance"). The receipt also has the Label number, also known as the USPS Tracking number.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/ssk_receipt_pme-2-trackingnumber_shortened.png)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/ssk_receipt_pme-2-trackingnumber.png) | **Insurance Under $500**
![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/PSForm3813_4-15_Front-trackingnumber.jpg) | | **eReceipt (email)**
[![An eReceipt for a retail transaction. This image shows the top half of the eReceipt to focus on the acceptance of a Parcel Return Service item and the purchase of a Priority Mail 2-Day postage. Highlighted in both is the tracking number associated with each item.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/8.9.23_retail_eReceipt_priority_prs_partial.jpg)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/8.9.23_retail_eReceipt_priority_prs_complete.jpg) | **Insurance Over $500**
![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sending_mail/PSForm3813p_4-15_Front-trackingnumber.jpg) | | **Printed receipt from a Rapid Dropoff Station**
**![Image of a receipt printed out by the Rapid Dropoff Station. The transaction recorded by the receipt is for a Pre-paid Mail label and the Tracking Number. The Tracking Number, which only has 4 digits visible with the remaining digits covered by a black box, is highlighted by a red box.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/rapid%20dropoff/rapid_dropoff_receipt_tracking_number.jpg)** | | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-to-find-your-tracking-number#top) **How do I access tracking information?** The type of tracking and delivery information available is based on the class of mail or add-on services you purchased at the time of mailing. Whether you are the sender or recipient, you can track your item: | | | | --- | --- | | **Online** | Use [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) on [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/). | | **Informed Delivery** | See article [Informed Delivery® Dashboard, section "Informed Delivery® and USPS Tracking®.](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-Dashboard) ["](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-Dashboard) | | **By text** | Send a text to 28777 (2USPS) with your tracking number as the content of the message. Standard message and data rates may apply. [Click here for USPS Text Tracking™ FAQs](https://www.usps.com/text-tracking/welcome.htm). | | **By smartphone** | Download the USPS Mobile® app for iPhone® and Android™. | | | | --- | --- | | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/apple_app_store.png)](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/usps-mobile/id339597578) | [![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/google_play.png)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.usps) | | | **By phone** | - To obtain tracking information, customers can go to [Contact USPS](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm#kilo) for the phone number and hours of operation.
- For telecommunications device for the Deaf/Teletypewriter (TDD/TTY), call 1-800-877-8339.
- Listen to automated information 24 hours/seven days per week. | **Can I receive automatic notifications?** Yes. For further information on automatic notifications, see [USPS Tracking® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Tracking-The-Basics). metadata.description: This FAQ explains the different ways you as a sender or recipient can find the tracking number of a mailpiece. Lost tracking numbers cannot be recovered.

## Identity Theft

## Identifying and protecting yourself from Identity Theft.      - Mar 15, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007958

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [**What is Identity Theft?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#What_is_Identity_theft "What is Identity Theft") | [**Steps to Take if the Crime DID NOT Involve the U.S. Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#didnotinvolveusmail) |
| [**Protecting Yourself from Identity Theft**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#potectmyselfformidentitytheft) | [**Change of Address and Identity Fraud**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#changeofaddress) |
| [**Steps to Take if the Crime Involved the U.S. Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#involveusmail) |  |

****What is Identity Theft?****

Identity theft involves acquiring key pieces of someone's personally identifying information (PII), such as name, address, birth date, Social Security number, or mother's maiden name, in order to impersonate them. The stolen PII enables the thief to commit numerous forms of fraud which may include:

- Taking over the victim's financial accounts
- Opening new bank accounts in the victim's name
- Purchasing automobiles
- Applying for loans, credit cards, and Social Security benefits
- Renting apartments
- Establishing utility or phone services
- Tax-related ID theft such as submitting income tax documents to obtain refunds

See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud) and [Mail Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud).

**How Do I Protect Myself From Identity Theft?**

There are several precautions you can take to protect yourself from identity theft:

- Retrieve mail as soon after delivery as possible, or have a friend do it.
- Sign up for [Informed Delivery®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics), a free feature allowing you to digitally preview your incoming mail and manage your arriving packages.
- Sign up for a [PO Box](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm "https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm") [™](https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm "https://www.usps.com/manage/po-boxes.htm"). PO Box services offer security and privacy, and many Post Office™ locations offer extended or 24/7 PO Box access.
- Install a locking mailbox. Many retailers offer such boxes for purchase.
- Place your outgoing mail in one of the Postal Service’s convenient collection boxes, use the mail service at your place of business, or visit your local Post Office™ location if security is a concern in your neighborhood.
- Be certain to place your mail on hold with your local Post Office location if you are going to be away on vacation or business.
- Call the sender of a valuable item to determine when the package should be expected.
- Familiarize yourself with your financial institutions’ policy on changes of address submitted by phone or mail. Make sure they will call customers before making a change, or honoring any large withdrawal, not received in person.

The [U.S. Postal Inspection Service website](https://www.uspis.gov/ "https://www.uspis.gov/") offers additional identity theft prevention tips, along with an informative brochure titled _[Publication 280 -  Identity Theft](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub280/welcome.htm) [, Safeguard your personal information](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub280/welcome.htm)._

The publication includes information that can help you in protecting your good name, including:

- Tips on protecting yourself from identity theft.
- Steps to take if you believe someone has stolen your identity.
- Phone numbers and website addresses for the three major credit reporting agencies.
- Links to web sites with more information on ID theft.
- Phone numbers to call to get publications dealing with identity theft from other organizations.
- Addresses to the U.S. Postal Inspection Service divisions.

You can get additional information at the [U.S. Postal Inspection Service Identity Theft website](https://www.uspis.gov/tips-prevention/identity-theft/).

See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud) and [Mail Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud).

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#top)

**Steps to Take if you think you are a Victim of Identity Theft**

- **If the crime involved the U.S. Mail**
  - Submit the [Identity Theft Complaint Form](https://ehome.uspis.gov/mailtheft/idtheft.aspx) online to lodge a complaint with the U.S. Postal Inspection Service.
  - Ask 1 of the 3 credit reporting companies to put a fraud alert on your credit report. They must tell the other 2 companies – make sure to confirm that this will be done. A fraud alert is free, but you must provide proof of your identity.
  - Order copies of your credit report from the credit bureaus to check whether any fraudulent accounts were opened without your knowledge or consent. Check your monthly financial statements for accuracy.
    - [Equifax](http://www.equifax.com/CreditReportAssistance/ "Equifax") \- **800-525-6285**
    - [Experian](https://www.experian.com/consumer/cac/InvalidateSession.do?code=SECURITYALERT "Experian") \- **888-397-3742**
    - [Trans Union](http://www.transunion.com/fraud-victim-resource/place-fraud-alert "Trans Union") \- **800-680-7289**
  - Contact your banks and creditors, by phone and in writing, and report the crime. You may be advised to close some or all of your accounts. At the least, alter your PIN numbers and passwords immediately.
  - Record the names and phone numbers of people with whom you discussed your case and retain all original reports and supporting documents. Keeping accurate and complete records are a big step toward helping you resolve your problem.
  - Contact your financial institutions and request they flag your accounts. Instruct them to contact you immediately if there is unusual activity on your accounts.
- **If the crime did NOT involve the US Mail**
  - Visit the Federal Trade Commission's identity theft website at [Identitytheft.gov](https://identitytheft.gov/) or their main website at [ftc.gov](https://www.ftc.gov/) for additional information on reporting and recovering from identity theft.
  - File your concern online with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) by email at [consumerline@ftc.gov](mailto:consumerline@ftc.gov), or call their Identity Theft Hotline at **1-877-IDTHEFT (1-877-438-4338)**. The FTC has counselors to assist identity theft victims with resolving financial and other problems that can result from this crime.

See related information on" [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft)", [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud), and [Mail Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud).

**Change of Address and Identity Theft**

Each year, the Postal Service™ processes millions of address changes. The U.S. Postal Service has adopted procedures that are designed to prevent false changes of address from being filed. By using confirmation letters and other methods of identity verification, the U.S. Postal Service® takes steps to help ensure that consumers and businesses are safe from would-be thieves.

However, identity thieves often prefer to change an address directly with a vendor, bank, or other financial institution, rather than risk being detected by the Postal Service’s verification process. Contact your financial institution to learn more about the security procedures they’ve adopted to protect your personal information.

See related information on [Mail Theft](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Theft), [Credit Card Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Credit-Card-Fraud), and [Mail Fraud](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Mail-Fraud).

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Identity-Theft#top)

- USPS Ground Advantage® \- Commercial | - Parcel Select®
- Media Mail® and Library Mail retail
- Media Mail and Library Mail commercial
- Every Door Direct Mail-Retail® flats
- USPS Marketing Mail®
- Bound Printed Matter
- Free Matter for the Blind and Other Physically Handicapped Persons
- Periodicals | | **International** | - Priority Mail Express International®
- First-Class Package International Service®
- First-Class Mail International®
- International Surface Air Lift® (ISAL®) Service, excluding ISAL M-bags®
- International Priority Airmail® (IPA®) Service, excluding IPA M-bags | - Priority Mail International®
- Free Matter for the Blind
- M-bags | \*Regardless of physical closure, the mailing of these items constitute consent by the mailer to inspection of the contents. Items that are opened and found to have been incorrectly charged a lower price (when they should have gone as a different class, such as First-Class Mail® or Priority Mail prices) will be charged additional postage and/or fees as applicable. USPS has [other methods to verify postage](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program). **Notes**: - Other restrictions (such as those on items that are hazardous or illegal to mail) still exist on all classes of mail. - All international mail is subject to inspection by customs officials of the destination country. - If you have questions about whether your item fits a specific mail class, you can contact the Mailing Requirements office of your local Post Office™. - $1.25 identify verification for online COA requests. | | **Permanent Change of Address** | A permanent Change of Address (COA) order provides for “piece-by-piece” forwarding of primarily First-Class Mail® service for 12 months and Periodicals for 60 days, but generally does not provide the forwarding of USPS Marketing Mail® service or Package Services Mail. The customer's new address is provided to business mailers upon request through mailer endorsements on a mailpiece. | **Free**
- $1.25 identify verification for online COA requests. | | Premium Forwarding Service Residential® Service (PFS-Residential® Service): | - PFS-Residential® Service provides a single weekly shipment of all mail for a residence via Priority Mail® service for a fee. The service can be extended for up to a year. PFS-Residential is temporary and is offered as an additional option to the free temporary or permanent Change of Address (COA) option.
- PFS-Residential can be started at a retail counter at your local Post Office™; PFS-Residential also has an online application that can be accessed at: [www.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/) \> Receive > Forward Mail.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000lVBAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Operation-Santa-Greetings-from-the-North-Pole-and-the-North-Pole-Postmark) ## USPS Operation Santa®, Greetings from the North Pole and the North Pole Postmark ## Special holiday services from USPS like how to mail a letter or package with a holiday-themed postmark and getting Santa to reply to your child's letter! - Sep 15, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007713 Customer Information **2025 USPS Operation Santa® Program** The USPS Operation Santa program encourages the public to adopt letters written to Santa. For 113 years, this program has helped children and families have a magical holiday when they otherwise might not. The Postal Service offers three very special holiday-focused services: 1. **USPS Operation Santa**\- allows people to adopt actual letters written to Santa online and send thoughtful, heartfelt gifts anonymously – acting as Santa. - To learn more about USPS Operation Santa® and adopting letters, please visit [https://www.uspsoperationsanta.com/](https://www.uspsoperationsanta.com/) and [https://usps.com/holidaynews](https://about.usps.com/holidaynews/). 2. **Greetings from the North Pole**\- allows people to keep the magic alive and seemingly have Santa Claus send a child a personal letter this holiday. - To learn more about Greetings from the North Pole, to learn more about obtaining a North Pole postmark, please visit: [https://about.usps.com/holidaynews/letters-from-santa.htm](https://about.usps.com/holidaynews/letters-from-santa.htm). 3. **North Pole Postmark**. \- allows people to have their holiday greetings cards postmarked with the North Pole postmark for extra holiday fun. - To learn more about obtaining a North Pole postmark please visit: [https://about.usps.com/holidaynews/letters-from-santa.htm](https://about.usps.com/holidaynews/letters-from-santa.htm). | | | --- | | If you have additional questions that weren't answered by the FAQs or technical difficulty:
- Please visit:  [USPS Operation Santa® FAQs](https://www.uspsoperationsanta.com/faqs)
