## Rapid Dropoff Station

## A device that allows you to quickly print Label Broker labels, receive acceptance scans for your prepaid labels and drop off those packages efficiently and without assistance.      - Sep 9, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000008348

Customer Information

The Rapid Dropoff Station (RDS) is a quick, one-stop-shop kiosk for customers looking to drop off packages with prepaid labels or Label Broker® labels without waiting in line at the retail window.

You can print Label Broker labels, receive prepaid mail acceptance scans for your shipment and drop off packages with these devices. You can complete as many label prints and Prepaid Mail Acceptance scans as needed in one session by selecting the \[Scan Another\] option before completing your transaction.

Rapid Dropoff Station Diagram

![Diagram of a Rapid Dropoff Station. It points out parts and features like the Touch-Screen Tablet on top of the table and the Barcode Scanner under the tablet. Other parts of the table include the ADA-Compliant Keypad and the Printer Drawer under the keypad, the Receipt Printer Slot in the center section of table and the Label Catch.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/rapid%20dropoff/rapid_dropoff_station_diagram.jpg)

|     |
| --- |
| **Note:** The RDS is not equipped to handle payment transactions like purchasing stamps or labels. You can receive assistance from the retail window to make these purchases or use Self-Service Kiosks (SSKs) which have additional functionalities, including payment transactions. |

**Print a Label Broker Label**

1. Scan your Label Broker QR Code using the barcode scanner below the screen. If you are having trouble scanning the QR Code, you can manually enter the Label Broker code.
2. Follow onscreen instructions. This includes confirming that your package does not contain any prohibited or restricted items.
   - If you confirm that your package does contain prohibited or restricted items, the RDS will direct you to visit the retail window for further instructions.
3. After confirming that a 4" x 6" label will fit on your package, the station prints a label that drops into the label catch.
4. Choose whether or not you want a receipt with the tracking number printed and/or emailed.
   - With the tracking number found on their printed or emailed receipt, you can visit [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and track the package.
5. Affix the label onto your package and drop it off in a parcel drum or in-wall drop before leaving the facility.

**Scan and Dropoff a Package with a Prepaid Label**

1. Scan the prepaid label using the barcode scanner below the screen. If you are having trouble scanning the label, you can manually enter the pre-paid acceptance code.
2. Follow onscreen instructions. This includes confirming that your package does not contain any prohibited or restricted items.
   - If you confirm that your package does contain prohibited or restricted items, the RDS will direct you to visit the retail window for further instructions.
3. After the station confirms successful label scan, choose whether or not you want a receipt with the tracking number printed and/or emailed.
   - With the tracking number found on their printed or emailed receipt, you can visit [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) and track the package.
4. Drop off the package in a parcel drum or in-wall drop before leaving the facility.

**What should I do if a printed label does not fit on the package?**

Repackage your mail in a larger envelope or box before attaching the label to your package.

Rapid Dropoff Station Receipt

![Image of a receipt printed out by the Rapid Dropoff Station. The transaction recorded by the receipt is for a Pre-paid Mail label and the Tracking Number.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/rapid%20dropoff/rapid_dropoff_receipt.jpg)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000luzAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Specific-Restrictions-for-Military-Mail) ## What are the Specific Restrictions for Military Mail? ## Restrictions on Military Mail - Sep 4, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007107 Customer Information Mail addressed to Military or Diplomatic Post offices overseas may be subject to restrictions regarding contents, preparation and handling. Individual military units may have additional restrictions which can vary by location. Military ZIP Code™ restrictions may be revised as military units move to different locations. Broadly, there are restrictions applicable to both Military Post Office (MPO) locations and Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) locations: | | | | --- | --- | | **MPO** | The maximum length of a package, no matter the mail class, is 72 inches. | | **DPO** | **Effective July 18th, 2025**, the Department of State added size restrictions for all packages going to or coming from any Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) locations, including the Department of State. The size restrictions are: | | | | --- | --- | | **Mail to U.S. Department of State (ZIP Codes 20521 and 20189)** | - Maximum length - 29 inches
- Maximum width - 29 inches
- Maximum height - 29 inches | | **U.S. Department of State mail to DPO Destinations** | - Maximum length - 27 inches
- Maximum width - 14 inches
- Maximum height - 14 inches | | | **Any package exceeding those dimensions is subject to being returned to the sender.** | The [APO / FPO / DPO Overseas Military and Diplomatic tool](https://postcalc.usps.com/MilitaryRestrictions?_gl=1*9c08w8*_gcl_au*MTI2NjA1ODkxMC4xNzM3NDgwODY5*_ga*OTA1NTY1ODA0LjE3Mzc0ODA4Njk.*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTczNzU2MTMwNy44LjEuMTczNzU2MTMyOS4wLjAuMA..) outlines these and other restrictions by Army Post Office and Fleet Post Office and DPO ZIP Codes. APO's, FPO's and DPO's are considered domestic ZIP Codes; therefore, the use of Priority Mail® Flat Rate Box packaging and prices is acceptable, subject to these restrictions. **Note**: Customers that would like to access this information online can be referred to the _[Postal Bulletin](https://about.usps.com/resources/postal-bulletin.htm)_ at: [http://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/welcome.htm](http://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/welcome.htm) (Military Mail updates are included in each release.) Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000joLAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) ## USPS.com® Account ## Learn how to create and manage your USPS.com® account, reset your password, and recover your username. Also, learn about other security features. - Aug 22, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006250 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Creating a personal or business account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#howtocreate)** | **[Logging In](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#login)** | **[Account Recovery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#accountrecovery)** | | **[Add Saved Payment](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#addpayment)** | [**Two-Factor Authentication**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#Two-Factor_Authentication) | [**Forgot Password or Username**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#forgot_username_password) | | [**Add a Contact to the Address Book**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#addcontact) | **[Find information on previous orders](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#findinfo)** | **[Modify from Activity History](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#modify)** | | **[Cancel from Activity History](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#cancel)** | **[Cancel PFS from Activity History](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#cancelpfs)** | [**Email Preferences**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#emailpref) | # **Create an Account** **How to create a personal account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®** 1. Hover over the “Register/Sign In” link on the grey header bar at the top of the Homepage and clicking “Sign Up Now.” 2. Select "Personal Account" and click continue 3. Enter a username and then click “Check This Name” to check if the username is available. If it is not available, the system will provide recommended usernames that are available. Choose a desired password and enter it in the two different fields. 4. Select two security questions and provide answers for each 5. Enter your contact information including; name, address, phone number, and email address 6. Click the “Create Account” button when completed **How to create a business account on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®** 1. Hover over the “Register/Sign In” link on the grey header bar at the top of the Homepage and clicking “Sign Up Now.” 2. Select "Business Account" and click continue 3. Enter your business email address and click submit 4. Enter your Company Information including your business name and address and click continue 5. Enter your contact information including: name, phone number, and email address and click Verify Account 6. Enter a username then click “Check This Name” to check if the username is available. If it is not available, the system will provide recommended usernames that are available. Choose a desired password and enter it in the two different fields. 7. Select two security questions and provide answers for each 8. Click the “Create Account” button when completed **NOTE:** Once created, a personal or business account cannot be changed from one to the other. If you have a personal account and need a business account instead, you will need a create a new business account and vice versa. **Managing the Account** [**Logging in**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#login) To access your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) Account: 1. Hover over the “Register/Sign In” link on the grey header bar at the top of the Homepage and type your username and password in the designated fields 2. The system will sign you in 3. You can hover over “Hi,” and click “My Profile” to view your account details 4. The Account contains left navigation links to access your: | | | | --- | --- | | - **Profile**
- **Saved Payment Info**
- **Address Book**
- **Activity History** | - **Legacy Orders**
- **Favorites**
- **Stamp Subscriptions** | **Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)** Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is an optional security feature for accounts. If enabled, you are sent an SMS text message to your mobile phone number after successfully entering your username and password. The message contains a 6-digit access code which must be successfully entered to complete the login process. - If enabled, the 6-digit access code must be entered every time you login to your account. - Sign in to your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account and go to the Preferences page to learn more and enable 2FA. # **Account Recovery** [Account Recovery](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/AccountRecoveryHelpAction_input) is a two-step process of validating your mobile phone number and opting in to the service. By opting in, you will have the option of using your validated mobile device to unlock your account. Account Recovery is enabled from the Preferences page of your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account. **Forgot your password or username?** - **If you forgot your password**, you can reset it by going to the [USPS.com Account sign-in page](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/), and select "Forgot your password?" Enter your username for a temporary password to be sent to the email address associated with your account. Please go to [How do I reset my password or change my username on USPS.com®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-reset-my-password-or-change-my-username-on-USPS-com) for more information. - **If you forgot your username**, you can retrieve it by going to the [USPS.com Account sign-in page](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=Phoenix&appURL=https://www.usps.com/), and select "Forgot your username?" Enter the email address associated with your account to have your username emailed to you. If you do not remember the email address you created the account with, you will need to create a new account. - **NOTE: Usernames cannot be changed.** # **Add Saved Payment** 1. Navigate to your profile and click “Saved Payment Info” from the left navigation menu 2. Click the "Add Card" button 3. Enter the following under the Credit Card Information section and the Billing Address section. Customers can also select if the card is their preferred card and if they have read, understood, and agreed to the Terms & Conditions: | | | | --- | --- | | **Credit Card Information** | **Billing Address** | | Cardholder's Name as it appears on card | Address | | Card Nickname (Business card, Personal card, etc.) - Optional | City | | Expires on (Expiration date) | State | | | ZIP Code™ | 4. Click the "Save Card" button. Up to 3 credit cards can be saved. - **Note:**Updates made to customers credit/debit card on their [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) main account will not automatically transfer to the card saved under the “Manage Po Box” tab of a customers POBOL account. # **Add a contact to the Address Book** 1. Navigate to your profile and click “Address Book” from the left navigation 2. Click the “Add a New Address” button 3. Enter the contact information; name, address, country, phone, and email 4. Indicate if this is your default shipping address 5. Click "Save Address" # **Find information on previous orders** - **Activity History will provide you with information on previous Hold Mail, Redelivery, Schedule a Pickup, The Postal Store®, and Premium Forwarding Service® orders (Click-N-Ship® labels can be viewed in CNS Shipping History)** | | | | --- | --- | | ![Screenshot of USPS.com Account page on Activity History. The focus of the page is a table with five columns. The headers of the first four columns are: Order#, Items, Order Placed, and Status. The last column has no header. In the first row of the table providing information, the Order# is o181500001, Items is Merchandise, Order Placed is 09/28/2015, and Status is Order Processing. The last column has either a single link or two links for each row; View Details and Edit.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/Postal_Store-Activity_History-1.jpg) | 1. Order number
2. Item or service ordered
3. Date order was placed
4. Status of order
5. Link to view additional order details | - To see additional details on a specific order, click “View Details” from the list view. The detailed information page provides information about the order number, dates, action (create/update/cancel), name, address, email, instructions, and confirmation number. An edit link is also available that will direct a customer to the cancel/modify page. Information for an order will stay in the Activity History for 1 year. - The Postal Store® account has been integrated with the [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® Account. Postal Store orders, including Subscriptions placed before January 22, 2012 will be available in the Legacy Orders tab of the [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) Account. Any orders placed 12 months prior will be pulled into the Account. All Postal Store orders placed after January 22, 2012 will be available in the Activity History of the [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) Account. - _**Note:** It is not possible to delete an item from the Global Account._ # **Modify a Hold Mail, Redelivery, Schedule a Pickup, or Premium Forwarding Service order from Activity History** 1. Navigate to your profile 2. Click “Activity History” from the left navigation 3. Find the order you would like to edit from the list of previous orders and click “View Details” 4. Click “Edit” and the system will take you the editable page 5. Edit the information necessary for modification 6. Click “Make Changes” 7. Confirmation page will be displayed with the update and confirmation number. Your confirmation number will also be located in the details of your Activity History. Any modification that is made to an existing order will appear in the Activity History as a new line item. # **Cancel a Hold Mail or Schedule a Pickup order from Activity History** 1. Navigate to your account 2. Click Activity History from the left navigation 3. Find the order you want to cancel from the list of previous orders and click “View Details” 4. Click “Edit” and the system will take you the editable page 5. Click “Need to cancel this scheduled pickup?” link 6. Click “Yes” to confirm you want to cancel the pickup 7. Confirmation page will be displayed with the cancellation and confirmation number. Your confirmation number will also be located in the details of your Activity History. A cancellation that is made to an existing order will appear in the Activity History as a new line item. # **Cancel a Premium Forwarding Service order from Activity History** 1. Navigate to your account 2. Click Activity History from the left navigation 3. Find the order you want to cancel from the list of previous orders 4. Click “Cancel Order” 5. Click “OK” on the pop-up box to verify that you want to cancel the Premium Forwarding Service Order # **Email Preferences** The Postal Service uses an opt-in marketing program that may be selected in the users account. If the customer wants to stop receiving email notifications from the Postal Service, the customer must edit their profile preferences: - Click "sign-in" from the top right corner of USPS.com. - Enter your username and password - Click "Sign In" - This will take you to your Profile page - Click "Preferences" on the left-hand column - Uncheck the boxes under " **Can we contact you**:" communication preferences and click Submit and continue again if everything looks correct on the summary page. [Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS.com® Account URL Name USPS-com-Account [USPS.com / Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFvGAM/uspscom-services)[Technical Support](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGVGA2/technical-support) ## Related Articles - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## Find USPS® Locations

## Find USPS Locations (or send directions) and hours of operation using www.usps.com.      - Jul 30, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006083

Customer Information

To locate a Post Office™, approved partner facilities, or other USPS® services such as stamps, passport acceptance, and Self-Service Kiosks, go to [Find USPS Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/home.htm "Post Office Locator") on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®. Then follow these steps:

1. Fill in your City and State or just your ZIP Code™.

2. In the drop-down list under “Location Types”, make a selection (Post Office™, Self-Service Kiosk, Collection Box, etc.). Default Location Type is Post Office.

3. Click on the “Filters” button and check or uncheck the [services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Find-USPS-Locations#services) and hours of operations that you want to filter by. After selecting, click the "Apply Filter(s)" button.
4. Click the “Search” button and you will see a list of Postal facilities that match your search criteria in order of distance to the location you entered.


**What do all these services mean?**

You are able to see a number of services listed for various locations on Find USPS Locations. In order to find more details about these services, there is a [glossary page](https://www.usps.com/locator/glossary.htm) with brief descriptions of these services. The direct URL is [https://www.usps.com/locator/glossary.htm](https://www.usps.com/locator/glossary.htm).

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000h6bAAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mailing-Addresses-for-Consumers) ## USPS Mailing Addresses for Consumers ## Mailing addresses for USPS consumers to send correspondence to USPS and high-ranking government officials, when no phone numbers or other information is available. - Aug 6, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006980 Customer Information **The following addresses have no phone numbers or other information that we can supply:** | | | | --- | --- | | **Organization** | **Address** | | **Consumer Advocate** | **Consumer Advocate**

## How do I Use or Reuse Boxes Properly?

## Information concerning the use or reuse of boxes.      - Sep 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006790

Customer Information

**What is the proper way to reuse a box?**

Recycling and re-using packaging is good for the environment, but it has to be done correctly to be sent through the mail. Don’t reuse mailing boxes; they can weaken in the shipping process. Re-used boxes and containers that once held liquor, wine, beer, cosmetics or cleaning supplies must have markings completely removed or obliterated. If they still display markings associated with hazardous, prohibited or restricted materials, United States Postal Service® employees are required to handle the item as if the markings reflect the contents. For the safety of employees, customers and the public, such an item will be removed from the mailstream and the sender will be responsible for retrieving it.

**Can I Use Priority Mail Express® Service or Priority Mail® Service Envelopes for Other Mail?**

If any Priority Mail Express service or Priority Mail service packaging or packaging supplies e.g., Priority Mail tape to seal a USPS Ground Advantage™ package, are used on any other class of mail, the applicable Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail price will be charged.

- Misuse may be a violation of federal law.
- Turning the packaging inside out to conceal the Priority Mail / Priority Mail Express insignia is _specific misuse_ that is _not allowed_.

Any matter mailed in these free boxes is charged the appropriate Priority Mail Express or Priority Mail price, regardless of how the packaging is reconfigured (including being turned inside out, cut, shrunk, enlarged, etc.) or how the markings are obliterated.

Mail dropped in a Collection Box receptacle using inappropriate packaging (including, for example, mailing Priority Mail items in an Priority Mail Express box) is _either_:

- Sent on as insufficient postage mail OR
- Returned to the sender.

Treatment of this mail is decided by the Post Office™ facility handling the mailpiece.

**Note:** The USPS® provides some packaging and supplies free of charge for:

- Priority Mail® (Domestic) items
- Priority Mail Express® (Domestic) items
- Priority Mail International® items
- Priority Mail Express International **®** (PMEI) items

**Can I Use Boxes Ordered at The Postal Store® for Other Purposes?**

Boxes ordered from the Postal Store can be used only for the class of mail specified on the box (Priority Mail Express **®** service or Priority Mail® service) and must be closed using the adhesive provided. These boxes should not be re-used and do not need to be as they can be ordered from the Postal Store at no cost. Misuse may be a violation of federal law.

**Note**: Priority Mail Flat Rate® Boxes can be used both domestically and internationally. The DVD Flat Rate box and the Large Video Flat Rate box are only available at a flat rate when used internationally.

The terms of Agreement for the use of United States Postal Service shipping supplies is as follows: I understand that Priority Mail Express service, Priority Mail service, Priority Mail Express International and Priority Mail International packaging is the property of the United States Postal Service and is provided solely for sending Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail, Priority Mail Express International and Priority Mail International. Misuse may be a violation of federal law.

## How can I use insurance?

## Information on domestic and international items where insurance can be used.      - Jul 16, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007248

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [**Insurance can be used with the following domestic mail classes**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#domestic_mail_classes) | [**Using insurance for international mailing**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#using_insurance_international_mailing) |
| [**Why wasn't I able to insure an international package?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#not_able_to_insure_international_package) | [**Designating insurance on a customs form**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#designating_insurance_on_customs_form) |

**Insurance can be used with the following Domestic Mail Classes**:

_**Priority Mail Express®**_

- Up to $100 insurance coverage included in price of postage with the following limits:
  - $100 for merchandise
  - $100 for document reconstruction
  - $15 for cash
- Additional insurance coverage may be purchased for up to $5,000 for merchandise only
- **Note**: Not checking "Signature Required" will void insurance indemnity

_**Priority Mail® Insurance**_

- Up to $100 insurance coverage included in price of postage.
- Additional coverage may be purchased at any Post Office in increments up to $5,000.

**_First-Class Mail®_**

- Available for merchandise only (i.e. you cannot insure correspondence)

_**USPS Ground Advantage™ - Retail and USPS Ground Advantage™ - Commercial**_

- Up to $100 insurance coverage included in price of postage.
- Additional coverage may be purchased in increments up to $5,000.

_**USPS Marketing Mail®**_

- Bulk insurance only

_**Package Services and Parcel Select**_

- Available for merchandise only (i.e. you cannot insure correspondence)
- _**Official government mail endorsed “Postage and Fees Paid.”**_

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#top)

**Using insurance for international mailing**

- Insurance is not available to all countries. Check the availability of insurance options for a specific item and destination by getting a quote: [Calculate Postage](https://postcalc.usps.com/) or check the [IMM Country Listings](http://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm).

- Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI) items include up to $200 insurance for merchandise and $100 for documents. Additional insurance may be purchased. Check IMM Country Listings for availability.

- For Priority Mail International® Flat Rate and variable weight parcels (not available for Flat Rate Envelopes or Small Flat Rate Boxes), insurance is included up to $200 for merchandise and up to $100 for nonnegotiable documents. Additional insurance is available for a fee. Check the IMM country listings for availability.

- Insurance provides coverage against loss, damage, and/or missing contents.

- The maximum insurance amount varies by individual country.

- Insurance fees are in addition to postage and vary depending on amount of coverage.

- For information on filing claims, please visit " [File an Insurance Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/claims.htm)”. Please note that for International lost articles, an inquiry needs to be filed before a claim can be submitted.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#top)

**Why wasn't I able to insure an international package?**

Many countries outside of the U.S. do not offer insured mail service, or accept insured parcels from other countries. Find more information in the IMM about extra services in the [Index of Countries](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm).

First-Class Mail International®, First-Class Package International™, Priority Mail International® Flat Rate Envelopes and Priority Mail International® Small Flat Rate Boxes do not have Insurance as an option but, depending on the destination, you may be able to send them Registered, which includes Indemnity (Insurance) coverage. See [International Mail Manual (IMM)](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) for more information.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#top)

**Designating insurance on a customs form**

Additional insurance may be purchased for a fee (for eligible flat rate boxes or variable weight articles) to many countries. International insured articles use [PS Form 2976-A](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976a.pdf) or [PS Form 2976-B](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976b.pdf) to record the purchase of insurance.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-can-I-use-insurance#top)

## What can I do in Click-N-Ship® Shipping History?

## Available options in the Click-N-Ship® Shipping History tab: print and reprint labels, request a refund of unused labels and more.      - Sep 19, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007317

Customer Information

**Shipping History Actions for Legacy Click-N-Ship**

**Shipping History allows you to manage your shipments easily and in one location. The Actions dropdown menu gives you the following tools:**

- **USPS Tracking Tool** - Use it for status of your shipment, without navigating away from the page.
- **Print/Re-Print\*** - Print or re-print labels quickly and easily.
- **Save as PDF\***\- Save a label in PDF format to print at your convenience.
- **Ship Again** \- Re-create frequently used labels for faster shipping.
- **Request a Refund** - Request a Refund for an unused Click-N-Ship® label.

**\*** Print/Re-print and Save as PDF features are only available until midnight CST of the label's ship date.

**Acquiring Shipping History Information in Legacy Click-N-Ship**

**Click-N-Ship Shipping History is separate from your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account Activity History.** No Click-N-Ship (CNS) labels will appear in Activity History. To see your Shipping History, you must be [logged into CNS](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=GSS&appURL=https://cns.usps.com/labelInformation.shtml).

The Legacy CNS application on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) no longer offers an export/download feature on the Shipping History page. To achieve similar results, please take one of the following steps:

- Take a screenshot of the Shipping History page and paste it into another document.
- Copy and paste the information from the Shipping History page into a spreadsheet. See image below.

![Screenshot of the Shipping History page on the Click-N-Ship (original) application. Two sets of directions have been added to the screenshot. The first set, which has an arrow pointed to a calendar icon to the left of the Search button, says: First, use the calendar icon to select the month of Shipping History you would like to see. The second set of directions, which has an arrow pointed to the word Date in top left corner of the Shipping History table, says: next, to save your Shipping History, copy and paste the information from the Shipping History table into a document or spreadsheet.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/cns_shipping_history/5.31.24_CNS%20Shipping%20History_How%20to%20Save.jpg)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000ctJAAQ%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-Library-Mail-Service-and-Media-Mail-Service) ## What is Library Mail Service? ## Library Mail service is an economical shipping solution for qualified items to be mailed between academic institutions, public libraries, museums and other qualified organizations. - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006791 Customer Information ****Features**** - Library Mail is an economical shipping solution for qualified items generally used by qualified libraries, schools, nonprofit organizations, universities, and museums to mail specific types of Package Services matter that meet additional eligibility standards. - Qualified items and Package Services matter eligible for Library Mail include books, sound recordings, and periodicals. - See the [_Domestic Mail Manual_](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/173.htm#ep1113509) for additional information on eligible senders and addressees and qualified mailings and items. - Packages can weigh up to 70 lbs. and measure up to 108 inches in combined length and distance around the thickest part. - Packages are to show the name of the school, non-profit organization, library, etc. in the address or return address. - See [Business Shipping - Rates, Discounts, Services \| USPS](https://www.usps.com/business/business-shipping.htm) for commercial customer information and [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c156) for commercial prices. **Notes** - Library Mail is not sealed against postal inspection. Contents can be inspected by the Postal Service to insure pricing eligibility. | | | --- | | For in-depth information on Library Mail, go to the [_Domestic Mail Manual_](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/173.htm). For pricing information, go to [the Price List (Notice 123)](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c059).

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000dRBAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Nonprofit-Mail) ## What is Nonprofit Mail? ## Nonprofit Mail is printed material eligible to be mailed as USPS Marketing Mail® at significantly reduced nonprofit postage rates. - Jul 12, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007682 Customer Information Nonprofit Mail is printed material eligible to be mailed as USPS Marketing Mail® by organizations authorized by the Postal Service™ to mail at significantly reduced nonprofit postage rates. Nonprofit mail cannot contain matter that is required to be mailed as First-Class Mail®. For example, handwritten or typewritten matter, or bills and statements of account, are required to be mailed as First-Class Mail. **What items can be mailed using Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail?** Self-mailers, letters, newsletters and booklets that weigh less than 16 ounces are otherwise eligible to be mailed as [USPS Marketing Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Marketing-Mail). Create Mail Online prints, inserts, applies postage, presorts, and enters your mail at one or more central processing facilities nationwide. Only material of the authorized organization can be mailed at nonprofit prices. Mailings cannot be done for an unauthorized party or on behalf of a cooperative enterprise. The cooperative mail rule prevents authorized organizations from sharing their authorizations with others who are not authorized. There are also restrictions on advertising for products and services. **Who is eligible to use****Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail****?** An organization is eligible to use Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail if it is not organized for profit and none of its net income accrues to the benefit of any private stockholder or individual. Only organizations that meet specific standards and have received special authorization from the United States Postal Service® may mail eligible matter at any Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail price. Simply being approved for nonprofit status by the IRS does not qualify an organization for the Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices. **There are 10 categories of organizations that may be approved for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices:** | | | | --- | --- | | 1. Agricultural
2. Educational
3. Fraternal
4. Labor
5. Philanthropic | 6. Religious
7. Scientific
8. Veterans
9. Qualified Political Committees
10. State or Local Voter Registration Officials | Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail must be presented for mailing (deposited) at a Post Office™ facility where the organization producing the mailing has an approved nonprofit authorization. A separate authorization is required at each Post Office location where Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail price mailings are deposited. **Note:**  When an organization submits proof that it is granted federal income tax exemption under 26 USC 501(c)(3), as a religious, educational, scientific, or philanthropic (charitable) organization; under 501(c)(5) as an agricultural or labor organization; under 501(c)(8) as a fraternal organization; or under 501(c)(19) as a veterans' organization, it is considered as qualifying for the Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, unless other evidence discloses some disqualification. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Nonprofit-Mail#top) **How do I apply to use Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail?** **Application Process** Specific authorization is required to mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices and may be used at any Post Office with _PostalOne!_ access. Mailers are required to confirm authorization at non- _PostalOne!_ Post Office locations before mailings will be accepted at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices. And note that mailers must still obtain mailing permits and pay the required fees for mailing at presorted prices. In order to apply for authorization to mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, the following steps must be completed: 1. Complete _PS Form 3624 - Application to Mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail Prices_, available on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® ( [http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3624.pdf](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3624.pdf)). 2. You will need to provide evidence that the organization is Nonprofit. Acceptable evidence includes: - An Internal Revenue Service (IRS) letter of exemption from payment of federal income tax. - If an IRS exemption letter is not available, a complete financial statement from an independent auditor (such as a certified public accountant) substantiating that the organization is nonprofit. A statement from a member of the organization is not sufficient (do not submit State tax exemption information). 3. You will need to provide documents describing the organization's primary purpose, such as: - Organizing instruments that state the purpose for which the group is organized, such as the constitution, articles of incorporation, articles of association, or trust indenture. The organizing instrument, including all amendments to the original, should bear the seal, certification, or signature of the Secretary of State or other appropriate state official. If one or more of these documents are not sealed, certified, or signed by state officials, an officer or other person authorized to sign for the applicant should submit a written declaration certifying that the documents are complete and accurate copies of the originals. - Materials showing how the organization actually operated during the previous 6 to 12 months and how it will operate in the future. Bulletins, financial statements, membership forms, publications produced by the organization, minutes of meetings, or a list of its activities may be used. 4. The application must be signed by someone in authority in the organization, such as the president or treasurer. It must not be signed by a printer or mailing agent. 5. Submit the completed _PS Form 3624_ to the Post Office where Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail mailings will be deposited. If the application is approved, the authorization will apply nationwide. **Using an agent** As an organization authorized to mail at Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices, you can use a commercial firm to prepare and present your mailing for you at those prices. The issue is whether the arrangement is a legitimate "principal-agent" relationship. - If you are paying a fee to the mailing house to prepare your mailing, and the mailing house has no other interest in the mailing, it should be eligible for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices. - However, if the mailing house shares in the advertising revenues, places its own advertisement(s) in the mailpiece at no cost, or at a reduced price, or will not receive any fees for its services (reimbursement for expenses), the mailpiece may not be eligible for Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail prices. Under this arrangement, the mailing house now has a vested interest in the mailing and is no longer functioning in an agent capacity. **Maintaining nonprofit status** The Postal Service™ will allow nonprofits to use professional fundraising organizations without jeopardizing their eligibility to mail Nonprofit USPS Marketing Mail. This decision will allow nonprofits to increase the potential pool of charitable donors when the economy is weak and public resources are being diverted. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Nonprofit-Mail#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is Nonprofit Mail? ## What is an Approved Postal Provider?

## Through a series of Approved Postal Provider™ agreements, customers can purchase stamps, mail a letter, or ship a package without ever visiting a traditional Post Office™.      - Jul 2, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007430

Customer Information

**The U.S. Postal Service® provides a multitude of mailing and shipping options for consumers.**

- Through a series of Approved Postal Provider™ agreements, customers can purchase stamps, mail a letter, or ship a package without ever visiting a traditional Post Office™.

**To locate an Approved Postal Provider™ near you, click** [here](http://usps.com/locator) and select from Location Types drop-down.

- "Village Post Office"
- "Contract Postal Unit", or
- "National Retailer"

[Go to USPS.com Site Index.](https://www.usps.com/globals/site-index.htm) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [Skip All Utility Navigation](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#skipallnav) [Locations](https://tools.usps.com/locations/) [Support](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) [Informed Delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/) [Skip to Main Content](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/C360-header.htm#endnav) [![Image of USPS.com logo.](https://www.usps.com/global-elements/header/images/utility-header/logo-sb.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [USPS.com Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/) [![USPS mobile logo](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/logo_mobile.svg)](https://www.usps.com/) [![Search Icon](https://www.usps.com/assets/images/home/search.svg)](https://www.usps.com/search/results.htm?keyword=Contact%20Us) Loading [×](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics# "Cancel and close") Sorry to interrupt CSS Error [Refresh](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics?nocache=https%3A%2F%2Ffaq.usps.com%2Fs%2Farticle%2FBusiness-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) FAQs Home How can we help? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000002jwYAA%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) ## Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics ## The top business solutions to assist you with your everyday small and medium business needs. - Feb 23, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000006087 Customer Information This article provides small and medium businesses URL links that lead to important USPS® resources to assist with everyday business needs, such as: marketing campaigns, Every Door Direct Mail® (EDDM®), shipping best practices, ordering supplies, package pick-ups, package drop-offs, a pricing calculator, and shipping solutions like Click-N-Ship®. Click image to make larger or save for reference. [![Small Business Onboarding Info](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6BY00000002jw&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000005QfDP)](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/business/SMB_Onboarding_DIGITAL.pdf) | | | | --- | --- | | **Additional Business Resources** | | **How does Direct Mail compare to other advertising methods?** | **Comparison between EDDM and other methods.**
Many marketers have found that a mailing's response will be higher when it's part of an integrated campaign which, also, includes advertising in the print or broadcast media.
- The resulting "recognition factor" can work in favor of a mailing and bolster its results.
- If Direct Mail is your only advertising vehicle, you may want to adopt a different creative approach than when your offers are supported by other media.
- It is true that, for mail, the expense per-thousand is greater than those of the print and broadcast media.
- However, mail's expense per response and in many cases its expense per sale is in fact the lowest of any advertising medium.
- Recall that, by definition, the CPM figure refers to the expense per thousand people reached. Most broadcast and print vehicles include a considerable amount of "waste circulation," bringing your message to thousands or even millions who are simply not interested in your product or service.
- But with mail's precision targeting, a marketer can zero in directly on the right people, and not spend funds reaching large numbers of the wrong people.
- With mail, you pay to contact fewer individuals, and every one of them really matters so the result is the industry's hardest-working advertising dollar. | | [What are the Shipping Options for Small Business Owners?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Shipping-Options-for-Small-Business-Owners) | | **How Do I Get Started with Every Door Direct Mail – Retail® Service and Every Door Direct Mail® Service?** | - You can learn more about Every Door Direct Mail – Retail® service and Every Door Direct Mail® service by visiting [_www.usps.com/everydoordirectmail_](https://www.usps.com/business/every-door-direct-mail.htm). Here you get started with the program, download the necessary documentation needed for your mailing, and begin planning which areas you want to target by using our free online mapping tool.
- [Please refer to the User Guide for more information.](https://www.usps.com/business/pdf/every-door-direct-mail-user-guide.pdf) If you still have questions, you can speak to your local Postmaster or call customer service at 877-747-6249. | | [Shipping Products and Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services) | | **eCommerce Web Tools** | The USPS Web Tools® API library gives eCommerce website shopping carts and shipping software access to valuable USPS® data - free of charge! You can check shipping rates, track packages, and schedule a package pickup all through USPS Web Tools.
- [Learn more about the Web Tools API Portal](https://www.usps.com/business/web-tools-apis/). | | [Additional Resources](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Additional-Resources) | | [Pay, Print and Ship Online](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Pay-Print-and-Ship-Online) | | **Marketing Mail Tools** | USPS® offers a variety of marketing campaign options for small businesses with a range of mail service classes and postage rates. Our products allow small businesses to engage consumers with integrated mail and digital marketing campaigns that generates additional consumer impressions, interactions, and insights that will help to grow your business and acquire new customers!
Learn about the variety of direct marketing campaigns to engage and connect with your customers:
- **What is Every Door Direct Mail®?**
Every Door Direct Mail is our free online mail route mapping tool that can target addresses for small businesses advertisements. Just create mail pieces on your own, select postal routes and pay for postage online, and bring them to your local Post Office for delivery. Learn more about how [EDDM](https://www.usps.com/business/every-door-direct-mail.htm) can help grow your business by targeting the right demographics in specific areas.
- **What is Informed Delivery** **® Marketing?**
USPS Informed Delivery® allows you to engage customers through integrated mail and digital marketing campaigns.  See how you can reinforce your call-to-action and drive customer responses with digital mail previews that are tied to physical mail pieces arriving in mailboxes soon.  Learn more on how [Informed Delivery marketing](https://www.usps.com/business/informed-delivery.htm) the averages at 94% open rate.
- **What is Customized Direct Mail®?**
Make your advertising mail piece work harder for your business. Build a [custom direct mail campaign](https://www.usps.com/business/customized-direct-mail.htm)by taking advantage of extras and add-ons offered by USPS® and many third-party websites. These programs add customization, appeal, and clear calls to action. 
- **What is Product Promotions & Incentives?**
USPS® has developed Promotions Calendar for marketers, printers, and mailers. With these [promotions](https://www.usps.com/business/promotions-incentives.htm), USPS® expects to increase overall mail value and help businesses increase their return on investment in mail. Each promotion period ranges between 3 to 6 months.
- **What is Irresistible Mail®?**
Memorable mail in vibrant colors and lifelike textures commands attention. We call it [Irresistible Mail®](https://irresistiblemail.com/). Direct mail delivers. Come explore the USPS® innovation gallery to see how far mail can take you—and what it will return. 
- **Who are approved printers?**
Want experts to help you get your message into the hands that matter most? [Third-party direct mail marketing professionals](https://www.usps.com/business/vendors.htm) can do all or part of your advertising campaign to reach current and new customers. | | [What is USPS Marketing Mail®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-USPS-Marketing-Mail) | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics URL Name Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Mail & Shipping Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGpGAM/mail-shipping-services) ## Related Articles - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [U.S. Passports - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/U-S-Passports-The-Basics) - [Change of Address - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Change-of-Address-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq00000000BJAAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail) ## Business Reply Mail ## Broad information on the Business Reply Mail option, its purpose, and how businesses can apply for the option. - Nov 8, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007686 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**What is Business Reply Mail?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#what_is_brm) | [**Getting Started with Business Reply Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#getting_started_brm) | [**Payment Options for Business Reply Mail and Qualified Business Reply Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#payment_options) | | [**Introducing the IMbA Rate**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#introducing_the_imba) | [**Business Reply Mail Resources and Contact Information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#brm_resources_contact_info) | | **What is Business Reply Mail?** _Business Reply Mail®_ (BRM) is a service provided by the United States Postal Service® that enables a sender (a permit holder) to provide a recipient with a convenient, prepaid method for replying to a mailing. Providing a prepaid reply envelope or postcard may increase the chances of receiving a reply from customers or potential customers. Business Reply Mail is frequently used by: - Direct marketers seeking to encourage orders - Researchers seeking survey response data - Publishers soliciting subscriptions - Businesses collecting receipts or documents from employees | | | | --- | --- | | **Business Reply® Mail  (BRM) Features** | **Qualified Business Reply® Mail (QBRM) Features** | | - First-Class® & Priority® Reply Mail Cards, Letters, Flats, and Parcel mail types allowed
- One ZIP+4® assigned for all mail types | - Only First-Class® Mail Card and Letter mail types are allowed
- One ZIP+4® assigned for Cards
- One ZIP+4® assigned for Letters up to 3.5 oz
- Intelligent Mail® barcode (IMb) Required
- Discounted pricing | Business Reply Mail options vary. For comparison of types and features, please review [Business Reply Mail Options](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#business_reply_mail_options "Business Reply Mail Options"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#top_faq) **Getting Started with Business Reply Mail and Qualified Business Reply Mail** You may apply for a BRM permit at any time by filling out PS Form 3615, _Mailing Permit Application and Customer Profile_, and paying the annual permit fee. Note: PS Form 3615 is a simple form requiring you to know the company name, address where the mail will be returned, contact person, and telephone number. The form is available for download at [https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3615.pdf](https://about.usps.com/forms/ps3615.pdf). You will then submit the form in email to the [Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center](https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions?_gl=1*lxwnk5*_gcl_au*MTM1MTM5NjYzNy4xNzEwMTczNzA3*_ga*MTE3MDkwMzY4Ny4xNjk2NTE1NDUx*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxMDQyNjA4OS44LjEuMTcxMDQyNzQ1NC4wLjAuMA..) (MSSC) at [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov) who will work with you to process your form and assign you a Permit number and ZIP+4. Customers who wish to mail QBRM must also reach out to the MSSC regarding submission of PS Form 6805. See [BRM PostalPro content](https://postalpro.usps.gov/brm) for more information on getting started. | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Comparison of Types of Business Reply Mail** | | **Business Reply Mail Options** | **Best For** | **Mail Service** | **ZIP+4 requirement** | **Mail Shape** | **Fee** | | Basic Business Reply® Mail (BRM) | Low volume return pieces expected | - First-Class® Mail
- Priority Mail®
- Ground Advantage® | - One ZIP+4® assigned for all mail types | - Cards
- Letters
- Flats
- Parcels | - Annual Permit Fee[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#table_note1) | | High Volume Business Reply® Mail (BRM) | Moderate volume return pieces expected | - First-Class® Mail
- Priority Mail®
- Ground Advantage® | - One ZIP+4® assigned for all mail types | - Cards
- Letters
- Flats
- Parcels | - Annual Permit Fee[\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#table_note1)
- Annual Account Maintenance Fee | | Basic Qualified Business Reply® Mail (QBRM) | Higher volume return pieces expected | - First-Class® Mail
- Priority Mail®
- Ground Advantage® | - Unique ZIP+4® for cards
- Unique ZIP+4® for letters | - Cards
- Letters | - Annual Account Maintenance Fee | | High-Volume Qualified Business Reply® Mail (QBRM) | High volume return pieces expected | - First-Class® Mail
- Priority Mail®
- Ground Advantage® | - Unique ZIP+4® for cards
- Unique ZIP+4® for letters | - Cards
- Letters | - Annual Account Maintenance Fee
- Processing (or Quarterly) Fee (paid quarterly) | | Qualified Business Reply® Mail (QBRM) IMbA | High volume return pieces expected with greater visibility offered (with IMbA enrollment) | - First-Class® Mail
- Priority Mail®
- Ground Advantage® | - Unique ZIP+4® for cards
- Unique ZIP+4® for letters | - Cards
- Letters | - Fees waived once enrolled | | Courtesy Reply Mail (CRM) | Respondents responsible for applying correct postage on the preaddressed, pre-barcoded materials | - First-Class® Mail | - N/A | - Cards
- Letters | - None | | Metered Reply Mail | Pre-paid reply postage via meter strip | - Priority Mail® Express
- Priority® Mail
- First-Class® Mail
- Media Mail®
- Library Mail | - N/A | - Cards
- Letters
- Flats
- Parcels | - None | | \*The annual permit fee and other annual or quarterly fees for each permit account type are non-refundable.
Fees vary between letters, flats, and parcels.
Actual Fee costs can be located at: [https://www.usps.com/business/return-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/return-services.htm).
More information can be found at [Fees Associated with Permit Imprint and Return Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Fees-Associated-with-Permit-Imprint-and-Return-Services). | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#top_faq) **Payment Options for Business Reply Mail and Qualified Business Reply Mail** For a complete listing of Business Reply Mail prices and fees, see _[Notice 123–Price List](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#Business%20Reply%20Mail%20(BRM))_. Per piece charge in addition to postage by mail type can be paid by: - Enterprise Payment System (EPS) - Payment Upon Delivery - Advance Deposit Trust Account - Dedicated BRM Advance Deposit Account See [505 Quick Service Guide](https://pe.usps.com/text/qsg300/Q505.htm) for additional details. Annual permit and/or account maintenance fees may be due depending on type of BRM service as referenced on the [Comparison of Types of Business Reply Mail table](https://postalpro.usps.gov/brm "Business Reply Mail Options"). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#top_faq) **Introducing the IMbA Rate** **What is IMbA?** Intelligent Mail® barcode Accounting (IMbA) is an automated solution for the counting, rating, invoicing, and billing processes for customers using QBRM letters and cards with a unique ZIP+4**®**. Using the unique ZIP+4 within the Intelligent Mail barcode**®** (IMb) on the mailpiece, the mail processing equipment counts destinations approved and assigned to the mailer. Invoicing is sent daily, directly to PostalOne!**®** **What are the benefits of IMbA?** There are many benefits to using QBRM with IMbA including: - Automated invoicing made available through Business Customer Gateway - More consistent mail delivery - Expedited mail processing - Reporting enhancements - Tracking through Informed Visibility**®** For additional information and assistance regarding IMbA, see the [IMbA Fact Sheet](https://postalpro.usps.com/imba-fact-sheet). **What is the IMbA Rate?** The Postal Service is incentivizing Qualified Business Reply Mail (QBRM) customers to enroll in Intelligent Mail barcode Accounting (IMbA) by waiving annual account maintenance and quarterly fees. Customers who link current QBRM permits to an Enterprise Payment Account (EPA) and successfully complete the onboarding process will have subsequent annual and quarterly fees waived and pay the QBRM IMbA per-piece fee. **How do I get started?** Reach out to the [MSSC](https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions?_gl=1*lxwnk5*_gcl_au*MTM1MTM5NjYzNy4xNzEwMTczNzA3*_ga*MTE3MDkwMzY4Ny4xNjk2NTE1NDUx*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxMDQyNjA4OS44LjEuMTcxMDQyNzQ1NC4wLjAuMA..) through: - Phone: 1-877-672-0007 or - Email: [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov). Inquiries submitted via email should expect initial contact within 24 hours. **Business Reply Mail Resources and Contact Information** **Where do I go for more information about Business Reply Mail?** For additional information and assistance regarding Reply Mail, visit [https://postalpro.usps.gov/brm](https://postalpro.usps.gov/brm). **Who do I contact to help me get started with BRM?** Refer to the [Mailing & Shipping Solutions Center](https://postalpro.usps.com/solutions?_gl=1*lxwnk5*_gcl_au*MTM1MTM5NjYzNy4xNzEwMTczNzA3*_ga*MTE3MDkwMzY4Ny4xNjk2NTE1NDUx*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxMDQyNjA4OS44LjEuMTcxMDQyNzQ1NC4wLjAuMA..): - Phone: 1-877-672-0007 - Email: [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov). Inquiries submitted via email should expect initial contact within 24 hours - **Hours of Operation:** - Monday through Friday: 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Eastern Time (7:00 am - 7:00 pm Central Time) - Saturdays: Closed - Sundays: Closed - Holidays: Closed **Who do I contact to help me with Mailpiece Design?** There are two options for mailpiece design for BRM: - Option 1: Create your own artwork and send to a [Mailpiece Design Analyst (MDA)](https://pe.usps.com/MailpieceDesign/Index?ViewName=MDAIntroduction) - Visit this [link](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/774) for more information on MDAs and where to find contact information for local MDAs - Option 2: Use the [ABRM tool](https://postalpro.usps.com/mailing/abrm-tool) located in the Business Customer Gateway **Who do I contact with my ZIP Code™ request for use with Business Reply Mail?** Reach out to the MSSC through: - Phone: 1-877-672-0007 or - Email: [MSSC@usps.gov](mailto:MSSC@usps.gov). Inquiries submitted via email should expect initial contact within 24 hours [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Reply-Mail#top_faq) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Business Reply Mail URL Name Business-Reply-Mail [Business](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFxGAM/business)[Return Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGhGAM/return-services) ## Related Articles - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Return to Sender Mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-to-Sender-Mail) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? Tracking Status Help](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [Delayed mail and packages?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Delayed-Mail-and-Packages) - [Informed Delivery® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Informed-Delivery-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) Loading Business Reply Mail

## How do I Obtain a Permit Imprint?

## Information detailing how to obtain a Permit Imprint.      - Jun 13, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007611

Customer Information

**How to get started using Permit Imprint:**

**If you already hold a mailing permit** (meaning you’ve already filled out a PS Form 3615) to pay postage using a meter or precanceled stamps, you will only need to amend your existing form and pay the one-time [permit imprint application fee](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198 "Fees Associated with Permit Imprint and Return Services"). You do not need to complete a second form. Contact the Post Office™ where you hold your permit to change your postage payment options.

**If you do not hold a mailing permit**, you can obtain it either online through the [Business Customer Gateway](https://gateway.usps.com/) portal or by performing the following steps:

1. Complete [_PS Form 3615, Mailing Permit Application and Customer Profile_](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3615.pdf "PS Form 3615, Mailing Permit Application and Customer Profile") available from your local business mail entry unit or Post Office.

2. Take the completed PS Form 3615 to your local business mail entry unit (BMEU) or Post Office. You will need 2 forms of [Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) as well as the completed form. You will be required to pay the permit imprint application fee. This fee covers the costs of setting up an account in your organization’s name. This is a one-time only fee, and it is separate from the annual mailing fee for select mail classes. For information on fees associated with permit imprint postage payment, contact your local Business Mail Entry Unit or Post Office.

3. The Postal Service will give you a permit imprint number and a receipt. Your receipt will be a perforated "stub" off the Form 3615, and it will have your permit imprint number, the date, and some additional information.

4. You can make your first deposit into your permit imprint account (called an "advance deposit account") when you bring in your form or when you’ve prepared and are sending your first mailing. You can put additional funds in the account so that when you bring each additional mailing to the Post Office, postage is deducted from your account. It’s like having a checking account at the Post Office. However, you don’t have to maintain a balance in this account and you do have the option to pay each time you mail.

5. The first time you mail you will also need to pay the [annual mailing fee for select mail classes](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#_c198 "Fees Associated with Permit Imprint and Return Services"). This fee is required in addition to the one-time permit imprint application fee and allows you to use the postage you’ve selected (Permit Imprint) to obtain a volume based price incentive at your mailing location.


**Preparation**

Each mailpiece must display your permit imprint. Embossed or unembossed permit imprints may be made by printing press, hand stamp, lithography, mimeograph, address plate, or similar device. They must not be typewritten or hand-drawn. A sample permit imprint is shown below:

![Presort Standard Indicia](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sbsc/presort_std_indicia.jpg)

**Revocation of Permit**

A permit may be revoked for use in operating any unlawful scheme or enterprise, if no mailings or payment of postage occurred during any consecutive 2-year period, for refusal to provide information about permit imprint use or mailings, and for noncompliance with any standard applicable to permit imprints. The permit holder may submit a written appeal to the postmaster within 10 days of receipt of the revocation notice.

## First-Class Mail International®

## First-Class Mail International® (FCMI®) is our most affordable option for sending postcards, letters weighing up to 3.5 ounces and large envelopes (flats) weighing up to 15.994 ounces.      - Dec 20, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007608

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**What is First-Class Mail International Service?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#WhatisFCMI) | [**Mailing Letters**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#MailingLetters) | [**Mailing Postcards**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#MailingPostcards) |
| [**Mail Large Envelopes (Flats)**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#MailingFlats) | [**Do FCMI® and FCPIS® have similarities?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#Similarities) |  |

**What is First-Class Mail International® Service?**

First-Class Mail International® (FCMI®) is our most affordable option for sending postcards, letters weighing up to 3.5 ounces and large envelopes weighing up to 15.994 ounces.

Notes:

- No merchandise may be mailed with FCMI.
- Tracking and insurance are not available with FCMI.
- Certificate of Mailing and Registered Mail are available as Extra Services (See [IMM 310](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc3_001.htm#ep368733) and [IMM 330](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc3_007.htm)); Return Receipt service is available when the FCMI item has Registered Mail service (See [IMM 340](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc3_012.htm))

**FCMI® Service Prices**

Postage prices depend on the size and weight of the mailpiece, and its destination. To calculate the letter's price, go to the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/ "Postage Price Calculator page"). For additional pricing information, go to " [First-Class Mail International](https://www.usps.com/international/first-class-mail-international.htm "First Class Mail International | USPS")."

**Mailing Letters**

A mailpiece is classified as a letter if it meets the following standards: See [IMM 241.2](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368090 "First-Class Mail International | Postal Explorer")

**Weight Limit**

The weight limit for a letter-size First-Class International mailpiece is 3.5 ounces. Letter-size items exceeding 3.5 ounces are charged the First-Class International flat-sized price.

**Dimensions**

Letter-sized mail must be rectangular and must meet the following dimensions:

- Not less than 5 1/2 inches long or 3 1/2 inches high or 0.007 inch thick
- Not more than 11 1/2 inches long or 6 1/8 inches high or 1/4 inch thick

**Note:** For the purpose of determining mailability or machinability of a letter-sized piece, the length is the dimension parallel to the delivery address as read, and the height is the dimension perpendicular to the length.

**FCMI and Nonmachinable Surcharges**

Regardless of a letter's weight, a per-piece surcharge (see [Notice 123, _Price List_](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm)) applies to a First-Class Mail International letter that is nonmachinable for any of the following reasons:

- Has an aspect ratio (length divided by height) of less than 1.3 or not than 2.5.
- Is polybagged, polywrapped or enclosed in any plastic material.
- Has clasps, strings, buttons or similar closure devices.
- Contains correspondence or nondutiable documents that cause the thickness of the mailpiece to be uneven.
- Is too rigid (does not bend easily when subjected to a transport belt tension of 40 pounds around an 11-inch diameter turn).
- Is more than 4-1/4 inches high or 6 inches long and less than 0.009 inch thick.
- Has a delivery address parallel to the shorter dimension of the mailpiece.

See [IMM 241.217](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368116 "First-Class Mail International | Postal Explorer")

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#top)

**Mailing Postcards**

A mailpiece is considered a postcard if it meets the following standards: See [IMM 241.22](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2396591).

**Dimensions**

Each postcard claimed at a card price must be rectangular and must meet the following dimensions:

- Not less than 3-1/2 inches high or 5-1/2 inches long or 0.007 inch thick.
- Not more than 4-1/4 inches high or 6 inches long or 0.016 inch thick.

**Note:** Unenclosed cards exceeding the size limits for postcards are mailable at the First-Class Mail International letter price if they do not exceed 4-3/4 inches high or 9-1/4 inches long.

**Addressing Postcards**

Right half of postcard:

- The right half of the address side of a card must be reserved for the address of the addressee and postal notations or labels.

Left half and reverse of postcard:

- The left half of the address side of the card and the reverse side can be used for a message or permissible attachments. If a return address is used, it must appear in the upper-left half of the address side.

For more information on addressing postcards, see [IMM 241.225](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368141 "First-Class Mail International | Postal Explorer") and [IMM 241.226](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368143 "First-Class Mail International | Postal Explorer").

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#top)

**Mailing Large Envelopes (Flats)**

A mailpiece is classified as a large envelope (flat) if it meets the following standards: See [IMM 241.23](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368157).

**Weight Limit**

The weight limit for a First-Class Mail International large envelope (flat) is less than 16 ounces (the actual weight limit is 15.994 ounces to accommodate Postal Service systems that round to three decimal places and thus round items that weigh 15.995-15.999 ounces up to 16 ounces).

**Dimensions and Characteristics**

Large envelopes (flats) must meet the following dimensions and characteristics:

- More than 11-1/2 inches long or 6-1/8 inches high or 1/4-inch thick
- Nor more than 15 inches long or 12 inches high or 3/4-inch thick
- Flexible (see [241.236](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368174))
- Rectangular
- Uniformly thick as stated in [241.235](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_016.htm#ep2368172)

**Note:** The length of a large envelope (flat) is the longest dimension. The height is the dimension perpendicular to the length. A First-Class Mail International large envelope (flat) that does not meet the standards above is not eligible for the large envelope (flat) size price and is charged the applicable First-Class Package International Service® (small packet) price.

Additional Information:

- No tracking service
- No insurance available: FCMI® large letters (flats) can have Registered Mail service added, which included limited indemnity (insurance) coverage. Registered Mail service is not available to all countries.

**Payment**

- Customers may use cash, check, credit / debit cards, stamps or postage meter strips for fees. Postage for FCMI® items is available online through authorized PC Postage vendors (not available using Click-N-Ship®).
- Check the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/ "Postage Price Calculator") or [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immctry.htm "Index of Countries and Localities | Postal Explorer") for availability of extra services such as Registered Mail, Return Receipt, and Certificate of Mailing.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#top)

**Do FCMI® and FCPIS® have similarities?**

- Both FCMI® and FCPIS® travel in the letter post mailstream and do not have tracking, except for FCPIS items going to countries specified as Electronic USPS Delivery Confirmation International Service (E-USPS DELCON INTL) countries in [IMM 252.2](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc2_022.htm#ep2850466).
- FCMI items may not weigh more than 15.994 ounces.
- FCPIS items may not weigh more than 4 pounds.

**Note:** Package Pickup and Pickup on Demand services are available for FCPIS® but **not** for FCMI®.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International#top)

## What are the Requirements for USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia?

## Detailed requirements for the Picture Permit Indicia.      - Jun 13, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007598

Customer Information

****What are the requirements for USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia?****

The requirements for USPS® Picture Permit™ Indicia are the following:

- Commercial Presort Permit Imprint
- Automation First-Class Mail letters and postcards
- Automation USPS Marketing Mail™ letters
- Customized portions of the proposed indicia design must be in color, e.g., brand images, corporate logos and trademarks.
- Required elements of the indicia must be in black, e.g., class of mail, U.S. Postage Paid, permit number, etc.

****What mail is eligible for Picture Permit?****

The following types of mail are eligible for Picture Permit Indicia:

- First-Class Mail Automation letters and postcards
- USPS Marketing Mail Automation letters

****What are the required elements for the Picture Permit space?****

The required elements for the Picture Permit Indicia space are the following:

- Mail Class (in all caps)
- U. S. Postage Paid
- Permit Number
- City and State (ZIP Code™ optional)
- Company Name – May be used in lieu of Permit Number, City, and State if specific requirements per [Sect. 604.5.3.9 of the Domestic Mail Manual (DMM)](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1171359) are met.
- See [DMM Section 604.5.4](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1175929) for additional information.

SAMPLES BELOW:

![Presort Standard Picture Permit](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sbsc/presort_std_picture_permit.png)![First Class Mail Picture Permit](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/sbsc/FCM_picture_permit.png)

**Please Note:** _These are samples only.  The images are not to scale._

Picture Permit is an enhancement for the indicia space; it does not replace the basic indicia elements. All existing permit indicia markings are still required, including the mail class, U.S. Postage Paid, City, State, and Permit Number or the company name in lieu of city, state, and permit number when using a company permit imprint as per [Sect. 604.5.3.9 of the DMM](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1171359). See [DMM Section 604.5.4](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1175929) for additional information.

****Why do the images in the Picture Permit need to be in color?****

Black and white images did not perform to satisfaction in operational tests; therefore color images and logos are required.

****Who is responsible for the image in the Picture Permit space?****

The mail owner has the responsibility for the image in the Picture Permit space. Images are not provided or endorsed by the Postal Service.

****Is there a fee to use Picture Permit?****

Yes, there is a fee to use Picture Permit.

- The fee for First-Class Mail Automation letters and postcards is one cent (1¢) per mailpiece (plus applicable postage)
- The fee for USPS Marketing Mail Automation letters is two cents (2¢) per mailpiece (plus applicable postage)
