## How do I use Precanceled Stamps?

## Description of how precanceled stamps are utilized.      - Oct 30, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007132

Customer Information

## What are Precanceled Stamps?

Precanceled stamps are special stamps that come in small denominations and are specifically for Presorted and automation First Class Mail® and all USPS Marketing Mail® mailings. Mailers apply these special stamps to envelopes at a lower postage price (say, 15 cents) and pay the difference when they drop off the mail at a Post Office™ location. The Business Mail Entry Unit clerk can help you fill out your postage statement to calculate how much you still owe. You can pay that difference with a check, a meter strip, or through a postage account.

Precanceled stamps are available in various non-denominations in coils of 500, 3,000, and 10,000.

## **How to get started using Precanceled Stamps?**

**_If you already hold a mailing permit_** (meaning you've already filled out a PS Form 3615) to pay postage using a meter or permit imprint, you will only need to amend your existing form and you do not need to complete a second form.  Contact the Post Office location where you hold your permit to change your postage payment options.

**_If you do not hold a mailing permit_**, then complete the following steps:

1. **Apply for a precanceled stamp permit** using _PS Form 3615, Mailing Permit Application & Customer Profile_. This form is available from your [local Post Office location](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action). The form includes step by step directions for how to complete it. Be sure to check the box that says “Precanceled Stamp Authorization.”

2. **Take your completed PS Form 3615 to your local Business Mail Entry Unit (BMEU) or Post Office location**. You will need 2 forms of **[Acceptable Forms of Identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification)** as well as the completed form. There is no application fee associated with this permit.

   - The Post Office will process your application and give you a precanceled stamp permit number and a receipt. You will need these with you when you buy your precanceled stamps from your local Post Office. The proof of permit is not required when purchasing from Stamp Fulfillment Services.
3. **Purchase stamps** from Stamp Fulfillment Services (1-800-782-6724) or from a local Post Office location. Precanceled stamps cannot be purchased online.

4. **Prepare your Mail**. Affix precanceled stamps just like regular stamps. Typically, precanceled stamps are applied to envelopes at a lower postage price (say, 10 cents) and then you’ll pay the difference between the price paid on each piece and total postage due when you enter the mail at a Post Office location. The acceptance clerk can help you fill out your postage statement to calculate how much postage you still owe. You may pay that difference with a check, a meter strip, or through a postage account.

5. **Presort your mail**.

6. **Complete a Postage Statement**. Paper Postage statements are available at the BMEU where you drop off your mail. High volume mailers can submit postage statements using the Business Customer Gateway.

7. **Pay your annual presort mailing fee** (separate fees for Presort First-Class Mail® and for USPS Marketing Mail®) when you take your first mailing to a Post Office location.

## What is a Waiver of Signature? What is Signature Required?

## A "Waiver of Signature" is initiated by the Sender and authorizes delivery of mail at the letter carrier's discretion without obtaining the recipient's signature.  "Signature Required" is initiated by the Sender and requires the addressee's signature for delivery of the item.      - Oct 11, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006914

Customer Information

**What is Waiver of Signature?**

A "Waiver of Signature" is initiated by the Sender and authorizes delivery of mail at the letter carrier's discretion (if area is secured and protected from weather) without obtaining the recipient's signature.

**Note:**

- Waiver of Signature must be checked at time of mailing by the Sender. It cannot be amended in transit. If the item is not claimed within five days by the Recipient, it will be returned to the Sender.
- For editions of the _Priority Mail Express® Label_ Label 11-F **printed before January 2012** (then referred to as _Express Mail Post Office to Addressee_), customers sending a Priority Mail Express item may instruct the USPS to deliver a Priority Mail Express 1 Day Delivery or Priority Mail Express 2 Day Delivery item without obtaining the signature of the addressee or the addressee’s agent by checking and signing the "Waiver of Signature" on Label 11-F, or indicating waiver of signature is requested on single-ply commercial label.

**Domestic Priority Mail Express®**

- The letter carrier will sign for the domestic Priority Mail Express item as proof of delivery and leave the item in a secure location.
- Waiver of Signature is not available with additional insurance, for Priority Mail Express Collect on Delivery (COD), or for Priority Mail Express COD Restricted Delivery.
- A waiver of signature will void any insurance claims for loss of the package. (However, a claim may be filed for damaged or missing contents or for service failures).

**Note:** Waiver of Signature is not available with any other mail services.

**What is Signature Required?**

A "Signature Required" is initiated by the Sender and authorizes requiring the addressee's signature for delivery of the item.

**Note:**

- For editions of Priority Mail Express Label 11-F printed **on or after January 2012**, a mailer sending a Priority Mail Express item and requiring the addressee’s signature ****must instruct USPS to obtain a signature from the addressee upon delivery of the item by checking the “signature required” box on Label 11-F**** or indicating signature is requested on single-ply commercial label.
- If the signature required box is selected, an image of the signature will be provided to mailers when accessing delivery information.
- If the signature required box is not checked the Priority Express item can be left.

A mailer **must** select Signature Required service for:

- Priority Mail Express COD
- Priority Mail Express COD Restricted Delivery
- Priority Mail Express with additional insurance
- A signature is required if additional insurance is purchased or for Priority Mail Express Collect on Delivery (COD)

If the signature is not required, any insurance claims for loss of the package is void if the item's status/scan is "Delivered" in USPS Tracking. (However, a claim may be filed for damaged or missing contents or for service failures).

## How is Change of Address and Forwarding of Military Mail Accomplished?

## Steps for completing a change of address from a military address, and how military mail is forwarded.      - Aug 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007129

Customer Information

**How Do I File a Change of Address for a Military Address?**

If you are moving to or from a Military (APO/FPO/DPO) address, you can complete your Change of Address (COA) form in one of two ways:

1. Online at [https://moversguide.usps.com](https://moversguide.usps.com/mgo/disclaimer), or
2. In person via hard-copy PS Form 3575 at a local Post Office™ location.

**Note:**

- A COA submitted online can only be viewed or canceled via the Internet. It cannot be changed.
- If you file your change of address online and are moving from a military address, your email address must end in .gov, .mil or .edu.
- If you do not have an email address ending .gov, .mil or .edu, you must complete a hard-copy Change of Address PS Form 3575.

**How is Military Mail Forwarded?**

Military Mail is forwarded according to the following guidelines:

- When mail is sent in bulk to a central military location, the military will be responsible for forwarding mail in accordance with postal regulations.
- When the United States Postal Service provides service to individual military receptacles, the Postal Service™ is responsible for forwarding mail.
- All Priority Mail Express® Service, First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail, USPS Ground Advantage™, Periodicals, and Package Services mailpieces addressed to persons in the U.S. Armed Forces (including civilian employees) serving where U.S. Mail service operates is forwarded at no added charge when the Change of Address is caused by official orders.
  - This free forwarding also applies to mail for household members whose Change of Address is caused by official orders to persons serving in or who are civilian employees of the U.S. Armed Forces
- If the official permanent change of station order is to an overseas APO/FPO/DPO address, military authorities forward mail between the United States and those addresses. Forwarding is limited to 60 days.

**Note:** Military ZIP Code™ restrictions may change as military units move to different locations. All applicable restrictions for about 3,000 overseas military ZIP Codes are entered into the U.S. Postal Service computer terminals and published in the [Postal Bulletin](http://about.usps.com/postal-bulletin/welcome.htm).

## What are Commonly Used Abbreviations for Military Mail?

## Abbreviations for Military Mail      - Jun 15, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007124

Customer Information

**_MPO_** **(Military Post Office™)**

- Provides postal services for military personnel

_**APO**_ **(Army/Air Force Post Office)**

- Military Post Office for Army/Air Force personnel

_**FPO**_ **(Fleet Post Office)**

- Military Post Office for Navy/Marine personnel

**_DPO_** **(Diplomatic Post Office)**

- The preferred designation for mail addressed to Department of State overseas Post Offices.

_**PAL**_ **(Parcel Air Lift)**

- An expedited service available for Package Services.
- Additional fee (contact the local Post Office™ for specific fee amounts)
- Must be identified "PAL" on address side

_**SAM**_ **(Space Available Mail)**

- An airlifted service available for any ground service to MPOs. Weight and size restrictions apply, see [section 700 of the Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/703.htm#ep1164353) for more information.
- No additional fee
- Must be identified "SAM" on address side

_**MOM**_ **(Military Ordinary Mail)**

- Department of Defense mail (originating from the Department of Defense) sent Periodical, USPS Marketing Mail™ item, or Package Services item.
- Must be identified “MOM” on the address side.

**_AA_** (Armed Forces of the Americas)

**_AE_** (Armed Forces of Europe)

**_AP_** (Armed Forces of the Pacific)

## Can I Change or Cancel Premium Forwarding Service® Residential?

## How to Cancel Or Change Premium Forwarding service Residential.      - Nov 5, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007118

Customer Information

Service can only be shortened or extended for the length of time within the guidelines of the Premium Forwarding Service Residential offering. PFS Residential can be shortened to no less than two weeks or extended up to one year.

**Retail Customers**

If you enrolled in PFS Residential by submitting [PS Form 8176](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps8176.pdf) at your local Post Office and wish to cancel, shorten, or extend the service, you must contact your local Post Office™, where your PFS Residential application should be filed. Contact your Post Office by phone or in writing prior to the last ship date noted on the PFS Residential application to request a service modification.  For service extensions, payment for the additional weeks must be made prior to the new service period.

**Online Customers**

You can change or cancel a PFS Residential request online at [https://www.usps.com/manage/forward.htm](https://www.usps.com/manage/forward.htm) **only if** your initial request was created online.

Please be aware of these deadlines for changes or cancelling:

- **Cancellation or modification of the start date**must be completed no later than Midnight CST before the start date.


  - **Note**: If you are unable to cancel the PFS Residential by Monday at 11:59pm CST before the Wednesday shipment date, your credit card will be charged.
- **Modification of your residential information** (individual/household) must be made no later than Midnight CST prior to the start date.
- **Modification of the end date to extend service** must be completed no later than Monday at 11:59pm CST prior to the final Wednesday shipment of the end week.
- **Modification of the end date to shorten service** must be completed no later than Monday at 11:59pm CST prior to the Wednesday shipment date of the week desired to be terminated.
- **Modification of the resume date** must be made prior to Monday at 11:59pm CST before the Wednesday of the last shipment date of the end week provided.

**To change an online PFS request:**

1. Sign in to your [usps.com](https://store.usps.com/store/myaccount/myOrders.jsp) [account](https://store.usps.com/store/myaccount/myOrders.jsp) **.**
2. Hover over your name in the Top Navigation Bar and click My Profile.
3. Click **Activity History** from the left navigation.
4. Click **View Details** of the PFS Residential order listed in the Activity History.
5. Click **Edit This Order.**
6. Use the calendar icon to edit the start or end date.
7. Review and click **Update Order.**

**To cancel an online PFS request:**

1. Sign in to your [usps.com account](https://store.usps.com/store/myaccount/myOrders.jsp) **.**
2. Hover over your name in the Top Navigation Bar and click My Profile.
3. Click **Activity History** from the left navigation.
4. Find the PFS Residential order and click **Cancel Order.**
5. A pop-up will appear asking to verify that you want to cancel the PFS, click **Yes.**
6. A confirmation email will be sent about the cancellation and your credit card will not be charged for the upcoming Wednesday payment.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Change-or-Cancel-Premium-Forwarding-Service-reg-Residential#top)

To prevent mail from entering foreign mail networks, do not include city or country names in APO/FPO/DPO shipping addresses. - Oct 20, 2022 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007105 Customer Information **Address Standards** Overseas military addresses must conform to domestic addressing standards. For additional information, visit the [Addressing Military & Diplomatic Mail](https://www.usps.com/ship/apo-fpo-dpo.htm). The delivery line (the second line from the bottom in the address) must show the word “UNIT”, “CMR” (Community Mail Room), or “PSC” (Postal Service Center) and number; and box number assigned. - The last line must contain the APO or FPO designation, the appropriate two-letter "state" abbreviation, (AA, AE, or AP) followed by the ZIP+4® or five-digit ZIP Code™ number (when applicable "United States" or "USA" would be the country name utilized). - " **AA**" (Armed Forces of the Americas) replaces the previous designation of Miami (340). - " **AE**" (Armed Forces of Europe) replaces the previous designation of New York (090-098). - " **AP**" (Armed Forces of the Pacific) replaces the previous designation of San Francisco (962-966). **Example of a Military (APO/FPO) Address** The Department of Defense (DoD) has requested that those who send mail use the service member’s full name (with or without grade, rank, etc.), or a specific title (e.g., Commanding Officer, Supply Officer, etc.). Also required is the unit designation and APO/FPO (Air/Army Post Office™ or Fleet Post Office location) information with the nine-digit ZIP Code™ number (if one is assigned) and a return street name/number, city, state, and ZIP Code. For parcels, mailers are asked to write on one side only with the recipient’s information in the lower right portion. **Note**:  Do not include the country or the base camp’s city, as it might be routed through the host country’s mail system. | | | | --- | --- | | **CORRECT**
SGT. JOHN SMITH
UNIT 2340 BOX 132
APO AE 09350 | **INCORRECT**
SGT. SMITH
**23RD BATTALION**
UNIT 2340, BOX 132
APO AE 09350
**BAGHDAD  IRAQ** | **Address Format for (APO/FPO) Military Mail Going to a Dependent** - **Mail addressed to dependents must include the following:** - Full name (includes first name, middle name or initial, and last names) - C / O full name (includes first name, middle name or initial, and last name) - "OPC", "UPR", "UNIT”, “CPR”, or “PSC” and number; and box number assigned - APO or FPO (“city”) designation and the appropriate two-letter AA, AE, or AP (“state”) abbreviation followed by the ZIP Code™ or ZIP+4® Code number. - **Address Format for Sending Mail to a Diplomatic Post Office (DPO)** - Effective January 18, 2009 the Postal Service makes a distinction between diplomatic overseas mail and military mail. - A Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) is a designated USPS® civilian Post Office™ location that falls under the jurisdiction of the postmaster of either New York City or San Francisco. These facilities are operated and managed by the Department of State to serve overseas personnel at American embassies and consulates. DPO is the preferred name for mail addressed to Department of State overseas offices. - Customers shipping items to a DPO address in a Priority Mail® large flat-rate box will pay the same price for the Priority Mail large flat-rate box as for a box shipped to an APO/FPO address. With this change, DPO last line address information must contain the DPO designation and the appropriate two-letter state abbreviation (AA, AE, or AP), followed by the ZIP+4® or 5-digit ZIP Code™ number. **Military Mail ZIP Code™ Areas** On July 15, 1991, the military APO/FPO/DPO (Army Post Office/Fleet Post Office/Diplomatic Post Office) overseas ZIP Code™ areas were aligned to reflect the overseas location of the ZIP Code number. Addresses served by the military postal system now have a standardized address format. Generally the three-digit service areas are: - **090-092**  Germany - **093** Contingency - **094**  United Kingdom - **095**  Atlantic / Mediterranean Ships - **096**  Italy, Spain - **097**  Other Europe, - **098**  Middle East/Africa - **340**  Americas (Not US or Canada) - **962**  Korea - **964**  Philippines - **965**  Other Pacific / Alaska - **966** Pacific / Indian Ocean Ships Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title How Do I Address Military Mail? ## How Do I Keep My Mailed Item Cold or Refrigerated?

## USPS® does not offer refrigeration services; frozen product must be packaged so as not to leak or cause odors. You may ship frozen articles using packaging supplies like Dry Ice at your own risk .      - Mar 18, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006816

Customer Information

USPS® does not offer refrigeration services. You may ship frozen articles using packaging supplies like Dry Ice at your own risk and according to [Publication 52 section on Dry Ice](http://pe.usps.gov/text/pub52/pub52c3_027.htm#ep900096 "Dry Ice"). The frozen product must be packaged so as not to leak or cause odors. You could be held liable for damage to other mail if yours is not properly packaged.

- Insurance may be purchased for perishable items. However, the claim will not be paid if the item is spoiled regardless of whether or not it arrived on time.
- Priority Mail® and Priority Mail Express® services are recommended for mailing items requiring refrigeration.
- Many restrictions apply to mailing produce to certain areas of the country (i.e., Florida, Hawaii, California). Please refer to the Department of Agriculture for current restrictions. Department of Agriculture phone numbers are listed in local telephone directories.

**In what Mail Classes can I Ship Items in Dry Ice?**

- Dry Ice is prohibited from International Mail.
- Dry ice is permitted in quantities of up to 5 pounds per mailpiece when shipped as Domestic Mail via air transportation (Priority Mail Express, Priority Mail®, and First-Class Mail®).
- Dry ice is permitted in quantities more than 5 pounds per mailpiece when shipped as Domestic Mail via surface transportation (Parcel Select®, USPS Marketing Mail™, and USPS Ground Advantage®).

****How Shipments Containing Dry Ice Should Be Sent?****

As mentioned above, your package should be sealed so as to not leak or cause odors. The container should be leak‐proof, but not air‐tight. It must be able to contain liquid condensation while it releases the carbon dioxide gas that is generated as the ice melts. If the container is air‐tight, the pressure can build until it ruptures or explodes.

In addition, good insulation is needed to ensure the effectiveness of the Dry Ice.

- An inexpensive Styrofoam cooler from the grocery store is **not suggested**:

  - It breaks easily.
  - It usually is not the right shape for shipping.
- A thicker poly styrene box, as seen below, **is suggested**:

  - It reduces the amount of dry ice needed
  - It allows extended shipping times

    ![A white, styrofoam box with a removable top](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000Pkn&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UJ1H)

A combination package, shown below, is an option that addresses several concerns.

- The inner packaging will hold any condensation.
- The outer cardboard box is securely closed without being air‐tight.
- Through The Postal Store®, you can purchase the [Priority Mail Express® Cold Chain Packaging](https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-express-cold-chain-packaging-P_CCB003). It consists of one Priority Mail Express® Box and one polystyrene cooler.

If you purchase a container specifically made to hold Dry Ice, it will come with closure instructions. To ensure safe transport, all parts of the packaging should be used according to the manufacturer's instructions.

![A packaging box with a styrofoam box inside to insulate the content inside.](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6eq0000000Pkn&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UJ1I)

**Do Shipments with Dry Ice Need to be Marked and Documented?**

1. Each mailpiece must be clearly marked on the address side with:
   - The name of the contents being cooled (such as frozen medical specimens, steaks)
   - The net weight of the dry ice
2. For air transportation, each mailpiece must:
   - Bear a Class 9 DOT miscellaneous hazardous material warning label
   - Be clearly marked "Carbon Dioxide Solid" or "Dry Ice"
   - Have a properly completed shipper's declaration for dangerous goods prepared in triplicate and affixed to the outside of the mailpiece
3. For surface transportation, each mailpiece must be clearly marked either:
   - "Surface Only" or
   - "Surface Mail Only" and "Carbon Dioxide Solid, UN1845" or
   - "Dry Ice, UN1845"

## What US Possessions, US Territories, and Freely Associated States are Considered Domestic?

## List of US Possessions, Territories, and Freely Associated States that are considered domestic for mailing.      - Jan 14, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006821

Customer Information

**Domestic mail** is mail transmitted within, among, and between

- the United States of America
- its [territories and possessions](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-US-Possessions-US-Territories-and-Freely-Associated-States-are-Considered-Domestic#territories_possessions)
- [Army Post Offices (APOs), Fleet Post Offices (FPOs), Diplomatic Post Offices (DPOs)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Military-Mail-Processed) and
- the United Nations, NY

The following are considered United States territories and possessions:

**American Samoa (AS)**

- Manua Island
- Swain's Island
- Tutuila Island

**Guam (GU)**

**Commonwealth of Northern Mariana Islands (MP)**

- Rota Island
- Saipan Island
- Tinian Island

**Commonwealth of Puerto Rico (PR)**

**U.S. Virgin Islands (VI)**

- Saint Croix Island
- Saint John Island
- Saint Thomas Island

**Wake Island Atoll**

- Wake Island

There is also mail that is **treated as domestic mail**. These are mailpieces originating from the Freely Associated States for delivery within, among, and between other Freely Associated States and the [above locations](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-US-Possessions-US-Territories-and-Freely-Associated-States-are-Considered-Domestic#domestic_mail_locations). Mail originating from the above locations for delivery in the Freely Associated States is also treated as domestic mail.

The following are Freely Associated States:

**Republic of the Marshall Islands (MH)**

- Ebeye Island
- Kwajalein Island
- Majuro Island

**Federated States of Micronesia (FM)**

- Chuuk (Truk) Island
- Kosrae Island
- Pohnpae Island
- Yap Island

**Republic of Palau (PW)**

- Koror Island

**Note**: Although mailpieces received from and delivered to the above locations are considered domestic mail or treated as domestic mail, specific rules apply to the mailpieces, such as customs forms requirements and eligibility for the Priority Mail Express® refund. For further information on:

- Generating customs forms, see [U.S. Customs Forms \| USPS](https://www.usps.com/international/customs-forms.htm) or [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics)
- Priority Mail Express refund eligibility, see [Priority Mail Express® Service](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Priority-Mail-Express-Service)

## What Things Should I Consider When Using Direct Mail?

## Considerations customers should be aware of when using Direct Mail.      - Sep 6, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006794

Customer Information

**Format and Content**

When you are evaluating a possible Direct Mail offer looking over formats, layouts, and headlines, here are some points to consider. Please do not think of these as hard-and-fast rules, but the following should provide guidelines for your initial discussions:

- Usually, Direct Mail items include a letter in addition to any brochure or other materials. While mailpieces can succeed without letters, they do provide the personal touch that is one of mail's greatest strengths.
- Consider adding a P.S. to the letter. You can use this to restate your basic message or offer. On a single-page letter, a reader's eyes may drop down to it first.
- Every key element in your mailpiece should include a mention of your offer, as well as your company's name, and phone number. (Remember, you never know what the reader will pull out of the envelope first. People do not always follow the reading sequence you hope they will.)
- In particular, be certain that your response device clearly states your offer. People are hesitant to respond to offers that they do not completely understand or that they feel may not be disclosing all prices and terms.
- Make it easy for people to respond. Your response device should be inviting, well-organized, easy to follow through, and as simple as possible to fill out. Be sure to allow enough space for people to clearly write their names and other information. A hard-to-follow or off-putting response device can lose you a customer.
- There is a lot to be said for making your letter look like a letter. That way, it will seem more like a personal, sincere communication rather than an advertisement that somebody happened to drop in the mail. For a typewritten look, consider using Courier or Elite for typefaces.
- Make your salutation as personal as possible. But do so without being offensive.
- Review:

**Here are a few suggestions:**

- Is it clear?
- Is it interesting?
- Does it speak to the recipient's needs and desires?
- Does the opening sentence irresistibly pull the reader in?
- Does it pile on the benefits?
- Is it concise? (By concise, we mean, does it avoid unnecessary verbiage?)
- Is it believable? (Remember, the purpose of all advertising is to generate believability. One exaggerated allegation or bit of excess "hype" can lose your prospect's trust right then and there.)

Is it totally, 100% about the recipient? (A good Direct Mail piece should appear completely selfless. It is not about you. It is about what you can do for the prospect. It is not about your product or service. It is about what your product or service can do for the prospect. Never brag or boast. Always show that you understand your prospect's needs, care about those needs, and that you are in business to meet them.)

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Things-Should-I-Consider-When-Using-Direct-Mail#top)

**Mailing Lists**

- **Importance**

Most Direct Mail professionals regard list choice as the single most critical element in determining the success of a mailing.  When using an identical Direct Mail advertising campaign, the best list can pull ten times as many responses as a poor list. No matter how well-crafted your mailing may be in other respects, if it is not reaching the right people, genuine prospects for your product or service results will suffer.

- **Sources**

Basically, there are three kinds of sources for mailing lists:

1. **Internal sources.** Here you work with your own list of current or previous customers those who are using or have used your product or service. Such lists, taken from your company's own files or database, are known as house lists. Because these people already have experience with your company, they should represent your very best prospects. They are also the people you know most about for highly targeted selling efforts. When mailing to a house list, you can segment it according to past purchasing patterns, and so tailor your message to an individual's demonstrated needs and desires. A house list is the opposite of an outside list, where you rent names from list brokers or from other companies.
2. **External sources**. These are names that come from outside your company. There are two subcategories here.
   - _Direct response lists_ consist of names of people who have previously responded to direct response offers. In other words, these consumers are comfortable with the idea of purchasing or inquiring by mail. Direct response lists can be tailored according to the types of things that have been sent for; they cover a broad range of subjects and interests, from magazine subscribers to catalog shoppers.
   - A second type of external list is the _compiled list_. Such a list represents a collection of names and addresses taken from a database that was originally established for a noncommercial purpose. Sources include motor vehicle registrations, government records, telephone directories and SIC compilations.
3. **List exchanges sources.** These lists are obtained via swaps between noncompeting companies. For example, a firm that makes car restraints for infants might benefit by mailing to people who recently subscribed to a parenting magazine and vice versa.

**Please Note:**  After your own internal lists, direct response lists almost always perform the best. But they tend to be two to three times as expensive as compiled lists.

- **Price of rent**

Generally, list fees are given in terms of prices per 1,000 names. For consumer lists (reaching people at their homes), the price-per-thousand tends to range between $50 and $200. For business lists (reaching people in their work environments), prices tend to be higher.

You can rent a list directly from the owner for example, from another company whose customers might also be interested in your product or service. Or you can go to a list broker, a specialist in providing lists. List brokers can give you a variety of lists to choose from, many of which enable highly sophisticated targeting. List brokers can also provide you with advice and guidance. Generally, list rental prices tend to be the same whether you deal with a private source or a broker.

- **Correct Mailing List**

As you might suspect, the art and science of list selection is a much written-about subject. But there are certain guidelines that can point you in the right direction. Start, for example, by forming a mental image of your ideal customers the ones most likely to need your product or service, and remain loyal over the years. Consider their demographics age, income, geographic location. Consider their psychographics lifestyle characteristics such as favorite activities, hobbies, or affiliations. The best initial guide to choosing a list is your hard-won knowledge of what your particular customers are like, based on your own business experience. Try to purchase lists of people whose characteristics match your "ideal customer" most closely. With this as a start, go on to fine-tune your list sensibilities by testing.

- **Updates to Mailing List**

When sending out a mailing, you want to minimize undeliverable pieces; each one costs you money and provides no possibility of a sale. With over 40 million Americans changing their addresses annually, businesses require a simple, reliable way to keep their mailing lists up to date. To meet this need, you can turn to the National Change of Address program, commonly known as NCOA. NCOA is administered by private companies certified and licensed by the United States Postal Service®. Every two weeks, they receive updated change-of-address information from the United States Postal Service (which, of course, receives it directly from the individuals who have moved). By checking your lists with the NCOA licensee of your choice, you can correct outdated addresses and thus reduce the postage and material expenses of including undeliverable packages in your drop. This can add up to an effective savings of thousands of dollars in a single mailing. Accuracy in addressing is an important component of Direct Mail marketing success

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Things-Should-I-Consider-When-Using-Direct-Mail#top)

- **Move Updates**

Every address being mailed comes from a list of some kind, except when the addressee has initiated contact that results in a mailpiece.

- Each list is updated on a cycle. The cycle may vary from three months (or 95 days) to one day.
- When a customer initiates contact that results in an addition to the list, the newly added address can "ride" with the list until the next cycle for update. This will only occur when the person for whom the mailpiece is prepared has asked to be added to the list by requesting services, literature, merchandise, etc. We anticipate the new names will not exceed one percent of the list total.

Addresses derived from any source other than directly from the individual customer must be one of the following:

- Mailed at single-piece prices
- Mailed at single-piece prices until a move update process is used to verify the address.
- Accompanied by the necessary documentation from the list source that identifies the method and timeframe of the move update process to determine the length of eligibility.
- If the address list is provided to another party for the purpose of mailing at discount [First-Class Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/first-class-mail.htm "What are the Types of First-Class Mail? - FID 6448") fees and that list has a blend of established and newly acquired customers, the list source must furnish documentation or evidence of the move update process used (to determine the length of eligibility) and additional information on the number of new names acquired directly from the customer since the time of the last update. We anticipate the new names will not exceed one percent of the list total.
- If the address list is further distributed to additional mailers (by the second party to others, by a third party to others, etc.), a copy of the original documentation with dates of processing and the number of additions to the list must accompany it.

A list or name can be updated anytime if it is updated within 95 days or three months **_before_** it is used for sending First-Class Mail® items at discounted prices. For example, if the mailer mails to a list once every two years, that list would not need to be processed **_every_** three months. But it would need to be processed 95 days (or three months) or more before the mailing date.  Entire lists do NOT have to be updated at once. Updating may be performed by any approved method appropriate for the class of the mailpiece. A valid update can be obtained regardless of the class of the mailpiece used for that purpose.

**The “Offer” in the Direct Mail**

Sometimes referred to as "the deal," the specific proposition that induces a customer or prospect to act on your mailing. Classic examples are "Send for our free catalog" and "Take any 12 CDs for a penny."

An offer may consist of:

- The product or service itself
- A money-back guarantee
- A specific promise such as a toll-free phone line to provide customers with assistance
- A price break or just the price itself
- A free sample or trial
- Favorable payment terms
- A premium for acting now

The offer is one of the most critical components of your Direct Mail advertising campaign. A mailing may be highly compelling in many ways in format, copy, selling strategy but it will only achieve its maximum potential when coupled with a sound offer. While it is hard to overestimate the importance of offer testing, the procedure is, fortunately, very simple. Just send out the same combination with a variety of others, and watch for differences in the results.

[Back to top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Things-Should-I-Consider-When-Using-Direct-Mail#top)

Customer Information

Every Door Direct Mail – Retail® service and Every Door Direct Mail® service give you the option to use USPS Marketing Mail™ (formerly Standard Mail®) Flats. The most popular formats are: postcards, oversized postcards and tri-fold brochure / menu.

## Finding Information about Former Postal Employees

## National Archives and National Personnel Records Center contact information.      - Jun 6, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007859

Customer Information

The United States Postal Service® Historian maintains information about Postmasters and Post Offices for a twenty-year period. After that the information is retired to the National Archives, Records Administration Office.

Postmaster information can be located here: [https://about.usps.com/who-we-are/postmasterfinder/welcome.htm](https://about.usps.com/who-we-are/postmasterfinder/welcome.htm).

You may be able to get additional information by writing this office at:

**NATIONAL ARCHIVES**

8601 Adelphi Road

College Park, MD 20740-6001

As information, personnel records for all former Federal employees, including Postal Service employees, are located in the National Personnel Records Center in St. Louis, Missouri. You may write to the Records Center at:

**NATIONAL PERSONNEL RECORDS CENTER**

1 Archives Drive

St. Louis, MO 63138-1002

Telephone: 314-801-0800

Fax: 314-801-9195

E-mail: [MPR.center@nara.gov](mailto:MPR.center@nara.gov)

The [National Personnel Records Center, Civilian Records Facility (NPRC-CPR)](https://www.archives.gov/st-louis/civilian-personnel) maintains and will furnish by mail extant personnel records for federal employees whose service ended after about 1910. Researchers requesting records from the center should provide the full name of the employee, date of birth, social security number if known, name of agency where last employed, and place and approximate dates of employment.

## I Received a Vacant Notice

## Explanation of a "Vacant" notice and steps to take.      - Feb 12, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007832

Customer Information

**Vacant Notices**

**Why did I receive a notice that says "Vacant" in my mailbox and no mail delivery?**

- A customer may receive a "Vacant" notice and stop receiving mail for one of the following common circumstances:
  - The address appears vacant (though someone may still be residing at the address or picking up mail from the address).
  - Mail is not being removed from the box.
  - The customer is residing at a new address.
  - The address is new construction.

**What should I do if I receive a "Vacant" Notice?**

- Follow the instructions on the notice (see image below).

Or you may:

- Take the notice and [valid photo identification](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification) to your [local Post Office® location](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action) to retrieve your mail. The notice must be presented in order to pick up your held mail. See also: [Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office)
- Contact your local Post Office® by email, click [Contact Us](https://www.usps.com/help/contact-us.htm) and select "Send Email"

Mail will be held for 10 days before being returned to sender.

**Image of the Vacancy Notice**

- Vacancy Notice Image

![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/vacant.jpg)

**Time Frame for Vacancy Notices**:

- Rural addresses are considered 'vacant' after 90 days have passed.
- City addresses are considered 'vacant' at the carrier's discretion.
- For apartments, the carrier is usually notified once a new customer moves into the vacant apartment.  The carrier will take the mail left in the box and it will be marked "Moved, Left No Address" and returned to sender.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000AIjAAM%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International) ## What is Insurance (International)? ## Description of insurance for International mailings. - Nov 14, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007511 Customer Information **What should I know about Insurance for International items?** - Insurance provides coverage against loss, damage and/or missing contents. - Insurance is not available to all countries and is not available for all mailing items. - Check the availability of insurance options for a specific item and destination by getting a quote: [Calculate Postage](http://ircalc.usps.gov/ "International Postal Postage Calculator") or check the IMM [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm "Individual Country Listings") for maximum limits. - Indemnity is limited to the lesser of the actual value of the contents or the maximum indemnity based on the weight of the article. - The maximum insurance amount varies by individual country. - Insurance fees are in addition to postage and vary depending on the amount of coverage (insured value). - For information on filing claims, please visit [File an International Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm "USPS.com - File a Claim"). Remember for International lost articles an inquiry needs to be filed before a claim can be submitted. - **If the item has been delivered to the addressee, payment for damage and missing contents is made to the sender unless the sender waives the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the addressee.** [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International#top) **Fees** Fees vary depending on the amount of insurance purchased.  Insurance is priced in incremental amounts for both domestic and international mail items. For additional information on the varying amounts of insurance coverage and their prices, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](http://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm). [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International#top) **International Mail Insurance Information** | | | | --- | --- | | **Mail Class** | **General Insurance Information** | | Priority Mail Express International | Priority Mail Express International® (PMEI) items include up to $200 merchandise insurance for loss, damage, and/or missing contents; included insurance for nonnegotiable document reconstruction is up to $100. Additional insurance may be purchased. Check the IMM Individual Country Listings for availability and maximum limits.
You can purchase additional merchandise insurance up to the maximum amount allowable for the destination country. However, that amount cannot exceed $5,000.
See [What is Priority Mail Express International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-Express-International) for more information | | Priority Mail International | For Priority Mail International® (PMI) items include up to $200 merchandise insurance for loss, damage, and/or missing contents; included insurance for nonnegotiable document reconstruction is up to $100. Additional insurance may be purchased. Check the IMM Individual Country Listings for availability and maximum limits.  
You can purchase additional merchandise insurance up to the maximum amount allowable for the destination country. However, that amount cannot exceed $5,000.
See [What is Priority Mail International®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International) for more information | | Registered First Class Mail International | Limited Indemnity.  See [Registered Mail® International](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Registered-Mail-International) for more information. Insurance cannot be purchased. | | First Class Package International Service | Not insurable.  See [First Class Package International Service® (FCPIS)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Package-International-Service-FCPIS) for more information. | | First Class Mail International | Not insurable.  See [First-Class Mail International®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/First-Class-Mail-International) for more information. | [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International#top) **Why wasn't I able to insure an International package?** First-Class Mail International® and First-Class Package International™ **do not have** Insurance as an option but, depending on the destination, you may be able to send them Registered, which includes Indemnity (Insurance) coverage. **See** [International Mail Manual (IMM)](http://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/welcome.htm) **for more information.** - **Designating Insurance on a Customs Form** Additional insurance may be purchased for a fee (for eligible flat rate boxes or variable weight articles) to many countries. International insured articles use PS Form 2976-A ( [complete version](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976a.pdf)) or [PS Form 2976-B](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976b.pdf) to record the purchase of insurance. [Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Insurance-International#top) **Filing Insurance Claims** - International claims are different than domestic claims - they have to be coordinated with the foreign postal administration. - There are two options for filing an international inquiry. Time limits for filing are listed in [Exhibit 921.2](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc9_002.htm#ep1129849). - Online at [usps.com/help/international-claims.htm](http://usps.com/help/international-claims.htm). The customer must be the U.S. sender and a registered [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account holder. U.S. senders without a registered [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) account must create an account at the beginning of the online inquiry process. - By phone - to start the inquiry process by telephone, the customer must be the U.S. sender.  Call 1-800-222-1811. The amount of insurance you can purchase for your item varies for each mail service and each country. You can use “Lookup a Country’s Price Group and Limits” ( [http://ircalc.usps.com/CountryInformationLookup.aspx](http://ircalc.usps.com/CountryInformationLookup.aspx)) to quickly find specific insurance availability and limits for the country that you are shipping to. [Image of PS-Form 2976-A, Customs Declaration and Dispatch Note](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps2976a.pdf), used to record the purchase of insurance: - As required under [IMM 123.6](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc1_009.htm#ep1000983), a fully completed electronically generated customs form is required for international mail.  A mailer may obtain an electronically generated customs form by using Click-N-Ship service, the Customs Form Online application, or USPS-approved vendor software, as described in [IMM 123.3](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc1_009.htm#ep1000921).  Alternatively, a mailer may obtain an electronically generated customs form by presenting a fully completed PS Form 2976-R at a USPS retail service counter.  The following forms are available for international mail. - PS Form 2976, _Customs Declaration CN22 – Sender’s Declaration_ - PS Form 2976-A, _Customs Declaration and Dispatch Note – CP72._ - PS Form 2976-B, _Priority Mail Express International Shipping Label and Customs_ - Note: For international mail, PS Form 2976-R is used only for Priority Mail Express International Items. - Handwritten Customs Worksheets: - PS Form 2976-R, _USPS Customs Declaration and Dispatch Note_.  This form is available only as a hard copy that customers can use as a worksheet that they must present with their mail item at a USPS service counter.  The retail associate enters the information into the retail system or Customs Form Online Application, and then generates and prints the applicable customs form to affix to the mail item.  Customer cannot use PS Form 2976-R as a stand-alone customs form. - Note:  All USPS-supplied hard copies of PS Form 2976, PS Form 2976-A, and PS Form 2976-B that were preprinted with a barcode and designed for mailers to complete by hand are obsolete and prohibited from use. - The following are the authorized customs forms – [IMM 123.12](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc1_009.htm#ep1258170) - Electronically Generated Customs Forms produced through an approved USPS system capable of electronically transmitting the associated customs data.  Current authorized editions are available in the Guideline for Privately Printed Customs Declaration Form at _[https://postalpro.usps.com/node/2713%postalpro.usps.com/node 2713](https://postalpro.usps.com/node/2713%25postalpro.usps.com/node%202713)_.  A mailer may not use privately printed customs forms without written authorization.  See [IMM 123.31 Authorization](https://pe.usps.gov/text/imm/immc1_009.htm#ep1000921) - Customs forms generated through the online service using Click-N-Ship. - Customs forms generated through the process of using the Customs Forms Online application. - The USPS-supplied hard copy edition PS Form 2976-R (dated April 2016), which a mailer uses to provide handwritten customs information when presenting a mailpiece at a USPS retail service counter. **Note:**  If there is insufficient space on the customs declaration form to list all contents of the parcel or item, a second form is used to continue listing the contents. The first form must be annotated to indicate two forms. Both forms are placed into PS Form 2976-E (envelope). **Note:**Pages 2-Customs Declaration, 3-Dispatch Note, 4- Post Office Copy and 5-Senders Copy are all exact duplicates of page 1-Customs Declaration and are not displayed for that reason. ## What is Priority Mail International®?

## Priority Mail International Service is a reliable, cost-effective way to send documents and merchandise.      - Dec 31, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007508

Customer Information

**_Priority Mail International®_** service is a reliable, cost-effective way to send documents and merchandise to more than 180 countries. Priority Mail International offers outstanding value and is priced lower than our competition's published rates for international shipping.

- Average business day delivery of 6 to 10 days. (Average number of days may vary based upon origin and destination.)
- Flat Rate options are available.
- Accepted at all United States Postal Service® retail locations (and can be deposited at all Post Office™ facilities, USPS® blue collection box, and given to letter carriers).
- Includes up to $200 merchandise insurance or up to $100 insurance for nonnegotiable documents; additional insurance can be purchased
- Tracking included
- Maximum weight acceptable is 70lbs, but each country has its own weight limit
- For detailed information on pricing, go to [https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm "USPS.com - Priority Mail International").
- Correspondence, documents and merchandise may be sent with Priority Mail International - this is the best value for international mail.
- Quick, Easy, Convenient! Customers can go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm) to do the following:

  - Order free shipping supplies.
  - Print shipping labels with the appropriate customs form (PS Form 2976-A) in [one document](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Which-Customs-Form-do-I-Use).
  - Request free Package Pickup™ service.
- Information on depositing Priority Mail International mailpieces that require customs declaration can be found under " [Customs Forms - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Customs-Forms-The-Basics)". Under certain conditions, one depositing option, Pickup On Demand® service, is available for a fee. For more information, see [What is Pickup on Demand®?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Pickup-on-Demand).
- Priority Mail International labels begin with two letters ("CA-CC", "CE-CZ", and "HA-HZ") followed by nine numbers and the 2-letter country code of originating country, located in the upper left-hand corner of the receipt.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#top)

**Pricing**

Priority Mail International prices are based on weight and destination. Size limitations may also apply to certain countries. For detailed information, go to the [Postage Price Calculator](https://postcalc.usps.com/?country=10440) or [Priority Mail International](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm "Priority Mail International - Features & Rates | USPS").

**Save money!**

- A **discount up to 5%** is available to customers who purchase postage through authorized PC Postage vendors (except Click-N-Ship).
- Permit Imprint Mailers who use Global Shipping Software will also receive up to a **5% discount** off of published prices.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#top)

**Extra Services Available**

**Insurance**

- [Insurance is included](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#insurance) for all PMI shipments.
- Additional insurance may be purchased. Fees for additional insurance vary depending on the amount of coverage and country
- Items are insured against loss, rifling, or damage for Priority Mail International items containing merchandise. Items containing only nonnegotiable documents are insured against loss, damage, or missing contents for document reconstruction.
- Insurance availability, limitations, and coverage varies depending on the destination country. See the [International Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.com/text/Imm/immctry.htm "International Mail Manual - Index of Countries and Localities") and the [International Postage Price Calculator](https://ircalc.usps.com/ "International Postage Price Calculator") for additional information.

Additional information on Insurance and Extra Services prices is available at [https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/insurance-extra-services.htm "International Shipping Insurance & Return Receipts | USPS"). More detailed information can be found in the [International Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc2_012.htm "232 Eligibility | Postal Explorer").

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#top)

**Insurance**

Insurance is included for **all** Priority Mail International shipments [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International). At no additional charge, Priority Mail International items containing merchandise are insured up to **$200** while PMI items with non-negotiable documents are insured up to **$100**. You can purchase additional merchandise insurance up to the maximum amount allowable for the destination country. However, that amount cannot exceed **$5,000**.

If the item has been delivered to the addressee, payment for damage and missing contents is made to the sender unless the sender waives the right to payment, in writing, in favor of the addressee.

**Note:** See the [Postage Price Calculator](http://postcalc.usps.com/ "Postage Price Calculator") and the [Individual Country Listings](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_toc.htm "Individual Country Listings | Postal Explorer") for coverage fee, availability and limitations.

- Variations in insurance limit can vary within a country

**\*** **Note:** This includes Priority Mail International Flat Rate® Envelopes and Flat Rate Small Boxes.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#top)

**The following options are available to mail items using Priority Mail International®.**

**Flat Rate Envelopes and Flat Rate Boxes**

Flat Rate postage options for Priority Mail International have a fixed price up to a maximum weight limit. That price for Flat Rate Envelopes and Flat Rate Boxes varies among the different price groups. For detailed information on pricing and available Flat Rate Products, go to [https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm "USPS.com - Priority Mail International").

- **Sealed Against Inspection**
Priority Mail International Flat Rate Envelopes and Flat Rate Boxes are not sealed against inspection. Regardless of physical closure and item, the mailing of any Priority Mail International item constitutes consent by the mailer to inspection of the contents.

**Note:** Additional services may determine if a mailpiece is sealed against inspection. For example, any mailpiece that is Free Matter for the Blind is **not** sealed against inspection.


**Priority Mail International® Parcels**

For detailed information on pricing and available Flat Rate Products, go to [https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm](https://www.usps.com/international/priority-mail-international.htm "USPS.com - Priority Mail International").

|     |
| --- |
| **Notes:** For personal correspondence, the Priority Mail International Flat Rate Envelope is available, as well as First-Class Mail International® letters. Merchandise is permitted within a Priority Mail International Flat Rate Boxes, but written communications having the nature of current and personal correspondence are not permitted. |

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Priority-Mail-International#top)

## Burial Flags

## Process for obtaining a burial flag for a deceased veteran.      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006982

Customer Information

The Department of Veterans Affairs, in cooperation with the United States Postal Service, issues nearly half a million burial flags annually to families of deceased veterans. The United States Postal Service is proud to partner with the VA in providing burial flags for our veterans.

**Applicants seeking a burial flag should:**

- Visit [https://www.vba.va.gov/pubs/forms/VBA-27-2008-ARE.pdf](https://www.vba.va.gov/pubs/forms/VBA-27-2008-ARE.pdf) to fill out VA Form 27-2008, Application for United States Flag for Burial Purposes
- Submit a completed VA Form 27-2008 and attach a copy of the veteran's DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty to your local [Post Office™](https://tools.usps.com/go/POLocatorAction!input.action)
- Applications can be submitted by family members or a funeral director acting on their behalf
- There is no time limit for requesting a flag


**Additional information regarding burial flags can be found at:**[https://www.va.gov/](https://www.va.gov/).

**NOTE:****_Only one burial flag per deceased veteran can be issued._**

## USPS® Credit Card Signature Policy

## Policies concerning signatures on the back of credit cards, transactions that require signatures and products and services that can't be purchased with credit cards.      - Oct 5, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006968

Customer Information

****The following policies apply when using a credit card for a USPS transaction:****

- For Visa, the signature on the back of the credit& card must match the signature on the receipt.

- For American Express, Discover Card, and MasterCard, the signature on the back of the credit card must be the same name embossed on the front of the card. It also must match the signature on the receipt.

- Unsigned cards will NOT be accepted. If the card is unsigned, two forms of identification must be presented to the clerk, one of which should include a [valid photo ID](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Acceptable-Form-of-Identification). The customer will then be required to sign the back of the credit card. The signatures must match. If there is a signature panel on the card, it must be signed.

- A card with the words “see ID”, "check ID", or two signatures on the signature panel is not acceptable. The card companies consider this to be an invalid card.

- Some credit cards are issued with the picture and signature of the customer laminated on the front of the card. The signature policy is the same for these cards. The card must be signed on the signature line on the back of the card.

****Please Note:****

- Debit Cards must be signed only when they are used like credit cards in transactions (i.e. without PIN numbers).
- Credit card signature policy is established by the credit card companies.
- The Postal Service™ thoroughly endorses these rules as these guidelines help prevent fraud and they protect the clerks as well as the customer.

****No Signature required on $25 or less****

- The Postal Service participates in the “No Signature Required Program” for VISA, MasterCard, American Express, Discover and Japan Credit Bureau (JCB) credit cards.

- Receipts for credit card transactions of $25 or less will print without a signature line on the USPS/Merchant copy of the receipt for these transactions.

- Not requiring the customer to sign their receipt or having the retail associate compare signatures for transactions $25 or less will speed up transactions and reduce wait time in line. Signatures will still be required for transactions over $25 and receipts will reflect this with a line provided for signature.

**Products/Services that cannot be paid using a credit card**

- COD's
- Money Orders
- Passport fees (only Postal Acceptance Fee (formerly Execution fee) portion - payable to "Postmaster" - can be paid with a credit card)
- Payment for bulk mailings
- Trust accounts (pre-canceled stamps, permit imprints, periodicals, business reply mail)
- Credit cards are not acceptable for purchases made by phone, except on the Stamps by Phone™ line
- Debit cards are accepted for all services, except passport fees to the State Department (debit cards may be used to pay the USPS processing fee). (Cash back allowed in $10 increments up to $50.)

****Gift Cards****

Some major credit card companies (like Visa and MasterCard) have begun issuing gift cards credited with a specific amount of money. The cards are used like a debit card, with an account number and a PIN. There are some Post Offices that are able to accept these cards and other Post Offices that are not. If you have any inquiries as to whether or not these cards can be used, please ask the local Post Office where you would like to use the card.

## Why was my mailpiece returned for a missing apartment number?

## Policy regarding delivery of mail without an apartment number.      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006965

Customer Information

The main function of the Postal Service is to deliver mail, and we make every reasonable effort to do just that. However, sometimes we can't tell to whom the mail should be delivered. In this case, without the apartment number, the mailperson probably did not know which mail box to put the mailpiece in.

With the heavy resident turn over in apartment buildings and because many have several hundred tenants, it is difficult for our personnel to remember each tenant's correct apartment number. That is why the Postal Service encourages the use of apartment numbers and requires apartment management to provide a current alphabetical directory if the complex has fifteen (15) or more mail receptacles. As a matter of policy, mail will be delivered if any one of the following elements is present:

- Apartment number is included on the envelope.
- Recipient's name appears on or inside mail receptacle.
- Recipient's name appears on an alphabetical directory furnished by the apartment management.
- The mailperson recognizes the information and remembers the correct apartment number.

## Postal Terms and Acronyms

## The United States Postal Service uses words and phrases that are either unique, or have special meaning within – including terms related to products and services, delivery and mail processing operations, automation technology, and strategic programs.      - May 20, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006783

Customer Information

The United States Postal Service uses words and phrases that are either unique, or have special meaning within – including terms related to products and services, delivery and mail processing operations, automation technology, and strategic programs.

For a comprehensive list of postal terms, acronyms, and abbreviations, check out [USPS Publication 32 - Glossary of Postal Terms](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub32/welcome.htm "USPS Publications 32 - Glossary").

- **Quick link to a comprehensive** [list of postal terms](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub32/pub32_terms.htm "Postal Terms").
- **Quick link to** [postal acronyms and abbreviations](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub32/pub32_acn.htm "Postal Acronyms").

## Relay Communication Services (TDD/TTY)

## Calls from a specialized operator who speaks on behalf of hearing impaired individuals.      - May 16, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006776

Customer Information

**For Federal Relay (Telecommunications Device for the Deaf/Teletypewriter (TDD/TTY), please call: 1-800-877-8339.**

**Hours of Operation:**

- **Monday through Friday:** 8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. Eastern
- **Saturday:** 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Eastern
- **Sunday:** Closed
- **Holidays:** Closed ( [click here for Postal Holiday information](https://about.usps.com/newsroom/events/))

## Why can't I purchase The Postal Service® Guide to U.S. Stamps?

## The 45th edition of The Postal Service Guide to U.S. Stamps.      - Mar 15, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006770

Customer Information

**We apologize _The Postal Service® Guide to U.S. Stamps_ is not available for purchase as USPS is evaluating our product offering to meet both business and customer needs. We will update the FAQs and Postal Store to notify customers if it becomes available.**

## Is there Mail Service for the Homeless?

## A homeless person may submit an application for PO Box™ service to a local Post Office™. The Postmaster may approve the application under certain conditions.      - May 14, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006183

Customer Information

**A homeless person may submit an application for PO Box™ service to a local Post Office™. The Postmaster may approve the application under any one of the following conditions:**

- The applicant is known to the window clerk or Postmaster.
- An unknown applicant submits proper ID.
- The applicant provides a verifiable point of contact (e.g., place of employment, shelter, charitable institution, or social services office).
- Customers receiving PO Box service must pay the fees listed in the most current [Price List - Notice 123](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm).

Applicants who cannot meet these conditions may be eligible to receive indefinite [General Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-General-Delivery) service, if approved by the local Postmaster. Customers should contact their local Post Office for more information.

## Is Signature Confirmation™ Service Eligible for a Refund?

## Refund process for Signature Confirmation that gives shippers extra peace of mind by requiring a signature from the recipients of their packages.      - Sep 20, 2019 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006178

Customer Information

Signature Confirmation™ service is eligible for a refund of the service fee, excluding the postage, if there is no signature, signature alternative or legal mark of the recipient on the Postal Service™ response letter or no record of delivery for the Signature Confirmation piece after 30 days from the date of mailing.

The refund process for Signature Confirmation with online labels has to be requested at [Online Refunds Application](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#extra).

To request a refund at your local Post Office:

- Submit the request in duplicate at the local Post Office using PS Form 3533, Application and Voucher for Refund of Postage and Fees
- Provide proof of mailing and the Signature Confirmation response letter.

## What does PS Form 3575 (Mail Forwarding Change of Address Order) Look Like?

## Images of PS Form 3575.      - Oct 16, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006176

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **PS Form 3575 (Back)** | **PS Form 3575 (Front)** |
| [**![Image of the back of PS Form 3575, the Official Mail Forwarding Change of Address Order. It is a form that has items that customers fill out, including the type of Change of Address (individual, entire family, or business), whether the forwarding is temporary, and new address. The bottom of the form has the message "Visit usps.com to change your address online".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/change_of_address/psform_3575_back-general.jpg)**](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/change_of_address/psform_3575_back-general.jpg) | ![Image of the back of PS Form 3575, Official Mail Forwarding Change of Address Order. It acts as an envelope with a "mailing address" to the Postmaster, United States Postal Service. A Note and Privacy Notice is also on this form. The text is below the image.](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/change_of_address/psform_3575_front-general.jpg) |

**Text from the front of the form is as follows:**

**PRIVACY ACT STATEMENT:** Your information will be used to provide mail forwarding and Change-of-Address (COA) services and move related advertisements, to verify your identity, to facilitate communication, to identify and mitigate potential fraud and to provide address correction services. Collection is authorized by 39 U.S.C. 401, 403, 404. Providing the information is voluntary, but if not supplied, we may not be able to process and fulfill your request for services. We do not disclose your information to third parties without your consent, except to facilitate the transaction, to act on your behalf or request, or as legally required. This includes the following standard and specific routine uses: Incident to legal proceedings; for law enforcement purposes; to a congressional office; to agents or contractors; to auction; to labor organizations; to federal, state, local, or foreign agencies; to agencies and entities for financial matters; for customer service purposes; for voter registration to a duly formed election board or registration commission; to a federal, state, or local government agency upon certification when required for the performance of its duties; to a law enforcement agency after the Postal Inspection Service has confirmed that the COA Information is needed for a criminal investigation; for service of process; for purpose of jury service; to agencies or entities at customer's request; to a disaster relief organization; pursuant to the order of a court of competent jurisdiction; for address correction services only if the matter is in possession of the name and old address; and to USPS licensed service providers to perform mailing list address correction services for lists containing your name and old address.

For a list of licensed service providers please visit [https://postalpro.usps.com/NCOALink\_Service\_Providers](https://postalpro.usps.com/NCOALink_Service_Providers).

For more information regarding our privacy policies visit [www.usps.com/privacypolicy](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-does-PS-Form-3575-Mail-Forwarding-Change-of-Address-Order-Look-Like).

VOTE: Remember to update your voter registration if you are filling a permanent Change-of-Address.

Visit [Moversguide.USPS.com](https://moversguide.usps.com/) to complete your USPS Change of Address Online

Or

Return this form with your valid ID to a Retail Location Near you.

\*Your request cannot be activated until your identity has been verified.

Visit [https://faq.usps.com](https://faq.usps.com/) for more information.

## Suspicious Mail

## Learn about the characteristics of suspicious mail and how to handle it.      - Apr 22, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007970

Customer Information

**What are characteristics of suspicious mail?**

Under certain circumstances, you should exercise more caution if you are concerned about the receipt of suspicious items. Be alert to mail pieces which are unexpected or sent by someone unfamiliar to you. The following characteristics may also dictate caution when handling an unknown mail piece:

- Items addressed to someone no longer with your organization or otherwise outdated.
- Items that are handwritten and have no return address, or have one that can't be verified as legitimate.
- Items containing restrictive endorsements, such as "Personal" or "Confidential".
- Items of unusual weight relative to their size, or that are lopsided or oddly shaped.
- Items which are sealed with excessive amounts of tape.
- An item containing a postmark which does not match the return address or bears an excessive amount of postage.
- Mailpiece is leaking an unknown powdery substance.

**NOTE**:  When you order Postal products through Stamp Fulfillment Services, a cornstarch in the form of a white powder is used in the processing function. The packing process includes adding food starch, a non-toxic white powder that inhibits moisture, cracking, and spotting. It protects the archival properties of our products.

**What do I do with a suspicious piece of mail?**

If you receive a suspicious mail piece, **do not handle it, shake it, bump it, or sniff it.**

**Follow these steps:**

1. Isolate the mail piece.
2. Evacuate the immediate area.
3. Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. This also applies to everyone that has handled the mail piece.
4. **Notify local law enforcement immediately.**

You should exercise **CAUTION** and **COMMON SENSE** when dealing with the mail.  When in doubt, call local law enforcement authorities.

**How does USPS® train individuals to handle suspicious mail? Can any of those techniques be used in other companies?**

The Postal Inspection Service offers [Publication 166, Mail Center Security Guide](http://about.usps.com/publications/pub166/welcome.htm) for information on how to handle suspicious mail, mail center security, and other security concerns of business and government mail centers.

Companies may also be interested in [Poster 84: Suspicious Mail](http://about.usps.com/posters/pos84/welcome.htm) which was designed for display in mail centers and contains visual examples and descriptions of the characteristics of suspicious mail.

Both items are available from your local Post Office or by calling the Topeka Material Distribution Center toll-free at **1-800-332-0317**.

## What is Anthrax and the USPS® Biohazard Detection System?

## Anthrax is a bacterial disease and Biohazard Detection System (BDS) will detect anthrax in the mail.      - Oct 26, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007961

Customer Information

**What is Anthrax?**

_**Anthrax is a bacterial disease caused by Bacillus (B.) anthracis.**_In humans, three types of anthrax infections can occur based on the route of exposure.  Click link for more information on [Anthrax](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub166/pub166_v04_revision_112019_tech_017.htm) [.](https://about.usps.com/publications/pub166/pub166_v04_revision_112019_tech_017.htm)

**What is the USPS® Biohazard Detection System?**

The United States Postal Service® is committed to keeping its employees and customers safe. To help counter the threat of anthrax in the mail, the Postal Service™ has used a Biohazard Detection System (BDS) that will detect anthrax in the mail. The BDS uses proven technology and was designed exclusively for the Postal Service.  The system is designed for the highest possible level of detection.

**The Department of Homeland Security will be notified of a positive test result.  Follow these links for more information on the**[Biohazard Detection System](http://about.usps.com/news/state-releases/sc/2013/FAQ-BDS.pdf) or on [Securing the Mail](http://about.usps.com/what-we-are-doing/securing-the-mail/welcome.htm).
