Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000003RyYAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup) ## gopost® Package Pickup ## The gopost® Package Pickup option allows you to pick up the package from a local gopost® self-service parcel locker unit. - Apr 23, 2024 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007960 Customer Information | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | [**When is the latest I can request a gopost® delivery online so that it is delivered the following day?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#4) | [**What are gopost® parcel lockers?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#6) | [**How does the gopost® Package Pickup option work?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#7) | | [**How long do I have before I have to pick up my package at the gopost® unit?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#8) | **[gopost®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost)** | **[Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics)** | | **[USPS® Smart Locker](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Smart-Package-Locker)** | | | | | | --- | | **\*Added 3/16/2021\* -** **Has the gopost® form been changed?**
Yes. The PS Form 3849G will be retired from production and will be replaced with the February 2021 version of PS Form 3849 for all customers. Letter carriers will be required to indicate on the updated forms if a customer is eligible to have their items redelivered to a gopost® parcel locker by checking "Parcel Locker Eligible". | **What is the gopost®** **Package Pickup option?** When you receive our notice of a missed package delivery, the gopost® Package Pickup option allows you to pick up the package from a local gopost® self-service parcel locker unit. Gopost® units are available for longer hours than our window hours and make it easier to get your packages on your schedule. | | | | --- | --- | | Since not all packages are eligible for delivery through gopost®, we'll check a box for "Parcel Locker Eligible" on the Redelivery form ( [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)) if the package is eligible.
You do not need to be a registered gopost user to utilize this option. | ![Image of a section of the front of the PS Form 3849, Redelivery Notice, the Feb. 2021 version. The focus is the "About the missed delivery:" section. Lines are available to check to indicate if the missed delivery was a "Package", "Letter", or "Large Envelope". On the next line, an "X" is over the checkbox next to "Parcel Locker Eligible". The next line has "Available for pickup date:" with a line next to it. The final lines has a checkbox next to "First attempt" and "Final notice".](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Feb21_parcel_locker_checked.jpg) | **Can I select the gopost® unit for the gopost® Package Pick Up Option?** No. We provide the specific gopost location associated with your delivery address directly on the Redelivery notice form and in our email/text notifications to you. In most cases, this will be the closest gopost unit to your delivery address. For the option of choosing any gopost unit for deliveries (as well as shipping), you may wish to become a registered gopost user. Just visit [https://gopost.usps.com/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go) to learn more and signup! Further information is also available at the article [gopost®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost). **What if my 3849 form is not checked "Parcel Locker Eligible"?** Your package cannot be loaded into a gopost unit. We may have determined that the package is too large to fit in a gopost parcel locker. Or the piece may be ineligible due to the services requested by the sender. Refer to items marked on the front of your 3849 form to determine the mail piece type. Follow instructions on the form for options other than gopost. See [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) for further information. **When is the latest I can request a gopost® delivery online so that it is delivered the following day?** In most cases, if you make your request before 12AM, your package will be available at gopost the following day. **Where can I find the hours of availability for the gopost® unit assigned to my delivery address?** Many of the gopost units are accessible 24/7. However, please refer to hours listed on the Redelivery notice form for any exceptions. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#top) **What are gopost® parcel lockers?** Gopost units are automated, secured, self-service parcel lockers placed in convenient locations. In addition to using the gopost Missed Delivery Pickup option, you may at some point choose to become a registered gopost customer - for both receiving and shipping of packages. It's FREE. Just visit [https://gopost.usps.com/go](https://gopost.usps.com/go) to learn more and signup! **How does the gopost® Package Pickup option work?** If we missed you when we tried to deliver your package, we will leave a Redelivery notice ( [PS Form 3849 Redelivery Notice](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PS-Form-3849-Redelivery-Notice)) containing options for redelivery and pickup. If the "Parcel Locker Eligible" option is [checked on the form](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Feb21_parcel_locker_checked.jpg) and you are interested in retrieving your package via gopost, first visit [https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action](https://gopost.usps.com/go/EPLGPSearchAction.action). 1. At the website, provide your original delivery address, contact preferences, and delivery information number found on the notice slip left by your carrier. 2. When your package has been loaded into gopost, you will receive a unique passcode and a reminder of the gopost unit location via email or text. 3. Visit the gopost unit, enter your unique passcode from the email/text, and retrieve your package. If your package requires a signature, you will be instructed to sign your name directly on the gopost kiosk screen using your finger before the locker will unlock. This service is only available in areas supported by gopost. Instructions to guide you to the website are also on the Redelivery form. ![](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/redelivery/PS_Form_3849_Feb21_Back_parcel_locker_instructions.jpg) **How long do I have before I have to pick up my package at the gopost® unit?** Packages will be kept in the gopost unit for the [normal Post Office hold period](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Second-and-Final-Notice-and-Return-Dates-for-Redelivery). **What size packages can be received via gopost®?** gopost unit lockers will fit packages up to 12"W x 15"D x 18½"H. **What types of items cannot be received via gopost® at this time?** Your Redelivery notice form will indicate if your package is eligible for gopost. Ineligible item include: - Letters or flat-sized envelopes less than ¾" thick (other than Priority Mail or Priority Mail Express) - Packages larger than the maximum size (12"W x 15"D x 18½"H) - Packages received with the following Extra Services: | | | | --- | --- | | - Certified Mail®
- Return Receipt (hardcopy "blue card")
- Collect On Delivery (COD)
- Registered Mail® | - Adult Signature
- Restricted Delivery
- Special Handling | Prohibited items may be returned to sender. For packages too large for gopost, USPS will hold the package at a nearby Post Office and will send you the Post Office's address via email/text. Inbound packages that require customs fees cannot be received via the gopost unit. International Extra Services that cannot be included are Restricted Delivery, Registered Mail, and Return Receipt. **For information or assistance:** For the most up-to-date information and usage terms, you may also call 1-USPS-MY GOPOST (1-877-769-4676). [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/gopost-Package-Pickup#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title gopost® Package Pickup URL Name gopost-Package-Pickup [Receiving Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFuGAM/receiving-mail-packages)[Mailboxes](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGLGA2/mailboxes) ## Related Articles - [Package Pickup & Pickup on Demand®](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Package-Pickup) - [USPS Package Intercept® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Package-Intercept-The-Basics) - [Where is my package? ## Who Do I Call for USPS Law Enforcement?

## Contact information for the United States Postal Service two law enforcement agencies; United States Postal Inspection Service and USPS Office of Inspector General.      - Oct 26, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007954

Customer Information

The United States Postal Service has two law enforcement agencies with distinct areas of investigative responsibilities to serve the needs of stakeholders, postal employees, and the American public.

****Office of Inspector General****

- Theft, delay, or destruction of mail by employees or contractors
- Injury compensation fraud
- Embezzlements and financial crimes
- Contract Fraud
- Kickbacks
- Computer Crimes
- Narcotics
- Employee misconduct
- Internal affairs and executive investigations
- Whistleblower reprisals

**Visit their website at [http://www.uspsoig.gov](http://www.uspsoig.gov/ "Home Page | USPS Office of Inspector General")**

888-USPS-OIG (888-877-7644)

**Postal Inspection Service**

- Security of employees, facilities, and equipment
- Revenue and postage fraud
- International mail security
- Violent Crimes
  - Threats and assaults of employees
  - Burglaries and robberies
- Mail theft by nonemployees
- Dangerous mail bombs
- Mail fraud
- Identity theft
- Narcotics in the mail
- Child pornography and obscenity

**Visit their website at [https://www.uspis.gov/](https://www.uspis.gov/)**

877-876-2455

## What Items can be Mailed Using Nonprofit Mail?

## Self-mailers, letters, newsletters and booklets that weigh less than 16 ounces are otherwise eligible to be mailed as USPS Marketing Mail™ (formerly Standard Mail®).      - Mar 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007671

Customer Information

Self-mailers, letters, newsletters and booklets that weigh less than 16 ounces are otherwise eligible to be mailed as [USPS Marketing Mail](https://pe.usps.com/StandardMailEligibility/Index) ™ (formerly Standard Mail®). Create Mail Online (formerly NetPost™) will print, insert, apply postage, presort, and enter your mail at one or more central processing facilities nationwide.

Only material of the authorized organization can be mailed at nonprofit prices. Mailings cannot be done for an unauthorized party or on behalf of a cooperative enterprise. The cooperative mail rule prevents authorized organizations from sharing their authorizations with others who are not authorized. There are also restrictions on advertising for products and services

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000GSzAAM%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Do-I-Ship-Food) ## How Do I Ship Food ## How to ship different types of food and where to find information on proper packaging of food for mailing. - Jan 18, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007506 Customer Information **The [USPS.com Shipping Restrictions page](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm) has information that is easily accessible on different hazardous materials.** | | | --- | | Effective January 19th, 2025, USPS implemented the [Live Animal and Perishable Handling Fee](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Live-Animal-Transportation-Fee) to cover the additional costs associated with handling and transporting of all live animals and perishables (fruit, meat, etc.). | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Domestic Shipping of Perishable Foods** | | **Types of Food** | **Examples** | **Shipping Information and Condition** | **Packaging** | | Meats and meat products | Frozen fish, steaks | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 55 Meats and Meat Products](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c5_011.htm) | See DMM [601.5](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1086073) and [601.8](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1140026) | | Eggs | Eggs | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 54 Eggs](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c5_010.htm) | See DMM [601.5](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1086073) and [601.8](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1140026) | | Fresh food and other perishables | Bananas, grapes, lettuce | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 53 Fresh Food and Other Perishables](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c5_009.htm) | See DMM [601.5](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1086073) and [601.8](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1140026) | | Plants | Trees, citrus, cactus | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 56 Plants](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c5_009.htm) | See DMM [601.5](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1086073) and [601.8](https://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/601.htm#ep1140026) | | Nonperishable, prepackaged foods | Canned food | Most nonperishable food items are mailable domestically if properly packaged. See [USPS.com Shipping Restrictions](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm). | Does not need to be packaged as an above perishable | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **International Shipping of Perishable Foods [\*](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-Do-I-Ship-Food#note-1)** | | **Types of Food** | **Examples** | **Shipping Information and Condition** | | Eggs | Fresh eggs, Dried whole eggs | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 64 Perishable Matter: International Mail](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_015.htm)
See the [Individual Country Listing](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_toc.htm) for shipping restrictions to any country. | | Fresh food and other perishables | Bananas, grapes, lettuce | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 64 Perishable Matter: International Mail](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_017.htm)
See the [Individual Country Listing](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_toc.htm) for shipping restrictions to any country. | | Plants and seeds | Trees, citrus, cactus | See [Publication 52 - Hazardous, Restricted, and Perishable Mail, 64 Perishable Matter: International Mail](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_016.htm)
See the [Individual Country Listing](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_toc.htm) for shipping restrictions to any country. | | Nonperishable food items | Canned food | See the [Individual Country Listing](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/ab_toc.htm) for shipping restrictions to any country. See the complete [International Mail Manual (IMM)](https://pe.usps.com/text/imm/welcome.htm) for complete International shipping information. | **Note:** - \*Fruits, vegetables, fresh meats, and other articles that easily decompose or that cannot reasonably be expected to reach their destination without spoiling are nonmailable. ## What are the Shipping Options for Small Business Owners?

## There are several shipping options to deposit shipment with the United State Postal Service.      - Sep 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007495

Customer Information

**There are five shipping options available in addition to depositing shipments at a Post Office™ facility.**

1. Leave it for your letter carrier when they deliver your mail. This may be limited to the thickness of the maipiece, the weight of the mailpiece and the room your letter carrier has in their vehicle.
2. Deposit in a Collection Box. This may be limited depending on the weight or thickness of the mailpieces.
3. Deposit in your business mailbox. This may be limited depending on the weight or thickness of the mailpieces.
4. Package Pickup is a requested next day (or other designated day) service. There is no extra charge for the pickup (regardless of the number of items requested for pickup). Pickup will occur with mail delivery or collection. Individual items cannot exceed 70 pounds or 130 inches in total length and girth. Free package pickup service can be scheduled up to a year in advance.
   - To qualify for free Package Pickup Service, your pickup must include a Priority Mail Express® shipment, a Priority Mail® shipment, an international shipment, or a returns package; packages of other mail classes may also be picked up, in combination with the four aforementioned products.
5. Scheduled same day pickup is available with Pickup on Demand® service. There is a fee for Pickup on Demand® service per trip regardless of the number of items scheduled for pickup. Individual items cannot exceed 70 pounds or 108 inches in total length and girth. Subject to availability.

For additional information on Pickup on Demand® fees, go to [www.usps.com/pickup](http://www.usps.com/pickup).

**Notes:**

- Mail pieces bearing postage stamps for postage and weighing more than 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness must be brought to an employee at a Post Office™ retail counter.
- All mail must have the same return information as the location where the mail is being picked up from.

****Eligibility of Classes/Services for Free Package Pickup:****

- Priority Mail Express® service
- Priority Mail® service
- Priority Mail Express® or Priority Mail Open and Distribute® shipments
- USPS Ground Advantage™
- International shipments:
  - Priority Mail Express International® service
  - Priority Mail International® service
  - First-Class Package International Service® shipments
- Returns Package:
  - USPS Ground Advantage™ Return service
  - Parcel Return Service
  - Bulk Parcel Return Service

**Eligibility of Classes/Services for Pickup On Demand® service:**

- Priority Mail Express®
- Priority Mail®
- Parcel Select®
- USPS Ground Advantage™
- Package Service parcels
  - Bound Printed Matter
  - Library Mail
  - Media Mail® service
- Priority Mail Express® or Priority Mail Open and Distribute® shipments
- Priority Mail Express International®
- Priority Mail International®
- First-Class Package International Service® shipments

**Note**: Mail pieces bearing postage stamps for postage and weighing more than 10 oz. or measuring more than 1/2-inch in thickness must be brought to an employee at a Post Office™ retail counter.

See [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) for additional information and resources.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6t0000000bmTvAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail) ## USPS® Mail Requiring a Signature - Accountable Mail ## Maintaining compliance to the policies regarding control of accountable mail that requires a signature of the addressee or addressee’s agent upon receipt to provide evidence of delivery or indemnification for loss or damage. - May 26, 2022 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007487 Customer Information Maintaining compliance to the policies regarding control of accountable mail that requires a signature of the addressee or addressee’s agent upon receipt to provide evidence of delivery or indemnification for loss or damage. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **[Accountable Mail Items](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#Accountable)** | **[Non-accountable Mail Items](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#nonaccountable)** | [**Refusing Accountable Mail**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#Refusing) | **Accountable Mail Items** **Accountable mail requires a signature and/or payment of fees from the recipient or the recipient's agent before delivery can be completed.** You or an [authorized agent](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Authorizing-Someone-to-Accept-Your-Redelivery) must be present to sign for accountable mail being **delivered by a mail carrier**. If delivery was attempted for your accountable mail item and **you want to schedule a redelivery**, signing a Redelivery Notice (PS Form 3849) and leaving it for the mail carrier **does not** meet the minimum requirements for redelivery. You or an authorized agent must be present to sign for accountable mail when the carrier is redelivering the item. See [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) for additional information on scheduling a redelivery. If delivery was attempted for your accountable mail item and **you want to pick it up from your local Post Office**, see [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) for additional information. If **you want someone else to pick up your accountable mail item**, either: - A [Standing Delivery Order](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Standing-Delivery-Order-PS-Form-3801) needs to be in place that authorizes the person to act as your agent or - Follow the instructions on the back of PS Form 3849 under option 6. See [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) for additional information. - **Note**: Either of these is required even for a person with the same last name and address as the recipient. **These items are accountable mail and require a signature and/or payment of fees:** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | - Adult Signature Required
- Adult Signature Restricted Delivery
- Insured Mail (insured for over $500)
- Collect On Delivery (COD)
- Registered Mail® | - Postage Due / Return Postage Due
- Certified Mail™
- Foreign Origin Mail with extra services
- Signature Confirmation™ service | - Restricted Delivery option[1](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#note-1)
- Domestic Priority Mail Express® service items ( [unless signature waived is not required](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-a-Waiver-of-Signature-What-is-Signature-Required))
- Return Receipt service | | 1 Restricted Delivery service is even more specific. It is delivered **only** to the addressee or to the person authorized in writing as the addressee's agent to receive the mail. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#Top) **Non-Accountable Mail Items** Non-accountable mail can be delivered to an address without a signature, or when the recipient is not present if the item is endorsed "CARRIER-LEAVE IF NO RESPONSE." Non-accountable mail includes: - Perishable Items - USPS Tracking® items - Uninsured Packages - Insured Mail (up to $500) Although a signature is not required for delivery of these items, a carrier may leave a redelivery notice PS Form 3849 if the item cannot fit in the mail receptacle or be left in a secure location. See [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) for additional information. **Refusing Accountable Mail** You (or your representative) may obtain the sender’s name and address while held by the USPS employee before either: - Accepting delivery and endorsing the delivery receipt or - Refusing delivery and endorsing the item "Refused" Once accepted, an accountable mail item is considered delivered and cannot be refused. However, it can be returned to sender with new postage. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail#Top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS® Mail Requiring a Signature - Accountable Mail URL Name USPS-Mail-Requiring-a-Signature-Accountable-Mail [Sending Mail & Packages](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFyGAM/sending-mail-packages)[Mail & Shipping Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGpGAM/mail-shipping-services) ## Related Articles - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [Missing Mail - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Missing-Mail-The-Basics) - [How is Undeliverable and Misdelivered Mail Handled?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Undeliverable-and-Misdelivered-Mail-Handled) - [Picking Up Mail that is Being Held at Your Post Office](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Picking-Up-Mail-that-is-Being-Held-at-Your-Post-Office) - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## Shipping Products and Services

## USPS® can help make shipping for your business simple. We offer a variety of domestic and international shipping products and services for small businesses. The USPS standard and express mail services allow you to choose delivery options that fit your needs. Find the fastest shipping rate or most affordable shipping cost depending on what items you’re sending and when you want them delivered.      - Mar 31, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007432

Customer Information

## Learn more about the products and services that can help make shipping for your business simple.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[USPS Shipping Products & Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services#USPSShippingProductsandServices)** | **[Package Tracking](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services#PackageTracking)** | [**Schedule a Pickup**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services#Schedulepackagepickup) |
| **[Free Shipping Supplies](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services#Freeshippingsupplies)** | **[Small Business Support](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Products-and-Services#Smallbusinesssupport)** |  |

## For a comparison of USPS shipping products and services, please see [usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm)

## **USPS Shipping Products & Services:**

## [**Priority Mail**](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail.htm)

## Shipping in 2 - 3 Business Days & Flat Rate Pricing

Priority Mail® is our bestselling mail service. Domestic deliveries arrive in 2-3 business days depending on where your package starts and where it's going. Tracking and insurance are included, shipping boxes and envelopes are free, and with Priority Mail Flat Rate®, you don't need to weigh shipments 70 lbs and under. Purchase Priority Mail postage and shipping labels at a Post Office or online.

### [**Priority Mail Express**](https://www.usps.com/ship/priority-mail-express.htm)

## 1-Day to 2-3 Day Guarantee by 6 PM

Our fastest domestic shipping service, Priority Mail Express® delivers 7 days a week, 365 days a year (with limited exceptions). Tracking and insurance are included, and a proof of delivery signature record is included if requested at time of purchase. Next-day delivery is available to most U.S. addresses and PO Boxes™ with a money-back guarantee. With our free Flat Rate Envelopes, you don't need to weigh shipments 70 lbs and under. Purchase Priority Mail Express postage and shipping labels at a Post Office™ location or online.

### [**First Class Mail**](https://www.usps.com/ship/first-class-mail.htm)

## Mail in 1–5 Business Days

First-Class Mail® is an affordable mail service for standard-sized, single-piece postcards, letters weighing up to 3.5 oz, and large envelopes (flats) weighing up to 13 oz.

### [**Media Mail**](https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm)

## Shipping in 2 - 8 Business Days

Media Mail® is a cost-effective way to send media and educational materials. This service has restrictions on the type of media that can be sent. Maximum weight is 70 lbs. Here's what you can send:

- Books (at least 8 pages)
- 16-millimeter or narrower width films
- Printed music and test materials
- Video and sound recordings
- Play scripts and manuscripts
- Printed educational reference charts
- Medical loose-leaf pages and binders
- Computer-readable media

**NOTE**: Video games, computer drives, and digital drives do not qualify for Media Mail prices.

### [**USPS Ground Advantage®**](https://www.usps.com/ship/ground-advantage.htm)

## Shipping in 2 - 5 Business Days

USPS Ground Advantage® is a reliable and economical way to ship less-than-urgent deliveries and over-sized packages. USPS Ground Advantage ships packages up to 70 lbs and up to 130 inches in combined length and girth.

- Includes USPS Tracking®
- $100 of included insurance, up to $5,000 in additional insurance can be purchased
- Available at USPS retail locations and in Click-N-Ship

**Package Tracking**

#### **[USPS Tracking®](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm)**

Provides tracking updates as an item travels to its destination, including the date and time of delivery or attempted delivery. Available online or at your Post Office depending on the mail class. There is no additional charge.

#### **[USPS Tracking Plus™](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm\#trackingplus)**

Purchase extended tracking history for your item and receive a Premium Tracking Statement via email upon request. Get access for up to 10 years.

- Purchase online through [USPS Tracking](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input) ®.
- Available for many mail classes with USPS Tracking. See [USPS.com](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm#trackingplus) for list of products eligible for USPS Tracking Plus.

## Do you require proof of delivery?

**[Certified Mail®](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm)**

Prove you sent it. See when it was delivered or that a delivery attempt was made, and get the signature of the person who accepts the mailing when combined with Return Receipt.

- Purchase at your Post Office.
- Present for mailing to a Postal employee if a postmarked sender's receipt is requested.
- Combine with Return Receipt to get an electronic or physical delivery record showing the recipient's signature.
- The recipient may be required to show an acceptable primary ID before USPS will deliver the mail piece.

**[Return Receipt](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm)**

Get an electronic or hard copy delivery record showing the recipient's signature.

- Purchase at a Post Office depending on the mail class.
- Available with Priority Mail Express using only [PS Form 3811 (54 KB)](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3811a.pdf).
- Electronic Return Receipts are not available to APO/FPO/DPO addresses or to certain U.S. territories or possessions, or Freely Associated States.

## How to schedule a package pickup?

Customers can submit a free [Package Pickup request](https://tools.usps.com/schedule-pickup-steps.htm) at USPS.com to have prepaid Express Mail, Priority Mail, International Mail, and Return Service packages picked up the next delivery day as part of the carrier's normal route.

## Free Shipping Supplies:

Find [**free shipping supplies**](https://store.usps.com/store/results/shipping-supplies/_/N-7d0v8v?_requestid=2108842) for your Priority Mail and Priority Mail Express shipping needs.

## Small Business Support:

For specific, fast, and professional support for your small business, see [Business Solutions with USPS® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Business-Solutions-with-USPS-The-Basics) or call the small business hotline at: 1-USPS-4-SMBIZ (1-877-747-6249).

Remaining boxes can be used for shipping at weight-and-rate packages. - Jan 21, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007280 Customer Information | | | --- | | **Effective January 22, 2023**, Regional Rate Box pricing has been discontinued. Remaining Regional Rate Boxes can still be used for shipping, but they will be treated as weight and rate packages. Customers who currently use these boxes are encouraged to place orders before supplies are depleted.
The recommended alternative for Regional Rate Box pricing is Priority Mail™ Cubic. Priority Mail Cubic is a sub-service of Priority Mail in which commercial customers ship items using their own packaging and the pricing is based on the outer dimensions of the package rather than weight. In addition:
- This option offers similar cost savings as Regional Rate boxes.
- Priority Mail Cubic packages must measure 0.5 cubic feet or less, weigh 20 pounds or less, and the longest dimension may not exceed 18 inches.
- Priority Mail Cubic has zone pricing with five price tiers up to 0.5 cubic feet | **Regional Rate Boxes** | | | | --- | --- | | ![Regional Rate Box A1](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6t0000000bmOc&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000008ONmS) | Regional Rate Box A1 – top loading

## How is Signature Confirmation™ and Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery Used?

## Mail class eligibility, eligibility of territories, and extra services compatibility with Signature Confirmation and Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery services. Also, information on how to obtain delivery information and the fees and payment methods for the services.      - Jan 21, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007279

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**Signature Confirmation™ Eligibility**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#signature_confirmation_eligibility) | [**Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery Eligibility**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#signature_confirmation_restricted_delivery_eligibility) | [**Additional services**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#additional_services) |
| [**Fees and payment methods**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#fes_and_payment_methods) | [**Accessing delivery information**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#accessing_delivery_information) |  |

**ELIGIBLE Items for Signature Confirmation™**

- First-Class Mail® parcels (electronic option only)
- Priority Mail®
- USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail
- USPS Ground Advantage™ (commercial)
- Bound Printed Matter parcels
- Library Mail parcels
- Media Mail® parcels
- Parcel Select®

**INELIGIBLE Items for Signature Confirmation™**

- International Mail
- Priority Mail Express®, Periodicals, and USPS Marketing Mail®
- First-Class Mail® letter-size and flat-size pieces.
- Mail paid with precanceled stamps
- Mail sent to **select** APO/FPO and DPO destinations.
- Mail addressed to ZIP Codes™ in the following U.S. territories or Freely Associated States:

| **Destination** | **Zip Codes** |
| --- | --- |
| **Palau, Republic of** | **96939 & 96940** |
| **Pohnpei Island** | **96941** |
| **Chuuk (Truk) Island** | **96942** |
| **Yap Island** | **96943** |
| **Kosrae Island** | **96944** |
| **Majuro Island** | **96960** |
| **Ebeye Island** | **96970** |

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#top)

**ELIGIBLE items for Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery**

- First-Class Mail® parcels (if purchased with Certified Mail, COD, insurance over $500.00 or Registered Mail)
- Priority Mail® (if purchased with Certified Mail, COD, insurance over $500.00 or Registered Mail)
- USPS Ground Advantage™-Retail
- USPS Ground Advantage™ (commercial)
- Bound Printed Matter parcels
- Library Mail parcels
- Media Mail parcels
- Parcel Select®

**INELIGIBLE items for Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery**

- International Mail
- Priority Mail Express®, Periodicals, and USPS Marketing Mail®.
- First-Class Mail® letter-size and flat-size pieces.
- Package Services letter-size and flat-size pieces.
- Mail paid with precanceled stamps
- Mail sent to APO/FPO and DPO destinations.
- Mail addressed to ZIP Codes™ in the following U.S. territories or Freely Associated States:

| **Destination** | **Zip Codes** |
| --- | --- |
| ****Palau, Republic of**** | **96939 & 96940** |
| ****Pohnpei Island**** | **96941** |
| ****Chuuk (Truk) Island**** | **96942** |
| ****Yap Island**** | **96943** |
| ****Kosrae Island**** | **96944** |
| ****Majuro Island**** | **96960** |
| ****Ebeye Island**** | **96970** |

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#top)

**Additional services**

**Signature Confirmation™ and Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery can be combined with:**

- Insurance (if less than or equal to $500)
- Collect on Delivery (COD)
- Registered Mail® service
- Hold for Pickup
- Return Receipt (PS Form 3811 only) (if the customer purchases insurance for more than $500, COD, or Registered Mail® service)

**Note:** Since the Signature Confirmation service provides the ability to request a delivery record, the purchase of a Return Receipt is generally an unnecessary expense.

Signature Confirmation and Signature Confirmation Restricted Delivery **cannot** be combined with:

- Certified Mail®
- Priority Mail Express (Priority Mail Express items includes signature option)
- USPS Tracking® service (Signature Confirmation provides all of the benefits and more of USPS Tracking)

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#top)

**Fees and payment methods**

- Fees vary for Signature Confirmation Service, depending on how it was purchased (Retail vs. Electronic options). For detailed information on fees, go to[https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm).
- Fees for Signature Confirmation may be paid with ordinary postage stamps (excluding precanceled stamps), meter stamps, PC Postage, or permit imprints.
- Retail vs. Electronic options for Signature Confirmation Service:
  - The **Retail Fee** can be purchased at your local Post Office™. The retail label, [PS Form 153](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps153.pdf), is hot pink with a detachable mailing receipt.  You can print online [Shipping Labels](https://reg.usps.com/entreg/LoginAction_input?app=GSS&appURL=https://cns.usps.com/labelInformation.shtml) for Priority Mail service and qualify for the lower-fee electronic option. High-volume shippers can also print their own labels or purchase preprinted labels from the United States Postal Service®.
  - The **Electronic Fee** is for mailers who purchase postage online (for example, using Click-N-Ship®) and privately print an electronic Signature Confirmation label.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#top)

**Accessing delivery information**

- Tracking Signature Confirmation En Route - While en route, scans **may** be provided when available. They are not a feature of Signature Confirmation service.
- Delivery Status Availability - Status is available the evening of the date of delivery or attempted delivery, similar to USPS Tracking® service.
- Delivery information will be available for one year from the delivery date. A complete delivery record--including **signature** image--can be requested two to three days after delivery.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-is-Signature-Confirmation-and-Signature-Confirmation-Restricted-Delivery-Used#top)

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6t0000000bmRoAAI%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues) ## USPS.com® Website Support - Browser and Download Issues ## Instructions on resolving a few technical issues, like clearing your browser's cache and cookies, enlarging text, and what may be causing slow down or messages like "Please try again". - Jan 20, 2022 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000007277 Customer Information ## Solving [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® Website Problems **Most website performance and visual issues can be corrected by clearing the cache and cookies in your internet browser.** 1. Clear the internet browser cache / cookies using the following Shortcut Keys: | | | | --- | --- | | **Operating System** | **Shortcut** | | Windows | Ctrl > Shift > Delete | | Mac | Command > Shift > Delete | 2. Close the browser and reopen it. | | | --- | | **NOTE:** There are numerous websites available to assist you in clearing your browser's history, cache and cookies. Please use your favorite search engine to locate additional resources and explanations on why clearing browsers occasionally is important. | | | | --- | | **Supported Browsers**
**All browsers must be the current version or its previous version** | | Microsoft Edge **®** | | Google Chrome **™** | | Mozilla **®** Firefox **®** | | Safari **®** | **Other [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® Website Problems** **What if I get a website message like "The system is unable to respond to that query" or "Please try again later"?** This message indicates the system is busy or out of service. As with any Internet system, performance can be adversely affected by many outside influences. If you receive this type of message, please try again. **Why am I having problems with speed as I move through the [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) website?** This website has been designed to perform at optimal speed. Heavy traffic on the Internet might affect the speed at which you are able to move through this website. We are constantly working to improve our performance and infrastructure. Try clearing your internet browser's history, cache and cookies. [**Click here**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues#shortcuts). **How can I make the text on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) larger?** If you are unable to read small text on the [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) website, there is a way to increase the text size by using your Browser Zoom feature. **Note**: increasing the font size will also increase the size of icons and graphics. - **PC users:** To increase the text size hold down the keyboard Ctrl key and press the plus (+) key button until text appears the desired size. To decrease, hold down the keyboard Ctrl key and press the minus (-) key button until text is reduced to the desired size. - **MAC users:** To increase the text size hold down the Apple key and press the plus (+) key button until text appears the desired size. To decrease, hold down the Apple key and press the minus (-) key button until text is reduced to the desired size. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues#top_faq) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title USPS.com® Website Support - Browser and Download Issues URL Name USPS-com-Website-Support-Browser-and-Download-Issues [USPS.com / Services](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HFvGAM/uspscom-services)[Technical Support](https://faq.usps.com/s/topic/0TOt00000004HGVGA2/technical-support) ## Related Articles - [USPS.com® Account](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-com-Account) - [Domestic Claims - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Domestic-Claims-The-Basics) - [USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics) - [PO Box™ - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/PO-Box-The-Basics) - [Redelivery - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Redelivery-The-Basics) ## Trending Articles - [Where is my package? ## Automated Package Verification Program for Domestic Packages

## Description of the Automated Package Verification system within Click-N-Ship®; what postage due charges in CNS are for, how to pay them, how to handle the dispute process and who to contact for assistance.      - Mar 14, 2025 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007268

Customer Information

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [**What is Automated Package Verification and who is impacted by the process?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#whatisit) | [**Disputes and disagreements**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#disputes) | [**Who do I contact with questions?**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#questions) |
| [**Tips to avoid postage fee differences**](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#postagefeetips) |  |  |

**What is Automated Package Verification and who is impacted by the process?**

Automated Package Verification (APV) streamlines how we check postage on certain packages with labels generated from Click-N-Ship® and PC Postage applications ( [PC Postage applications include Click-N-Ship, Endicia, Stamps.com. eBay, PayPal, Easy Post, and Pitney Bowes](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-PC-Postag-ePostage)). Previously, the Postal Service only sent notifications to shippers when differences in package characteristics are detected.

Now, USPS® notifies shippers through Click-N-Ship® (CNS) or their PC Postage entity when differences in package characteristics are detected. USPS makes postage payment adjustments for affected items and these pieces are no longer subjected to the [manual postage due process](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#improve). Payment adjustments are facilitated through those platforms.

Notifications are sent via email from your PC Postage vendor or are displayed upon logging into the appropriate account, whether it is USPS Click-N-Ship or a shipping option like eBay that uses PC Postage.

To get more information on this new feature and how it affects you, please visit [https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm). Scroll down the page to the “About Automated Package Verification” section. Customer service contact information is [below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#questions).

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#top)

**How does this improve upon the postage due process?**

USPS relies heavily on a manual postage due process. Mail with postage discrepancies is either returned to the senders/shippers to be corrected or delivered to its destination for the recipient/customer to pay the postage due. This process may delay shipments and/or your customer/recipient may have to pay extra, unexpected postage, which erodes the quality of the customer experience.

**Why is USPS implementing an automated postage due process?**

In addition to the reasoning mentioned above, implementing an automated postage due process helps us ensure we can maintain competitive and economical shipping prices for our customers.

**How will I be notified?**

USPS notifies shippers through Click-N-Ship® or their PC Postage entity when differences in package characteristics are detected. USPS has begun making postage payment adjustments for affected items and these pieces are no longer subjected to the manual postage due process. Payment adjustments are facilitated through Click-N-Ship and PC Postage platforms.

**When does a postage difference occur?**

Generally, a postage difference occurs when the package characteristics (e.g. weight, dimensions, zone, etc.) used for label creation do not match measurements of the actual package mailed as captured by USPS processing equipment. Most commonly, this is for one of the following reasons that results in a different mail class or rating category:

1. Incorrect weight on label resulting in a price difference due to different rating or exceeding limitations for the class chosen.
2. Incorrect dimensions vs those of the actual package shipped.
3. Incorrect service or packaging entered for label creation vs actual service/packaging material used (e.g., First-class labels cannot be used on Priority Mail or Priority Mail Express packaging).

[Tips to avoid postage fee shortages or overpayments are below](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#postagefeetips).

**How accurate is the automated package verification process?**

The state-of-the-art mail processing network has been tested extensively. The equipment is checked daily for calibration of scales to ensure we get the most accurate information that will help us to best streamline your shipping experience.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#top)

**Disputes and disagreements**

If you believe you have received a notification from your PC Postage provider in error, please follow the instructions on the notice to submit a dispute. USPS may review all scan data relating to your piece in question, including image analysis, and, if such an error has occurred, USPS will work through our PC Postage partners to return the amount in question.

**To submit a dispute:**

Submit the required information to the APV Dispute Webform page at [https://apvdisputes.usps.com](https://apvdisputes.usps.com/). If you need assistance obtaining the information, please reach out to your PC Postage Entity/Vendor.

**R** **equired informatio** **n:**

- Shipper's **First and Last Name**
- Shipper's **Email Address**
- Shipper's **Telephone Number with Area Code**
- **Revenue Assurance ID / USPS Adjustment ID Number** (an unique number for each tracking number and is a 15-digit number currently with 5 possible leading zeroes, DO NOT include the zeroes when adding this information on the website).
- **USPS Tracking Number / IMPB(Tracking Number begins with 94 - The IMPB is the last 4-digits of the USPS Tracking Number)**
- **Reason(i.e. Weight, Package, Zone, Dimensions, etc.)**

Except for Click-N-Ship, USPS does not have access to any of these entities systems, nor any of their customer accounts.  Below is a list of the PC Postage entities with their contact information, however we do not have any third-party vendors contact information:

- Click-N-Ship:  1-800-275-8777, 1-800-344-7779, (or) [Click-N-Ship@usps.gov](mailto:Click-N-Ship@usps.gov)
- eBay:  [https://ebay.com/](https://gcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Febay.com%2F&data=05%7C02%7CSuzanne.M.Bobetsky%40usps.gov%7Cdf6aab20ec7f4ac50ecb08dd04bf9787%7Cf9aa5788eb334a498ad076101910cac3%7C0%7C0%7C638671943131616602%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=vcrQJsFjwiLSZm6RN8UkqlThEJJj7451otKpVVHHxnk%3D&reserved=0) \- Click on “Help & Contact” at the bottom portion of their webpage (or) 1-866-540-3229
- EasyPost:  [Support@EasyPost.com](mailto:Support@EasyPost.com)
- Endicia:  1-800-576-3279
- PayPal:  1-888-221-1161
- Pitney Bowes:  1-844-256-6444
- [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/):  1-855-889-7867 or 1-888-434-0055


**How long will it take USPS to review my dispute? How can I check the status of my dispute?**

USPS may review all scan data relating to your piece in question, including image analysis, and if such an error has occurred, we will work through our PC Postage partners to return the amount in question. Most responses can be expected in 2-5 business days, and at most 15 business days. The status of your dispute claim is available from your PC Postage provider and may be updated daily.

**When will I receive my refund?**

If your dispute is upheld you may be due a refund. All payments are processed by your PC Postage provider on behalf of USPS so please reach out to your PC Postage provider directly for questions regarding billing or your account.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#top)

**Who do I contact with questions?**

If you have questions about your notice or the postage due process, please:

- Email [apvsupport@usps.gov](mailto:verifypostagehelp@usps.gov)
- Visit [https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm](https://www.usps.com/business/verify-postage.htm)
- Call 1-844-819-5187 for the APV Help Desk

APV customer service representatives are available as follows:

- Monday thru Friday: 7:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. CT
- Saturday: Closed
- Sunday: Closed
- Holidays: Closed

If you have any questions about your Click-N-Ship® account, you can contact the Click-N-Ship Help Desk by:

- [Email Us](https://emailus.usps.com/) and click " [USPS.com](http://usps.com/)" as the reason for your email
- Phone at 1-800-344-7779

CNS customer service representatives are available as follows:

- Monday thru Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. ET
- Saturday: 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET
- Sunday: Closed
- Holidays: Closed

For any questions about your PC Postage account, please contact your PC Postage® provider directly.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#top)

**Tips to avoid postage fee differences**

**Label your package correctly**

USPS provides a wide range of shipping options to help you meet your mailing needs. Make sure that the label you are using matches the packaging. For example, if you are using a Flat Rate box, you should also use a Flat Rate label. Or anything shipped in Priority Mail® packaging requires a Priority Mail label. Also, ensure you place the label on the long side of the box. Labels should not wrap over the ends or edges and all barcodes should face up in the same direction.

**Measure weight and dimensions**

When shipping using your own packaging, accurately measuring and entering weight and dimensions (length, width, and height) is essential to ensure postage is calculated correctly.

USPS® rounds up to either the next pound (lb) or ounce (oz). For example, if your package weighs 3 lbs 5 oz, it would be charged at the 4 lb-rate. If you have a USPS Ground Advantage™ that weighs 7.3 oz, it must be charged at the 8 oz-rate. This applies to all mail classes — domestic and international.

You can find more information on proper shipping techniques at [Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards).

Dimensional (DIM) weight may also apply to your packaging. DIM weight is determined by multiplying a package's length, width, and height and applying a volumetric divisor. See [Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards) for more information.

**Select the right mail class**

For example, USPS Ground Advantage™ delivery can only be used for packages under 15.999 oz; anything over the weight limit must be shipped with Priority Mail® service.

**Double check the zone**

If you are using PC Postage® software, verify that the origin (or “sent from”) address is up to date before printing the label. Please note USPS charges for zones, not by ZIP Code™. To get a zone chart for your ZIP Code, use the [Zone Chart form](https://postcalc.usps.com/DomesticZoneChart) found under the Postage Price Calculator. You can also refer to " [What are the Zone Charts and how can I obtain one?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-are-the-Zone-Charts-and-how-can-I-obtain-one)" for more information.

[![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Automated-Package-Verification-Program#top)

## Electronic Return Receipt

## Mail classes that are eligible for Electronic Return Receipt and how customers can obtain the service for a single mailpiece or in bulk.      - Jan 20, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007257

Customer Information

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **[How do I obtain the Return Receipt (Electronic)?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#obtain_Return_Receipt_Electronic)** | **[When will I receive the Return Receipt (Electronic)?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#receive_Return_Receipt_Electronic)** |
| **[Would it not be easier to get my email address for Return Receipt (Electronic) at purchase?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#email_address)** | **[Is there an option to receive Return Receipt (Electronic) in bulk?](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#option_to_receive_RRE_bulk)** |

Return Receipt (Electronic) is an electronic alternative to the [Return Receipt (green postcard)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Return-Receipt-The-Basics).

- This service can be purchased at the time of mailing with:
  - Priority Mail Express® (Form 3811 only)
  - Priority Mail®, First-Class Mail®, USPS Ground Advantage™ - Commercial, Parcel Select® (if purchased with Certified Mail®, Collect on Delivery (COD), Insurance over $500, or Registered Mail®)
  - USPS Marketing Mail™ (parcels only, excluding Marketing Parcels; if purchased with Bulk Insurance over $500)
  - USPS Ground Advantage™, Bound Printed Matter, Library Mail, Media Mail® (if purchased with Collect on Delivery or Insurance over $500)
- As with Return Receipt service, the electronic option provides a mailer with evidence of delivery (to whom the mail was delivered and date of delivery), along with information about the recipient's actual delivery address.
- Return Receipt (Electronic) is an official United States Postal Service® document designed to be equivalent to the hardcopy Return Receipt that has been in use for many years. However, its legal status is not determined by the Postal Service™. That decision is made by the individual courts.
- Return Receipt (Electronic) records are kept for two years from the date of mailing.  After 60 days the record will take longer to retrieve.

**Notes:**

- Return Receipt (Electronic) currently cannot be purchased through [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ®. It is available for purchase from Post Offices™ at the time of mailing. Electronic return receipts requested at the time of mailing are available individually online through USPS Tracking. [Bulk Proof of Delivery](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#option_to_receive_RRE_bulk) is available to authorized commercial mailers.
- Return Receipt (Electronic) is not available for items mailed to APO/FPO/DPO locations or U.S. territories, possessions, or freely associated states.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#top_faq)

**How do I obtain the Return Receipt (Electronic)?**

You need to request a Return Receipt (Electronic) at the time of mailing. You must wait for delivery to take place and for the signature record to be fully completed before we can email it to you.

**To get a copy of the delivery record:**

- Go to the [USPS Tracking website](http://www.usps.com/go/tools/track-confirm "USPS Tracking page").
- Enter the Certified Mail, COD, Insured Mail (over $500), or Registered Mail® article number from the mailing label or receipt.
- Input your email address when presented with the Return Receipt (Electronic) option.
- Return Receipt emails include a link to the USPS Tracking prepopulated with tracking/delivery information on the mail item.
- The signature will arrive as a proof of delivery letter via email attachment (rather than the green postcard otherwise used).

**Note:** To request your Return Receipt (Electronic), you **must** have your article barcode number. This can be found on your purchase receipt, depending on the Post Office of issuance, or on your accountable mail label receipt. If you do not have the article barcode number, you will not be able to obtain a Return Receipt (Electronic).

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#top_faq)

**When will I receive the Return Receipt (Electronic)?**

The amount of time it takes to receive the Return Receipt (Electronic) request depends on when the Return Receipt on the [USPS Tracking website](http://www.usps.com/go/tools/track-confirm "USPS Tracking page") at [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® is requested. If a record is requested on an article that has already been delivered and all related documentation has been completed, we will process it immediately. Depending on the email server, the email request should be received within hours. If the record is requested prior to delivery, then we must wait for delivery to happen and for the signature record to be completed. The Return Receipt letter is usually available to be sent by email within 48 hours of delivery.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#top_faq)

**Would it not be easier to get my email address for Return Receipt (Electronic) at purchase?**

To ensure that we capture your email correctly, we ask customers to visit our website and enter the requested information. Some customers may choose not to receive an email response for each Return Receipt purchased. Instead, they may want to get an email response only for the ones that they have questions about. Allowing customers to enter their email address as opposed to the United States Postal Service® provides this flexibility.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#top_faq)

**Is there an option to receive Return Receipt (Electronic) in bulk?**

Customers that purchase Return Receipt (Electronic) from a Post Office are required to enter each request individually and will receive an email address for each Return Receipt (Electronic) purchased. Electronic return receipt customers that mail large volumes, such as 25 items per day, can participate in the USPS Bulk Proof of Delivery Program and obtain signature data through a signature extract file. Customers interested in participating in the Bulk Proof of Delivery service can refer to your local Post Office or to to [PostalPro](https://postalpro.usps.com/shipping/bulk-proof-delivery-program) for more details.

[Back to Top](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-Electronic-Return-Receipt#top_faq)

## How Should I Mail Items Using PC Postage®?

## Domestic mailpieces with a return address and labels prepared using PC Postage service can be deposited in the Collection Box as long as it fits through the opening. Larger packages may be dropped at the local Post Office.      - Dec 8, 2021 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000007251

Customer Information

**How soon after printing a PC Postage® label should I use it?**

Some PC Postage providers require that the customer mail their item the same day a postage label is printed.  However some vendors will allow the customer to print labels to use at a later date. You should check your vendor to find out when a label has to be used by. Below are links to the websites and additional information on label usage requirements of some PC Postage vendors:

- [Endicia.com](http://endicia.com/)  --  [http://www.pictureitpostage.com/](http://www.pictureitpostage.com/)
- [Stamps.com](http://stamps.com/) -- [http://photo.stamps.com/PhotoStamps](http://photo.stamps.com/PhotoStamps)
- Pitney Bowes -- [https://www.pitneybowes.com/us/shipping/software-and-systems/sendpro-online.html](https://www.pitneybowes.com/us/shipping/software-and-systems/sendpro-online.html)
- EasyPost -- [https://www.easypost.com/](https://www.easypost.com/)

**If my postage label is from Click-N-Ship®, does it need to be used the same day?**

Customers using Click-N-Ship postage labels must mail their item on the date selected for the Click-N-Ship® label. An electronic record is generated on that date indicating that the mailpiece has been mailed. Packages shipped with labels that have incorrect ship dates may be returned to the sender and will not be eligible for a refund. If the customer is unable to use the label, they should request a refund within 30 days of the printed label and create another label with the correct ship date.

**Can I deposit my PC Postage® labeled mail into the USPS Collection Box?**

Domestic mailpieces with a return address and with Priority Mail Express® or Priority Mail® labels prepared using PC Postage service (postage paid for with a credit card) can be deposited in the Collection Box as long as it fits through the opening. Larger packages may be dropped at the local Post Office, handed to a letter carrier, or picked up by USPS® via Pickup on Demand®.

When mailing Priority Mail Express® pieces in Collection Boxes, Priority Mail Express® packaging is highly recommended. Stamped items and international pieces must follow existing mailing

requirements.

**How do I deposit my other PC Postage® labeled mail?**

- Priority Mail Express®
- Flat-rate Priority Mail®,
- single-piece priceFirst-Class Mail®
- single-piece price Media Mail® and
- single-piece price Library Mail

Items prepared through PC Postage® may be deposited at any postal facility, but preferably at your local Post Office™.

All mail not specified above must be deposited at the authorized mailing office (for presort price mail) or at a postal facility within the ZIP Code™ on which the price was based.

## How Can Found Key and Identification Devices Be Mailed to the Owner?

## There is a Return Service dedicated to returning lost keys, identification cards, and uncovered identification tags to the owner.    The key or ID device must bear, contain, or have securely attached the name and complete address of a person, organization, or concern. The device must also have instructions to return it to an address and a statement guaranteeing payment of postage due on delivery.      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006898

Customer Information

A return service is available for keys and identification devices, such as identification cards and uncovered identification tags.

The key or ID device must bear, contain, or have securely attached the name and complete address of a person, organization, or concern. The device must also have instructions to return it to an address and a statement guaranteeing payment of postage due on delivery.

The prices for mailing a key or ID device at different weights and either First-Class Mail or Priority Mail service can be found in the [_Price List, Notice 123_](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#a_Keys%20and%20Identification%20Devices "Notice 123 | Postal Explorer"). The following chart provides a breakdown of the pricing.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| ****Key and Identification Devices Weight**** | ****Postage Charge**** |
| 13 ounces or less | The applicable First-Class Mail or Priority Mail price and a fee |
| More than 13 ounces but not more than 1 pound (16 ounces) | 1 pound Priority Mail price for zone 4 plus the fee |
| More than 1 pound but not more than 2 pounds | 2 pound Priority Mail price for zone 4 plus the fee |

## How do I Request a Refund for Every Door Direct Mail® (EDDM®)?

## Requesting a refund for EDDM.      - Aug 14, 2020 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006886

Customer Information

**Refunds are processed when:**

- USPS® systems do not indicate any barcode scan events for the Every Door Direct Mail mailing and when there is no other evidence that the mailing was delivered.
- USPS® systems do not indicate that a refund has been previously requested or processed for the Every Door Direct Mail® mailing.

[**Instructions to request a refund for orders placed and paid for online**](https://www.usps.com/help/refunds.htm#eddm)

**How do I request a refund for an order paid at a Post Office™?**

- **Instructions to request a refund at the local Post Office™:**

For Every Door Direct Mail – Retail® mailings purchased at a Post Office, whether initiated online or directly at the Post Office, refunds can only be provided at the Post Office of Mailing where the purchase occurred with a completed PS Form 3533.

- **Instructions to cancel Pay at Post Office order that has not been inducted:**
  - If the customer is paying at their local Post Office or offices (depending upon the number of drop-off locations) they can cancel their order by simply not taking their bundles to the Post Offices.
  - Alternately, they could log into EDDM and cancel their order there by navigating to the order history tab. Then they could either click the “Cancel Order” button to cancel the entire order or they can click the “Remove from Order” button next to the Post Office drop off location they want to cancel.
  - It is not possible to cancel individual routes within each order.
  - No refund request is required because the order has not been inducted or paid for.

**How do I request a refund for an order paid by permit?**

- **Instructions to request a refund when using permit:**
  - If the order has not been completed in the Postal Wizard, or the mailing has not been inducted at a BMEU, your account will not be charged.
  - If you have inducted your BMEU mailing and would like to request a refund for a mailing that you reclaimed, you will need to request a refund through the BMEU associated with the permit used to pay for the order and fill out a PS 3533 form, _Application for Refund of Fees, Products and Withdrawal of Customer Accounts_.
  - For more information, please visit [http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1080980](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/604.htm#1080980) to review the “Exchanges and Refunds” section in the _Domestic Mail Manual_.

**How long will it take to receive a refund?**

Please allow 30 days for your credit card account statement to reflect this refund, as the processing of the refund is dependent upon your financial institution's policy.

**Can I request a refund for an individual route if I change my mind after completing an order?**

No. Refunds can only be granted for full orders or for individual Post Office™ drop-off locations at this time.

**I paid for my order online--why can’t I request a refund for it?**

Online refunds can only be granted for mailings that have not been inducted or refunded. If you feel you should be entitled to a refund, please call 877-747-6249.

## What Address Management Tools Are Available?

## Address Quality Solutions for City, State and Zip + four by carrier route and number of deliveries.      - Mar 29, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006873

Customer Information

The United States Postal Service® is interested in providing business mailers with tools to ensure that mail is sent correctly to the appropriate recipient the first time. This practice provides the following benefits:

- Maximizes USPS® ability to efficiently deliver mail as addressed.
- Costs less for business mailers.
- Limits unnecessary mail being resent, forwarded, returned and clogging up the mailstream.

The Office of Address Management is located at the National Customer Support Center (NCSC) in Memphis, Tennessee. The NCSC can be contacted at **1-800-238-3150 or email** [cds.ncsc@usps.gov](mailto:cds.ncsc@usps.gov). Address Management provides value-added product and service offerings, including:

- Address Information Systems (AIS) Database Products - Products to enhance address standardization.
- Address Management Products - Tools for addressing software solutions.
- Address Management Services - Evaluation, grading and certification services.
- Address Management Publications - Publications related to Address Management products and services.
- Addressing Standards for Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
- ZIP Code™ Lookup and Address Information
- Contact Address Management

You can find out more information about this by consulting with the [Address Quality](https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality) home page or [Address Information System Products Technical Guide](https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality/AIS_Products_Technical_Guide).

**Address Element Correction**

_Address Element Correction (AEC)_ is a service that corrects and standardizes address elements. AEC processing transforms problem addresses into accurate, standardized addresses, which allows mailers to take full advantage of automation discounts.

Further information on this process can be obtained from the [Address Element Correction website](https://postalpro.usps.com/address-quality/aec).

## What are cookies and security features on usps.com®?

## Basic encryption technology used on usps.com®. Explanation of cookies, how and why they are used, and opting out them.      - Nov 1, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006251

Customer Information

****Information about Cookies****

**What are cookies and why are they useful?**

A _**Cookie**_ is a tiny text file stored on your computer when you visit a website.  Cookies contain information such as your login or registration information, online shopping cart information, user preferences, and the last website you visited.

Cookies allow websites to maintain information on a particular user as they move from one web page to another.  This enables websites to recognize visitors and serve them based on their predetermined user preferences. See [USPS Privacy Policy](http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/welcome.htm#informationcollected) for more information on cookies.

**What are cookies and how does the United States Postal Service® use them?**

Cookies may be categorized as session or persistent, which describes the length of time that they stay on your system.  Both session and persistent cookies are used on the [usps.com](http://usps.com/) website. The following are detailed descriptions of how we use these cookies:

**_Session Cookie_**

- A session cookie is a small piece of textual information that a server places temporarily on your browser during the time your browser is open.  The cookies are erased once you close all browsers. We use session cookies in the following manner:

**_Log-on and log-off administration_**

- Log-on and registration are not required to browse our website.  If you decide to register with our website, so as, for example, to use one of our online services, session cookies help with the log-on and log-off process.  The cookies enable us to recognize your log-on ID when you log on so that we do not establish a duplicate registration record for you.

**_Transactions and website usability_**

- We use session cookies to improve how you navigate through [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) to maintain your online session as you browse over several pages; to store and prepopulate information so that you do not have to reenter the same information twice; and to retain your shopping cart information.  Session cookies may also be used to collect referral statistics when you click on a link or ad banner to or from usps.com.

**_Persistent and conduct transactions_**

- As examples, session cookies are used Cookie - A persistent cookie is a small piece of text stored on your computer's hard drive for a defined period of time, after which the cookie is erased.  We do not collect or link to personal information through persistent cookies without your express consent.

**_Ad Banner Cookie_**

- On behalf of the United States Postal Service™, persistent cookies and beacons may be set and managed by third party advertising suppliers to support ad banners.  Ad banner cookies are set when you view or click on an ad banner on [usps.com](http://usps.com/), or an ad banner on another website for a **USPS** product, and persist for no more than five years.

**Can I reject (opt-out of) ad banner cookies?**

Yes, you can reject or opt-out of ad banner cookies.  If you choose to do so we will no longer track any information from ad banners related to your visit.

For information on how to reject/opt-out of ad banner cookies visit: [Opt Out of Cookies](http://optout.networkadvertising.org/?c=1#!/)

**Can I reject (opt-out of) all cookies?**

Yes, you may opt-out of the use of all cookies.  You will still be able to access most features of [usps.com](http://usps.com/). However, certain features such as the shopping cart and personalization options will not work as well.

For information on how to reject/opt-out of all cookies visit: [Opt Out of All Cookies](http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/cookie-opt-out.htm)

**Using Secured Socket Layer (SSL)**

The United States Postal Service secures your information in transit to us and upon receipt by using the industry standard encryption software, Secured Socket Layer (SSL). The URL in your browser will change to HTTPS instead of HTTP when this security feature is invoked.  Your browser may also display a lock symbol on its bottom task bar line to indicate this secure transmission is in place.

**P3P Technology**

The Platform for Privacy Preferences Project (P3P) technology provides an automated way for users to gain more control over the use of personal information on websites they visit.  It is a web-based language for describing the privacy policy of a website in XML.  Using this language, P3P enables users to compare a website's privacy policy with the user's privacy preferences before deciding to share personal information with the website.

Visit our [Privacy Office Business Resource page](http://about.usps.com/who-we-are/privacy-policy/business-resources.htm#H2) for more information about P3P.

Customer Information 2
