## How Much Does Signature Confirmation™ Cost?

## Find the price for online and retail Signature Confirmation™ services.      - Nov 13, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006248

Customer Information

Fees vary for Signature Confirmation Service, depending on how it was purchased (Retail vs. Electronic). For detailed information on fees, go to [https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm](https://www.usps.com/ship/insurance-extra-services.htm) and click on "Show Details" under Signature Confirmation.

**Note:**  Postage may be paid with ordinary postage stamps, meter stamps or permit imprints.

**Retail vs. Electronic price for Signature Confirmation Service**

- The **retail fee** can be purchased at your local Post Office™.  The retail label, PS Form 153, is hot pink with a detachable receipt.  To see an image of PS Form 153, [click here](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps153.pdf). Or, you can print online [Shipping Labels](http://www.usps.com/shipping/label.htm) for Priority Mail service and qualify for the lower-fee electronic option. High-volume shippers can also print their own labels or purchase preprinted labels from the United States Postal Service®

- The **electronic fee** is for mailers who purchase postage online (for example, using Click-N-Ship®) or are capable of sending and receiving electronic files of their shipments.


Whichever fee you choose, you will have access to the same Signature Confirmation features and benefits.

## How Can I Find Delivery Information for Adult Signature Restricted Delivery?

## What delivery information is available, how long it is accessible, and the different ways it can be accessed.      - Jul 12, 2023 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006243

Customer Information

**What information is available?**

You can request the following information following delivery:

- Recipient’s signature.
- Recipient’s printed first initial and last name.
- Date, time and location of the delivery.

**How can I find out about the delivery status of _Adult Signature Required_ and _Adult Signature Restricted Delivery_?**

- Enter the article barcode number into the appropriate field in [USPS Tracking® available on USPS.com®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®")
- Call **1-800-222-1811**

Customer Service representatives are available as follows:

- Monday thru Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 8:30 p.m. ET
- Saturday:  8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET
- Sundays:  **Closed**
- Holidays:  **Closed** \[ [Click here for Postal Holidays](http://about.usps.com/news/events-calendar/postal-holidays.htm "When are Postal Holidays?")\]

**Is the Recipient’s Signature Viewable Online?**

The recipient's signature cannot be viewed online. A Proof of Delivery (POD) letter, which has a copy of the signature, can be obtained by email at no additional expense. Customers can request the POD by going to the [USPS Tracking®](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input "USPS.com® - USPS Tracking®") website on [USPS.com](http://usps.com/), entering the article barcode number of the mailpiece, and requesting a POD via email. PODs can be sent in the following ways:

Email requests are processed 24 hours a day, 365 days a year.

**How long is the delivery information available?**

The Proof of Delivery letter, which provides evidence of delivery (to whom the mail was delivered and date of delivery) and information about the recipient’s actual delivery address along with signature, is available up to 2 years from the date of mailing.

Delivery information that shows the progress of a mailpiece in transit to its destination remains on the USPS Tracking website for 45 days and will be available upon request up to 2 years after the item was mailed.

**How do I request the delivery status for multiple records?**

Each article barcode number is entered into the USPS Tracking website separately. However, Bulk Proof of Delivery is available to authorized commercial mailers when using a Signature Confirmation™ service, such as Adult Signature Required Service, Registered Mail®, or Restricted Delivery.

Customers receiving Bulk Proof of Delivery may obtain signature data through a signature extract file format. Customers interested in participating in Bulk Proof of Delivery service can refer to the [Postal Pro page on the Bulk Proof of Delivery Program](https://postalpro.usps.com/shipping/bulk-proof-delivery-program) for more details.

## What is a Firm Mailing Book?

## For multiple pieces being mailed at the same time with Extra Services. Eliminates the need to generate individual mailing receipts for each article.      - Sep 18, 2024 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000006229

Customer Information

**What is a Firm Book?**

**PS Form 3877, Firm Mailing Book for Accountable Mail**, is a multipage bound book used to mailing multiple pieces of mail at the same time that have Extra Services such as Certified Mail, COD, Priority Mail Express, and Insured Mail pieces. The use of the book or electronic facsimiles eliminates the need to generate individual mailing receipts for each article.

- For three or more pieces with Extra Services presented for mailing at one time, a mailer may use [PS Form 3877](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3877.pdf) [(Firm Mailing Book for Accountable Mail)](http://about.usps.com/forms/ps3877.pdf) or privately printed firm sheets, subject to payment of the applicable fee for each item listed. The sheets become the sender's receipts.
- For 50 pieces and more, PS Form 3877 may be submitted electronically to PostalOne! and processed at the BMEU.

Pricing for items listed in a Firm Mailing Book are per piece and can be found in Notice 123 under Certificate of Mailing under both [Domestic Extra Services and Fees](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#2746995) and [International Extra Services and Fees](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/Notice123.htm#2084579).

For more information about Certificate of Mailing, please see [Certificate of Mailing - The Basics](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certificate-of-Mailing-The-Basics)

## Where Should I put the Endorsement if I want my Mail Returned?

## Description of the proper location for mail endorsements.      - Nov 14, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000005007

Customer Information

Ancillary service endorsements are used by mailers to request an addressee’s new address and to provide the USPS with instructions on how to handle undeliverable-as-addressed pieces. Use of an ancillary service endorsement on a mailpiece obligates the mailer to pay any applicable fees for forwarding, return, and separate address notification charges.

Proper placement of the endorsement is required. If the endorsement cannot be seen or understood, you might not receive the service requested. A return address must be used and placed in the upper left corner of the address side of the mailpiece or the upper left corner of the addressing area.

The exhibit below shows the options for placing an endorsement. The endorsement shown (ADDRESS SERVICE REQUESTED) is just one of several options. See DMM [507.1.5](http://pe.usps.com/text/dmm300/507.htm#a_1_5) for a complete list of endorsements, treatment, and fee consequences according to the class of mail. The endorsement must be placed in one of these four positions:

![This graphic describes Meter Reply Mail layout standards as described in the text.](https://faq.usps.com/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka6t0000000bmTJ&feoid=00Nt0000001WgZD&refid=0EMt0000000UJJi)

1. Directly below the return address.
2. Directly above the delivery address area (which includes the delivery address block and any related nonaddress elements such as a barcode, keyline, or optional endorsement line).
3. Directly to the left of the postage area and below or to the left of any price marking.
4. Directly below the postage area and below any price marking.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6BY00000002ANYAY%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-is-the-Best-Way-to-Prepare-a-Parcel-for-Shipping) ## What is the Best Way to Prepare a Parcel for Shipping? ## Tips for preparing a parcel for mailing. - Dec 14, 2023 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000004868 Customer Information | Tips for Preparing a Parcel for Shipping | | --- | | **Box** | Select a box strong enough to protect the contents and large enough to accommodate cushioning. If reusing a box, cover all previous labels and markings with a heavy black marker or adhesive labels. Don’t reuse mailing boxes; they can weaken in the shipping process.
**Note**: Parcels cannot be accepted if markings visibly indicate that the box was used for shipping hazardous material. | | **Protecting** | Padding the contents with newspaper is a novel way to send news from home. Styrofoam and bubble wrap are also good choices. Close and shake the box. If it rattles, add additional padding to keep items from shifting. Use tissue and ink-free packing paper for delicates or pictures. Carefully pack glass and fragile hollow items, like vases, with newspaper or packing material to avoid damage due to shock. When mailing framed photographs, take the glass out of the frame and wrap it separately. | | **Sealing** | Tape the opening of the box and reinforce all seams with 2" wide tape. Use clear or brown packaging tape, reinforced packing tape or paper tape (do not use scotch tape). Cord, string or twine should not be used as it causes the package to get caught and possibly damaged in sorting. Reinforce the bottom of boxes with at least one strip of packing tape. | | **Addressing** | Packages should be addressed similar to the way cards and letters are addressed. For more information refer to [Addressing Mailpieces](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Addressing-Mailpieces). | Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title What is the Best Way to Prepare a Parcel for Shipping? ## What is a Large Envelope (Flat)?

## A Large Envelope is a rectangular mailpiece no thicker than 3/4 inch. They are commonly referred to as “Flats.”      - Nov 8, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004855

Customer Information

**A Large Envelope is a rectangular mailpiece no thicker than 3/4 inch. They are commonly referred to as “Flats.”  Flats must be:**

- Flexible
- Have four square corners (or finished corners not exceeding a radius of 0.125 (1/8) inch.
- Uniformly thick.
- Unwrapped, sleeved, wrapped, or enveloped.

Flats not meeting the above criteria are not eligible for large envelope pricing and would be charged the applicable parcel price.

The dimensions of a flat must exceed at least one of the minimum dimensions listed below (under the "More than" column). Length is the longest dimension of the large envelope. The height is the dimension perpendicular to the length.

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Measurement** |  | **More than** |  | **Maximum** |
|  |
| **Height** |  | **6-1/8"** |  | **12"** |
| **Length** |  | **11-1/2"** |  | **15"** |
| **Thickness** |  | **1/4"** |  | **3/4"** |

[Back to Top](https://usps.my.salesforce.com/ckeditor/ckeditor-4.x/rel/#topofthedocument)

## How does USPS® handle nonmailable items?

## Items considered "nonmailable" and how they're handled.      - Sep 26, 2022 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004851

Customer Information

**The USPS considers a mailpiece** _**“nonmailable”**_ **if it cannot be accepted for processing even in non-automation areas, such as mailpieces not meeting minimum sizes or overweight mailpieces.**

Items may be considered nonmailable based on different conditions, including:

- Violating minimum / maximum size or weight, or shape restrictions.
- Illegible address.
- Prohibited material.

If presented for mailing at a local Post Office™, nonmailable items are refused and not placed into the mail system.

**What happens if a nonmailable item is mailed?**

Occasionally a nonmailable item is mailed anyway (such as dropped in a Collection Box receptacle). Generally, nonmailable pieces are returned to the sender immediately as soon as the reason for nonmailability is discovered.

There may be rare cases where a nonmailable item is not directly returned to the sender, based on:

- [Hazard of sending through the mail](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/What-Does-USPS-Classify-as-Hazardous-Materials).
- Nonmailability is due to a legal violation.
- Packaging does not allow return or provide sufficient address to return.

For a complete listing of all mailing standards, please visit [Postal Explorer](https://pe.usps.gov/text/dmm300/601.htm).

## Can I Mail a Colored Envelope?

## Using colored cards and envelopes for mailing has no interference with reading information or postmark.      - Nov 9, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004847

Customer Information

You may use colored cards and envelopes for mailing (as well as different ink colors) if there is **no interference** with the **reading of the information or postmark**.

- First and foremost, the mailing recipient's information should be clear and legible.
- The color contrast between the written information and the mail piece background must be kept at a reasonable degree.
- A brilliant colored background or reverse printing is not permitted.

If you require further information on colored cards and envelopes (including printing), refer to the Mailing Requirements area of your local Post Office™ facility.

Close Search for a topic SearchSearchClose Search for a topic Loading Search for a topic End of Search Dialog ![](https://faq.usps.com/file-asset/FAQHero?v=1) [Share via email](mailto:?subject=ka6eq0000000MVBAA2%20Article%20Link&body=https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Ship-Lithium-Batteries) ## Can I Ship Lithium Batteries? ## Where to locate current regulations regarding the mailability of lithium batteries. - Feb 26, 2025 - Knowledge ### Information Article Number 000004846 Customer Information **Domestic Shipments** Yes, lithium batteries can be shipped domestically, including Alaska and Hawaii. See the ["Lithium Batteries" entry on the _Domestic Shipping Prohibitions & Restrictions_ page](https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm#lithium-batteries) for USPS' requirements regarding domestic transportation and packaging of lithium batteries. **Domestic Lithium Battery Mailability** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Small, Consumer-Type Lithium Battery Shipped...** | **Surface Transportation** | **Air Transportation** | | **Contained in (properly installed in equipment)** | Mailable | Mailable | | **Packed with equipment, but not installed in the equipment** | Mailable | Mailable | | **Without the equipment they operate (individual batteries in originally sealed packaging)** | Mailable | Prohibited\* | | **Damaged, Defective, or Recalled Batteries** | Prohibited, unless approved by the director, Product Classification. | Prohibited, unless approved by the director, Product Classification. | | \*For small, rechargeable consumer-type batteries, mailable intra-Alaska via air transportation is limited to 8 cells or 2 batteries
See the [Domestic Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c3_028.htm#ep1016000) for detailed information | **International Shipments** Customers can take to their local Post Office packages containing **new** lithium batteries properly installed in, but not packed with, electronic devices that are bound for **many** international destinations and Army (APO), Fleet (FPO) and Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) locations. See the ["Lithium Batteries" entry on the _International Shipping Restrictions, Prohibitions, & HAZMAT_ page](https://www.usps.com/international/shipping-restrictions.htm#lithium-batteries) for USPS' requirements regarding international transportation of lithium batteries. | | | --- | | **What is prohibited and may not be mailed internationally or to, from, or between APO, FPO, or DPO locations?**
- New lithium batteries packed with, but not installed in, or sent separately from equipment.
- Used, damaged, defective, or recalled lithium batteries installed in, packed with, or sent separately from equipment.
- All pre-owned, damaged, or defective electronic devices containing or packaged with lithium batteries. | **International Lithium Battery Mailability** | | | | --- | --- | | **Small, Consumer-Type Lithium Battery Shipped...** | **International and APO/FPO/DPO Transportation** | | **Contained in (properly installed in equipment)** | Mailable | | **Packed with equipment, but not installed in the equipment** | Prohibited | | **Without the equipment they operate (individual batteries in originally sealed packaging)** | Prohibited | | See the [International Mail Manual](https://pe.usps.com/text/pub52/pub52c6_004.htm#ep273018) for detailed information | Also, different countries and APO/FPO/DPO locations may have specific requirements. If that is the case: - See the [Postal Explorer](https://pe.usps.gov/) or the [Individual Country Listings of the International Mail Manual](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/welcome.htm) for information on specific country restrictions. - See the information for the destination ZIP Code™ in the article titled "Overseas Military/Diplomatic Mail" published in the latest issue of the [_Postal Bulletin_](https://about.usps.com/resources/postal-bulletin.htm) for specific APO, FPO, or DPO restrictions. [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Ship-Lithium-Batteries#top) **What kind of electronic devices contain lithium batteries?** Examples of common electronic devices containing lithium batteries include: cameras, video cameras, mobile/smart phones, Bluetooth headsets, laptop computers, shavers, portable DVD players, tablets, two-way radios, and GPS devices. ![Description: Examples of items that use lithium batteries](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/AI9090.jpg) **Where can I find the requirements for shipping lithium batteries?** The Federal Register final rule as well as the IMM, DMM and Publication 52 revisions will be available at [_pe.usps.com_](https://pe.usps.gov/). | | | --- | | Watch this [video on mailing lithium batteries](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_5EIjOwbZ_U) on the [USPS TV YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/user/uspstv/custom) channel. | [![Back to top icon](https://www.usps.com/c360/images/iconography/top_icon.png)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Can-I-Ship-Lithium-Batteries#top) Customer Information 2 Customer Information 3 Customer Information 4 Customer Information 5 Title Can I Ship Lithium Batteries? ## How do I address mail "In care of"?

## Proper format for sending mail to someone "in care of" another person.      - Nov 14, 2018 - Knowledge

### Information

Article Number

000004841

Customer Information

If you would like to send mail to an individual (John Doe) in care of another person (Robert Smith), use the following format:

**JOHN DOE**

**C/O ROBERT SMITH**

**123 APPLE WAY**

**CITY ST 99999-9999**

If a signature is required, either person may sign for mail addressed to one person in care of another. Forwarding of mail using this format will be forwarded to the new address of the c/o person or entity address (that is on the envelope).
